title: fix(tests/tenant): _sentinel_value_for missing 'bool' branch — breaks ExpenseApproved cross-delivery test
description:
## Problem
`tests/tenant/test_event_subscriptions.py::TestEventCrossDelivery::test_b_bound_subscriber_drops_a_scoped_event[expense.approved]` fails because the test helper `_sentinel_value_for` falls through to the default ULID-string sentinel for any annotation it doesn't recognise. The cd-9guk reshape of `ExpenseApproved` introduced a `had_edits: bool` field, and Pydantic v2 strict bool parsing rejects the ULID string.

## Fix
Add a `bool` branch to `_sentinel_value_for` returning `False` (matching the `Literal` and `list` branches added previously for `task_comment`).

## Acceptance
- [ ] `tests/tenant/test_event_subscriptions.py` passes for every parametrised event kind.
- [ ] No regression elsewhere.

## Test plan
`uv run pytest tests/tenant/test_event_subscriptions.py -x -q`

title: Self-review: fix(redact): JWT regex over-broad — redacts dotted event names
description:
Selfreview paired with the JWT regex tightening task.

`/selfreview` in autofix mode against the working-tree changes.

title: fix(redact): JWT regex over-broad — redacts dotted event names like 'worker.tick.start'
description:
## Problem
The JWT credential regex in `app/util/redact.py` matches any three dot-separated alphanumeric+underscore+dash segments. This matches legitimate structured-logging event names like `worker.tick.start` / `worker.tick.end`, causing them to be replaced with `<redacted:credential>` before downstream consumers (log assertions, observability) see them.

This breaks `tests/integration/worker/test_idempotency_sweep.py::TestSweepJobEndToEnd::test_expired_row_deleted_heartbeat_advances_count_logged` in the full suite (passes in isolation because the redactor isn't initialized in the narrower test context).

## Root cause
`_JWT_RE: Final[re.Pattern[str]] = re.compile(r"\\b[A-Za-z0-9_\\-]+\\.[A-Za-z0-9_\\-]+\\.[A-Za-z0-9_\\-]+\\b")`

Real JWTs have base64url-encoded segments that are at minimum ~10 chars each (header is typically 30+, payload 50+, signature 40+). The current regex allows segments as short as 1 char, so `a.b.c` and `worker.tick.end` match.

## Fix
Tighten the JWT regex to require minimum segment lengths typical of real JWTs (e.g. 16+ chars per segment, or at least 30+ chars total). Real JWTs are well above any such floor; legitimate dotted event names ('app.worker.task') are well below.

Add explicit unit tests in `tests/unit/test_redact_jwt_boundary.py`:
- Real-shape JWT redacted.
- Dotted event names ('worker.tick.start', 'app.expense.created', 'task.completed') NOT redacted.
- Short faux-JWT 'a.b.c' NOT redacted.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `tests/integration/worker/test_idempotency_sweep.py` passes in the full suite.
- [ ] Existing redact unit tests still pass.
- [ ] New boundary tests cover the dotted-event-name surface.

## Test plan
`uv run pytest tests/unit/test_redact_jwt_boundary.py tests/integration/worker/test_idempotency_sweep.py -x -q`
`uv run pytest -x -q` (full suite to confirm)

title: fix(boundaries): allow-list new domain→adapters edges in import-linter (cd-7rfu/cd-9guk/cd-95zb/cd-cfe4/cd-0bnz/cd-dv2)
description:
## Problem
The import-linter test gate (`tests/boundary/test_import_gate.py::test_clean_tree_passes`) is failing on `main` because recent domain modules introduce `app.domain → app.adapters` edges that aren't in the v1 stopgap allow-list at `pyproject.toml [tool.importlinter]`.

## Failing edges
**"Domain forbids adapters (except ports)" contract:**
- app.domain.expenses.approval → app.adapters.db.expenses.models
- app.domain.expenses.approval → app.adapters.db.workspace.models
- app.domain.expenses.approval → app.authz
- app.domain.expenses.autofill → app.adapters.db.expenses.models
- app.domain.expenses.autofill → app.adapters.db.llm.models
- app.domain.expenses.claims → app.adapters.db.expenses.models
- app.domain.expenses.claims → app.adapters.db.workspace.models
- app.domain.expenses.claims → app.authz
- app.domain.identity.membership → app.services.employees.service (transitive via membership → employees)
- app.domain.messaging.push_tokens → app.adapters.db.messaging.models
- app.domain.messaging.push_tokens → app.adapters.db.workspace.models
- app.domain.tasks.comments → app.adapters.db.identity.models
- app.domain.tasks.comments → app.adapters.db.tasks.models
- app.domain.tasks.comments → app.adapters.db.workspace.models
- app.domain.tasks.comments → app.authz
- app.services.employees.service → app.adapters.db.workspace.models (covered by domain → adapters too)

**"Domain forbids handlers" contract:**
- app.domain.expenses.claims → app.authz (transitive to app.api.deps)
- app.domain.expenses.approval → app.authz (transitive to app.api.deps)
- app.domain.tasks.comments → app.authz (transitive to app.api.deps)

## Fix
Add allow-list entries to `pyproject.toml` under each contract's `ignore_imports`, mirroring the existing v1 stopgap pattern (one comment block per service explaining the temporary nature, all tracked by cd-duv6 for the long-term Protocol-seam refactor).

## Acceptance
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/boundary/test_import_gate.py -x -q` passes.
- [ ] `uv run lint-imports` exits 0.
- [ ] No real architectural change — only allow-list entries.

## Test plan
`uv run pytest tests/boundary/test_import_gate.py -x -q`

title: feat(api/identity): work_engagements / work_roles / user_work_roles HTTP routers
description:
Ships the first tranche of the identity-adjacent HTTP routers from spec `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"Users / work roles / settings":

```
GET    /work_engagements          # ?user_id=…
GET    /work_engagements/{id}
PATCH  /work_engagements/{id}
POST   /work_engagements/{id}/archive
POST   /work_engagements/{id}/reinstate

GET    /users/{id}/user_work_roles
POST   /user_work_roles
PATCH  /user_work_roles/{id}
DELETE /user_work_roles/{id}

GET    /work_roles
POST   /work_roles
PATCH  /work_roles/{id}
```

Scope covers the three tables whose SQLAlchemy models already exist (cd-5kv4 work_role + user_work_role; cd-4saj work_engagement). Routers mount under `/w/<slug>/api/v1` with tag `identity` + resource-specific tag, cursor-paginated listings, `work_roles.manage` authz gate on mutations + `scope.view` on reads.

### Split-offs (filed as follow-up Beads tasks)

The remaining spec §12 endpoints need DB schema work first:

- **cd-e4m3** + **cd-za6n** — property_work_role_assignment table + router
- **cd-l2r9** — user_leave + user_availability_override + user_weekly_availability + public_holiday tables
- **cd-oydd** — /user_leaves router (depends on cd-l2r9)
- **cd-uqw1** — /user_availability_overrides router (depends on cd-l2r9)
- **cd-rd68** — /public_holidays router (depends on cd-l2r9)
- **cd-6uij** — /me/schedule + /me/leaves + /me/availability_overrides (depends on cd-oydd + cd-uqw1)

Paired selfreview tasks: cd-a5a3, cd-y8m5, cd-4fue, cd-zen7, cd-7v7w, cd-59bd, cd-mpyw.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] work_engagements GET list + GET {id} + PATCH + POST archive + POST reinstate
- [ ] work_roles GET list + POST + PATCH {id}
- [ ] user_work_roles GET /users/{id}/user_work_roles + POST + PATCH + DELETE
- [ ] Routes tagged `identity` + resource-specific tag
- [ ] Cursor-paginated GET listings (`{data, next_cursor, has_more}` envelope)
- [ ] Action-key `work_roles.manage` gates mutations; default-allow gate on reads
- [ ] Pydantic Read/Create/Update/Patch schemas per resource
- [ ] Tests cover happy path, auth denial, validation, edge cases (unknown id, cross-workspace probe collapses to 404, etc.)

Parent: cd-rpxd

title: feat(api/identity): permission_groups + role_grants + /permissions HTTP routers
description:
Spec `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"Users / work roles / settings" pins a large block of identity HTTP surface that has NO router today:

```
GET    /users/{id}/role_grants
POST   /role_grants
PATCH  /role_grants/{id}
DELETE /role_grants/{id}

GET    /permission_groups
POST   /permission_groups
GET    /permission_groups/{id}
PATCH  /permission_groups/{id}
DELETE /permission_groups/{id}
GET    /permission_groups/{id}/members
POST   /permission_groups/{id}/members
DELETE /permission_groups/{id}/members/{user_id}

GET    /permission_rules
POST   /permission_rules
DELETE /permission_rules/{id}

GET    /permissions/action_catalog
GET    /permissions/resolved
```

The domain services already exist (`app/domain/identity/permission_groups.py`, `app/authz`); what's missing is the router layer + pydantic Read/Create/Update/Patch schemas.

Implementation notes:
- Mount under `/w/<slug>/api/v1` via a new router file (`app/api/v1/identity/role_grants.py` + `.../permission_groups.py`, or fold into a single `app/api/v1/identity/` subpackage).
- Tag each route with both `identity` (spec §01 context map) and a resource-specific tag (`role_grants`, `permission_groups`, `permissions`).
- Honour the `api_tokens.manage`-style action-gate pattern from `app/api/v1/auth/tokens.py`: `groups.create`, `groups.edit`, `groups.manage_members`, `groups.manage_owners_membership`, `permissions.edit_rules` per §05.
- Cursor pagination on listings that can exceed the fixed-cap budget (permission_rules especially).

title: feat(db/llm): priority / enabled / tuning on model_assignment + llm_capability_inheritance table
description:
## Problem / goal
**cd-k0qf** (LLM router resolver) requires schema additions that
cd-cm5 deliberately deferred as part of its v1-slice scope. Without
this foundation:

- cd-k0qf cannot order the per-capability chain (needs
  \`priority\`).
- cd-k0qf cannot satisfy its *"Disabling every assignment for a
  capability raises CapabilityUnassignedError"* criterion (needs
  \`enabled\`).
- cd-k0qf cannot implement its *"chat.admin inherits chat.manager's
  chain"* criterion (needs \`llm_capability_inheritance\`).
- cd-k0qf's \`ModelPick\` payload (\`max_tokens\`, \`temperature\`,
  \`extra_api_params\`, \`required_capabilities\`, \`assignment_id\`)
  has nowhere to read from.

## Technical context
- **Key files**:
  - \`app/adapters/db/llm/models.py\` (extend \`ModelAssignment\`,
    add \`LlmCapabilityInheritance\`).
  - \`app/adapters/db/llm/__init__.py\` (register the new table +
    re-export).
  - \`migrations/versions/20260424_1400_e5f6a7b8c9d0_llm_cd_cm5.py\`
    — current head; revises to \`e5f6a7b8c9d0\`.
- **Spec anchors**:
  - \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §\"model_assignment\" /
    §\"llm_assignment\" / §\"llm_capability_inheritance\" /
    §\"llm_provider_model\" — authoritative shape.
  - \`docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md\` §\"Model assignment\",
    §\"Capability inheritance\", §\"Capability defaults (seeds)\",
    §\"Provider / model / provider-model registry\".
- **Prior art**: cd-cm5's migration
  (\`20260424_1400_e5f6a7b8c9d0_llm_cd_cm5.py\`) — mirror its
  \`CheckConstraint\` / \`Index\` / FK CASCADE / \`JSON\`
  \`server_default\` patterns; cd-pjm's
  \`tests/integration/test_db_messaging.py::TestRegistryIntent\` —
  template for re-import idempotence coverage.

## Implementation notes

### Part A — extend \`ModelAssignment\`
Add columns on \`app/adapters/db/llm/models.py\`:
- \`priority: Mapped[int]\` — \`Integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\`;
  \`server_default=sa.text(\"0\")\`; ORM \`default=0\`. Lower =
  higher priority. CHECK \`priority >= 0\` (sanity).
- \`enabled: Mapped[bool]\` — \`Boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT true\`;
  \`server_default=sa.true()\`; ORM \`default=True\`.
- \`max_tokens: Mapped[int | None]\` — \`Integer nullable\`.
  Null = use model default.
- \`temperature: Mapped[float | None]\` — \`Float nullable\`.
- \`extra_api_params: Mapped[dict[str, Any]]\` — \`JSON NOT NULL
  DEFAULT '{}'\`; \`server_default=\"{}\"\` (bare string, matching
  the \`agent_token.scope_json\` / \`approval_request.action_json\`
  pattern); ORM \`default=dict\`.
- \`required_capabilities: Mapped[list[str]]\` — \`JSON NOT NULL
  DEFAULT '[]'\`; \`server_default=\"[]\"\`; ORM \`default=list\`.

Drop the existing \`uq_model_assignment_workspace_capability\`
unique index on \`(workspace_id, capability)\`. Replace with a
non-unique index \`ix_model_assignment_workspace_capability_priority\`
on \`(workspace_id, capability, priority)\` to back the resolver's
sorted scan (and preserve the per-capability lookup).

### Part B — new \`LlmCapabilityInheritance\`
\`\`\`
id                 String PK (ULID, 26 chars — plain String per
                   the cd-yesa harmonisation)
workspace_id       String FK(workspace.id) ON DELETE CASCADE NOT NULL
capability         String NOT NULL
inherits_from      String NOT NULL
created_at         DateTime(timezone=True) NOT NULL DEFAULT utcnow
\`\`\`
Constraints / indexes:
- CHECK \`ck_llm_capability_inheritance_no_self_loop\`:
  \`capability <> inherits_from\`.
- Unique index \`uq_llm_capability_inheritance_workspace_capability\`
  on \`(workspace_id, capability)\` — one inheritance edge per
  capability per workspace.

Cycle detection across multiple rows is a write-path concern (out
of scope here — the API / admin layer that writes this table must
validate).

### Part C — registration & migration
- Register \`llm_capability_inheritance\` in the tenancy registry in
  \`app/adapters/db/llm/__init__.py\` (mirror the existing \`register\`
  calls for the cd-cm5 tables). Both new columns on
  \`ModelAssignment\` already inherit the row's existing workspace
  scoping.
- Extend \`__all__\` to export \`LlmCapabilityInheritance\`.
- Migration file:
  \`migrations/versions/20260424_1500_f6a7b8c9d0e1_llm_assignment_extras_cd_<NEW>.py\`
  with \`revision = \"f6a7b8c9d0e1\"\`, \`down_revision = \"e5f6a7b8c9d0\"\`.
- Upgrade:
  1. \`op.drop_index(\"uq_model_assignment_workspace_capability\", ...)\`
  2. Add the 6 new columns on \`model_assignment\`
     (\`batch_alter_table\` so SQLite can round-trip cleanly).
  3. Add CHECK \`priority >= 0\`.
  4. Create new non-unique composite index on
     \`(workspace_id, capability, priority)\`.
  5. \`op.create_table('llm_capability_inheritance', ...)\` with
     CHECK + unique index + FK CASCADE.
- Downgrade reverses all of the above in FK-safe order.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`alembic upgrade head\` + \`downgrade -1\` + \`upgrade head\`
      round-trips cleanly on SQLite **and** Postgres
      (\`CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres\`).
- [ ] Two \`ModelAssignment\` rows with the same
      \`(workspace_id, capability)\` but different \`priority\`
      coexist (regression test).
- [ ] A second \`LlmCapabilityInheritance\` row with the same
      \`(workspace_id, capability)\` is rejected by the unique
      index.
- [ ] Inserting \`capability = inherits_from\` is rejected by CHECK
      (SQLite + Postgres).
- [ ] A bare insert (all nullable + defaulted fields omitted) yields
      \`priority=0\`, \`enabled=True\`, \`extra_api_params={}\`,
      \`required_capabilities=[]\` on the hydrated ORM row.
- [ ] Tenancy filter auto-injects \`workspace_id\` on reads against
      \`llm_capability_inheritance\` (new row in the
      \`TestTenantFilter\` parametrize list).
- [ ] Cross-workspace isolation: a row in workspace A is invisible
      under a workspace-B context.
- [ ] \`tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py\` green on both
      backends.
- [ ] \`test_reimport_is_idempotent\` in \`tests/unit/test_db_llm.py\`
      extended to cover \`LlmCapabilityInheritance\`.

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_llm.py tests/integration/test_db_llm.py \
  tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py -x -q
CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_llm.py \
  tests/integration/test_db_llm.py tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py -x -q
uv run alembic downgrade -1
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run mypy --strict app/adapters/db/llm/
uv run ruff check
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. Insert two \`ModelAssignment\` rows with
   \`(workspace, 'chat.manager', priority=0)\` + \`priority=1\`.
2. \`SELECT * FROM model_assignment WHERE workspace_id=… AND
   capability='chat.manager' ORDER BY priority ASC\` → both rows,
   ordered.
3. Insert \`(workspace, capability='chat.admin', inherits_from='chat.manager')\`
   then try the same pair again → \`IntegrityError\` from the unique
   index.
4. Try \`(workspace, capability='x', inherits_from='x')\` → rejected
   by CHECK.

title: Self-review: chore(worker): wire prune_expired_idempotency_keys into APScheduler daily tick

title: Self-review: feat(auth/recovery): workspace kill-switch
description:

Focus areas from coder:
1. Residual timing side-channel: kill-switch skips mailer — distinguishable from hit/miss by latency. Spec doesn't forbid but should we close?
2. Fresh-UoW audit failure swallowed via except Exception — mirrors pattern from _audit_recovery_refusal.
3. 'Non-archived' = every extant role_grant for v1; future cd-x1xh soft-retire needs a WHERE clause extension.
4. settings_json raw read vs future cd-n6p cascade resolver.
5. §03 step 5 'every' → 'any' edit — confirm intended semantics.
6. Option A (modified): uses workspaces.settings_json column (§02 canonical) instead of new workspace_setting table. Spec-aligned.
7. Migration a1b2c3d4e5f6 — ALTER TABLE workspace ADD settings_json JSON NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'. SQLite + Postgres compat.
8. Audit row cause taxonomy: recovery.disabled_by_workspace.

Quality gates + scoped pytest (tests/unit/auth/test_recovery.py, tests/integration/auth/test_recovery_full_flow.py, tests/integration/test_db_workspace.py).

title: Self-review: feat(auth/tokens): delegated + PAT token kinds
description:
Auto-fix selfreview of cd-i1qe. Depends on cd-i1qe. Do NOT commit.

Focus areas from coder:
1. Kind vocabulary scoped/delegated/personal (vs task's standalone/delegated/pat) — spec wins, confirm.
2. TTL defaults per spec (scoped 90d, delegated 30d, personal 90d) — task said 24h for delegated. Spec wins here; reconcile or flag.
3. PAT on /api/v1/me/tokens bare-host vs workspace-scoped.
4. workspace_id nullability + CHECK constraints (ORM + DDL dual-layer).
5. Cap model: 5 workspace tokens + 5 PATs.
6. revoke() refuses PAT ids (404) vs revoke_personal() refuses workspace ids.
7. list_tokens excludes PATs.
8. PAT mint/revoke write NO audit row — tracked as cd-t2su. Confirm this is acceptable to ship.
9. verify() does NOT gate on delegating-user liveness — tracked as cd-et6y. Confirm acceptable.
10. FK ON DELETE: user_id CASCADE; delegate_for + subject_user SET NULL.
11. /auth/tokens routes lack operation_id (not added) — pre-existing.
12. Two CHECK constraints (ORM + DDL) — defensive duplication.
13. Test isolation patterns.

Run quality gates: ruff, mypy --strict, scoped pytest (tests/unit/auth/test_tokens.py + tests/integration/auth/ + tests/integration/test_migration_cd_i1qe.py + tests/integration/test_db_identity.py + tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py).
close_reason: Autofix selfreview complete. Fixed migration downgrade data-loss bug (DELETE PAT rows before narrowing workspace_id NOT NULL). Added DB-level CHECK constraint tests (7 XOR branches) + 401 coverage for /me/tokens GET/DELETE. Raised cd-t2su (PAT audit), cd-et6y (delegator liveness) to P1 blockers. Filed cd-nld2, cd-a2o3, cd-qj5u. All quality gates green.

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text:
Autofix complete. Findings fixed:
1. BUG: migration downgrade would fail on Postgres with PAT rows (NULL workspace_id → NOT NULL). Added op.execute('DELETE FROM api_token WHERE kind=personal') before narrowing; updated module docstring. New test test_downgrade_purges_personal_rows_preserves_scoped verifies the scenario.
2. MISSING: DB-level CHECK constraint coverage. Added TestApiTokenKindShapeConstraint with 7 tests exercising every XOR branch of ck_api_token_kind_shape + allowlist rejection via raw INSERT — catches a drift between the migration and the ORM CheckConstraint.
3. MISSING: /me/tokens 401 coverage on GET and DELETE. Added test_get_without_session_cookie_is_401 and test_delete_without_session_cookie_is_401.
4. NITPICK: stale docstring in me_tokens.py referencing _ERR_SCOPES_REQUIRED / _ERR_ME_SCOPE_CONFLICT that never existed. Replaced with the actual spec error codes.
5. RISKY: comments claiming PATs 'write a tenant-agnostic audit row' when the branch is pass. Rewrote to explicitly flag the gap + cd-t2su as a prod blocker, fixed the stale cd-i1qe-pat-audit reference to cd-t2su in both mint() and revoke_personal() docstrings.
6. RISKY: verify() docstring did not flag the cd-et6y delegator-liveness gap. Added a .. note block explicitly stating it is a prod blocker.
7. Beads grooming: raised cd-t2su + cd-et6y to P1 with blocker comments; filed cd-nld2 (operation_id on /auth/tokens), cd-a2o3 (50-workspace vs 5-per-user cap reconciliation), cd-qj5u (DELETE vs POST /revoke verb reconciliation for /me/tokens).

All 125 scoped tests pass (115 prior + 10 new). ruff / ruff format / mypy --strict / lint-imports all clean. Spec divergences (cap-50, PAT verb) flagged as Beads, not patched — those are cross-cutting decisions the user should own.

description:

Auto-fixing self-review of cd-iojn. Catch bugs, missing pieces, unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-iojn**.

## How to run
Run `/selfreview` in **autofix mode**. Do NOT commit or push — Director invokes commiter.

## Focus areas flagged by cd-iojn coder
- **Per-user vs per-session invalidation**: `invalidate_for_user` logs out ALL sessions for that user (other tabs, saved curl). Intentional per task, but confirm this matches §03 Sessions semantics and spec text doesn't mandate per-session.
- **`validate()` side-effect of sliding-refresh audit row firing right before invalidation**: harmless but unusual. Confirm ordering in the UoW.
- **`secure=True` on clear header**: dev http cookies won't actually clear via this header. Browser clears them on navigation but follow-up from saved curl could still present.
- **Empty cookie_value shape**: relied on implicit contract in build_session_cookie. Consider explicit build_clear_cookie helper.
- **CSRF**: /api/v1/* is in SKIP_PATHS — verify logout is OK to skip (matches login pattern).
- **Spec docs edits**: changes to §15 and §12 correct and complete?

- [ ] ruff/mypy/scoped pytest all green

title: fix(auth/signup): complete_signup must forward 'now' into passkey.register_finish_signup
description:
## Problem / goal
tests/unit/auth/test_signup.py::TestCompleteSignupHappy::test_creates_all_rows_in_one_transaction
fails on main whenever the wall-clock passes 2026-04-20 12:11 UTC —
i.e. ~11 min after midnight UTC on the test's pinned _PINNED date.
The test is a time bomb and on today's run (2026-04-20 12:52 UTC) it
already blows up.

Root cause: app/auth/signup.py::complete_signup resolves
resolved_now = now if now is not None else _now(clock), then calls
app/auth/passkey.py::register_finish_signup(..., clock=clock). The
test passes now=_PINNED+2min but NOT a Clock, so
register_finish_signup's _now(clock) reads the real wall-clock, which
is well past the challenge's expires_at (_PINNED + 11min). Result:
ChallengeExpired, test FAILS.

Three other signup tests around line 1185/1241 share the same shape
and will likely fail next time they're run past 12:11 UTC on the
pinned day.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md §"Challenge TTL",
  §"Time pinning in tests".
- **Files**: app/auth/signup.py:962 (the offending call site);
  app/auth/passkey.py:878 register_finish_signup (needs a now param,
  or needs to accept a Clock that the tests can freeze).

## Fix options
1. **Plumb `now` through** (preferred — cheapest + no new abstraction):
   - Add now: datetime | None = None to register_finish_signup.
   - complete_signup forwards resolved_now to it.
   - Tests unchanged.
2. **Inject a freezing Clock**: overbuild for one signature.

Prefer option 1.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] test_creates_all_rows_in_one_transaction passes at any wall-clock
- [ ] All other time-pinned signup tests pass at any wall-clock
- [ ] register_finish_signup still falls back to _now(clock) when no
      now is given (auth router path)
- [ ] uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_signup.py -x -q green

## Test plan
- Set system time to 2026-04-30 (past all _PINNED challenges):
  date -u; uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_signup.py -x -q

Discovered via cd-x7fa self-review of cd-q1be. The bug predates
cd-q1be — surfaced because today's date (2026-04-20) sits on the
_PINNED day and run time is past 12:11 UTC.

title: feat(web/types): split + copy mocks/web/src/types/api.ts into domain modules
description:
## Problem / goal
The shared TypeScript types used by every web surface live in
`mocks/web/src/types/api.ts` — a single 1,548-line file covering
properties, bookings, tasks, payroll, expenses, assets, approvals,
agents, LLM wiring, admin surfaces, and more. 74 files import from
`@/types/api`. Without it, nothing that the other web tasks
"copy verbatim" will compile.

Promote these types into `app/web/src/types/` — behaviour-preserving —
**split by domain** into smaller, reviewable modules so the next
editor doesn't have to scroll through a 1.5k-line file to find
`Booking` vs. `ApprovalRequest`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Data layer" (types
  mirror the FastAPI dataclasses; dates are ISO-8601 strings, enums
  are literal string unions).
- **Reference (source of truth for shape)**: `mocks/web/src/types/api.ts`.
- The types mirror the backend dataclasses — once the real API is
  up, cross-check shape parity against the OpenAPI schema.

## Implementation notes
- **Split by domain** into `app/web/src/types/` with one file per
  bounded context, each re-exported from a barrel `index.ts` so
  consumers can keep their existing `from "@/types/api"` imports
  via a shim. Suggested split (final names negotiable but keep
  each file under ~250 lines):
  - `core.ts` — `Role`, `Theme`, `ResolvedTheme`.
  - `property.ts` — `Property`, `PropertyColor`, `PropertyKind`,
    `PropertyWorkspace*`, `PropertyClosure`, `Stay*`.
  - `employee.ts` — `Employee`, `Leave`, `PayRule`, `PayPeriod`,
    `PaySlip`.
  - `task.ts` — `Task*`, `ChecklistItem*`, `TaskTemplate`,
    `Schedule`, `Instruction`, `Issue`, `TaskComment`.
  - `booking.ts` — `Booking*`, `StayLifecycleRule`.
  - `expense.ts` — `Expense*`, `ExchangeRate*`,
    `PendingReimbursement*`, `AutofillField`, `ExpenseScanResult`.
  - `asset.ts` — asset / inventory / documents types
    (`InventoryItem`, `InventoryMovement*`, etc.).
  - `approval.ts` — `ApprovalRequest`, `GateSource`,
    `GateDestination`, `InlineChannel`.
  - `llm.ts` — `ModelAssignment`, `LLMCall`, `LlmProvider*`.
  - `index.ts` — barrel re-exporting every type.
- **Preserve every name and shape verbatim.** No renames, no shape
  tweaks, no optional-vs-required flips. If something looks wrong,
  file a separate bug — do NOT fix it here.
- Keep the spec comments (`// §02 —`, `// §06 —`, etc.) attached to
  the types they annotate; they're load-bearing for future readers.
- Update `tsconfig` path aliases only if needed; default
  `@/types/...` must still resolve.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every named export from `mocks/web/src/types/api.ts` is
      reachable from `@/types/api` in `app/web/`.
- [ ] Each per-domain file is under ~250 lines; `index.ts` barrel
      re-exports everything.
- [ ] `tsc --noEmit` passes with `strict: true` +
      `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`.
- [ ] All 74 `from "@/types/api"` imports in the component / page
      tasks resolve without edits to the consumers.
- [ ] No behaviour drift — a structural-type diff vs mocks is empty.

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npx tsc --noEmit
# Spot-check import resolution for each domain:
grep -rE "from ['\"]@/types/api['\"]" app/web/src | head
```

title: feat(ops): /healthz, /readyz, /version endpoints + worker heartbeat in readiness
description:
## Problem / goal
Reverse proxies, container orchestrators, and the `crewday admin
status` CLI all need a stable contract for "is this app alive and
ready?". `/healthz` is liveness (process up), `/readyz` is
readiness (DB reachable, migrations current, worker heartbeat
fresh, root key loaded), `/version` exposes the build digest.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe A"
  (Caddy reverse-proxy posture; `app:8000` healthcheck assumption),
  §"Recipe B" (DB + MinIO healthchecks point at the
  app-side endpoints implicitly).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Rate limiting"
  ("Non-auth health endpoints: unlimited but with expensive DB
  calls deferred").

## Implementation notes
- New `app/api/health.py`:
  - `GET /healthz` → 200 with `{"status": "ok"}` if process is
    serving requests; bypasses every middleware that could 5xx
    (auth, tenancy).
  - `GET /readyz` → 200 only when:
    - DB engine reachable (`SELECT 1`, < 100 ms timeout)
    - Migrations current (`alembic current` matches head)
    - Worker heartbeat written within the last 60 s
      (a row in a small `worker_heartbeat` table the worker
      updates every 30 s)
    - Root key loaded
    - Otherwise 503 with a JSON body listing failing checks.
  - `GET /version` → `{"version": "<semver>", "git_sha": "<sha>",
    "build_at": "<utc>", "image_digest": "<sha256>"}`. Built by
    the release pipeline and baked into the image.
- All three are exempt from rate-limit, no DB calls on `/healthz`
  (so a degraded DB does not 5xx liveness), no auth, no tenancy
  context.
- Capability `runtime.background_worker` keys whether the readiness
  check waits for the heartbeat (single-container with in-process
  worker → yes; orchestrated worker container → also yes; demo →
  yes).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `/healthz` returns 200 even when the DB is down (asserted via
      a fault-injected DB)
- [ ] `/readyz` returns 503 when migrations lag head (asserted by
      stamping an older `alembic_version`)
- [ ] `/readyz` returns 503 when the worker heartbeat is older than
      60 s (asserted by stopping the worker)
- [ ] `/version` returns the baked build metadata
- [ ] All three endpoints respond < 50 ms p95 under steady load
- [ ] Cookies / auth not required; CSP not blocking JSON response

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/test_health.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_readyz.py -xvs
```

title: test(integration): testcontainers PG harness + dual-backend CI matrix
description:
## Problem / goal
Every test that touches the DB must run on both SQLite (default
backend) and Postgres (the larger-deployment + RLS backend), so
SQL portability and RLS-defence-in-depth bugs are caught in CI,
not on a customer's instance.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"Integration"
  (real SQLite + PG via `testcontainers`, matrix runs both),
  §"Cross-tenant regression test" (runs on both backends).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md`
  §"Row-level security (RLS)" (PG only; SQLite runs the
  app-filter alone).

## Implementation notes
- Add `testcontainers[postgres]` to dev deps.
- Extend `tests/integration/conftest.py` to honour
  `CREWDAY_TEST_DB={sqlite|postgres}` (default `sqlite`):
  - `sqlite`: a per-test temp file (already in p21.pytest-scaffold)
  - `postgres`: a session-scoped `PostgresContainer`
    (`postgres:15-alpine`) with `RLS` policies enabled; per-test
    schema reset via `TRUNCATE … RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE`.
- New CI job `pytest-integration-pg` runs the suite under
  `CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres`. The existing `pytest-integration`
  job (SQLite) is unchanged. Both block PR merge.
- Add a marker `@pytest.mark.pg_only` for tests that exercise RLS
  or PG-specific syntax; SQLite job skips them, PG job runs them.
- Migration runner gets a `--for-tests` flag that creates the
  schema on a fresh DB without reading `alembic_version` history.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres uv run pytest tests/integration`
      passes against a freshly-spun container
- [ ] CI runs both backends; failure on either blocks merge
- [ ] `@pytest.mark.pg_only` selectively runs only on PG
- [ ] Per-test reset is < 50 ms after the first test in a session
- [ ] Migrations apply identically on SQLite and PG (asserted by a
      schema-fingerprint test)

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration -xvs
CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres uv run pytest tests/integration -xvs
```

title: test(scaffold): unit + integration test dirs, shared fixtures, factory-boy factories per context
description:
## Problem / goal
Phase 0 ships skeleton tests but no organised harness. This task
lays the §17 test pyramid scaffolding: per-context unit dirs that
import only their context's public surface, an integration dir
with real-DB fixtures, and a `factory-boy` factory module per
bounded context so tests build coherent rows in one line.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"Unit" (one test
  package per bounded context, fakes for `Clock`/`Storage`/
  `Mailer`/`LLMClient`, parametrised RRULE edge cases,
  `hypothesis` for money math, exhaustive truth tables for
  capability resolution / assignment / approval detection),
  §"Integration" (real SQLite + PG via testcontainers, per-test DB,
  migrations once per worker, snapshot+truncate between tests,
  real filesystem Storage in `tmp_path`, real Jinja renders).
- **Spec**: §"Test data" (`crewday admin demo` deterministic seed
  for e2e).

## Implementation notes
- New layout under `tests/`:
  - `tests/unit/<context>/` — one dir per `app/domain/<context>/`,
    each with its own `conftest.py` providing the in-memory fakes.
  - `tests/integration/` — shared `conftest.py` with the real-DB
    fixture and per-test schema reset.
  - `tests/factories/<context>.py` — `factory-boy` factories for
    every entity, building a coherent graph (workspace + user +
    role grant + property + …).
  - `tests/api/`, `tests/property/`, `tests/llm/` — placeholders
    with one pilot test each.
- Add `factory-boy` and `hypothesis` to dev deps.
- `pytest.ini` sets the `testpaths` and the import-mode to
  `importlib` so per-context dirs don't collide.
- Fakes (`FakeClock`, `FakeStorage`, `FakeMailer`, `FakeLLMClient`)
  live in `tests/_fakes/` and are imported by every context's
  conftest.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/unit/<any_context>/` runs in isolation
      without importing siblings
- [ ] At least one factory + one example test per existing context
- [ ] `FakeClock`, `FakeStorage`, `FakeMailer`, `FakeLLMClient`
      satisfy their port interfaces (mypy passes)
- [ ] Integration fixture spins a per-test DB (SQLite default) in

title: sec(auth): brute-force throttles, disposable-domain blocklist, suspicious-activity alerts
description:
## Problem / goal
Open self-serve signup, magic-link recovery, and passkey login are
the highest-blast-radius public surfaces. This task lands the
shared throttle + alert path: per-IP and per-email rate limits on
magic-link send and passkey login attempts, the disposable-domain
blocklist (already partly in §15), and `/admin/signups`
suspicious-activity surfacing.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Rate limiting and
  abuse controls" (10/min/IP login begin, 5/min/IP magic-link send,
  3 fails → 10 min IP lockout), §"Self-serve abuse mitigations"
  (the per-IP/per-email/global signup caps, disposable-domain list
  with weekly refresh + 30-day staleness CI gate, magic-link 15-min
  TTL one-use, abuse signals to audit + `/admin/signups`,
  `signup_gc` worker), §"Per-IP aggregate LLM spend cap" (3×
  per-WS cap default, `402 payment_required`, verify-ownership
  flow).
- **Phase 2**: `p2.signup.abuse`, `p2.magic-link`, `p2.passkey.login`
  already cover the per-feature pieces; this task unifies them
  through a shared throttle path so adding a new endpoint is one
  decorator, not three.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/abuse/throttle.py`: a `@throttle(scope, key_fn,
  budget_per_window, window_s)` decorator backed by a sliding-window
  counter (sqlite table or Postgres `bigserial` table — pick
  whichever the storage layer prefers; per-row TTL via the existing
  GC worker).
- Wire `@throttle` into magic-link send, passkey
  begin/finish, signup start, recovery start. Each instantiation
  cites the §15 budget value.
- Disposable-domain list under `app/abuse/data/disposable_domains.txt`
  with the leading-comment freshness pin (`# generated YYYY-MM-DD`);
  a CI step `refresh-disposable-domains.yml` regenerates weekly.
  CI build asserts the in-repo file is < 30 days old.
- Suspicious-activity rollups (burst-rate trips, same IP across
  distinct emails, repeat provisioning) write to
  `audit.signup.suspicious` and surface on `/admin/signups`.
- Per-IP LLM spend cap landed here too: rolling 30-day sum across
  every workspace whose `signup_ip` matches; default 3×; `402`
  + verify-ownership banner.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Magic-link send: 6th attempt in a minute returns 429
- [ ] Passkey login: 11th begin attempt in a minute returns 429
- [ ] 3 failed magic-link consumptions trigger a 10-minute IP
      lockout
- [ ] Signup with a disposable domain returns
      `400 disposable_email`
- [ ] CI fails when the disposable-domain file is > 30 days old
- [ ] Per-IP LLM spend cap returns `402 payment_required` with the
      spec banner copy when exceeded
- [ ] Verify-ownership flow promotes the workspace and removes it
      from the per-IP cap pool
- [ ] `/admin/signups` shows burst-rate + duplicate-email signals

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/abuse/test_throttle.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_abuse_guards.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_signups_dashboard.py -xvs
```
close_reason: Shared @throttle + disposable-domain freshness + passkey-login 10/min/IP landed. Deferred items tracked as cd-ovt4 (admin dashboard), cd-1i7s (per-IP LLM cap), cd-7bws (weekly refresh workflow), cd-5i2e (package-data wheel entry).

title: test(tenant): cross-workspace regression matrix on SQLite + PG (404 byte-identical, ±5 ms timing)
description:
## Problem / goal
The single most expensive bug class in a multi-tenant app is a
cross-tenant data leak. This task lands the §17 cross-tenant
regression test matrix: every workspace-scoped HTTP endpoint,
worker job, event subscription, and repository method exercised
against a wrong-workspace context, asserting `404` (never `403`,
never `200`) with a byte-identical envelope and timing bands that
overlap within ±5 ms — on **both** SQLite and Postgres.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"Cross-tenant
  regression test" (the four cases: HTTP, jobs, events,
  repositories; surface-parity gate clauses).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md`
  §"Constant-time cross-tenant responses" (the timing + envelope
  invariants this test enforces),
  §"Row-level security (RLS)" (app filter always on; PG adds RLS
  as defence-in-depth).

## Implementation notes
- New test package `tests/tenant/`:
  - `conftest.py` seeds workspaces `A` and `B` with deliberately
    colliding rows (same property names, same emails where
    uniqueness allows) so a wrong-tenant read shows up as a wrong
    row, not just an empty one.
  - `test_http_surface.py` walks every entry in `_surface.json`
    and issues an A-token request against a B-slug path,
    asserting `404` + identical envelope across at least 100
    sample endpoints. Timing bands measured via a `pytest-benchmark`
    helper; ±5 ms tolerance.
  - `test_worker_jobs.py` parametrises over the worker registry
    and asserts every emitted SQL statement on a workspace-scoped
    table carries the `WHERE workspace_id = '<ctx>'` clause via a
    `sqlalchemy.event.listen` hook.
  - `test_event_subscriptions.py` publishes an A-scoped event,
    asserts a B-scoped subscriber does not receive it.
  - `test_repository_parity.py` parametrises over every
    workspace-scoped repository method.
- Test matrix runs on SQLite and on Postgres via `testcontainers`;
  CI runs both. RLS-clearing test (PG only) asserts `SET LOCAL
  crewday.workspace_id = ''` raises on the next query.
- Surface-parity gate: a `tests/tenant/_optouts.py` registry holds
  the explicit deployment-/identity-scope opt-outs with
  `# justification:` comments.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All four cases (HTTP, jobs, events, repositories) implemented
- [ ] Sample of ≥100 HTTP endpoints exercised; envelope
      byte-identical across "missing" and "exists-elsewhere"
- [ ] Timing bands overlap within ±5 ms under a steady-load harness
- [ ] Suite runs on SQLite + PG; both pass in CI
- [ ] RLS clearing on PG raises rather than returns rows
- [ ] Adding a new repository method without extending the suite
      fails the parity gate
- [ ] Opt-outs in `_optouts.py` carry `# justification:` comments

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/tenant/ -xvs
CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres uv run pytest tests/tenant/ -xvs
```

title: sec(privacy): PII redactor for outbound LLM payloads, logs, exports
description:
## Problem / goal
PII (legal names, emails, phones, IBAN/PAN, payout account
numbers, door codes, wifi passwords) must never reach an upstream
LLM provider without explicit consent, must never appear in logs,
and must be scrubbed from user-facing exports the same way. A
single redactor module is the seam.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Logging and
  redaction" (regex-match-redact email/phone/IBAN/PAN; redact
  `password|token|secret|cookie|account_number|...` keys at any
  depth), §"Privacy and data rights" (export + erasure paths),
  §"Audit log" (`before_json`/`after_json` pass through redaction).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` consent gating —
  redactor runs on every outbound LLM call; consent flag set per
  workspace lets through specific fields the user opted in to.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/util/redact.py`:
  `redact(payload: Any, *, scope: Literal["log","llm","export"],
  consents: ConsentSet) -> Any`. Walks dict/list, applies key-name
  rules and free-text regexes (email, phone E.164, IBAN, PAN with
  Luhn). Each redacted value becomes `<redacted:reason>` so logs
  remain debuggable.
- Wire into:
  - `structlog` root processor (scope=log)
  - LLM client (scope=llm) — runs after the §11 prompt builder,
    before serialisation; consents read from
    `agent_preferences.upstream_pii_consent` (workspace-scoped)
  - export serialiser (scope=export) — `POST /me/export` and
    CSV exports
  - `audit_log` writer (scope=log) on `before_json`/`after_json`
- Property-based tests with `hypothesis` over realistic payloads
  to catch any unredacted leak across module boundaries.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Email, phone, IBAN, PAN regexes redact in free-text payloads
- [ ] `password`, `token`, `secret`, `cookie`, `account_number`,
      `pan`, `iban` keys redact at any nesting depth
- [ ] LLM payload with consent OFF redacts; with consent set for
      `legal_name` lets that one field through
- [ ] Audit log `before_json`/`after_json` pass through redactor
      before persistence (asserted on a sample row)
- [ ] Export bundle for a user contains no other user's PII
- [ ] Hypothesis fuzzer cannot find a leak after 1000 trials

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/util/test_redact.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/llm/test_outbound_redaction.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/property/test_redact_fuzz.py -xvs
```

title: sec(http): strict CSP + HSTS + nonce-based scripts + headers middleware
description:
## Problem / goal
Every HTML response must carry the §15 strict CSP and the full
security-header set: HSTS (post-opt-in), `X-Frame-Options`,
`Referrer-Policy`, `Permissions-Policy`, `X-Content-Type-Options`,
`COOP`, `CORP`. The CSP is nonce-based — every inline `<script>`
or `<style>` carries a per-request nonce; CI fails a build that
emits an unhashed inline.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"HTTP security
  headers" (the canonical list and exact values),
  §"Shared-origin XSS containment" (nonce-based CSP, no inline
  without nonce, `bleach` sanitiser on UGC, `__Host-` cookies),
  §"TLS" (HSTS opt-in flow).
- **Demo carve-out**: §24 `frame-ancestors` allowlist for
  `demo.crew.day` only — the middleware reads
  `caps.has("runtime.demo_mode")` and the
  `CREWDAY_DEMO_FRAME_ANCESTORS` env to widen `frame-ancestors`
  on that surface only.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/api/middleware/security_headers.py`: emits the full
  header set on every response, generates a per-request 128-bit
  nonce, exposes it on `request.state.csp_nonce` for the renderer.
- React app's HTML shell template reads the nonce from a meta tag
  injected by the server; any inline `<script>` (the bootstrap)
  carries `nonce={nonce}`.
- HSTS: emitted only when the `settings.hsts_enabled` workspace
  toggle is on (per spec — opt-in once Caddy/TLS is verified).
- `Permissions-Policy: camera=(self)` only on worker pages —
  middleware reads route group; default elsewhere is empty
  (`camera=()`). `geolocation=()` everywhere (v0 geofence is gone).
- Linter check: scans `mocks/web/dist` post-build for any
  `<script>` lacking `nonce=` or `src=`; fails the build.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every HTML response carries the §15 header set with the exact
      values; asserted in a test that hits 5 representative routes
- [ ] CSP nonce is unique per request and matches the inline
      bootstrap's `nonce` attribute
- [ ] Build-time linter rejects a deliberately-introduced unhashed
      inline `<script>`
- [ ] `frame-ancestors 'none'` on prod surface; widened on demo
      only when env is set
- [ ] HSTS absent until opt-in; present with the spec value once on
- [ ] No CSP report-uri violations on the styleguide route

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_security_headers.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/test_csp_nonce.py -xvs
```
close_reason: Strict CSP + HSTS + nonce-based scripts + headers middleware landed. Self-review in cd-eqog applied the §24 X-Frame-Options demo carve-out fix.

title: sec(session): cookie flags, idle/absolute timeout, fingerprint, invalidate-on-credential-change
description:
## Problem / goal
Session cookies and lifetime policy are load-bearing for every
authenticated surface. Phase 2 ships a working session; this task
hardens it: `__Host-` prefix with the right flag set, idle +
absolute timeouts, a device fingerprint sanity check, and
mandatory invalidation of every live session when the underlying
credential changes (passkey added/revoked, password — none today,
recovery code consumed).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Cookies"
  (`__Host-crewday_session`, `Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax;
  Path=/`, opaque 192-bit value), §"Shared-origin XSS containment"
  (`__Host-` cookies, origin-locked).
- **Spec**: §"Passkey specifics" (sign-count rollback auto-revoke
  → must invalidate sessions for the rolled credential).
- **Phase 2**: `p2.session` already exists; this task extends it.

## Implementation notes
- Cookie writer enforces every flag at one chokepoint
  (`app/api/auth/session_cookie.py`); a unit test reads the
  resulting `Set-Cookie` header and asserts each attribute.
- Idle timeout: 30 days default (configurable per workspace via a
  setting in §15 reach). Absolute timeout: 90 days. Both stored
  on the `session` row; middleware bumps `last_seen_at` on each
  request and refuses sessions exceeding either.
- Device fingerprint: hash of `(User-Agent, Accept-Language)` at
  mint time, stored on the row, mismatch → log
  `audit.session.fingerprint_mismatch` and require re-auth.
- Wire `session.invalidate_for_user(user_id)` into:
  - passkey register / revoke (§03)
  - magic-link recovery consumption
  - sign-count rollback auto-revoke (§15) — invalidates only the
    sessions tied to the revoked credential id
- Audit `session.invalidated` with cause.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `Set-Cookie` carries `__Host-`, `Secure`, `HttpOnly`,
      `SameSite=Lax`, `Path=/`, no `Domain`, no `Max-Age` past 90 days
- [ ] Idle past 30 days → 401 + `session_idle_timeout`; absolute
      past 90 days → 401 + `session_absolute_timeout`
- [ ] Fingerprint mismatch logs audit and forces re-auth
- [ ] Adding or revoking a passkey kills every other live session
      for the same user
- [ ] Sign-count rollback (§15 auto-revoke) kills sessions tied to
      the revoked credential id
- [ ] Tenant isolation regression still passes after the changes

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_session_cookie.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_session_lifetime.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_session_invalidation.py -xvs
```
close_reason: All cd-geqp acceptance criteria met: __Host- cookie, idle+absolute timeouts, fingerprint (now wired through middleware), invalidate-on-credential-change. cd-u2rj self-review closed the last gap.

title: feat(web/manager): approvals queue — task amendments + HITL agent actions
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager decides every approval request the workspace generates: worker self-amendments above threshold, agent actions paused for human-in-the-loop, expense flags, etc. The queue is the manager's compliance trail.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ApprovalsPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/approvals` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/approvals, POST /api/v1/approvals/{id}/approve|reject.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ApprovalsPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The mock surfaces `risk` tone via APPROVAL_RISK_TONE — keep that mapping; do not recolour by category.
- HITL agent approvals carry an `agent_run_id` link back to the LLM trace (§11) — render it as the mock does.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/approvals`.

title: feat(web/manager): task templates with checklist editor + photo / timer steps
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager builds the task templates that materialised tasks instantiate from — ordered checklist steps, required photos, timed steps, priority defaults.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/templates` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, lucide-react Camera/Timer icons.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/task_templates, POST/PATCH /api/v1/task_templates.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The PRIORITY_TONE map in the mock ties priorities to tone tokens; reuse, do not re-derive.
- Drag-to-reorder steps must keep server-side ordering stable — debounce the PATCH.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/templates`.

title: feat(api/static): mount app/web/dist as SPA catch-all; dev proxies Vite HMR
description:
## Problem / goal
Switch the FastAPI app's static-asset mount from `mocks/web/dist/` (the
Phase 0 placeholder) to `app/web/dist/`, the production build. In
the dev profile, proxy `/` to the Vite dev server so HMR keeps
working. This is the cut-over moment from mocks to real frontend.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Serving the SPA",
  `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"FastAPI static mount".
- **Refs**: Phase 0 `app/main.py` currently mounts
  `mocks/web/dist/` — this task replaces that wiring.

## Implementation notes
- Edit `app/main.py`:
  - In **prod profile**: mount `app/web/dist/` as a `StaticFiles`
    instance with a SPA catch-all route that returns `index.html`
    for any unmatched non-API GET (so client-side routing works).
  - In **dev profile** (`CREWDAY_PROFILE=dev`): instead of the
    static mount, proxy `/` (and `/@vite/`, `/src/`, `/node_modules/`)
    to the Vite dev server on `127.0.0.1:5173` (or the port set in
    `vite.config.ts`); preserve websockets for HMR.
  - Remove the `mocks/web/dist` import / mount.
- Update `mocks/docker-compose.yml` so the dev container mounts the
  app/web build output (or runs Vite dev) — keep loopback-bound,
  never the public IP.
- API routes (`/api/*`, `/w/<slug>/api/*`, `/events`, `/auth/*`)
  must take precedence over the SPA catch-all.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Prod profile serves `index.html` for unknown GETs (SPA)
- [ ] API routes still answer correctly (not shadowed by SPA fallback)
- [ ] Dev profile proxies HMR; editing a `.tsx` reloads in browser
- [ ] `mocks/web/dist` no longer referenced by `app/main.py`
- [ ] Bind-guard still passes (no public-interface bind)
- [ ] Smoke test: `curl http://127.0.0.1:8100/anything` returns SPA HTML

## Test plan
```bash
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build
curl -sI http://127.0.0.1:8100/foo/bar | head
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8100/ | grep -i 'crew'
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_static_mount.py -xvs
```
close_reason: SPA seam landed: prod mounts app/web/dist with catch-all, dev profile proxies Vite HTTP (WS HMR follow-up tracked as cd-354g)

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: Heads-up: cd-sgpl (chore(dev): point dev.crew.day to app/web Vite; serve mocks/web at /mocks/) covers the dev-side `mocks/docker-compose.yml` re-wiring of the `web-dev` Vite container (Traefik → app/web, plus a second Vite service for mocks at /mocks/). That overlaps with this task's "Update mocks/docker-compose.yml so the dev container mounts the app/web build output (or runs Vite dev)" bullet — keep this task focused on the **FastAPI static mount** in `app/main.py` (prod profile: serve app/web/dist with SPA catch-all) so the two don't double-edit the compose file.

  - `mocks/web/src/components/BottomTabs.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/components/SideNav.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/components/WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/components/AgentSidebar.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/components/AgentApprovalModePanel.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/components/AgentPreferencesPanel.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/components/AppearancePanel.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/components/TaskListCard.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/components/NewTaskModal.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/components/PersonalTokensPanel.tsx`

## Implementation notes

  relative path. **Do not refactor on the way in.**

  - Replace mock-data imports with hooks from `lib/api.ts` +
    `queryKeys.ts` (Task `p16.api-client`).
  - Swap mock `useRole`/`useWorkspace` for the real ones (Task
    `p16.contexts`).
  - Fix any TS strict errors that surface — keep behaviour identical.
- No JSX structure changes, no class-name changes, no inline styles
  added.

  the import block and a handful of identifier swaps.

## Test plan
```bash
ls app/web/src/components/
diff -ru mocks/web/src/components app/web/src/components | head -300
cd app/web && npm run build
```

description:

Wire passkey-based authentication into the SPA: silent token refresh
where possible, redirect to `/login` on any 401 (except public
routes), and surface a workspace-selector when the user has no active
slug.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Auth", §"Workspace
  selector"; `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Passkey",
  §"Sessions".
- **Reference**: any auth-aware bits in `mocks/web/src/pages/login*`
  (verbatim where present) and `WorkspaceSwitcher`.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/web/src/auth/` module:
  - `authStore.ts` — current user, token, expiry; persists token to
    `httpOnly`-equivalent storage where supported, falls back to
    `localStorage` with sensible CSRF posture.
  - `useAuth()` hook returns user + login/logout helpers.
  - `RequireAuth.tsx` route guard — wraps protected route
    branches; on 401 from `api.ts`, redirects to `/login?next=...`.
  - `LoginPage.tsx` — passkey ceremony via WebAuthn API; on success
    populates `authStore` and replays `next`.
  - `WorkspaceGate.tsx` — when authenticated but no slug active,
    show `WorkspaceSwitcher` modal with the user's workspace list
    (from `/me/workspaces`); blocks render until a slug is picked.
- `api.ts` calls a single `onUnauthorized()` hook so the redirect
  is centralised.
- Public routes (login, public booking pages, share links) are
  whitelisted in `RequireAuth`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Hitting any protected route while logged-out → `/login?next=`
- [ ] Successful passkey login lands on `next` (or `/`)
- [ ] 401 mid-session triggers redirect + clears auth store
- [ ] Workspace selector appears when user has no active slug
- [ ] Logout clears storage + closes SSE
- [ ] Unit + Playwright tests cover the full ceremony

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npm run test -- src/auth
# Playwright: register passkey, log out, log back in, switch workspace
```

title: feat(web/sse): EventSource('/w/<slug>/events') → queryClient.invalidateQueries by event kind
description:
## Problem / goal
A single `EventSource('/w/<slug>/events')` per tab feeds
`queryClient.invalidateQueries()` so cross-client coherence works
without polling. Each event kind maps deterministically to one or
more query keys.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"SSE-driven
  invalidation", §"Event kinds".
- **API**: `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"SSE stream".
- **Reference**: `mocks/web/src/lib/sse.ts` + Phase 12 SSE backend.

## Implementation notes
- Extend `app/web/src/lib/sse.ts` (copied from mocks) with the
  invalidation map:
  ```ts
  const INVALIDATIONS: Record<EventKind, (e: Event, q: QueryClient) => void> = {
    'task.updated':       (e, q) => q.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: keys.tasks.all }),
    'task.created':       ...,
    'shift.changed':      ...,
    'expense.decided':    ...,
    'inventory.changed':  ...,
    'workspace.changed':  (e, q) => q.invalidateQueries(),
    // ... full table per spec §"Event kinds"
  }
  ```
- The `SseContext` provider runs `INVALIDATIONS[event.kind]?(...)`
  on every message; unknown kinds log + ignore (do NOT crash).
- Reconnect with exponential backoff (1s → 30s cap), preserve
  `Last-Event-Id` so we replay missed events on resume.
- `queryClient.invalidateQueries` is fired with `refetchType:
  'active'` to keep idle queries cheap.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every event kind in spec §"Event kinds" has an invalidation
- [ ] Unknown kinds logged but never throw
- [ ] Backoff caps at 30s; `Last-Event-Id` honoured on resume
- [ ] Workspace switch closes + reopens the stream
- [ ] Unit tests cover invalidation map + reconnect logic

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npm run test -- src/lib/sse
# Manual: open two tabs, mutate in one, watch the other update
```
close_reason: Shipped: web SSE client at app/web/src/lib/sse.ts with full INVALIDATIONS map per spec §14 §'Event kinds', jittered exponential backoff capped at 30 s, workspace-switch teardown, unknown-kind warn-don't-throw, provider wired to authenticated routes only, Last-Event-ID preserved via native EventSource behaviour. 43 new lib tests + 11 updated provider tests green.

title: feat(web/manager): employees list with today's bookings + invite CTA
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager sees the full roster, who is booked today and where, and invites a new employee via magic-link onboarding.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeesPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/users` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Avatar, Chip, Loading.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/employees, GET /api/v1/properties, GET /api/v1/bookings, POST /api/v1/employees (invite).

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeesPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The invite CTA opens the shared invite modal (Phase 16 shared-components); do not inline a new form.
- The `bookings` query is per-day (today only) — pass the same query params the mock implies.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/users`.

title: feat(web/lib): api.ts + queryClient.ts + queryKeys.ts wired to /w/<slug>/api/v1/*
description:
## Problem / goal
The web app's data layer: a typed `api.ts` fetch wrapper, a TanStack
Query v5 client with sensible defaults, and a single `queryKeys.ts`
factory so SSE invalidation has one source of truth.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Data layer",
  §"TanStack Query", §"Optimistic mutations",
  §"SSE-driven invalidation".
- **Reference (COPY THEN ADAPT)**:
  - `mocks/web/src/lib/api.ts`
  - `mocks/web/src/lib/queryClient.ts`
  - `mocks/web/src/lib/queryKeys.ts`
  - `mocks/web/src/lib/{dates,money,preferences,strings,tones,
        routeParents,agentTyping,useBannerHeightVar,
        useDecideMutation}.ts` (also copy verbatim — used by
        components).
  - **Not in scope here**: `mocks/web/src/lib/sse.ts` — it is
    copied and extended by `cd-y4g5` (SSE wiring). Don't also
    copy it from this task; defer to `cd-y4g5` so the invalidation
    map and reconnect logic land together.

## Implementation notes
- Copy each `mocks/web/src/lib/*.ts` into `app/web/src/lib/`
  **except `sse.ts`** (handled by `cd-y4g5`).
- **Adaptations**:
  - `api.ts` — base URL `/w/<slug>/api/v1`; slug resolved from
    `WorkspaceContext`; attach `Authorization: Bearer <token>` from
    the auth store; throw a typed `ApiError` on non-2xx mirroring
    the CLI's shape.
  - `queryClient.ts` — TanStack Query v5 with `staleTime: 30s`,
    `retry: 2 (exp backoff)`, `refetchOnWindowFocus: false` (SSE
    invalidates instead).
  - `queryKeys.ts` — keyed factory `tasks.list(filters)`,
    `tasks.detail(id)`, `properties.list()`, ... — every key
    starts with the workspace slug so workspace switching evicts
    cleanly.
  - Mutations support optimistic updates via the `onMutate` /
    `onError rollback` pattern; helper `useDecideMutation` (copied
    verbatim) wraps approval-style decisions.
- No mock-data imports remain.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All `lib/*.ts` files present in `app/web/src/lib/`
- [ ] `api.ts` builds the URL from `useWorkspace().slug`
- [ ] `queryKeys` always namespace by slug
- [ ] Optimistic mutation pattern works end-to-end against dev API
- [ ] `tsc --noEmit` passes; ESLint clean
- [ ] Unit tests cover key factory + error mapping

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npm run test -- src/lib
cd app/web && npm run build
```

title: feat(web/context): copy + wire SseContext, RoleContext, WorkspaceContext, ThemeContext, NavHistoryContext, ShellNavContext
description:
## Problem / goal
The contexts power role gating, workspace selection, theme, SSE
fan-out, navigation history (for back/forward), and the shell nav
bar. Copy them verbatim from `mocks/web/src/context/` and rewire to
the real API + auth surfaces.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Contexts",
  §"SSE-driven invalidation", §"Workspace switching".
- **Reference (COPY THEN ADAPT)**:
  - `mocks/web/src/context/SseContext.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/context/RoleContext.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/context/WorkspaceContext.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/context/ThemeContext.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/context/NavHistoryContext.tsx`
  - `mocks/web/src/context/ShellNavContext.tsx`

## Implementation notes
- Copy each file into `app/web/src/context/` unchanged where
  possible.
- **Adaptations** (kept tight; each called out in the PR):
  - `SseContext` — open `EventSource('/w/<slug>/events')` (real
    workspace slug from `WorkspaceContext`); reconnect with
    exponential backoff; expose typed event subscription API as in
    mocks. Real query-key invalidation lands in Task
    `p16.sse-wiring`.
  - `WorkspaceContext` — load `/me/workspaces` for the slug list;
    persist active slug in `localStorage`; fire `onChange` so SSE +
    api client reconfigure.
  - `RoleContext` — derive role from `/me` for the active workspace
    (no more mock role toggle); keep the same hook signature.
  - `ThemeContext`, `NavHistoryContext`, `ShellNavContext` —
    verbatim; they don't touch the API.
- Public hook signatures (`useSse`, `useRole`, `useWorkspace`,
  `useTheme`, `useNavHistory`, `useShellNav`) must remain identical
  so component imports don't churn.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each context file in `app/web/src/context/` exports the same
      hook signatures as the mock
- [ ] `SseContext` reconnects with backoff; closes on logout
- [ ] `WorkspaceContext` persists + restores the active slug
- [ ] No mock-data imports left behind
- [ ] Unit tests cover hook behaviour with mocked fetch / EventSource

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npm run test -- src/context
cd app/web && npm run build
```

title: feat(web/manager): property detail — units, areas, closures, staff, sharing
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager edits one property's facts (units, areas, default checklist, settings) and shares or revokes access to peer workspaces (cross-workspace property sharing per §02).

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/property/<pid>` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, EntitySettingsPanel.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/properties/{pid}, GET /api/v1/employees, GET /api/v1/properties/{pid}/settings, GET /api/v1/settings/catalog, POST /api/v1/property_workspaces/share, POST /api/v1/property_workspaces/revoke.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**,
  but the mock is ~550 lines — **split it** during promotion into a
  tree under `app/web/src/pages/manager/property/`, keeping
  `PropertyDetailPage.tsx` itself under ~250 lines. Suggested split:
  - `SettingsOverridePanel.tsx` — the catalog-driven override panel
    sub-component already defined in the mock.
  - `SharingPanel.tsx` — the share / revoke cross-workspace panel.
  - `AreasPanel.tsx`, `UnitsPanel.tsx`, `StaffPanel.tsx`,
    `ClosuresPanel.tsx` — one per top-level section visible on the
    page.
  - `lib/propertyFormatters.ts` — `fmtDayMon`, `fmtDayMonTime`,
    `formatValue`.
  - `PropertyDetailPage.tsx` — orchestrator: fetches detail, wires
    mutations + SSE, composes the panels.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons —
  rendered DOM and class names must be byte-equivalent to the mock.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The share/revoke mutations operate on the cross-workspace `property_workspace` join — verify the Phase 14 router exposes them with the same body shape the mock posts.
- The settings panel relies on the catalog-driven `EntitySettingsPanel` shared component; do not duplicate its logic locally.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.
- [ ] No single file in the new tree exceeds ~250 lines.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/property/<pid>`.

title: feat(web/manager): properties list with membership chip + stay glance
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager scans every property they touch — owned, managed, observed — sees today's stay status per row, and drills into one to make changes.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
