- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/properties` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/properties, GET /api/v1/stays, GET /api/v1/me, GET /api/v1/workspaces, GET /api/v1/organizations, GET /api/v1/property_workspaces.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertiesPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The membership chip vocabulary (`Owner`/`Managed`/`Observer`) is fixed in the mock's `MEMBERSHIP_LABEL` map; keep it — do not switch to enum values.
- The page joins six queries; respect React Query's automatic parallelism and don't introduce dependent waterfalls.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both

      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan

  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.

title: feat(api/tasks): templates, schedules, occurrences, completion, evidence, comments, approvals
description:
## Problem / goal
The tasks router — the product's centre of gravity. Covers
templates, schedules (RRULE), generated occurrences, occurrence
completion (with evidence), comments, and the approval-required
flow when a manager review is needed.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Tasks / templates /
  schedules" (full subsection).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulerPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/worker/MyDayPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/worker/TaskDetailPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/tasks.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`tasks\`.
- Sub-resources nested only one level (e.g.
  \`POST /occurrences/{id}/complete\`,
  \`POST /occurrences/{id}/evidence\`,
  \`POST /occurrences/{id}/comments\`,
  \`POST /occurrences/{id}/approve\`).
- Pydantic Read/Create/Update/Patch per resource. Occurrences
  expose computed fields (\`overdue\`, \`time_window_local\`) via
  a separate \`OccurrenceRead\` model populated by the service.
- Completion endpoint is idempotent via \`Idempotency-Key\` —
  re-submitting the same payload returns the same occurrence.
- Approval endpoint returns 409 \`approval_required\` with
  \`approval_request_id\` if the caller lacks the cap.
- Listings support \`?state=\`, \`?assignee_user_id=\`,
  \`?property_id=\`, \`?scheduled_for_utc_gte=\` per spec.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Routes for templates, schedules, occurrences, evidence,
      comments, approvals
- [ ] Completion is idempotent; re-submission returns same
      occurrence
- [ ] Schedule POST validates RRULE via the Phase 0 util port
- [ ] Filtering + cursor pagination on every list endpoint
- [ ] OpenAPI tag \`tasks\` matches spec; schemathesis passes
- [ ] Cross-tenant occurrence access returns 404 (not 403, to
      avoid existence leak)

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_tasks.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_tasks_routes.py -xvs
uv run schemathesis run --checks all http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/openapi.json --tag tasks
\`\`\`

title: feat(web): scaffold app/web/ — Vite + React 19 + TS strict (mirror mocks/web)
description:
## Problem / goal
Stand up the production web SPA package `app/web/` so every later web
task has the same toolchain, lint config, and folder layout as
`mocks/web/`. The final app MUST look exactly like the mocks; the
quickest way to guarantee that is to mirror the toolchain first.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Stack", §"Dev/build",
  §"Layout".
- **Architecture**: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"web.*",
  §"Repo layout" (places the SPA at `app/web/`).
- **Reference (copy verbatim)**:
  - `mocks/web/package.json`
  - `mocks/web/vite.config.ts`
  - `mocks/web/vitest.config.ts`
  - `mocks/web/tsconfig.json`
  - `mocks/web/tsconfig.app.json`
  - `mocks/web/tsconfig.node.json`
  - `mocks/web/index.html`
  - `mocks/web/eslint.config.js` (if present) or align rules
  - `mocks/web/src/test/setup.ts` (vitest jsdom polyfill for
    `EventSource` — referenced from `vitest.config.ts`
    `setupFiles`)

## Implementation notes
- Create `app/web/` with the same top-level structure as
  `mocks/web/`: `index.html`, `package.json`, `vite.config.ts`,
  `vitest.config.ts`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.app.json`, `src/`,
  `src/test/setup.ts`, `public/`.
- **Copy the config files verbatim** from `mocks/web/`; the only
  intentional diffs are:
  - `package.json#name` → `@crewday/web`.
  - `vite.config.ts` `base` / `build.outDir` left as-is so FastAPI
    can serve `app/web/dist/` (Task `p16.fastapi-static`).
  - Path alias `@` → `src/` matches mocks.
- `package.json` deps mirror `mocks/web/package.json` exactly
  (React 19, TanStack Query v5, vitest, etc.). No Tailwind, no
  Alpine, no HTMX, no Vue.
- `npm install` (or `pnpm` matching mocks) cleanly; `npm run build`
  produces `app/web/dist/`.
- Add an empty `app/web/src/main.tsx` placeholder so the build
  completes; later tasks fill it in.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `app/web/package.json` deps == `mocks/web/package.json` deps
- [ ] `app/web/vite.config.ts` is byte-equal to mocks (modulo
      `base`/`outDir` if needed)
- [ ] `tsconfig.json` is `strict: true` with `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`
- [ ] `npm run build` succeeds and emits `app/web/dist/`
- [ ] `eslint` + `tsc --noEmit` pass
- [ ] `npm run test` (vitest) runs a trivial placeholder spec against
      the copied `src/test/setup.ts` without crashing on missing
      `EventSource`

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npm ci
cd app/web && npm run build
cd app/web && npm run lint
cd app/web && npx tsc --noEmit
cd app/web && npm run test -- --run
```

title: feat(api/places): properties, units, areas, closures + multi-belonging
description:
## Problem / goal
Places router covering properties, their units and areas,
closures, and the multi-belonging surface (a property can be
shared across workspaces via \`property_workspace\`).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Properties / areas /
  stays" (properties, units, areas, closures, multi-belonging
  share endpoints).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertiesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\`
  (Units / Areas / Closures tabs + Sharing tab).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/places.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`places\`.
- Pydantic schemas split per resource. \`PropertyCreate\` accepts
  the canonical \`address_json\`; the back-fill of
  \`property.country\` happens in the service, not the router.
- Multi-belonging endpoints (\`POST /properties/{id}/share\`,
  \`DELETE /properties/{id}/share/{workspace_slug}\`) live here
  but are gated by an explicit \`places.share\` capability.
- Closure endpoints emit SSE \`property_closure.upserted\` so the
  scheduler view invalidates.
- All listings cursor-paginated; every mutation idempotent.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD routes for property, unit, area, closure, share
- [ ] \`address_json\` validated against canonical shape
- [ ] Cross-workspace operations denied unless caller has
      \`places.share\` cap
- [ ] Sharing endpoint creates one \`property_workspace\` row,
      audits, fires SSE
- [ ] Closure endpoint refuses overlap with confirmed-stay
      window unless \`force=true\`
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`places\`

title: feat(api/identity): users, tokens, passkeys, sessions, role-grants, permission-groups, invites
description:
## Problem / goal
Identity router covering everything the auth + people-management UIs
need: users, passkeys, sessions, API tokens (PATs), role grants,
permission groups, invites, and the click-to-accept flow. Endpoints
defined under spec §12 §"Auth" + §"Users / work roles / settings"
(permission groups + rules subsection).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Auth" (including
  \`/auth/email-change\`, \`/auth/invites/accept\`, PATs, push
  tokens), §"Users / work roles / settings" (permission groups +
  rules), §"Settings" (workspaces).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/UsersPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/RolesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/InvitesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/PermissionGroupsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/me/SecurityPage.tsx\` (passkeys, PATs,
  sessions, email change),
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/auth/AcceptInvitePage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/identity.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`identity\`.
- Pydantic schemas in \`app/api/v1/schemas/identity.py\` —
  separate \`Read\`, \`Create\`, \`Update\`, \`Patch\` per resource;
  PATs surface only the prefix on listing, full secret only at
  creation.
- All mutations honour \`Idempotency-Key\`; all listings are
  cursor-paginated.
- Email-change endpoint enforces passkey-session-only via the
  Phase 2 auth dependency (PATs/agent tokens denied at the
  router edge).
- Invite acceptance endpoint runs anonymously, validates token
  purpose=='accept', then either kicks off a passkey ceremony
  (new user) or returns the Accept card payload (existing user).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All routes mounted under \`/w/{slug}/api/v1/{resource}\`
      with correct verbs (POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE)
- [ ] Per-resource pydantic Read/Create/Update/Patch schemas
- [ ] PAT secret returned only on creation; subsequent reads
      show prefix only
- [ ] Email-change rejects PATs / agent tokens with 403
      \`forbidden\`
- [ ] Invite-accept rejects mismatched purposes with 403
- [ ] OpenAPI tag \`identity\` lists every operation; schemathesis
      passes

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_identity.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_identity_routes.py -xvs
uv run schemathesis run --checks all http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/openapi.json --tag identity
\`\`\`

title: feat(api): RFC 7807 problem+json envelope and domain→HTTP exception translation
description:
## Problem / goal
Every error response across every router uses the same
problem+json envelope (RFC 7807) with the canonical \`type\` URIs
listed in spec §12 (\`validation\`, \`not_found\`, \`conflict\`,
\`unauthorized\`, \`forbidden\`, \`rate_limited\`,
\`upstream_unavailable\`, \`idempotency_conflict\`,
\`approval_required\`). Domain exceptions raised by services are
translated to the matching HTTP status without router-by-router
boilerplate.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Errors".
- **Domain exceptions**: a base \`DomainError\` hierarchy in
  \`app/domain/errors.py\` (already conceptualised in Phase 0 ports);
  this task adds the HTTP layer.
- **Mocks consuming**: \`mocks/web/src/lib/api.ts\` parses
  \`problem+json\`; \`mocks/web/src/components/ErrorBanner.tsx\`
  renders the \`title\` + \`detail\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/errors.py\`: registers \`add_exception_handlers(app)\`
  for \`DomainError\` subclasses + \`pydantic.ValidationError\` +
  starlette \`HTTPException\`. Always emits
  \`Content-Type: application/problem+json\`.
- Maps \`NotFound -> 404\`, \`Conflict -> 409\`,
  \`Forbidden -> 403\`, \`Unauthorized -> 401\`,
  \`Validation -> 422\`, \`RateLimited -> 429\`,
  \`UpstreamUnavailable -> 502\`,
  \`ApprovalRequired -> 409\` with \`approval_request_id\` in the
  body.
- Includes \`instance\` (request path), \`type\` URI, \`status\`,
  \`title\`, \`detail\`, optional \`errors[]\` for field-level.
- Echo \`X-Correlation-Id\` on every error response.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every router uses the same envelope without local handlers
- [ ] All canonical \`type\` URIs appear in OpenAPI examples
- [ ] \`pydantic.ValidationError\` maps to 422 with \`errors[]\` array
- [ ] Unknown DomainError subclass falls back to 500 \`internal\`
- [ ] Correlation id propagates from request to error body
- [ ] Snapshot tests cover one example per canonical type

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/test_errors.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_error_envelope.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(api): idempotency-key middleware with persisted replay cache
description:
## Problem / goal
Every \`POST\` mutating endpoint accepts \`Idempotency-Key\`. The
middleware persists \`(token_id, key) -> (status, body_hash, body)\`
for 24h and replays the stored response on retry. Different body hash
with the same key returns 409 \`idempotency_conflict\`. Exempt
endpoints (e.g. \`POST /payslips/{id}/payout_manifest\`, §11) skip the
cache but still accept the header.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Idempotency".
- **Domain model**: a new \`idempotency_key\` table —
  \`(token_id, key, status, body_hash, body, created_at)\`, TTL 24h.
- **Mocks consuming**: every mutation in \`mocks/web/src/lib/api.ts\`
  generates an Idempotency-Key (ULID) — the production API must
  honour them on the first network retry.

## Implementation notes
- New Alembic migration \`xxxx_idempotency_key\` (small table, indexed
  on \`(token_id, key)\` UNIQUE, partial index on \`created_at\` for
  TTL sweep).
- \`app/api/middleware/idempotency.py\`: ASGI middleware. Runs after
  auth (so \`token_id\` is known) and before the handler. Skips
  non-POST and an explicit allow-list of exempt paths.
- TTL sweep job registered with the Phase 0 scheduler — daily, deletes
  rows where \`created_at < now() - 24h\`.
- Body hash uses sha256 of the canonical JSON serialisation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] First POST with a key persists the response; second POST with
      same key + same body returns the cached response (and the
      \`Idempotency-Replay: true\` response header)
- [ ] Same key + different body returns 409 \`idempotency_conflict\`
- [ ] Exempt path bypasses cache; header is accepted but ignored
- [ ] TTL sweep removes rows older than 24h
- [ ] Works under concurrent retries (DB-level uniqueness wins races)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests cover all four paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/middleware/test_idempotency.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_idempotency_replay.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(api): FastAPI app factory wires per-context routers under /w/<slug>/api/v1
description:
## Problem / goal
A single FastAPI app factory that mounts every per-context APIRouter
under \`/w/<slug>/api/v1/<ctx>\`, exposes the merged OpenAPI 3.1 doc
at \`/api/openapi.json\`, and centralises the dependency that hands
each handler the current \`WorkspaceContext\` resolved by the Phase 0
tenancy middleware. The factory is the single import seam between
\`app/main.py\` and the router modules; routers never touch the global
app object.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Versioning",
  §"Base URL", §"Authentication", §"OpenAPI",
  §"`operationId` convention", §"Common conventions",
  §"Request/response shape".
- **Architecture**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §api.v1.*,
  §web.*, §"Handlers are thin".
- **Mocks consuming**: \`mocks/web/src/lib/api.ts\` (single fetch
  client; expects \`/w/<slug>/api/v1/...\`), every page under
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/{manager,worker,admin}/*\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/factory.py\`: \`create_app(settings) -> FastAPI\`.
  Mounts a workspace router at \`/w/{slug}\` and an admin router at
  \`/admin\`; each prefixes \`/api/v1\` for the context routers.
- \`app/api/deps.py\`: \`def current_workspace_context() ->
  WorkspaceContext\` — reads the ContextVar set by the Phase 0
  tenancy middleware. Routers depend on this, NEVER read the request
  directly for tenancy.
- Custom OpenAPI generator (\`app.openapi = custom_openapi\`) merges
  per-context tags, sets the \`openapi: 3.1.0\` version, and emits
  the canonical \`operationId\` (\`<context>.<verb>_<resource>\`) per
  spec §"`operationId` convention".
- Mounts the openapi.json at \`/api/openapi.json\` (no slug — the
  schema is identical across workspaces).
- Routers are discovered via an explicit registry in
  \`app/api/v1/__init__.py\` (no autoload — keeps import-linter happy).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`create_app()\` returns a FastAPI instance with all 13 context
      routers mounted under \`/w/{slug}/api/v1\` and admin under
      \`/admin/api/v1\`
- [ ] \`GET /api/openapi.json\` returns valid OpenAPI 3.1 with one tag
      per context
- [ ] \`current_workspace_context\` dependency raises 404 when slug
      is unknown, 403 when caller lacks membership
- [ ] All routes carry the correct \`operationId\` (snake-case,
      \`<ctx>.<verb>_<resource>\`)
- [ ] Import-linter contract: \`app.api.v1.*\` may import \`app.services.*\`
      but not \`app.domain.*\` directly
- [ ] Smoke test boots the app + asserts every router is mounted

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/test_factory.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_openapi_shape.py -xvs
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/openapi.json | jq '.openapi'
\`\`\`

title: feat(agent/approval): HITL approval pipeline (workspace + per-user)
description:
## Problem / goal
Two layers of gating intercept agent writes: the workspace policy
(committee, owner/manager-curated) and the per-user agent approval
mode (self, `bypass | auto | strict`). On gate, the runtime
returns 202 with an approval id; deciders approve/reject from
`/approvals` (desk) or inline in chat (web sidebar). On approve,
the recorded payload is replayed under the original idempotency
key. **Decisions travel through the human session, not the agent
token.**

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Agent action
  approval", §"Approval decisions travel through the human
  session, not the agent token", §"Action confirmation
  annotation", §"Per-user agent approval mode",
  §"Workspace policy: which actions", §"Flow", §"Model",
  §"Bypass", §"Inline approval UX", §"TTL".
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`approval_request`
  (a.k.a. `agent_action`) — fields enumerated in §11 "Model".
- Mocks: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ApprovalsPage.tsx` (desk),
  `mocks/web/src/components/AgentApprovalModePanel.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/components/AgentSidebar.tsx` (inline cards).

## Implementation notes
- Middleware on every mutating route reads `x-agent-confirm` /
  `x-agent-forbidden` / `x-interactive-only` annotations + the
  workspace always-gated and configurable lists + the calling
  user's mode → decides execute / gate / forbid.
- On gate: write `agent_action` (resolved payload, idempotency
  key, `pre_approval_source`, `inline_channel` from
  `X-Agent-Channel`, `for_user_id`, `resolved_user_mode`,
  rendered `card_*` fields). Return `202 { approval_id, status:
  pending, expires_at }`.
- Notifications: SSE `agent.action.pending` to the delegating
  user's tabs (inline channels) **and** email + `approval.pending`
  webhook to managers (desk).
- Decision endpoints `POST /api/v1/approvals/{id}/{approve,deny}`
  reject delegated tokens with `403 approval_requires_session`;
  PATs accepted only when carrying `approvals:act` (off by
  default, never on agent tokens).
- On approve: replay the original handler with the recorded
  payload under the recorded idempotency key; persist
  `result_json`, flip state to `executed`, emit
  `approval.decided`. On reject: emit
  `approval.decided{decision=rejected}`, no replay. On TTL
  expiry: worker flips to `expired`,
  `decision_note_md='auto-expired'`.
- Audit rows: `audit.approval.granted`, `audit.approval.denied`,
  `audit.approval.credential_rejected` (the bypass-attempt
  surface) per the spec.
- TTL default 7 days; per-action overrides allowed via the route
  annotation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A delegated-token write to a route with `x-agent-confirm`
  + caller mode `auto` returns 202 with an `approval_id`.
- [ ] A workspace-always-gated route gates regardless of caller
  mode (including `bypass`).
- [ ] Approve replays the recorded payload exactly once under the
  recorded idempotency key (a duplicate replay is a no-op).
- [ ] Decision endpoints reject delegated-token submissions with
  `403 approval_requires_session` and write
  `audit.approval.credential_rejected`.
- [ ] PAT with `approvals:act` accepted; PAT without is
  rejected.
- [ ] TTL expiry flips state and emits the right event.
- [ ] Inline channel set in `X-Agent-Channel` is persisted on
  the row and used by the SSE payload.
- [ ] Per-user `bypass` does not unlock workspace always-gated
  actions.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/agent/test_approval.py`.
- Integration test: end-to-end agent → 202 → manager approves
  from `/approvals` → row executed → audit chain joinable on
  correlation_id.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(agent/runtime): plan loop, tool dispatch, audit on every action
description:
## Problem / goal
The agent runtime executes a turn: render system + history + user
message, ask the LLM for a plan / tool call, dispatch the tool
against the CLI/REST surface using the **delegating user's
delegated token**, append the tool result, loop until reply or
gate. Two trigger modes — event-driven (chat-gateway / message
arrived) and scheduled (cron capabilities like daily digest).

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Embedded agents",
  §"Agent turn lifecycle", §"Conversation compaction",
  §"Agent audit trail", §"The agent-first invariant",
  §"Agent authority boundary".
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`agent_token` (the
  delegated token), §`audit_log`, §`chat_message`.
- Auth: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Agent tokens".
- Mocks: `mocks/web/src/components/AgentSidebar.tsx` (turn
  indicator, message thread), `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/ChatGatewayPage.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- New package `app/domain/agent/` with `runtime.py` exposing
  `run_turn(ctx, scope, thread_id, user_message, *,
  trigger: 'event'|'schedule') -> TurnOutcome`.
- Per turn: emit `agent.turn.started` SSE → resolve capability
  via `p12.model-router` → assemble context (system prompt from
  `llm_prompt_template`, compacted history, knowledge tool
  results) → loop `llm.complete + tool_dispatch` until reply,
  pending action, error, or server cap → emit
  `agent.turn.finished` (`replied | action | error | timeout`).
- Tool dispatch invokes the OpenAPI surface (`app/api/v1/...`) with
  the delegated token. Routes carrying `x-agent-confirm` or matching
  the workspace always-gated list raise the approval flow
  (`p12.approval-flow`) instead of executing.
- Every executed tool call writes an `audit_log` row attributed to
  the delegating user (`actor_kind=user`, populated `token_id`,
  `agent_label`, `agent_conversation_ref`, `reason`,
  `correlation_id`). Reads do **not** audit.
- Compaction is a system operation, not a turn — runs on a separate
  worker, replaces the single live summary row, never emits turn
  events.
- Cap on per-turn LLM calls (default: 8 tool-loop iterations) and a
  wall-clock timeout (default 60 s); cap exhaustion emits
  `agent.turn.finished` with `outcome=timeout` and a friendly
  "the request was too large" reply.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A turn that needs no tools emits started + finished
  (`replied`) and one `chat_message` row.
- [ ] A turn that calls one read tool emits started + finished
  (`replied`); zero audit rows for the read; the turn is one
  `llm_call` minimum.
- [ ] A turn that calls a write tool that is **not** gated emits
  one `audit_log` row attributed to the delegating user, with
  `token_id` and `agent_label` populated.
- [ ] A turn that calls a gated write tool emits started + finished
  (`action`), creates an `agent_action` row, and pauses.
- [ ] A scheduled trigger (no chat thread) records the turn and
  audits per above.
- [ ] Tool-loop cap and wall-clock timeout both emit
  `outcome=timeout`.
- [ ] Reads against rows the delegating user can't see return
  empty / 403 — the runtime does not bypass tenant/permission
  filters.
- [ ] `X-Agent-Channel`, `X-Agent-Conversation-Ref`,
  `X-Agent-Reason` headers propagated to every dispatched tool
  call.

## Test plan

title: feat(llm/usage): every call writes llm_call and updates the budget
description:
## Problem / goal
The thin glue that sits between the LLM client (`p0.llm`) and the
budget module: turn a provider response into a persisted
`llm_call` row plus a `budget.record_usage` call. Carries the
correlation id end-to-end so audit, llm_call, and the originating
agent action all join up.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Cost tracking",
  §"Cost tracking — extended", §"Workspace usage budget".
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`llm_usage` (a.k.a.
  `llm_call`) — fields `workspace_id`, `capability`,
  `provider_model_id`, `assignment_id`, `prompt_tokens`,
  `completion_tokens`, `cost_cents`, `fallback_attempts`,
  `finish_reason`, `correlation_id`, `actor_user_id`,
  `token_id`, `agent_label`, `at`.
- Cross-ref: §11 "Client abstraction" — the wrapper that this
  module hooks into.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/llm/usage_recorder.py` with
  `record(ctx, request, response, *, capability, model_pick,
  fallback_attempts, correlation_id) -> LlmCallRow`.
- Hooks installed on the LLM client (`p0.llm`) so every successful
  call invokes the recorder; the wrapper also calls
  `budget.check_budget` *before* dispatch and `budget.record_usage`
  *after* a 2xx.
- Captures `finish_reason`, `fallback_attempts` (rung index that
  succeeded), `X-LLM-Fallback-Attempts` echoed to the caller.
- Propagates `correlation_id` from the inbound request
  (`X-Correlation-Id`), generating one if absent and echoing
  `X-Correlation-Id-Echo`.
- On retryable failures the recorder is **not** invoked per attempt
  — only the final outcome (success or terminal error) writes a row.
  `budget_exceeded` writes nothing per the budget spec.
- Retention: `llm_call` kept 90 days (§15); only the first 30
  contribute to the meter — enforced by the budget query, not by
  delete.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Successful call writes one `llm_call` row with non-null
  cost, provider_model, assignment, correlation_id.
- [ ] Failed-then-fallback-success writes one row with
  `fallback_attempts > 0`.
- [ ] Pre-flight refusal writes no row but increments the
  `llm.budget_exceeded` log counter.
- [ ] `X-Correlation-Id-Echo` header round-trips through the
  caller.
- [ ] When the call is delegated-token, `token_id` and
  `agent_label` are populated.
- [ ] A row written by the recorder is visible to
  `budget.check_budget` on the next call (read-after-write).

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/llm/test_usage_recorder.py`
  with a fake LLM client.
- Integration test: end-to-end agent turn writes one row whose
  correlation id matches the audit row from the resulting tool
  call.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(llm/budget): rolling-30d workspace envelope (check + record)
description:
## Problem / goal
Every LLM call is gated by a workspace-wide rolling 30-day USD
envelope. The envelope wraps every capability, so a noisy chat
thread can't burn another capability's headroom. Pre-flight refuses
any call where `cost_30d_usd + projected_call_cost > cap_usd_30d`;
post-flight records actual usage and updates the cached aggregate.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Workspace usage
  budget" (Meter, Cap, At-cap behaviour, Pricing source, Visible
  surfaces, Demo mode overrides).
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`budget_ledger`
  (a.k.a. `workspace_budget` + `workspace_usage` materialised
  aggregate), §`llm_call`, §`workspace.quota_json`.
- Mocks: `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/LlmPage.tsx` (deployment
  spend tile), workspace `/settings` usage tile (no edit
  control).

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/llm/budget.py` exposing
  `check_budget(ctx, projected_cost_cents) -> None | raises
  BudgetExceeded` and `record_usage(ctx, usage: LlmUsage) ->
  None`. `LlmUsage` carries `prompt_tokens`,
  `completion_tokens`, `cost_cents`, `provider_model_id`,
  `assignment_id`, `capability`.
- Aggregate `workspace_usage.cost_30d_cents` refreshed by the
  background worker every 60 s; warm-started from `llm_call` on
  process boot. `check_budget` reads cached aggregate + projected
  cost (estimated from `prompt_tokens + max_output_tokens` × the
  serving `llm_provider_model` per-million prices).
- Cap row sourced from `workspace_budget` (defaults: prod 5.0 USD,
  demo 0.10 USD), inserted in the same transaction as the workspace
  row by the workspace-create handler. `workspace.quota_json
  .llm_budget_cents_30d` is the legacy mirror — keep it in sync
  for older readers.
- `BudgetExceeded` carries `{ capability, window: '30d_rolling',
  message }` per the spec; refused calls do **not** write
  `llm_call` (the meter only counts calls that left the client).
- `record_usage` writes `llm_call` with provider-reported actual
  tokens and recomputes `cost_cents` from current pricing; bumps
  the cached aggregate atomically.
- Free-tier (`:free` suffix) priced at zero; unknown
  `api_model_id` falls back to (0,0) per-million and emits a
  WARNING per call (no per-call audit row — operational telemetry
  only). Refusals log `event="llm.budget_exceeded"` at INFO.
- Per-IP aggregate cap (§15) is *out of scope here* — it lives in
  its own module; `check_budget` only enforces the per-workspace
  envelope.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] First call against a fresh workspace passes; aggregate ticks
  up post-call.
- [ ] Pre-flight refuses when projected + cached aggregate > cap.
- [ ] Refused call writes no `llm_call` row.
- [ ] Aggregate respects the 30-day window: a 31-day-old call no
  longer contributes.
- [ ] `record_usage` is idempotent on `(workspace_id,
  correlation_id, attempt)` — a retry doesn't double-count.
- [ ] Lowering the cap below the current aggregate immediately
  refuses the next call (within the 60 s refresh window).
- [ ] Free-tier model contributes 0 to the aggregate; the row is
  still written for telemetry.
- [ ] Demo workspaces seed cap = 0.10 USD.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/llm/test_budget.py` for every
  branch above; uses fake clock and fake pricing.
- Integration test: 50 concurrent `record_usage` calls don't
  drift the aggregate (atomicity).

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(llm/router): capability → provider_model resolver with fallback chain
description:
## Problem / goal
Every LLM caller asks for a *capability* (e.g. `chat.manager`,
`expenses.autofill`, `tasks.intake`), never a model id. The router
resolves capability → ordered list of `llm_provider_model` rows
(priority-ascending) so the client can walk the chain on retryable
upstream failures, and falls back through capability inheritance
when the capability itself has no enabled assignments.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Model assignment",
  §"Capability inheritance", §"Capability defaults (seeds)",
  §"Provider / model / provider-model registry".
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`model_assignment`
  (a.k.a. `llm_assignment`), §`llm_provider_model`,
  §`llm_capability_inheritance`.
- Mocks: `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/LlmPage.tsx` (assignment
  table, reorder, inheritance rows).

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/llm/router.py` exposing
  `resolve_model(ctx, capability) -> list[ModelPick]` (chain) and
  `resolve_primary(ctx, capability) -> ModelPick` (head only).
  `ModelPick` carries `provider_model_id`, `api_model_id`,
  `max_tokens`, `temperature`, `extra_api_params`,
  `required_capabilities`, `assignment_id`.
- Resolver order: enabled `llm_assignment` rows for the requested
  capability ordered by `priority ASC`; if none, walk
  `llm_capability_inheritance.inherits_from` (cycle-safe; reject
  cycles at write time elsewhere) and repeat; if still none, raise
  `CapabilityUnassignedError` (mapped to `503 capability_unassigned`
  by the API layer, plus a `CRITICAL` audit row at the call site).
- Caches the per-capability chain in-process for 30 s with an SSE-
  invalidation hook (`llm.assignment.changed`) so admin edits land
  fast without per-call DB reads.
- Inheritance resolution returns the **child**'s prompt overrides if
  any, else the parent's (`llm_prompt_template` lookup is delegated
  to the prompt-library task).
- Pure read path; no audit on resolve. Records resolve metadata on
  the eventual `llm_call` row (assignment id, fallback rung) for
  observability.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `resolve_model(ctx, 'chat.manager')` returns the seeded
  Gemma assignment when only the seed exists.
- [ ] Adding a higher-priority assignment (priority 0) demotes the
  seed (priority 1) on the next call without restart.
- [ ] `chat.admin` with no enabled assignments inherits
  `chat.manager`'s chain (per the v1 seed).
- [ ] An unknown capability raises `CapabilityUnassignedError`.
- [ ] Disabling every assignment for a capability with no parent
  raises `CapabilityUnassignedError`.
- [ ] Cache invalidates on `llm.assignment.changed` SSE event.
- [ ] Resolver does not perform any I/O against the LLM provider
  itself.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/llm/test_router.py` exercise
  every branch above with seeded `llm_provider_model` and
  `llm_assignment` fixtures.
- Property test: random chains of length 1..5 always tried in
  ascending priority order.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(web/public): RecoverPage — email -> magic link recovery
description:
## Problem / goal
Self-service recovery for users who lost every passkey device (§03). Workers/clients/guests submit email only; managers and owners-group members must additionally enter an unused break-glass code (step-up). Server replies 'sent_if_exists' to avoid role enumeration.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `.surface--login`, `.login__card`, the step-up toggle for owners.
- **API endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/auth/recover/begin` (returns 202 sent_if_exists).

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Magic links never issue a session on their own; the link lands on `/recover/enroll` (a separate page, out of scope here) and walks the user through a fresh passkey ceremony.
- Preserve the step-up branch (`stepUp` state) exactly as in the mock.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-public/RecoverPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-public/RecoverPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-public/RecoverPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/public/RecoverPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: feat(tasks): completion service (state machine + checklist + skip)
description:
## Problem / goal
"Mark done" is the highest-traffic worker action. The service must
gate-check the state machine, enforce evidence + checklist policy,
write the completion tombstone, fire `task.completed`, and handle
the concurrent-completion race per §06.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md`
  §"Task row" → "State machine", §"Completing a task",
  §"Skipping and cancellation", §"Concurrent completion",
  §"Evidence policy inheritance".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx`
  for the worker UX (Mark done, Skip with reason, Cancel).

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/completion.py`.
- All transitions are explicit `transition_to_<state>` functions
  on the service, each enforcing the §06 graph:
  - `start(ctx, task_id)` — `pending → in_progress`
  - `complete(ctx, task_id, *, note_md, evidence: list[EvidenceRef]
    | None = None)` — `pending | in_progress → completed`. Enforces:
    - Resolved `evidence.policy` from §06 cascade (forbid first,
      else most-specific) — reject if `policy == 'require'` and no
      photo, reject any photo when `policy == 'forbid'`.
    - When `tasks.checklist_required` is true, every required
      `task_checklist_item` must have `completed_at` set.
    - Apply `inventory_consumption_json` via `inventory_movement`
      rows unless `inventory.consume_on_task = false`.
    - When `task.asset_action_id` is set, update
      `asset_action.last_performed_at|last_performed_task_id`.
    - Insert a `task_completion` tombstone row (history survives
      later edits).
  - `skip(ctx, task_id, *, reason)` — only when
    `tasks.allow_skip_with_reason = true` resolves to true at the
    task scope.
  - `cancel(ctx, task_id, *, reason)` — owners/managers only.
  - `revert_overdue(ctx, task_id, target_state)` — clears
    `overdue_since`.
- All transitions are `enum -> enum` functions with a single
  `_assert_transition(current, target)` validator; raise typed
  `InvalidStateTransition` carrying both states.
- **Concurrent completion** (§06): the second writer wins —
  `UPDATE ... SET completed_at = ..., completed_by_user_id = ...`
  unconditionally. The loser receives a 200 with the final row;
  audit `task.complete_superseded` against the displaced row.
- Emit `task.completed`, `task.skipped`, `task.cancelled` on the
  bus; one audit row per mutation; `WorkspaceContext` everywhere.
- Permission checks (§06 "State transitions and who may trigger
  them"): worker may drive their own assigned tasks through
  `pending ↔ in_progress ↔ completed`; owners/managers may move
  any task to `cancelled`; only the scheduler worker may drive
  the auto `scheduled → pending` flip.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every illegal transition raises `InvalidStateTransition`
- [ ] Required-photo policy enforced (and forbid is absolute)
- [ ] Required checklist items enforced when the resolved setting is on
- [ ] Inventory movements written by default, suppressed when
      `inventory.consume_on_task = false`
- [ ] Asset-action `last_performed_*` updated when applicable
- [ ] `task_completion` tombstone written
- [ ] Concurrent-completion race produces the audit
      `task.complete_superseded` row
- [ ] Three terminal events emitted; audit on every mutation
- [ ] Unit + integration tests cover all branches

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_completion.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_tasks_completion_race.py -xvs
```

title: feat(tasks): one-off task creation (no schedule)
description:
## Problem / goal
Workers, managers, and the NL-intake agent need a path to create a
single task that is not attached to a schedule. The quick-add modal
on `/today` and `/schedule`, the manager's "+ New task" button, and
`POST /api/v1/tasks/from_nl/commit` all land here.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Task kinds"
  (kind 1 — One-off), §"Self-created and personal tasks",
  §"Natural-language intake (agent)".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/components/NewTaskModal.tsx` for the
  quick-add shape; `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/TodayPage.tsx`
  for the surface.

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/oneoff.py`.
- Function: `create_oneoff(ctx: WorkspaceContext, payload:
  TaskCreate) -> TaskView`.
- Payload: `template_id` (optional — when present, copy `title`,
  `description_md`, `priority`, `photo_evidence`,
  `duration_minutes`, `linked_instruction_ids`,
  `inventory_consumption_json`, and seed checklist items via
  `expand_checklist_for_task(..., is_ad_hoc=True)`); explicit
  `title`, `description_md`, `priority`, `property_id`, `area_id`,
  `unit_id`, `expected_role_id`, `assigned_user_id` (optional —
  defer to `p5.assignment` if null), `scheduled_for_local`,
  `duration_minutes`, `photo_evidence`, `is_personal` (default
  `False` per §06 — only the quick-add UI sets `True` explicitly).
- Always sets `created_by = ctx.user_id`, `state = 'pending'` if
  `scheduled_for_local <= now`, else `'scheduled'` (the
  `scheduled → pending` worker handles the future case).
- Permission: caller must hold `tasks.create` (§05); enforced by
  the route layer, but assert it here too for defence-in-depth.
- When `assigned_user_id` is `null` and `expected_role_id` is set,
  delegate to `p5.assignment` to pick an assignee.
- Publish `task.created` (and `task.assigned` when applicable).
- Audit `task.create_oneoff` with the resolved fields.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Template-backed and template-less paths both work
- [ ] `is_personal=True` requires `assigned_user_id == ctx.user_id`
      (per §06 "personal" semantics)
- [ ] Past `scheduled_for_local` lands in `state = 'pending'`
      immediately
- [ ] Checklist seeding uses `is_ad_hoc=True`
- [ ] Audit + events emitted
- [ ] Unit tests cover all branches above

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_oneoff.py -xvs
```

title: feat(tasks): generate_task_occurrences worker job
description:
## Problem / goal
The hourly worker that walks every active schedule, expands its
RRULE/RDATE/EXDATE inside the workspace horizon, and inserts a `task`
row per new occurrence. Idempotent (re-runs do not duplicate).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Generation"
  (steps 1–5), §"Pause vs active range";
  `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"occurrence" / §"task" rows.
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/SchedulerPage.tsx` for the
  workspace timeline; `mocks/app/mock_data.py` for occurrence shape
  (`assignee`, `state`, `starts_at`, `ends_at`, `checklist_items`).
- Boundary: lives in `app/workers/tasks/generator.py`; uses
  `app/domain/tasks/schedules.py` reads only — no direct table writes
  outside its own DAO.

## Implementation notes
- Entry point: `generate_task_occurrences(ctx: WorkspaceContext, *,
  now: datetime | None = None) -> GenerationReport`.
- Horizon: workspace setting `tasks.generation_horizon_days`
  (default 30; §06 says "now + 30 days").
- For each schedule where `paused_at IS NULL`, `deleted_at IS NULL`,
  `active_from <= today`, and (`active_until IS NULL OR active_until
  >= today`):
  1. Compute next occurrences in property timezone via
     `dateutil.rrule.rrulestr(schedule.rrule, dtstart=...)`.
  2. Apply `exdate_local`, then add `rdate_local`.
  3. For each candidate `(schedule_id, scheduled_for_local)` not
     already present in `tasks`, materialise a row with
     `originally_scheduled_for = scheduled_for_local`,
     `state = 'scheduled'`, fields copied from template + schedule
     defaults, and call `expand_checklist_for_task(...)` from
     `p5.checklist-template` to seed `task_checklist_item` rows.
  4. Skip days covered by an active `property_closure` matching the
     task's `(property_id, unit_id)`; record a
     `schedules.skipped_for_closure` audit event (no task row).
  5. Call the assignment service (`p5.assignment`) on each new task.
- **Idempotency**: enforce a partial unique index on `tasks
  (schedule_id, scheduled_for_local) WHERE schedule_id IS NOT NULL`
  in the migration; the worker uses `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` and
  reports skipped duplicates in `GenerationReport.skipped_duplicate`.
- Publish `task.created` (one event per new row) on the bus from
  Phase 0 events.
- Schedule via the APScheduler instance from §16 — hourly tick.
- Audit summary row per run: `schedules.generation_tick` with the
  count of new tasks, skipped duplicates, skipped-for-closure days.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Two consecutive runs over the same window create rows once
- [ ] Paused / past-active-until / deleted schedules are skipped
- [ ] Property closures suppress occurrences and emit the audit
      event
- [ ] `task.created` published per new row
- [ ] Unit tests cover the idempotency invariant and an RRULE that
      crosses a DST boundary in the property tz
- [ ] WorkspaceContext threaded through; never reads tenancy from
      the env

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_generator.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_tasks_generator_run.py -xvs
```

title: feat(tasks): Schedule CRUD with RFC 5545 RRULE + DTSTART/UNTIL
description:
## Problem / goal
A schedule says "create a task from this template on these dates at
these times". Stored as RFC 5545 RRULE plus the property-local
DTSTART. Without it, recurring tasks cannot be authored.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Schedule",
  §"UI expose-levels", §"Pause / resume", §"Deleting and editing",
  §"Pause vs active range"; §"Assignment algorithm" for the
  `default_assignee` + `backup_assignee_user_ids` shape.
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx` renders
  `name`, `template_id`, `property_id`, `rrule_human`,
  `default_assignee_id`, `duration_minutes`, `paused`, `active_from`.

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/schedules.py`.
- Pydantic DTOs: `ScheduleCreate`, `ScheduleUpdate`, `ScheduleView`.
  Fields per §06 table: `name`, `template_id`, `property_id`,
  `area_id`, `default_assignee` (user id),
  `backup_assignee_user_ids` (ordered list), `rrule`,
  `dtstart_local`, `duration_minutes`, `rdate_local`, `exdate_local`,
  `active_from`, `active_until`, `paused_at`.
- RRULE validation: parse with `dateutil.rrule.rrulestr` against
  `dtstart_local`; reject if it does not produce at least one
  occurrence or if it is unbounded with no `active_until` AND no
  `COUNT/UNTIL` (configurable safeguard — record an audit warning
  but allow, since §06 expects open-ended weeklies).
- `RDATE` / `EXDATE` are line-separated ISO-8601 local timestamps;
  store as `text` exactly as written and re-parse on use.
- `default_assignee` resolution: validate the user holds a
  `user_work_role` matching `template.role_id` at the schedule's
  workspace; same for every entry in `backup_assignee_user_ids` —
  return 422 `error = "backup_invalid_work_role"` per §06.
- `pause(id)` / `resume(id)` toggle `paused_at`. Pause does not
  cancel already-materialised tasks.
- `delete(id)` soft-deletes and cancels every linked task with
  `state IN ('scheduled')` to `cancelled` with
  `cancellation_reason = 'schedule deleted'` (per §06).
- `update(id, payload, *, apply_to_existing=False)` — when
  `apply_to_existing=True`, patch tasks with
  `state IN ('scheduled', 'pending')` only (per §06 "Deleting and
  editing").
- Helper: `preview_occurrences(rrule, dtstart_local, *, n=5,
  rdate, exdate)` returning the next N local datetimes — drives the
  `/schedules` "Preview — next 7 days" panel and the editor's "next
  5 occurrences" preview.
- `WorkspaceContext` for every method; audit on every mutation
  (`schedule.create | update | pause | resume | delete | apply_to_existing`).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD + pause/resume working end-to-end
- [ ] Backup-list validation rejects users without the right
      work-role at write time
- [ ] `delete()` cascades to `state=scheduled` task cancellation
- [ ] `apply_to_existing` patches `scheduled|pending` only
- [ ] `preview_occurrences` honours RDATE/EXDATE
- [ ] Audit row per mutation
- [ ] Unit tests cover RRULE round-trip, pause-vs-active-range
      precedence, and each invariant

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_schedules.py -xvs
```

title: feat(payroll/payslip): compute gross / multipliers / deductions / net
description:
## Problem / goal
For a `(pay_period, user)` pair: aggregate the user's shifts within
the window, apply the matching `PayRule` (with overtime / night /
weekend multipliers), subtract deductions, store the resulting
`Payslip` row.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Payslip",
  §"components_json schema", §"Pay-rule selection when multiple
  rules overlap".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/payroll/models.py`
  (`Payslip`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PayPage.tsx` shows the
  payslip line items.

## Implementation notes
- Pure function `app/domain/payroll/compute.py::compute_payslip`:
  - Inputs: `(ctx, period, user_id)`.
  - Pulls shifts via `shifts.list_for_period(ctx, user_id, period)`.
  - Splits each shift into base / overtime / night / weekend buckets
    (helpers in `app/util/timewindow.py`).
  - Looks up effective `PayRule` per shift start.
  - Builds `components_json` per spec.
  - Computes `gross_cents`, `deductions_cents` (start with empty
    JSON list — populated by deduction plugins later), `net_cents
    = gross - sum(deductions)`.
- Wraps in `payslip_recompute(ctx, period_id)` that iterates all
  engaged users and upserts (`unique(period_id, user_id)`).
- Recompute is idempotent and **only allowed on `draft` or
  `locked` periods** (paid → 409).
- Emits `payslip.computed` event.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Shifts split correctly across midnight, weekend boundaries
- [ ] Overtime applied above weekly threshold per rule
- [ ] Multi-rule windows pick the rule active at shift start
- [ ] Idempotent recompute (running twice yields identical row)
- [ ] Money math in integer cents only — no floats
- [ ] Unit tests cover canonical cases from §09

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_payslip_compute.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_payslip_recompute.py -xvs
```

title: feat(tasks): TaskTemplate CRUD service
description:
## Problem / goal
A `task_template` is the reusable definition that schedules and stay
lifecycle rules materialise from. Without a CRUD service there is no
way to author "Weekly kitchen deep clean" or "Standard turnover".

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Task template",
  §"Checklist template shape", §"Evidence policy inheritance";
  `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"task_template".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx`
  (renders `name`, `role`, `priority`, `photo_evidence`,
  `duration_minutes`, `property_scope`, `checklist`).
- **Boundary** (`docs/specs/01-architecture.md`): service lives in
  `app/domain/tasks/templates.py`; only this service writes
  `task_template` rows.

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/templates.py`.
- Pydantic DTOs: `TaskTemplateCreate`, `TaskTemplateUpdate`,
  `TaskTemplateView`. Fields: `name`, `description_md`, `role_id`,
  `duration_minutes`, `property_scope`
  (`any | one | listed`), `listed_property_ids`, `area_scope`,
  `listed_area_ids`, `checklist_template_json` (validated as a list
  of items — see `p5.checklist-template`), `photo_evidence`
  (`disabled | optional | required`), `linked_instruction_ids`,
  `priority` (`low | normal | high | urgent`),
  `inventory_consumption_json`, `llm_hints_md`.
- All operations take `ctx: WorkspaceContext` and resolve
  `workspace_id` from it — never from the request payload.
- Validate `property_scope` consistency: `listed` requires non-empty
  `listed_property_ids`; `one` requires exactly one; `any` requires
  empty.
- Same shape rule for `area_scope` / `listed_area_ids`.
- `delete()` is soft (sets `deleted_at`); refuse delete when any
  active `schedule.template_id` or active `stay_lifecycle_rule
  .template_id` references the template — return a typed
  `TemplateInUseError` with the offending ids.
- Every mutation calls `audit.record(...)` with the actor and the
  before/after diff (`task_template.create | update | delete`).
- No business logic about scheduling here — that's `p5.schedule.crud`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Create / read / list (with `q`, `role_id`, `deleted` filters) /
      update / soft-delete all working
- [ ] Returns 422 with typed errors on scope inconsistency
- [ ] Returns 409 with `TemplateInUseError` when delete is blocked
- [ ] Audit row written on every mutation
- [ ] Unit tests cover happy path + every validation branch
- [ ] WorkspaceContext is the only source of `workspace_id`

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_templates.py -xvs
uv run mypy app/domain/tasks/templates.py
```

title: feat(authz): permission enforcement dependency for routers
description:
## Problem / goal
Every protected endpoint needs the same authorization decision: does
the caller hold a `role_grants` row + appropriate `permission_rule` /
`owners` membership for this action on this scope? Today each router
would re-implement the check; we need one canonical FastAPI
dependency / decorator that takes an `action_key` and refuses
calls that fail.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"Permission resolution",
  `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` §"Action catalog"
  (canonical action keys), §"How a rule narrows or widens a default";
  `docs/specs/15-audit-and-rls.md` for audit row format.
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PermissionsPage.tsx`
  (manager UI inspecting effective rules — this task only ships the
  enforcement engine the page eventually targets).

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/services/authz/enforce.py`:
  - `Permission(action_key)` FastAPI dependency.
  - `require(ctx: WorkspaceContext, action_key: str, *, scope_kind, scope_id)`
    callable for service-layer use (not every check is router-bound).
- Resolution order per spec §02:
  1. Reject if `action_key` not in catalog → 422 internal misuse
     (caller bug).
  2. If `root_only` and caller not in `owners` group on the scope
     (or any containing scope for property targets) → 403.
  3. Walk `permission_rule` rows scope-by-scope (most-specific
     first): explicit `deny` wins unless `root_protected_deny=True`
     and caller is in `owners`. First `allow` ends the walk.
  4. Fall back to `default_allow` system groups.
- Wire to existing `WorkspaceContext` (`p0.tenancy.ctx`); never
  trust a body field for the calling subject.
- Emit one structured log line per denied check (action_key,
  scope_kind, scope_id, subject) — useful for "who can do this?"
  preview later.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `Permission("expenses.approve")` dependency rejects callers
      without the action; allows owners; allows users matched by an
      `allow` rule.
- [ ] `root_only` actions deny non-owners even with an explicit
      allow rule.
- [ ] `root_protected_deny` actions ignore `deny` rules targeting
      owners.
- [ ] Catalog membership validated at app boot (startup hook fails
      fast on unknown keys in `permission_rule` rows).
- [ ] Unit tests cover every quadrant: allowed, denied, root-only,
      root-protected, unknown-action, no-rule fallback.
- [ ] Integration test wires the dependency into one sample router
      and confirms 403 for an under-permissioned user.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_authz_enforce.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_authz_router_dep.py -xvs
```
close_reason: Permission enforcement dependency landed — Permission(action_key, scope_kind, scope_id_from_path) FastAPI dep + require() service-layer callable; resolver order per §02 (unknown-action 422 → invalid-scope 422 → root-only gate → scope walk with deny-within-scope wins + owner-immunity on root_protected_deny → default_allow fallback via is_member_of); 76 actions in ActionSpec catalog; EmptyPermissionRuleRepository ships as v1 default, SQL adapter lands with permission_rule table in a follow-up PR.

title: feat(time/shifts): clock-in / clock-out + manager manual edit
description:
## Problem / goal
Workers open a shift (clock-in) and close it (clock-out); managers can
create or amend shifts after the fact. Shifts feed timesheets and
payroll downstream. Without this the whole §09 stack is dead.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Bookings",
  §"Pay basis (per-engagement)", §"Owner and manager adjustments".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx` (worker
  clock-in/out widget), `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PayPage.tsx`
  (timesheet table the edits feed into).
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/time/models.py` (`Shift`).

## Implementation notes
- New service `app/domain/time/shifts.py`:
  - `open_shift(ctx: WorkspaceContext, *, user_id, property_id?,
    source='manual')` — rejects if the user already has an open shift
    (`ends_at IS NULL`).
  - `close_shift(ctx, shift_id, *, ends_at=None)` — defaults to
    `now()`; refuses to close a shift that is not the caller's own
    unless caller has `time.edit_others`.
  - `edit_shift(ctx, shift_id, **fields)` — manager-only; validates
    `ends_at >= starts_at`; writes audit row.
  - `list_open_shifts(ctx, *, user_id?)`.
- REST surface under `app/api/time/`:
  - `POST /api/time/shifts/open`, `POST /api/time/shifts/{id}/close`,
    `PATCH /api/time/shifts/{id}`, `GET /api/time/shifts`.
- Capabilities (Phase 0 caps): `time.clock_self`, `time.edit_others`.
- SSE event `time.shift.changed` on every mutation (consumed by the
  manager pay page for live updates).
- Every mutation calls `audit.write(ctx, 'shift.<verb>', shift_id,
  diff)`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Worker can open exactly one shift at a time
- [ ] Worker can close their own shift; cannot close someone else's
- [ ] Manager can create / amend any shift in the workspace
- [ ] `ends_at < starts_at` rejected with 422
- [ ] Audit row written on every state change
- [ ] SSE `time.shift.changed` fan-out verified
- [ ] OpenAPI regenerated; mock pages still pass type-check

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_shifts_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_shifts_api.py -xvs
# end-to-end against the mock UI
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/time/shifts/open \
  -H 'authorization: Bearer <session>' \
  -d '{"property_id":"prop_..."}'
```

title: feat(tenancy): replace Phase-0 stub with real slug→workspace + membership check
description:
## Problem / goal
Phase 0 shipped a tenancy middleware stub that synthesised a
`WorkspaceContext` from the slug alone. With identity now real,
the middleware must resolve the slug → `workspace`, look up the
authenticated user (session or token), verify a `user_workspace`
membership exists, and bind the full `WorkspaceContext` (including
`is_owner`). Non-members get 404, not 403, so workspace existence is
not leaked.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"Multi-tenancy runtime",
  §"Workspace addressing".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Sessions" + §"API
  tokens" auth wiring.
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md`
  §"Constant-time cross-tenant responses".
- **Phase 0**: `app/tenancy/middleware.py` (stub),
  `app/tenancy/context.py::WorkspaceContext`.

## Implementation notes
- Replace `resolve_workspace_stub()` with `resolve_workspace()`:
  - Look up `workspace` by slug; **constant-time** miss handling —
    same response shape + timing as a non-member-of-known-workspace.
  - Resolve actor: session cookie → `user_id`; bearer token →
    `user_id` (api_token) or `delegate_for_user_id` (agent_token).
  - Look up `user_workspace(user_id, workspace_id)`; missing or
    non-`active` → 404 `workspace_not_found`.
  - Compute `is_owner` via `permission_group_member` in `owners@<ws>`.
  - Build `WorkspaceContext(workspace_id, slug, user_id,
    is_owner, actor_kind, agent_label, session_id, token_id)` and
    bind to the request scope.
- Bare-host routes (signup, magic-link, recovery, login) keep using
  `tenant_agnostic()`; the middleware skips them by route prefix.
- Reject mismatched session/token + slug: a session for user X used
  against `/w/<other-slug>/` returns 404 (constant-time).
- Update existing route handlers to assert
  `ctx.user_id is not None` for any non-public route.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Slug miss + non-member responses indistinguishable in body and
      timing (cross-tenant constant-time test from §15 passes)
- [ ] `WorkspaceContext.is_owner` populated correctly for both
      sessions and delegated tokens
- [ ] Bare-host signup / magic-link routes unaffected
- [ ] Phase-0 stub removed; only callers go through the real resolver
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/tenancy/test_middleware_real.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/tenancy/test_membership_404_constant_time.py -xvs
```
Manual: in dev, log in as a member of `wsA`, hit `/w/wsB/today`,
confirm 404 with the same body shape as a slug that doesn't exist.
close_reason: Real slug→workspace + membership check shipped. Middleware resolves /w/<slug>/... via Workspace + UserWorkspace with owners-group + role_grant derivation; constant-time 404 envelope via _not_found; timing-equalisation dummy read; Phase-0 stub retained behind settings.phase0_stub_enabled (default off per cd-iwsv). 55 unit+integration tests passing.

title: feat(identity/membership): user_workspace invite/accept/remove + workspace switch
description:
## Problem / goal
Users join workspaces via invitation. The membership service governs
invite, accept, remove, and the in-session "switch workspace" action
(both web SPA and API).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md`
  §"Additional users (invite → click-to-accept)".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` (membership +
  surface grants).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/team/Invite.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/workspace/Switcher.tsx`.
- Phase 1 tables: `user_workspace`, `role_grant`.

## Implementation notes
- Module `app/identity/membership.py`:
  - `invite(ctx, *, email, grant_role, scope_property_id=None)` —
    creates a pending `user_workspace` row (state=`invited`) +
    sends a magic link `purpose=invite_accept`. Existing `user`
    is reused; new user row created on accept.
  - `accept(token)` — bare host, consumes the magic link, flips
    `user_workspace.state` to `active`, creates the matching
    `role_grant` in one tx. Issues / refreshes the session.
  - `remove(ctx, *, user_id)` — owners can remove anyone except the
    last owner; managers can remove workers/clients/guests; users
    can remove themselves except the last owner.
  - `list_for_user(user_id)` — used by the workspace switcher.
  - `switch(session_id, *, workspace_id)` — updates
    `Session.workspace_id` after verifying membership.
- Routes:
  - `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/team/invite`
  - `POST /invite/accept` (bare host)
  - `DELETE /w/<slug>/api/v1/team/{user_id}`
  - `GET /api/v1/me/workspaces`
  - `POST /api/v1/me/workspaces/switch` `{workspace_id}`
- Audit on every mutation; redact email in audit (hash only).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Invite → accept end-to-end works on both DBs
- [ ] Removal respects owner-protection rules (cross-checked with
      `p2.role-grants` + `p2.owners-group`)
- [ ] Switcher updates the session's `workspace_id` and the response
      `Set-Cookie` reflects it
- [ ] A user who is not a member of `<slug>` gets 404 from
      `/w/<slug>/...` (covered by `p2.tenancy-identity-wire`)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/identity/test_membership.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/identity/test_membership_invite_accept.py -xvs
```
Manual: from `/team`, invite a fresh email, accept in a private
window, switch workspaces, confirm `/today` reflects it.
close_reason: user_workspace invite/accept/remove + workspace switch shipped. Invite-accept end-to-end works on SQLite + PG; owner-protection via shared LastOwnerMember guard; switcher updates Session.workspace_id; 1502 tests pass (unit + integration + API). Self-review cd-o5u applied follow-up fixes inline.

title: feat(authz/owners): owners-group bootstrap + governance + WorkspaceContext flag
description:
## Problem / goal
The `owners` group is the workspace's root authority. It must be
created on every workspace bootstrap, protected from deletion, and the
"is owner" bit must be carried on `WorkspaceContext` so downstream
authority checks are O(1).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md`
  §"Permissions: surface, groups, and action catalog"
  (system groups), §"Root-only actions (governance)".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Self-serve signup"
  step 2 (places the first user in `owners@<ws>`).
- **Phase 0**: `app.tenancy.context.WorkspaceContext`.
- Reuses `p2.permission-groups` for the protected-group machinery.

## Implementation notes
- Hook `bootstrap_owners_group(ws_id, first_user_id)` invoked from:
  - `bootstrap_workspace` fixture (test path).
  - `p2.signup` `GET /signup/verify` transaction.
  - First-owner self-hosted bootstrap (§03 §"First owner").
- The hook seeds `owners@<ws>` (system=True, slug=`owners`,
  capabilities = full action catalog) + the four other system groups
  (`managers`, `workers`, `clients`, `chat-gateway-agents`). Slugs
  are workspace-namespaced via the `workspace_id` foreign key — no
  "@" substrings in storage.
- Last-owner protection: removing the only member of `owners@<ws>`
  via `p2.permission-groups` returns 409 `last_owner_member`. This
  is a separate guard from `p2.role-grants`'s last-owner-grant rule
  — both must hold.
- `WorkspaceContext.is_owner` populated at request time by checking
  `permission_group_member` membership in `owners@<ws>`. Cache for
  the lifetime of the request.
- Audit: `audit.workspace.owners_bootstrapped` written once per
  workspace, in the same tx as the seed inserts.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every new workspace has all five system groups seeded
- [ ] First user is a member of `owners@<ws>`
- [ ] Removing the last owner member → 409
- [ ] `WorkspaceContext.is_owner` is True for owners, False otherwise
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/identity/test_owners_bootstrap.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/identity/test_owners_governance.py -xvs
```
Manual: in the dev container, complete signup, then attempt to leave
the owners group via the UI — confirm refusal.
close_reason: Shipped: owners-group bootstrap + last-owner-member guard + WorkspaceContext.is_owner seam (via app.authz.owners). All AC met; tests green on SQLite (Postgres run via CREWDAY_TEST_DB).

title: feat(authz/groups): permission group CRUD + members + system-group protection
description:
## Problem / goal
Permission groups are the deployable unit of authority. Owners + managers
must be able to define custom groups, add/remove members, and never be
able to delete or rename the system groups.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md`
  §"Permissions: surface, groups, and action catalog",
  §"Action catalog".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"permission_group",
  §"permission_group_member".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/team/PermissionGroups.tsx`.
- Phase 1 table: `permission_group`, `permission_group_member`.

## Implementation notes
- Module `app/identity/permission_groups.py`:
  - `list(ctx)`, `get(ctx, group_id)`, `create(ctx, slug, name,
    capabilities)`, `update(ctx, group_id, …)`, `delete(ctx, group_id)`.
  - `add_member(ctx, group_id, user_id)` /
    `remove_member(ctx, group_id, user_id)`.
- System groups (`owners`, `managers`, `workers`, `clients`) are
  flagged `system=True`. Mutations refuse: `delete` → 409
  `system_group_protected`, `update` allows only `name` (slug +
  capabilities frozen), `members` are mutable except the
  last-owner rule (defer to `p2.owners-group`).
- `capabilities_json` validated against the action catalog
  (§05); unknown capability → 422 `unknown_capability`.
- Routes mirror the methods at `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE
  /w/<slug>/api/v1/permission-groups[/{id}[/members]]`.
- Audit on every mutation
  (`audit.permission_group.{created|updated|deleted}` and
  `…member_added|member_removed`).
- Capability gate: `permission_groups.manage` (default: owners only).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] System groups cannot be deleted; slug + capabilities cannot
      change
- [ ] `capabilities_json` validated against the action catalog
- [ ] Member add / remove writes audit
- [ ] Capability gate enforced; non-owner mutation → 403
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/identity/test_permission_groups.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/identity/test_permission_groups_routes.py -xvs
```
Manual: drive `/team/groups` in dev; attempt to delete `owners@<ws>`,
confirm 409.

title: feat(authz/role-grants): grant CRUD service + owner-authority policy
description:
## Problem / goal
Role grants are the per-user authority model: who is a manager / worker
/ client / guest, and on which property scope. The service must enforce
the owner-authority policy (only owners can mint manager grants) and
validate scope shape.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` §"Role grants",
  §"Surface grants at a glance", §"Permissions: surface, groups,
  and action catalog".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"role_grant".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/team/RoleGrantsAdmin.tsx`.
- Phase 1 table: `role_grant`.

## Implementation notes
- Module `app/identity/role_grants.py`:
  - `list(ctx, *, user_id=None, scope_property_id=None)`
  - `grant(ctx, *, user_id, grant_role, scope_property_id=None)`
  - `revoke(ctx, *, grant_id)`
- Policy:
  - Only members of `owners@<ws>` may grant `manager`.
  - Only owners + managers may grant `worker` / `client` / `guest`.
  - `scope_property_id` must reference a `property_workspace` row in
    the calling workspace; cross-workspace property → 422.
  - Cannot remove the last owner grant — 409 `last_owner_grant`.
- Routes:
  - `GET /w/<slug>/api/v1/role-grants`
  - `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/role-grants`
  - `DELETE /w/<slug>/api/v1/role-grants/{id}`
- Audit `audit.role_grant.granted` / `…revoked` in the same tx as
  the row mutation.
- Capability check via the action catalog (`users.grant_role` /
  `users.revoke_grant`, §05).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Owner-only authority enforced for manager grants
- [ ] Last-owner protection prevents lockout
- [ ] Cross-workspace scope_property_id rejected
- [ ] Audit row written for every mutation
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/identity/test_role_grants.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/identity/test_role_grants_policy.py -xvs
```
Manual: drive `/team` admin in dev, attempt to remove the only owner,
confirm 409.

title: feat(auth/tokens): API token mint / list / revoke (sha256 hash + prefix)
description:
## Problem / goal
Workspace-scoped API tokens for managers and headless integrations.
Mint shows the plaintext exactly once; list shows only the prefix;
revoke is an audit-tracked, irreversible flag.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"API tokens" /
  §"Creation", §"Scopes", §"Usage", §"Revocation and rotation",
  §"Observability fields on `api_token`", §"Guardrails".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/tokens/*`.
- Phase 1 table: `api_token` (id, user_id, workspace_id, label,
  scope_json, prefix, hash, expires_at, last_used_at, revoked_at).

## Implementation notes
- Routes:
  - `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/tokens` `{name, scopes,
    expires_at, note}` → returns `{token, key_id, prefix,
    expires_at}` once.
  - `GET /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/tokens` → list (no secret).
  - `DELETE /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/tokens/{id}` → sets `revoked_at`
    (no hard delete; preserves audit links).
- Token format per §03: `mip_<key_id>_<secret>`; `key_id` ULID,
  `secret` 256 bits base32. Store argon2id hash of the secret per
  §15 §"Token hashing"; `prefix` = first 8 chars of `secret` for
  list display. (Fix the spec/Phase-1 mismatch on hash function in
  the PR — argon2id, not sha256.)
- Permission gate: `api_tokens.manage` action (default: owners +
  managers, §05).
- `expires_at`: required; default 90 days; workspace cap
  enforced (§03 Guardrails). "never" requires the noisy warning
  flag.
- Service signature `mint(ctx: WorkspaceContext, …) -> MintedToken`;
  audit row `audit.api_token.minted` written in the same tx.
- Auth middleware: parse `Authorization: Bearer mip_<key>_<secret>`;
  O(1) lookup by `key_id`; verify `argon2id`; bump `last_used_at`
  every N minutes (debounced, not every request).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Plaintext returned exactly once; never logged or stored
- [ ] List endpoint returns prefix + label, never the secret
- [ ] Revoke is idempotent and immediate (cached parses invalidate)
- [ ] Token auth path is constant-time on bad secret (no early return)
- [ ] Permission gate enforced; non-managers get 403
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_api_token.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_api_token_auth.py -xvs
```
Manual: mint a token in dev, hit `/w/<slug>/api/v1/healthz`
authenticated, then revoke and confirm 401 on next call.
close_reason: API token mint / list / revoke with argon2id hash landed. 68 tests passing (64 unit + 4 integration, both DBs). selfreview applied 1-min debounce fix. Follow-ups: cd-wd78 (spec §12), cd-i1qe (delegated/PAT kinds).
