title: sec(auth/signup): rate limits, disposable-email blocklist, CAPTCHA stub, reserved slugs
description:
## Problem / goal
Signup is the only fully open POST surface on the SaaS. It must
absorb scripted abuse without leaking through to billing
(workspace bloat) or to the LLM budget (downstream consequence). This
task hardens `p2.signup` against the §15 self-serve abuse vectors.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Self-serve abuse
  mitigations", §"Per-IP aggregate LLM spend cap".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Self-serve signup"
  reserved slugs + homoglyph guard.
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/signup/SignupStart.tsx` (CAPTCHA
  placeholder).
- Phase 0 capability: `app.config.capabilities.signup_enabled`.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/auth/signup_abuse.py`:
  - `check_rate(ip, email)` — token bucket per IP and per email
    (Redis-backed in prod, in-memory in dev).
  - `is_disposable(email)` — bundled blocklist file under
    `app/auth/disposable_domains.txt` (cite source + refresh date in
    a header comment).
  - `check_captcha(token)` — verifies a Turnstile/HCaptcha token via
    `httpx`; in dev a fixed test token always passes.
  - `check_reserved_slug(slug)` — wraps `app.tenancy.slug.is_reserved`
    + `…is_homoglyph_collision` so callers get one-call coverage.
- Wire into `p2.signup` `POST /signup/start`. Failures return 429
  for rate, 422 for disposable email + bad CAPTCHA, 409 for slug
  rejections (already covered by signup itself — verify wiring).
- Audit: `audit.signup.rate_limited`, `…disposable_email`,
  `…captcha_failed`. Each row records `ip_hash` + `email_hash`,
  never raw values.
- Capabilities entry `captcha_required` (default true on
  `crew.day`, false on self-host) so self-hosters can disable it.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Per-IP + per-email rate limits enforced; bypassable in tests via
      a clear fixture
- [ ] Disposable-email blocklist loaded once at startup; reload via
      SIGHUP test passes
- [ ] CAPTCHA stub returns 422 on failure; success path threads
      through cleanly
- [ ] Reserved-slug check matches the existing `app.tenancy.slug`
      single source of truth (no duplicate list)
- [ ] Audit rows carry only hashes, never raw IP/email
- [ ] Unit + integration tests cover every refusal path

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_signup_abuse.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_signup_abuse_wired.py -xvs
```
Manual: hammer `/signup/start` with `hey` from a fixture IP; expect
429 once the bucket is empty.
close_reason: Landed signup abuse mitigations: rate limits, disposable-email blocklist, CAPTCHA stub, reserved slugs (cd-3dc7 followup note in commit).

title: feat(auth/signup): self-serve signup (email → magic → passkey → slug)
description:
## Problem / goal
Open self-service signup creates a workspace + first user atomically
when `settings.signup_enabled` is true. The flow is the public face of
the SaaS; correctness here is load-bearing for everything downstream.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Self-serve signup"
  (full step list, reserved slugs, homoglyph guard, grace period).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Self-serve abuse
  mitigations" (covered separately by `p2.signup.abuse`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/signup/*` (full storyboard already
  exists).
- Phase 1 tables: `workspace`, `user`, `user_workspace`,
  `permission_group`, `permission_group_member`, `role_grant`.

## Implementation notes
- Endpoints (all bare-host, `tenant_agnostic()`):
  - `POST /signup/start` `{email, desired_slug}` → magic link sent;
    `signup_attempt` row keyed by `(email, desired_slug)`,
    15-minute TTL.
  - `GET /signup/verify?token=…` → consumes the magic link, atomically
    inserts the new `workspace`, `user`, four system permission groups,
    `permission_group_member` row in `owners@<ws>`, and a
    `role_grant(scope_kind=workspace, grant_role=manager)`. Issues
    a signup-scoped session cookie (no `__Host-crewday_session` yet).
  - `POST /signup/passkey/start` + `/finish` reuse `p2.passkey.register`
    via the bootstrap path; on success the session is upgraded to a
    full `__Host-crewday_session`.
- Gate every endpoint with `app.config.capabilities.signup_enabled`
  — when false, return 404 (not 403) so the surface is invisible.
- Validate `desired_slug` against the §02 regex + the reserved-slug
  blocklist constant in `app.tenancy.slug` (do **not** duplicate the
  list — import the single source of truth).
- 409 codes: `slug_taken`, `slug_reserved`, `slug_in_grace_period`,
  `slug_homoglyph_collision` (homoglyph guard logic in
  `app.tenancy.slug.normalise_for_collision`).
- All inserts wrapped in one transaction; rollback on any failure.
- Audit on every step: `audit.signup.requested`, `…verified`,
  `…completed`, `…aborted`. `actor_kind = "system"` until the
  passkey ceremony succeeds.
- `signup_gc` background job (APScheduler) prunes 1-hour-old
  workspaces with no user.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All inserts in one transaction; partial failures roll back fully
- [ ] `signup_enabled = false` → 404 on every signup route
- [ ] Reserved + homoglyph + grace-period rejections covered by tests
- [ ] First user becomes a member of `owners@<ws>` and holds a
      `manager` role grant on the workspace scope
- [ ] Quotas seeded at 10 % of free-tier (verification_state=
      `email_verified`)
- [ ] `signup_gc` removes orphaned workspaces after 1h
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DB backends

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_signup.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_signup_full_flow.py -xvs
```

title: feat(auth/magic-link): request + single-use consume + nonce store
description:
## Problem / goal
Magic links are the second factor for self-service recovery and the
sole credential during signup's email-verification step. They must be
single-use, time-bounded, and rate-limited.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Magic link format",
  §"Self-serve signup" (step 1 → 2), §"Recovery paths".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Rate limiting and
  abuse controls".
- **Phase 0** dependency: `app.mail` transport (`p0.mail`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/recover/RecoverRequest.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/recover/RecoverConsume.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- Routes: `POST /auth/magic/request` and `POST /auth/magic/consume`
  (both bare-host, tenant-agnostic — `tenant_agnostic()` context).
- Token format per §03: `itsdangerous`-signed blob `{purpose,
  subject_id, jti, exp}` — purposes are `signup_verify`,
  `recover_passkey`, `email_change_confirm`.
- `magic_link_nonce` table (new) keyed by `jti`; insert on consume
  inside a `SERIALIZABLE`/`UPGRADE` lookup so a parallel consume
  loses the race and gets `409 already_consumed`.
- 10-minute window (`exp` capped server-side regardless of caller
  preference); expired tokens return 410.
- Rate limits: per-IP and per-email per §15 (lean on the rate-limit
  primitive from Phase 0; add the keys).
- Mail send uses `app.mail.send(to, template, ctx)` — never raw
  email bodies in code; templates live under `app/mail/templates/`.
- Audit: `audit.magic_link.sent`, `…consumed`, `…rejected` (no
  email or token in payload).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Token purpose enforced — a `signup_verify` token cannot be
      consumed by `/auth/magic/consume?purpose=recover_passkey`
- [ ] Single use guaranteed under concurrent consume
- [ ] 10-minute hard ceiling on `exp`
- [ ] Per-IP + per-email rate limits enforced
- [ ] Email never logged; only sha256(email) for correlation
- [ ] Unit + integration tests on both backends

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_magic_link.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_magic_link_concurrent.py -xvs
```
Manual: trigger recovery from `/recover`, confirm the dev-mail
log captures the URL, and replay it twice — second consume must 409.
close_reason: Magic-link request + single-use consume + nonce store landed. Rejected-audit uses fresh UoW pattern; quality gates green.

title: feat(auth/passkey): assertion challenge + verify (login) creates session
description:
## Problem / goal
Implement the passkey login ceremony. A successful assertion verifies
a `passkey_credential`, bumps `sign_count` + `last_used_at`, and
issues a session via `app.auth.session.issue`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Login",
  §"WebAuthn specifics" (UV=required, conditional UI), §"Sessions".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/login/Login.tsx`.
- Phase 1 table: `PasskeyCredential` (per-user lookup by credential id
  byte-string).

## Implementation notes
- Routes: `POST /api/v1/auth/passkey/login/start` (bare host;
  conditional-UI lookup is by credential id, not by username) and
  `.../login/finish`.
- `start` returns options with `userVerification: "required"` and an
  empty `allowCredentials` (discoverable credentials).
- `finish`: look up `passkey_credential` by raw credential id, verify
  assertion against stored public key, reject if `sign_count` regresses
  (clone-detection per §15 §"Passkey specifics"), bump counter +
  `last_used_at` in the same transaction, then call
  `session.issue(user_id, has_owner_grant=resolve_owner(user_id))`.
- Whether the user holds an owner grant on **any** workspace decides
  the longer lifetime (§03). Resolve via `permission_group_member`
  membership in any `owners` system group.
- Audit: `audit.passkey.assertion_ok` on success; `…rejected` with
  the failure reason on bad assertion (no PII in payload).
- Rate-limit failed attempts per credential id and per source IP
  (§15 §"Rate limiting and abuse controls").

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Successful assertion issues a session + cookie
- [ ] Sign-count regression triggers `audit.passkey.cloned_detected`
      and refuses login
- [ ] Failed attempts are throttled (per IP + per credential id)
- [ ] Audit rows written in the same tx as `passkey_credential` update
- [ ] No bare-host route requires `WorkspaceContext`
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DB backends

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_login.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_passkey_login_pg.py -xvs
```
Manual: Playwright with virtual authenticator drives `/login` and
verifies the cookie + redirect.

title: feat(auth/session): cookie lifecycle + sliding refresh + idle/absolute timeouts
description:
## Problem / goal
Issue, refresh, and revoke web sessions. The session cookie is the
substrate every authenticated request rides on; sliding refresh keeps
working users logged in, idle/absolute timeouts cap the blast radius.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Sessions", §"Login",
  §"Recovery paths" (logout-everywhere semantics).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Cookies",
  §"HTTP security headers".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/lib/session.ts` (placeholder).
- Phase 1 table: `Session` (id, user_id, workspace_id, expires_at,
  last_seen_at, ua_hash, ip_hash).

## Implementation notes
- Module `app/auth/session.py`:
  - `issue(user_id, *, workspace_id=None, has_owner_grant: bool)`
    creates the row + sets cookie. Lifetime: 7d when
    `has_owner_grant`, otherwise 30d (configurable via Settings).
  - `refresh(session_id)` extends `expires_at` only after half the
    lifetime has elapsed; updates `last_seen_at` on every hit.
  - `revoke(session_id)` and `revoke_all_for_user(user_id)`.
- Cookie name `__Host-crewday_session`; flags `Secure; HttpOnly;
  SameSite=Lax; Path=/`; **no** `Domain`. Value is a 192-bit random
  token; row stores its sha256.
- CSRF double-submit: emit `crewday_csrf` cookie + require `X-CSRF`
  header on every non-GET (middleware in `app/auth/csrf.py`).
- `ua_hash` = sha256(User-Agent), `ip_hash` = sha256(client_ip + salt)
  — never the raw values (§15 redaction).
- Audit: `audit.session.created`, `…revoked`, `…expired` via
  `app.audit.write_audit`.
- Logging: structured event on issue/refresh/revoke
  (`p0.logging` redaction layer).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Owner / non-owner lifetimes match spec (7d / 30d)
- [ ] Sliding refresh only fires past the half-life mark
- [ ] Cookie flags exactly per spec; rejected if `Domain` is set
- [ ] CSRF double-submit enforced for non-GET routes
- [ ] `revoke_all_for_user` invalidates concurrent sessions
- [ ] No raw IP / UA persisted; only hashes
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DB backends

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_session.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_session_csrf.py -xvs
```
Manual: `curl -c jar -b jar` round-trip through `/login` (test
fixture) and confirm cookie flags via `curl -v`.
close_reason: Delivered: app/auth/session.py, app/auth/csrf.py, tests/unit/auth/test_session.py, tests/integration/auth/test_session_csrf.py, app/config.py (Settings.session_owner_ttl_days, session_user_ttl_days). All AC met.

title: feat(auth/passkey): registration challenge + verify endpoints
description:
## Problem / goal
Implement the passkey registration ceremony so an authenticated user
(or a freshly verified signup session) can enrol a credential. Two
endpoints — challenge issue + assertion verify — produce a
`passkey_credential` row.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"WebAuthn specifics",
  §"Self-serve signup" step 3, §"Additional passkeys", §"Privacy"
  (whitelist of stored fields).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/onboarding/PasskeyEnroll.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/profile/Passkeys.tsx`.
- Phase 1 table: `app/adapters/db/identity/models.py::PasskeyCredential`.
- Depends on the RP module from `p2.webauthn-rp`.

## Implementation notes
- Routes mounted at `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/passkey/register/start`
  and `.../register/finish`. A bare-host equivalent under
  `/api/v1/auth/passkey/register/*` exists for the signup path
  (tenant-agnostic, scoped to the in-progress signup session).
- `start`: generates challenge via `app.auth.webauthn`, stashes
  `(user_id, challenge, exclude_credentials)` in a short-lived
  `webauthn_challenge` row (Redis-style row in the identity DB,
  10-min TTL).
- `finish`: verifies attestation, inserts `passkey_credential`
  (credential_id, public_key, sign_count, transports, AAGUID, label).
  Writes `audit.passkey.registered` in the same transaction
  (`app.audit.write_audit`).
- Per-user cap: 5 passkeys (§03). Returns 422
  `error = "too_many_passkeys"` on the 6th.
- Service signature `register_finish(ctx: WorkspaceContext, …)`
  for the in-workspace path; `register_finish_signup(signup_ctx, …)`
  uses `tenant_agnostic()` for the bootstrap path.
- No PII in audit beyond the user id; AAGUID and label are fine.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `start` returns options matching `PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptions`
- [ ] `finish` rejects mismatched challenge / origin / rp_id with 400
- [ ] Successful finish writes one `passkey_credential` row + one
      audit row in the same transaction
- [ ] 6th passkey for a user returns 422 `too_many_passkeys`
- [ ] Replay of the same `finish` request returns 409
- [ ] Unit + integration tests on SQLite and Postgres

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_register.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_passkey_register_pg.py -xvs
```
Manual: in the dev container, drive `/onboarding/passkey` with
Playwright virtual authenticator and confirm a row lands.
close_reason: Registration challenge + verify endpoints landed; all acceptance criteria met.

title: feat(auth/webauthn): relying-party config (rp_id, origin, attestation policy)
description:
## Problem / goal
Centralise the WebAuthn relying-party configuration so every passkey
ceremony (registration + assertion) reads the same `rp_id`, expected
origin, user-verification policy, and algorithm list. This is the
foundation every other passkey task depends on.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"WebAuthn specifics",
  §"Login", §"Privacy" (what we are allowed to store).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Passkey specifics".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/login/` (UI affordance only — no
  real ceremony today).
- **DB tables already present** (Phase 1): `passkey_credential` rows
  carry `public_key`, `sign_count`, transports, AAGUID, last_used_at.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/auth/webauthn.py` exposing:
  - `RelyingParty(rp_id, rp_name, origin, allowed_origins)` dataclass
    derived from `app.config.Settings` (`crewday_public_origin`,
    `crewday_rp_id`).
  - `policy()` returning a frozen `WebAuthnPolicy` (UV=`required`,
    attestation=`none`, attachment=`platform` preferred + cross-platform
    allowed, resident_keys=`preferred`, algs `[ES256, RS256]`).
  - Thin wrappers around the `webauthn` package's `generate_*` /
    `verify_*` helpers so callers never reach the lib directly.
- Validate `rp_id` against `origin` at startup (registers a check via
  `app.config.capabilities`); refuse to boot if mismatched.
- Tenant-agnostic: the RP is process-wide, not per-workspace.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `RelyingParty` populated from settings; startup fails fast on
      mismatched origin / rp_id
- [ ] Policy values match §03 `WebAuthn specifics` exactly
- [ ] No call site imports `webauthn` directly — only via this module
- [ ] Unit tests cover policy frozenness + origin/rp_id validation
- [ ] `mypy --strict` clean

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_webauthn_rp.py -xvs
uv run mypy app/auth/webauthn.py
```
Manual: boot the dev container with a deliberately mismatched
`CREWDAY_RP_ID` and confirm the bind-guard refuses to start.

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: Autofix complete. Findings fixed: (1) origin not normalised — uppercase host, path/query/fragment, userinfo would each produce a runtime mismatch with clientDataJSON.origin (py_webauthn does byte-exact comparison); added _canonical_origin() that parses + rebuilds scheme://host[:port] with lowercasing and validates scheme in {http,https} + non-empty host + valid port. (2) rp_id override compared case-sensitively even though DNS is case-insensitive; now casefold-ed on both sides and stored lowercased. (3) Dev fallback scheme heuristic flipped https:// for non-loopback bindings which would mismatch plaintext dev; simplified to unconditional http://. Added 10 new tests covering each edge case (30 total, all pass). 749 unit+integration tests pass total (was 739), mypy strict, ruff + format, lint-imports kept. cd-65n7 capability probe remains deferred as noted.

(RRULE) + generated occurrences + per-occurrence completion + evidence +
comments.

## Technical context

## Implementation notes

  - \`TaskTemplate\`: id, workspace_id, title, description_md,
    default_duration_min, required_evidence (enum), photo_required
    (bool), default_assignee_role (nullable), checklist_template_json.
  - \`ChecklistTemplateItem\`: id, template_id, label, position,
    requires_photo.
  - \`Schedule\`: id, workspace_id, template_id, property_id (nullable),
    rrule_text, dtstart, until, assignee_user_id (nullable),
    assignee_role (nullable), enabled, next_generation_at.
  - \`Occurrence\`: id, workspace_id, schedule_id (nullable; null for
    one-off), template_id, property_id, assignee_user_id, starts_at,
    ends_at, state (\`pending|in_progress|done|skipped|approved\`),
    completed_at, completed_by, reviewer_id, reviewed_at.
  - \`ChecklistItem\`: id, occurrence_id, label, position,
    requires_photo, checked (bool), checked_at, evidence_blob_hash.
  - \`Evidence\`: id, occurrence_id, kind (\`photo|note|voice|gps\`),

      assignee_user_id, starts_at)\`, \`occurrence(workspace_id, state,

title: feat(db/stays): reservation + ical_feed + stay_bundle
description:
## Problem / goal
Stays are the bridge between external calendars and internal task
generation. Reservations drive turnover tasks.

## Technical context

title: feat(db/places): property + unit + area + property_workspace + closures
description:
## Problem / goal
Places tables. \`property_workspace\` implements the multi-belonging
invariant (same villa, multiple workspaces).

    created_at.

    created_at. **The workspace_id filter attaches here.**
  - \`Unit\`: id, property_id, label, type, capacity.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A property can have multiple \`property_workspace\` rows

title: feat(db/identity): user / passkey_credential / session / api_token
description:
## Problem / goal
Identity tables for authentication: users, their passkey credentials,
web sessions, and long-lived API tokens.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"user",
  §"passkey_credential", §"session", §"api_token",
  \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` §"Data model".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/identity/models.py\` (extend Phase 1 workspace file):
  - \`User\`: id, email (unique), display_name, locale, timezone,
    avatar_blob_hash, created_at, last_login_at.
  - \`PasskeyCredential\`: id (credential_id bytes), user_id,
    public_key, sign_count, transports, backup_eligible, created_at,
    last_used_at, label.
  - \`Session\`: id, user_id, workspace_id (nullable — users pick a
    workspace post-login), expires_at, last_seen_at, ua_hash, ip_hash.
  - \`ApiToken\`: id, user_id, workspace_id, label, scope_json, prefix
    (first 8 chars of hashed secret, for listing), hash (sha256),
    expires_at, last_used_at, revoked_at.
- Alembic migration \`0004_identity\`.
- Register in tenancy registry: \`session\` / \`api_token\` /
  \`passkey_credential\` are user-scoped but live in the identity
  context — tag them with their workspace via \`user_workspace\` at
  query time; the filter treats them as **user-scoped** (skip
  registration) and the identity module owns its own access rules.
  \`user\` itself is tenant-agnostic (one row globally, many workspace
  memberships).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Tables present with indexes on lookup columns
- [ ] Email unique + case-insensitive
- [ ] \`PasskeyCredential.public_key\` stored as bytes, not text
- [ ] Migration reversible
- [ ] Unit tests cover CRUD + uniqueness

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_identity.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(security): public-interface bind guard
description:
## Problem / goal
Server MUST refuse to bind to a public interface unless explicitly
allowed. Default bind is \`127.0.0.1:8000\`; tailscale* interfaces pass;
\`0.0.0.0\` / public IPs require \`CREWDAY_ALLOW_PUBLIC_BIND=1\`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Runtime invariants" #6,
  \`docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md\` §"Bind guard",
  \`docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md\` §"Bind model".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/security/bind_guard.py\`: \`assert_bind_allowed(host, port,
  trusted_globs, allow_public)\` reads
  \`/proc/net/dev\` + \`netifaces\` to enumerate interfaces, matches
  host IP to an interface, checks the interface name against
  \`trusted_globs\`. Loopback always passes. \`0.0.0.0\` / \`::\`
  require \`allow_public\`.
- Called from \`create_app()\` before Uvicorn binds; raises
  \`BindGuardError\` with a clear message.
- CLI: \`crewday serve\` exposes the same guard.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Binding to \`127.0.0.1\` always passes
- [ ] Binding to a tailscale IP passes (integration test mocks
      interfaces)
- [ ] Binding to \`0.0.0.0\` fails unless \`allow_public\` is True
- [ ] Binding to a public IP without \`allow_public\` fails
- [ ] Clear error message: "refusing to bind to public interface eth0
      (IP ...). Set CREWDAY_ALLOW_PUBLIC_BIND=1 to override."
- [ ] Unit tests cover every branch

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_bind_guard.py -xvs
\`\`\`
close_reason: Bind guard delivered: loopback short-circuit, tailscale* glob match, 0.0.0.0/:: + hostname require CREWDAY_ALLOW_PUBLIC_BIND=1. Unit + main integration tests cover every branch including non-Linux refusal and SIOCGIFCONF ceiling truncation.

title: chore(boundaries): import-linter config + CI gate
description:
## Problem / goal
Boundary rules in §01 must be enforced mechanically — no good-will
allowed. \`import-linter\` (or equivalent) runs in CI on every PR.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Enforcement",
  \`docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md\` §"Import-linter contracts".

## Implementation notes
- \`pyproject.toml\` \`[tool.importlinter]\` contracts:
  1. \`domain\` forbids \`app.adapters\` (except \`ports\`).
  2. \`domain\` forbids \`app.api\`, \`app.web\`, \`app.cli\`.
  3. \`domain.<X>\` forbids \`domain.<Y>.<submodule>\` — only the
     \`__init__\` surface.
  4. \`adapters\` forbids \`app.api\`, \`app.web\`, \`app.cli\`.
  5. Allowed cross-cutting: \`app.util\`, \`app.audit\`,
     \`app.tenancy\`, \`app.events\`, \`app.adapters.*.ports\`.
- CI job \`boundaries\` (part of \`ci.yml\`) runs \`uv run
  lint-imports\` and fails on any violation.
- Add a deliberate failing test: \`tests/boundary/bad_import.py\`
  imports \`app.adapters.db.session\` from \`app.domain.identity\` —
  assert that \`lint-imports\` rejects it.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`uv run lint-imports\` passes on the empty context tree
- [ ] Deliberate cross-boundary import fails \`lint-imports\`
- [ ] CI job wired and red on a broken branch

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run lint-imports
uv run pytest tests/boundary/test_import_gate.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(app): FastAPI factory wiring middleware + routers + worker
description:
## Problem / goal
Central \`create_app()\` composes the FastAPI app: CORS, security
headers, bind-guard, tenancy middleware, SSE handler, SPA catch-all.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"High-level picture",
  §"Repo layout", §"web.*".
- Worker (APScheduler) starts in-process by default; external when
  \`settings.worker = external\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/main.py\`:
  - \`create_app()\` returns \`FastAPI\`.
  - Wires middleware in order: logging/correlation, security headers,
    session, bind-guard, tenancy.
  - Mounts API routers (empty for now; context routers land later).
  - Mounts SSE endpoint at \`/events\` (placeholder).
  - Mounts SPA catch-all serving \`mocks/web/dist\` (temporary; the
    web-foundation task later repoints to \`app/web/dist\`).
  - \`/healthz\`, \`/readyz\`, \`/version\` endpoints.
- Uvicorn entrypoint: \`uv run uvicorn app.main:create_app --factory
  --host \${CREWDAY_BIND_HOST:-127.0.0.1} --port
  \${CREWDAY_BIND_PORT:-8000}\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`GET /healthz\` returns 200
- [ ] \`GET /readyz\` returns 200 when DB up, 503 when down
- [ ] \`GET /version\` returns \`{"version": "..."}\` with the pyproject
      version
- [ ] Visiting \`/\` returns the SPA \`index.html\`
- [ ] Unit tests via \`TestClient\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_main.py -xvs
uv run uvicorn app.main:create_app --factory --port 8000 &
curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8000/healthz
kill %1
\`\`\`
close_reason: FastAPI factory + middleware + routers + worker wired; selfreview (cd-d25) complete

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text:
Autofix complete. Findings:
- BUG: _probe_features used 'postgres or _sqlite_has_fts5()' which returned True on unsupported backends (mysql/oracle) whenever the interpreter's SQLite had FTS5. Fixed by gating on an explicit _is_sqlite() check; added mysql/oracle regression tests.
- RISKY: refresh_settings mutated fields as it coerced them, so a bad JSON payload on any later field left earlier fields half-applied. Fixed by staging every coercion into locals and only writing back to self.settings after all four succeed; added integration test with a non-numeric budget payload.

cd-3yhd flake investigation: narrowed root cause to _collect_scoped_targets mis-walking the _ORMJoin's left side on cold cache paths (only one of the two scoped tables is collected). Any side-effect inside the walker (print, sys.stderr.write) warms the state and the second table gets collected. Tried 5 non-trivial fixes (list(), and_() single-where, session-scoped engine, dispose teardown, getattr touches) — none help. Left cd-3yhd OPEN with detailed analysis + suggested next-step fixes (replace get_final_froms with a manual visitor traversal). The flake still reproduces on the full suite at ~20% rate; isolated tenancy tests pass consistently.

title: feat(capabilities): env probes + deployment_setting + frozen registry
description:
## Problem / goal
Features consult a **capability registry** — they never sniff the DB
URL, check env vars, or branch on "SaaS vs self-host". Registry
combines immutable env probes (RLS, FTS, storage, LLM caps) with
mutable operator settings from \`deployment_setting\`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Capability registry".
- **Settings** (initial): \`settings.signup_enabled\` (default true),
  \`settings.signup_throttle_overrides\`,
  \`settings.require_passkey_attestation\`,
  \`settings.llm_default_budget_cents_30d\`.
- **Probes** (initial): \`features.rls\`, \`features.fulltext_search\`,
  \`features.concurrent_writers\`, \`features.object_storage\`,
  \`features.wildcard_subdomains\`,
  \`features.email_bounce_webhooks\`, \`features.llm_voice_input\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/capabilities/models.py\`: \`DeploymentSetting\`
  table — key (PK string), value (JSON), updated_at, updated_by.
  **Not** workspace-scoped — deployment-wide.
- Alembic migration \`0002_deployment_setting\`.
- \`app/capabilities.py\`: frozen dataclass \`Capabilities\` populated
  once at boot by \`probe(settings, db)\`. Exposes \`features\` and
  \`settings\` namespaces; \`refresh_settings()\` re-reads the DB for
  the mutable subset (called by admin settings endpoints).
- Boot log: print the capability snapshot at INFO level (redaction
  filter keeps it safe).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`capabilities.features.rls\` is True on Postgres, False on SQLite
- [ ] \`capabilities.settings.signup_enabled\` reflects
      \`deployment_setting\` rows
- [ ] \`refresh_settings()\` picks up a runtime change
- [ ] Boot log shows the snapshot once
- [ ] Unit tests cover every probe + refresh

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_capabilities.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(audit): append-only audit_log writer + migration
description:
## Problem / goal
Every mutation writes one row to \`audit_log\` in the **same
transaction**. Reads are never audited. Writer is a tiny append-only
API, called by every domain service on the write path.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"audit_log",
  \`docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md\` §"Audit log",
  \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Runtime invariants" #3.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/audit/models.py\`: \`AuditLog\` table — id (ULID),
  workspace_id, actor_id, actor_kind, actor_grant_role,
  actor_was_owner_member, entity_kind, entity_id, action, diff JSON,
  correlation_id, created_at. No FKs for speed; soft refs only.
- \`app/audit/__init__.py\`: \`write_audit(ctx, entity_kind,
  entity_id, action, diff)\` function taking a
  \`WorkspaceContext\` and a JSON-serialisable \`diff\`.
- Alembic migration: \`0001_audit_log\` — creates \`audit_log\` with
  indexes on \`(workspace_id, created_at desc)\` and
  \`(workspace_id, entity_kind, entity_id)\`.
- Register \`audit_log\` in the workspace-scoped table registry.
- Domain adapters already open a UoW; the writer must use the **same**
  session so audit lands in the same transaction.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Table + indexes present after \`alembic upgrade head\`
- [ ] \`write_audit\` inside a UoW commits together with the mutation
- [ ] Rollback of the UoW rolls back the audit row too
- [ ] Attempting \`write_audit\` without a \`WorkspaceContext\` raises
      \`TenantFilterMissing\`
- [ ] Unit tests cover success + rollback + context-missing cases

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_audit_writer.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(tenancy): SQLAlchemy before_compile filter + tenant_agnostic escape
description:
## Problem / goal
Auto-inject \`AND workspace_id = :current_workspace_id\` on every query
touching a workspace-scoped table. Developers never thread the filter
by hand. Queries without a context **and** outside
\`tenant_agnostic()\` raise \`TenantFilterMissing\`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Tenant filter
  enforcement".
- **Sibling spec**: \`docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md\` §"Tenant
  isolation".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/tenancy/orm_filter.py\`: register a SQLAlchemy
  \`do_orm_execute\` / \`before_compile\` event on \`Session\` that:
  1. Walks the compiled \`Select\` / \`Update\` / \`Delete\` targets.
  2. If any target table is in
     \`tenancy.registry.WORKSPACE_SCOPED_TABLES\`:
     - If \`tenant_agnostic\` is set, skip.
     - Else, if \`get_current()\` is None, raise
       \`TenantFilterMissing(table=...)\`.
     - Else, inject \`AND workspace_id = :ctx_workspace_id\`.
- Wire the event hook in the engine factory (Phase 0 db task).
- Grep CI: a \`scripts/check_tenant_agnostic.py\` greps for
  \`tenant_agnostic(\` calls lacking a \`# justification:\` comment
  within 3 lines; fails the build. Plumb it into the \`lint\` CI job.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Query against a scoped table without a context raises
      \`TenantFilterMissing\`
- [ ] Same query inside a \`WorkspaceContext\` compiles with the
      workspace_id filter in the SQL
- [ ] Same query inside a \`tenant_agnostic()\` block compiles without
      the filter
- [ ] The grep script fails on an unjustified \`tenant_agnostic()\`
      call
- [ ] Unit tests cover every path, on both SQLite and Postgres

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tenancy_orm_filter.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_tenancy_e2e.py -xvs
uv run python scripts/check_tenant_agnostic.py
\`\`\`

title: feat(tenancy): WorkspaceContext + slug resolver + scoped registry
description:
## Problem / goal
Every mutation flows through a \`WorkspaceContext\` carrying
\`workspace_id\`, \`actor_id\`, \`actor_kind\`, \`actor_grant_role\`,
\`actor_was_owner_member\`, and \`audit_correlation_id\`. Ships the
primitive + URL slug resolver + a registry of workspace-scoped table
names.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Multi-tenancy runtime",
  §"Workspace addressing", §"WorkspaceContext", §"Tenant filter
  enforcement".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/tenancy/context.py\`: \`@dataclass(slots=True) class
  WorkspaceContext\` matching the Protocol in §01.
- \`app/tenancy/slug.py\`: slug regex
  \`^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{1,38}[a-z0-9]$\`, reserved-word blocklist as
  listed in §01.
- \`app/tenancy/registry.py\`: \`WORKSPACE_SCOPED_TABLES: set[str]\`
  with helpers \`is_scoped(table_name)\` and \`register(table_name)\`.
  Migration hooks call \`register\` (Phase 1 tasks wire this).
- \`app/tenancy/current.py\`: \`contextvars.ContextVar[WorkspaceContext |
  None]\` plus \`get_current()\` and \`set_current()\`. Also
  \`tenant_agnostic()\` context manager flipping a separate
  \`ContextVar[bool]\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Slug validator accepts \`villa-sud\`, rejects \`w\`, \`API\`,
      \`_foo\`, \`villa--sud\`
- [ ] \`get_current()\` returns the last \`set_current\` value, scoped
      per task
- [ ] \`tenant_agnostic()\` nesting restores the outer flag
- [ ] Unit tests cover every branch

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tenancy_slug.py tests/unit/test_tenancy_ctx.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(adapters/db): SQLAlchemy 2.0 base + UnitOfWork + Alembic bootstrap
description:
## Problem / goal
Wire the DB adapter: SQLAlchemy 2.0 \`DeclarativeBase\`, session maker,
Unit-of-Work context manager, and Alembic timeline bootstrapped (env.py
gathers per-context models). SQLite and Postgres dialects both work.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Adapters", §"Migrations
  stay shared", \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"Conventions".
- Backend selection is by \`settings.database_url\`; the same code path
  runs on both.
- Capability registry (separate task) sets \`features.rls\` for
  Postgres.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/base.py\`: \`Base = DeclarativeBase\`, shared
  metadata with a naming convention for constraints (required for
  Alembic autogenerate on SQLite).
- \`app/adapters/db/session.py\`: engine factory (connect args per
  dialect — \`check_same_thread=False\` for SQLite, pool defaults for
  PG), \`SessionLocal\` maker, \`UnitOfWorkImpl\` implementing the
  port.
- \`migrations/\` via \`alembic init\`, \`alembic.ini\` pointing at
  \`app.config.settings.database_url\`. \`env.py\` imports every
  per-context \`models\` module (initially empty, will expand as
  contexts land) and passes their metadata to \`context.configure\`.
- No tables yet — Phase 1 tasks add them.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`alembic upgrade head\` is a no-op on an empty DB
- [ ] \`alembic revision --autogenerate -m test\` produces an empty
      diff
- [ ] UoW rolls back on exception, commits on clean exit
- [ ] Works on both \`sqlite:///:memory:\` and a live PG container

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_uow.py -xvs
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run alembic revision --autogenerate -m test && rm migrations/versions/*test*
\`\`\`

title: feat(adapters): define DbSession / Storage / Mailer / LLMClient / Clock ports
description:
## Problem / goal
Adapter Protocols pin the domain\u2194side-effect seam. Domain code depends
on these Protocols; SQLAlchemy / S3 / SMTP / OpenRouter implementations
live behind them.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Adapters",
  §"Shared kernel".
- These Protocols are the **only** adapter-layer modules a domain
  context may import (the import-linter rule).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/ports.py\`:
  - \`DbSession\` Protocol with \`execute\`, \`flush\`, \`commit\`,
    \`rollback\`, \`add\`, \`get\`, \`scalar\`, \`scalars\` methods
    typed against SQLAlchemy 2.0 \`Session\` signatures.
  - \`UnitOfWork\` Protocol with \`__enter__\`/\`__exit__\` producing a
    \`DbSession\`.
- \`app/adapters/storage/ports.py\`:
  - \`Storage\` Protocol: \`put(hash, stream) -> Blob\`,
    \`get(hash) -> Stream\`, \`exists(hash) -> bool\`,
    \`sign_url(hash, ttl) -> str\`.
- \`app/adapters/mail/ports.py\`:
  - \`Mailer\` Protocol: \`send(to, subject, body, html, headers)\`.
- \`app/adapters/llm/ports.py\`:
  - \`LLMClient\` Protocol: \`complete\`, \`chat\`, \`ocr\`, streaming
    variants; each accepts a \`model_id\` and returns usage stats.
- \`app/util/clock.py\` already has \`Clock\` (see the util task) —
  re-export from \`app/adapters/clock/ports.py\` for uniformity.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every port is a \`Protocol\` (not ABC), \`runtime_checkable\`
      disabled by default
- [ ] \`lint-imports\` (when wired up) allows \`domain.*\` to import
      \`app.adapters.*.ports\` but nothing else under \`adapters\`
- [ ] Unit tests use dummy implementations of each port to prove the
      contract is satisfiable without external services

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_adapter_ports.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(util): clock / ULID / hashids / money helpers
description:
## Problem / goal
Shared kernel helpers that every context depends on. Pins the abstractions
so domain code never reaches for \`datetime.now()\` or raw \`secrets\`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Shared kernel",
  §"Runtime invariants" #2 (domain never touches \`datetime.now()\`).
- Money handling: spec §09 pins \`decimal.Decimal\` with minor-unit
  integers at the DB layer.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/util/clock.py\`: \`Clock\` Protocol + \`SystemClock\` impl +
  \`FrozenClock\` test double. Exposes \`now()\` returning aware UTC
  \`datetime\`.
- \`app/util/ulid.py\`: \`new_ulid() -> str\`, deterministic in tests
  via \`ulid.new(timestamp=clock.now())\`.
- \`app/util/hashids.py\`: short-lived stay / guest token encoder.
- \`app/util/money.py\`: \`Money\` value object (amount minor units +
  ISO-4217 currency), addition, formatting per locale.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`Clock\` protocol + \`SystemClock\` + \`FrozenClock\` (tests can
      freeze time)
- [ ] \`new_ulid()\` is monotonic and URL-safe
- [ ] \`Money(100, "EUR") + Money(200, "EUR") == Money(300, "EUR")\`
- [ ] Cross-currency math raises \`CurrencyMismatchError\`
- [ ] Unit tests cover every helper

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_util_clock.py tests/unit/test_util_ulid.py \
              tests/unit/test_util_money.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(config): pydantic-settings config module
description:
## Problem / goal
Central config loader for every adapter, worker, and API router. Pulls
from env (\`CREWDAY_*\`) and a \`.env\` file; enforces types via pydantic.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md\` §"Environment
  variables", \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Runtime invariants".
- All env vars prefixed \`CREWDAY_\` — DB URL, data dir, bind address,
  trusted interfaces, public URL, SMTP, OpenRouter key, etc.
- \`CREWDAY_DATA_DIR\` defaults to \`./data\`. Never write outside it.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/config.py\` exposes \`settings: Settings\` (a pydantic
  \`BaseSettings\` singleton). Fields include:
  \`database_url\`, \`data_dir\`, \`bind_host\`, \`bind_port\`,
  \`trusted_interfaces\` (list of glob patterns, default
  \`['tailscale*']\`), \`allow_public_bind\` (bool),
  \`public_url\`, \`smtp_host\`, \`smtp_port\`, \`smtp_user\`,
  \`smtp_password\`, \`openrouter_api_key\`, \`demo_mode\`,
  \`worker\` (\`internal | external\`), \`storage_backend\`
  (\`localfs | s3\`).
- Use \`SecretStr\` for every credential field.
- \`.env.example\` committed at repo root listing every var with an
  empty/default value and a one-line comment.
- Never log \`Settings()\` directly — add a \`safe_dump()\` helper
  returning a dict with secrets masked.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Missing required env var raises a clear \`ValidationError\`
- [ ] \`settings.safe_dump()\` hides every \`SecretStr\`

\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_config.py -xvs
CREWDAY_DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///:memory: \
  uv run python -c "from app.config import settings; print(settings.safe_dump())"
\`\`\`

title: chore(ci): GitHub Actions \u2014 lint, type, test, import-linter
description:
## Problem / goal
Every PR must be blocked from merging until lint, type, unit tests, and
the import-linter boundary gate pass. Wire this up now so downstream
tasks cannot regress it.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md\` §"CI gates",
  \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Enforcement".
- Matrix: Python 3.12 + 3.13; SQLite + Postgres 15 (via
  \`services: postgres\`).
- Cache \`~/.cache/uv\` keyed on \`uv.lock\`.

## Implementation notes
- Create \`.github/workflows/ci.yml\` with jobs:
  - \`lint\`  — \`uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check .\`
  - \`type\`  — \`uv run mypy app cli\`
  - \`boundaries\` — \`uv run lint-imports\` (config from the
    import-linter task; can start as a placeholder that always passes
    and be made strict by the import-linter task)
  - \`unit\`  — \`uv run pytest tests/unit\` on SQLite
  - \`integration\` — \`uv run pytest tests/integration\` on both
    SQLite and Postgres via service container
- Use \`actions/setup-python@v5\`, \`astral-sh/setup-uv@v3\`.
- Fail the workflow on any red job.
- Gate merges in branch protection (repo admin action; call it out in
  the task comment).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] PR against main runs all five jobs and they all pass on a clean
      tree
- [ ] A deliberately broken \`app/util/_bad.py\` makes \`lint\` fail
- [ ] A type violation makes \`type\` fail
- [ ] The workflow uses the matrix and completes in under 10 min on
      the empty tree

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
# Run locally via act or just push a PR and watch the checks.
act -j lint
act -j type
act -j unit
\`\`\`

title: Self-review: fix(auth) commit-time leak magic-link tokens (cd-9slq)
description: Selfreview on cd-9slq. Run /selfreview autofix on the working-tree implementation: confirm SMTP send is lifted across UoW commit for all 5 callers (signup, recovery, users.reissue, membership invite, email_change request/revert), each has commit-failure unit test mirroring TestRequestLinkOutboxOrdering, and the implementer-flagged 'cd-9i7z follow-up' comments are removed. Quality gates: pytest tests/unit/auth tests/api/v1/test_users.py tests/domain/identity, mypy --strict, ruff.

title: fix(auth): commit-time failure leaks magic-link tokens on signup/recovery/email_change/membership/users-reissue (cd-9i7z analogs)
description:
## Problem

cd-9i7z closed the cd-t2jz fail-open on the bare-host magic-link HTTP router (`/auth/magic/request`): the SMTP send now fires *after* the caller's UoW commits, so a commit-time failure (e.g. schema drift on `audit_log`) leaves the user inbox untouched.

The same fail-open pattern persists on every **non-router** caller of `magic_link.request_link`. Each one calls `pending.deliver()` *inside* the FastAPI handler, which means the actual UoW commit (driven by the `db_session` dependency at handler exit) happens AFTER the SMTP send.

## Affected call sites

* `app/auth/signup.py` — `pending.deliver()` at line 754 fires before the signup-attempt commit.
* `app/auth/recovery.py` — `_mint_and_send_recovery_link` at line 902–942: `_CapturingMailer` fires under `pending.deliver()`; `_send_recovery_link` (real SMTP) fires before the recovery audit row commits.
* `app/api/v1/users.py` — `reissue_magic_link` at line 491–506: same shape as recovery; `_send_recovery_link_email` is the real SMTP touch.
* `app/domain/identity/membership.py` — invite flow at line 692–724: `pending.deliver()` is a no-op (`send_email=False`) but `_send_invite_email` is the real SMTP touch and runs before the invite-row commit.
* `app/domain/identity/email_change.py` — both `request_change` (line 561–581) and `request_revert` (line 802–829): real SMTP via `_send_notice_to_old` / `_send_revert_link_to_old` runs before the `EmailChangePending` row commits.

## Concrete repro shape

Same as cd-t2jz: induce a commit-time failure (schema drift, FK constraint, transient driver error) and observe that:

1. The user receives a working magic-link token in their inbox.
2. The corresponding nonce row + audit row are rolled back.
3. The token cannot be consumed (409 `already_consumed`, because the conditional UPDATE matches zero rows).

The exposure is partially bounded by upstream gates:

* **Signup**: behind disposable-domain + CAPTCHA + per-IP throttle. Bounded.
* **Recovery / users.reissue**: §15 enumeration guard already mandates 202-on-miss; the leak surface is the actual hit branch.
* **Email change**: behind a logged-in passkey. Lower abuse surface.
* **Membership invite**: manager-gated.

But the §15 invariant ("no working token without a matching nonce") applies uniformly. The cd-9i7z fix lifted the deferred-send across one router; this task lifts it across the rest.

## Fix direction

For each caller, lift the SMTP send across the FastAPI handler's commit boundary. Two options:

1. **Caller-managed UoW** (the cd-9i7z router pattern): replace the `db_session` FastAPI dep with an explicit `with make_uow() as session:` block, then call `pending.deliver()` (and any sibling `_send_*` template) *after* the `with` block exits.
2. **Outbox table**: persist the deferred send as a row in a new `mail_outbox` table inside the same UoW, then have a worker (or a post-commit hook) drain it. Cleaner long-term, larger surface.

Option 1 mirrors the cd-9i7z fix and keeps the diff scoped per caller. Option 2 is a separate ADR.

## Acceptance

* Each of the 5 callers has a unit + integration test pair locking "commit failure → no email leaves the host" (mirror `tests/unit/auth/test_magic_link.py::TestRequestLinkOutboxOrdering::test_commit_failure_before_deliver_does_not_send_email`).
* The implementer-flagged comments in `app/auth/signup.py`, `app/auth/recovery.py`, `app/api/v1/users.py`, `app/domain/identity/membership.py`, `app/domain/identity/email_change.py` get removed when the analog is closed.

## Provenance

Surfaced by /selfreview on cd-9i7z (cd-aavd). The implementer flagged each site with a `cd-9i7z follow-up` comment in the same patch.

title: Sniff Content-Type server-side on task evidence uploads (§15)
description:
**Context.** Spec §15 says "MIME sniffed server-side; we trust the sniff, not the header". The cd-jl0g `add_file_evidence` seam currently trusts the multipart-form-part `Content-Type` header — an attacker can upload an executable as `image/png` and it lands in the blob store under that declared type.

**Scope.** Add a sniffer port (e.g. python-magic or filetype) consumed by `app.domain.tasks.completion.add_file_evidence` (and any other route that takes a binary upload — me_avatar already has its own narrower magic-bytes check). The sniffed type MUST match one of the per-kind allow-list entries (§06 "Evidence", §15 "Task evidence — per-kind caps and allow-lists"); a mismatch is `415 evidence_content_type_rejected` with the sniffed type in the envelope.

**Acceptance**
- Upload of `evil.exe` declared as `image/png` → 415, scanner sees `application/x-dosexec`.
- gps payload that is JSON-shaped but mis-declared → 415.
- Existing happy-path tests still pass (TINY_PNG / TINY_WAV / GPS payload).
- spec §15 stopgap note removed.

**Notes.** The dependency choice (libmagic via python-magic vs pure-Python `filetype`) is a vendoring decision — `filetype` is small, no system dep, fewer edge cases supported; `python-magic` covers more types but needs libmagic. Pick when implementing.

title: fix(auth/magic): commit-time failure leaks working token to mailbox (audit-write order)
description:
## Problem
In `request_magic_link` the SMTP send happens before the unit-of-work commit that includes the audit_log row. If commit fails for a non-mailer reason (e.g. schema drift on audit_log), the user receives a working magic-link token in their inbox while the system has no record of it (the nonce row gets rolled back too).

## Concrete repro from cd-t2jz
1. Dev-stack container running with stale schema (missing audit_log.scope_kind column).
2. POST /api/v1/auth/magic/request → 202 Accepted.
3. Email arrives in mailpit with a real token.
4. POST /api/v1/auth/magic/consume with that token → 409 already_consumed (because UPDATE matched zero rows — the nonce was rolled back).

The user sees an email with a token they can never consume — fail-open behaviour, not the §15 enumeration-guard intent.

## Fix direction
Either:
- Re-order: write audit row → commit → THEN dispatch SMTP. (Standard outbox pattern; SMTP failure becomes its own concern.)
- Move audit-write to a separate UoW like the 'rejected' branch already does, so commit-time failures fail-closed for the success path.

## Acceptance
- A simulated commit failure (e.g. schema drift) does NOT result in a working token reaching the user inbox.
- Test added to lock the invariant.

## Notes
Surfaced by cd-t2jz selfreview during dev-stack drift investigation.

title: fix(util/redact): hash-shaped literal survives redaction in property fuzz
description:
## Problem
`tests/property/test_redact_fuzz.py::test_redactor_hides_every_injected_literal`
fails on a clean tree. Hypothesis falsifies with a literal that looks
like a 64-char hex digest under the key `hashed`:

```
literal 'deadbeefcafebabedeadbeefcafebabedeadbeefcafebabedeadbeefcafebabe'
survived redaction; input [{'hashed': '<literal>'}], output [{'hashed': '<literal>'}]
```

The redactor's heuristic appears to whitelist hash-shaped strings
(legitimate when the column name is `hashed` / `_hash`), but the
property fuzzer expects every injected literal to be redacted
unconditionally regardless of key.

## Repro
```
uv run pytest tests/property/test_redact_fuzz.py -q
```

## Acceptance
- Either the redactor stops exempting hash-shaped values under
  `hashed` / `*_hash` keys, OR the property test learns the exemption
  contract and excludes those keys from the literal-injection set.
- The fuzz test passes deterministically (Hypothesis seed-stable).

## Notes
Surfaced by selfreview cd-e7hx as a pre-existing failure; out of scope
for that task. The redactor sits in front of the LLM client per §15;
either side of the fix needs an explicit choice and a paper-trail
comment in `app/util/redact.py`.

notes: Same root as the already-closed cd-5l5f (closed today, 2026-04-25). The fix added 47 covered methods; selfreview cd-e7hx confirms the test regressed — likely a new repository method has landed since cd-5l5f closed. title: fix(domain): import-gate violations — domain importing adapters / api
description:
## Problem
`tests/boundary/test_import_gate.py::test_clean_tree_passes` fails on a
clean tree: the lint-imports contract walk reports two broken contracts.

### "Domain forbids adapters (except ports)"

* app.domain.expenses.claims -> app.adapters.db.identity.models
* app.domain.identity.email_change -> app.adapters.db.identity.models
* app.domain.identity.email_change -> app.auth.magic_link (-> ...s.models)
* app.domain.identity.user_availability_overrides -> app.adapters.db.availability.models
* app.domain.identity.user_availability_overrides -> app.authz (-> app.authz.owners -> app.adapters.db.authz.models)
* app.domain.identity.user_leaves -> app.adapters.db.availability.models
* app.domain.identity.user_leaves -> app.authz (-> app.authz.deployment_admin -> app.adapters.db.authz.models)
* app.domain.places.property_work_role_assignments -> app.adapters.db.places.models
* app.domain.places.property_work_role_assignments -> app.adapters.db.workspace.models

### "Domain forbids handlers (api/web/cli/worker)"

* app.domain.identity.user_leaves -> app.authz (-> app.authz.enforce -> app.api.deps)

## Repro
```
uv run pytest tests/boundary/test_import_gate.py::test_clean_tree_passes -q
```

## Acceptance
- Domain modules listed above route their adapter / API needs through the
  Protocol ports defined in `app.adapters` (or move callers out of
  `app.domain`).
- `lint-imports` reports 5 contracts kept, 0 broken.
- `tests/boundary/test_import_gate.py::test_clean_tree_passes` passes.

## Notes
Surfaced by selfreview cd-e7hx as a pre-existing failure; out of scope
for that task. Likely impacts each domain module's authz wiring — the
refactor that landed `app.authz` (cd-c91 / cd-i1qe family) appears to
have pulled handlers in via `app.authz.enforce`.

title: spec-drift(llm): reconcile llm_assignment scoping (deployment vs workspace)

Added paragraph describing deployment-seed + workspace-override layered resolve, mirroring the cd-tw7k fix on llm_capability_inheritance.

title: fix(mocks): add jinja2 to mocks/Dockerfile.app-api dependency list

title: fix(web/sse): task.updated/completed/skipped reducer reads non-existent payload.task — drop merge, invalidate by task_id
description:
**Context.** cd-43wv landed `TaskUpdated` (`app.events.types.TaskUpdated`)
with payload `{task_id: str, changed_fields: tuple[str, ...]}` plus the
SSE-stamped envelope keys (`kind`, `workspace_id`, `invalidates`). The
canonical event payload is intentionally minimal — subscribers re-fetch
via REST under per-row authz.

The mocks SPA reducer at `mocks/web/src/lib/sse.ts:168-182` handles
`task.updated` (along with `task.completed` / `task.skipped`) by
reading `payload.task` as a full `Task` object:

    case "task.updated":
    case "task.completed":
    case "task.skipped": {
      const payload = data as { task: Task };
      client.setQueryData(...) // payload.task.id, payload.task fields
      client.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: qk.tasks() });
      ...
    }

When the production SSE actually fires `task.updated`, `payload.task`
is `undefined` and `payload.task.id` throws — the reducer either
crashes the dispatcher or no-ops the merge into `qk.task(id)` cache.
The fallback `invalidateQueries(qk.tasks())` would still fire if the
crash is caught at the `dispatch` boundary, but the per-detail
optimistic merge is broken.

`task.completed` and `task.skipped` events on the server side also
publish `{task_id}` only (see `app.events.types.TaskCompleted` /
`TaskSkipped`); the same mismatch is pre-existing on those kinds.

**Resolution options.**

1. **Wire the full task on every event.** Change the server-side
   `TaskUpdated` / `TaskCompleted` / `TaskSkipped` payload to embed
   the rendered `TaskPayload`. Pros: SPA optimistic merge works.
   Cons: payload bloat, PII leak surface (description_md /
   completion_note_md flow over the SSE stream), recipients see
   every field even when they only need `invalidates`.

2. **Drop the per-detail merge in the SPA reducer.** Treat
   `task.updated` as a pure invalidation signal — invalidate
   `qk.task(task_id)` and `qk.tasks()` keyed off the bare `task_id`.
   Matches the §14 "SSE-driven invalidation" posture and the
   canonical event minimality contract. Most likely the right
   answer.

3. **Two-tier event.** Emit `task.updated` (skinny) and
   `task.<field>.changed` (richer for hot fields) so the SPA picks
   the right reducer per kind. Overkill for v1.

Recommend option (2). The SPA reducer should consume `{task_id,
changed_fields}` and invalidate the matching cache keys; never peek
into a hand-shaped `task` field on the SSE payload.

**Files**

- `mocks/web/src/lib/sse.ts:168-182` — rewrite the
  `task.updated`/`completed`/`skipped` reducer to invalidate
  `qk.task(task_id)` rather than merge a bare `Task`.
- `mocks/web/src/types/api.ts` — drop the `{task: Task}` typing if
  it lives there.
- `app/api/transport/sse.py` — once the SPA is correct, narrow the
  `_INVALIDATIONS` map for `task.updated` to also drop
  `("task", task_id)` so the reducer doesn't have to derive the
  cache key from a payload field. Optional follow-up.
- `tests/api/transport/test_sse.py` — pin the wire shape with a
  contract test (`payload` keys are `{kind, workspace_id, task_id,
  changed_fields, invalidates}`; no `task` field).

**Acceptance**

- SPA `dispatch` for `task.updated`/`task.completed`/`task.skipped`
  reads `payload.task_id` (string) and invalidates the affected
  TanStack Query keys, never reaches into a hand-shaped `payload.task`.
- A contract test pins the `task.updated` wire shape.

Surfaced during cd-43wv `/selfreview autofix`. Pre-existing for
`task.completed` / `task.skipped` (cd-sn26 era), made visible by
the new `task.updated` event.

title: feat(web/employee): ReceiptScanPanel must POST FormData on /expenses/scan
description: POST /api/v1/expenses/scan is now wired (cd-65ib) and expects multipart/form-data with an image part. app/web/src/pages/employee/expenses/ReceiptScanPanel.tsx currently calls fetchJson<ExpenseScanResult>(/api/v1/expenses/scan, { method: POST }) with no body. The API returns 422 (blob_empty / blob_mime_not_allowed) and the worker UI never reaches the review phase. The <input type='file'> is in the JSX but handleFileSelect never reads event.target.files. Fix: handleFileSelect reads the picked file from the change event, POSTs FormData with the image part, and surfaces scan errors (scan_not_configured 503, blob_* 422) inline with a retry. Playwright smoke for happy + 503 paths.

title: feat(api/places): worker-visible properties surface for HistoryPage / NewTaskModal / SubmitExpenseForm
description:
## Problem
cd-lzh1 introduced GET /api/v1/properties gated by manager-only `properties.read`. Three worker SPA pages call `fetchJson<Property[]>('/api/v1/properties')` and now 403:

- `app/web/src/pages/employee/HistoryPage.tsx` (line 37) — renders past task property names. With 403, propsById is empty so workers see raw `property_id` UUIDs in the History tab instead of "Villa Sud".
- `app/web/src/components/NewTaskModal.tsx` (line 26) — workers create personal tasks pinned to a property. With 403, the property dropdown is empty so the worker quick-add cannot pin a property.
- `app/web/src/pages/employee/expenses/SubmitExpenseForm.tsx` (line 117) — optional property tag on submitted expenses. Same outcome: dropdown empty, feature degraded.

## Why this is a bug
A worker scoped to property X already legitimately sees X's id (assigned tasks, stays, expenses). The current 403 doesn't add privacy — it just breaks the rendering. Meanwhile, the manager-only gate exists because the cross-roster view leaks governance fields (`client_org_id`, `owner_user_id`, `settings_override`) that workers shouldn't enumerate.

## Two viable fixes
1. **Worker-scoped projection**: same endpoint, but for non-managers the result set narrows to "properties this worker has scope.view on" and the projection drops governance-adjacent fields (`client_org_id`, `owner_user_id`, `settings_override`). Single endpoint, payload varies by role. Closer to the §05 "scope.view" semantics.
2. **Separate worker endpoint**: e.g. GET /api/v1/properties/visible (or piggyback the manager roster on `scope.view`). Two endpoints, two SPA call paths.

Recommend (1) because the SPA already calls one URL and per-role projections are a precedent.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Worker calls to GET /api/v1/properties return 200 with the (narrowed) properties they have scope on.
- [ ] Manager / owner projection unchanged.
- [ ] Governance-adjacent fields (`client_org_id`, `owner_user_id`, `settings_override`) absent from the worker projection or hidden behind a separate `properties.read.governance` gate.
- [ ] HistoryPage renders property names again, NewTaskModal + SubmitExpenseForm dropdowns populate.
- [ ] Spec §12 + §05 updated; OpenAPI regenerated.

## Discovered from
cd-lzh1 self-review.

title: fix(api/tasks): SchedulePayload missing default_assignee_id + rrule_human fields the SPA reads
description:
## Problem
Discovered during cd-dzte selfreview. The SPA `Schedule` type and `SchedulesPage` rendering expect:
- `default_assignee_id` (the SPA reads `default_assignee_id`)
- `rrule_human` (human-readable schedule cadence)

The production `SchedulePayload` in `app/api/v1/tasks.py` exposes neither. The SPA renders `undefined` for these fields against real data.

## Affected
- app/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx (renders both)
- app/web/src/types/task.ts (declares both)
- app/api/v1/tasks.py (SchedulePayload — missing both)
- mocks/app/mock_data.py (mock has rrule_human; production lags)

## Acceptance criteria
- SchedulePayload exposes default_assignee_id (alias to existing default_assignee field, or rename — pick whichever matches the spec).
- SchedulePayload exposes rrule_human computed from the schedule's ruleset.
- /api/v1/schedules listing surfaces both.
- Tests cover both fields populated correctly.
- Verbatim-port property preserved (mocks SPA == app SPA).

## References
- cd-dzte selfreview surfaced this
- app/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx:70,96
- app/api/v1/tasks.py SchedulePayload

title: fix(web/expenses): align SubmitExpenseForm POST body with cd-t6y2 ExpenseClaimCreate
description:
## Problem
app/web/src/pages/employee/expenses/SubmitExpenseForm.tsx still POSTs the legacy mock-era body

```
{ merchant, amount, currency, category, note, ocr_confidence }
```

to `POST /api/v1/expenses`, but the server's `ExpenseClaimCreate` schema (`app/domain/expenses/claims.py:417`) requires:

```
{ work_engagement_id, vendor, purchased_at, currency, total_amount_cents, category, property_id?, note_md? }
```

Discovered while shipping cd-75cv (which fixed the GET-side envelope drift). The submit path will reject with 422 against a real server. The GET-side renames are already in flight.

## Acceptance criteria
- SubmitExpenseForm.tsx posts a body that matches `ExpenseClaimCreate`.
- The form collects (or derives) `work_engagement_id` (from the active worker session), `vendor`, `purchased_at`, `total_amount_cents`, `category`, optional `property_id`, optional `note_md`, `currency`.
- Tests cover the field mapping (vitest).
- E2E spot-check: dev login as a worker, submit an expense at /w/<slug>/expenses/new, server accepts.

## References
- app/web/src/pages/employee/expenses/SubmitExpenseForm.tsx
- app/domain/expenses/claims.py:417 (ExpenseClaimCreate)
- app/api/v1/expenses.py (POST handler)
- cd-75cv (sibling fix on the GET side)

title: Stabilise schedule dialogs against SSE-driven re-init mid-edit
description:
## Problem
\`OverrideDialog\` and \`LeaveDialog\` in \`app/web/src/components/ScheduleDialogs.tsx\`
re-initialise their form state in a \`useEffect(..., [iso, pattern])\`
or similar dep on data-derived references. After cd-ops1 wired SSE
to invalidate \`[\"my-schedule\"]\` on \`task.created\`, \`task.assigned\`,
\`task.updated\`, \`task.completed\`, \`task.skipped\`, \`task.overdue\`,
\`stay.upcoming\`, \`stay_task_bundle.*\`, and \`approval.*\`, the
schedule infinite-query refetches all loaded pages on those events,
\`merged\` regenerates a fresh \`weekly_availability\` array, and the
\`pattern\` reference passed into \`OverrideDialog\` becomes a new
object — clobbering the worker's half-typed override request.

\`BookingProposeDialog\` had the same hazard with \`properties\` and
was fixed in cd-3w83 by routing through a \`propertiesRef\` ref so
the re-init only fires on \`iso\` flipping from null. Apply the same
pattern to \`OverrideDialog\` (\`pattern\` ref) and audit \`LeaveDialog\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Opening \`OverrideDialog\` and typing in the time fields, then firing
      a \`task.created\` SSE event in DevTools, leaves the typed values
      untouched.
- [ ] \`OverrideDialog\` still picks up the latest pattern when re-opened
      on a different ISO date.
- [ ] Same audit + fix applied to \`LeaveDialog\` if any data-derived prop
      drives a re-init effect.

title: feat(web/expenses): align SPA list calls with cd-t6y2 envelope (data/next_cursor/has_more)
description:
## Problem
GET /api/v1/expenses (shipped under cd-t6y2) returns ExpenseClaimListResponse — { data: ExpenseClaim[], next_cursor, has_more } — but ExpensesApprovalsPage.tsx (and any other consumer) calls fetchJson<Expense[]>('/api/v1/expenses') and reads the response as a bare array.

At runtime the cast succeeds (the response is an object with no .length, .filter, etc.) and the page hits the 'Failed to load.' / blank-list branch. The expense type itself diverges too: cd-t6y2 returns ExpenseClaimPayload (with work_engagement_id, claim states like pending|approved|rejected|reimbursed), while the SPA reads the legacy mock-era Expense shape (employee_id, status submitted|approved|...).

## Decision needed
Two viable directions; whoever picks this up should run AskUserQuestion first:

1. SPA adapts — wrap /api/v1/expenses calls in a helper that unwraps the envelope and maps ExpenseClaimPayload → Expense shape. Cheaper, isolated to the SPA.
2. Backend exposes a legacy alias — a separate non-paginated route (or a query param) that returns the SPA's flat shape. Heavier, but lets multiple SPA call-sites stay verbatim with their mocks.

## Acceptance criteria
- ExpensesApprovalsPage actually paints rows against seeded fixtures at http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/expenses.
- Type for what the page consumes matches the chosen direction (no unknown casts).
- Pending-reimbursement total still reflects approvals/reimbursements (SSE invalidation already present in app/web/src/lib/sse.ts).
- Tests cover the unwrap/map (or the alias).

## Out of scope
- Approving / rejecting / reimbursing endpoints (POST /{id}/{decision}) — those exist and the SPA calls them correctly.
- The /api/v1/employees gap (separate Beads task).

## References
- app/web/src/pages/manager/ExpensesApprovalsPage.tsx:26-43
- app/api/v1/expenses.py:498-539 (list_expense_claims_route)
- app/web/src/types/expense.ts vs ExpenseClaimPayload

title: feat(api/v1/employees): GET /api/v1/employees workspace roster endpoint
description:
## Problem
Two manager pages (ExpensesApprovalsPage.tsx, SchedulesPage.tsx) call fetchJson<Employee[]>('/api/v1/employees') but no router under app/api/v1/ provides GET /api/v1/employees. Both pages currently hit the 'Failed to load.' branch in production.

The list shape the SPA expects is the legacy bare array from the mock layer: Employee[] matching app/web/src/types/employee.ts (id, name, roles, properties, avatar_url, avatar_initials, capabilities, etc.).

Discovered while running /selfreview against cd-rift's verbatim port of the expenses approvals page — the page itself is byte-identical to the mock; the backing endpoint just isn't there yet.

## Acceptance criteria
- New router under app/api/v1/ (probably employees.py or folded into users.py) exposing GET /api/v1/employees mounted at /w/<slug>/api/v1/employees.
- Returns Employee[] matching app/web/src/types/employee.ts shape.
- Permission gate: at minimum employees.read (manager+).
- OpenAPI updated (/update-openapi).
- ExpensesApprovalsPage and SchedulesPage load against seeded fixtures (smoke test in tests/e2e/manager/).

## Out of scope
- Reshape of the SPA call sites (they will consume the new endpoint as-is).
- Pagination — current SPA pages expect a bare array; if the new endpoint paginates, file a separate task to wrap the SPA call.

## References
- app/web/src/pages/manager/ExpensesApprovalsPage.tsx:32
- app/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx:28
- app/web/src/types/employee.ts

title: feat(web/manager): align TemplatesPage with backend /api/v1/task_templates contract
description:
## Problem / goal
`app/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx` (verbatim-ported from the mock under cd-65kn) calls `fetchJson<TaskTemplate[]>("/api/v1/task_templates")`, but the backend (cd-sn26) returns:

- An **envelope**: `TaskTemplateListResponse = { data: TaskTemplatePayload[], next_cursor: string | null, has_more: bool }` (`app/api/v1/tasks.py:547`).
- A **payload shape** that diverges from the SPA's `TaskTemplate` type:
  - `description_md` vs `description`
  - `role_id` vs `role`
  - `checklist_template_json: list[dict]` vs `checklist: ChecklistItem[]`
  - `inventory_consumption_json: dict[str, int]` vs `inventory_effects: InventoryEffect[]`
  - extras: `workspace_id`, `listed_property_ids`, `area_scope`, `listed_area_ids`, `linked_instruction_ids`, `llm_hints_md`, `created_at`, `deleted_at`

The SPA's `TaskTemplate` type was authored against the mocks; the backend (cd-0tg / cd-sn26) shipped its own canonical projection. They must reconcile.

## Technical context
- `app/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx:25` (consumer)
- `app/web/src/types/task.ts:75-86` (`TaskTemplate`)
- `app/api/v1/tasks.py:244-297` (`TaskTemplatePayload`)
- `app/api/v1/tasks.py:547-552` (`TaskTemplateListResponse` envelope)
- `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"Task templates" (canonical contract)

## Implementation notes
- Decide one canonical wire shape (likely the backend's `TaskTemplatePayload` with the envelope). Update `TaskTemplate` (or split into `TaskTemplate` for runtime + `TaskTemplatePayload` for wire) and the mocks accordingly.
- TemplatesPage reads from `response.data`; switch the page to render the canonical fields (name, description_md → render markdown or strip, role_id → role lookup via roles query, checklist_template_json → mapped to ChecklistTemplateItem[]).
- `inventory_consumption_json` (dict) is incompatible with `inventory_effects` (array of {item_ref, kind, qty}). Choose: either widen the backend to emit a richer effects array (matches the spec §08 inventory hooks) or narrow the SPA to just consume-with-qty. The spec wins.
- `/update-openapi` after backend changes; `pnpm -C app/web typegen` if the SPA pulls from the OpenAPI client.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] SPA's TaskTemplate type 1:1 matches the OpenAPI `TaskTemplatePayload`.
- [ ] TemplatesPage consumes the cursor envelope (`{ data, next_cursor, has_more }`).
- [ ] Cards render every backend field meaningfully (name, description, role, duration, scope, photo evidence, priority, checklist, inventory hooks).
- [ ] Mocks updated in lockstep so `mocks/web` and `app/web` stay byte-for-byte identical at the JSX level.
- [ ] Type tests + e2e still pass.

## Test plan
- `pnpm -C app/web typecheck && pnpm -C app/web test -- --run`
- Playwright e2e: load `/w/<slug>/templates` against the seeded fixture and assert the cards render the backend's data without console errors.

## Discovered during
selfreview cd-s1i8 of cd-65kn — verbatim port preserved the mock's contract; backend reconciliation deferred.

title: fix(api/tasks): GET /api/v1/schedules envelope mismatches frontend SchedulesPayload
description:
## Problem
The manager SchedulesPage (app/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx, ported verbatim from the mock under cd-5d51) decodes the response as:

  interface SchedulesPayload {
    schedules: Schedule[];
    templates_by_id: Record<string, TaskTemplate>;
  }

The mock backend (mocks/app/main.py:1860) returns exactly that shape. The production router (app/api/v1/tasks.py, ScheduleListResponse @ ~line 555) returns the standard cursor envelope:

  { data: SchedulePayload[], next_cursor: string | null, has_more: bool }

…with no templates_by_id sidecar. Result: when the page hits the real API, schedQ.data is malformed and the page falls to the 'Failed to load.' branch (or worse, a runtime 'cannot destructure templates_by_id of undefined' if the destructure runs).

## Decision needed
Either:
- (a) Add a templates_by_id sidecar to GET /schedules (or a sibling endpoint) so the frontend keeps its single-call form, OR
- (b) Refactor SchedulesPage to fetch /api/v1/schedules + /api/v1/task_templates separately and join client-side. Breaks verbatim parity with the mock until the mock catches up.

Spec §12 should pin one path. Note: the same trade-off will hit other pages that piggy-back on a 'fetch the whole table-of-contents in one call' shape.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Decision recorded in docs/specs/12-rest-api.md (Schedules section) and the matching mock updated to mirror it.
- [ ] SchedulesPage renders against the real /api/v1/schedules without falling to 'Failed to load.'.
- [ ] Verbatim-port property re-asserted: diff mocks/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx app/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx is empty.
- [ ] Discovered during /selfreview of cd-5d51 (paired with cd-7a63).

title: feat(api/identity): expose /api/v1/employees for manager pages
description:
## Problem / goal
The manager SchedulesPage (live at app/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx) calls GET /api/v1/employees to look up the default-assignee avatar + name for each schedule row. The verbatim port from cd-5d51 kept the call as-is, but the v1 surface in app/api/v1/ has no /employees router — identity primitives live under /users / /work_engagements / /user_work_roles instead.

## Affected callers
- app/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx (cd-5d51)
- Almost every manager page with an assignee column (EmployeesPage, EmployeeDetailPage, EmployeeLeavesPage — cd-ptgv, cd-9mxo, etc).

## Decision needed
Either:
- (a) Add a thin /api/v1/employees router that returns the Employee shape the frontend declares (app/web/src/types/employee.ts), backed by users + work_engagements joined, OR
- (b) Refactor the frontend to consume the existing /api/v1/users + /api/v1/work_engagements pair (would break verbatim-port parity with the mock).

Spec §12 should pin one path before we wire more callers. Default to (a) for the verbatim-port era; revisit when the mock catches up.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Decision recorded in docs/specs/12-rest-api.md (Employees section).
- [ ] If (a): GET /w/<slug>/api/v1/employees returns Employee[] (or {data, next_cursor, has_more}); auth = session + workspace scope; 403 otherwise.
- [ ] If (b): SchedulesPage + sibling manager pages refactored to the underlying primitives in a paired follow-up.
- [ ] OpenAPI updated.
- [ ] Discovered during /selfreview of cd-5d51 (paired with cd-7a63).

title: feat(api/places): expose /api/v1/properties for manager pages
description:
## Problem / goal
The manager SchedulesPage (live at app/web/src/pages/manager/SchedulesPage.tsx) calls GET /api/v1/properties to render the property chip on every schedule row plus the preview list. The verbatim port from cd-5d51 left this call in place (matches the mock byte-for-byte), but the production v1 surface has no /properties router yet — only the mock backend serves it.

- Cursor-paginate per app/api/pagination.py if the workspace can have a long list; the SchedulesPage call expects a flat array so wrap inside the workspace's listed properties (small N) and return Property[] directly only if the spec §12 endorses it — otherwise return the standard {data, next_cursor, has_more} envelope and update the frontend caller in a paired beads task.

- [ ] Auth: requires session + workspace scope; 403 otherwise.
