- [ ] Discovered during /selfreview of cd-5d51 (paired with cd-7a63).

title: fix(tests): cascade of order-dependent audit/log assertions surfaces in full-suite mode
description:
## Problem
After fixing the root-logger isolation (cd-4qvy) and the JWT regex (cd-pzr1), the full suite still has order-dependent failures across audit/log-asserting tests:

- `tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_register.py::TestRegisterFinishHappyPath::test_writes_audit_row`
- `tests/unit/auth/test_session_invalidate.py::TestInvalidateForUser::test_audit_row_carries_cause`
- `tests/unit/test_util_hashids.py::TestTampering::test_flipped_byte_raises_token_invalid`
- `tests/unit/api/v1/auth/test_me_avatar.py::TestDeleteAvatar::test_delete_avatar_clears_hash_and_preserves_blob`
- (and likely more — each `-x` run halts at a different first failure)

All pass in isolation. All are tests that read back persisted audit rows and assert on content that previously got redacted differently.

## Hypothesis
A test earlier in the suite seeds a process-level redactor (or modifies one — e.g. via pickling Settings, monkey-patching, etc.) and the autouse handler-isolation only catches root-logger handlers, NOT mutated module-level constants in `app.util.redact` or `app.audit`.

## Investigation
1. Run `pytest -x --co` to see resolution order.
2. Bisect by file using `pytest tests/A tests/B failing_test`.
3. Identify the polluter that mutates module state.
4. Either fix the polluter (proper teardown) or extend `tests/conftest.py` autouse to snapshot module state (a heavier autouse).

## Acceptance
- [ ] Full suite `uv run pytest -x -q` passes deterministically across 3 consecutive runs.
- [ ] No new `monkeypatch.undo()` strewn across tests — the fix should be at the polluter level.

## Test plan
`for i in 1 2 3; do uv run pytest -x -q; done`

title: Self-review: fix(tests): two flaky/regressed unit tests
description: Selfreview paired.

title: fix(tests): two flaky/regressed unit tests after JWT regex tightening
description:
## Problem
After cd-pzr1 tightened the JWT credential regex, two unit tests fail when run in suite order:

### 1. `tests/unit/domain/stays/test_ical_service.py::TestRegister::test_register_happy_path`
**Deterministic regression**, NOT a flake. The test asserts `'airbnb.com' not in audit_after['url_preview']`. The previous over-broad JWT regex matched `www.airbnb.com` (3 dot-separated segments) and redacted it. The new 16-char-per-segment floor doesn't match this hostname.

**Fix**: this assertion was relying on accidental redaction. The principled fix is one of:
- (preferred) drop the assertion; document that audit logs may carry hostnames since they're not credentials. The test's other assertions (`abc.ics not in repr(audit.diff)`, encrypted plaintext URL never persisted) still cover the security-relevant invariants.
- OR add an explicit URL/hostname redaction rule in `app/util/redact.py`.

Choose the first option for v1 — hostnames are not credentials. Update the test + module docstring accordingly. File a Beads follow-up if a future privacy review wants explicit hostname scrubbing.

### 2. `tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_login.py::TestAutoRevokeCredentialFreshUow::test_db_failure_is_swallowed_without_raising`
**Order-dependent flake**. Passes in isolation, fails when `tests/unit/api/...` runs first. The test monkeypatches `passkey_api_module.make_uow` to raise; in a polluted state, the import target is something different (probably a leftover patch from another test, or the module is already imported with a different binding).

**Investigation**: bisect by `pytest tests/unit/api/... tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_login.py` and narrow which api test is the polluter. Likely a fixture that patches `passkey_api_module` without proper teardown.

**Fix**: depends on root cause — usually `monkeypatch.undo()` needed somewhere, or a yield-style fixture that didn't restore.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `tests/unit/domain/stays/test_ical_service.py::TestRegister::test_register_happy_path` passes both alone and in the full suite.
- [ ] `tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_login.py::TestAutoRevokeCredentialFreshUow::test_db_failure_is_swallowed_without_raising` passes both alone and in the full suite.
- [ ] No regression in either file's other tests.

## Test plan
`uv run pytest -x -q`

title: feat(api/expenses): pending-reimbursement totals /api/v1/expenses/pending_reimbursement
description:
## Problem
The web SPA's `OwedToYou` panel (`app/web/src/pages/employee/expenses/OwedToYou.tsx`) and the manager `PayPage` both call `GET /api/v1/expenses/pending_reimbursement` (worker passes `?user_id=me`, manager omits the param to get the workspace-wide aggregate).

The expenses router shipped in cd-t6y2 has `/expenses/pending` (a list of pending claims) but does not expose a per-currency total nor a per-user breakdown.

Until this lands, the "Owed to you" panel never renders (the page treats a missing query as "no data") and the manager pay surface lacks its summary number.

## What the SPA expects
- `GET /api/v1/expenses/pending_reimbursement` returns:
  ```ts
  {
    user_id: string | null,
    claims: Expense[],
    totals_by_currency: { currency: string; amount_cents: number }[],
    by_user?: PendingReimbursementUserBreakdown[],  // workspace aggregate only
  }
  ```
  See `app/web/src/types/expense.ts` (`PendingReimbursement`).
- `?user_id=me` narrows to the caller; `?user_id=<uuid>` requires manager permission; no param returns the workspace-wide aggregate including `by_user`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Endpoint added under the workspace prefix.
- [ ] Per-user filter (`?user_id=me` and `?user_id=<uuid>`) and workspace-wide aggregate paths covered.
- [ ] Authorisation: workers see only `me`; managers see anyone in the workspace.
- [ ] `totals_by_currency` only includes claims whose status is `approved` and unreimbursed (§09 "Amount owed to the employee").
- [ ] OpenAPI + `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` updated; `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` already specifies the contract.

title: feat(api/expenses): receipt scan endpoint /api/v1/expenses/scan
description:
## Problem
The web SPA's `ReceiptScanPanel` (`app/web/src/pages/employee/expenses/ReceiptScanPanel.tsx`) calls `POST /api/v1/expenses/scan` (multipart upload) and expects an `ExpenseScanResult` back: `{vendor, purchased_at, currency, total_amount_cents, category, note_md}` each as an `AutofillField<T> = {value, confidence}`, plus an optional `agent_question`.

The OCR autofill router shipped in cd-95zb is mounted at `/expenses/autofill` (no `/scan` alias) and the contract shape may not match the `ExpenseScanResult` the SPA's review form consumes.

Until this lands, scanning a receipt in the worker UI fails — `fetchJson<ExpenseScanResult>("/api/v1/expenses/scan")` 404s and the form never reaches the `review` phase.

## What the SPA expects
- `POST /api/v1/expenses/scan` (multipart with the receipt image) returns `ExpenseScanResult` (see `app/web/src/types/expense.ts`).
- 200 with the autofill payload on success.
- 401 / 403 / 422 (file too large, wrong mimetype) RFC 7807 problems.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Either rename `/expenses/autofill` to `/expenses/scan` (preferred — matches §09 spec wording "scan") or add an alias.
- [ ] Response body is `ExpenseScanResult` per `app/web/src/types/expense.ts`.
- [ ] OpenAPI updated; `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` reflects the path.
- [ ] Tests: happy path, oversize image → 413, non-image → 422, anonymous → 401.

title: feat(api/expenses): worker-self-list /api/v1/expenses?mine=true
description:
## Problem
The web SPA's worker-side expense surface (`app/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx` and its `expenses/RecentExpenses.tsx` panel) calls `GET /api/v1/expenses?mine=true` to fetch the active worker's own claims (queryKey `qk.expenses("mine")`).

The router shipped in cd-t6y2 mounts the list under `/api/v1/expenses` but exposes only the workspace-scoped list (every claim in the workspace) without a `?mine=true` filter or a self-only `/me/expenses` endpoint.

Until this lands, MyExpensesPage's "My recent expenses" section renders "Failed to load." against a real backend.

## What the SPA expects
- `GET /api/v1/expenses?mine=true` returns a JSON array of `Expense` rows for `request.user.id` only.
- 200 with empty array when the worker has no claims.
- 401 / 403 enforced via the same workspace permission check the workspace-wide list uses, narrowed to self.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `GET /api/v1/expenses?mine=true` returns only the caller's claims.
- [ ] Mounted under the workspace prefix (`/w/<slug>/api/v1/...`) like the rest of the expenses router.
- [ ] OpenAPI updated; `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` reflects the query param.
- [ ] Tests: happy path, empty result, anonymous → 401, wrong-workspace → 403.

title: feat(api/tasks): wire photo / voice / gps evidence through the asset pipeline
description:
**Context.** cd-sn26 shipped `POST /tasks/{id}/evidence` wired end-to-end for `kind=note` only; photo / voice / gps uploads return `501 evidence_kind_not_implemented` (see `app/api/v1/tasks.py::upload_task_evidence_route`). The domain-layer seam `app.domain.tasks.completion.add_note_evidence` is note-only.

**Scope.** Add a sibling `add_file_evidence` (or similar) in the completion domain that:

- accepts a file stream + kind,
- delegates to the asset pipeline (`app.domain.assets.*`) for blob storage + content hashing,
- inserts the `Evidence` row with the resolved `blob_hash`,
- audits `task.evidence.\<kind\>.add`.

Then lift the 501 branch in the router — drop the `kind != "note"` guard and call the new seam. Checklist item attachment (`evidence_blob_hash` on `ChecklistItem`) piggybacks on the same pipeline; keep an eye on the parallel `required_evidence` three-value enum.

**Acceptance**
- `POST /tasks/{id}/evidence` with `kind=photo` + multipart file → 201 `EvidencePayload`.
- Integration tests with a tiny PNG / WAV fixture.
- Virus scan / size cap per the asset pipeline contract.

title: feat(api/tasks): PATCH /tasks/{id} widens to full spec §06 mutable set
description:
**Context.** cd-sn26 shipped a narrow `PATCH /tasks/{id}` that only writes `title` + `description_md` (`app.domain.tasks.oneoff.update_task`). Per §06 "Task row" the spec-allowed mutable set also includes `scheduled_for_local`, `property_id`, `area_id`, `unit_id`, `expected_role_id`, `priority`, `duration_minutes`, and `photo_evidence`.

**Scope.** Extend `TaskPatch` + `update_task` to accept the remaining fields. The gnarly bits are:

- `scheduled_for_local` requires recomputing `scheduled_for_utc` via the property timezone and re-running the scheduler-flip state gate. Probably needs to fan out to a `task.updated` / `task.rescheduled` event (the `/scheduler/tasks/{id}/reschedule` verb is a different slice — cd-scheduler).
- `property_id` / `area_id` / `unit_id` cross into availability / rota checks.
- Must emit a `TaskChanged` (or equivalent) event once the shape lands so SSE listeners refresh.

**Acceptance**
- `TaskPatch` DTO carries every §06 mutable field.
- `update_task` writes + audits + emits.
- Integration tests for each field + the cross-field interactions.

**Files**
- `app/domain/tasks/oneoff.py` — `TaskPatch`, `update_task`.
- `app/api/v1/tasks.py` — `patch_task_route`.
- `tests/integration/api/test_tasks_routes.py`.

title: feat(api/me): GET /me/schedule + POST /me/leaves + /me/availability_overrides
description:
Spec `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"Self-service shortcuts":

```
GET    /me/schedule                       # self-only calendar feed for /schedule
POST   /me/leaves                         # creates user_leave with approval_required always true
GET    /me/availability_overrides         # self-only list of every user_availability_override
POST   /me/availability_overrides         # server computes approval_required per §06
```

Each endpoint is syntactic sugar over the generic endpoint with `user_id = current_user.id` enforced server-side — a worker cannot use them to author requests for another user.

Depends on cd-oydd (user_leaves) and cd-uqw1 (user_availability_overrides) routers.

/me/schedule returns aggregated calendar view: rota slots, assigned tasks, approved leaves + overrides + holidays covering the window, plus pending items flagged separately. This aggregation pipeline doesn't exist yet and will need its own domain helper.

Split from cd-dcfw.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `/me/schedule` aggregator returns {rota, tasks, leaves, overrides, holidays, pending} for (from, to) window with defaults
- [ ] `/me/leaves` forces user_id = ctx.actor_id and approval_required=true
- [ ] `/me/availability_overrides` server-computes approval_required per §06 matrix
- [ ] Tests prove a worker cannot author requests for another user

title: feat(api/identity): user_availability_overrides HTTP router + hybrid approval logic
description:
Spec `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md`:

```
GET    /user_availability_overrides   # ?user_id=…&from=…&to=…&approved=true|false
POST   /user_availability_overrides
PATCH  /user_availability_overrides/{id}
POST   /user_availability_overrides/{id}/approve
POST   /user_availability_overrides/{id}/reject
DELETE /user_availability_overrides/{id}
```

Domain logic: §06 "user_availability_overrides" hybrid approval model. Server determines `approval_required` on create by comparing the override against the weekly pattern. Owner/manager-created overrides auto-approve.

Depends on cd-l2r9 (DB model for both user_availability_override and user_weekly_availability).

Split from cd-dcfw.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Domain service implementing approval-required computation per §06 table (adding hours auto-approved; reducing requires approval)
- [ ] Approve / reject endpoints with authz gates
- [ ] Cursor pagination on GET
- [ ] Tests covering each row of the approval matrix + auth denial

title: feat(api/identity): user_leaves HTTP router + approval domain service
description:
Spec `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"Users / work roles / settings":

```
GET    /user_leaves               # ?user_id=…&from=…&to=…&approved=true|false
POST   /user_leaves
PATCH  /user_leaves/{id}
POST   /user_leaves/{id}/approve
POST   /user_leaves/{id}/reject
DELETE /user_leaves/{id}
```

Approval logic from §06 "user_leave": self-submitted leaves land with `approved_at = null`; owner/manager approves or rejects. Pending leaves do NOT affect assignment.

Depends on cd-l2r9 (DB model).

Needs a minimal domain service under app/domain/availability/ or app/services/leaves/ with:
- list_leaves(ctx, filters) with cursor pagination
- create_leave(ctx, body) — validates category enum + starts_on <= ends_on
- update_leave(ctx, id, body) — only while still pending, or by owner/manager
- approve_leave(ctx, id) — stamps approved_at + approved_by
- reject_leave(ctx, id) — stamps approved_at + approved_by = null (or a rejection marker)
- delete_leave(ctx, id) — soft delete

Wire under /w/<slug>/api/v1/. Tag identity + user_leaves. Add action-keys to `_action_catalog.py` for `leaves.approve` / `leaves.manage` if not already covered (also update §05 in lockstep — spec drift check needed).

Split from cd-dcfw.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] DB model + domain service + router + tests
- [ ] Action-key gates per §05 (may need new keys — coordinate with /specs)
- [ ] Cursor pagination on GET
- [ ] Self-submit path auto-approves for owner/manager per §06
- [ ] Tests cover approval state transitions + auth denial

title: feat(db): user_leave + user_availability_override + user_weekly_availability + public_holiday tables
description:
Spec `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"user_leave" / §"Weekly availability" / §"user_availability_overrides" / §"public_holidays" defines four tables that don't yet exist as SQLAlchemy models or migrations.

Required entities (see §06 for full column lists):

1. `user_leave` — (id, workspace_id, user_id, starts_on, ends_on, category enum, approved_at?, approved_by?, note_md?, created_at/updated_at, deleted_at?)
2. `user_weekly_availability` — (id, workspace_id, user_id, weekday 0..6, starts_local?, ends_local?, updated_at) with BOTH-OR-NEITHER check on hours
3. `user_availability_override` — (id, workspace_id, user_id, date, available, starts_local?, ends_local?, reason?, approval_required, approved_at?, approved_by?, created_at/updated_at, deleted_at?) with UNIQUE(workspace_id, user_id, date)
4. `public_holiday` — (id, workspace_id, name, date, country?, scheduling_effect enum, reduced_starts_local?, reduced_ends_local?, payroll_multiplier?, recurrence enum?, notes_md?, created_at/updated_at, deleted_at?) with UNIQUE(workspace_id, date, country)

Follow the workspace-scoped pattern under `app/adapters/db/` (new package or extend an existing one — likely `app/adapters/db/availability/` for the three user_* tables and `app/adapters/db/holidays/`). Register each as workspace-scoped in the package __init__. Alembic migration forward + downgrade.

Split from cd-dcfw (scoped down to work_engagements/work_roles/user_work_roles only).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] SQLAlchemy models for all four tables with proper constraints (CHECK, UNIQUE, indexes)
- [ ] Alembic migration forward + downgrade, roundtrips on sqlite + pg
- [ ] Workspace-scoped registration
- [ ] Unit tests covering each model's invariants
- [ ] Cross-reference §06 sections in docstrings

title: feat(api/identity): property_work_role_assignments HTTP router
description:
Spec `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"Users / work roles / settings":


title: feat(db/stays): extend ical_feed to §04 columns (last_error, poll_cadence, unit_id, gcal provider)
description:
## Problem / goal

The v1 `ical_feed` ORM (cd-1b2) lands a minimal slice: `url`, `provider`, `last_polled_at`, `last_etag`, `enabled`. §04 §"iCal feed" calls for additional columns that cd-1ai and cd-d48 will need:

- **`last_error text`** — the §04 error code (`ical_url_timeout`, `ical_url_private_address`, ...). cd-1ai's `probe_feed` currently drops the error code into the audit row instead of persisting it against the feed.
- **`poll_cadence text`** — per-feed cron (default `*/15 * * * *`). cd-d48 needs this to drive APScheduler.
- **`unit_id text FK`** — per-unit feed mapping. §04 §"iCal feed" / §"Unit": "Stays are upserted by `(unit_id, source, external_id)` when `unit_id` is set".
- **`provider` CHECK widening** — today: `airbnb | vrbo | booking | custom`. Spec also lists `gcal` (Google Calendar); cd-1ai collapses `gcal`/`generic` to `custom` as a stopgap so the CHECK doesn't fire.

## Technical context

- **Spec**: `docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md` §"iCal feed" — full field list.
- **Code**: `app/adapters/db/stays/models.py` (IcalFeed v1 slice).
- **Stopgap callsite**: `app/domain/stays/ical_service.py` (collapses gcal→custom at `_to_db_provider`; drops `last_error` into audit only).

## Implementation notes

- New alembic migration adds the four columns (+ extends CHECK).
- `poll_cadence` NOT NULL with default `*/15 * * * *`.
- `last_error` nullable text.
- `unit_id` nullable FK to `unit.id` (ON DELETE SET NULL) — feeds outlive unit churn.
- Extend `_PROVIDER_VALUES` tuple with `gcal`.
- Update cd-1ai service: remove the `_to_db_provider` gcal→custom collapse; persist `last_error` on probe failure; drop the `_LAST_ERROR_HINT` sentinel.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Alembic migration lands against sqlite + postgres
- [ ] ORM model carries all four new columns
- [ ] `provider` CHECK admits `gcal`
- [ ] Domain service persists `last_error` on probe failure
- [ ] `mypy --strict` passes
- [ ] Migration has a rollback test

## Test plan

```
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_stays.py tests/integration/test_db_stays.py tests/unit/domain/stays/test_ical_service.py -xvs
```

title: feat(api/me): GET /me/workspaces + /users/{id}/magic_link + /users/{id}/reset_passkey
description:
Spec `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §Auth + §"Users" pins three routes that have no router today:

```
GET    /api/v1/me/workspaces                     # switcher payload (session-user + their workspace grants)
POST   /users/{id}/magic_link                    # owner/manager re-mails a magic-link for a user
POST   /users/{id}/reset_passkey                 # owner-initiated worker passkey reset (§03)
```

Owner/manager guard + audit rows:
- `/users/{id}/magic_link`: `users.edit_profile_other` action, writes `user.magic_link.issued`.
- `/users/{id}/reset_passkey`: sends TWO magic links — the enrolment link to the worker AND a non-consumable notification copy to the owner (§03 "Owner-initiated worker passkey reset"). Writes `user.reset_passkey.initiated`.

The `GET /me/workspaces` shape overlaps with `GET /auth/me`'s `available_workspaces` — confirm whether this should be the same payload extracted into its own endpoint or a richer one (with `last_seen_at`, `current_role`).

Parent: cd-rpxd

title: feat(api/identity): GET /invites/{token} introspect + URL-shape alignment on accept
description:
Spec `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §Auth pins:
```
GET  /api/v1/invites/{token}            # pre-accept introspection
POST /api/v1/invites/{token}/accept     # token in path
```

Current `app/api/v1/auth/invite.py` implements:
```
POST /api/v1/invite/accept              # token in body
```

Gap:
- GET introspect is missing entirely (mocks/web/src/pages/auth/AcceptInvitePage needs to render inviter / workspace / grants before the user clicks Accept).
- URL-shape divergence: singular `/invite` + body-carried token vs spec's plural `/invites/{token}` path-carried token.

Resolution approach (pick one; prefer the first):
1. Add a second router mounting the plural `/invites` shape + GET introspect; keep the singular `/invite/accept` alive for back-compat during the SPA cutover; deprecate it in a follow-up.
2. Spec-patch `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §Auth to formalise the singular body-carried token (only acceptable if there is a genuine reason the URL shape chose token-in-body).

Parent: cd-rpxd

title: feat(api/email-change): bare-host email self-service (change_request / verify / revert)
description:
Spec `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §Auth pins three bare-host routes (§03 "Self-service email change"):

```
POST /api/v1/me/email/change_request
POST /api/v1/auth/email/verify
POST /api/v1/auth/email/revert
```

None exist today — the magic-link purpose `n_confirm` is wired in the domain but no HTTP router consumes it. Blocks cd-rpxd acceptance criterion #4 (email-change rejects PATs / agent tokens with 403 `forbidden`) which depends on the routes existing first.

Implementation notes:
- Passkey session only: reject PAT / delegated / agent tokens with 403 `forbidden` at the auth dep edge (§03).
- 72h revert TTL via magic-link `n_revert` purpose (spec §03 "Self-service email change").
- Mails notice-to-old on change_request, confirmation-to-new on verify.
- Audit rows: `email.change_requested`, `email.change_verified`, `email.change_reverted`.

Parent: cd-rpxd

title: test(tenant): register llm + places + assignment domain methods in repository parity gate
description:
## Problem

`tests/tenant/test_repository_parity.py::TestRepositoryParityGate::test_every_method_covered_or_opted_out` fails on `main`. The gate sweeps `app.domain` for ctx-taking public functions and asserts each appears in either `COVERED_METHODS` or `tests/tenant/_optouts.REPOSITORY_METHOD_OPTOUTS`.

The following methods are discovered but unaccounted for (caught while running cd-kqa8 self-review on cd-7am7; my own completion methods were added to `COVERED_METHODS` to prevent further regression — this task covers the rest):

- app.domain.llm.budget.check_budget
- app.domain.llm.budget.record_usage
- app.domain.llm.budget.refresh_aggregate
- app.domain.llm.budget.warm_start_aggregate
- app.domain.llm.router.resolve_model
- app.domain.llm.router.resolve_primary
- app.domain.llm.usage_recorder.record
- app.domain.places.property_service.create_property
- app.domain.places.property_service.get_property
- app.domain.places.property_service.list_properties
- app.domain.places.property_service.soft_delete_property
- app.domain.places.property_service.update_property
- app.domain.tasks.assignment.assign_task
- app.domain.tasks.assignment.availability_for
- app.domain.tasks.assignment.reassign_task
- app.domain.tasks.assignment.unassign_task

Most likely all are covered by the ORM-filter seam (each method takes a `WorkspaceContext` and runs SELECTs scoped on `ctx.workspace_id`), but each needs an explicit audit before being added to `COVERED_METHODS`. Methods that are genuinely cross-workspace (e.g. `router.resolve_model` if it reads global model config) belong in `REPOSITORY_METHOD_OPTOUTS` with a justification.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each of the 16 listed methods is added to either `COVERED_METHODS` (with a comment naming the originating Beads task) or `REPOSITORY_METHOD_OPTOUTS` (with a one-line justification).
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/tenant/test_repository_parity.py -x -q` passes.
- [ ] No method appears in both sets.

## Test plan
`uv run pytest tests/tenant/test_repository_parity.py -x -q`

title: feat(worker): wire llm.budget.refresh_aggregate as 60s APScheduler job (cd-irng follow-up)
description:
## Problem / goal
cd-irng shipped `app.domain.llm.budget.refresh_aggregate(session, ctx, clock)` — a 60 s worker tick that re-sums the last 30 days of `llm_usage` and writes the result back to `budget_ledger.spent_cents`. The function is implemented and unit-tested, but it is not registered in `app/worker/scheduler.py::register_jobs`. That means the cached aggregate only advances on the `record_usage` post-flight bump path. Today that is OK (every write bumps the ledger atomically) but the acceptance criterion "Aggregate `workspace_usage.cost_30d_cents` refreshed by the background worker every 60 s" from cd-irng is unverified in production.

The 60 s refresh also reconciles drift from:
- Calls that write `llm_usage` but crash before bumping the ledger.
- Cap/window edits where the ledger's `period_start/period_end` need to roll forward without a concurrent call triggering `refresh_aggregate` itself.
- Warm-starts on workers that were offline when an out-of-window row aged out of the 30-day window.

## Technical context
- Spec: docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md §"Workspace usage budget" §"Meter": "Aggregate `workspace_usage.cost_30d_cents` refreshed by the background worker every 60 s".
- Spec: docs/specs/01-architecture.md §"Worker" — APScheduler-based worker wrapped by `app/worker/scheduler.py::wrap_job`.
- Existing pattern: `GENERATOR_JOB_ID` (hourly cron fan-out placeholder), `IDEMPOTENCY_SWEEP_JOB_ID` (daily cron), `HEARTBEAT_JOB_ID` (30 s interval). The LLM budget refresh follows the same interval-trigger + per-workspace fan-out pattern.

## Implementation notes
- Add a `LLM_BUDGET_REFRESH_JOB_ID` constant + `IntervalTrigger(seconds=60)` registration in `register_jobs`.
- Body iterates every active workspace (same fan-out pattern as `GENERATOR_JOB_ID` will have once cd-p5's follow-up lands), builds a system-actor `WorkspaceContext`, opens a per-workspace UoW, calls `refresh_aggregate`. Log fan-out summary at INFO (`event=llm.budget.refresh.tick`, `workspaces`, `total_cents`).
- Fan-out must be crash-safe: a broken workspace does not starve the others. `wrap_job`'s per-tick UoW already isolates sessions; the fan-out loop catches Exception per workspace and logs at WARNING without aborting.
- Consider coalesce=True and max_instances=1 so a slow refresh doesn't stack ticks.
- misfire_grace_time=90 — allow a one-tick late run (it's idempotent: second run rewrites the same sum) but a multi-tick-late run is a signal the scheduler is stuck.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `app/worker/scheduler.py::register_jobs` registers a 60 s interval job keyed `LLM_BUDGET_REFRESH_JOB_ID`.
- [ ] The body calls `refresh_aggregate` for every active workspace with a `budget_ledger` row.
- [ ] A workspace with no ledger row is logged at DEBUG and skipped (not an error — it's the seeding-bug signal `cd-tubi` tracks).
- [ ] Unit test under `tests/unit/worker/test_scheduler.py` asserts the job id is registered at the configured interval and survives re-registration (idempotent).
- [ ] Integration test under `tests/integration/test_worker_llm_budget.py` seeds two workspaces, writes one `llm_usage` row outside each workspace's current aggregate, triggers the job body manually, asserts both ledgers' `spent_cents` rewrite.

## Related
- cd-irng: shipped `refresh_aggregate` without the worker-wiring.
- cd-tubi: workspace-create seeds the ledger row the fan-out reads.
- cd-6bcl: `/api/v1/workspace/usage` reads the same cached aggregate.

title: feat(workspace/create): seed budget_ledger cap row in workspace-create handler (cd-irng follow-up)
description:
cd-irng shipped the rolling-30d budget envelope (check_budget, record_usage) but left the cap-seeding responsibility to callers because no workspace-create handler exists yet. Every fresh workspace currently has NO budget_ledger row, which makes check_budget fail closed with a WARNING ('llm.budget.ledger_missing') and refuse every LLM call.

## Technical context
- Spec: docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md §'Workspace usage budget' §'Cap' — 'This row is inserted in the same transaction as the workspace row.'
- Prod default: cap_cents = 500 (5.00 USD).
- Demo default: cap_cents = 10 (0.10 USD) — see §24 'Demo mode overrides'.
- Module: app/domain/llm/budget.py exports the ledger shape; the seed insert should call into a domain-workspace handler (once that lands) rather than reach into BudgetLedger directly from whatever signup / create flow wires it.

## Implementation notes
- A new app/domain/workspace/create.py (or equivalent) should take the workspace row + an environment-derived cap_cents default (CREWDAY_DEPLOYMENT_MODE or similar) and emit the BudgetLedger insert inline with the Workspace.
- Tests that today directly seed BudgetLedger (see tests/domain/llm/test_budget.py::_seed_ledger) should migrate to the new handler once it ships.
- Mirror workspace.quota_json.llm_budget_cents_30d for legacy readers per §11 'Cap'.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every fresh workspace has a budget_ledger row at end-of-create.
- Prod seed is 500 cents; demo seed is 10 cents, governed by the deployment-mode env var.
- cd-irng's test fixtures are updated to call the handler instead of _seed_ledger directly.

## Related
- cd-irng: shipped the budget envelope but not the seeding path.
- cd-xuk2: demo-mode overrides; needs the demo cap value here.

title: feat(api/me/avatar): bare-host POST/DELETE /api/v1/me/avatar
description:
## Problem / goal
The frontend already calls `POST /api/v1/me/avatar` and `DELETE
/api/v1/me/avatar` (see `app/web/src/components/AvatarEditor.tsx:144,159`),
and the underlying `users.avatar_blob_hash` column already exists
(`app/adapters/db/identity/models.py:95`), plus the LocalFsStorage
adapter is in place. There is just no router wired in the backend —
so the UI fails when a user tries to upload an avatar.

This task adds the bare-host router. It is deliberately split out
from cd-dv2 (employees service) because §05 is explicit: `POST
/me/avatar` is **self-only** and therefore lives on the bare host,
not under the workspace-scoped `/w/<slug>/…` tree.

## Technical context
- **Spec anchors**:
  - `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` §"Worker surface" — self-only
    rule for avatar upload.
  - `docs/specs/12-api.md` §`/me/avatar`.
- **Existing pieces (do not duplicate)**:
  - Column: `app/adapters/db/identity/models.py:95` — `avatar_blob_hash: Mapped[str | None]`.
  - Storage port: `app/adapters/storage/ports.py` (abstract interface).
  - Storage impl: `app/adapters/storage/localfs.py` (content-addressed
    LocalFsStorage with signed-URL support).
  - Frontend caller: `app/web/src/components/AvatarEditor.tsx:144,159`
    (POST + DELETE; response shape `{ avatar_url: string | null }`).
- **Where the router mounts**: `app/api/factory.py` — wire it at the
  **bare-host** tier (i.e. alongside other `/api/v1/me/…` routes,
  NOT under the workspace router). Do not attempt to mount under
  `/w/<slug>/`.

## Implementation notes
- **Limit**: enforce ≤ 2 MB on the uploaded body. Reject with `413
  Payload Too Large`. Check `Content-Length` first, then guard the
  streaming read to avoid OOM on bogus-length uploads.
- **Content-type**: only accept `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `image/webp`
  (confirm exact allowlist against spec §12). Reject others with
  `415 Unsupported Media Type`.
- **Hashing**: SHA-256 over the raw bytes; the digest IS the blob key
  (content-addressed). Store via `LocalFsStorage.put(hash, bytes)`.
- **DB update**: set `users.avatar_blob_hash = <new_hash>` and commit.
  **Do NOT delete the previous blob** — orphans are GC'd by a
  separate process (noted in spec). DELETE clears the column only;
  it does NOT delete the blob.
- **Response**: `{ "avatar_url": <signed_url> }` on POST;
  `{ "avatar_url": null }` on DELETE. Signed URL minted via the
  storage adapter's signing method.
- **Audit**: write `identity.avatar.updated` on POST;
  `identity.avatar.cleared` on DELETE (via `app/adapters/audit`).
- **Session cookie**: bare-host routes use the identity session cookie
  (no workspace context); enforce authenticated caller only — the
  target user IS the session user, no ID path param.
- **Integration test**: use `scripts/dev_login.py` to mint a cookie
  (see CLAUDE.md §"Session bootstrap").

## Depends on
Nothing — column + storage + frontend already exist; this task only
adds the missing router. Can land in parallel with Task D (cd-dv2).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /api/v1/me/avatar` accepts PNG/JPEG/WebP up to 2 MB,
      SHA-256 hashes the bytes, stores via LocalFsStorage, updates
      `users.avatar_blob_hash`, returns `{ "avatar_url": <signed> }`.
- [ ] Oversize body → `413 Payload Too Large`; `Content-Length`
      pre-check AND streaming guard.
- [ ] Non-image content-type → `415 Unsupported Media Type`.
- [ ] `DELETE /api/v1/me/avatar` clears `users.avatar_blob_hash`;
      returns `{ "avatar_url": null }`. Blob is NOT deleted.
- [ ] Replacing an avatar orphans the old blob (new hash stored; old
      blob remains on disk for GC).
- [ ] Audit rows: `identity.avatar.updated` on POST,
      `identity.avatar.cleared` on DELETE.
- [ ] Router is mounted at the bare-host tier in
      `app/api/factory.py` (NOT under `/w/<slug>/`).
- [ ] Integration test using `scripts/dev_login.py` for the session
      cookie passes.
- [ ] Unauthenticated caller → `401`.

## Test plan

### Automated
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/test_me_avatar.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_me_avatar.py -xvs
```

### Manual (against dev stack)
```bash
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build
cookie=$(docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml exec app-api \
  python -m scripts.dev_login --email me@dev.local --workspace smoke)

# POST: ≤ 2 MB PNG
curl -b "$cookie" -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/v1/me/avatar \
  -F file=@/path/to/avatar.png
# → 200, {"avatar_url": "..."}

# POST: > 2 MB → 413
head -c 3M < /dev/urandom > /tmp/big.bin
curl -b "$cookie" -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/v1/me/avatar \
  -F file=@/tmp/big.bin
# → 413

# POST: text/plain → 415
curl -b "$cookie" -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/v1/me/avatar \
  -H "Content-Type: text/plain" --data "hi"
# → 415

# DELETE
curl -b "$cookie" -X DELETE http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/v1/me/avatar
# → 200, {"avatar_url": null}
```

title: Self-review: feat(db/work_engagement): work_engagement table + migration + partial unique
description:

title: feat(db/work_engagement): work_engagement table + migration + partial unique
description:
## Problem / goal
`work_engagement` is referenced only as a string literal in
`app/adapters/db/workspace/models.py:65` (as the `user_workspace.source`
enum value), but no ORM class or migration exists. cd-dv2 (employees
service) cannot seed a pending engagement at invite-accept time, and
the §05 invariant "first user_work_role creates work_engagement if
missing" cannot be implemented without the table.

## Technical context
- **Spec anchor (canonical shape)**:
  `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`work_engagement` (lines 843-873).
  12 columns, partial unique on `(user_id, workspace_id) WHERE
  archived_on IS NULL`, FKs to `user`, `workspace`, `organization`,
  `pay_destination`.
- **Spec invariant**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` line
  152 — "creating the first user_work_role for a (user, workspace)
  creates the work_engagement row if missing".
- **Cross-reference**: `app/adapters/db/workspace/models.py:65` — the
  `user_workspace.source` enum literal that references this table
  conceptually.
- **Prior-art migration**:
  `migrations/versions/20260424_1500_f6a7b8c9d0e1_llm_assignment_extras_cd_u84y.py`
  (cd-u84y) — batch_alter_table / CheckConstraint / Index / FK
  CASCADE / JSON server_default style.
- **Alembic head at time of writing**: `f6a7b8c9d0e1`; this migration
  revises from Task A's (cd-5kv4) migration once that lands.

## Missing-prereq note (IMPORTANT — READ BEFORE IMPLEMENTING)
The `organization_id` and `pay_destination_id` FKs point at tables
that **do not yet exist** in the schema.

Decision for v1: **make those two columns NULLABLE with the FK
declarations deferred** — i.e. define the columns as nullable without
real FK constraints for now, and file a follow-up Beads task to add
the FKs + backfill semantics once `organization` and `pay_destination`
land. Call out the follow-up explicitly in the PR description.

Do NOT block this migration waiting on those tables; cd-dv2 only
needs the engagement row to exist for status tracking (pending /
active / archived), not for payroll wiring.

## Implementation notes
- Workspace-scoped table → tenancy filter auto-injects `workspace_id`.
- Partial unique `(user_id, workspace_id) WHERE archived_on IS NULL`:
  - Postgres: real partial index (`CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ... WHERE ...`).
  - SQLite: use the filtered-index idiom (SQLite supports partial
    indexes since 3.8.0). Alembic op: `op.create_index(..., unique=True,
    sqlite_where=sa.text("archived_on IS NULL"), postgresql_where=...)`.
  - Verify on BOTH backends with an explicit test (two active rows
    for same user+workspace → IntegrityError; one active + one
    archived → OK).
- FK to `users.id` CASCADE; FK to `workspaces.id` CASCADE.
- `archived_on` nullable TIMESTAMPTZ / ISO-8601 text.
- Add re-import idempotence.

## Depends on
- **cd-5kv4** (Task A: work_role + user_work_role) — the spec
  invariant "first user_work_role creates work_engagement if
  missing" requires the user_work_role table to exist.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `WorkEngagement` ORM class with 12 columns matching spec §02
      (org_id and pay_destination_id NULLABLE, no FK yet; everything
      else wired).
- [ ] Alembic migration upgrades cleanly from Task A's revision on
      SQLite AND Postgres.
- [ ] Round-trip: upgrade → downgrade → upgrade on both backends.
- [ ] Partial unique `(user_id, workspace_id) WHERE archived_on IS
      NULL` enforced on BOTH backends — covered by an explicit
      integrity test (see Implementation notes).
- [ ] Tenancy filter auto-injects `workspace_id`.
- [ ] Cross-workspace isolation test passes.
- [ ] `schema_parity` test green on both backends.
- [ ] Follow-up Beads task filed for the `organization_id` /
      `pay_destination_id` FK wiring (title prefixed `feat(db/…):
      add FK …`). Link in PR description.

## Test plan

### Automated
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/adapters/db/test_work_engagement.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/db/test_migrations.py::test_schema_parity -xvs
```

### Partial-unique verification (both backends)
```python
# Insert active engagement for (u1, w1) → OK
# Insert second active engagement for (u1, w1) → IntegrityError
# Archive first, then insert new active for (u1, w1) → OK
```

title: feat(db/work_role): work_role + user_work_role tables + migration
description:
## Problem / goal
The `work_role` and `user_work_role` tables are referenced throughout
the code and specs, but no ORM class or Alembic migration exists.
This is a hard blocker for cd-dv2 (employees service), cd-31c (leave
requests), schedule backup-list validation (app/domain/tasks/schedules.py),
and the §05 invariant that "creating the first user_work_role for a
(user, workspace) creates the work_engagement row if missing".

Land the DB layer (ORM + migration + tenancy plumbing) so the
dependent feature work can build on top.

## Technical context
- **Spec anchors**:
  - `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`work_role`, §`user_work_role`
    (canonical column shape, constraints, indexes).
  - `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` lines 82-114 ("Work roles"),
    line 152 (invariant: first user_work_role creates the
    work_engagement row).
- **Existing code references to honour**:
  - `app/domain/tasks/schedules.py:42,122,147` — validator stub that
    expects the real `user_work_role` table.
  - The stale comment referencing `cd-65kn` inside
    `app/domain/tasks/schedules.py` is wrong (cd-65kn is a
    frontend-templates task). Update the comment to point at THIS
    task id once created.
- **Prior-art migration template**:
  `migrations/versions/20260424_1500_f6a7b8c9d0e1_llm_assignment_extras_cd_u84y.py`
  (cd-u84y) — shows the expected style: `batch_alter_table`,
  `CheckConstraint`, `Index`, FK `CASCADE`, JSON `server_default`,
  SQLite + Postgres parity.
- **Current alembic head**: `f6a7b8c9d0e1` — the new migration revises
  from that.
- **ORM location**: add models under `app/adapters/db/workspace/models.py`
  (or a new `work_role.py` module in the same package) alongside
  existing workspace-scoped models; follow the tenancy base class
  pattern (workspace_id + tenancy filter).

## Implementation notes
- Both tables are workspace-scoped → tenancy filter auto-injects
  `workspace_id`; re-export from the workspace models registry so
  `select(WorkRole)` / `select(UserWorkRole)` gets the filter for
  free.
- FKs: `work_role.workspace_id → workspaces.id` (ON DELETE CASCADE);
  `user_work_role.user_id → users.id`, `user_work_role.work_role_id
  → work_role.id`, `user_work_role.workspace_id → workspaces.id`
  (all CASCADE).
- Indexes per spec §02 (e.g. `(workspace_id, name)` unique for
  work_role; `(user_id, work_role_id)` unique for user_work_role — confirm
  in spec before writing).
- Re-import idempotence: importing the module twice must not
  double-register tables (follow existing pattern).
- Do NOT wire the work_engagement auto-creation invariant here — that
  belongs to Task B (work_engagement) + cd-dv2 (employees service).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `WorkRole` and `UserWorkRole` ORM classes exist, registered with
      the workspace metadata, with columns + constraints + indexes
      matching spec §02.
- [ ] Alembic migration upgrades cleanly from head `f6a7b8c9d0e1` on
      SQLite AND Postgres.
- [ ] Round-trip: `alembic upgrade head → alembic downgrade -1 →
      alembic upgrade head` succeeds on both backends with no
      residual artefacts.
- [ ] `schema_parity` test green on both backends.
- [ ] Tenancy filter auto-injects `workspace_id` for both tables
      (covered by an explicit unit test).
- [ ] Cross-workspace isolation test: inserting a `WorkRole` in
      workspace A is invisible to a `select(WorkRole)` from
      workspace B's context.
- [ ] Re-importing the module is idempotent (no `InvalidRequestError:
      Table already defined`).
- [ ] The stale `cd-65kn` comment reference in
      `app/domain/tasks/schedules.py` is updated to point at this
      task's id.

## Test plan

### Automated
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/adapters/db/test_work_role.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/db/test_migrations.py::test_schema_parity -xvs
CREWDAY_DB_URL=postgresql://... uv run pytest tests/integration/db/test_migrations.py -xvs
```

### Manual round-trip
```bash
# SQLite
rm -f /tmp/crewday-mig.sqlite
CREWDAY_DB_URL=sqlite:////tmp/crewday-mig.sqlite alembic upgrade head
CREWDAY_DB_URL=sqlite:////tmp/crewday-mig.sqlite alembic downgrade -1
CREWDAY_DB_URL=sqlite:////tmp/crewday-mig.sqlite alembic upgrade head

# Postgres (dev stack)
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml exec app-api alembic upgrade head
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml exec app-api alembic downgrade -1
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml exec app-api alembic upgrade head
```

title: feat(db/llm): llm_provider + llm_model + llm_provider_model registry so ModelPick.api_model_id can resolve to provider wire names
description:
## Problem / goal

``app/domain/llm/router.py`` ships ``ModelPick`` with both
``provider_model_id`` and ``api_model_id`` populated from the
same ``ModelAssignment.model_id`` ULID because the deployment-
scope registry tables (``llm_provider``, ``llm_model``,
``llm_provider_model``) from §11 "Provider / model /
provider-model registry" have not landed yet. Today's value is
a soft reference; the adapter can't distinguish the provider-
specific wire name (e.g. ``google/gemma-3-27b-it`` on
OpenRouter vs a native vendor adapter's own string) from the
abstract model identity.

Land the three registry tables and update ``_to_pick`` (one
JOIN through ``llm_provider_model``) so
``ModelPick.api_model_id`` carries the provider's wire name and
``ModelPick.provider_model_id`` resolves to the real
``llm_provider_model.id``. The rest of the resolver stays
untouched.

## Technical context

- Router stub: ``app/domain/llm/router.py`` ``ModelPick`` docstring
  (~line 116) and ``_to_pick`` (~line 313).
- Spec: ``docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md:603`` (``llm_model``),
  ``:653`` (``llm_provider_model``).
- Current model assignment: ``app/adapters/db/llm/models.py``
  ``ModelAssignment.model_id`` — soft ULID, will become a real
  FK to ``llm_provider_model.id``.
- Dependent task: cd-6bcl (REST API for model assignments) also
  needs to surface the provider wire name.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Alembic migration adds ``llm_provider``, ``llm_model``,
      ``llm_provider_model`` per §11 schemas.
- [ ] ``ModelAssignment.model_id`` becomes an FK to
      ``llm_provider_model.id`` (deployment scope).
- [ ] ``_to_pick`` joins through the registry; ``api_model_id``
      is the provider wire name, ``provider_model_id`` is
      ``llm_provider_model.id``.
- [ ] Router test ``provider_model_id != api_model_id`` when the
      fixture wires a distinct wire name.
- [ ] No behavioural change to the resolver beyond those two
      fields.

## Cross-cutting

- ``docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md`` already documents the
  target schemas; no changes needed beyond what the migration
  lands.
- Seeds (``app/fixtures/``) must pre-create at least one
  ``llm_provider_model`` row for ``chat.manager`` so existing
  tests keep passing.

title: spec-drift(llm): reconcile llm_capability_inheritance scoping (deployment vs workspace)
description:
## Problem

Spec `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Capability inheritance" (lines ~874-878) pins `llm_capability_inheritance` with:
```
capability         text PK         -- child
inherits_from      text
created_at
```
This is **deployment-scoped** — no `workspace_id`, `capability` is the PK.

cd-u84y implemented the DB with a **workspace-scoped** shape:
- ULID `id` PK (matching cd-yesa harmonisation)
- `workspace_id` FK `workspace.id` CASCADE
- Unique `(workspace_id, capability)`

Rationale for the implementer's choice: the spec text around that block (§"Capability inheritance" narrative, and the `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Model assignment" overrides context) suggests operators should be able to tie their own capabilities without leaking into a sibling workspace's chain. The §20 glossary also calls `llm_capability_inheritance` an override row — implying per-workspace.

## What needs to happen

Pick one:
1. Endorse the workspace-scoped shape → update §11 line ~874 to match: ULID PK, workspace_id FK, unique (workspace_id, capability). Add a sentence to the "Capability defaults (seeds)" block explaining that deployment-level seeds live elsewhere (or that the workspace edge composes over the deployment seed at read time, which is what cd-u84y's model docstring says).
2. Endorse the deployment-scoped shape → undo cd-u84y's workspace scoping; the resolver composes across deployment edges only.

Paired with cd-u84y (implemented) + cd-k0qf (resolver — needs to know the final shape before it can compose).

## Context
- cd-u84y landing commit: pending commiter (cd-3hku self-review → commiter pipeline).
- Referenced in `app/adapters/db/llm/models.py::LlmCapabilityInheritance` docstring.
- Impacts cd-k0qf (resolver) — composing workspace edges over deployment seeds is the workspace-scoped read; a pure deployment-scoped shape makes the composition trivial.

Close by updating the spec in the chosen direction; code is already live on the workspace side.

title: feat(db/messaging): email_opt_out + email_delivery tables
description:
## Problem / goal
Spec §10 requires per-user per-category unsubscribe
(\`email_opt_out\`) and per-message delivery tracking
(\`email_delivery\`). Neither table exists today — \`cd-pjm\`
scoped itself to notification / push_token / digest_record /
chat_channel / chat_message. Downstream:

- **cd-y1ge** (notification service fanout) has an acceptance
  criterion *"Email path skipped when recipient has matching
  `email_opt_out` row"* that cannot be satisfied without the
  table.
- **cd-f0ue** (daily digest worker) needs
  \`email_delivery.first_error\` / \`retry_count\` /
  \`delivery_state\` to drive its retry loop.
- **cd-48k**-adjacent mailer callers need a place to record
  \`provider_message_id\` for bounce-reply correlation.

## Technical context
- **Spec**:
  - \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"email_opt_out"
    (line 90-106),
    §"Delivery tracking" (line 108-126).
  - \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"Comms" bounded context
    (line 296) — \`digest_run, email_delivery, email_opt_out,
    push_subscription, chat_thread, chat_message,
    notification\`.
- **Existing pattern to mirror**:
  - \`app/adapters/db/messaging/models.py\` — \`Notification\`,
    \`ChatChannel\`, \`ChatMessage\` for column style, CHECK
    constraints, index naming (\`ix_\*\`, \`ck_\*\`, \`fk_\*\`,
    \`pk_\*\`), FK CASCADE conventions.
  - \`app/adapters/db/messaging/__init__.py\` — tenancy registry
    registration (lines 57-64).
  - \`migrations/versions/20260424_1200_c3d4e5f6a7b8_messaging_cd_pjm.py\`
    — style for CheckConstraint, Index, FK CASCADE syntax.

## Implementation notes

### \`email_opt_out\`
Columns (per spec §10):

| field          | type                 | notes                                           |
|----------------|----------------------|-------------------------------------------------|
| id             | \`String(26)\` PK    | ULID; mirror \`Notification.id\`                |
| workspace_id   | \`String(26)\` FK CASCADE to workspace | NOT NULL               |
| user_id        | \`String(26)\` FK CASCADE to user      | NOT NULL               |
| category       | \`String(120)\`      | matches \`email_delivery.template_key\` family  |
| opted_out_at   | \`DateTime(timezone=True)\` | NOT NULL; default \`utcnow\`             |
| source         | \`String(20)\`       | CHECK IN (\`unsubscribe_link, profile, admin\`) |

Constraints / indexes:
- Unique index \`uq_email_opt_out_user_category\` on
  \`(workspace_id, user_id, category)\` — one row per user+category.
- Non-unique index \`ix_email_opt_out_workspace_user\` on
  \`(workspace_id, user_id)\` for per-user lookups.

### \`email_delivery\`
Columns (per spec §10):

| field                  | type                   | notes                                     |
|------------------------|------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| id                     | \`String(26)\` PK      | ULID                                      |
| workspace_id           | \`String(26)\` FK CASCADE to workspace | NOT NULL          |
| to_person_id           | \`String(26)\`         | soft reference (plain String, no FK — §10 "to_person_id" can be a client_user not yet in \`user\`) |
| to_email_at_send       | \`String(320)\`        | NOT NULL; snapshot at send time           |
| template_key           | \`String(120)\`        | NOT NULL                                  |
| context_snapshot_json  | \`JSON\`               | NOT NULL; \`server_default='{}'\`         |
| sent_at                | \`DateTime(timezone=True)\` | nullable — set on actual dispatch    |
| provider_message_id    | \`String(255)\`        | nullable; unique per workspace when set   |
| delivery_state         | \`String(20)\`         | NOT NULL; CHECK IN (\`queued, sent, delivered, bounced, failed\`) |
| first_error            | \`Text\`               | nullable                                  |
| retry_count            | \`Integer\`            | NOT NULL default 0                        |
| inbound_linkage        | \`String(255)\`        | nullable; reply-tracking token            |
| created_at             | \`DateTime(timezone=True)\` | NOT NULL; default \`utcnow\` — row created when queued |

Constraints / indexes:
- CHECK \`ck_email_delivery_state\` on \`delivery_state\`.
- Non-unique index \`ix_email_delivery_workspace_person_sent\` on
  \`(workspace_id, to_person_id, sent_at)\` for audit.
- Non-unique index \`ix_email_delivery_workspace_provider_msgid\`
  on \`(workspace_id, provider_message_id)\` for bounce-reply
  correlation.
- Non-unique index \`ix_email_delivery_workspace_state_sent\` on
  \`(workspace_id, delivery_state, sent_at)\` for retry scheduling.
- NO enum type (portable). NO FK on \`to_person_id\`.

### Registration
- \`app/adapters/db/messaging/__init__.py\`: register both tables
  with the tenancy registry (both workspace-scoped).
- Idempotent re-import preserved (registry is set-backed).

### Migration
- Filename: \`20260424_1300_d4e5f6a7b8c9_email_opt_out_delivery_cd_<ID>.py\`.
- \`revision = \"d4e5f6a7b8c9\"\`, \`down_revision = \"c3d4e5f6a7b8\"\`
  (messaging_cd_pjm).
- Upgrade adds both tables (with indexes + CHECKs + FKs); downgrade
  drops them in reverse FK order.
- No server-side enum types — CHECK only, for SQLite portability.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Alembic \`upgrade head\` succeeds on SQLite.
- [ ] Alembic \`upgrade head\` succeeds on Postgres
      (\`CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres\`).
- [ ] \`downgrade -1\` drops both tables cleanly; \`upgrade head\`
      after downgrade yields identical shape.
- [ ] \`EmailOptOut\` and \`EmailDelivery\` are registered in the
      tenancy registry; a query not scoped to a workspace is
      auto-filtered or raises \`TenantFilterMissing\` (mirror the
      cd-pjm test pattern).
- [ ] CHECK rejects \`email_opt_out.source = 'other'\` and
      \`email_delivery.delivery_state = 'retrying'\` (one invalid
      case per table, on both backends).
- [ ] Unique index \`uq_email_opt_out_user_category\` rejects a
      second insert for the same \`(workspace_id, user_id,
      category)\`.
- [ ] All three documented indexes exist on \`email_delivery\`
      (verified via \`inspect(engine).get_indexes(table)\`).
- [ ] Unit + integration tests cover CRUD + cross-workspace
      isolation for both tables.
- [ ] \`tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py\` stays green.
- [ ] \`test_reimport_is_idempotent\` (from cd-6m5) still passes
      after extending the package.

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_messaging.py tests/integration/test_db_messaging.py \
  tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py -x -q
CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_messaging.py \
  tests/integration/test_db_messaging.py tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py -x -q
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. \`uv run alembic upgrade head\`
2. \`uv run alembic downgrade -1\` — both tables gone.
3. \`uv run alembic upgrade head\` — back to the same shape; hash
   fingerprint stable.
close_reason: Shipped: email_opt_out + email_delivery tables + alembic migration d4e5f6a7b8c9. Both tenancy-scoped; portable CHECK constraints on source / delivery_state; unique index on (workspace_id, user_id, category); delivery.to_person_id stays a soft reference per spec §10 (client_user may not yet be in user); all spec-required indexes landed; SQLite + Postgres green; 107/107 tests.

title: Self-review: feat(api/admin): GET /admin/api/v1/me + /me/admins (minimal deployment admin surface)

title: feat(api/admin): GET /admin/api/v1/me + /me/admins (minimal deployment admin surface)
description:
## Problem / goal
\`mocks/web/src/layouts/AdminLayout.tsx\` and \`AgentSidebar\`
reference \`/admin/api/v1/me\`, but the endpoint doesn't exist —
the SPA admin shell 404-loops on load. \`/auth/me\` already returns
an \`is_deployment_admin\` field that has nowhere to be populated
from until the schema/auth layer lands (cd-wchi, cd-xgmu). This
task is the **minimum viable deployment admin surface** that
unblocks the SPA shell and exercises the new auth dep end-to-end.

## Technical context
- **Key files**:
  \`app/api/admin/__init__.py\` (currently empty router),
  \`app/api/v1/auth/me.py\` (populate \`is_deployment_admin\`),
  \`mocks/web/src/layouts/AdminLayout.tsx\` (consumer — read-only
  reference),
  \`app/api/factory.py\` (router mount — should already be at
  \`/admin/api/v1\`).
- **Spec anchors**:
  - \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Admin surface":
    \`GET /admin/api/v1/me\` and \`GET /admin/api/v1/me/admins\`.

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/api/admin/me.py\`:
  - \`GET /admin/api/v1/me\` — returns
    \`{user: {id,email,name,avatar_url}, capabilities: [...]}\`.
    Uses the Task C (cd-xgmu) \`DeploymentContext\` to collect
    capabilities from the caller's role_grants + group
    memberships.
  - \`GET /admin/api/v1/me/admins\` — returns the deployment admin
    listing: rows from \`role_grants\` with
    \`scope_kind='deployment'\`, plus the owners + managers
    deployment groups (if those groups exist in cd-wchi scope; if
    they're deferred to a later task, return empty \`groups\` and
    document that in a follow-up comment on this task).
- Both routes protected by the \`current_deployment_admin_principal\`
  dep (cd-xgmu). 404 for non-admin callers per spec.
- Writes to \`audit_log\` are **reads**, not mutations — no audit
  needed for happy-path calls. If an admin tampers (e.g. replays a
  malformed token), the error path should emit an
  \`admin.auth.denied\` deployment-audit row
  (using cd-kgcc's writer).
- **Update \`/auth/me\`**: populate \`is_deployment_admin\` by
  calling \`is_deployment_admin(session, user_id)\` from cd-wchi.
  Current frontend contract: \`app/web/src/types/me.ts:29\`.
- **OpenAPI**: tag \`admin\`; operation_ids \`admin.me.read\`,
  \`admin.admins.list\`. After the route lands, run
  \`/update-openapi\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`GET /admin/api/v1/me\` with a deployment-admin session →
      200 + \`{user, capabilities}\`; non-admin → 404; unauth → 404.
- [ ] \`GET /admin/api/v1/me/admins\` returns members of the
      deployment admin grants + groups.
- [ ] \`GET /api/v1/auth/me\` correctly sets \`is_deployment_admin\`
      for a user with a deployment grant (regression).
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`admin\`.
- [ ] OpenAPI updated (\`/update-openapi\`).
- [ ] mypy strict + ruff clean.
- [ ] SPA admin shell at \`http://127.0.0.1:8100/admin\` loads
      without 404 on \`/admin/api/v1/me\` (manual check via
      \`scripts/dev-login.sh\` + browser).

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/admin/test_me.py \
  tests/integration/api/admin/test_me_routes.py \
  tests/unit/api/v1/auth/test_me.py -xvs
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. \`docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build\`
2. \`scripts/dev-login.sh admin@dev.local smoke --role owner\` →
   session cookie.
3. Promote user to deployment admin via a one-liner.
4. Visit \`http://127.0.0.1:8100/admin\` — shell loads without
   console 404s.
5. \`curl -b "$cookie" http://127.0.0.1:8100/admin/api/v1/me\` →
   200 JSON.
6. Same curl without the grant → 404.

## Dependencies
Depends on:
- **cd-wchi** — \`is_deployment_admin\` + scope_kind column.
- **cd-kgcc** — deployment audit writer (for the error-path audit
  row).
- **cd-xgmu** — admin auth dep.

title: feat(api/deps): deployment admin auth dep (session + deployment-scope PAT → principal)
description:
## Problem / goal
There is no FastAPI dep that resolves a caller on
\`/admin/api/v1/*\` to *"is this a deployment admin?"*. Spec §12
requires two principals: (1) a passkey session whose user has an
active \`scope_kind='deployment'\` role_grant, (2) a
deployment-scoped \`api_token\`. Currently \`/admin/api/v1\` sits
in SKIP_PATHS and no tenancy middleware runs there. cd-jlms and
every downstream admin route need this seam before they can land.

## Technical context
- **Key files**:
  \`app/api/deps.py\` (existing dep shape — \`current_workspace_context\`),
  \`app/api/middleware/\` (where SKIP_PATHS lives),
  \`app/auth/session.py\` / \`app/auth/tokens.py\` (session + PAT
  validation),
  \`app/tenancy/context.py\` (pattern for WorkspaceContext).
- **Spec anchors**:
  - \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Admin surface" — auth principals,
    404 (not 403) semantics, scoped-token scope family
    \`deployment:*\`, mixed-scope 422.
  - \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` — session + PAT plumbing.
- **Prior art**: workspace-scoped dep \`current_workspace_context\`
  in \`app/api/deps.py\`.

## Implementation notes
- Add \`app/tenancy/deployment.py\` (or similar):
  \`DeploymentContext(principal, user_id, capabilities)\`
  dataclass. Parallel shape to \`WorkspaceContext\`.
- Add \`app/api/admin/deps.py\`:
  - \`current_deployment_admin_principal(...)\` reads
    \`__Host-crewday_session\` → session → user →
    \`role_grants where scope_kind='deployment' AND active\`. Uses
    cd-wchi's \`is_deployment_admin\`.
  - Alt path: \`Authorization: Bearer <token>\` with one or more
    scopes in the \`deployment:*\` family
    (\`deployment.llm:{read,write}\`, \`deployment.usage:read\`,
    \`deployment.workspaces:{read,write,archive}\`,
    \`deployment.signup:{read,write}\`,
    \`deployment.settings:write\`, \`deployment.audit:read\`).
  - Return **404** (not 403) on unauthenticated / non-admin per
    spec: *"the surface does not advertise its own existence to
    tenants"*.
  - **422 \`deployment_scope_conflict\`** if a token mixes
    \`deployment:*\` with workspace scopes.
  - Delegated tokens minted by a deployment admin inherit
    deployment grants — honour that path.
- Do **not** remove \`/admin/api/v1\` from SKIP_PATHS yet if the
  workspace-tenancy middleware would incorrectly try to resolve a
  slug; instead the new dep runs per-route.
- Provide a throwaway \`GET /admin/api/v1/_ping\` route guarded by
  this dep purely for integration tests (remove / gate under debug
  before Task D lands, or promote to \`/me\` in cd-7e7g).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Unit tests:
  - session → admin principal → success;
  - session → non-admin → 404;
  - invalid session → 404;
  - scoped token with \`deployment.llm:read\` → success for a route
    requiring that scope;
  - scoped token with only workspace scopes → 404;
  - token mixing workspace + \`deployment:*\` scopes → 422
    \`deployment_scope_conflict\`.
- [ ] Integration test: \`/admin/api/v1/_ping\` (or equivalent)
      returns 200 for the two valid principals and 404 for all
      others.
- [ ] mypy strict clean; ruff clean.
- [ ] New error code \`deployment_scope_conflict\` registered in the
      shared error-envelope catalog.

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/admin/test_deps.py \
  tests/integration/api/admin/test_auth.py -xvs
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. \`docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build\`
2. \`scripts/dev-login.sh me@dev.local smoke\` — session cookie.
   Hit \`/admin/api/v1/_ping\` without a deployment grant → 404.
3. Add a deployment grant via a one-liner DB script (Task A helper),
   re-request → 200.
4. Mint a scoped token with a workspace scope and
   \`deployment.llm:read\` → request returns 422
   \`deployment_scope_conflict\`.

## Dependencies
Depends on: **cd-wchi** (\`is_deployment_admin\` helper + scope_kind
column required to resolve the session-principal arm).

title: feat(audit): deployment-scoped audit writer (workspace_id NULL + scope_kind='deployment')
description:
## Problem / goal
\`app/audit/__init__.py:29\` \`write_audit()\` requires a
\`WorkspaceContext\`; \`app/adapters/db/audit/models.py:52\` has
\`audit_log.workspace_id NOT NULL\`. Spec §12 "Deployment audit"
promises *every admin mutation writes a deployment-scoped audit
row* exposed by \`GET /admin/api/v1/audit\`, but the writer has no
surface for it today.

\`cd-t2su\` tackles the adjacent identity-scoped PAT mint/revoke
seam (workspace_id NULL + actor_kind='user'); this task is the
deployment-scope sibling with an explicit \`scope_kind\` tag so the
two rows can be distinguished cleanly.

## Technical context
- **Key files**:
  \`app/adapters/db/audit/models.py\` (\`AuditLog\`, line 52 — NOT NULL
  workspace_id), \`app/audit/__init__.py:29\` (\`write_audit\`
  signature).
- **Consumers**: any admin mutation handler that lands under
  cd-jlms, cd-6bcl admin-scoped branches, cd-rpxd where the identity
  router mints deployment-scope PATs.
- **Spec anchors**:
  - \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Admin surface" → Deployment audit
    feed \`GET /admin/api/v1/audit\`.
  - \`docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md\` §"Audit log".
  - \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` — \`audit_log\` entity.
- **Related task**: \`cd-t2su\` (PAT mint/revoke audit seam). Align
  \`scope_kind\` wording between the two.

## Implementation notes
- **Alembic migration**:
  - Add \`audit_log.scope_kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace'\`
    with CHECK \`scope_kind IN ('workspace','deployment')\`.
  - Backfill existing rows to 'workspace'.
  - Relax \`workspace_id\` to NULLABLE with CHECK
    \`(scope_kind='deployment' AND workspace_id IS NULL)
     OR (scope_kind='workspace' AND workspace_id IS NOT NULL)\`.
  - Add index \`(scope_kind, created_at DESC)\` to back the
    \`/admin/api/v1/audit\` feed.
- **Writer seam** (preferred — one seam):
  - Extend \`write_audit(ctx: WorkspaceContext | DeploymentContext, ...)\`
    so deployment callers pass a \`DeploymentContext\` (see Task C
    cd-5tzf). The row is persisted with
    \`workspace_id=NULL, scope_kind='deployment'\`.
  - Keep the existing \`WorkspaceContext\` call sites unchanged.
- **Query isolation**: the workspace-scoped audit listing must
  exclude \`scope_kind='deployment'\` rows (prevent deployment audit
  leaking into tenant surfaces). Audit of this: add a regression
  test that seeds one row of each scope and verifies both queries.
- **Schema parity**: update \`tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py\`
  or the equivalent to cover the new column.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Migration applies forward and backward on SQLite **and**
      Postgres.
- [ ] \`write_audit(ctx=DeploymentContext(...))\` persists a row
      with \`workspace_id IS NULL\` and \`scope_kind='deployment'\`.
- [ ] Existing workspace-scoped audit tests remain green.
- [ ] A query scoped to \`workspace_id=X\` does not surface any
      deployment rows (regression test).
- [ ] \`tests/integration/test_audit_writer.py\` covers both scopes
      (happy path, scope isolation, required-fields validation).
- [ ] Schema-parity test green.

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_audit_writer.py tests/unit/audit/ \
  tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py -xvs
CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres uv run pytest \
  tests/integration/test_audit_writer.py \
  tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py -xvs
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. Seed one workspace audit row + one deployment audit row.
2. \`GET /w/<slug>/api/v1/audit\` returns only the workspace row.
3. (After Task D lands) \`GET /admin/api/v1/audit\` returns only the
   deployment row.

## Dependencies
Depends on: **cd-wchi** (scope_kind modelling stays consistent
between \`role_grants\` and \`audit_log\`; migrations likely share
a helper).

title: feat(db/authz): deployment-scoped role_grants (scope_kind column + admin-scope invariants)
description:
## Problem / goal
`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"Admin surface" requires authorisation
via *any active `(scope_kind='deployment')` role_grants row*, but
`app/adapters/db/authz/models.py:151` models `role_grants.workspace_id`
as `NOT NULL` with **no `scope_kind` column** — the schema cannot
express a deployment-scope grant.

Downstream blocked: `cd-jlms` (admin router), `cd-rpxd` admin-team
endpoints, `cd-ylfd` AdminsPage, `/auth/me → is_deployment_admin`.

## Technical context
- **Key file**: `app/adapters/db/authz/models.py` (`RoleGrant`, line 151).
- **Helpers to extend**: `app/authz/` (add `is_deployment_admin`).
- **Spec anchors**:
  - `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"Admin surface" — auth principal #1
    (`scope_kind='deployment'`).
  - `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` §"Permissions: surface,
    groups, and action catalog" — deployment admin team / owners +
    managers deployment groups.
  - `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` — `role_grants` entity.
- **Prior art**: `cd-ckr` (owners-group bootstrap) introduced
  `WorkspaceContext.is_owner`; follow the same "O(1) flag on the
  request-scoped context" pattern for deployment.

## Implementation notes
- **Alembic migration** (follow `/new-migration`):
  - Add `role_grants.scope_kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'workspace'`
    with CHECK `scope_kind IN ('workspace','deployment')`.
  - Backfill all existing rows to `scope_kind='workspace'` in the
    same migration.
  - Relax `workspace_id` to NULLABLE with CHECK
    `(scope_kind='deployment' AND workspace_id IS NULL)
     OR (scope_kind='workspace' AND workspace_id IS NOT NULL)`.
  - Partial unique index: `(user_id, grant_role)` WHERE
    `scope_kind='deployment'` (SQLite + Postgres).
  - Keep the existing workspace-scoped uniqueness intact.
- **Role enum**: keep existing `{manager, worker, client, guest}` —
  the spec uses `managers` + `owners` deployment **groups**, not a new
  role. Extend the CHECK to accept `manager` at `scope_kind='deployment'`
  (owners-group membership is modelled via `permission_group_member`
  as already done at workspace scope).
- **Helper**: `app.authz.is_deployment_admin(session, user_id) -> bool`
  — returns True iff there is an active `role_grant` with
  `scope_kind='deployment' AND user_id=:uid` OR the user is a member
  of the deployment-scope owners/managers permission group (if that
  group lands under this task; otherwise defer to Task C).
- **Spec check**: read §02 `role_grants` and §05; if they don't
  already describe `scope_kind`, update them in the same PR (spec
  drift ships alongside code per `AGENTS.md` §"Spec-first").

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Migration applies cleanly forward and backward on SQLite.
- [ ] Migration applies cleanly on Postgres
      (`CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres`).
- [ ] Existing workspace-scoped grants unchanged; all
      `tests/integration/test_db_authz.py` and `tests/unit/authz/`
      tests still pass.
- [ ] `is_deployment_admin()` returns True for a seeded deployment
      grant, False otherwise.
- [ ] Inserting a 2nd deployment grant with the same
      `(user_id, grant_role)` is rejected by the partial unique
      index.
- [ ] `tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py` still green
      (SQLite ↔ Postgres column parity).
- [ ] Specs §02 and §05 describe `scope_kind` accurately (or a
      dedicated `/audit-spec` task is filed if drift can't be
      reconciled in-scope).

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_db_authz.py tests/unit/authz/ \
  tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py -xvs
# and on Postgres:
CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres uv run pytest tests/integration/test_db_authz.py \
  tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py -xvs
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. `docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build`
2. `uv run alembic upgrade head` → add a deployment grant via a
   one-liner script against the dev DB.
3. `uv run alembic downgrade -1` → scope_kind column gone, table
   still queryable.

title: feat(auth/tokens): verify-time enforcement of delegating-user liveness
description: §03 'Delegated tokens': 'If the delegating user is archived, globally deactivated, or loses every non-revoked grant, requests with the token return 401 with a clear message.' Today app.auth.tokens.verify only checks the token row itself (revoked_at / expires_at / hash). Extend verify() or its caller (the future tenancy middleware, cd-ika7) to look up delegate_for_user_id / subject_user_id and return 401 when archived_at is set or the user has no live grants. Same rule for PATs (subject_user_id). Acceptance: verify() returns a new domain error (e.g. DelegatingUserArchived / SubjectUserArchived) when the user is archived; router maps to 401 with a typed error code.

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: Upgraded to P1 during cd-8ax2 selfreview — delegated tokens and PATs currently continue to verify after the delegating/subject user is archived (archived_at column does not exist yet; landing under users-hardening follow-up). §03 'Delegated tokens' and 'Personal access tokens' both require a 401 in this case. Must land before any prod release.

title: feat(auth/tokens): tenant-agnostic audit seam for PAT mint/revoke
description: cd-i1qe left PAT mint/revoke auditing as a TODO because the only audit surface today is workspace-scoped (write_audit requires a WorkspaceContext). §03 pins 'every enrollment, login, rotation, and revocation writes to the audit log' — PATs don't today. Add an identity-scoped audit seam (audit_log rows with workspace_id=NULL + actor_kind='user' + actor_id=subject_user_id) and wire it into app.auth.tokens.mint / revoke_personal. Acceptance: api_token.minted and api_token.revoked rows land for every PAT mint / revoke; workspace-scoped audit views still exclude them; the /me 'Personal access tokens' audit-log view (§14, §03) becomes buildable.

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: Upgraded to P1 during cd-8ax2 selfreview — shipping PAT mint/revoke without this seam violates §03 'every rotation and revocation writes to the audit log'. Must land before any prod release that exposes /api/v1/me/tokens to real users.

title: feat(worker): fan-out generate_task_occurrences across workspaces
description:
## Problem / goal
cd-7c0p landed the APScheduler bootstrap + ``register_jobs`` hook. The hourly tick for ``generate_task_occurrences`` is registered but its body is a placeholder (``app.worker.scheduler._generator_fanout_placeholder``) that just logs — no workspace is actually walked.

## Scope
Replace the placeholder with a real fan-out:

1. Open a fresh ``make_uow()``.
2. Inside ``tenant_agnostic()``, enumerate every ``Workspace`` (skip demo-expired ones per §24).
3. For each workspace, resolve a system-actor :class:`WorkspaceContext` (the pattern the idempotency-sweep path will use too — see cd-j9l7) and call :func:`generate_task_occurrences` with an injected Clock and the workspace's horizon.
4. Log per-workspace totals via the ``GenerationReport``; aggregate counts under a single ``worker.generator.tick.summary`` log at the end.

## Acceptance criteria
- Placeholder removed; tick produces occurrences against a seeded multi-workspace fixture.
- Per-workspace errors do not abort the full tick — one bad workspace logs + continues.
- Integration test: two workspaces, one with a schedule, one without — ``tasks_created`` is attributed correctly in the per-ws log payload.

## Blocks / depends
- Depends on cd-7c0p (landed).
- Unblocks the operator dashboards the ``GenerationReport`` shape was pinned for.

title: Self-review: fix(authz): harden last-owner guard against count-then-delete TOCTOU race
