missing pieces, and unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-mb5n** (main task must be complete first).

## How to run
Run `/selfreview` in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from cd-mb5n.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Specific checks:
  - **Cross-dialect correctness**: the locking primitive works on both
    Postgres (`FOR UPDATE` / advisory lock) AND SQLite (serialisable
    isolation / explicit transaction). Run the concurrency test on
    both (`CREWDAY_DB_URL=postgres://...` + default SQLite).
  - **Lock scope**: the lock covers both the count read AND the
    DELETE/revoke in the same transaction. Verify the SQL or the
    session pattern, not just the test outcome.
  - **DRY**: the two guards (`permission_groups.remove_member` and
    `role_grants.revoke`) call the same locking helper, not duplicate
    copies. Per cd-duv6 (proper-repository refactor is pending, but
    the helper here should be factored out now, not left duplicated).
  - **Test coverage**: the concurrency test actually RACES — spawns
    real threads, not sequential sync simulation. Assert at least one
    refusal and zero empty-owners windows. Mirror for role_grants.
  - **Backwards compatibility**: existing callers of `remove_member`
    and `revoke` still work (unit tests pass).
- Run the quality gates after fixing.
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

See `.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All BUGS from the self-review fixed
- [ ] All MISSING pieces completed
- [ ] All RISKY items mitigated (or justified in a task comment)
- [ ] Linter, formatter, type checker, affected tests all pass
- [ ] Fixes committed and pushed
close_reason: Autofix self-review complete: TOCTOU fix verified on both SQLite and Postgres via disable-and-retry spot-check; 1 minor mypy unused-ignore autofixed; 4 follow-ups filed (cd-nj8m, cd-j5pu, cd-a3oa logic holes out-of-TOCTOU-scope; cd-p46e pre-existing flake)

title: feat(api/admin): admin router scaffold + workspace-scoped /admin/signups placeholder
description:
## Problem / goal
§15-security-privacy.md specifies `/admin/signups` (+ future admin surfaces). Today there is no `/admin/*` router in `app/api/v1/` — `rg '/admin/signups|admin_signups' app/` returns zero hits. Every admin dashboard task downstream (cd-ovt4 signup suspicious-activity rollups; future user admin, workspace-policy admin, etc.) is blocked on this scaffolding landing.

## Scope
Mount a workspace-scoped `/admin/*` router behind an owner / manager Permission gate, with the first page being a placeholder `/admin/signups` skeleton that the downstream tasks fill in.

- Spec: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md`, `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md`.
- Router shape: `build_admin_router()` factory, `/w/<slug>/api/v1/admin/*` prefix.
- Auth: scope_kind = 'workspace', action_key = 'admin.view' (new scope bit?) — confirm with §05.
- SPA side: `mocks/web/` (and future `app/web/`) needs a `/admin` route entry + link in the nav. `/frontend-design:frontend-design` mandatory.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `app/api/v1/admin/__init__.py` + `signups.py` with placeholder GET `/admin/signups`
- [ ] Mounted in `app/api/factory.py` with permission gate
- [ ] Tenancy-middleware opt-out / opt-in configured correctly
- [ ] Unit test: authorised actor returns 200; unauthorised returns 403
- [ ] Frontend: `/admin` entry in the SPA sidebar (owner / manager only)

## Blocks (once this lands)
- cd-ovt4 — suspicious-activity surfacing on `/admin/signups`
- (+ future admin dashboards)

## Why file this now
cd-ovt4's description already names this as the blocker: "whoever files the broader admin surface task first should link this one via `bd dep <new-admin> --blocks cd-<this>`". Filing it now keeps the graph honest.

title: feat(worker): bootstrap APScheduler + register_jobs hook + worker_heartbeat
description:
## Problem / goal
`app/worker/__init__.py` is currently a docstring-only module. `app/worker/tasks/generator.py` (cd-p5) exists as a pure callable but has no scheduler to invoke it. `prune_expired_idempotency_keys` (cd-z6fk) exists but has no sweep tick. `user_workspace` derive-refresh (cd-yqm4) has no reconciliation loop.

Every job downstream of "the app has a scheduler" is effectively blocked on this task. File it as the shared dependency so `bd ready` doesn't keep resurfacing job tasks that can't actually run.

## Scope
Bootstrap APScheduler in a way both the HTTP app (for in-process dev) and a standalone worker process (for prod) can use.

- Spec: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"Worker", `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Worker process".
- Choose between `BackgroundScheduler`, `AsyncIOScheduler`, or `APScheduler 4.x` based on whether FastAPI runs the jobs in-process or as a separate `worker` container.
- Decide the registration seam: a single `register_jobs(app_or_scheduler)` call is probably the cleanest hook for downstream tasks (cd-j9l7, cd-yqm4) to plug into.
- Wire a `worker_heartbeat` row per job so `/readyz` can read liveness (§16).
- Dev stack (`mocks/docker-compose.yml`): confirm whether the scheduler runs in the `app-api` container or a new `app-worker` service.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] APScheduler instance created on app / worker startup
- [ ] `register_jobs(scheduler)` hook that downstream tasks import and add jobs to
- [ ] `worker_heartbeat` table + per-job heartbeat writes
- [ ] `/readyz` reads worker_heartbeat and returns stale/fresh correctly
- [ ] Unit tests: scheduler boots, jobs register, heartbeat writes
- [ ] Integration test: a seeded fake job runs and its heartbeat advances

## Blocks (once this lands)
- cd-j9l7 — prune_expired_idempotency_keys daily sweep
- cd-yqm4 — user_workspace derive-refresh worker
- (+ future occurrence generator tick, overdue sweeper, etc.)

## Why file this now
Four P1 tasks (cd-j9l7, cd-yqm4, plus two future sweeps) all wait on this. Explicit parent keeps the graph honest.
close_reason: AsyncIOScheduler bootstrap + register_jobs + heartbeat wired; /readyz flips green end-to-end; 39 scoped tests + 291 broader pass; follow-ups: cd-dcl2 (generator fan-out), cd-8euz (failure alerting).

title: Self-review: feat(api/proxy): WebSocket upgrade for Vite HMR dev proxy
description:
## Problem / goal
Auto-fixing self-review of the changes made under cd-354g. Catch bugs,
missing pieces, and unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-354g** (main task must be complete first).

## How to run
Run `/selfreview` in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from cd-354g.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Pay particular attention to:
  - **Path guard**: the new websocket route must NOT hijack `/api/*`,
    `/w/<slug>/api/*`, or `/events` SSE. The HTTP guard logic lives in
    `app/api/proxy.py::_is_api_path` — the WS route needs the same
    filter.
  - **Dev-only registration**: profile guard must still keep the route
    out of prod (`profile != 'dev'` → no registration).
  - **Upstream failure handling**: connect-refused upstream should
    close the client WS with a sane code (1011 or 1013), not throw a
    500.
  - **Bidirectional forwarding**: neither direction blocks indefinitely
    when the other closes; both sides cancel cleanly.
  - **Resource leak**: no dangling asyncio tasks on failure / early
    close.
- Run the quality gates after fixing.
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

See `.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All BUGS from the self-review fixed
- [ ] All MISSING pieces completed
- [ ] All RISKY items mitigated (or justified in a task comment)
- [ ] Linter, formatter, type checker, affected tests all pass
- [ ] Fixes committed and pushed
close_reason: Autofix self-review complete. Path guard gap closed (/events + /w/<slug>/events); shared _is_non_spa_path helper added; guard drift tests pinned. Follow-ups filed as cd-g1cy (compose topology) and cd-wl8u (spec docs).

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text:
Self-review complete. Findings + fixes:

**BUGS (fixed):**
- Path guard drift: `_is_api_path` doesn't cover `/events` or
  `/w/<slug>/events`, so the WS (and HTTP) catch-all would proxy
  those to Vite. The module docstring claimed coverage that didn't
  exist. Fix: introduced `_is_sse_path` + `_is_non_spa_path`
  helpers in `app/api/proxy.py`, used by both the WS and HTTP
  legs so they cannot drift again.

**MISSING (fixed):**
- No unit coverage for the SSE guard. Added
  `TestWebSocketPathGuard.test_bare_events_sse_path_rejected` +
  `test_workspace_events_sse_path_rejected` and a new
  `TestNonSpaPathPredicate` class pinning the shared predicate.

**RISKY (out of scope — filed as Beads):**
- Compose topology has browser dialing Vite directly → no live
  HMR verification → cd-g1cy.
- §14 / §16 don't document the dev proxy → cd-wl8u.

**Verified:**
- Dev-only registration is truly conditional (`factory.py:632`
  imports `register_vite_proxy` only when `cfg.profile == 'dev'`).
- Exception tuple `(OSError, WebSocketException, TimeoutError)`
  covers `asyncio.TimeoutError` (aliased in 3.11+),
  `ConnectionClosedError` (subclass of `WebSocketException`),
  and `InvalidHandshake`. Generic exceptions would bubble as 500
  which is the right failure mode for a library bug.
- `asyncio.wait(FIRST_COMPLETED)` + `gather(return_exceptions=True)`
  supervises the pump tasks correctly — no dangling tasks.
- Subprotocol echo uses `upstream.subprotocol` (what upstream
  chose), not what the client requested — correct per the review
  prompt.
- Scheme rewrite handles `http→ws`, `https→wss`,
  `ws→ws`/`wss→wss` idempotently via `scheme_map.get(scheme,
  scheme)`.
- Test isolation clean — each test builds its own `create_app`
  with in-memory SQLite, no shared `app.state` keys.

Quality gates: ruff check, ruff format --check, mypy --strict on
`app/api/proxy.py` and `tests/api/test_proxy_ws.py` all clean.
Tests: 109 passed (was 82 before; +27 from new guard + predicate
coverage).

title: docs(spec): reconcile §03 delegated-token body example — `delegate` field not on MintTokenBody
description:
## Problem

§03 `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md:603-610` teaches delegated-token creation via:

```json
POST /api/v1/auth/tokens
{
  "label": "chat-agent",
  "delegate": true,
  "expires_at_days": 30,
  "scopes": {}
}
```

But `MintTokenBody` (`app/api/v1/auth/tokens.py:99-115`) only accepts `{label, scopes, expires_at_days}` — there is no `delegate` field, and the router has no separate delegated-token mint path. Surfaced during cd-geob's scoped-token body reconciliation (the `delegate: true` line was kept because it was outside cd-geob's scope).

## Options

- **(a) Extend the router**: add `delegate: bool = False` to `MintTokenBody` + a delegated-token branch in the mint handler (sets `delegate_for_user_id = ctx.actor_id`, requires a passkey session per §03, enforces empty `scopes`, 30-day default TTL). The §03 example becomes correct as-is.
- **(b) Update the spec**: delegated tokens aren't in v1 — remove the JSON example or mark it "planned, not yet wired" and explain the mechanism in prose only. Keep the §03 columns and audit wiring sections since those are forward-looking.

## Technical context

- **Key files**:
  - `app/api/v1/auth/tokens.py:99-115` (MintTokenBody schema)
  - `app/api/v1/auth/tokens.py:209-256` (post_tokens handler)
  - `app/auth/tokens.py::mint` (domain mint — no `delegate_for_user_id` plumbing yet)
  - `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md:596-653` (Delegated tokens section)
- **Related**: cd-geob reconciled `{name, note, ISO expires_at}` → `{label, scopes, expires_at_days}`. This task handles the remaining `delegate` drift that wasn't in cd-geob's scope.

## Acceptance criteria (exactly one of)

### Path A — Implement delegated tokens

- [ ] `MintTokenBody.delegate: bool = False` + validation (`delegate=True` requires passkey session, rejects any `scopes` entries)
- [ ] Mint handler branches on `delegate`: sets `delegate_for_user_id = ctx.actor_id`, 30-day default TTL
- [ ] `api_token.delegate_for_user_id` column writes honoured
- [ ] Unit + integration tests cover both branches
- [ ] §03 example stays as-is

### Path B — Defer delegated tokens in spec

- [ ] §03 JSON example replaced with a prose-only description or marked "not yet implemented"
- [ ] §03 §"Key properties" for delegated tokens retained as forward-looking
- [ ] Matching note in §12's `/auth/tokens` block
- [ ] No code change

## Refs

- Surfaced by cd-geob (reconciled scoped-token body fields; delegated-token drift surfaced but left for this task)
- Related: cd-wd78 (scopes shape), cd-vbls (passkey-route rename)
notes: Closed as subsumed by cd-i1qe (implement delegated + PAT token kinds). cd-i1qe will implement the delegated-token mint path and make the §03 example correct. Per director AskUserQuestion on 2026-04-23.

title: feat(api/auth): POST /api/v1/auth/logout endpoint
description:
## Problem / goal

app/web/src/auth/useAuth.ts:107 calls
POST /api/v1/auth/logout as part of logout(). The endpoint is not
wired in app/api/factory.py — every logout path currently 404s.
The SPA catches the 404 / error and clears local state anyway, so
the UX appears to work, but the server-side session row is NOT
invalidated: the cookie remains valid until its TTL expires, and
any saved copy (another tab, curl, etc.) keeps authenticating.
That is the exact failure mode §03 "Sessions" forbids.

## Technical context

- Domain service: app/auth/session.invalidate_for_user is the
  ready-made revoker (cause="logout"). No new primitive needed.
- Session cookie parsing pattern: app/api/v1/auth/invite.py:269
  (Cookie alias auth_session.SESSION_COOKIE_NAME).
- Cookie-clear builder: app/auth/session_cookie.build_session_cookie
  accepts empty value + Max-Age=0 per §15; add a helper if it
  doesn't already expose that shape.
- Spec: docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md §"Sessions" (invalidation),
  docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md §"Cookies" (clear cookie on
  logout).

## Implementation notes

- Mount alongside /auth/me in app/api/factory.py (bare-host).
- Best-effort semantics: 204 No Content on success, still 204 on
  no-cookie / invalid-cookie (clears the client cookie either
  way). This matches the SPA's expectation (any non-401 is treated
  as success).
- Emit Set-Cookie header that clears the __Host- cookie (empty
  value, Max-Age=0, Path=/, Secure, SameSite=Lax).
- Write audit.session.invalidated row with cause="logout".

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] POST /api/v1/auth/logout returns 204 + Set-Cookie clearing
      the session cookie.
- [ ] The underlying session row has invalidated_at set after the
      call; a follow-up GET /auth/me with the same cookie returns
      401.
- [ ] Integration test covers both happy path and no-cookie path.

## Test plan

- uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_logout.py -x -q
close_reason: POST /api/v1/auth/logout implemented: best-effort 204 + clearing Set-Cookie, invalidates session server-side, audit logged. 8 integration tests pass.

title: sec(auth/mail): wrap mailer.send in signup/magic/invite per §15 enumeration guard
description:
## Problem / goal

Sibling to cd-h2t0 + the recovery fix (app/auth/recovery.py:584).
Several send call sites still propagate MailDeliveryError directly
as a 500, violating the §15 constant-time / enumeration-guard
contract:

- app/auth/magic_link.py:644 — generic magic-link send inside
  request_link().
- app/auth/signup.py — signup_start mail send (disposable-email +
  hit branch sends verification link).
- app/api/v1/auth/invite.py — invite-email send inside
  users.invite().

A mailer outage (SMTP down, DNS miss, relay refusal) today surfaces
as a 500 on the public endpoints. For signup + magic + invite the
leak is the same kind as was fixed on recovery: hit vs miss become
observable because the miss branch (or an absent-user branch)
doesn't send a mail, so the 500 only fires on the "real" path.

## Technical context

- Recovery fix for precedent: app/auth/recovery.py:584.
- SMTP adapter wraps unclassified transport errors as
  MailDeliveryError since the recovery fix (app/adapters/mail/smtp.py:308).
- Spec: docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md §"Rate limiting and abuse
  controls" + docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md §"Self-serve signup"
  / §"Self-service lost-device recovery".

## Implementation notes

- Wrap each send call in try/except MailDeliveryError + _log.warning
  so the trace is visible in ops logs.
- Preserve the audit row on failure — write before the send in the
  hit branch, or split the try so only the send is inside it.
- Do NOT revert when only the caller rolls back its UoW — the audit
  row must survive. See recovery for the pattern.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] signup_start returns 202 on mailer failure (verified by a unit
      test injecting MailDeliveryError from a double mailer).
- [ ] magic.request_link returns 202 on mailer failure (same shape).
- [ ] users.invite returns 2xx on mailer failure; the invite row
      still commits so an operator can re-issue the mail manually.
- [ ] Audit rows (signup.requested, magic_link.sent, invite.sent)
      commit regardless of mailer outcome.

## Test plan

- Unit tests mirroring tests/unit/auth/test_recovery.py
  TestRequestRecoveryEnumerationGuard.test_hit_branch_swallows_
  mail_delivery_error — inject a MailDeliveryError-raising mailer,
  assert the caller does NOT raise and audit row is written.
close_reason: Wrapped magic_link.request_link, signup (via magic_link), and membership.invite mailer sends in try/except MailDeliveryError + WARNING log. Audit rows commit before send. 3 new enumeration-guard unit tests mirror recovery pattern. 124 scoped tests pass.

title: feat(web/public): /recover/enroll page to complete passkey recovery
description:
## Problem / goal

The backend recovery flow works end-to-end (/api/v1/recover/passkey/
{request, verify, start, finish}), and the recovery email points
visitors to {base_url}/recover/enroll?token=... (app/auth/recovery.py
:425). But app/web/src/App.tsx only registers /recover (the
email-entry page) — visiting /recover/enroll lands on the catch-all
<Navigate to='/' /> and the auth gate punts to /login. The token
query-param is dropped, the user can't enrol a passkey, and the
whole self-service recovery path from §03 is unusable in the SPA.

## Technical context

- Backend endpoints and shapes:
  - GET /api/v1/recover/passkey/verify?token=... → returns
    {recovery_session_id} (app/api/v1/auth/recovery.py:438).
  - POST /api/v1/recover/passkey/start {recovery_session_id} →
    WebAuthn creation options (PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptionsJSON).
  - POST /api/v1/recover/passkey/finish {recovery_session_id,
    credential} → completes recovery; session cookie stamped on the
    response.
- Frontend plumbing to reuse:
  - app/web/src/auth/passkey.ts already has base64url helpers and a
    login ceremony. Extract the shared decode/encode into a helper
    if the register ceremony needs a parallel shape.
  - Visual contract: mirror mocks/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx
    or app/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx — same login__card
    layout, login__notice error slot, login__primary button.
- Spec: docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md §"Self-service lost-device
  recovery" — the /recover/enroll step registers a fresh passkey and
  revokes the rest atomically.

## Implementation notes

- New file: app/web/src/pages/public/EnrollPage.tsx — reads
  token from the URL, calls /recover/passkey/verify, then drives
  navigator.credentials.create() against the server challenge, then
  /recover/passkey/finish. On success: redirect to the user's
  workspace landing (same ROLE_LANDING map as LoginPage).
- Register /recover/enroll in app/web/src/App.tsx alongside /recover.
- Error mapping must surface: invalid/expired token (400 / 410),
  rate-limited (429), passkey cancel / unsupported, and mailer-
  independent transport errors. Follow LoginPage's messageFor()
  idiom.
- Respect §15 rate-limit envelope: if the server responds 429 on
  /start or /finish, tell the user to slow down rather than
  retrying.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Visiting /recover/enroll?token=<valid> walks the verify →
      start → create() → finish chain and lands the user on their
      workspace landing page, authenticated.
- [ ] An invalid or expired token renders a clear error message with
      a 'request a new link' affordance (back to /recover).
- [ ] The page reuses the existing semantic CSS classes — no new
      class names beyond enroll-scoped variants if needed.
- [ ] The passkey register ceremony helpers live in
      app/web/src/auth/ (factored, not inlined in the page).
- [ ] Playwright smoke confirms the happy path against the dev stack.

## Test plan

- Unit: component-level tests mocking fetchJson + navigator.credentials
  — mirror RecoverPage.test.tsx.
- Integration: record a Playwright tape at .playwright-mcp/ that
  requests a recovery link, consumes the Mailpit message, and
  registers a synthetic virtual authenticator via CDP.
- Manual: docker compose up dev stack; run
  scripts/dev-login.sh email=servers@courcelle.com slug=courcelle
  to seed; trigger recovery; click the enroll link.
close_reason: Shipped /recover/enroll SPA route (EnrollPage.tsx + passkey-register.ts). Verified end-to-end against dev.crew.day. Follow-up: cd-o73q (tests).

title: feat(api/auth): GET /api/v1/auth/me endpoint for SPA bootstrap
description:
## Problem / goal

The production SPA (app/web/) calls GET /api/v1/auth/me during
authStore bootstrap to discover the cookied user. The endpoint does
not exist — app/api/factory.py wires passkey/magic/recover/signup/
invite but no /auth/me. A 404 is treated by
app/web/src/auth/onUnauthorized.ts:51 as an unauth signal, bouncing
users to /login even with a valid session cookie. Effect: nobody can
log into the SPA end-to-end today.

## Technical context

- Frontend caller: app/web/src/auth/authStore.ts and useAuth.ts.
  Types: app/web/src/auth/types.ts (AuthMe) — already defines
  display_name, available_workspaces[].slug, .grant_role.
- Session validation seam: app/auth/session.py — lookup from the
  __Host-crewday_session cookie.
- Role-grants source: app.adapters.db.authz.models.RoleGrant +
  UserWorkspace (app.adapters.db.workspace.models).
- Spec: docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md §"Sessions",
  docs/specs/14-ux-ia.md §"Role selector / landing".

## Implementation notes

- New router: app/api/v1/auth/me.py, mounted on the bare host (no
  workspace prefix) because the SPA calls it before any /w/<slug>
  context exists.
- Accept the session cookie via the same dep used by /passkey/login/
  finish (see app.auth.session_cookie). Return 401 when the cookie
  is absent / invalid so the SPA's onUnauthorized path works as
  intended.
- Body shape must match AuthMe in app/web/src/auth/types.ts so the
  frontend does not need a client-side migration.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] GET /api/v1/auth/me returns 200 + AuthMe shape for a valid
      session cookie.
- [ ] Returns 401 (not 404) when the session is absent or expired.
- [ ] Response includes every workspace the user has a RoleGrant on,
      with the corresponding grant_role.
- [ ] Integration test hits the real app factory and asserts both
      shapes.

## Test plan

- Unit: app/auth/session.validate for cookie parse.
- Integration: tests/integration/api/test_auth_me.py — seed one
  user + two workspaces + one grant each; assert shape.
- Manual: docker compose exec app-api, dev_login to mint cookie,
  curl http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/v1/auth/me -b cookie.
close_reason: Shipped /api/v1/auth/me endpoint (app/api/v1/auth/me.py) and mount in app/api/factory.py. Follow-ups: cd-q4pr (tests), cd-govu (workspace column cleanup).

title: Pre-existing flake: tests/unit/auth/test_session.py::TestBuildSessionCookie::test_secure_false_falls_back_to_dev_cookie
description: Full-suite run fails, passes in isolation. Observed on the run that also exposed cd-0dyv (now fixed) and cd-szxw. Likely another test-ordering leak — investigate whether shared module-level state (settings singleton, get_settings cache, cookie-helper defaults) gets mutated by an earlier test and leaks in. Repro: uv run pytest -q. Diagnose by bisecting pytest --lf or pytest -p no:randomly to find the specific preceding test that tips it over.
close_reason: Duplicate of cd-75q5 (same test, same repro, same suspected root cause). Consolidating on cd-75q5.

title: Pre-existing flake: tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_login.py::TestAutoRevokeCredentialFreshUow::test_db_failure_is_swallowed_without_raising
description: Full-suite run fails with 'assert False' on caplog.records check. Passes in isolation. Same root cause pattern as cd-0dyv — the integration fixture's 'alembic upgrade head' runs logging.config.fileConfig with disable_existing_loggers=True, which flips propagate=False / disabled=True on every logger not listed in alembic.ini. The test uses caplog.at_level(logging.ERROR, logger=passkey_api_module.__name__) but records never reach the root logger. Fix pattern (see cd-0dyv resolution): force propagate=True + disabled=False on the passkey_api logger before the with-block. Reference applied fixes in tests/unit/test_capabilities.py::TestProbeLogsSnapshotOnce, tests/unit/authz/test_enforce.py::TestStructuredLogging, tests/unit/test_main.py::TestSpaCatchAll::test_missing_dist_logs_warning. Repro: uv run pytest -q tests/integration/ tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_login.py::TestAutoRevokeCredentialFreshUow::test_db_failure_is_swallowed_without_raising
close_reason: Fixed via shared allow_propagated_log_capture fixture. Test passes in isolation and under integration-fixture ordering (alembic fileConfig poisoning).

title: Self-review: fix pre-existing flake in test_missing_dist_logs_warning
description:
## Problem / goal

title: Self-review: feat(dev): scripts/dev-login force-auth CLI
description:
## Problem / goal

Auto-fixing self-review of the changes delivered under cd-w1ia. Catch
bugs, missing pieces, and unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-w1ia** (main task must be complete first).

## How to run

Run /selfreview in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from cd-w1ia.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Run the quality gates after fixing.
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

Watch especially for:

- Any path where the gate (`CREWDAY_DEV_AUTH=1` AND profile=dev AND
  sqlite URL) can be partially bypassed — e.g. `profile` check reading
  `os.environ["CREWDAY_PROFILE"]` directly instead of `get_settings().profile`.
- The cookie value being logged / printed anywhere except stdout — it's
  a live bearer token.
- Session audit missing `dev_force_login=True` context, so a production
  audit cannot distinguish a real passkey login from a force login
  that shipped.
- Script-dir tests: running them shouldn't require the real OpenRouter
  key or SMTP server; fixture-level isolation must be airtight.
- A misread of `--output` flag that prints the cookie on a TTY
  without a warning (agents will copy+paste the entire terminal into
  logs).
- `scripts/dev-login.sh` failing silently in CI (`set -euo pipefail`
  should be on).
- `AGENTS.md` mention pointing at an outdated usage string.

See `.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All BUGS from the self-review fixed
- [ ] All MISSING pieces completed
- [ ] All RISKY items mitigated (or justified in a task comment)
- [ ] Linter, formatter, type checker, affected tests all pass
- [ ] Fixes committed and pushed

LABELS: area:auth, area:dev, selfreview, type:chore

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text:
Autofix findings and fixes applied (commiter will ship).

BUGS fixed:
- app/web/vite.config.ts: added /w to API_PATHS (was returning SPA index for /w/<slug>/api/* instead of proxying to app-api). Also extended PWA navigateFallbackDenylist.
- mocks/web/vite.config.ts: same /w proxy + PWA denylist added for symmetry.
- scripts/dev-login.sh: default interpreter flipped from python → python3; probes python3 then python (pyenv / Alpine / Debian 12+ hosts lack the legacy symlink).
- scripts/dev_login.py: fingerprint_hash is wiped on the minted session row so curl / Playwright / httpx all validate despite not matching the stamped UA. Covered by new TestMintSessionFingerprintWiped.
- scripts/dev_login.py: default --ua / --accept-language flipped to empty strings (were crewday-dev-login/1.0 + en-US which broke validation against any real client).
- AGENTS.md + scripts/dev_login.py module docstring: documented both host-side and docker-exec invocations; docker exec uses python -m scripts.dev_login (script-path form puts scripts/ on sys.path and from app.… imports miss).
- scripts/dev_login.py main(): TTY-only stderr banner 'Dev-only session cookie — do not commit or log this value.' (silent on pipe so cookie=$(…) capture stays clean).

Live smoke (all pass):
- ./scripts/dev-login.sh foo bar (no flag) → exit 1 gate error
- docker compose exec app-api python -m scripts.dev_login --email smoke@dev.local --workspace smoke → prints cookie
- curl -b $cookie http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/smoke/api/v1/auth/tokens → 200, body []
- Second mint → distinct cookie (idempotent on user/workspace rows)

Quality gates:
- pytest tests/unit/auth/{test_dev_login,test_signup,test_session}.py → 90 passed
- ruff check + format --check on scripts/ app/auth/signup.py tests/unit/auth/ → clean
- mypy --strict scripts/dev_login.py app/auth/signup.py → clean

Left for commiter: git add + commit + push on main + bd sync + bd close cd-ib3n.

title: feat(dev): scripts/dev-login force-auth CLI (no-passkey) for local agents + Playwright
description:
## Problem / goal

Local agents and Playwright tests need a *one-shot* way to get an authenticated
browser / curl session against the **production app** now running at
`dev.crew.day` (`127.0.0.1:8100`) without walking through the real passkey
ceremony. The signup flow requires a magic-link email + a WebAuthn
authenticator device; neither is reachable from a headless agent.

Goal: a tiny, dev-only force-auth script that:

- takes `--email` + `--workspace` (slug),
- creates the user / workspace / grant rows on demand if missing,
- calls the real `app.auth.session.issue(...)` to mint a fresh session row,
- prints the `__Host-crewday_session=<value>` cookie pair on stdout
  for direct `curl -H "Cookie: ..."` or Playwright
  `context.addCookies([...])` consumption,
- is hard-gated so it **cannot** run in a production build.

## Current state

- `app/auth/session.py::issue(...)` is the session-mint seam. It writes a
  `Session` row and returns a `SessionIssue` with the opaque cookie value
  plus the `Set-Cookie` header.
- Users, workspaces, and grants are concrete SQLAlchemy models under
  `app/adapters/db/identity/` and `app/adapters/db/workspace/`.
- `app.auth.signup.complete_signup(...)` already has the canonical
  provisioning path (user + workspace + grant + session + audit) — **reuse
  its helpers** rather than replicating; don't bypass audit.
- There is no CLI dir yet; scripts live under `scripts/` (see
  `scripts/check_csp_inlines.py` for the pattern — executable Python with
  a top-module docstring + `if __name__ == "__main__"` entry).

## What this task delivers

### 1. `scripts/dev_login.py`

```
python scripts/dev_login.py --email me@example.com --workspace myhome
# stdout: __Host-crewday_session=<base64url token>
```

- Flags:
  - `--email` (required)
  - `--workspace` (required — slug. Created if missing.)
  - `--display-name` (optional; defaults to email local-part)
  - `--timezone` (optional; defaults to `UTC`)
  - `--role` (optional; one of `owner` / `manager` / `worker`; default `owner`
    so the default dev user gets full access)
  - `--output` (`cookie` prints `name=value` (default); `json` prints
    `{"name": ..., "value": ...}`; `curl` prints `-b '__Host-...=...'`
    flag-ready; `header` prints a full `Cookie: ...` header)
  - `--ua`, `--ip`, `--accept-language` (optional; defaults safe for
    Playwright's Chromium)

- Hard gate (**critical**):
  1. `os.environ["CREWDAY_DEV_AUTH"]` must be `"1"` (or the key word
     `yes`/`true`) **and**
  2. `get_settings().profile == "dev"` **and**
  3. `get_settings().database_url` matches the dev sqlite path
     (defensive — refuses to touch a Postgres URL by accident).
  Any miss → exit 1 with a clear stderr explanation. Emit an audit row
  `audit.dev.force_login` on success so the action is never invisible.

- Creates user / workspace / role_grant rows idempotently:
  - look up the `User` by `canonicalise_email(email)`; create if missing.
  - look up `Workspace` by slug; create if missing (same shape as
    `app.auth.signup.complete_signup` — re-use the helpers, don't
    hand-roll).
  - ensure a `role_grant` (and workspace membership) exists for the
    requested role.
  - mint the session via `app.auth.session.issue(...)`.

- Opens its own `make_uow()` (SQLAlchemy UoW from
  `app.adapters.db.session`) — does not try to share the server's
  connection pool, this is an out-of-process tool.

### 2. `scripts/dev-login.sh`

Thin Bash wrapper so local agents can one-liner it:

```bash
./scripts/dev-login.sh me@example.com myhome
# Same stdout as the Python script, but `--output curl`.
```

- Bails out with a clear error if `CREWDAY_DEV_AUTH` isn't `1`.
- Under the hood: `exec python -m scripts.dev_login --email "$1"
  --workspace "$2" --output curl "${@:3}"` (the trailing `$@:3` lets
  callers pass through extra flags.)
- Respect `PYTHON=` and `UV_RUN=` overrides (default: plain `python`).

### 3. `.env.example` + `mocks/docker-compose.yml`

- Add `CREWDAY_DEV_AUTH=0` to `.env.example` with a prominent comment
  ("Dev-only; must stay 0 in production. Used by `scripts/dev-login.sh`").
- Set `CREWDAY_DEV_AUTH=1` on the `app-api` service in
  `mocks/docker-compose.yml` so agents running inside the compose
  network can `docker compose exec app-api python scripts/dev_login.py
  ...` if they prefer that over host-run.

### 4. Tests

`tests/unit/auth/test_dev_login.py`:

- Happy path: given a fresh DB, `dev_login(email, workspace)` creates
  user + workspace + grant + session and returns a cookie value parsed
  by `app.auth.session_cookie.parse_session_cookie` (or equivalent).
- Idempotent: running twice with the same email/workspace returns two
  distinct session tokens but does NOT create two users / workspaces.
- Gate: `CREWDAY_DEV_AUTH` unset or `0` → `SystemExit(1)` with the
  expected stderr. `profile=prod` → same.
- Audit: one `audit.dev.force_login` row per call with `email` and
  `workspace_slug` in the structured context.

### 5. Docs

- Add a short section to `AGENTS.md` §"Ask first" or §"Session bootstrap"
  pointing local agents at `./scripts/dev-login.sh <email> <workspace>`
  for a curl / Playwright cookie. One sentence + the one-liner.
- Mention the gate flag: "requires `CREWDAY_DEV_AUTH=1` and profile=dev".

## Technical context

- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Sessions",
  §"Self-serve signup". The `/signup/passkey/*` flow is the real
  provisioning path — dev-login is an escape hatch, not a replacement.
- **Related models**: `User`, `Workspace`, `RoleGrant`,
  `UserWorkspace`, `Session` in `app/adapters/db/identity/models.py`
  and `app/adapters/db/workspace/`.
- **Reusable helpers**: whatever `app.auth.signup.complete_signup`
  uses to create user / workspace / grant. Extract if a private helper
  is hidden inside that function — the extraction IS in scope, keeps
  DRY.

## Implementation notes

- **Do not** add `CREWDAY_DEV_AUTH` to `app/config.py` as a typed
  setting — a real Settings field implies it's part of the public
  surface. Read via `os.environ` directly in the script so it's
  unambiguously a script-only lever. Document it in `.env.example`.
- Use `click` (already a dep) for argument parsing so the UX matches
  the future CLI surface (§13).
- Script must also be importable (`from scripts.dev_login import
  mint_session`) for tests. Put the click command at the bottom; the
  helper function in the middle.
- Async or sync? Prefer **sync** — `alembic`/`scripts/` are all sync,
  and `make_uow()` has a sync variant. Avoids an asyncio event loop
  just for one-shot session mint.
- The script runs OUTSIDE the server process. It needs `pyproject`
  installable with `pip install -e .` (or `uv sync`). Test with both.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `python scripts/dev_login.py --email smoke@dev.local --workspace smoke`
      exits 0 and prints `__Host-crewday_session=<32+ chars>`.
- [ ] The cookie value is accepted by the running stack:
      `curl -sS -H "Cookie: $(./scripts/dev-login.sh smoke@dev.local smoke)"
      http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/v1/me` returns 200 and the authenticated
      user row.
- [ ] Re-running the script with the same email+workspace yields a
      SECOND session (both valid) without duplicating user/workspace
      rows.
- [ ] `CREWDAY_DEV_AUTH=0 python scripts/dev_login.py ...` exits 1
      with a stderr message naming the flag.
- [ ] `CREWDAY_PROFILE=prod CREWDAY_DEV_AUTH=1 python scripts/dev_login.py
      ...` exits 1 with a stderr message naming the profile gate.
- [ ] An `audit.dev.force_login` row exists per successful mint.
- [ ] `pytest tests/unit/auth/test_dev_login.py -x -q` passes.
- [ ] `ruff check scripts/dev_login.py tests/unit/auth/test_dev_login.py`
      clean.
- [ ] `mypy --strict scripts/dev_login.py` clean (add to mypy config
      if needed).

## Test plan

```bash
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build
# Inside the dev compose:
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml exec app-api \
  python scripts/dev_login.py --email e2e@dev.local --workspace dev

# Outside (host-side, against mounted SQLite):
CREWDAY_DEV_AUTH=1 CREWDAY_PROFILE=dev \
CREWDAY_DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///./data/crewday.db \
  python scripts/dev_login.py --email me@dev.local --workspace dev

# Full round-trip with curl:
cookie="$(CREWDAY_DEV_AUTH=1 ./scripts/dev-login.sh me@dev.local dev)"
curl -sS -b "$cookie" http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/v1/me
```

LABELS: area:auth, area:dev, type:chore

title: docs(spec): reconcile §03 scoped-token creation body example (label, expires_at_days, no note)
description:
## Problem / goal

\`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` §\"Creation\" (around line 571-580) shows a scoped-token body example like:

\`\`\`json
{
  \"name\": \"prod-ingest-bot\",
  \"scopes\": { ... },
  \"expires_at\": \"2026-07-01T00:00:00Z\",
  \"note\": \"for the ingest worker\"
}
\`\`\`

The actual router body \`MintTokenBody\` in \`app/api/v1/auth/tokens.py:109-115\` takes:

- \`label: str\` (1-160 chars) — NOT \`name\`
- \`scopes: dict[str, Any]\` — dict-shape (already reconciled by cd-wd78)
- \`expires_at_days: int\` (1-3650, default 90) — NOT \`expires_at\` ISO timestamp
- **No \`note\` field** — the router has no such concept

The §03 example teaches the wrong body shape. Code is authoritative (already shipped); §03 is the documenter.

## Technical context

- **Key files**:
  - \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` — §\"Creation\" (~ line 571-580), §\"Personal access tokens\" (~line 657-665), any other \`POST /auth/tokens\` body example
  - \`app/api/v1/auth/tokens.py:109-115\` — \`MintTokenBody\` Pydantic schema (authoritative)
- **Related recent work**: cd-wd78 reconciled the \`scopes\` shape list→dict, cd-8p4y (selfreview) flagged the remaining body-field drift.
- **Spec reference**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §\"Auth/tokens\" block preamble now correctly describes \`{label, scopes, expires_at_days?}\` — §03 should match.

## Implementation notes

- Update the body example to the real fields:
  \`\`\`json
  {
    \"label\": \"prod-ingest-bot\",
    \"scopes\": {\"tasks:read\": true, \"tasks:write\": true, \"stays:read\": true},
    \"expires_at_days\": 90
  }
  \`\`\`
- Surrounding prose: any \"pass a name\", \"ISO-8601 expires_at\", \"note field for operator comments\" → rewrite to match. The label doubles as the human-readable operator hint — there is no \`note\`.
- Check the PAT creation example around line 657-665 for the same drift. If it uses \`name\` / \`note\` / ISO \`expires_at\`, fix it too (the PAT router body may be the same \`MintTokenBody\` or a sibling).
- **Do NOT** add \`note\` to the router. The spec is the documenter.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] §\"Creation\" body example uses \`label\`, \`scopes\` (dict), \`expires_at_days\` (int)
- [ ] No \`note\` field appears in any §03 token body example
- [ ] Surrounding prose matches (no \"name\" / \"note\" / ISO \"expires_at\" language)
- [ ] \`rg -n '\"name\":' docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` returns zero scoped-token matches
- [ ] \`rg -n '\"note\":' docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` returns zero token-body matches
- [ ] If the PAT body example has the same drift, it's also fixed
- [ ] Integration tests at \`tests/integration/auth/test_tokens_pg.py\` still pass (they're exercising the real router body; no change needed there)

## Test plan

### Automated

\`\`\`bash
rg -n '\"name\"|\"note\"|\"expires_at\":' docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md
# Expect: zero hits in any token body example
pytest tests/integration/auth/test_tokens_pg.py -x -q
# Expect: still passing (the spec change is doc-only)
\`\`\`

### Manual

1. Grep the file top to bottom for any remaining \`POST /auth/tokens\` body example.
2. Read each prose paragraph around the examples — confirm no lingering \"name\" or \"note\" references.

## Refs

- Surfaced by cd-8p4y (selfreview of cd-wd78)
- Companion: cd-wd78 (reconciled scopes shape), cd-wd78 → cd-c4nn (error envelope fix)

title: Self-review: fix(api/errors): HTTPException(detail=dict) must spread into problem+json envelope
description:
## Problem / goal
Auto-fixing self-review of the changes made under cd-c4nn. Catch bugs,
missing pieces, and unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-c4nn** (main task must be complete first).

## How to run
Run \`/selfreview\` in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from cd-c4nn.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Specific checks:
  - **Every existing 4xx-body assertion across the test suite** (pytest tests/integration + tests/unit) — grep for \`\"detail\":\` + \`\"error\":\` in test files. The fix changes the envelope shape; some tests may need updating (this is expected, not a bug).
  - **HTTPException(detail=string) still renders as a string detail** — regression guard.
  - **HTTPException(detail=None)** still suppresses — regression guard.
  - **Edge cases**: list-shaped detail, tuple-shaped detail, int — str() fallback preserved.
  - **problem_response signature** — if cd-c4nn added a new kwarg / extras handling, confirm every caller's typing matches.
  - **Cross-envelope-field conflicts** — if an \`HTTPException(detail={\"title\": \"...\"})\` is raised anywhere, the dict-spread would clobber the envelope's own \`title\`. Protect against this in the handler (e.g. skip spreading reserved envelope keys like title/status/type/detail/instance).
  - **RFC 7807 §12 compliance**: read \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §\"Errors\" and confirm the fixed envelope matches the spec'd example shape.
- Run the quality gates:
  - \`pytest tests/unit tests/integration -x -q\`
  - \`ruff check app tests\`
  - \`mypy --strict app/api/errors.py\`
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

See \`.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All BUGS from the self-review fixed
- [ ] All MISSING pieces completed
- [ ] All RISKY items mitigated (or justified in a task comment)
- [ ] Linter, formatter, type checker, affected tests all pass
- [ ] Fixes committed and pushed

title: fix(api/errors): HTTPException(detail=dict) must spread into problem+json envelope instead of str()
description:
## Problem / goal

\`app/api/errors.py::_handle_http_exception\` (lines 397-403) renders \`exc.detail\` via plain \`str()\`:

\`\`\`python
detail_value: str | None
if exc.detail is None:
    detail_value = None
else:
    detail_value = str(exc.detail)
\`\`\`

When a router raises \`HTTPException(detail={\"error\": \"token_not_found\"})\`, the problem+json envelope returned to the client contains:

\`\`\`json
{\"detail\": \"{'error': 'token_not_found'}\", \"title\": \"Not found\", ...}
\`\`\`

— a **Python-repr string**, not a parseable JSON object. Clients that want the \`error\` symbol must string-parse a repr, which is a shipping-blocker bug for any SDK, CLI, or smart dashboard built against the spec.

The spec §12 is authoritative and documents \`{\"error\": \"<symbol>\"}\` as the error-condition symbol convention (see the preamble cd-wd78/cd-8p4y landed before the /auth/tokens block). The handler must make that true on the wire.

## Impact + affected routers

Every router that uses the \`HTTPException(detail={\"error\": ...})\` idiom is affected. \`rg -n 'HTTPException\\(.*detail=\\{\"error\"' app/api/v1/\` at task time — at least:

- \`app/api/v1/auth/tokens.py:247-250, 295-298\`
- \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\` (multiple)
- \`app/api/v1/auth/signup.py\`
- \`app/api/v1/auth/magic.py\`
- \`app/api/v1/auth/recovery.py\`
- \`app/api/v1/time.py\`
- \`app/api/v1/users.py\`

Integration tests at \`tests/integration/auth/test_tokens_pg.py:248-305\` (and siblings) mount a bare \`FastAPI()\` WITHOUT \`add_exception_handlers\`, so they see the raw dict-shaped \`{\"detail\": {\"error\": \"...\"}}\` and **do not exercise** the problem+json seam. This is why the bug slipped — the tests that would catch it don't exist.

## Fix options

### Option (a) — recommended: fix the handler to spread dict details

Detect dict-shaped \`exc.detail\` in \`_handle_http_exception\`, spread its keys into the problem+json envelope's \`extra\`, and set \`detail\` to the dict's \`message\` key (or None if not present). The envelope already supports \`extra\` via \`problem_response\`.

Sketch:

\`\`\`python
if isinstance(exc.detail, dict):
    envelope_extras = {k: v for k, v in exc.detail.items() if k != \"message\"}
    detail_value = exc.detail.get(\"message\")
else:
    envelope_extras = {}
    detail_value = str(exc.detail) if exc.detail is not None else None
# ... thread envelope_extras through problem_response(...)
\`\`\`

The router call sites do not change — the fix is entirely in the handler.

### Option (b) — deferred to a separate task: retire the idiom

Per the module docstring on \`app/api/errors.py:27-32\`, every handler that currently raises \`HTTPException(detail={\"error\": ...})\` SHOULD raise a typed \`DomainError\` subclass that this module translates to problem+json at the envelope level. Retiring the idiom is a larger change (many files); file a follow-up Beads task, do NOT do it here.

## Technical context

- **Key files**:
  - \`app/api/errors.py\` — \`_handle_http_exception\`, \`problem_response\`, type-map tables
  - Every file under \`app/api/v1/\` that grep hits above
- **Related**: RFC 7807 seam originally landed in cd-waq3 (now closed). This task completes what cd-waq3 started.
- **Spec reference**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §\"Errors\" (lines ~234-254) + the \`/auth/tokens\` block preamble added by cd-wd78 (line 406).

## Implementation notes

- Prefer option (a). Keep the change local to \`app/api/errors.py\`.
- The envelope's \`extra\` may already exist — read \`problem_response\` before writing.
- Preserve backward compat for \`HTTPException(detail=\"plain string\")\` — plain-string details still render as the \`detail\` field.
- Preserve backward compat for \`HTTPException(detail=None)\` — \`detail\` stays absent.
- Edge case: \`exc.detail\` is a non-dict non-string (list, tuple, int). Current code str()s it; keep that fallback.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] \`_handle_http_exception\` treats dict-shaped \`exc.detail\` as structured envelope fields; the \`error\` symbol surfaces at the envelope top level (or in \`extra\`), NOT as a repr'd string inside \`detail\`
- [ ] Plain-string details still render in the \`detail\` field as today
- [ ] None-detail still suppresses the \`detail\` field as today
- [ ] A new integration test under \`tests/integration/api/test_problem_json.py\` (or similar) mounts the real \`create_app()\` factory, hits a route that raises \`HTTPException(detail={\"error\": \"...\"})\`, and asserts the wire body has \`\"error\": \"...\"\` as a **structured JSON field**, NOT a repr string
- [ ] Every existing integration test that asserts on 4xx bodies still passes (the fix is backwards-compatible — old callers see the same field, just at the envelope level)
- [ ] Grep confirms no router call-site needed a signature change — all the work stayed in \`app/api/errors.py\`

## Test plan

### Automated

\`\`\`bash
pytest tests/integration/api/test_problem_json.py -xvs    # new test
pytest tests/integration -x -q                             # full integration
pytest tests/unit -x -q                                    # unit regressions
ruff check app tests
mypy --strict app/api/errors.py
\`\`\`

### Manual

\`\`\`bash
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/unknown/api/v1/auth/tokens/nope | jq .
# Expect: structured {\"type\": \"not_found\", \"title\": \"Not found\", \"error\": \"token_not_found\", ...}
# NOT:    {\"type\": \"not_found\", \"detail\": \"{'error': 'token_not_found'}\", ...}
\`\`\`

## Refs

- Surfaced by cd-8p4y (selfreview of cd-wd78)
- Companion: cd-waq3 (RFC 7807 envelope — closed; this closes the last gap)
- Module docstring on \`app/api/errors.py:27-32\` that describes the preferred \`DomainError\` idiom (out of scope here — file separately for the retirement pass)
close_reason: Implementation landed (docstring + dict-spread branch in app/api/errors.py); new integration tests in tests/integration/api/test_problem_json.py pin the envelope contract. Self-review (cd-c0a5) found no BUGS/MISSING/RISKY. All quality gates green.

title: Self-review: sec(auth/passkey): auto-revoke credential on clone detection (sign-count rollback)
description:
## Problem / goal
Auto-fixing self-review of the changes made under cd-cx19. Catch bugs,
missing pieces, and unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-cx19** (main task must be complete first).

## Scope of review

cd-cx19 shipped:
- \`_auto_revoke_credential_fresh_uow\` helper in \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\` — fresh UoW, audit-before-delete ordering, idempotent no-op (no false audit claim), broad Exception catch
- Wired into the \`except CloneDetected\` branch after the challenge-burn step
- Option B (inline) chosen over Option A (reuse revoke_passkey) because revoke_passkey's LastPasskeyCredential invariant would wrongly block a cloned-last-credential auto-revocation
- §15 rename webauthn→passkey (audit.passkey.auto_revoked, setting auth.passkey_rollback_auto_revoke), §02 settings table same rename
- Integration + unit test coverage for revocation, replay impossibility, audit row presence

## How to run

Run \`/selfreview\` in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from cd-cx19.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Specific checks:
  - **Idempotency-no-audit-on-no-op decision**: confirm the "no audit row if row already gone" logic is correct — an operator investigating a rollback sees exactly one auto_revoked per credential per incident. Edge case: what if the audit row write succeeds but the delete fails? The current order is audit-first-then-delete (matches revoke_passkey). Verify what happens on delete-raise AFTER audit-write: is the audit row committed in the same fresh UoW as the delete, or separate? If separate, the audit would land without the delete (phantom auto-revoke). Read the helper's commit-boundary carefully.
  - **Loading-query consistency**: with hard-delete and no \`deleted_at\`, the existing login path returns "unknown credential" on retry — confirm in \`app/auth/passkey.py::login_finish\` and its downstream loaders. NO query should special-case "deleted but still loadable" because there's no soft-delete.
  - **Double-audit avoidance**: cd-9t9q's \`_invalidate_for_credential_fresh_uow\` call still runs first — it emits \`audit.session.invalidated\` with cause \`clone_detected\`. cd-qx1f's \`_delete_challenge_fresh_uow\` also runs. Plus the new auto-revoke. Plus \`cloned_detected\` audit. Confirm there's NO duplicate \`passkey_revoked\` audit from the inline path (it should emit \`auto_revoked\`, not \`revoked\`).
  - **\`passkey_revoked\` session-invalidation cause collision**: cd-9t9q's §15 table lists both \`passkey_revoked\` and \`clone_detected\` as separate causes. Confirm the clone-detection path emits ONLY \`clone_detected\` (not both). Grep the code.
  - **Spec table completeness**: the §15 Session-invalidation causes table now says the clone_detected path mentions the sibling auto-revoke helper. Confirm the prose is accurate — it should read as complementary, not as if auto-revoke invalidates sessions (the session invalidation runs separately via \`_invalidate_for_credential_fresh_uow\`).
  - **Test coverage gap**: the coder added 2 integration tests. Is the "helper swallows a DB error silently" path tested? If not, add one — fixture-injected failing session factory, assert the outer 401 still returns, and assert a loud log appears.
  - **Race condition**: two concurrent clone-detected refusals on the same credential. One runs auto-revoke (audit + delete). Second runs auto-revoke, finds the row gone, logs no-op (no audit). Is this the right posture? — confirmed by the design decision, but verify behaviour via a unit test that simulates concurrent fresh-UoWs.
  - **Setting-key rename side-effects**: the coder confirmed no code references \`webauthn_rollback_auto_revoke\`. Re-grep to be sure. Also check there's no docs cross-link that still points at the old name.
- Run the quality gates:
  - \`pytest tests/unit/auth tests/integration/auth -x -q\`
  - \`ruff check app tests\`
  - \`mypy --strict app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py app/auth/passkey.py\`
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

See \`.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md\`.

- [ ] All RISKY items mitigated (or justified in a task comment)

title: schema(auth/passkey): add passkey_credential.deleted_at column + migration (blocks cd-cx19)
description:
## Context

cd-cx19 ships the clone-detection auto-revoke half of §15 'Passkey specifics' — on FIDO2 sign-count rollback, the server must set `passkey_credential.deleted_at = now()` and emit an auto-revoke audit row so the cloned credential cannot attempt login again.

## Blocker

`app/adapters/db/identity/models.py::PasskeyCredential` currently has no `deleted_at` column. Every other soft-deletable table in the project (`task_template`, `schedule`, `inventory_item`, etc.) uses a `deleted_at: datetime | None` column, but the passkey table has no such field and no credential-loading queries filter on it.

Grep evidence:
```
$ rg 'deleted_at' app/adapters/db/identity/models.py
(no matches)
```

Without the column, cd-cx19 cannot mark the credential as auto-revoked in any forward-compatible way.

## Scope

1. Add `deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True)` to `PasskeyCredential` in `app/adapters/db/identity/models.py`.
2. Add a matching Alembic migration that:
   - Adds the nullable `deleted_at` column.
   - Back-fills `NULL` for every existing row (implicit; nullable default).
   - Adds a partial / covering index on `(user_id, deleted_at)` — match the shape of `ix_task_template_workspace_deleted` so credential-load queries stay cheap.
3. Update every credential-loading query to filter `deleted_at IS NULL`:
   - `app/auth/passkey.py::_load_passkey_by_credential_id` (login path)
   - `app/auth/passkey.py::_count_passkeys` (cap check)
   - `app/auth/passkey.py::_existing_credential_ids` (excludeCredentials)
   - `app/auth/passkey.py::revoke_passkey` (consider soft-delete vs hard-delete — document choice)
4. Audit unit + integration tests for passkey loading to cover the `deleted_at IS NOT NULL` case (revoked credential is invisible to the login path).

## Out of scope

- The auto-revoke write itself (cd-cx19 picks that up once this blocker lands).
- Migrating the existing `revoke_passkey` hard-delete to a soft-delete is a follow-up decision — it is orthogonal to this task's schema change and changes the audit trail semantics. File a separate task if that migration is wanted.

## Links

- cd-cx19 (clone-detection auto-revoke — blocked on this)
- `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §'Passkey specifics' line 330: 'sets passkey.deleted_at = now() in the same transaction that detected the rollback'
- `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` (update once the column lands)

## Acceptance

- [ ] `PasskeyCredential.deleted_at` column exists, nullable, `TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE`
- [ ] Alembic migration adds column + partial / covering index; revision file under `app/adapters/db/migrations/`
- [ ] Credential-loading queries (`_load_passkey_by_credential_id`, `_count_passkeys`, `_existing_credential_ids`) filter `deleted_at IS NULL`
- [ ] Unit + integration tests verify a credential with `deleted_at IS NOT NULL` is invisible to login + registration caps + excludeCredentials
- [ ] cd-cx19 unblocked
close_reason: Superseded: user chose hard-delete (reuse revoke_passkey) instead of soft-delete via deleted_at. Schema migration unnecessary for cd-cx19.

title: Self-review: sec(auth/passkey): delete challenge row on verification failure (single-use on failure)
description:
## Problem / goal
Auto-fixing self-review of the changes made under cd-qx1f. Catch bugs,
missing pieces, and unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-qx1f** (main task must be complete first).

## Scope of review

cd-qx1f shipped:
- `_delete_challenge_fresh_uow(challenge_id)` helper in \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\` (DELETE ... WHERE id = ? via make_uow, idempotent, broad except + log-and-swallow)
- Wired into the \`except _DomainError\` blocks of \`post_register_finish\` + \`post_signup_register_finish\`, the \`except CloneDetected\` branch AND the \`except (InvalidLoginAttempt, ChallengeNotFound, ChallengeAlreadyConsumed, ChallengeExpired, ChallengeSubjectMismatch)\` branch of \`post_login_finish\`
- PasskeyLoginLockout deliberately NOT covered (fires pre-DB-read, challenge kept so rate-limited caller can retry)
- §03 "WebAuthn specifics" new bullet explicitly documenting single-use-even-on-failure + the PasskeyLoginLockout exception
- 15 new tests (7 register + signup, 8 login) asserting post-failure challenge is gone
- 2 integration tests tightened with row-gone assertions
- domain \`app/auth/passkey.py\` unchanged — fresh-UoW at the router seam

## How to run

Run \`/selfreview\` in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from cd-qx1f.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Specific failure modes:
  - **Idempotency**: confirm the delete helper really is idempotent — build a test that runs it twice on the same id and assert the second call is silent.
  - **Exception swallowing**: the helper does \`except Exception: log-and-swallow\` — verify the log is loud enough to catch a real DB outage, and that a genuinely-broken delete doesn't hide an ongoing exploit. Consider whether a narrower except is safer (e.g. \`except SQLAlchemyError\`).
  - **Completeness**: grep every place \`login_finish\` / \`register_finish\` / \`register_finish_signup\` is called. If any call-site (e.g. inside \`complete_signup\`, \`complete_recovery\`) can emit those exceptions without the router's fresh-UoW delete catching them, the challenge survives the failure. The coder flagged this as a follow-up — confirm it's filed as a separate Beads task (cd-\\w+ reference) OR file it now.
  - **Extra failure shapes**: py_webauthn's verifier can raise \`InvalidRegistrationResponse\`, \`InvalidAuthenticationResponse\`, library-internal errors. Confirm the \`except _DomainError\` + enumerated exception list covers every raise shape the domain translates verifier errors to. If a library exception escapes untranslated, the handler returns 500 AND the challenge survives.
  - **Test coverage gaps**: the new tests seed challenges and drive failures directly. Do they also cover the case where \`/start\` is posted, \`/finish\` is posted with a maliciously-different challenge_id (attacker-controlled challenge_id in request body)? The spec single-use posture should apply to that too.
  - **PasskeyLoginLockout decision**: the coder excluded this from the delete. Is that right? A locked-out caller with a leaked challenge_id can wait out the 60s lockout and replay the challenge until 10-min TTL. This may be fine (rate-limit is the primary defense) but the selfreview should verify the reasoning matches the threat model in §15 or the task's intent.
- Run the quality gates:
  - \`pytest tests/unit/auth tests/integration/auth -x -q\`
  - \`ruff check app tests\`
  - \`mypy --strict app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\`
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

See \`.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All BUGS from the self-review fixed
- [ ] All MISSING pieces completed
- [ ] All RISKY items mitigated (or justified in a task comment)
- [ ] Linter, formatter, type checker, affected tests all pass
- [ ] Fixes committed and pushed

title: chore(auth/signup): clarify or retire dual /auth/passkey/signup/register/* surface
description:
## Problem / goal

Two **distinct** bare-host passkey signup endpoints exist in the codebase, both delegating to the same domain functions (\`app.auth.passkey.register_start_signup\` / \`register_finish_signup\`), but with different outer wiring. Neither has a SPA caller yet (grep of \`app/web\` + \`mocks/web\` returns zero hits for either URL) — but both have tests and both are behind the \`__all__\` of their respective modules, so they are load-bearing against future wiring and deserve a clear design rationale.

### Surface 1: \`/api/v1/auth/passkey/signup/register/{start,finish}\`

- Owner module: \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\` (\`signup_router\` declared at line 363)
- Handlers: \`register_start_signup\` / \`register_finish_signup\` route entries at ~lines 371-388 and 396-416
- \`finish\` here ONLY persists the passkey row — takes a caller-supplied \`user_id\` (so a \`User\` row must already exist)
- Tests: \`tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_router.py:306,322\` (direct-router unit tests), \`tests/unit/test_tenancy_middleware.py:424,429\` (middleware opt-out), \`tests/tenant/_optouts.py:47-48\` (cross-tenant-test opt-outs)
- SPA-side hint: \`app/web/src/auth/onUnauthorized.ts:55-57\` comment says "Signup passkey ceremony is mounted under /auth/passkey/signup" and the 401-handler skips redirect for \`/api/v1/auth/passkey/signup/\` paths → the SPA's mental model is "this is the signup path"

### Surface 2: \`/api/v1/signup/passkey/{start,finish}\`

- Owner module: \`app/api/v1/auth/signup.py\` (\`router\` declared in \`build_signup_router\`, ~line 500+)
- Handlers: \`@router.post("/passkey/start")\` at ~line 130, \`@router.post("/passkey/finish")\` at ~line 147
- \`finish\` here wraps \`complete_signup\` — creates the \`User\` row + workspace + seeds owners permission group + emits audit
- Tests: \`tests/integration/auth/test_signup_full_flow.py:282,295\` (canonical end-to-end signup), \`tests/unit/auth/test_signup.py:1369,1379\` (unit)
- Module docstring at \`app/api/v1/auth/signup.py:14-25\` explicitly calls these "the SPA signup completion endpoints"

### The contradiction

- \`signup.py\`'s docstring + integration test claim \`/signup/passkey/*\` is the canonical SPA entry point
- The SPA's \`onUnauthorized.ts\` comment claims \`/auth/passkey/signup/*\` is "mounted under" the signup ceremony
- Both routes exist, both have tests, neither is called by any committed SPA code

This is either:
- (a) **Deliberate agent/admin-facing seam**: \`/auth/passkey/signup/register/finish\` is for an admin or agent flow that creates the user through some OTHER path (e.g. invite acceptance) and then needs to attach the first passkey without going through \`complete_signup\`. If so, it needs a spec paragraph and a test confirming it refuses to create-user-on-the-fly.
- (b) **Accidental parallel surface from a refactor**: one is leftover from an older design where the signup router didn't exist. If so, delete the stale one, update \`onUnauthorized.ts\` to point at the canonical URL, update the opt-out and middleware tests.
- (c) **Intentional stable alias**: both are meant to be permanent, callers choose based on "do I already have a user row?". If so, §12 + §03 need to say that explicitly.

## Technical context

- **Key files**:
  - \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\` — signup_router at line 363+ + handlers 371-416
  - \`app/api/v1/auth/signup.py\` — router with passkey sub-routes + module docstring
  - \`app/api/factory.py:277-281, 294-302\` — where each router is mounted
  - \`app/auth/passkey.py:1004-1125\` — shared domain functions
  - \`app/web/src/auth/onUnauthorized.ts:55-57\` — SPA 401-handler (update if routes change)
  - \`tests/tenant/_optouts.py:47-48\` + \`tests/unit/test_tenancy_middleware.py:424\`
- **Spec sections to touch**: \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` §"Self-serve signup", \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` bare-host auth block

## Implementation notes

Step 1 is **investigate**, not implement. Use \`git log -- app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py app/api/v1/auth/signup.py\` to see which router came first and whether the commit messages name a design rationale. If the history is silent, use \`AskUserQuestion\` — this is a design call with long-lasting impact (§AGENTS.md says auth-surface decisions are not guess-territory).

Options, in preference order:

- **(b) delete the stale one** if the grep shows zero callers and git log shows one is a leftover. Ship the deletion, update \`onUnauthorized.ts\`, clean up the tenant-opt-outs and middleware unit test. Small commit, big cognitive-load win.
- **(a) / (c) document** if both are deliberately kept. Add a short §03 paragraph naming each surface's contract ("use X when Y; use X' when Y'") and a test asserting the per-endpoint contract (\`/auth/passkey/signup/register/finish\` returns 4xx when no matching \`User\` row exists; \`/signup/passkey/finish\` returns 4xx when called with a stale signup_session_id that already resolved).

## Acceptance criteria (exactly one of)

### Path A — Retire \`/auth/passkey/signup/register/*\`

- [ ] \`signup_router\` + its two handlers removed from \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\`
- [ ] \`app/api/factory.py\` no longer mounts the signup router from passkey.py
- [ ] \`app/web/src/auth/onUnauthorized.ts\` + its test updated to the canonical \`/api/v1/signup/passkey/\` prefix
- [ ] \`tests/tenant/_optouts.py\` entries for the deleted paths removed
- [ ] \`tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_router.py\` signup-flow tests removed or migrated to the canonical router
- [ ] \`tests/unit/test_tenancy_middleware.py\` test example updated
- [ ] \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` — \`/auth/passkey/signup/register/*\` entries removed (cd-vbls added them; they come out in this commit)
- [ ] Rationale in the commit message names the stale-path git history

### Path B — Document the distinction

- [ ] \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` §"Self-serve signup" has an explicit paragraph naming each endpoint and when to use it
- [ ] \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` bare-host Auth block has a matching inline note on both routes
- [ ] New unit test: \`/api/v1/auth/passkey/signup/register/finish\` refuses to create a user — returns 4xx (shape defined by the spec clarification) when the caller-supplied \`user_id\` doesn't exist
- [ ] New unit test: \`/api/v1/signup/passkey/finish\` refuses when the signup_session has already resolved
- [ ] \`app/web/src/auth/onUnauthorized.ts\` comment updated to match the spec's final decision rule

## Test plan

### Automated

\`\`\`bash
# Before changes — record current state of call graph
rg -n '/api/v1/auth/passkey/signup/|/api/v1/signup/passkey/' app tests

# After changes (whichever path)
pytest tests/integration/auth/test_signup_full_flow.py tests/unit/auth/test_signup.py tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_router.py tests/unit/test_tenancy_middleware.py -xvs

# Path A only
pnpm -C app/web test onUnauthorized.test.ts
\`\`\`

### Manual

1. Read \`git log --follow app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\` and \`git log --follow app/api/v1/auth/signup.py\`.
2. Identify which router landed first and whether the commit messages give a design reason.
3. If history is silent, ask the user for the design call (path A vs B) before committing.
4. After landing, \`rg -n 'passkey/signup/register'\` should either vanish (path A) or only appear in the clarifying spec paragraphs + their test (path B).

## Refs

- Surfaced by cd-fub0 (selfreview of cd-vbls)
- cd-vbls just renamed the \`/auth/webauthn/*\` ghost routes to \`/auth/passkey/signup/register/*\` — the ghost routes have been in §12 for months, which is one more reason to confirm this path really is live-and-used, not resurrected drift
- Related: cd-g71h (missing \`/signup/passkey/*\` in §12 — separate task)

title: docs(spec): reconcile §12 bare-host exhaustive list with /signup/passkey/* routes
description:
## Problem / goal

\`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` lines 24-31 claim an **exhaustive** list of bare-host routes:

\`\`\`
/api/openapi.json, /api/v1/signup/{start,verify},
/api/v1/auth/{login,logout,magic-link/redeem,passkey/*},
/api/v1/me/workspaces, /api/v1/healthz, /api/v1/readyz,
/api/v1/version
\`\`\`

This is incomplete. \`app/api/v1/auth/signup.py\` mounts two more bare-host routes via \`build_signup_router\` (wired in \`app/api/factory.py:294-302\`):

- \`POST /api/v1/signup/passkey/start\` — SPA signup completion, step 2 (after \`/signup/verify\`)
- \`POST /api/v1/signup/passkey/finish\` — SPA signup completion, step 3 (wraps \`complete_signup\`: creates user + workspace + seeds owners group + audit)

Both are exercised by \`tests/integration/auth/test_signup_full_flow.py:282,295\` and \`tests/unit/auth/test_signup.py:1369,1379\`.

Pure doc reconciliation — no code change. Companion to cd-vbls (which fixed the webauthn→passkey rename in the same §12 block).

## Technical context

- **Authoritative source**: \`app/api/v1/auth/signup.py\` — \`@router.post("/passkey/start")\` at ~line 130, \`@router.post("/passkey/finish")\` at ~line 147. The module docstring at lines 14-25 already describes the delegations clearly.
- **§12 line 24-31** is the exhaustive-list claim that needs to be updated.
- **§12 lines ~348-395** is the bare-host \`Auth\` block where the new rows go. Style template: neighbouring entries like \`/auth/recover/start\` and \`/auth/magic/send\`, plus the newly-landed \`/auth/passkey/*\` entries from cd-vbls (lines 351-354).
- **Cross-link target**: §03 "Self-serve signup" — the prose flow description (\`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md:90\`).

## Implementation notes

- Either expand the line-27 list to \`/api/v1/signup/{start,verify,passkey/start,passkey/finish}\`, or — cleaner — collapse to \`/api/v1/signup/*\` if the spec is willing to say all \`/signup/*\` sub-paths are bare-host. Given that other entries use \`*\` shorthand (\`/auth/passkey/*\`), the collapsed form seems consistent — but check whether a future signup route might NOT be bare-host before over-committing to the wildcard.
- For the §"Auth" bare-host block, list the two routes with one-line inline comments:
  - \`/signup/passkey/start\` — "signup step 2: mints the registration challenge for the signup_session_id; no session cookie"
  - \`/signup/passkey/finish\` — "signup step 3: completes signup atomically (user + workspace + owners group + audit) and persists the first passkey; sets \`__Host-crewday_session\` on success"
  - Verify these claims against the handlers in \`app/api/v1/auth/signup.py\`.
- Do NOT delete or alter the \`/auth/passkey/signup/register/*\` entries cd-vbls just landed — those are a separate route (see cd-geg7 for the dual-surface question).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Line 27 bare-host exhaustive list updated (either expanded or wildcarded) to include \`/signup/passkey/start\` and \`/signup/passkey/finish\`
- [ ] §"Auth" bare-host route block enumerates \`/signup/passkey/start\` and \`/signup/passkey/finish\` with accurate inline comments
- [ ] Style matches neighbouring entries (same inline-comment voice, column alignment)
- [ ] Cross-link to §03 "Self-serve signup" resolves (anchor at \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md:90\`)
- [ ] \`rg -n 'exhaustive' docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` — the "exhaustive" claim is still honoured (or deliberately relaxed with a \`(non-exhaustive)\` caveat if that's the right call — see cd-geg7 which may add more bare-host routes)

## Test plan

### Automated

\`\`\`bash
# The exhaustive list actually covers what the code exposes:
rg -n '@(?:app|router)\\.post' app/api/v1/auth/signup.py app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py
\`\`\`

Cross-read against §12's bare-host + workspace-scoped blocks; every bare-host decorator should have a matching §12 row.

### Manual

1. Open \`app/api/v1/auth/signup.py\` and \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` side by side.
2. For each \`@router.post("/passkey/...")\` in the .py, confirm the corresponding line in §12.
3. Read the updated §12 §"Auth" bare-host block top-to-bottom — confirm no ordering or grouping surprises.

## Refs

- Surfaced by cd-fub0 (selfreview of cd-vbls)
- Related: cd-vbls (just reconciled webauthn→passkey in the same block), cd-geg7 (dual /auth/passkey/signup/register/* vs /signup/passkey/* design question)
- \`app/api/v1/auth/signup.py\` module docstring (lines 14-25) is the best cheat-sheet
