title: fix(api/auth): annotate passkey routes with operation_id + openapi_extra {x-cli, x-interactive-only}
description:
## Problem / goal

§12-rest-api.md §\"Rule for mutating routes\" + §\"CLI surface
extensions\" (lines 68-100, 173-186) mandates every route carry
\`operation_id\` + \`openapi_extra={\"x-cli\": {...}}\` in its FastAPI
decorator; every mutating route must additionally carry exactly one of
\`x-agent-confirm\` | \`x-agent-forbidden\` | \`x-interactive-only\`.

The CI gate (\`openapi-agent-annotations\`) specified in §17 is not yet
wired (the \`cli/\` directory + generator don't exist in v1 yet), but
§12 is authoritative — routes landed now WILL be retroactively audited
when the gate is wired, and annotating as we go prevents a big-bang
retrofit.

\`rg -n 'openapi_extra' app/\` returns **zero hits** — no route in the
codebase currently carries the \`x-cli\` extension. That's a systemic
gap; this task scopes to **\`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\`** (the file
cd-hiko just touched, so the annotation debt is freshly visible). The
wider repo sweep should be filed separately.

## Technical context

- **Target file**: \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\` — three routers:
  workspace-scoped (\`/auth/passkey\` prefix), bare-host signup
  (\`/auth/passkey/signup\`), bare-host login (\`/auth/passkey/login\`)
- **Routes to annotate** (confirm by grep; do not miss any):
  - POST /auth/passkey/register/start (workspace-scoped)
  - POST /auth/passkey/register/finish (workspace-scoped)
  - DELETE /auth/passkey/{credential_id} (workspace-scoped, cd-hiko)
  - POST /auth/passkey/signup/register/start (bare-host)
  - POST /auth/passkey/signup/register/finish (bare-host)
  - POST /auth/passkey/login/start (bare-host)
  - POST /auth/passkey/login/finish (bare-host)
  - (+ any email-verify routes co-located there)
- **Pattern to mimic** (operation_id only, x-cli missing): \`app/api/v1/time.py\` — \`@router.post(... operation_id=\"time.open_shift\", summary=\"…\")\`
- **operation_id format** (§12 line 68-73): \`{group}.{verb}\` dot-separated, first segment matches a CLI group name. Suggested:
  - \`auth.passkey.register_start\` / \`auth.passkey.register_finish\`
  - \`auth.passkey.revoke\` (for DELETE)
  - \`auth.passkey.signup_register_start\` / \`.signup_register_finish\`
  - \`auth.passkey.login_start\` / \`.login_finish\`
- **x-cli extension schema** (§12 lines 82-95):
  \`\`\`python
  openapi_extra={
      \"x-cli\": {
          \"group\": \"auth\",
          \"verb\": \"passkey-revoke\",
          \"summary\": \"Revoke one of your passkeys\",
          \"mutates\": True,
          \"hidden\": True,  # browser-only ceremony — see §12 line 91
      },
  }
  \`\`\`
- **Which extension applies per route** (§12 lines 173-186):
  - **DELETE /auth/passkey/{credential_id}** → \`x-interactive-only: true\` (credential revocation requires a passkey session per §03 \"Additional passkeys\"; PATs and delegated tokens must reject). NOT hidden — CLI users should be able to revoke passkeys.
  - **POST register/start + register/finish (workspace-scoped)** → \`x-interactive-only: true\` AND \`hidden: true\` (browser WebAuthn ceremony; no CLI surface).
  - **Bare-host signup/login/register/finish** → \`hidden: true\` + NO agent extension needed because they're pre-session (no delegated/PAT caller possible — §12 table rule says mutating routes need one of the three, but pre-session is outside that scope). If the gate complains, \`x-interactive-only\` applies transitively (they can't be reached by a token because there IS no session yet). Confirm which way the gate interprets pre-session mutating routes; if ambiguous, add \`x-interactive-only: true\` as the safe default.
- **hidden routes** go in \`cli/crewday/_exclusions.yaml\` with a reason per §13 / §12 line 91 — but \`cli/\` doesn't exist yet. Leave the \`hidden: true\` on each ceremony route; when \`cli/\` lands the exclusions file will pick them up.

## Implementation notes

- Don't add \`operation_id\` / \`openapi_extra\` to GET-only endpoints unless they're already mutating-looking; for GETs, \`x-cli.mutates: false\` is enough.
- **Pre-existing annotation audit**: the file already has \`operation_id\`? Re-check my grep — if some are present, extend rather than duplicate.
- Write a regression test (below) that parses the generated OpenAPI and asserts every passkey route carries both annotations — this guards against the next passkey route landing un-annotated.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every route defined in \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\` carries \`operation_id\` matching the \`{group}.{verb}\` format
- [ ] Every route carries \`openapi_extra={\"x-cli\": {...}}\` with at minimum \`group\`, \`verb\`, \`summary\`, and \`mutates\` fields
- [ ] Every **mutating** route (POST/DELETE) carries exactly one of \`x-agent-confirm\` / \`x-agent-forbidden\` / \`x-interactive-only\` in its \`openapi_extra\`
- [ ] DELETE /auth/passkey/{credential_id} is explicitly \`x-interactive-only: true\`
- [ ] Browser-only ceremonies (register/start, register/finish, signup/*, login/*) carry \`x-cli.hidden: true\`
- [ ] A new unit test parses \`app.openapi()\` (or whatever factory returns the dict) and asserts every \`/auth/passkey/\` path has both \`operationId\` and \`x-cli\` populated; fails loudly if a future route omits either
- [ ] Existing passkey integration tests still pass (behaviour is unchanged)

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
# New regression test
pytest tests/api/v1/auth/test_passkey_openapi_annotations.py -xvs

# Existing passkey tests still pass
pytest tests/api/v1/auth/test_passkey.py tests/auth/test_passkey.py -xvs

# OpenAPI generation still succeeds
python -c 'from app.main import create_app; create_app().openapi()'
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. \`curl http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/openapi.json | jq '.paths | to_entries[] | select(.key | contains(\"auth/passkey\")) | {path: .key, ops: (.value | to_entries | map({verb: .key, id: .value.operationId, x_cli: .value.\"x-cli\"}))}'\`
2. Every entry should have a populated \`id\` AND a populated \`x_cli\` dict.
3. DELETE entry should have \`\"x-interactive-only\": true\` at the same level as \`x-cli\`.

## Out of scope (file separately if desired)

- Wiring the \`openapi-agent-annotations\` CI gate itself (§17) — needs \`cli/\` directory + generator.
- Repo-wide annotation retrofit of every other \`app/api/v1/*.py\` route — \`rg openapi_extra\` showed zero hits, so this is a systemic gap worth its own task once the gate lands.

## Refs

- §12 §\"operationId convention\" (lines 66-73)
- §12 §\"CLI surface extensions (x-cli)\" (lines 75-100)
- §12 §\"Rule for mutating routes\" (lines 173-186)
- §12 §\"Interactive-only extension\" (lines 156-172)
- §17 \`openapi-agent-annotations\` gate (not yet wired)
- Related: cd-hiko (DELETE shipped un-annotated), cd-a3ux (docs update)
- Example of partial compliance: \`app/api/v1/time.py\` (has operation_id, missing x-cli)

title: docs(spec): reconcile §12 bare-host /auth/* routes with code (passkey, not webauthn)
description:
## Problem / goal

\`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` lists stale bare-host auth routes that
don't match \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\`:

- **Line 27** (overview): names \`/api/v1/auth/{login,logout,magic-link/redeem,webauthn/*}\` — \`webauthn/*\` never existed in the code; the historical names were \`/passkey/*\` from day one.
- **Lines 351-354** (bare-host auth listing):
  \`\`\`
  POST   /api/v1/auth/webauthn/begin_registration
  POST   /api/v1/auth/webauthn/finish_registration
  POST   /api/v1/auth/webauthn/begin_login
  POST   /api/v1/auth/webauthn/finish_login
  \`\`\`
  These are ghost routes. The actual bare-host passkey surface in
  \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\` is:
  - \`POST /api/v1/auth/passkey/signup/register/start\` — begin the
    signup-flow passkey ceremony (fresh account, no session yet)
  - \`POST /api/v1/auth/passkey/signup/register/finish\` — verify +
    persist the first passkey for a fresh account
  - \`POST /api/v1/auth/passkey/login/start\` — conditional-UI login
    ceremony (no session yet)
  - \`POST /api/v1/auth/passkey/login/finish\` — verify login assertion
    and mint a session
  - (+ any email-verification routes co-located there — confirm by reading the file)

This is pure spec drift — the code is authoritative, the doc is wrong.

## Technical context

- **Authoritative source**: \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\` — grep every
  \`@router.*\` / \`@signup_router.*\` / \`@login_router.*\` / \`@app.*\`
  decorator; the prefixes on the routers tell you bare-host vs
  workspace-scoped.
- **§12 §\"Bare-host routes\"** block is at lines ~345-395.
- **Related recent work**: cd-hiko (just shipped DELETE), cd-a3ux (just
  shipped the workspace-scoped /auth/passkey block right after the
  /auth/tokens listing — use that as the style template).
- **Do not touch** the workspace-scoped passkey block added by cd-a3ux
  (lines ~408+) — that's already correct.

## Implementation notes

- Preserve the inline-comment style of surrounding routes (e.g. the
  \`# scoped; same token lands new users in a passkey ceremony\` style).
- Reflect whatever \`x-interactive-only\` / CSRF posture actually applies
  — login/signup pre-session routes are pre-auth so CSRF is handled
  differently; confirm by reading the middleware chain in
  \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\`.
- If any bare-host \`/auth/passkey/*\` route is present in the code but
  missing from §12 (e.g. email-verification), add it in the same pass.
- Line 27's overview list gets \`webauthn/*\` replaced with \`passkey/*\`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every \`webauthn\` string removed from \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\`
      (grep verifies)
- [ ] Every bare-host route defined in \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\` is
      enumerated in §12 §\"Bare-host routes\" with matching HTTP verb,
      path, and a one-line description
- [ ] Line 27 overview list updated to \`passkey/*\`
- [ ] Style matches neighbouring blocks (inline comment shape, indent,
      column alignment)
- [ ] Cross-references to §03 (where the passkey ceremonies are
      described in prose) still resolve

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
rg -n 'webauthn' docs/specs/12-rest-api.md || echo 'clean'
\`\`\`
Should print \`clean\`.

\`\`\`bash
# spot-check every bare-host passkey route from code is in §12
rg -n '@(?:router|signup_router|login_router)\.' app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py
\`\`\`
Cross-read against §12 to confirm coverage.

### Manual
1. Open \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` and \`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py\`
   side by side.
2. For each decorator in the .py, confirm the corresponding line in §12.
3. Confirm no dead \`webauthn\` reference remains anywhere in the file.

## Refs
- Discovered during cd-a3ux coder pass (surfaced by the subagent)
- Related: cd-hiko (DELETE route), cd-a3ux (workspace-scoped listing),
  cd-geqp (invalidate_for_user seam)

title: Self-review: spec: document DELETE /auth/passkey/{credential_id} in §12 REST + §03 Additional passkeys
description:
## Problem / goal
Auto-fixing self-review of the changes made under cd-a3ux. Catch bugs,
missing pieces, and unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-a3ux** (main task must be complete first).

## Scope of review

  rationale

  revoke semantics, last-credential refusal with recovery fallback,
  session invalidation with cause `passkey_revoked`, caveat that
  `passkey_registered` also invalidates the caller's own session
- `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` — new "Session-invalidation
  causes" subsection with a 4-row catalogue (`passkey_registered`,
  `passkey_revoked`, `recovery_consumed`, `clone_detected`)

## How to run
Run `/selfreview` in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from cd-a3ux.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Pay particular attention to:
  - Catalogue completeness: grep \`invalidate_for_user\` and
    \`invalidate_for_credential\` across the codebase; every cause
    emitted MUST appear in the §15 table.
  - Cross-link integrity: §03, §12, §15 now reference each other;
    confirm anchors / section names resolve.
  - Style drift: §12 has a consistent inline-comment style; confirm the
    new block matches neighbours.
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

See .claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md.

title: spec: document DELETE /auth/passkey/{credential_id} in §12 REST + §03 Additional passkeys
description:
## Problem / goal

cd-hiko shipped the passkey-delete route (DELETE /auth/passkey/{credential_id}) and the selfreview (cd-aww6) confirmed the behaviour but flagged that neither docs/specs/12-rest-api.md (REST surface) nor docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md §"Additional passkeys" documents the route. The code is ahead of the spec.

## Scope

- Add the DELETE route to §12-rest-api.md alongside the other /auth/passkey/* endpoints. Include:
  - 204 on success
  - 404 passkey_not_found (covers both unknown id AND wrong-owner, to avoid enumeration oracle)
  - 422 last_credential (refusing to revoke the only remaining passkey)
  - CSRF-guarded (global middleware)
- Extend §03 "Additional passkeys" (docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md:262) with a short paragraph:
  - "users may remove their own passkeys; removing the last one is refused and requires recovery; every active session is invalidated on remove (cause passkey_revoked)."
  - Cross-link §15 "Shared-origin XSS containment" (credential-population change → invalidate_for_user).
- Capture the invalidation-cause catalogue somewhere visible (likely §15 "Cookies and sessions" or §03 "Sessions"): passkey_registered, passkey_revoked, recovery_consumed, etc. — otherwise future causes drift.

## Acceptance criteria
- DELETE route documented in §12 with full error table
- §03 "Additional passkeys" references the revoke flow + session invalidation
- Invalidation-cause catalogue exists and lists passkey_revoked
- /update-openapi run after the §12 edit so the generated schema tracks

## Refs
- app/auth/passkey.py revoke_passkey()
- app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py delete_passkey()
- docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md §"Shared-origin XSS containment" (cd-geqp)

title: feat(api/recover): accept and gate break_glass_code on /recover/passkey/request for step-up users
description:
## Problem

RecoverPage (app/web) collects a `break_glass_code` when the user
toggles the step-up checkbox (§03 "Entry point" — managers and
owners-group members must submit both email AND an unused
break-glass code). The UI serialises it into the request body, but
the server's `RecoveryRequestBody` pydantic schema only declares
`email` — the extra `break_glass_code` field is silently dropped
by pydantic's default (no `extra="forbid"`).

Net effect: a manager believes they completed the step-up
ceremony, receives a magic link, and the recovery path wasn't
actually gated on their role. That violates spec §03 which says
managers/owners MUST present the break-glass code for a recovery
magic link to be minted; for workers/clients/guests, the code is
ignored and never burnt.

## Scope

- Extend `RecoveryRequestBody` with `break_glass_code: str | None`.
- Route through to `recovery.request_recovery()` which is
  responsible for the gating logic per spec §03 step 3/4:
  - If target user is a manager or owners-group member on any
    shared scope, require `break_glass_code` to match an unused
    row, burn it on match, mail the magic link on success, and
    log `auth.recover.stepup_missing` / `auth.recover.stepup_invalid`
    on failure (no mail sent).
  - If target user has NO manager/owners grant anywhere, ignore
    the code and always mail the link.
- Keep the 202 envelope stable regardless — the enumeration guard
  still wins over any role-based discriminator in the response.
- Audit log must distinguish the branches so operators can see
  which step-up validation rejected which request.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `RecoveryRequestBody` accepts `break_glass_code`.
- [ ] Manager without code → 202 on response; no magic link mailed;
      `auth.recover.stepup_missing` audited.
- [ ] Manager with wrong code → 202; no mail;
      `auth.recover.stepup_invalid` audited.
- [ ] Manager with correct code → magic link mailed; code burnt
      (`used_at` set); `auth.recover.requested` audited with
      `hit=True` + `stepup=True`.
- [ ] Worker with code → code ignored and NOT burnt; magic link
      mailed as per existing path.
- [ ] Spec §03 §"Request" vocabulary matches the implementation
      (endpoint + payload).
- [ ] Integration test in `tests/integration/auth/` covers all four
      branches above.

## Context

- Existing test `tests/integration/auth/test_recovery_full_flow.py`
- Spec: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Self-service
  lost-device recovery" → "Request" step 3/4.
- Found during /selfreview of cd-gids (RecoverPage implementation).

title: feat(web/auth): LoginPage — sanitize next param before <Navigate to={next}>
description:
Defence-in-depth for the `?next=` round-trip.

## Context

Both `RequireAuth` and `createOnUnauthorized` (cd-kc7u) now route the
`here` path through `sanitizeNext` in `app/web/src/auth/onUnauthorized.ts`
before emitting `?next=<...>`. That covers the two emission points, but
the **consumption point** — the LoginPage (cd-4z54) — still has to re-validate
the query-param value before handing it to `<Navigate to={next}>`, because
a user could land on `/login?next=<attacker-controlled>` via phishing.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] LoginPage imports `sanitizeNext` from `@/auth` and filters the
      `?next=` query param through it before using it in `<Navigate>`
- [ ] Unsafe values (`//evil.example`, `https://evil.example`,
      `/javascript:...`, `/\evil.example`) fall back to `/`
- [ ] A test verifies that a crafted `?next=https://evil.example/` does
      NOT cause `<Navigate>` to land off-origin

## References

- `app/web/src/auth/onUnauthorized.ts` — `sanitizeNext()` export
- `app/web/src/auth/onUnauthorized.test.ts` — same sanitiser tests

title: chore(worker): wire prune_expired_idempotency_keys into APScheduler daily tick
description:
## Problem / goal
`app.api.middleware.idempotency.prune_expired_idempotency_keys()`
exists as a callable but is not yet scheduled. Without a recurring
sweep, the `idempotency_key` table grows unbounded — each retry
adds a row and nothing deletes old ones.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"Idempotency" — TTL 24 h.
- **Code**: `app/api/middleware/idempotency.py` (cd-z6fk). The
  callable opens its own UoW when no session is supplied and
  returns the number of rows deleted.
- **Scheduler**: Not yet wired (`app/worker/__init__.py` is a
  docstring-only module). Depends on the broader APScheduler
  bootstrap.

## Implementation notes
- Register `prune_expired_idempotency_keys` as a daily job on the
  in-process APScheduler instance once the scheduler bootstrap lands.
- Bump `worker_heartbeat` under a stable worker name
  (`idempotency_sweep`) so `/readyz` knows the job is alive.
- Log the deletion count at INFO so operators can correlate the
  table's steady-state size with the sweep cadence.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] APScheduler job registered on app startup (or worker process)
- [ ] Runs once per day
- [ ] `worker_heartbeat` row updates on each tick
- [ ] Deletion count logged at INFO with `event=idempotency.sweep`
- [ ] Integration test: seed an expired row, let the job run,
  assert it's gone

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/worker/test_idempotency_sweep.py -xvs
```

title: sec(llm): per-IP 30-day LLM spend cap, 402 payment_required + verify-ownership flow
description:
## Problem / goal
Spec §15 "Per-IP aggregate LLM spend cap" + cd-7huk AC: *"Per-IP LLM
spend cap returns `402 payment_required` with the spec banner copy
when exceeded"* and *"Verify-ownership flow promotes the workspace
and removes it from the per-IP cap pool"*. Deferred out of cd-7huk
because no LLM agent runtime exists yet (`cd-6bcl` = LLM router,
`cd-nyvm` = agent runtime, `cd-irng` = per-workspace rolling 30-day
budget).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Per-IP aggregate LLM
  spend cap" — rolling 30-day sum across every workspace whose
  `signup_ip` matches; default 3× the per-workspace free-tier cap;
  `402 payment_required` + verify-ownership banner copy.
- **Blocked by**:
  - `cd-6bcl` — feat(api/llm): agents, approvals, preferences, model
    assignments, usage, budget, outbound webhooks.
  - `cd-irng` — feat(llm/budget): rolling-30d workspace envelope
    (check + record). The per-IP cap is a 3× sum over per-workspace
    budgets, so cd-irng's write path is the natural seam.

## Implementation notes
- Extend `cd-irng`'s budget check to also accumulate a per-IP total
  across every `signup_ip = ?` workspace whose
  `verification_state in (unverified, email_verified)`.
- When the per-IP total > 3× the per-workspace free-tier cap, every
  `llm.call` from an affected workspace returns `402 payment_required`
  with the spec banner copy (see §15).
- Verify-ownership flow: the human verification transition flips
  `verification_state = human_verified`; at that point the workspace
  drops out of the per-IP cap pool.
- Instrument the shared `ShieldStore` (or a sibling class) if a
  rolling-counter model is easier than a SQL aggregate — measure
  before deciding.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Per-IP LLM spend cap returns 402 payment_required with the
      spec banner copy when exceeded
- [ ] Verify-ownership flow promotes the workspace and removes it
      from the per-IP cap pool

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/llm/test_per_ip_spend_cap.py -xvs
```

title: sec(admin/signups): suspicious-activity surfacing on /admin/signups (burst-rate, duplicate email)
description:
## Problem / goal
Spec §15 "Self-serve abuse mitigations" / cd-7huk AC: "/admin/signups
shows burst-rate + duplicate-email signals". The shared @throttle +
disposable-domain seams landed in cd-7huk but the admin dashboard
surfacing was deferred because no `/admin/signups` route currently
exists in the app tree.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Self-serve abuse
  mitigations" — suspicious-activity rollups write to
  `audit.signup.suspicious` and surface on `/admin/signups`.
- **Parent / predecessor**: cd-7huk landed the shared throttle +
  fresh-domain file + passkey-login rate limit. This task picks up
  the (d) admin-dashboard piece that was split out.
- **Blocker**: no admin-signups Beads task exists yet; whoever files
  the broader admin surface task first should link this one via
  `bd dep <new-admin> --blocks cd-<this>`. Until then this task is
  effectively waiting on the admin route existing at all.

## Implementation notes
- Write `audit.signup.suspicious` rows from the existing signup +
  abuse code paths in `app/auth/signup.py` / `app/auth/signup_abuse.py`:
  - Burst rate trip (any `SignupRateLimited` raise — scope in
    {ip, email, global}).
  - Same IP across >= 3 distinct emails in the 1-hour window.
  - Repeat provisioning from one email (email-cap trip).
  - Quota near-breach (LLM / upload / email approaching the
    pre-verification caps).
- Build the `/admin/signups` page that filters `audit_log` for the
  `audit.signup.suspicious` action and renders the rollup.
- The cd-7huk shared store (`app/abuse/throttle.py::ShieldStore`)
  already retains the counter shape; the admin surface reads them
  via a read-only snapshot seam TBD.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `audit.signup.suspicious` rows land on every burst-rate trip
- [ ] `/admin/signups` page renders the signals
- [ ] Duplicate-email rollups collapse per-email counts into one row

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_signups_dashboard.py -xvs
```

title: [bug] Pre-existing flake: tests/unit/auth/test_session.py::test_secure_false_falls_back_to_dev_cookie fails in full-suite run but passes in isolation

title: passkey-delete route invalidates user sessions
description:
When cd-geqp lands, the invalidate_for_user wiring is added to passkey.register_finish (cause "passkey_registered") but there is no existing route for deleting a passkey. Per cd-geqp spec §15 Cookies + Shared-origin XSS containment, a passkey revocation is also a credential-population change and MUST trigger invalidate_for_user with cause "passkey_revoked".

Scope:
- Add a DELETE endpoint at /auth/passkey/{credential_id} (or similar) that drops the credential row and calls invalidate_for_user(..., cause="passkey_revoked") in the same UoW.
- Enforce ownership (actor must own the credential or be an admin on all shared scopes).
- Audit passkey_credential.revoked before invalidate, then session.invalidated.
- Tests: unit + integration.

Follow-up from cd-geqp.

title: feat(web): stamp CSP nonce onto the SPA bootstrap when the index is server-rendered
description:
## Problem / goal
The strict CSP landed in cd-wv2v accepts inline `<script>` / `<style>` **only** when tagged with the per-request `nonce-` value the middleware now stamps into the `Content-Security-Policy` header. The current `app/web/dist/index.html` is a static Vite build with only external (`src=...`) scripts, so today the SPA still boots — nothing is inline.

The moment the SPA index becomes server-rendered (Jinja template, route handler streaming HTML, or SSR), **any** inline bootstrap — even a trivial `<script>window.__CREWDAY__=...</script>` — must read `request.state.csp_nonce` and stamp it onto the tag, or the browser will refuse to execute it and the app will break in the wild without a server-side error.

## Technical context
- Middleware: `app/api/middleware/security_headers.py` — exposes `request.state.csp_nonce`.
- Build-time gate: `scripts/check_csp_inlines.py` — walks `app/web/dist/` and fails on un-nonced inlines.
- Spec: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"HTTP security headers" / §"Shared-origin XSS containment".

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Server-rendered SPA index template reads the nonce from `request.state.csp_nonce` and stamps it onto every inline `<script>` / `<style>` tag it emits
- [ ] Integration test: fetch the SPA index, assert the CSP header's `nonce-` value matches the `nonce=` attribute on the bootstrap tag
- [ ] `scripts/check_csp_inlines.py` run against the rendered output (not just the Vite build) stays green

## Why file this now
Trivially passes on `main` today (no server-rendered index exists), but the seam is a future requirement and we'd rather track it than forget.

title: Pre-existing flake: tests/unit/test_main.py::TestSpaCatchAll::test_missing_dist_logs_warning fails in full-suite run but passes in isolation
description:
Running 'uv run pytest -q' on clean main (no cd-5bhy fix applied) yields:

FAILED tests/unit/test_main.py::TestSpaCatchAll::test_missing_dist_logs_warning
    assert len(events) == 1
E   assert 0 == 1

Running the test alone ('uv run pytest tests/unit/test_main.py::TestSpaCatchAll::test_missing_dist_logs_warning') passes. The test uses caplog to assert an SPA-missing warning fires during app startup — log capture likely leaks between tests, or the lifespan startup log fires before caplog is attached when another test has already constructed a FastAPI app.

Discovered while verifying cd-5bhy. Unrelated to the auth/signup fix but still a real bug — the full suite is not green on main.

## Repro
- main at b8d0187 (or later)
- uv run pytest -q
- Observe the test failing

## Fix hint
Likely needs caplog.at_level() around the startup or to use a separate propagate=True logger. Investigate tests/unit/test_main.py:298 and its caplog fixture.
close_reason: Fixed by forcing propagate=True + disabled=False on app.api.factory logger in test_missing_dist_logs_warning. Root cause: integration fixture's 'alembic upgrade head' runs logging.config.fileConfig with disable_existing_loggers=True (alembic.ini default), which flipped propagate=False on loggers not in the config file — caplog attaches to the root logger so records on non-propagating child loggers never hit the capture. Same pattern already applied in tests/unit/test_capabilities.py and tests/unit/authz/test_enforce.py; this is the third occurrence. Verified: 10 consecutive runs in the polluted-ordering context green; full test_main.py module 3× green. Filed cd-szxw for the same pattern in test_passkey_login.py and cd-g4kp for an unrelated session cookie flake.

title: feat(api/proxy): WebSocket upgrade for Vite HMR dev proxy
description:
## Problem / goal
The dev-profile Vite proxy in app/api/proxy.py (cd-q1be) forwards HTTP
only. Vite's HMR client connects to /@vite/client via a WebSocket —
without the upgrade, save-triggered hot updates do **not** land in the
browser. A manual reload (F5) picks up the latest modules through the
HTTP proxy, but the dev loop is noticeably slower than editing Vite
directly.

Add WebSocket upgrade support so HMR (module replacement on save)
works end-to-end through the FastAPI dev seam.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md §"Serving the SPA";
  docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md §"FastAPI static mount".
- **Current code**: app/api/proxy.py register_vite_proxy — HTTP-only
  proxy via httpx.AsyncClient, stashed on app.state.vite_client. The
  module docstring's 'Not yet implemented' block flags the gap.
- **FastAPI WS**: @app.websocket('/{path:path}') + an httpx.AsyncClient
  (no direct WS support) or starlette.websockets + websockets library.
  The existing /{full_path:path} GET route is registered on a shared
  path — a WS registration on the same path needs to sit beside it.

## Implementation notes
- Add websocket_route('/{full_path:path}') that forwards the upgrade
  to vite_dev_url with scheme rewritten to ws://.
- Proxy the bidirectional frames verbatim (Vite HMR protocol is JSON
  strings — no need to inspect).
- Reuse app.state.vite_dev_url; do NOT reuse the httpx.AsyncClient
  (it does not handle WS).
- Keep the path guard — _is_api_path must still win. Workspace SSE
  (/events) would otherwise get proxied.
- Dev-only: profile='dev' gates the registration exactly as today.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Editing app/web/src/**/*.tsx triggers a visible HMR update in
      the browser (no reload) while the app is up in dev profile
- [ ] /api/* and /w/<slug>/api/* WS paths (if any) are not hijacked
- [ ] Upstream WS failure → 502-ish close code, not a 500 stack
- [ ] Unit tests: path guard, forward-then-close, upstream connect
      refused

## Test plan
- Unit: starlette TestClient.websocket_connect against a mock WS
  upstream; assert bidi forwarding + close-on-refuse.
- Manual: docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build;
  edit a .tsx; confirm the browser console logs "[vite] hot updated"
  instead of "[vite] server connection lost".

Discovered via cd-x7fa self-review of cd-q1be.

title: Self-review: chore(dev): point dev.crew.day to app/web Vite; serve mocks/web at /mocks/ for comparison
description:

Catch bugs, missing pieces, and unintended consequences before

**Depends on: cd-sgpl** (main task must be complete first).

## How to run

- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.

close_reason: Autofix self-review complete. Fixed: (1) mocks SPA BrowserRouter missing basename — escaped to /today on the app/web SPA when navigated to /mocks/. (2) mocks SPA fetch/EventSource used absolute paths bypassing the base, hit app-api instead of mocks-api. Added withBase() helper in mocks/web/src/lib/api.ts and wired it through api.ts, preferences.ts, sse.ts, AgentSidebar.tsx. (4) Added --no-package-lock to both web-dev / mocks-web-dev compose commands so container-side npm install no longer mutates the host lockfile. title: chore(dev): point dev.crew.day to app/web Vite; serve mocks/web at /mocks/ for comparison
description:
## Problem / goal
Right now `https://dev.crew.day` (and `http://127.0.0.1:8100`) serves
the **mocks** SPA (`mocks/web/`) via the `web-dev` Vite container in
`mocks/docker-compose.yml`. The real production frontend scaffold has
landed under `app/web/` (cd-ul7f), but the operator still can't see
or click-test it at `dev.crew.day`.

We want to cut the dev entry point over to the real app and keep the
mocks reachable at `/mocks/` on the same host so the operator can
**compare the gap** page-for-page while Phase-1 fills in.

## Current state (factual)
- `mocks/docker-compose.yml` defines `web-dev` bind-mounting
  `./web:/web` (= `mocks/web`) and publishing 127.0.0.1:8100 →
  container :5173. Traefik labels route `Host(dev.crew.day)` → that
  container. See `mocks/docker-compose.yml:53-82`.
- `web-dev` proxies `/api`, `/events`, `/switch`, `/theme`,
  `/agent`, `/healthz`, `/readyz`, `/metrics` to the `mocks` service
  (which actually runs the real `app.main:app`, not mock code) via
  `VITE_BACKEND_URL=http://mocks:8000`.
- `app/web/` is a Vite + React 19 + TS strict scaffold (mirror of
  `mocks/web`). `app/web/package.json` has `"dev": "vite"`;
  `app/web/vite.config.ts` already proxies the same API paths to
  `VITE_BACKEND_URL` and allowlists `dev.crew.day` under
  `server.allowedHosts`.

## What this task changes
Edit **only** `mocks/docker-compose.yml`:

1. Repoint the existing `web-dev` service so it serves `app/web`:
   - `volumes`: `./app/web:/web` (replace `./web:/web`)
   - Keep everything else (env, Traefik labels, port, network,
     `depends_on`).
   - Keep `VITE_BACKEND_URL=http://mocks:8000` — the `mocks`
     backend container continues to be the API target.

2. Add a second Vite container `mocks-web-dev` that serves
   `mocks/web` at `dev.crew.day/mocks/`:
   - `image: node:22-alpine` (match existing)
   - `volumes: ./web:/web` (the mocks SPA — resolved relative to
     `mocks/docker-compose.yml`, which is its current path)
   - `VITE_BACKEND_URL=http://mocks:8000`
   - Command: `npm install --no-audit --no-fund && npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --base /mocks/`
     (Vite accepts `--base`; verify against the pinned version —
     alternative is to pass the base in `vite.config.ts` or via the
     `VITE_BASE` env if the config already reads it)
   - Join `default` + `traefik-proxy` networks.
   - Traefik labels:
     - Router `crewday-dev-mocks` with rule
       `Host(dev.crew.day) && PathPrefix(/mocks)`, priority HIGHER
       than the existing `crewday-dev` router (so `/mocks/...`
       wins).
     - Service pointing at port 5173.
     - **Do NOT** add a `StripPrefix` middleware — Vite's `base`
       expects the prefix to remain in the URL.
     - Keep the `badger@file` forward-auth middleware (same as
       `crewday-dev`).
   - Bind nothing to the host — routed via Traefik only.

3. Verify HMR websockets work on both:
   - `dev.crew.day` (root) → `wss://dev.crew.day/@vite/...`
   - `dev.crew.day/mocks/` → `wss://dev.crew.day/mocks/@vite/...`
   - If Vite's auto-detected HMR path breaks under the prefix, set
     `server.hmr.path` in `mocks/web/vite.config.ts` to
     `'/mocks/@vite/ws'` or equivalent. Do **not** modify
     `app/web/vite.config.ts` for this — the root mount needs no
     changes there.

## Out of scope
- cd-q1be (`feat(api/static): mount app/web/dist as SPA catch-all`)
  handles the **production** path where FastAPI itself serves the
  baked `app/web/dist`. That path is not exercised by the
  `web-dev` Vite container. This task is dev-only plumbing. Leave
  `app/main.py` alone here.
- Do not change the `mocks` service (the FastAPI backend).
- Do not delete the mocks SPA or `mocks/web/` — it stays as a
  reference surface.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Serving the SPA";
  `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Environment
  variables" and the bind guard (never public bind; Traefik proxy
  fine on `traefik-proxy` network).
- **Related tasks**:
  - cd-ul7f (app/web scaffold) — DONE, unblocks this.
  - cd-q1be — overlapping **but distinct**: it targets FastAPI's
    static mount. Note in its comments that dev.crew.day routing
    is handled here so its implementer doesn't double-edit
    `mocks/docker-compose.yml`.
  - cd-3wj (Dockerfile single + docker-compose dev/prod profiles)
    — future profile rework; not a blocker.
- **Key files**:
  - `mocks/docker-compose.yml` (primary edit)
  - `app/web/vite.config.ts` (reference — no edit expected)
  - `mocks/web/vite.config.ts` (may need `base` or `hmr.path` to
    work under `/mocks/`)

## Implementation notes
- Never bind anything to the public interface (217.182.203.57).
  Traefik covers external access; published ports stay on
  `127.0.0.1` when present.
- Both Vite containers need `--host 0.0.0.0` (container-local
  bind, reachable on the compose network — Traefik routes in).
- HMR via Traefik needs the websocket upgrade. The existing
  `crewday-dev` router already works, so mirror its entrypoints +
  TLS resolver on the new router.
- Priority: Traefik routes are longest-rule-wins by default, but
  when two routers share the same host you must set explicit
  `priority` on `crewday-dev-mocks` so `PathPrefix(/mocks)` beats
  the bare `Host(...)` rule of `crewday-dev`.
- Test with `docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build`.
- On first boot both `web-dev` containers will `npm install`
  (slow); subsequent restarts are fast thanks to the bind-mount.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `https://dev.crew.day/` loads **app/web** (the real SPA),
      not the mocks SPA. Confirm via the app's title / a known
      route that only exists in `app/web/src/`.
- [ ] `https://dev.crew.day/mocks/` loads **mocks/web** and
      renders its own SPA shell correctly (assets, fonts, CSS
      load from `/mocks/...` paths, no 404s in DevTools
      network).
- [ ] Editing a `.tsx` under `app/web/src/` hot-reloads the
      browser at `dev.crew.day/` within ~1 s.
- [ ] Editing a `.tsx` under `mocks/web/src/` hot-reloads the
      browser at `dev.crew.day/mocks/` within ~1 s.
- [ ] API calls from either SPA (`/api/v1/...`, `/events`, etc.)
      still reach the `mocks` FastAPI container — same backend,
      both frontends.
- [ ] `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8100/` returns the app/web
      index (same container serves both host-forward and
      Traefik paths).
- [ ] No port is published on the public interface
      (217.182.203.57). `ss -tlnp | grep -v 127.0.0.1 | grep -v 100.72.198.118`
      shows nothing new.
- [ ] Badger forward-auth still guards both routes.

## Test plan

### Automated / smoke
\`\`\`bash
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml ps

# App at root
curl -sI http://127.0.0.1:8100/ | head -1
curl -s  http://127.0.0.1:8100/ | grep -i 'crew'

# Confirm nothing binds publicly
ss -tln | awk '\$4 !~ /(127\\.0\\.0\\.1|100\\.72\\.198\\.118|\\[::1\\])/ { print }'
\`\`\`

### Manual (behind Tailscale / badger auth)
1. `docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build`
2. Browse `https://dev.crew.day/` — expect **app/web** SPA.
3. Browse `https://dev.crew.day/mocks/` — expect **mocks/web** SPA.
4. Open DevTools → Network: confirm both SPAs load their own
   assets without cross-bleed (no `/src/...` 404s under the
   `/mocks/` page, and no `/mocks/src/...` 404s under the root).
5. Edit `app/web/src/App.tsx`: HMR toast on `dev.crew.day/`.
6. Edit `mocks/web/src/App.tsx`: HMR toast on `dev.crew.day/mocks/`.
7. Hit a known API path from the app SPA and confirm a JSON
   response (even if it's a 401 / 404 — means the proxy reached
   the `mocks` FastAPI container).
notes:
### UPDATE (2026-04-21): scope broadened — ALSO swap the backend

User (via /director) wants dev.crew.day to serve the PRODUCTION app end-to-end
for Playwright smoke-testing. The original cd-sgpl plan only swapped the Vite
frontend; the backend stayed on the mocks FastAPI at `mocks/app/main.py`.
Under the broadened scope:

- add a new service `app-api` to `mocks/docker-compose.yml` that runs
  `app.main:create_app --factory` from the repo-root `app/` package
  (NOT `mocks/app/`). Bind-mount `./app:/app/app:ro` on the parent dir
  so `uvicorn --reload` picks up .py edits;
- `app-api` env:
  - `CREWDAY_DATABASE_URL=sqlite+aiosqlite:////data/crewday.db` (note
    4 slashes — absolute path)
  - `CREWDAY_DATA_DIR=/data`
  - `CREWDAY_BIND_HOST=0.0.0.0`, `CREWDAY_BIND_PORT=8000`,
    `CREWDAY_ALLOW_PUBLIC_BIND=1` (container-internal only; still gated
    behind Traefik + badger externally)
  - `CREWDAY_PROFILE=dev`, `CREWDAY_VITE_DEV_URL=http://web-dev:5173`
  - `CREWDAY_PUBLIC_URL=https://dev.crew.day`
  - `CREWDAY_ROOT_KEY` — generate a dev-only hex key at compose level
    (env default OK for now)
- run `alembic upgrade head` on boot via entrypoint (not a separate
  init container — keep the stack minimal);
- volume `crewday-app-data:/data` for SQLite persistence;
- the `web-dev` (app/web Vite) container's `VITE_BACKEND_URL` now
  targets `http://app-api:8000` — override the current
  `http://mocks:8000`;
- rename the current `mocks` service to `mocks-api` for clarity, keep
  it backing the `/mocks/` SPA (mocks-web-dev) via its own
  `VITE_BACKEND_URL=http://mocks-api:8000`;
- keep badger forward-auth on both routers.

Acceptance criteria additions:
- `curl -sI http://127.0.0.1:8100/healthz` → 200 and body says it's the
  production app (distinctive marker: `GET /version` returns the real
  `importlib.metadata` version, not the mocks stub);
- `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8100/api/v1/signup/start` hits the real
  self-serve signup router (422 on bad payload, not 404);
- `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8100/mocks/api/v1/...` still reaches the
  MOCKS backend (distinct backend containers);
- the SQLite DB file is created on first boot under `/data`;
- Alembic is at head after boot.

This broadens cd-sgpl rather than creating a new task because the
changes are the same file (`mocks/docker-compose.yml`) and the two
frontend / backend swaps need to land together to avoid a broken
intermediate state.

title: feat(identity): user_workspace derive-refresh worker
description:
Replace the inline `user_workspace` writes in `app.domain.identity.membership` (cd-jpa) and other callers with a proper reconciliation worker that derives the junction from upstream grants.

## Technical context
`user_workspace` is documented as derived (§02) but until a refresh worker exists, every domain service writes the row inline. Each inline touch is flagged `TODO(cd-nmr)` in `app/domain/identity/membership.py` and `tests/factories/identity.py`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Worker reconciles `user_workspace` from `role_grant` + (future) `work_engagement` + `property_workspace`
- [ ] Inline `TODO(cd-nmr)` writes in `app/domain/identity/membership.py` + factories removed
- [ ] Tests cover add + revoke cycles through the worker
- [ ] Runs on interval via APScheduler

title: docs(spec): §12 auth/tokens routes + reconcile scope_json shape
description:
Cd-c91 shipped POST/GET/DELETE /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/tokens with a dict-shaped `scopes` body (matches the ApiToken.scope_json column).

docs/specs/12-rest-api.md does not yet enumerate these routes.
docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md shows the body example as a list
(`scopes: ["tasks:read"]`); the router accepts dict. Documenter to:

1. Add the three routes + error shapes (401/403/404/422) to §12.
2. Decide whether §03 should be updated to the dict form, or whether
   the router should be extended to accept lists as sugar and coerce
   on the way in. Current DB shape is dict; that is the tiebreaker.
3. Run /update-openapi after landing.

Out of scope for cd-c91 (pure doc reconciliation).

title: feat(auth/tokens): delegated + PAT token kinds
description:
§03 describes three token families — standalone (cd-c91, landed), delegated (acts for a user_id with short TTL), and personal-access (user-scoped, no workspace pin). cd-c91 shipped only the standalone kind.

Follow-up:
- Add `kind` column to api_token (`standalone|delegated|pat`) via new Alembic migration.
- Add `delegate_for_user_id` FK column; enforce short TTL (default 24h) at mint time.
- Router: separate endpoints or a `kind` parameter on POST /auth/tokens.
- verify(): short-circuit expiry for delegated to the tighter TTL.
- Tests: both new kinds through the same /auth/tokens surface.

Depends on cd-c91. Blocked by cd-tvh already on the board — link once a owner claims it.
close_reason: Three token kinds (scoped/delegated/personal) landed: migration f7c9e1a4b5d8 adds kind + delegate_for_user_id + subject_user_id + nullable workspace_id + 2 CHECK constraints. mint/verify/revoke branch per-kind. New /api/v1/me/tokens surface for PAT. Scoped: workspace cap 5; PAT: per-subject cap 5. 446 auth tests pass. Follow-ups: cd-t2su (PAT audit seam), cd-xawm (UI), cd-et6y (delegator liveness in verify()).

title: sec(auth/passkey): delete challenge row on verification failure (single-use on failure)
description:
**Context**

cd-hwi shipped passkey login. Challenge rows (`webauthn_challenge`) are only deleted on the success path of `login_finish` / `register_finish` / `register_finish_signup`. On any verification failure (bad signature, clone-detected, subject mismatch, etc.) the caller's UoW rolls back and the challenge survives until its 10-minute TTL expires.

**Concern**

An attacker with a leaked `challenge_id` can keep replaying assertion attempts against the same challenge until TTL. Throttle (3 fails / 10-min per credential + IP) limits the blast radius, but the spec prefers single-use even on failure so the window is zero.

**What to do**

- On any `login_finish` / `register_finish*` failure, delete the challenge row on a fresh UoW (sibling pattern to the `passkey.login_rejected` audit write) before the caller's UoW rolls back.
- Confirm idempotency: two concurrent finish calls must not both succeed after one succeeds — the domain service already loads fresh, but the delete-on-failure path needs to tolerate "row already gone" without raising.
- Extend tests: `test_passkey_login.py` + `test_passkey_register.py` assert the challenge is gone after any failure shape (`InvalidLoginAttempt`, `CloneDetected`, `ChallengeSubjectMismatch`, verifier rejection).

**Acceptance**

- [ ] Challenge deleted on all failure paths across login + register finishers
- [ ] Fresh-UoW pattern matches the existing fresh-UoW audit writes
- [ ] Concurrent-finish race handled (delete is idempotent)
- [ ] Tests assert post-failure challenge gone

**References**

- `app/auth/passkey.py` `login_finish`, `register_finish`, `register_finish_signup`
- `app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py` `_write_login_audit_fresh_uow` (fresh-UoW pattern)
- `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"WebAuthn specifics"

title: sec(auth/passkey): auto-revoke credential on clone detection (sign-count rollback)
description:
**Context**

cd-hwi shipped passkey login with FIDO2 sign-count clone detection: `app/auth/passkey.py` `login_finish` raises `CloneDetected` when the returned counter does not exceed the stored one (and the stored counter is non-zero). The HTTP router (`app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py`) writes an `audit.passkey.cloned_detected` row on a fresh UoW and returns 401.

**Gap**

`docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Passkey specifics" calls for "sign-count rollback auto-revokes the credential". The detection half is in, but the revocation half is not — a cloned credential can still attempt login until the operator manually revokes it (each attempt now refuses at verify and writes a `cloned_detected` audit row, but the credential row is not marked deleted).

**What to do**

- On `CloneDetected`, set `passkey_credential.deleted_at = now()` on a fresh UoW (sibling to the existing `cloned_detected` audit write).
- Emit a second audit row `passkey.auto_revoked` with `reason: "clone_detected"`.
- Trigger operator notification (TBD channel — follow existing spec hook for security alerts).
- Test coverage: extend `tests/integration/auth/test_passkey_login_pg.py` to assert the credential row carries `deleted_at` after a clone-detected refusal, and that a subsequent login with the same credential hits 401 with `no passkey credential` rather than `sign_count rollback` (the revoked row must no longer load).

**Acceptance**

- [ ] `passkey_credential.deleted_at` set to now on clone detection
- [ ] `audit.passkey.auto_revoked` row written alongside `cloned_detected`
- [ ] Integration test covers revocation + inability to reuse
- [ ] Spec §15 cross-reference updated if needed

**References**

- `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Passkey specifics"
- `app/auth/passkey.py` `login_finish` (CloneDetected raise site)
- `app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py` `post_login_finish` (CloneDetected handler)
notes:
---

**Blocked on cd-ue6l (2026-04-21).** `app/adapters/db/identity/models.py::PasskeyCredential` has no `deleted_at` column and the credential-loading queries do not filter on it. Per cd-cx19 task instructions, adding the schema column + migration is out of scope for this task — filed cd-ue6l to land that first. Resume cd-cx19 once cd-ue6l closes.

Secondary spec mismatch noted for the resumption: §15 line 333 names the audit event `audit.webauthn.rollback_auto_revoke`, but the cd-cx19 task body asks for `passkey.auto_revoked`. The resumption should either align the code to the spec name (`webauthn.rollback_auto_revoke`) or tighten the spec to `passkey.auto_revoked`. Decision belongs to the resumer.

title: feat(auth/recovery): workspace kill-switch (auth.self_service_recovery_enabled)
description:
## Problem / goal
Spec §03 'Workspace kill-switch' requires that if ANY workspace the target user holds a non-archived grant in has \`auth.self_service_recovery_enabled = false\`, self-service recovery is refused for that user (no email sent, 202 returned, \`auth.recover.disabled_by_workspace\` audited).

cd-atj landed the base recovery flow; the kill-switch gate is deferred here because the workspace setting itself isn't landed yet.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` §'Workspace kill-switch'.
- **Depends on**: the \`auth.self_service_recovery_enabled\` workspace setting being defined in the settings catalog (§05).

## Implementation notes
- Add a helper to walk the user's non-archived grants and short-circuit on any \`false\` flag (most-restrictive-wins).
- Wire into \`app.auth.recovery.request_recovery\` after the user lookup, before the hit branch.
- Audit \`recovery.disabled_by_workspace\` on the fresh UoW (not primary — the primary UoW will not commit any row).
- 202 response unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A user with the flag \`false\` in any workspace sees 202 but no mail + audit \`recovery.disabled_by_workspace\`
- [ ] A user with the flag \`true\` everywhere sees normal flow
- [ ] A user with archived grants where the flag is \`false\` is NOT blocked (non-archived filter)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests on both DBs
close_reason: Workspace kill-switch wired via spec-canonical workspaces.settings_json column (§02). Migration a1b2c3d4e5f6 adds settings_json. is_self_service_recovery_disabled() walks non-archived grants most-restrictive-wins. Fresh-UoW audit row recovery.disabled_by_workspace. 103 scoped tests pass (SQLite+Postgres). Reconciled §03 step 5 spec drift (any vs every). Follow-up: cd-x1xh (role_grant soft-retire columns).

title: feat(auth/recovery): step-up break-glass code flow for managers/owners
description:
## Problem / goal
Spec §03 'Self-service lost-device recovery' + §15 'Step-up bypass is not a fallback' require that users holding a \`manager\` surface grant anywhere OR members of any \`owners\` group must supply a valid \`break_glass_code\` on \`POST /recover/passkey/request\`; without one, no email is sent (same 202 shape).

cd-atj landed the base recovery flow — request/verify/finish for the non-step-up population (workers, clients, guests). The step-up gate + break-glass-code redemption is deferred here.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` §'Self-service lost-device recovery' steps 3-4 (break_glass_code lookup, \`auth.recover.stepup_missing\` / \`auth.recover.stepup_invalid\` audit).
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md\` §'Self-service lost-device & email-change abuse mitigations' — 'Step-up bypass is not a fallback'.
- **Depends on**: the \`break_glass_code\` table (§03 'Recovery paths') — check whether the model already exists, file a prior dependency if not.

## Implementation notes
- Add optional \`break_glass_code\` to \`RecoveryRequestBody\`.
- In \`app.auth.recovery.request_recovery\`: classify the user's step-up population (manager/owner in any workspace) before deciding whether to mint the link or silently swallow.
- If step-up required and code missing/invalid: audit \`recovery.stepup_missing\` or \`recovery.stepup_invalid\` (fresh UoW so it survives), return 202 (enumeration guard).
- If step-up required and code valid: burn the code (\`used_at = now()\`), proceed with the normal hit branch.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Manager submitting \`/recover/passkey/request\` without code sees 202 + audit \`recovery.stepup_missing\`, no mail
- [ ] Manager submitting with wrong code sees 202 + audit \`recovery.stepup_invalid\`, no mail
- [ ] Manager submitting with valid unused code sees 202 + mail + code \`used_at\` stamped
- [ ] Worker (non-step-up) with a code attached: code is ignored, flow proceeds normally
- [ ] Unit + integration tests on both DBs

title: fix(authz): harden last-owner guard against count-then-delete TOCTOU race
description:
## Problem

Both `app.domain.identity.permission_groups.remove_member` (cd-ckr)
and `app.domain.identity.role_grants.revoke` (cd-79r) implement the
"don't orphan `owners@<ws>`" invariant as a two-step read-then-write:

```python
owner_count = _count_members(...)   # or _count_owner_members
if owner_count <= 1:
    raise LastOwnerMember(...)
# … delete / revoke happens below
```

Under concurrent transactions (two admins hitting the UI
simultaneously, an API token script in a tight loop) both transactions
can observe `owner_count == 2` and both commit their DELETE, leaving
the `owners` group empty. The spec (§02 "permission_group"
§"Invariants") forbids that state.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Either a `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` lock on `permission_group_member`
      (Postgres) / serialisable isolation upgrade (SQLite), or an
      advisory-lock pattern, wrapping both guards
- [ ] Concurrency test that spawns two threads racing `remove_member`
      on the sole owners row and asserts the invariant holds
      (at least one refusal, zero empty-`owners` windows)
- [ ] Same concurrency test for `role_grants.revoke` of a manager
      grant whose removal would leave fewer managers than owners
- [ ] Both guards share the locking primitive (DRY — the v1 duplication
      also tracks under cd-duv6 proper-repository refactor)

## Refs

- `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"permission_group" §"Invariants"
- `app/domain/identity/permission_groups.py` `remove_member` / `_count_members`
- `app/domain/identity/role_grants.py` `revoke` / `_count_owner_members`
- Related: cd-ckr (last-owner guard v1), cd-79r (role-grants guard v1)
- Reviewer non-blocking nit carried forward from cd-ckr self-review

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: cd-jkak selfreview complete. TOCTOU fix verified on SQLite (5/5 runs green; 5/5 fail with SQLite UPDATE lock disabled) and Postgres (3/3 green; 3/3 fail with FOR UPDATE disabled). Filed logic-hole follow-ups cd-nj8m + cd-j5pu + cd-a3oa (not in scope for cd-mb5n's TOCTOU-narrow fix); filed pre-existing flake cd-p46e (test_passkey_login full-suite PII-redaction clash, confirmed on clean main). One unused `# type: ignore` on tests/integration/identity/test_last_owner_race.py:272 removed as autofix. No code-correctness bugs in the diff.

title: Self-review: test(auth/magic-link): dev Mailpit round-trip verifies magic-link email
description:
## Problem / goal
Auto-fixing self-review of the changes made under **cd-o62m**.
Catch bugs, missing pieces, and unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-o62m** (main task must be complete first).

## How to run
Run \`/selfreview\` in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from cd-o62m.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Run the quality gates after fixing.
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

See \`.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md\`.

## Specific things to double-check
- The Mailpit helper is genuinely reusable (no hidden magic-link
  assumptions baked into it).
- Assertions on email content are strict enough to catch realistic
  template regressions, not just \"body is not empty\".
- No race conditions — the polling loop needs a Message-ID filter, not
  \"first message wins\".
- Skip path for \"no docker / no stack\" surfaces a useful reason.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All BUGS from the self-review fixed
- [ ] All MISSING pieces completed
- [ ] All RISKY items mitigated (or justified in a task comment)
- [ ] Linter, formatter, type checker, affected tests all pass
- [ ] Fixes committed and pushed
close_reason: Selfreview was performed inline as part of cd-o62m (commit b10c94f) per director skill workflow — autofixed flake-handling for per-IP rate limit, added skip semantics; all gates green. No additional changes needed.

title: test(auth/magic-link): dev Mailpit round-trip verifies magic-link email
description:
## Problem / goal
Magic-link email is the primary auth bootstrap path (§03). Today the
only end-to-end coverage is \`tests/integration/test_mail_smtp.py\`,
which proves \`SMTPMailer\` can hit a sink — it does **not** exercise
the magic-link flow (token mint → email render → SMTP send → UI link).
Add a dedicated test that drives the real magic-link bootstrap endpoint
against the dev \`mocks\` stack with Mailpit as the sink, then asserts
the delivered email contains a working magic-link URL that, when
consumed, issues a session cookie.

Gives future agents a fast feedback loop: \"I changed the email
template / the token format / the SMTP wiring — did the real user-facing
flow still work?\"

## Technical context
- **Depends on: cd-5ypd** (needs Mailpit up on 127.0.0.1:8025).
- **Key files**:
  - \`app/api/v1/auth/magic.py\` — bootstrap + consume endpoints (new,
    un-pushed as of this task — check \`git status\`).
  - \`app/auth/magic_link.py\` — nonce issuance + consumption.
  - \`app/mail/templates/magic_link.py\` — HTML/text body.
  - \`tests/integration/auth/\` — existing integration tests live here.
  - Reference pattern: \`tests/integration/test_mail_smtp.py\` for the
    Mailpit polling idiom (update when cd-5ypd lands).
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` §\"Magic link\" and
  \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §\"Transport\".

## Implementation notes
- Prefer running against the already-up \`mocks\` compose stack over
  spawning a per-test testcontainer — the dev stack is the canonical
  place to smoke-test email. Guard with
  \`pytest.importorskip\` / skip-if-port-closed so CI hosts without the
  stack up simply skip.
- Extract the \"poll Mailpit for a message with this Message-ID\"
  helper into a small conftest / \`tests/integration/mail.py\` module
  so cd-qhgw-next (signup / recover / quote) can reuse it — do **not**
  copy-paste the polling loop four times.
- Assert on:
  1. Message arrives within 10 s deadline (Message-ID match).
  2. \`Subject\` matches the template (\`\"Sign in to crew.day\"\` or
     whatever the template actually renders — read the template, don't
     hard-code past).
  3. Plain-text body contains a URL whose path is
     \`/auth/magic/consume\` and whose token query-param is 32+ chars.
  4. A \`GET\` on that URL to the mocks app returns a 302 with a
     \`Set-Cookie\` for the session cookie (or whatever
     \`app/api/v1/auth/magic.py\` actually emits — check before asserting).
- Do **not** weaken the assertion to \"email was sent\" / \"body contains
  \'magic\'\". Every assertion must catch a realistic regression.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New test file \`tests/integration/auth/test_magic_link_mailpit.py\`
      (or similar) that drives bootstrap → Mailpit → consume end-to-end.
- [ ] Reusable Mailpit helper extracted to \`tests/integration/mail.py\`
      (or folded into \`conftest.py\`) with typed function signatures.
- [ ] The test skips cleanly when Mailpit / mocks stack isn't running,
      with a clear skip reason.
- [ ] \`pytest tests/integration/auth/test_magic_link_mailpit.py -xvs\`
      passes locally against the running \`mocks\` compose stack.
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` and \`ruff check\` pass on new files.

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build
pytest tests/integration/auth/test_magic_link_mailpit.py -xvs
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. \`docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build\`
2. Open \`http://127.0.0.1:8100\` and request a magic link for a known
   test address.
3. Open \`http://127.0.0.1:8025\`, confirm the email landed with the
   expected subject.
4. Click the link in the rendered email; confirm you land signed-in.

title: Self-review: chore(mocks): replace mailhog with mailpit in dev docker compose
description:
## Problem / goal
Auto-fixing self-review of the changes made under **cd-5ypd** (Mailpit
swap). Catch bugs, missing pieces, and unintended consequences before
they ship.

**Depends on: cd-5ypd** (main task must be complete first).

## How to run
Run \`/selfreview\` in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from cd-5ypd.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Run the quality gates after fixing.
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

See \`.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md\`.

## Specific things to double-check
- No lingering \"mailhog\" / \"MailHog\" strings anywhere (\`rg -i mailhog\`).
- The testcontainer actually waits for Mailpit to be ready before the
  first send — first-connection flakes eat an attempt of the 3-retry
  SMTP budget and can mask a real bug.
- Port binds are still \`127.0.0.1\` only (AGENTS.md bind guard).
- Mailpit's \`/api/v1/messages\` shape is parsed correctly (\`messages\`
  not \`items\`; \`From.Address\` not bare \`From\`).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All BUGS from the self-review fixed
- [ ] All MISSING pieces completed
- [ ] All RISKY items mitigated (or justified in a task comment)
- [ ] Linter, formatter, type checker, affected tests all pass
- [ ] Fixes committed and pushed

No BUGS/MISSING/RISKY. Return-Path coverage concern verified already covered at the unit layer (tests/unit/adapters/test_mail_smtp.py::test_send_runs_full_session_and_returns_message_id asserts <bounce+…@example.com> shape, and ::test_bounce_domain_override_wins_over_from_domain covers override), so dropping it from the Mailpit-rewrites-ingest integration check is safe. title: chore(mocks): replace mailhog with mailpit in dev docker compose
description:
## Problem / goal
The dev compose currently runs \`mailhog/mailhog:latest\` as the SMTP sink
for inspecting outbound email in the loopback dev stack. MailHog has
been unmaintained since 2020; **Mailpit** (\`axllent/mailpit\`) is the
actively-maintained drop-in replacement: same SMTP-on-1025 / HTTP-on-8025
shape, far better web UI, useful search and tagging, and a modern REST
API. Swap the container so local agents (and humans) can visually
confirm that magic-link, signup, recovery, login and billing emails
actually leave the app during dev.

## Technical context
- **Key files**:
  - \`mocks/docker-compose.yml:83-99\` — the \`mailhog\` service block,
    comment banner, and env-var pointers (\`CREWDAY_SMTP_HOST=mailhog\` …).
  - \`tests/integration/test_mail_smtp.py\` — the only in-repo integration
    test that spawns a MailHog testcontainer; uses
    \`mailhog/mailhog:latest\`, SMTP :1025, HTTP :8025, and polls
    \`/api/v2/messages\`.
  - \`app/adapters/mail/smtp.py\` — SMTP implementation, **unchanged by
    this task** (port is spec-driven, not sink-driven).
  - \`app/config.py:66-87\` — \`smtp_host\` / \`smtp_port\` config surface.
- **Mailpit facts** (verify against the image's README before editing):
  - Image: \`axllent/mailpit:latest\` (multi-arch, amd64 + arm64).
  - SMTP on \`:1025\`, Web/API on \`:8025\` — **same ports as MailHog**,
    so no app-side env changes required.
  - REST API differs: \`/api/v1/messages\` returns
    \`{\"messages\": [...], \"total\": N, ...}\` — **not** MailHog's
    \`/api/v2/messages\` \`{\"items\": [...]}\` shape. Message envelope
    fields (\`From\`, \`To\`, \`Subject\`, \`Content.Headers\`, \`Content.Body\`)
    are similarly different — consult
    https://mailpit.axllent.org/docs/api-v1/ when translating the test.
  - Health probe: \`GET /livez\` returns 200 when ready.
- **Bind guard** (AGENTS.md): publish ports on \`127.0.0.1\` only — never
  \`0.0.0.0\`.

## Implementation notes
- Rename the compose service from \`mailhog\` to \`mailpit\`, update
  \`container_name\` to \`crewday-mailpit\`, and rewrite the leading
  comment banner so the next reader doesn't hunt for a deleted name.
- Keep \`CREWDAY_SMTP_HOST=mailpit\` / \`_PORT=1025\` as the pointer in
  comments so other agents know what to set in \`.env\`.
- Update \`tests/integration/test_mail_smtp.py\`:
  - \`_MAILHOG_IMAGE\` → \`_MAILPIT_IMAGE = \"axllent/mailpit:latest\"\`
    (and rename the fixture + constants accordingly).
  - Switch the polling loop to \`/api/v1/messages\` and the Mailpit
    payload shape (\`messages[].From.Address\`, \`.To[].Address\`,
    \`.Subject\`, \`.MessageID\`).
  - Keep the same skip-if-no-docker behaviour and the
    \`Message-ID\`-keyed match — do not downgrade the assertion to
    \"first message wins\".
- If \`mocks/README.md\` or any spec references \"MailHog\" in prose,
  update those too (grep \`rg -i mailhog\`).
- Do **not** change \`app/adapters/mail/smtp.py\` — the mailer talks raw
  SMTP and doesn't care about the sink's identity.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build\` brings
      up a \`crewday-mailpit\` container that is reachable on
      \`127.0.0.1:1025\` (SMTP) and \`127.0.0.1:8025\` (web UI).
- [ ] The service is bound to \`127.0.0.1\` only — \`docker port
      crewday-mailpit\` shows no \`0.0.0.0\` entries.
- [ ] \`curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8025/livez\` returns 200.
- [ ] \`pytest tests/integration/test_mail_smtp.py -xvs\` passes against
      the Mailpit image (or skips cleanly when Docker is absent) — the
      envelope is matched by Message-ID, not the first-message fallback.
- [ ] No remaining references to \"mailhog\" / \"MailHog\" in
      \`mocks/\`, \`tests/\`, \`docs/\` (verified with \`rg -i mailhog\`).
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` on the touched files; \`ruff check\` clean.

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
pytest tests/integration/test_mail_smtp.py -xvs
ruff check mocks/ tests/integration/test_mail_smtp.py
mypy --strict tests/integration/test_mail_smtp.py
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. \`docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build\`
2. \`curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8025/livez\` — expect 200.
3. Open \`http://127.0.0.1:8025\` in a browser and confirm the Mailpit UI
   loads (dark header, empty inbox).
4. From the \`mocks\` container (or any host tool), open an SMTP
   session to \`127.0.0.1:1025\` and send a one-line message; confirm
   it appears in the Mailpit UI within a second.
5. \`docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml down\` to tidy up.

title: Refresh mocks/web pins + configs to match app/web scaffold
description:
## Problem / context
`app/web/` (cd-ul7f) was scaffolded by copying `mocks/web/` verbatim
and running a fresh `npm install --legacy-peer-deps`. The build only
succeeds after two deviations that `mocks/web/` today silently avoids
because its `node_modules/` still holds packages installed before its
`package.json` was bumped to vite 8 / TS 6.

### Forced deviations in app/web vs mocks/web
1. `tsconfig.app.json` — `baseUrl` removed (TS 6 deprecates it to an
   error unless `ignoreDeprecations: "6.0"` is set); `paths` values
   prefixed with `./` so they remain relative to the tsconfig dir.
2. `vite.config.ts` — `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks`
   rewritten from the record form (`{ vendor: [...], query: [...] }`)
   to the function form. Rollup 4's TypeScript typings (bundled with
   Vite 8) only accept the function form; the record form is a type
   error.
3. `package.json` — `@testing-library/dom@^10.4.1` added as an explicit
   devDependency. Under npm 11 with `--legacy-peer-deps`, peer deps of
   `@testing-library/react` aren't auto-installed.

### Evidence
`mocks/web/node_modules/vite/package.json` reports `5.4.21` while
`mocks/web/package-lock.json` pins `8.0.8` and `package.json` declares
`^8.0.8`. Same for `typescript` (installed 5.9.3 vs declared ^6.0.3).
A fresh `npm ci` or `rm -rf node_modules && npm install` in
`mocks/web/` would hit the same three errors I hit in `app/web/`.

## Proposal
Sync `mocks/web/` to the same three fixes (or pick the alternative fix
— e.g. add `ignoreDeprecations` instead of dropping `baseUrl`), then
refresh its node_modules + lockfile so `npm ci` produces the same
tree the lockfile claims.

## Acceptance criteria
- `rm -rf mocks/web/node_modules && npm ci --legacy-peer-deps` inside
  `mocks/web/` succeeds.
- `npm run build`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test -- --run` all
  pass from a clean `mocks/web/` install.
- `mocks/web/` and `app/web/` stay in lockstep on toolchain config
  (no diff beyond name/description/title/entry content).

## Context links
- cd-ul7f task + spec (docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md).
- app/web/vite.config.ts + tsconfig.app.json + package.json carry
  comments pointing at this follow-up.

title: refactor(authz/groups): replace direct adapter import with PermissionGroupRepository Protocol
description:
## Problem

v1 ships `app.domain.identity.permission_groups` with a direct SQLAlchemy model import from `app.adapters.db.authz.models`. This violates import-linter contract 1 (`app.domain -> app.adapters`); the violation is currently masked by a narrow `ignore_imports` exception in `pyproject.toml` (cd-zkr stopgap).

## Goal

Introduce the spec-sanctioned Protocol seam so the domain module stops touching adapters directly:

1. Declare `PermissionGroupRepository` (+ member sub-protocol) under `app/adapters/db/authz/ports.py`.
2. Implement `SqlAlchemyPermissionGroupRepository` under `app/adapters/db/authz/repositories.py`.
3. Migrate `app/domain/identity/permission_groups.py` to accept `repo: PermissionGroupRepository` instead of an SA session; pipe audit through a similar port if needed.
4. Update `pyproject.toml` — drop the `app.domain.identity.permission_groups -> app.adapters.db.authz.models` ignore_imports entry.
5. Every call site (integration tests, future routers) wires the concrete SA-backed repo.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `permission_groups.py` has no `app.adapters` imports (only `app.adapters.db.authz.ports` indirectly via the Protocol).
- [ ] The ignore_imports stopgap is gone; `uv run lint-imports` passes without it.
- [ ] Unit tests inject a stub repo; integration tests wire the real SA repo.
- [ ] No behaviour change on the public surface (signatures + error shapes + audit semantics match).

## Context
- Spec: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` sec. "Adapters" and "Shared kernel" (Protocol seam rule).
- Related: cd-zkr (v1 implementation that introduced the stopgap).

title: chore(boundaries): add per-context sibling-boundary import-linter contract
description:
## Problem / goal
Spec `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"Boundary rules" rule 1 says
each `domain.<X>` may only reach sibling contexts through the
sibling's `__init__.py` public surface — never by importing
`domain.<Y>.<submodule>`. cd-ev0 landed the three universal contracts
(domain→adapters, domain→handlers, adapters→handlers) but deferred
this per-context sibling contract because every `app/domain/<ctx>/__init__.py`
is still a docstring-only stub with no submodules to forbid.

Once domain contexts grow submodules (models.py, services.py, policy.py, etc.),
add the per-context sibling contract. The simplest shape is one
`forbidden` contract per context pair:

```toml
[[tool.importlinter.contracts]]
name = "identity forbids siblings' submodules"
type = "forbidden"
source_modules = ["app.domain.identity"]
forbidden_modules = [
  "app.domain.places.models", "app.domain.places.services", ...
  "app.domain.tasks.models", ...
]
```

A cleaner alternative is import-linter's `independence` contract, which
handles the N×(N-1) expansion automatically:

```toml
[[tool.importlinter.contracts]]
name = "Domain contexts are independent (siblings via __init__ only)"
type = "independence"
modules = ["app.domain.identity", "app.domain.places", "app.domain.tasks", ...]
ignore_imports = ["app.domain.* -> app.domain.*"]  # allow the public __init__ edge
```

Pick based on what actually reads cleanest once a handful of domain
contexts have real submodules.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"Boundary rules" rule 1,
  `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"Independence contract".
- Depends on domain contexts growing submodules
  (cd-chd, cd-w92, cd-i6u, cd-ijj, etc. have already added
  adapter-side tables; the domain-side service/policy/model split
  is still pending).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Per-context sibling contract present in `pyproject.toml`
- [ ] `uv run lint-imports` passes on the clean tree
- [ ] A deliberate `app.domain.tasks._bad -> app.domain.places.models`
      import fails `lint-imports`
- [ ] Existing tests in `tests/boundary/` still pass

## Test plan
```bash
uv run lint-imports
uv run pytest tests/boundary/ -xvs
```

title: fix(tests): test-pollution between integration DB tests and tenancy ORM-filter unit tests
description:
After cd-od3 (capabilities) landed, running the full `uv run pytest` suite began exposing a latent test-pollution bug in `tests/unit/test_tenancy_orm_filter.py`.

Symptom: one of the tenancy join/update tests fails with `DID NOT RAISE TenantFilterMissing` or `assert 'foo.workspace_id = ?' in flattened` when the integration capabilities tests run first. The tenancy hook verifiably fires (verified by instrumenting `_do_orm_execute` with print); `_collect_scoped_targets` returns the scoped tables; `ctx` is set; and the rewritten statement with the WHERE clause is assigned back to `orm_execute_state.statement`. Yet the SQL that reaches `sql_capture` lacks the predicate.

Heisenbug characteristic: adding `print()` statements inside `_do_orm_execute` makes the failure disappear — strong indicator of a timing / race / order-of-events issue inside SQLAlchemy's internal execution pipeline rather than a straightforward cache-hit.

Diagnosis attempts that did NOT fix it:
- `engine._compiled_cache.clear()` at module teardown.
- `engine.dispose()` at module teardown.
- Forcing `.execution_options(compiled_cache=None)` on the rewritten statement (broke 10 unrelated tests).
- Rearranging test order inside `TestRefreshSettings`.
- Clearing `sys.modules` caches.

Repro:
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_capabilities.py tests/unit/test_tenancy_orm_filter.py
# => 1-2 failed (test_join_two_scoped_tables_filters_both, test_self_join_with_alias_filters_both_sides,
#    test_update_scoped_without_ctx_raises, or a sibling join/update/alias test)
```

Narrowing: requires 8+ cumulative `Session` operations against the integration engine's DB connection before the tenancy tests run. Running any 7-or-fewer subset first is fine.

Follow-up candidates:
1. Hoist the tenancy test's `engine` fixture to session scope (avoids per-test engine rebuild, which is the only piece of state the integration conftest and the unit file share via SQLAlchemy internals).
2. Switch the tenancy test to a fresh process via `pytest-forked` for the affected tests.
3. Investigate SQLAlchemy's `_compile_state_attrs` or `_has_events` internal caches — the heisenbug characteristic matches an execution-time assertion that's racing with a cache.
4. Long-term fix: move tenant filter injection into a `before_compile` / `SchemaEvents` hook rather than `do_orm_execute`, so the predicate is part of the original compile step (and therefore cannot be silently bypassed).

Capabilities (cd-od3) unit + integration tests themselves are stable in isolation and with the full suite — they simply act as a reliable reproducer for this latent regression.
close_reason: Fixed: WeakSet idempotency for install_tenant_filter + defensive rewrite of _collect_scoped_targets

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text:
Additional selfreview investigation (cd-njs autofix, 2026-04-19):

Narrowed the failure: _collect_scoped_targets() returns only ['foo_with_bar'] for the join when the flake triggers, instead of ['foo', 'foo_with_bar']. The _walk_from recursion mis-handles the _ORMJoin's left side somehow — when we add ANY side-effect (print, sys.stderr.write) inside _walk_from or after _collect_scoped_targets, the correct two-table result is returned and the test passes.

Reconfirmed tried-and-failed fixes:
- .list() wrapping around _collect_scoped_targets — still fails with missing foo filter
- Using and_(*predicates) single .where() call instead of chained .where() — still fails (because scoped only has one target)
- @pytest.fixture(scope='session') on engine — causes 3 different test failures
- Explicit eng.dispose() in engine teardown — no effect
- new_stmt._generate_cache_key() / new_stmt.compile() before assignment — 1-2 unrelated tests break

Root cause sketch: get_final_froms() on the _ORMJoin returns a Join object whose left AnnotatedTable is not getting walked on a cold path. Adding a side-effect warms some internal state. Strong candidate: SQLAlchemy's ORM annotation deserialization lazily materializes the Join's .left property only after a compile / visit, which the print triggers via getattr.

Workarounds worth trying next:
- Use stmt.columns_clause_froms (returns implied FROMs from columns) instead of get_final_froms, then walk joins manually from stmt._from_obj.
- Use sqlalchemy.inspect() or visitors.traverse() to walk the FROM tree instead of get_final_froms (which triggers a full ORM compile that interferes).
- Call stmt.froms first (deprecated, but may not trigger the same compile path).

Leaving cd-3yhd OPEN because the fix is non-trivial — probably needs a rewrite of _collect_scoped_targets to avoid get_final_froms entirely. The capabilities (cd-od3) tests ship as the reliable reproducer.

title: Fix flaky test_update_scoped_without_ctx_raises in tests/unit/test_tenancy_orm_filter.py
description: When tests/unit/ is run as a whole, test_update_scoped_without_ctx_raises fails because a prior test (likely test_tenancy_registry or similar) registers 'foo' as a scoped table. The TenantFilterMissing exception is not raised because context is leaking across test modules. Fixture reset or module isolation likely needed. Fails on the current main branch (commit 70d7847), unrelated to cd-t5xe.

title: Self-review: feat(ops): crewday admin init/user-invite/workspace-bootstrap composite commands
description:
## Problem / goal
