title: feat(ops): crewday admin init/user-invite/workspace-bootstrap composite commands
description:
## Problem / goal
First-boot of a self-host deployment needs a deterministic CLI
path: `crewday admin init` provisions the deployment-scope row +
the root key + the first owner; `crewday admin user invite`
mints a magic-link for a new user without going through the HTTP
surface; `crewday admin workspace bootstrap` is the recovery hook
when an operator needs to re-create a workspace from scratch.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe A"
  ("Lockout recovery" is host-CLI-only; this task lands the
  composite verbs that recovery uses), §"Recipe D"
  ("admin worker container") for the SaaS path.
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth.md` (referenced via §02) — magic
  link generation; this task reuses the same code path,
  `via='cli'` actor.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/admin/init.py`:
  - `crewday admin init [--data-dir /data] [--root-key-file
    <path>]`: idempotent. If no root key found in env or
    `--root-key-file`, generates one and prints it once with a
    "store this immediately" warning. Creates the `deployment_setting`
    seed row, applies migrations, writes initial capability flags.
  - `crewday admin user invite --email … --workspace <slug>
    [--role owner|manager|worker|client]`: mints a magic-link
    using the §03 generator with `via='cli'`, prints the URL to
    stdout (no email send — operator may copy it manually).
  - `crewday admin workspace bootstrap --slug … --name …
    --owner-email …`: composite of `workspace.create` +
    `user_workspace.add(role=owner)` + `user invite`. Useful
    for re-seeding a deployment without web UI.
- Each command writes `audit_log` rows directly with `actor_kind=
  'system'`, `via='cli'`. Composite commands wrap their work in a
  single transaction.
- Argv parsing uses the existing CLI surface (§13 group `admin`).
- When `CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE=1`, every command refuses with the spec
  message ("admin commands not available in demo").

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `crewday admin init` is idempotent — second run is a no-op
- [ ] Generated root key is printed once; not stored anywhere on
      disk by the command
- [ ] `crewday admin user invite` prints a working magic-link URL;
      consumed once → workspace member
- [ ] `crewday admin workspace bootstrap` produces a usable
      workspace + owner row in a single transaction
- [ ] Every command writes audit rows under `actor_kind='system'`,
      `via='cli'`
- [ ] All three refuse to run under `CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE=1`

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_init.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_user_invite.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_workspace_bootstrap.py -xvs
```

title: feat(ops): structured logs + /metrics endpoint + OpenTelemetry trace hook
description:
## Problem / goal
Operators running crewday past trial size need the standard ops
trifecta: structured logs (already partly via `structlog`),
a Prometheus-compatible `/metrics` endpoint with workspace-tagged
counters, and an OpenTelemetry trace exporter wired to a
configurable collector. None of this is mandatory for self-host;
all of it is mandatory for the managed SaaS.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe D —
  Managed multi-tenant deployment" (where the observability stack
  matters most), §"Recipe A/B" (still benefit from logs + a
  scrape endpoint).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Logging and
  redaction" — the redactor must run before metrics labels (no
  per-user PII in label values).

## Implementation notes
- Logging: extend `app/util/logging.py` to add a request-id
  processor (per-request UUID, propagated to subprocess workers)
  and a workspace-id processor sourced from
  `WorkspaceContext`. Format JSON, one line per record.
- Metrics: new `app/observability/metrics.py` exposes a
  `prometheus_client` registry. Counters / histograms:
  - `crewday_http_requests_total{workspace_id, route, status}`
  - `crewday_http_request_duration_seconds{route}`
  - `crewday_llm_calls_total{workspace_id, capability, status}`
  - `crewday_llm_cost_usd_total{workspace_id, model}`
  - `crewday_worker_jobs_total{job, status}`
  - `crewday_worker_job_duration_seconds{job}`
  Endpoint `GET /metrics` is gated by a deployment setting
  (`settings.metrics_enabled`, default `true` in SaaS, `false`
  in self-host). When on, accessible only from
  `CREWDAY_METRICS_ALLOW_CIDR` (default `127.0.0.0/8` plus
  Tailscale ranges). Workspace label values pass through
  `redact()` to ensure no PII slips in (workspace slug is fine;
  user emails are not).
- Tracing: optional `opentelemetry-sdk` exporter targeting an
  OTLP endpoint configured via `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`.
  Auto-instrument FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, httpx. Disabled when no
  endpoint is configured.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every log line is JSON with `request_id` and (when in scope)
      `workspace_id` keys
- [ ] `GET /metrics` returns Prometheus format with the listed
      counters under load
- [ ] Workspace label values never include PII (asserted by a
      redaction round-trip test)
- [ ] `/metrics` returns 404 when disabled, 403 from a non-allowed
      CIDR
- [ ] Setting `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` produces traces in a
      local Jaeger container (manual verification step documented)
- [ ] No measurable overhead on the hot path (< 2% under load)

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/observability/test_metrics.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/observability/test_metrics_endpoint.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/observability/test_tracing.py -xvs
```

title: feat(ops): secret rotation CLI for SMTP, OpenRouter, HMAC, session secret + docs
description:
## Problem / goal
Operators need a documented, low-risk way to rotate the
deployment's third-party secrets — SMTP credentials, OpenRouter
key, the HMAC signing key (for guest welcome URLs and
file-signed-URL handouts), and the session-cookie signing
secret — without an outage. Today only the root-key rotation has
a CLI; this task adds parity for the others.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Secrets
  management" (root-key rotation is the model: never via argv,
  file or stdin only, zero-fill in-memory copy); §"Cookies"
  (session cookie value is opaque random — rotation = invalidate
  all sessions).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"TLS"
  + Recipes A/B for where the env vars are wired.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/admin/rotate_secret.py` with one entrypoint per secret:
  - `crewday admin rotate-smtp --new-cred-file <path>`: validates
    the new credentials by issuing a benign `EHLO` to `SMTP_HOST`,
    then writes them via the deployment-setting writer (atomic),
    audits `secrets.smtp.rotated`.
  - `crewday admin rotate-openrouter --new-key-file <path>`:
    validates by calling `models.list` against OpenRouter,
    then atomic-swap, audits `secrets.openrouter.rotated`.
  - `crewday admin rotate-hmac --new-key-file <path>`: writes the
    new key into the active slot and keeps the old key in a 72-hour
    legacy slot so in-flight signed URLs continue to verify;
    audits `secrets.hmac.rotated`.
  - `crewday admin rotate-session-secret --new-key-file <path>`:
    swap atomically AND invalidate every live session row (the
    session cookie value is opaque so a fresh secret does not by
    itself invalidate anything; we explicitly clear `sessions`).
- Same argv / stdin / file rules as `rotate-root-key` (no key on
  argv, file mode `0600`, stdin must not be a TTY, zero-fill).
- New docs section `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md →
  "Secret lifecycle"` (added in this task, cross-referenced from
  §15) listing each secret + rotation cadence + verification step.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each rotation command runs end-to-end against the dev stack
      and audits its action
- [ ] Argv-delivered key forms refuse with the spec error message
- [ ] HMAC rotation keeps a 72-hour legacy slot — old signed URLs
      still verify until expiry
- [ ] Session-secret rotation clears every live session row
- [ ] OpenRouter rotation validates the new key with a real probe
      before persisting
- [ ] Docs section published with each command + cadence

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_rotate_smtp.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_rotate_openrouter.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_rotate_hmac.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_rotate_session.py -xvs
```

title: feat(ops): crewday admin backup/restore — sqlite dump or pg_dump + uploads tarball
description:
## Problem / goal
A deployment that cannot be backed up and restored from scratch is
a deployment that will lose data. `crewday admin backup` produces
a single `.tar.zst` archive containing the DB dump, the uploads
tree, and the encrypted `secret_envelope` rows; `crewday admin
restore` validates the bundle (root-key fingerprint match) and
reconstructs the deployment.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe A →
  Backup" (SQLite WAL checkpoint, copy `crewday.db` + `data/files/`
  + `secret_envelope` into tar.zst, rotate last 30 daily + 12
  monthly), §"Recipe B → Backup" (`pg_dump -Fc` + `mc mirror`),
  §"Restore-with-wrong-key guard" (`crewday admin restore` refuses
  to start if any envelope's `key_fp` does not match the current
  root key or a loaded legacy key).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md`
  §"Root key compromise playbook" — restore is the inverse path of
  rotation; the `key_fp` machinery is shared.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/admin/backup.py`:
  - `backup(out_dir, *, encrypt_with_passphrase=None)`:
    1. SQLite: `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(FULL)` then sqlite3 backup
       API; PG: `pg_dump -Fc` via subprocess.
    2. tar.zst the DB dump + `data/files/` + a JSONL of every
       `secret_envelope` row.
    3. Manifest file with timestamps, row counts, current `key_fp`,
       and a content hash.
    4. Optional `--encrypt-with-passphrase` (age-encrypted) for
       ad-hoc transfer.
  - Rotation: keep last 30 daily + 12 monthly (configurable).
- New `crewday admin restore <bundle>`:
  - Read manifest, verify content hash.
  - Walk every envelope's `key_fp`; refuse start if any does not
    resolve under the current root key or a loaded legacy key
    (`--legacy-key-file <path>` repeatable).
  - Apply DB dump into a fresh DB, copy uploads, run migrations,
    then verify.
- Composite CLI command exposed via the §13 CLI surface
  (delegates to the admin module).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `crewday admin backup --to /tmp` produces a tar.zst
      containing the DB dump + uploads + `secret_envelope` JSONL +
      manifest
- [ ] Restore round-trip on SQLite reconstructs identical data
      (row-count fingerprint match)
- [ ] Restore round-trip on PG reconstructs identical data
- [ ] Restore refuses to proceed if a `key_fp` doesn't match
- [ ] `--legacy-key-file` allows restore against an older key
- [ ] Rotation deletes backups beyond the keep window
- [ ] Cron snippet from spec works end-to-end

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_backup_restore.py -xvs
CREWDAY_TEST_DB=postgres uv run pytest tests/integration/admin/test_backup_restore.py -xvs
```

title: test(e2e): playwright scaffold + auth helper + screenshot diff harness
description:
## Problem / goal
End-to-end browser tests gate the §17 release journeys: install +
first-boot enrollment, property/area/user creation + invite flow,
iCal → turnover → completion → guest-welcome flow, expense → autofill
→ approval → payslip flow, and an agent-driven task lifecycle. The
visual-regression harness keeps the styleguide pixel-stable.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"End-to-end"
  (Playwright Python bindings, headed locally, headless in CI; five
  GA journeys; passkey via WebAuthn virtual authenticator),
  §"Visual regression" (Playwright + `pixelmatch`,
  `/styleguide` baseline at 0.1% diff, other routes at 0.5%),
  §"360 px viewport sitemap" (full sitemap walk at 360×780, fail
  on any horizontal scroll / tap target < 44×44 / unreachable nav).

## Implementation notes
- New `tests/e2e/` with `playwright.config.ts` and Python bindings.
  Browsers: Chromium + WebKit (passkey ceremony coverage).
- `tests/e2e/_helpers/auth.py`:
  - `enroll_owner(page, email)` — full first-boot magic-link →
    passkey via WebAuthn virtual authenticator
  - `login_as(page, email)` — re-auth via virtual authenticator
- `tests/e2e/_helpers/visual.py`:
  - `compare_screenshot(page, name)` — pixelmatch against
    committed baseline; threshold per route (0.1% on
    `/styleguide`, 0.5% elsewhere)
- Pilot journey: install + first-boot owner enrollment lands in
  this task; the other four are filed as follow-up Beads tasks.
- 360 px sitemap walk: parametrise over the full authenticated
  sitemap (built from `_surface.json`), fail on any horizontal
  scroll or undersized tap target.
- Headless in CI; tracing on (first failure → trace.zip artefact).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `pnpm playwright test` runs locally (headed) and in CI
      (headless) against the dev stack
- [ ] First-boot owner-enrollment journey passes end-to-end on
      Chromium + WebKit
- [ ] WebAuthn virtual authenticator successfully completes both
      registration and assertion
- [ ] `/styleguide` screenshot diff fires on a deliberate
      pixel change
- [ ] 360×780 sitemap walk fails on a deliberately-introduced
      horizontal scroll
- [ ] CI uploads trace.zip on failure

## Test plan
```bash
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d
pnpm playwright test --project=chromium tests/e2e
pnpm playwright test --project=webkit tests/e2e
```

title: test(api): schemathesis contract tests against /openapi.json
description:
## Problem / goal
Schemathesis property-tests every endpoint declared in
`openapi.json`: status codes match the spec, response schemas
validate, idempotency keys are honoured, ETag round-trips behave.
This catches drift between the OpenAPI document and the actual
handler.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"API contract"
  (`schemathesis run --checks all ./openapi.json` against a live
  dev server seeded with fixture data; custom hooks enforce
  Authorization, idempotency, ETag), §"Quality gates"
  (`schemathesis` is a PR gate).

## Implementation notes
- Add `schemathesis` to dev deps.
- New `scripts/schemathesis_run.sh`:
  1. Boots the app under `pytest`'s `app_client` fixture (same
     factories as the integration suite seed).
  2. Runs `schemathesis run --checks all
     http://localhost:<port>/openapi.json --data-generation-method=
     positive,negative --hypothesis-max-examples=20`.
  3. Custom hooks (`tests/contract/hooks.py`):
     - assert `Authorization: Bearer …` is present on every
       non-public path
     - assert the `Idempotency-Key` header round-trips: a second
       call returns the cached response
     - assert `ETag` round-trip on GET → If-None-Match → 304
- CI runs schemathesis once per PR (the §17 < 5 min budget).
- Findings are surfaced via JUnit XML for the GitHub Action
  summary.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `make schemathesis` runs end-to-end and reports zero unhandled
      failures on the seeded fixture set
- [ ] Authorization, idempotency, ETag custom checks fire
- [ ] CI step `schemathesis` blocks merge on failure
- [ ] A deliberately broken handler (wrong status code) is caught
- [ ] Generated cases include negative inputs (bad enums, missing
      required fields)

## Test plan
```bash
make openapi
bash scripts/schemathesis_run.sh
```

title: feat(privacy): user data export, right-to-be-forgotten purge, retention policy worker
description:
## Problem / goal
GDPR-like rights are mandatory because much of the data is
personal: a user can request their own data as a JSON + files
bundle, an owner/manager can purge a person from a workspace
(anonymising free-text and scrubbing routing identifiers while
preserving financial rows), and a daily worker enforces the
spec retention defaults across `audit_log`, `session`, `llm_call`,
`email_delivery`, `webhook_delivery`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Privacy and data
  rights" (export endpoint, erasure command, payout-specific erasure
  steps, retention defaults table), §"Audit log" (purge writes to
  the audit log; archived rows land in
  `$DATA_DIR/archive/<table>.jsonl.gz`).
- **Spec**: §"Payout-specific erasure" — drop `secret_envelope`
  rows, clear `payout_destination` columns, scrub
  `payslip.payout_snapshot_json`, return 410 on subsequent
  payout-manifest requests.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/api/me/export.py`:
  - `POST /api/v1/me/export` queues a worker job, returns 202 +
    poll url. The job builds a JSON document covering every row
    where the user is the actor or the subject, plus referenced
    file blobs zipped under `attachments/`.
  - Email delivery on completion via the §10 notification service.
  - Bundle written to storage and downloadable for 7 days via a
    one-time signed URL (re-using §15 signed-URL plumbing).
- New CLI `crewday admin purge --person <id>`:
  - Anonymises `users.legal_name`, `email`, `phone`.
  - Scrubs free-text fields in tasks, comments, expenses,
    bookings the user authored.
  - Drops referenced `secret_envelope` rows from
    `payout_destination`; clears `display_stub`, `secret_ref_id`,
    `country`, `label`; preserves `id`/`kind`/`currency`/timestamps.
  - Scrubs `payslip.payout_snapshot_json` per spec — keeps amounts
    + dates + destination_id.
  - `POST /payslips/{id}/payout_manifest` returns `410 Gone` for
    affected payslips after purge.
  - Writes `audit.privacy.purge.complete`.
- New worker `rotate_operational_logs` (daily): applies the
  spec retention table per workspace; archives to
  `$DATA_DIR/archive/<table>.jsonl.gz`.
- Retention overrides per workspace via a settings row.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /me/export` produces a complete JSON + file bundle for
      the requester only (cross-user isolation tested)
- [ ] `crewday admin purge` anonymises the user row and scrubs
      free-text fields in dependent tables in a single transaction
- [ ] Payout manifest endpoint returns 410 after purge for
      affected payslips
- [ ] Financial amounts and timestamps survive purge (legal
      retention)
- [ ] Daily worker applies the spec retention to each table on
      both SQLite and PG
- [ ] Archive files are gzipped JSONL with one row per line
- [ ] Audit row written for both export-issued and purge-complete

## Test plan

Deployment-scope key/value settings: self-serve signup policy
(open / invite / closed), rate limits, passkey attestation policy,
default workspace budget cap. The capability registry is also
read out here.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is

title: feat(web/admin): LlmPage — providers, capability→model, prompts, sandbox
description:
## Problem / goal
The deployment-wide LLM control panel: configure providers, map
capabilities to models (`model_assignment`), preview prompt
templates, and run a test prompt against any provider for parity
checking. Without it the operator can't switch providers safely.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin" →
  `/admin/llm`; §11 LLM and agents §"Capability registry"; §12
  model-router.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/LlmPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `StatCard`, `Chip`, `Loading`;
  three-column miller layout (provider → model → assignment →
  capability) with selection state.
- **API endpoints**:
  `GET /admin/api/v1/llm/graph`,
  `GET /admin/api/v1/llm/calls`,
  `GET /admin/api/v1/llm/prompts`,
  `POST /admin/api/v1/llm/sync_pricing`,
  `POST /admin/api/v1/llm/assignments` (body: `{ capability,
  model_id, provider }`),
  `POST /admin/api/v1/llm/sandbox` (test sandbox call).
- **Types**: `LLMCall`, `LlmAssignment`, `LlmGraphPayload`,
  `LlmPromptTemplate`, `LlmSyncPricingResult`.

## Implementation notes
- The mock is ~825 lines — **split it** during promotion into a
  tree under `app/web/src/pages/admin/llm/`, keeping
  `LlmPage.tsx` itself under ~300 lines. Suggested split:
  - `ProviderColumn.tsx` — providers list (left column in the
    miller layout).
  - `ModelColumn.tsx` — models list for the selected provider.
  - `AssignmentColumn.tsx` — capability→model assignments for the
    selected model.
  - `CapabilityChain.tsx` — capability chain render (right column).
  - `ProviderModelPricing.tsx` — "Provider-model pricing" section
    (sync-pricing button + table at h2 `Pricing sync`).
  - `RecentCalls.tsx` — `Recent calls` table.
  - `PromptLibraryDrawer.tsx` — prompts drawer toggled by
    `promptsOpen`.
  - `lib/llmIndexes.ts` — the `assignmentsByCapability` / graph
    index helpers.
  - `LlmPage.tsx` — orchestrator composing the columns and owning
    query / SSE wiring.
- Refactoring is **only allowed** if rendered DOM + class names are
  unchanged — visual-diff is the hard gate.
- Preserve the column-selection mechanics + `useLayoutEffect` scroll
  pinning behaviour from the mock — visual parity matters.
- Mutations: invalidate `qk.adminLlmGraph()`, `qk.adminLlmCalls()`,
  `qk.adminLlmPrompts()` on success.
- SSE `llm.assignment.changed` invalidates the graph; SSE
  `llm.call.appended` prepends to the call list.
- Sandbox call posts the prompt + selected model and renders the
  raw provider response inline; do **not** persist to `llm_usage`
  unless the user opts in (mock has the toggle).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Provider / model / assignment / capability columns sync
      identically to mock.
- [ ] Capability→model assignment writes back and refreshes the
      graph.
- [ ] Sandbox call returns latency + tokens + cost preview.
- [ ] `sync_pricing` triggers a server refresh and shows a toast
      with the result.
- [ ] Permission gate: deployment-admin only.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes.
- [ ] No single file in the new tree exceeds ~300 lines.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_admin_llm.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/admin-llm.spec.ts
```

title: feat(web/admin): AuditPage — deployment-scope audit log + filters
description:
## Problem / goal
Every admin action is auditable; this page is the read-side. Filter
by actor kind (user / agent / system), scope, action, time range.
Required for incident response and compliance per §0 audit spec.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin" →
  `/admin/audit` — deployment-scope audit log; §0 audit
  requirements.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AuditPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Chip`, `Loading`, ACTOR_TONE map
  (`user→moss`, `agent→sky`, `system→ghost`).
- **API endpoints**:
  `GET /admin/api/v1/audit?actor_kind=&action=&since=&until=`.
- **Types**: `AuditEntry` (must include `scope_kind`,
  `actor_kind`, `actor_id`, `action`, `payload_json`,
  `created_at`).

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/admin/AuditPage.tsx`;
  filter UI controls + URL search-params sync.
- Server filters where `scope_kind = 'deployment'` only; this is
  enforced server-side, not client-side.
- TanStack Query keyed by the filter object so URL ↔ cache stay
  in sync.
- Wire SSE `audit.appended` (with `scope_kind=='deployment'` filter)
  to invalidate the current query.
- Preserve every column, the actor-tone chip, and the timestamp
  format from the mock.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Page lists deployment-scope rows only; cross-tenant data
      never appears in the response.
- [ ] Filter controls round-trip via URL search-params.
- [ ] SSE appends a fresh entry to the top of the list without a
      full re-render.
- [ ] Non-admin gets the standard not-authorised card.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes.

title: feat(web/manager): workspace settings — profile, appearance, agent prefs, usage
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager configures the workspace-wide knobs: workspace profile, default appearance / locale, agent behaviour preferences, plan & usage.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/SettingsPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/settings` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, SettingsCatalogSection, UsageMeter.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/settings, GET /api/v1/settings/catalog, GET /api/v1/properties, GET /api/v1/employees, GET /api/v1/workspace/usage, PATCH /api/v1/settings.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/SettingsPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- All mutations are catalog-driven via `SettingsCatalogSection` — do not duplicate per-field forms.
- Usage meter must respect the workspace plan's quota_json (Phase 1 `p1.workspace`).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/settings`.

title: feat(web/manager): permission groups + role grants + capability resolver
description:
## Problem / goal
Owner / manager curates permission groups (e.g. Cleaners, Senior Manager), grants capabilities by action catalog, and previews the effective permission set for any user.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PermissionsPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/permissions` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, PermissionRulesEditor, ResolverPanel.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/workspaces, GET /api/v1/users, GET /api/v1/permissions/groups, GET /api/v1/permissions/groups/{gid}/members, GET /api/v1/permissions/action_catalog, PATCH /api/v1/permissions/groups/{gid}/rules, POST /api/v1/permissions/resolve.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PermissionsPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**,
  but the mock is ~450 lines and already has internal
  sub-components (`GroupsTab`, `RulesTab`, `RuleChip`,
  `WhoCanDoThis`) — **split it** during promotion into a tree
  under `app/web/src/pages/manager/permissions/`, keeping
  `PermissionsPage.tsx` itself under ~200 lines. Suggested split:
  - `GroupsTab.tsx` — groups list + editor (from mock l.71).
  - `RulesTab.tsx` — rules list + editor (from mock l.218).
  - `RuleChip.tsx` — inline chip (from mock l.352).
  - `WhoCanDoThis.tsx` — capability resolver panel (from mock l.374).
  - `lib/usePermissionIndexes.ts` — `useWorkspaces`,
    `useUsersIndex` hooks (from mock l.53 / l.60).
  - `PermissionsPage.tsx` — orchestrator: tab state, route-level
    guard, SSE wiring.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons —
  rendered DOM and class names must be byte-equivalent to the mock.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The resolver call is read-only and idempotent — never fire it on every keystroke; debounce 300ms.
- Editing rules opens a draft state; nothing persists until "Save" — preserve the mock's draft model.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.
- [ ] No single file in the new tree exceeds ~200 lines.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/permissions`.

title: feat(web/admin): DashboardPage — deployment-wide stats landing
description:
## Problem / goal
The deployment admin (operator) lands on `/admin/dashboard` after
log-in and needs a single-pane view of deployment health: 30-day LLM
spend, workspace count, paused / stressed workspaces, and the most
recent deployment-scope audit entries. This is the `AdminLayout` home
page — without it, every admin nav target is an island.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin (bare host,
  deployment-admin grant only)" → `/admin/dashboard` — operator
  landing page: deployment health, recent audit, usage.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/DashboardPage.tsx`.
- **Components used**: `DeskPage`, `StatCard`, `ProgressBar`, `Chip`,
  `Loading` from `@/components/common`; `Link` from
  `react-router-dom`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /admin/api/v1/usage/summary`,
  `GET /admin/api/v1/workspaces`, `GET /admin/api/v1/audit`.
- **Types** (from `@/types/api`): `AdminUsageSummary`,
  `AdminWorkspaceRow`, `AuditEntry`.
- **Layout**: mounted under `AdminLayout` (no workspace prefix) per
  §14 "Admin" route block.

## Implementation notes
- Copy the mock TSX verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/admin/DashboardPage.tsx`.
- Replace the mock fixture handlers with real API calls via the
  shared TanStack Query client + `fetchJson` (Phase 16
  `p16.api-client`). Query keys come from `qk.adminUsageSummary()`,
  `qk.adminWorkspaces()`, `qk.adminAudit()`.
- Wire SSE invalidation: subscribe to
  `EventSource('/admin/api/v1/events')` in `AdminLayout` and
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: qk.adminUsageSummary() })`
  on `usage.summary.updated`, `qk.adminWorkspaces()` on
  `admin.workspace.changed`, `qk.adminAudit()` on `audit.appended`
  (filter to `scope_kind == 'deployment'`).
- Permission gate: route returns 404 from the API for non-admins
  (§14); render the "you don't have access" card defined in §14
  "Admin surface" when `GET /admin/api/v1/me` 404s instead of
  bouncing to /login.
- Preserve every CSS class, `grid--stats`, `Chip` tone, breakpoint,
  and copy string from the mock — visual parity is the test.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Page mounts at `/admin/dashboard`; `/admin` redirects there.
- [ ] All three queries load and render with mock-equivalent layout.
- [ ] Loading and error states use `<DeskPage>` shell with `<Loading />`
      / "Failed to load." identical to the mock.
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes summary, workspaces, audit on the
      relevant events (verified with a manual emitter).
- [ ] Non-admin user sees the "ask your operator" card, never `/login`
      bounce.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock at desktop breakpoint passes.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_admin_dashboard.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/admin-dashboard.spec.ts
# Manual: visual parity against `http://127.0.0.1:8100/admin/dashboard`.
```

title: feat(web/manager): stays calendar with iCal feeds + reservation drawer
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager sees the unit calendar across all properties, drills into a reservation, manages the per-property iCal feeds (Airbnb, Booking.com, Vrbo) and the manual booking flow.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/StaysPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/stays` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, StayCalendar, IcalFeedPanel.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/stays, GET /api/v1/properties, GET /api/v1/employees, GET /api/v1/me, POST /api/v1/stays, PATCH /api/v1/stays/{id}, GET /api/v1/ical_feeds.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/StaysPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The stay-task-bundle markers (§06) appear on the calendar; respect their colours and tooltip copy from the mock.
- iCal feed health is shown inline (`last_polled_at`, error chip) — wire to the SSE `ical.poll.completed` stream.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/stays`.

title: feat(api/admin): rotate tokens, export, purge, resend magic link, deployment_setting CRUD, workspaces list
description:
## Problem / goal
Admin router for deployment-level operations: rotate tokens,
trigger workspace exports / purges, resend a magic link, manage
deployment settings (capability registry, feature flags, mail
provider), and list / inspect workspaces. Distinct from the
chat-gateway, which lives in messaging, and the LLM admin, which
lives in llm.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Admin surface"
  (Workspace lifecycle, Self-serve signup, Deployment settings +
  capability registry, Admin team, Deployment audit).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/WorkspacesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/DeploymentSettingsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AdminTeamPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AuditPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/SignupPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/admin.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`admin\`. Mounted
  under \`/admin/api/v1\` (no slug — these endpoints are
  deployment-scoped).
- Workspace operations:
  - \`GET /workspaces\` — paginated list (admin cap only).
  - \`POST /workspaces/{slug}/exports\` — async; returns job id.
  - \`POST /workspaces/{slug}/purge\` — destructive; requires
    confirmation token; audited at deployment level.
  - \`POST /workspaces/{slug}/rotate-tokens\` — invalidates all
    PATs and agent tokens for the workspace.
  - \`POST /auth/resend-magic-link\` — admin-only resend (the
    user-facing one is rate-limited per email).
- Deployment settings: \`GET /settings\` + \`PATCH /settings\`
  for the capability registry, feature flags, mail provider,
  storage backend, etc. Reads OK for any admin team member;
  writes require \`deployment.settings.edit\` cap.
- Admin team CRUD; \`POST /admin-team/{user_id}/grant\` /
  \`/revoke\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All admin routes require admin-team membership; non-admin
      returns 403
- [ ] Purge requires \`confirm=<workspace-slug>\` body field
- [ ] Rotate-tokens invalidates and emits SSE
      \`api_token.bulk_revoked\`
- [ ] Settings PATCH validates against the capability registry
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`admin\`
- [ ] Deployment audit shows every admin mutation

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_admin.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_admin_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: Blocked behind foundation gaps surfaced during cd-jlms implementation attempt (2026-04-24). Current task description has stale acceptance criteria that contradict docs/specs/12-rest-api.md §'Admin surface' (spec forbids HTTP purge — host-CLI only; no /rotate-tokens; no /auth/resend-magic-link admin route; settings are per-key PUT not bulk PATCH; admin-team is /admins not /admin-team; workspaces addressed by {id} not {slug}). Blockers added: cd-wchi (scope_kind on role_grants), cd-kgcc (deployment audit writer), cd-xgmu (admin auth dep), cd-yj4k (GET /admin/api/v1/me). Rescope cd-jlms to spec-aligned surface (workspaces list/trust/archive + get, signup settings, per-key settings PUT, admin team CRUD, deployment audit feed, usage summary) once foundation lands.

title: feat(api/llm): agents, approvals, preferences, model assignments, usage, budget, outbound webhooks
description:
## Problem / goal
LLM router — everything the agent runtime, the per-user agent
preferences, and the deployment LLM-graph admin need: agent
runtime invocation, human-in-the-loop approval flow, per-user
preferences, model assignments (capability → model chain),
usage feed, budget ledger, and outbound webhook subscriptions.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"LLM and approvals",
  §"Agent preferences", §"Admin surface" (LLM graph + chat
  gateway + usage aggregates).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/LLMGraphPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/ProvidersPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/ModelsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AssignmentsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/PromptLibraryPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AgentDocsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/CallFeedPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/UsagePage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/BudgetPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/me/AgentPreferencesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ApprovalsPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/llm.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`llm\`.
- Agent invocation: \`POST /agents/{slug}/invoke\` — accepts
  prompt + page-context; returns either a result or an
  \`approval_request_id\` (409 \`approval_required\`).
- Approvals: \`POST /approvals/{id}/decision\` with
  \`approve|reject|amend\`; audited; SSE
  \`approval_request.decided\` fires.
- Preferences: \`/me/agent-preferences\` (per-user toggles).
- Model graph endpoints (providers / models / pricing /
  assignments / prompt library / agent docs) are
  deployment-scoped and live under \`/admin/api/v1/...\`,
  schema-defined here.
- Outbound webhooks: \`POST /webhooks\` registers a URL +
  HMAC secret; signed deliveries per spec §12 §"Webhook
  signatures".
- Budget endpoint exposes per-period spent_cents + cap_cents.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Agent invoke returns either result or 409
      \`approval_required\` with \`approval_request_id\`
- [ ] Approval decision audits + emits SSE
- [ ] Preferences PATCH per-user
- [ ] Provider / model / assignment CRUD on admin surface
- [ ] Outbound webhook delivery signs payload with HMAC
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`llm\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_llm.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_llm_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The decision mutation is shared with the dashboard quick-decide; reuse the exact same hook to keep cache invalidation coherent.

      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.

      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests

title: feat(api/payroll): pay rules, periods, payslips, exports
description:
## Problem / goal
Payroll router — per-user pay rules, pay periods, computed
payslips, and CSV exports for the bookkeeper.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Time, payroll,
  expenses" (Pay rules + Periods + Payslips + Exports
  subsections).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/PayRulesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PayPeriodsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PayslipDetailPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/me/PayslipsPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/payroll.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`payroll\`.
- \`POST /pay-periods/{id}/lock\` triggers payslip computation
  (audited; idempotent).
- \`POST /pay-periods/{id}/exports\` returns a job; client polls
  for completion. Emits SSE \`payroll_export.ready\`.
- \`POST /payslips/{id}/payout_manifest\` is exempt from
  idempotency cache per spec §12 §"Idempotency".
- Money fields serialise as integer cents; currency on every
  monetary object.
- Payslip read scoped to \`me\` for workers; managers see all.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD routes for pay-rule, pay-period, payslip
- [ ] Lock endpoint computes payslips; second lock is no-op
- [ ] Export endpoint returns job id; SSE event fires on ready
- [ ] payout_manifest bypasses idempotency cache
- [ ] Worker can read own payslip but not peers'
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`payroll\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_payroll.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_payroll_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(web/pwa): vite-plugin-pwa service worker + IndexedDB offline queue + background sync
description:
## Problem / goal
Workers complete tasks in low-connectivity properties; the SPA must
queue completions in IndexedDB and replay them via background sync
when connectivity returns. Also gives the app installable PWA
behaviour (install prompt, offline shell).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"PWA", §"Offline
  queue", §"Background sync".

## Implementation notes
- Add `vite-plugin-pwa` to `app/web/package.json`; configure in
  `vite.config.ts` with:
  - `registerType: 'autoUpdate'`
  - `manifest`: name, short_name, icons (256/512), theme_color from
    `tokens.css`, display `standalone`.
  - `workbox.runtimeCaching` — stale-while-revalidate for
    `/static/*`; network-first for `/w/*/api/*`; cache-first for
    `/icons/*`.
- New `app/web/src/lib/offlineQueue.ts`:
  - IndexedDB store `pending-mutations` with `id, kind, payload,
    createdAt, attempts`.
  - `enqueue(mutation)` from `useDecideMutation` /
    `useCompleteOccurrence` when navigator is offline.
  - Background-sync registration (`sw-sync`) drains the queue when
    online; per-item retry with exponential backoff; `Idempotency-
    Key` per mutation so server dedupes safely.
  - Surface a small "queued — will sync" badge in the shell when
    pending count > 0.
- Service worker stays tiny — defer heavy logic to the page.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] App is installable (Lighthouse PWA audit passes)
- [ ] Offline `complete occurrence` enqueues and replays on reconnect
- [ ] Each enqueued mutation carries a stable `Idempotency-Key`
- [ ] Pending-count badge visible while queue is non-empty
- [ ] Unit tests cover queue + retry; Playwright covers offline →
      online round-trip

## Test plan
```bash
cd app/web && npm run build
# Lighthouse PWA audit on the built dist
# DevTools offline → mutate → online → verify replay
```

title: feat(api/instructions): KB CRUD + versions + scope query
description:
## Problem / goal
Instructions KB router: CRUD for instructions, version history,
and the scope-resolution query that the worker app calls to fetch
"all instructions visible at this template/property/area/asset/
stay/role" in one request.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Instructions",
  \`docs/specs/07-instructions-kb.md\` §"Scope resolution".
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/worker/TaskDetailPage.tsx\` (sidebar
  fetches by scope), \`mocks/web/src/components/ScopeBadge.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/instructions.py\`: APIRouter with tag
  \`instructions\`.
- \`POST /instructions\` creates v1; \`PATCH /instructions/{id}\`
  bumps version (server-managed, monotonic).
- \`GET /instructions/{id}/versions\` lists history;
  \`GET /instructions/{id}/versions/{n}\` returns a specific one.
- \`GET /instructions:scope?template=...&property=...&area=...&
  asset=...&stay=...&role=...\` runs the scope-resolution
  algorithm and returns the merged list.
- All listings cursor-paginated.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD + versions endpoints with proper schemas
- [ ] PATCH bumps version, never overwrites in place
- [ ] Scope query resolves the union per spec rules
- [ ] Slug uniqueness enforced per workspace; collision → 409
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`instructions\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_instructions.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_instructions_scope.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(api/stays): iCal feeds, reservations, stay bundles, guest tokens
description:
## Problem / goal
Stays router — registration of iCal feeds, reservation listing
(read-mostly; rows come from the poller), stay bundles (the
generated turnover/welcome/deep-clean groupings), and the
short-lived guest tokens that gate the welcome surface.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Properties / areas /
  stays" (iCal + reservations + bundles + guest tokens
  subsections).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/CalendarsPage.tsx\` (iCal feeds),
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ReservationsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/StayBundleDetailPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/guest/web/src/pages/WelcomePage.tsx\` (consumes guest
  tokens).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/stays.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`stays\`.
- iCal endpoints: \`POST /ical-feeds\` accepts URL +
  property_id; \`POST /ical-feeds/{id}/poll\` triggers a manual
  poll (audited).
- Reservation listing supports the standard filters
  (\`?check_in_gte=\`, \`?status=\`, \`?property_id=\`).
- Stay bundle endpoint exposes regen
  (\`POST /stay-bundles/{id}/regenerate\`) — idempotent.
- Guest token issuance returns a short-lived (24-72h) opaque
  token + the public welcome URL. Issuance is audited; tokens
  cannot be listed (only issued / revoked).
- Welcome read endpoint accepts an anonymous bearer (the guest
  token); responds with curated read-only payload.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] iCal feed CRUD + manual poll
- [ ] Reservation listing with filters + cursor pagination
- [ ] Stay bundle regen idempotent
- [ ] Guest token issued + revocable; never re-listed
- [ ] Anonymous welcome read works only with a valid token
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`stays\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_stays.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_stays_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: chore(ci/cli): parity gate — crewday --help ≡ _surface.json ≡ openapi.json
description:
## Problem / goal
The CLI is only useful if it stays in lockstep with the API. A CI gate
must fail when any of these three diverge:
1. `crewday --help` (the resolved Click command tree),
2. `_surface.json` (the codegen output, committed),
3. `openapi.json` (the API source of truth, committed).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"Parity & CI",
  `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"CLI parity".
- **Builds on**: Task `p15.codegen --check` mode + a help-tree dump.

## Implementation notes
- New script `scripts/cli_parity_check.py`:
  1. Re-run codegen in `--check` mode (fails if `_surface.json`
     would change).
  2. Dump `crewday --help` recursively to a stable tree
     representation (resource → command → param signatures).
  3. Build the same tree from `_surface.json`.
  4. Diff (1) vs (2); diff (2) vs (3) by `operationId` set minus
     exclusions.
  5. Non-empty diff → exit 1 with a focused report (added /
     removed / changed signatures).
- New CI job `cli-parity` in the GitHub Actions workflow runs the
  script after the build step.
- README pointer in `cli/README.md` explains how to refresh
  (`python -m crewday._codegen` + commit) when the API changes.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `scripts/cli_parity_check.py` exits 0 on a clean tree
- [ ] Adding an op to `openapi.json` without regenerating fails CI
- [ ] Adding an exclusion without regenerating fails CI
- [ ] Failure report names the offending operation(s)
- [ ] Wired as a required check in CI

## Test plan
```bash
uv run python scripts/cli_parity_check.py
# induce drift then re-run to confirm failure mode
```

title: feat(cli/output): json | yaml | table | ndjson formatters
description:
## Problem / goal
`crewday --output <fmt>` (default `table`) routes every command's
response through a formatter. Tables render to a TTY-aware width;
NDJSON streams cleanly through `jq`; YAML and JSON for scripting.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"Output formats", §"Tables",
  §"NDJSON streaming for paginated lists".

## Implementation notes
- `cli/crewday/_output.py`:
  - `format_response(value, mode, schema_hint=None) -> str`.
  - `json` — `json.dumps(value, indent=2, sort_keys=False, default=…)`.
  - `yaml` — `yaml.safe_dump(value, sort_keys=False)`.
  - `table` — pick visible columns from `schema_hint` (the response
    schema's `x-cli-columns` extension) or fall back to top-level
    scalar keys; render with `rich.table.Table` width-aware.
  - `ndjson` — one object per line, no indent (used by `--all` +
    streamed iterators).
  - Colour off by default when stdout is not a TTY (`NO_COLOR` env
    respected).
  - Error formatter for `ApiError` — table for humans, JSON for
    scripts.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each mode round-trips a sample payload deterministically
- [ ] Table mode honours `x-cli-columns` schema extension
- [ ] NDJSON mode is line-buffered + safe for `| jq`
- [ ] Colour disabled when stdout is not a TTY or `NO_COLOR` set
- [ ] Unit tests cover all four modes + error formatting

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_output.py -xvs
crewday tasks list --output json | jq .
crewday tasks list --output ndjson | head -5
```

title: feat(cli/client): httpx async client — token auth, retries, streaming, idempotency, pagination
description:
## Problem / goal
Single shared HTTP client used by every generated command and every
composite override. Handles bearer/token auth from the active profile,
retries with backoff for transient failures, request streaming
(downloads), idempotency-key plumbing, and cursor pagination helpers.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"HTTP client", §"Retries",
  §"Streaming", §"Pagination".
- **API**: `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"Auth", §"Idempotency",
  §"Pagination", §"Errors".

## Implementation notes
- `cli/crewday/_client.py`:
  - `class CrewdayClient` wrapping `httpx.AsyncClient` with sync
    facade for Click commands.
  - Base URL + token loaded from `_config.py` active profile.
  - Default headers: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`,
    `User-Agent: crewday-cli/<version>`,
    `X-Workspace: <slug>` when present.
  - Retries: 3 attempts on connect-error / 502/503/504 with
    exponential backoff + jitter; never retry non-idempotent verbs
    unless an explicit `Idempotency-Key` is set.
  - Streaming: `download(path, dest)` honours `Range` resume.
  - Pagination: `iterate(path, params)` async generator returning
    pages until `next_cursor` is empty.
  - Error mapping: structured `ApiError(status, code, message,
    details)` raised on non-2xx.
- All requests log to the structured logger from Phase 0 with the
  correlation id echoed back from the server.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Token auth uses the active profile's token
- [ ] Retries do NOT clobber non-idempotent calls
- [ ] `iterate()` follows cursors; `download()` resumes via Range
- [ ] `ApiError` carries server `code` + `message` + `details`
- [ ] Unit tests cover retry, pagination, error mapping (use
      `pytest-httpx`)

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_client.py -xvs
uv run mypy --strict cli/crewday/_client.py
```

title: feat(api): per-token / per-IP rate-limit middleware (leaky-bucket)
description:
## Problem / goal
Per-token and per-IP rate limit on every API call. Returns 429 with
\`Retry-After\` per spec §"Rate limiting". v1 ships an in-memory
leaky-bucket per process (sticky session friendly); when running
multi-process, the bucket state is persisted in Postgres
(\`rate_limit_bucket\` table) so quotas survive a worker restart and
are coherent across workers. Deployed Redis-free in v1.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Rate limiting",
  §03 "Personal access tokens" for per-token limits.
- **Mocks consuming**: \`mocks/web/src/lib/api.ts\` retry hook
  honours \`Retry-After\` and surfaces a toast.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/middleware/rate_limit.py\`:
  - Bucket keyed by \`(token_id or ip_hash, route_class)\`.
  - Per-token defaults: 60 req/min standard, 600 req/min
    \`me:*\` PATs; per-IP fallback: 30 req/min.
  - Backend chosen by config: \`memory\` (single process) or
    \`postgres\` (multi-process). Postgres uses an UPSERT with
    advisory lock to keep bucket math atomic.
- Adds \`X-RateLimit-Remaining\` and \`X-RateLimit-Reset\`
  response headers on every call.
- 429 response uses the shared problem+json envelope and
  \`Retry-After\` header (seconds, integer).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] In-memory backend works in tests + single-worker dev
- [ ] Postgres backend correct under 4 concurrent workers
- [ ] 429 with correct \`Retry-After\` after burst
- [ ] PATs scoped \`me:*\` get the higher quota
- [ ] Anonymous (no token) requests fall back to per-IP bucket
- [ ] Header values match the actual remaining tokens

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/middleware/test_rate_limit.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_rate_limit_concurrent.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(cli/runtime): _runtime.py dynamic Click command builder
description:
## Problem / goal
At CLI startup, `_runtime.py` reads the committed `_surface.json` and
attaches every generated command to the Click root, so `crewday
<resource> <verb>` works without any hand-written wiring. Each command
maps its parameters to Click options, wires the HTTP client, and
invokes the formatter.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"Runtime", §"Parameter mapping",
  §"Pagination", §"Idempotency-Key".
- **Inputs**: `_surface.json` (Task `p15.codegen`), `_client.py`
  (Task `p15.http-client`), `_output.py` (Task
  `p15.output-formatters`).

## Implementation notes
- `cli/crewday/_runtime.py`:
  - `register_generated_commands(root: click.Group) -> None`.
  - For each surface entry: build `click.Command` with
    type-converted Click options for path params, query params, and
    body fields. Required flags map to required Click options.
  - JSON body construction: `--field key=value` repeating option +
    `--body-file <path>` escape hatch.
  - Pagination: when response is a list with `next_cursor`, expose
    `--all` to follow cursors and stream NDJSON or aggregate JSON
    depending on `--output`.
  - Mutating verbs (POST/PATCH/DELETE): auto-attach
    `Idempotency-Key` header (ULID); allow `--idempotency-key`
    override.
  - Errors: 4xx/5xx mapped to `click.ClickException` with the
    server-supplied message.
- Wire from `_main.py` so generated commands appear under the root
  group automatically.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every entry in `_surface.json` becomes a callable command
- [ ] Required + optional params map to Click options correctly
- [ ] `--field key=value` builds JSON bodies; `--body-file` works
- [ ] Mutating calls send `Idempotency-Key` automatically
- [ ] `--all` follows pagination cursors
- [ ] Unit tests cover parameter mapping + pagination

## Test plan
```bash
uv run crewday tasks list --help
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_runtime.py -xvs
```

title: feat(api): cursor-based pagination helpers (ULID cursors, no offset)
description:
## Problem / goal
Spec §"Pagination" mandates cursor-based pagination on every
collection endpoint, with \`limit\` default 50 / max 500 and
opaque \`next_cursor\`. This task provides a single helper that
every router calls: encode/decode cursors over (sort key, ULID),
clamp limit, and assemble the \`{data, next_cursor, has_more,
total_estimate}\` envelope.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Pagination",
  §"Request/response shape".
- **Mocks consuming**: every paginated list in
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/*\` and \`worker/*\` (tasks,
  occurrences, reservations, expenses, payslips, work orders…)
  uses TanStack Query's \`useInfiniteQuery\` keyed off
  \`next_cursor\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/pagination.py\`:
  - \`@dataclass class Cursor:\` — opaque base64url of
    \`(last_sort_value, last_id_ulid)\`. Signed with the
    deployment secret to prevent tampering.
  - \`paginate(query, *, sort, limit, cursor) -> Page[T]\` —
    SQLAlchemy generic helper.
  - \`Page[T]\` pydantic generic with \`data\`, \`next_cursor\`,
    \`has_more\`, optional \`total_estimate\` (only when caller
    explicitly opts in via \`?include_total=true\`).
- Limit clamp: default 50, max 500; out-of-range returns 400
  \`validation\`.
- ULID-based tie-break ensures stable pagination across writes.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Helper works for any sort field documented per resource
- [ ] Tampered cursor → 400 \`validation\`
- [ ] limit > 500 → 400; limit absent → 50
- [ ] \`has_more=false\` when fewer than \`limit\` rows returned
- [ ] \`total_estimate\` only present when opted-in
- [ ] Tests cover stable-pagination-under-write scenario

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/test_pagination.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_pagination_stability.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(cli/codegen): build _surface.json from openapi.json + _exclusions.yaml
description:
## Problem / goal
The CLI command tree is **derived** from the API surface — every public
REST resource gets `crewday <resource> <verb>` for free, with an
exclusion list for things that don't make sense on the CLI (file
uploads via raw multipart, browser-only routes, etc.). A build-time
generator reads `openapi.json`, applies the exclusions, and emits
`_surface.json` (committed to the repo) consumed by `_runtime.py`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"Codegen pipeline",
  §"Exclusions", §"Surface JSON shape".
- **Source**: `app/api/openapi.json` (Phase 14 produces this).
- **Convention**: codegen runs at build time, not at CLI startup;
  `_surface.json` is committed so users get a deterministic command
  tree without needing the API on disk.

## Implementation notes
- `cli/crewday/_codegen.py` — script + `python -m crewday._codegen`
  entrypoint. Reads `app/api/openapi.json`, walks paths, groups by
  tag → resource, maps HTTP verb → Click subcommand
  (`GET /tasks` → `crewday tasks list`, `GET /tasks/{id}` →
  `crewday tasks get`, `POST` → `create`, `PATCH` → `update`,
  `DELETE` → `delete`, `POST /…/close` → `tasks close`, etc.).
- `cli/crewday/_exclusions.yaml` — list of `operationId`s and path
  patterns the CLI must not generate (e.g. browser-only `/login/*`,
  raw blob uploads — composites cover those).
- Output: `cli/crewday/_surface.json` — array of command specs:
  `{group, name, http: {method, path}, params, body_schema_ref,
   response_schema_ref, summary, idempotent}`. Stable sort.
- CI step: `python -m crewday._codegen --check` re-runs and fails
  if `_surface.json` would change without being regenerated.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `python -m crewday._codegen` regenerates `_surface.json`
- [ ] `_exclusions.yaml` honoured (excluded ops never appear)
- [ ] Stable, deterministic output (idempotent re-run, sorted keys)
- [ ] `--check` mode exits non-zero on drift
- [ ] Unit tests cover verb→command mapping + exclusion logic

## Test plan
```bash
uv run python -m crewday._codegen
uv run python -m crewday._codegen --check
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_codegen.py -xvs
```

title: feat(cli): scaffold crewday Click-based entrypoint + core layout
description:
## Problem / goal
Stand up the `cli/crewday/` Python package and the `crewday` Click
entrypoint so every later CLI task has a stable shell to plug into.
This is the foundation for the codegen-driven command tree, the HTTP
client, output formatters, profiles, and composite commands.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` (entire file — esp. §"Package
  layout", §"Command shape", §"Auth & profiles").
- **Architecture**: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"cli/" and
  §"Repo layout".
- **Conventions**: project uses `click` + `httpx` + `pydantic` (see
  Phase 0 `pyproject.toml`). Python 3.12+, `mypy --strict`.

## Implementation notes
- New package `cli/crewday/` (entry point declared in `pyproject.toml`
  as `[project.scripts] crewday = "crewday._main:main"`).
- Core modules — leading underscore so they are not part of the
  generated command surface:
  - `_main.py`     — Click root group, version flag, `--profile`,
                     `--output (json|yaml|table|ndjson)`,
                     `--workspace <slug>`, common error handling.
  - `_client.py`   — placeholder (Task `p15.http-client` fills it in).
  - `_output.py`   — placeholder (Task `p15.output-formatters`).
  - `_config.py`   — placeholder (Task `p15.profile-config`).
  - `_globals.py`  — Click context dataclass: profile, workspace,
                     output mode, idempotency-key generator, logger.
- Wire `_main.main()` so `crewday --help` and `crewday --version`
  work end-to-end without any subcommand registered yet.
- `cli/README.md` — one-paragraph overview pointing at §13.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `crewday --help` prints the root help with the global flags
- [ ] `crewday --version` prints the package version
- [ ] `_main.py` exposes a `main()` function and is importable
- [ ] `_globals.CrewdayContext` carries profile / workspace / output
- [ ] `mypy --strict cli/` passes; `ruff check cli/` clean
- [ ] Unit test invokes the Click runner against `crewday --help`

## Test plan
```bash
uv run crewday --help
uv run crewday --version
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_main.py -xvs
uv run mypy --strict cli/
```
close_reason: Shipped: crewday CLI scaffolding — Click root group with --profile/--workspace/--output/--version, frozen CrewdayContext, ExitCode + CrewdayError hierarchy, handle_errors decorator, PEP 561 py.typed, hatchling build target. Downstream tasks (HTTP client, codegen, formatters, config) plug into this root.

title: feat(transport/sse): /w/<slug>/events stream with role + kind allowlist
description:
## Problem / goal
A single `EventSource('/w/<slug>/events')` per browser tab
carries every cross-client coherence signal: TanStack Query
invalidations, agent turn indicators, pending approvals, LLM
budget refused notices. Filters by the connecting actor's role
and an event-kind allowlist so a worker does not see manager-only
streams.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Agent turn
  lifecycle" (`agent.turn.started/finished`),
  §"Inline approval UX" (`agent.action.pending`),
  §"Workspace usage budget" (`llm.budget_exceeded` log /
  client banner), §"Embedded agents" (`agent.message.appended`).
- Frontend spec: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (TanStack
  Query invalidation feed).
- Cross-ref: `p0.events` (in-process pub/sub) is the source;
  this task wires the HTTP transport.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/api/transport/sse.py` with
  `@router.get('/w/{slug}/events')` returning a
  `text/event-stream` response.
- Resolves the calling user → membership in workspace `slug` →
  effective role; subscribes to `p0.events` and forwards only
  events matching `{role}` + the per-event `kind` allowlist
  (declared on the event class).
- Scoped events (e.g. `agent.turn.*`,
  `agent.action.pending`, `agent.message.appended`) carry an
  `actor_user_id` and are forwarded only to the matching
  user's tabs.
- Heartbeat comment every 15 s; reconnection via standard
  `Last-Event-ID` (events buffered in-memory for 60 s).
- Resilience: backpressure drops the slowest consumer with a
  closing `event: dropped` frame; client reconnects.
- Companion endpoint `/admin/events` for the deployment-admin
  surface; same code, different scope (no workspace slug).
- TanStack Query integration shipped as part of the web bundle:
  on each event, call `queryClient.invalidateQueries` keyed by
  the event's `invalidates` field.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Two tabs of the same user receive identical event
  streams.
- [ ] A worker tab does not receive a manager-only event.
- [ ] An `agent.action.pending` event scoped to user A is not
  delivered to user B.
- [ ] Heartbeat keeps an idle connection alive past common
  proxy timeouts (60+ s).
- [ ] `Last-Event-ID` reconnect replays the missed events
  within the 60 s buffer.
- [ ] Slow consumer drops cleanly; the rest of the fanout is
  not blocked.
- [ ] No PII in event payloads beyond what the user could
  already read.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/api/transport/test_sse.py`.
- Integration test: two browsers (Playwright) on the same
  workspace see a TanStack invalidation fire when one creates a
  task.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").
close_reason: Shipped: SSE transport at /w/<slug>/events. In-process fan-out built on app.events.bus; role + kind allowlist via ClassVar on Event; user-scoped events filtered by actor_user_id; 15 s heartbeat; 60 s Last-Event-ID replay with per-process stream token so a restart forces a full replay; 256-slot bounded queue with event:dropped frame on backpressure; TanStack Query invalidation map per §14. /admin/events twin explicitly deferred to cd-yj4k per director brief.

title: feat(integrations/webhooks): outbound HMAC-signed delivery + retries
description:
## Problem / goal
External integrations (and the per-spec `approval.pending`,
`approval.decided` notifications) need an outbound webhook
channel: subscription CRUD, HMAC-SHA256 signed payloads, retry
with exponential backoff, dead-letter on permanent failure.
Used by the agent runtime to notify external systems and by the
approval flow to push pending decisions.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Agent action
  approval" (mentions `approval.pending`, `approval.decided`
  webhooks); cross-ref §10 "Webhooks".
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` —
  `webhook_subscription`, `webhook_delivery` (DLQ row when
  retries exhausted).
- Auth scope: `webhook.manage` on the workspace agent token.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/integrations/webhooks.py` with
  `sign(payload_bytes, secret) -> sig`,
  `enqueue(workspace_id, event, payload) -> delivery_id`, and
  the worker `deliver(delivery_id)`.
- Signature format: `X-Crewday-Signature: t=<unix>,v1=<hex>`,
  HMAC-SHA256 over `f"{t}.{body}"` with the subscription
  secret. (Stripe-style; documented in `docs/specs/12`).
- Retry schedule: 0 s, 30 s, 5 m, 1 h, 6 h, 24 h (six attempts
  total). Permanent failures (HTTP 4xx other than 408/429) skip
  retries and dead-letter immediately.
- Dead-letter rows visible in `/admin/webhooks` (managed
  elsewhere). Manual replay via `POST
  /admin/api/v1/webhooks/deliveries/{id}/replay`.
- CRUD endpoints for subscriptions: `POST/GET/PUT/DELETE
  /api/v1/webhook_subscriptions`. Secrets are returned only at
  create time (subsequent reads return `secret_last_4`).
- Subscription mutations write `audit.webhook_subscription.{
  created,updated,deleted}`. Successful deliveries do not audit
  (operational telemetry); permanent failures audit
  `audit.webhook_delivery.dead_lettered`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Sign / verify round-trips with the documented header
  format.
- [ ] A 500 from the receiver triggers the retry schedule;
  delivery succeeds on the third attempt.
- [ ] Six failures dead-letter the row + audit.
- [ ] A 400 dead-letters immediately (no retries).
- [ ] Replay re-attempts the same payload with a new signature
  timestamp.
- [ ] Subscription secret is returned only at create.
- [ ] Workspace at-cap does not block webhook delivery (envelope
  is LLM-only).

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/integrations/test_webhooks.py`
  with a stub HTTP server.
- Integration test: agent triggers `approval.pending` → mock
  receiver gets a signed POST.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").
