title: feat(agent/staff-chat): worker chat assistant with delegated tool use
description:
## Problem / goal
The worker-side embedded chat agent (§11 "Worker-side agent")
runs on the same runtime as the manager agent but with a tighter
tool catalog scoped to the calling worker's permissions: today's
tasks, mark done, report issue, get instruction, my bookings,
inventory low. Per-channel context and turn semantics from
`p12.agent-runtime`.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Worker-side
  agent", §"Staff chat assistant".
- Auth: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Agent tokens".
- Cross-ref: §10 "Chat" — consumed by `p10.chat-message.crud`
  (message persistence).

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/agent/staff_chat.py` registering the
  worker tool catalog (`get_tasks_today`, `mark_task_done`,
  `report_issue`, `get_instruction`, `get_my_bookings`,
  `amend_booking`, `decline_booking`, `propose_booking`,
  `get_inventory_low`). Each tool is a thin facade over the
  REST surface invoked under the delegated token.
- Capability key: `chat.worker` (seeded per §11).
- `X-Agent-Channel: web_worker_chat` set on every dispatched
  call so inline approval cards land in the worker's chat
  surface.
- Voice input gated by `voice.enabled` workspace setting +
  `voice.transcribe` assignment (off by default).
- Cannot create arbitrary `chat_message` rows — message
  persistence flows through `p10.chat-message.crud`. Cannot
  fabricate tasks: only the exposed tools are reachable.
- Per-channel context: each thread carries its own compaction
  state (one live summary per thread, span-based, recent-window
  floor of 24 h or 20 turns).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A worker asks "what's on my plate today" → exactly
  `get_tasks_today` is called → reply lists the tasks.
- [ ] "Mark the kitchen task done" → resolves to one task →
  calls `mark_task_done` → workspace policy gates the action
  if configured.
- [ ] Tool catalog scoped to the worker — no admin / payroll /
  expense-write tools reachable.
- [ ] Each turn brackets correctly with
  `agent.turn.started/finished`.
- [ ] Compaction kicks in once the recent-window floor is
  exceeded; one live summary per thread.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/agent/test_staff_chat.py`.
- Integration test: worker session → chat thread → marks a task
  done → audit row attributes to the worker user + delegated
  token.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(llm/tasks-intake): parse free text → template + schedule + assignee
description:
## Problem / goal
`POST /api/v1/tasks/from_nl` turns a free-text instruction ("Have
Maria deep-clean the guest bath every Tuesday 9am at Villa Sud")
into a resolved `{property, assignee, template, schedule}` plus
an explicit `assumptions` and `ambiguities` list. Always
dry-run-first; the caller confirms via `POST /from_nl/commit`.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Natural-language
  task intake".
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`task_template`,
  §`task_schedule`.
- Cross-ref: §05 "Schedule CRUD" — consumed by
  `p5.schedule.crud` (the commit endpoint).
- Mocks: NL intake composer in the manager web.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/llm/capabilities/tasks_intake.py`
  with `draft(ctx, text) -> NlPreview` and
  `commit(ctx, preview_id, edits) -> ScheduledTask`.
- LLM resolves property name, assignee name, RRULE, evidence
  flags. Resolver also performs a deterministic post-pass: every
  named entity (property, user, evidence kind) must resolve to a
  concrete row or land in `ambiguities` — no free-text
  fabrication into the resolved object.
- Preview persisted to a short-lived table `nl_task_preview`
  (TTL 24 h) keyed by `preview_id`.
- Commit honours `Idempotency-Key` per §12.
- Budget + audit via the standard wrapper. Reads of the workspace
  graph (properties / users) flow through tenant filters.
- Failure modes: ambiguity raised when more than one match for a
  named entity; `assumptions` filled when exactly one match for
  an unspecified field.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Sample text in the spec produces the expected `resolved`
  shape and `assumptions` list.
- [ ] Two properties whose names both match raise an ambiguity.
- [ ] `commit` with an unmodified preview creates the
  scheduled task; `commit` after edits applies the edits.
- [ ] Idempotency-Key dedup works on commit.
- [ ] Expired preview returns `410 preview_expired`.
- [ ] Audit on commit attributes to the calling user with token
  + agent_label when delegated.

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/llm/capabilities/test_tasks_intake.py`.
- Integration test: end-to-end from NL to a row in `task_template`
  + `task_schedule`.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(web/employee): MyExpensesPage — create, OCR-autofill receipts, list claims
description:
## Problem / goal
Worker's expense-claim surface (§09 expenses). Upload a receipt photo, OCR populates the form fields, the worker reviews and submits. Lists past claims with status chips; tracks pending reimbursements.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `PageHeader`, `AutoGrowTextarea`, `Chip`, `Loading`, `Search` icon, `formatMoney`, `fmtDate`. Types: `Expense`, `ExpenseCategory`, `ExpenseScanResult`, `ExpenseStatus`, `PendingReimbursement`. ScanPhase state machine: `upload | processing | review | submitted`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me/expenses`; `POST /api/v1/me/expenses/scan` (multipart -> OCR result); `POST /api/v1/me/expenses` (create); `GET /api/v1/me/expenses/pending-reimbursements`.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Preserve the `ScanPhase` state machine exactly — visual feedback is tied to it.
- OCR call is async (LLM); show the processing spinner.
- Preserve `STATUS_TONE` mapping for chip colours.
- The mock is ~450 lines — **split it** during promotion into a
  tree under `app/web/src/pages/employee/expenses/`, keeping
  `MyExpensesPage.tsx` itself under ~200 lines. Suggested split:
  - `SubmitExpenseForm.tsx` — "Submit an expense" form (mock l.218).
  - `ReceiptScanPanel.tsx` — OCR / ScanPhase state machine + preview.
  - `OwedToYou.tsx` — "Owed to you" reimbursement section (mock l.387).
  - `RecentExpenses.tsx` — "My recent expenses" list (mock l.407).
  - `lib/expenseHelpers.ts` — `confidenceClass`, `STATUS_TONE`,
    `CATEGORIES`.
  - `MyExpensesPage.tsx` — orchestrator: query wiring, SSE, layout.
  Rendered DOM + class names must remain byte-equivalent to the mock.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.
- [ ] No single file in the new tree exceeds ~200 lines.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: feat(web/employee): MePage — profile + avatar + shift clock + personal tokens
description:
## Problem / goal
Worker's account hub (§14 worker me). Profile editing, avatar upload, language selection, shift clock-in/clock-out widget, agent approval-mode panel, agent preferences, appearance, chat-channels card, and personal API tokens.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `AgentApprovalModePanel`, `AgentPreferencesPanel`, `AppearancePanel`, `AvatarEditor`, `ChatChannelsMeCard`, `PersonalTokensPanel`, `Chip`, `Loading`, `Pencil` icon. Types: `Me`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me`; `PATCH /api/v1/me` (profile updates); `POST /api/v1/me/avatar` (multipart); `POST /api/v1/me/shifts/clock-in` + `/clock-out`; `GET|POST|DELETE /api/v1/me/tokens`.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —

  must be empty.

  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- The page composes many sub-panels; copy each as it stands.
- Personal tokens panel must show prefix only after creation (secret displayed once).

title: feat(llm/receipt-ocr): vendor / amount / date / category / currency parsing
description:
## Problem / goal
The `expenses.autofill` (a.k.a. receipt OCR) capability lifts
structured fields from a receipt image. Used by the receipt
upload flow to pre-fill the expense form; the user always
confirms before save.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Capability
  catalog" (`expenses.autofill`), §"Capability defaults
  (seeds)" (Gemma 3 27B IT with vision tag).
- Cross-ref: §08 "Expenses & receipt OCR" — consumed by
  `p8.receipt.ocr` (the upload pipeline).
- Mocks: existing receipt upload UI under `mocks/web/src/pages/`
  expenses.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/llm/capabilities/receipt_ocr.py` with
  `extract(ctx, image_bytes) -> ReceiptDraft`.
- Prompt template registered under capability
  `expenses.autofill` in `llm_prompt_template` (self-seeded
  per §11).
- Structured output via JSON-mode (Gemma supports it); pydantic
  schema enforces `vendor`, `amount_cents`, `currency_iso4217`,
  `occurred_on`, `category` (free text), `confidence_pct`.
- Calls the LLM via `p12.usage-recorder` (so budget + audit
  apply); image is redacted from prompt logs (PII posture per
  §11 "Redaction / PII").
- On parse failure (model returned non-JSON or schema mismatch),
  falls through the assignment chain; if the chain is exhausted,
  raises `ReceiptParseError` (the upload caller falls back to
  manual entry, no partial fill).
- Currency defaults to the workspace currency (§09) when the
  model can't determine it; `occurred_on` defaults to "today"
  in the property's local tz.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A canonical-shape receipt parses to a `ReceiptDraft` with
  every field populated.
- [ ] A receipt missing the vendor name returns
  `vendor=None` (no fabrication) and a low confidence score.
- [ ] A non-receipt image (e.g. a selfie) raises
  `ReceiptParseError`.
- [ ] Workspace at-cap blocks the call with
  `budget_exceeded` and the upload caller surfaces the
  message verbatim.
- [ ] An admin disabling the assignment for
  `expenses.autofill` makes the capability return
  `capability_unassigned` (the upload UI hides autofill).

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/llm/capabilities/test_receipt_ocr.py`
  with stubbed LLM responses.
- Integration test: a fixture receipt image flows through and the
  resulting `Expense` row matches the draft.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(chat-gateway): event-bus subscriber that dispatches inbound chat to agent runtime
description:
## Problem / goal
Decouple the webhook from agent execution. A subscriber on
\`chat.message.received\` enqueues an agent dispatch job and stamps
the message with \`dispatched_to_agent_at\` for the audit trail.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/23-chat-gateway.md\` §"Routing" →
  §"Inbound", §"Events".
- Recent commit \`f869841 spec(chat-gateway): decouple agent
  dispatch from webhook request\` codified this split — preserve
  it.

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/chat_gateway/dispatcher.py\` registered against
  the event bus on \`chat.message.received\`.
- Loads the message + binding context (workspace, channel, source
  provider, language hint) and enqueues an
  \`AgentDispatchJob(message_id, capability='inbound_chat')\` for
  the agent runtime worker (Phase 12).
- On enqueue success, stamps \`chat_message.dispatched_to_agent_at\`
  (UTC).
- On exception: logs + audits \`chat_gateway.dispatch.failed\` and
  leaves \`dispatched_to_agent_at\` NULL so the sweep job retries
  (see \`p13.gateway.sweep\`).
- Idempotent on \`message_id\` (don't double-stamp; don't double-
  enqueue when the row is already dispatched).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] One enqueue per inbound message under steady state
- [ ] \`dispatched_to_agent_at\` set iff enqueue succeeded
- [ ] Failed enqueue leaves the field NULL + emits failure audit
- [ ] Re-firing the event for an already-dispatched message is a
      no-op
- [ ] Unit tests cover happy-path, failure-path, and idempotency

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/chat_gateway/test_dispatcher.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/chat_gateway/test_dispatcher_event.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(chat-gateway): inbound webhook normalising provider payloads → ChatMessage
description:
## Problem / goal
A single \`POST /webhooks/chat\` endpoint terminates inbound traffic
from WhatsApp, SMS, and email-to-chat providers, verifies the
provider signature, normalises the payload to a \`ChatMessage\` row
in a \`channel.kind='chat_gateway'\` channel, and acks fast.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/23-chat-gateway.md\` §"Channel catalog",
  §"Bindings" → §"\`chat_channel_binding\`",
  §"Routing" → §"Inbound", §"Provider abstraction",
  §"Secret envelopes", §"Security".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/admin/ChatGatewayPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- New router \`app/api/chat_gateway/webhooks.py\` mounted under
  \`/webhooks/chat/{provider}\` (one URL per provider — Twilio,
  Meta-WA, Postmark inbound, …). Provider name resolves the
  signature verifier + payload adapter.
- Adapters in \`app/adapters/chat_gateway/<provider>.py\` implement
  \`verify(headers, raw_body, secret) -> bool\` and
  \`normalise(payload) -> NormalisedInboundMessage\`.
- HMAC verification is mandatory; failure returns 401, no body
  written.
- Look up or auto-create the gateway channel (per spec link
  ceremony) using the binding (\`provider\`, \`external_contact\`).
- Persist a \`chat_message\` row (\`author_user_id=NULL\`,
  \`author_label=<phone or email>\`, \`source=<provider>\`).
- Publish \`chat.message.received\` (separate from
  \`chat.message.sent\` — inbound gets its own bus event for the
  gateway dispatcher).
- Audit \`chat_gateway.message.received\` with provider + binding.
- Reply with \`200\` ASAP; downstream agent dispatch is async (see
  \`p13.gateway.dispatcher\`).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Unsigned / wrong-signature requests rejected, no row written
- [ ] Idempotent on the provider's message id (replay-safe)
- [ ] Auto-creates a \`chat_gateway\` channel + binding on first
      contact
- [ ] Returns < 1 s under normal payloads (no agent dispatch
      inline)
- [ ] Unit tests per provider adapter; integration test with at
      least one real-shape Twilio payload

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/chat_gateway/test_webhook.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/unit/chat_gateway/test_adapters.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/chat_gateway/test_webhook_e2e.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(messaging): notification service with multi-channel fanout
description:
## Problem / goal
Single \`NotificationService\` that creates a \`Notification\` row and
fans out to (web inbox, email, web push, SSE). Reads a \`kind →
template\` map so callers don't repeat per-channel formatting.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"Channels",
  §"Email" → §"Template system", §"In-app messaging", §"Agent-message
  delivery".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/components/ChatChannelsMeCard.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/ChatPage.tsx\` (consumer surfaces).

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/messaging/notifications.py\`:
  \`NotificationService(ctx: WorkspaceContext)\` with
  \`notify(recipient_user_id, kind, payload)\`.
- Persist a \`notification\` row (Phase 1 table), then dispatch to:
  - **inbox**: row already persisted, SSE event \`notification.created\`
    so the web client invalidates \`['notifications','unread']\`.
  - **email**: render template via Phase 0 mailer, respect
    \`email_opt_out\`.
  - **push**: enqueue web-push job (see \`p10.web-push.worker\`).
- Templates in \`app/domain/messaging/templates/<kind>.{subject,body_md,push}.j2\`.
  Lookup by \`kind\` enum; fail loudly on missing template.
- Honour the user's \`locale\` for subject/body rendering.
- Audit every dispatch (\`messaging.notification.dispatched\`,
  channel, kind, recipient).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`notify(...)\` writes one \`notification\` row + emits exactly
      one SSE event per recipient
- [ ] Email path skipped when recipient has matching
      \`email_opt_out\` row
- [ ] Push path enqueued only when recipient has \>=1 active
      \`push_token\`
- [ ] Missing template raises \`TemplateNotFound\` (no silent skip)
- [ ] Unit tests cover all four channels with one fanout call
- [ ] Integration test: end-to-end via SSE client subscriber

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/messaging/test_notification_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/messaging/test_notification_fanout.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(tasks): manager approval service for completed tasks
description:
## Problem / goal
Some templates flag completed tasks for manager review (e.g. a
deep clean before a high-value stay). The approval service holds
the `pending_review` state machine and exposes
approve / reject / request-changes verbs.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Completing
  a task" (approval gate behind `template.required_approval` /
  resolved-setting `tasks.required_approval`);
  `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"task" (the `state` enum holds
  `completed` — `pending_review` lives on a sibling
  `task_approval` row, since completion is terminal in §06).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ApprovalsPage.tsx`
  enumerates the queue and exposes the three verbs.

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/approvals.py`.
- New row `task_approval`:
  - `id`, `workspace_id`, `task_id` (unique partial when
    `state IN ('pending', 'changes_requested')`),
    `requested_at`, `requested_by_user_id`,
    `state` enum (`pending | approved | rejected |
    changes_requested`), `decided_at`, `decided_by_user_id`,
    `note_md`, `created_at`, `updated_at`.
- Functions:
  - `request_review(ctx, task_id)` — invoked from
    `p5.completion.complete()` when the resolved setting
    `tasks.required_approval = true`. Inserts a `pending` row
    and emits `task.approval_requested`.
  - `approve(ctx, approval_id, *, note_md)` — owners/managers
    only; sets `state = 'approved'`, `decided_at`,
    `decided_by_user_id`; emits `task.approved`.
  - `reject(ctx, approval_id, *, note_md)` — sets
    `state = 'rejected'`; emits `task.rejected`. Worker is told
    via the `task.rejected` notification that the task needs
    redoing; the task itself stays `completed` (the reject is a
    review judgment, not a re-open — re-opens are out of scope
    of v1).
  - `request_changes(ctx, approval_id, *, note_md)` — sets
    `state = 'changes_requested'`; emits
    `task.changes_requested`. Worker may post evidence /
    comments; manager re-decides via `approve` / `reject`.
- Permission: the route layer (Phase 12) checks
  `tasks.review.decide` on the deciding user; assert here too
  for defence-in-depth.
- `WorkspaceContext` everywhere; one audit row per mutation.
- Event payloads carry `task_id`, `approval_id`,
  `decided_by_user_id`, `state`, `note_md`.
- The approvals queue read used by `ApprovalsPage`:
  `list_pending(ctx, *, property_id=None) -> list[ApprovalView]`
  joining `task_approval` + `task` + `template` for the surface
  fields the manager needs (title, property, completed_by,
  completed_at, evidence count).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Partial-unique constraint prevents two open reviews on the
      same task
- [ ] Three verbs each emit the right event + audit row
- [ ] Permission asserts present; tests cover the unauthorised
      branch
- [ ] `request_review` called automatically from the completion
      service when the resolved setting is on
- [ ] Unit tests cover the state machine and read path

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_approvals.py -xvs
```

title: feat(tasks): detect_overdue worker job
description:
## Problem / goal
`overdue` is a soft state that lights up `/today` and the manager
dashboard the moment a task slips past `due_by_utc + grace`. A
periodic worker flips eligible tasks and emits `task.overdue` so
notifications + dashboards can react.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"State machine"
  (overdue is soft, never terminal; manual transitions clear
  `overdue_since`); `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` for
  APScheduler.
- Boundary: `app/workers/tasks/overdue.py`. Reads + writes
  `tasks.state, tasks.overdue_since` only.

## Implementation notes
- Entry: `detect_overdue(ctx: WorkspaceContext, *, now: datetime |
  None = None) -> OverdueReport`.
- Pulls every `task` row where:
  - `state IN ('scheduled', 'pending', 'in_progress')`
  - `due_by_utc IS NOT NULL`
  - `due_by_utc + tasks.overdue_grace_minutes < now`
  - `deleted_at IS NULL`
- `tasks.overdue_grace_minutes` is a workspace setting (default 15).
- For each match: set `state = 'overdue'`, `overdue_since = now`;
  emit `task.overdue` with `task_id`, `workspace_id`,
  `assigned_user_id`, `slipped_minutes`.
- Idempotent: a task already in `overdue` is skipped (no second
  event).
- APScheduler interval: 5 minutes (configurable via
  `tasks.overdue_tick_seconds`).
- Audit summary row per run: `tasks.overdue_tick` carrying the
  count and per-property breakdown.
- The completion / skip / cancel paths in `p5.completion` already
  clear `overdue_since` on any manual transition (per §06); make
  sure the reverse is also true here — manual edits made between
  ticks should not be re-overdued unless they slip again.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Tick is idempotent (re-running with no new slips emits no
      events)
- [ ] Soft state never overwrites a manual transition that
      happened between ticks
- [ ] `task.overdue` carries `slipped_minutes`
- [ ] Audit summary written per tick
- [ ] WorkspaceContext threaded through; multi-workspace runs
      iterate per tenant
- [ ] Unit + integration tests cover slip / clear / reslip cycles

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_overdue.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_tasks_overdue_tick.py -xvs
```

title: feat(tasks): task-scoped comments + agent thread
description:
## Problem / goal
Per-task notes are the agent inbox: every message is a row in
`task_comment` ordered by `created_at`, kind `user | agent | system`,
with `@mentions` resolving to workspace members. This service backs
the `Notes (chat)` panel on the worker task-detail page and the
manager's task drawer.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md`
  §"Task notes are the agent inbox", §"Comments";
  `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"comment".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/TaskDetailPage.tsx`
  uses `ChatLog` + `ChatComposer` at `/api/v1/tasks/{id}/chat/log`.
- Email fallback for offline `@mentions` lives in §10
  (out-of-scope here — emit an event the messaging service
  consumes).

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/comments.py`.
- DTOs: `CommentCreate` (markdown body, optional attachments as
  evidence-style `file_id`s), `CommentView` (kind, author, body,
  created_at, attachments, mentions).
- Functions:
  - `post_comment(ctx, task_id, payload, *, kind='user') ->
    CommentView`. `kind='agent'` requires that the caller is the
    embedded workspace agent (token-checked at the route layer);
    `kind='system'` is internal-only (called by the completion /
    assignment services to render state-change markers inline).
  - `list_comments(ctx, task_id, *, after: datetime | None)` —
    paginated, oldest-first.
  - `edit_comment(ctx, comment_id, body)` — only the author within
    a small grace window; never edits agent / system rows.
  - `delete_comment(ctx, comment_id)` — soft; author or
    workspace owner.
- `@mention` resolution: parse `@<slug>` patterns to user ids in
  the workspace; refuse mentions of users not in the workspace.
  Persist resolved ids in `mentioned_user_ids` for the messaging
  fanout.
- Attachments reuse the file storage from `p5.evidence` — keep
  the upload path identical (one file pipeline, two consumers).
- Emit `task.comment_added` on the bus (consumed by §10 for
  offline-mention email fallback). Audit `task_comment.create |
  edit | delete`.
- WorkspaceContext required; personal-task visibility (§06
  §"Self-created and personal tasks") gates read access — only
  the creator and workspace owners see comments on a personal task.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `kind='agent' | 'system'` paths gated by the right caller
- [ ] `@mentions` rejected for non-members
- [ ] Edit window enforced; agent/system rows never editable
- [ ] Attachments share the storage path with `p5.evidence`
- [ ] Personal-task visibility honoured on list/post
- [ ] Event + audit per mutation
- [ ] Unit tests cover each path

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_comments.py -xvs
```

title: feat(tasks): evidence upload (photo / voice / note / gps)
description:
## Problem / goal
A `task_evidence` row is how the system proves a task happened — a
photo, a voice note, a free-form note, or a checklist snapshot.
Files travel through the storage port (local disk by default,
S3-compatible later) and the policy cascade decides what is allowed.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Evidence",
  §"Evidence policy inheritance"; `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md`
  §"evidence", §"file"; `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` for
  the storage adapter contract.
- Storage port lives in `app/ports/storage.py` (Phase 0
  `p0.storage`). Service must depend on the port, never the
  filesystem directly.

## Implementation notes
- Module: `app/domain/tasks/evidence.py`.
- DTOs: `EvidenceUpload` (kind: `photo | voice | note |
  checklist_snapshot | gps`, `bytes`, `mime`, `text`,
  `gps_lat`, `gps_lon`), `EvidenceView`.
- Functions:
  - `upload_evidence(ctx, task_id, payload) -> EvidenceView` —
    resolves the §06 evidence cascade
    (`workspace → property → unit → work_engagement → task`,
    forbid-first then most-specific). When `forbid`, reject the
    upload with `EvidencePolicyError`. When `require` and no
    eligible kind attached, defer the rejection to the completion
    service (it may still be a partial save before completion).
  - `list_evidence(ctx, task_id)` — ordered by `created_at`.
  - `delete_evidence(ctx, evidence_id)` — soft; only the
    uploader, manager, or owner.
  - `snapshot_checklist(ctx, task_id)` — invoked by the completion
    service; writes one `task_evidence{kind=checklist_snapshot,
    checklist_snapshot_json=...}` row from the current
    `task_checklist_item` rows so reports survive later edits.
- File handling: stream to `storage.put(...)`, capture the
  returned `file_id`; store `task_evidence.file_id`. Compute and
  store `sha256` for dedup.
- Image rotation/EXIF stripping happens server-side for `photo`
  kind (privacy — see §15) before the file is handed to the port.
- GPS-kind rows must include both `gps_lat` and `gps_lon`; the
  service rejects partial coordinates.
- Audit `task_evidence.upload | delete | snapshot`; `task.evidence_added`
  on the bus.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Storage interactions go through the port (mockable in tests)
- [ ] Cascade resolution honoured (forbid first, then most-specific)
- [ ] EXIF strip happens before the put call
- [ ] Snapshot helper captures the live checklist state
- [ ] Audit + event per mutation
- [ ] Unit tests cover policy branches and dedup-by-sha256

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_evidence.py -xvs
```

title: feat(expenses/ocr): receipt OCR via LLM (default model) → autofill claim
description:
## Problem / goal
On receipt upload, run an OCR / extraction pass via the LLM port
(default model — full router lands in Phase 12) and autofill the
draft claim's `vendor_name`, `amount_cents`, `currency`, `spent_at`,
suggested `category`. Store raw output + confidence on the receipt
row.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Submission
  flow (worker)" (OCR autofill), §"LLM accuracy & guardrails";
  `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` (LLM port shape).
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/expenses/models.py`
  (`Receipt.ocr_json`, `Receipt.ocr_confidence`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx`
  shows the OCR loading state + accept/reject.

## Implementation notes
- Worker job `extract_receipt(ctx, receipt_id)`:
  - Loads blob from Storage.
  - Calls `llm.extract(prompt='receipt-extract', image=...)` using
    the **default extraction model** registered in Phase 0
    (`p0.llm`). The capability→model `ModelAssignment` lookup is
    Phase 12 (`p12.model-router`) — for now, read a fallback model
    id from config (`LLM_OCR_MODEL`).
  - Parses to a pydantic schema (`ReceiptExtraction`):
    `vendor`, `amount_cents`, `currency`, `spent_at`, `category`,
    `confidence` (0-1 per field).
  - Writes `receipt.ocr_json`, `receipt.ocr_confidence`.
  - If overall confidence > threshold AND claim is still in `draft`
    AND fields are empty → autofill claim fields. Otherwise leave
    fields untouched and emit a UI hint.
- Job is enqueued from `attach_receipt` (Phase 8 task
  `p8.expense.crud`) without blocking the HTTP request.
- Audit `receipt.ocr_completed` with token / cost / latency
  (records LLM usage row per `p1.llm`).
- PII safety: only the receipt image goes to the model — no other
  workspace data; respect `LLM_REDACT` workspace setting.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Job runs end-to-end against a stubbed LLM port
- [ ] Pydantic schema rejects malformed model output
- [ ] Autofill only happens on draft + empty + high confidence
- [ ] LLM usage row recorded
- [ ] No PII beyond the image leaves the process
- [ ] Failure modes (LLM timeout, parse error) leave the claim
      untouched and audit the failure

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_receipt_extraction.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_receipt_ocr_job.py -xvs
```

title: feat(expenses): manager approval inbox + diff view + reimbursement state
description:
## Problem / goal
Managers triage submitted claims: approve, reject (with reason), or
edit-then-approve (diff captured for audit). Approved claims walk
through `approved → reimbursed`; reimbursement records the channel
(cash, bank, card, other) and timestamp.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Approval
  (owner or manager)", §"Reimbursement".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/expenses/models.py`
  (`Expense.status`, `decided_*`, `reimbursed_via`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ExpensesApprovalsPage.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/expenses/approval.py`:
  - `approve_claim(ctx, expense_id, *, edits=None)` — applies edits
    inline; stores diff in audit row (`expense.approved` with
    `before/after` JSON); status → `approved`.
  - `reject_claim(ctx, expense_id, *, reason_md)` → `rejected`.
  - `mark_reimbursed(ctx, expense_id, *, via, paid_at=None)` →
    `reimbursed`; emits `expense.reimbursed`.
- Inbox query `list_pending(ctx, *, claimant?, property?,
  category?)` ordered by `submitted_at desc`.
- Capability `expense.approve` (Phase 4 enforcement).
- Notification on decision (Phase 10) — emit a domain event
  `expense.decided`; the messaging context turns it into a notif.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] State machine enforced (no skipping states)
- [ ] Edit-on-approve preserves before/after diff in audit
- [ ] Reject without reason rejected with 422
- [ ] Reimbursement updates ledger filters in CSV export
- [ ] Permission gate enforced
- [ ] Events emitted on every decision

title: feat(tasks): assignment service (primary + backups + candidate pool)
description:
## Problem / goal
Assigning a task is more than `task.assigned_user_id = ?`. §06
defines a five-step algorithm: walk the schedule's primary +
backups, then a candidate pool filtered by work-role + property
rota + availability stack, then load-balance with rotation
tiebreakers. Every path emits an event the manager can act on.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md`
  §"Availability precedence stack", §"Assignment algorithm",
  §"Pull-back logic for before_checkin tasks".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/SchedulerPage.tsx` shows the
  end result on a workspace timeline; `mocks/app/mock_data.py`
  for the `assignee` shape on occurrences.
- Boundary: `app/domain/tasks/assignment.py`. Reads from
  identity, places, and the availability tables; writes only to
  `tasks` (and an audit row).

## Implementation notes
- Entry: `assign_task(ctx, task_id, *, override_user_id: UUID |
  None = None) -> AssignmentResult`.
  - When `override_user_id` is set (manager UI), skip the algorithm
    and assign directly; emit `task.assigned` with
    `assignment_source = 'manual'`.
  - Else run the §06 algorithm:
    1. Walk `[default_assignee, *backup_assignee_user_ids]`; for
       each, run the availability precedence stack
       (`leave → override → public_holiday → weekly_pattern`) and
       the rota filter (`schedule_ruleset` covering weekday + local
       window, or generalist fallback). First available wins;
       record `assignment_source = 'primary' | 'backup[N]'`.
    2. Else build a candidate pool: users with the right
       `user_work_role`, scoped via
       `property_work_role_assignment` (or generalist), passing
       the same availability + rota filters; exclude users already
       tried in step 1.
    3. Tiebreaker: fewest tasks at this property in the 7-day
       window around `scheduled_for_local`; then rotation by oldest
       last-task-here.
  - When zero candidates: leave `assigned_user_id = NULL`, emit
    `task.primary_unavailable` (step-1 attempted) **or**
    `task.unassigned` (pool was empty from the start).
- `reassign_task(ctx, task_id, new_user_id)` — explicit move; same
  validation; emits `task.reassigned` with the previous user.
- `unassign_task(ctx, task_id, *, reason)` — emits `task.unassigned`.
- `WorkspaceContext` everywhere; one audit row per mutation
  carrying the `assignment_source` and the candidate pool size.
- All event payloads carry `task_id`, `workspace_id`,
  `assigned_user_id`, `assignment_source`, `candidate_count`.
- Helper `availability_for(user, date_local, property_id)` reused
  by the scheduler UI (kept here so there is one source of truth).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Primary-then-backup ordering verified by unit tests
- [ ] Candidate pool excludes users already tried via primary/backup
- [ ] Rota filter respected (and generalist fallback works)
- [ ] Tiebreakers (fewest-tasks, rotation) deterministic in tests
- [ ] Three event variants emitted in the right cases
- [ ] Audit row carries `assignment_source` and pool size
- [ ] Unit tests for each branch (primary, each backup, pool,
      empty)

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_assignment.py -xvs
```

title: Self-review: feat(tasks): assignment service (primary + backups + candidate pool)
description:

title: feat(expenses): claim CRUD (draft / submit) with Storage attachments
description:
## Problem / goal
Workers create expense claims (vendor, amount, currency, category,
date, optional property, description) in `draft`, attach receipt
images via Storage, then `submit` for manager approval.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Expense
  claims" §"Submission flow (worker)", §"Model".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/expenses/models.py`
  (`Expense`, `Receipt`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MyExpensesPage.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/expenses/claims.py`:
  - `create_claim(ctx, **fields)` → `draft`.
  - `update_claim(ctx, expense_id, **fields)` — only while `draft`.
  - `attach_receipt(ctx, expense_id, blob_hash)` — references an
    already-uploaded blob; up to 10 receipts per claim.
  - `submit_claim(ctx, expense_id)` → `submitted`; emits
    `expense.submitted` event.
  - `cancel_claim(ctx, expense_id)` — owner only, only while `draft`
    or `submitted` (not after `approved`).
- Currency in ISO 4217; amount stored as integer cents.
- REST under `app/api/expenses/claims.py`.
- Capabilities `expense.claim_self`.
- Audit on every transition.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Worker can CRUD their own draft claims; not other users'
- [ ] Submitted claims become read-only
- [ ] Receipt attachments validated (image/* mime, < 10 MB)
- [ ] Event emitted on submit
- [ ] OpenAPI regenerated

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_expense_claims.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_expense_claims_api.py -xvs
```

title: feat(payroll/exports): CSV export — timesheets, payslips, expense ledger
description:
## Problem / goal
Accountants want flat CSVs they can drop into their bookkeeping
software. Three exports for a date window: shifts (timesheets),
payslips, expense ledger.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Reports
  and exports".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PayPage.tsx` (export
  dropdown).

## Implementation notes
- Module `app/domain/payroll/exports.py`:
  - `export_timesheets_csv(ctx, *, since, until)` — columns:
    `shift_id, user_email, property_label, starts_at_utc, ends_at_utc,
     hours_decimal, source, notes`.
  - `export_payslips_csv(ctx, *, period_id)` — columns:
    `payslip_id, user_email, period_starts_at, period_ends_at,
     hours, overtime_hours, gross_cents, deductions_cents,
     net_cents, currency, paid_at`.
  - `export_expense_ledger_csv(ctx, *, since, until,
    status_filter='approved')` — columns:
    `expense_id, claimant_email, vendor, spent_at, category,
     amount_cents, currency, property_label, decided_at,
     reimbursed_via`.
- All exports stream (`StreamingResponse`) to avoid memory blow-up.
- REST: `GET /api/payroll/exports/{kind}.csv?...`.
- Capability `payroll.export`.
- Audit each export with row count.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Three CSV endpoints stream correctly
- [ ] Header row present; UTF-8 BOM optional via query flag
- [ ] Empty windows return only the header
- [ ] Audit row records `since`, `until`, `kind`, `row_count`

title: feat(payroll/payslip): PDF generation (weasyprint) + Storage put
description:
## Problem / goal
After a payslip is computed, render a PDF (legal payslip layout) and
store it via the Storage port. The blob hash is recorded on the
payslip row for retrieval.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"PDF",
  §"Distribution".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/payroll/models.py`
  (`Payslip.pdf_blob_hash`).
- **Storage port**: from Phase 0 (`p0.storage`).

## Implementation notes
- Add `weasyprint` to runtime deps (use `uv add`); document the
  system fonts/cairo dependency in the Dockerfile (already
  installed for the FastAPI image — verify).
- Template `app/adapters/pdf/templates/payslip.html` (Jinja2);
  workspace-tunable header (logo, registered name, address) read
  from workspace settings.
- Service `app/domain/payroll/pdf.py::render_payslip(ctx, payslip_id)`:
  - Loads payslip + components + workspace + user.
  - Renders PDF bytes.
  - `storage.put(...)` returns blob hash → updates
    `payslip.pdf_blob_hash`.
  - Idempotent: if hash already set, return without re-rendering
    unless `force=True`.
- Capability check: only managers + the recipient may fetch the PDF.
- Audit `payslip.pdf_rendered`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] PDF renders for the §09 canonical test fixture
- [ ] Hash stored on payslip row; storage object retrievable
- [ ] Idempotent regeneration
- [ ] Permission denied for unrelated users
- [ ] Worker can fetch own payslip PDF

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_payslip_pdf.py -xvs
# manual smoke: open the PDF and check layout
```

title: feat(tasks): checklist-template editor + RRULE-filtered items
description:
## Problem / goal
A template's `checklist_template_json` is a JSON array of items, each
with an optional RFC 5545 RRULE that controls whether the item appears
on a given materialised task. The template service needs a typed
editor for these items so the manager UI and the generator both speak
the same shape.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Checklist
  template shape", §"Checklist items" (seeding rules);
  `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"checklist_item".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx` renders
  `tpl.checklist[]` with `label` + optional `guest_visible`;
  `mocks/web/src/components/NewTaskModal.tsx` previews items.

## Implementation notes
- Pydantic model `ChecklistTemplateItem` with fields:
  - `key: str` — stable per-template slug, unique within the template.
    Validate `^[a-z0-9_]{1,64}$` and uniqueness across the array.
  - `text: str`
  - `required: bool` (default `False`)
  - `guest_visible: bool` (default `False`)
  - `rrule: str | None` — optional RFC 5545; validated by parsing
    with `dateutil.rrule.rrulestr` (round-tripped for normalisation).
  - `dtstart_local: date | None` — anchor for the RRULE. Resolution
    rule lives in §06; the template editor accepts an optional date
    only.
- Service helpers in `app/domain/tasks/templates.py`:
  - `validate_checklist_template(items)` — runs at every
    `TaskTemplate{Create,Update}`.
  - `expand_checklist_for_task(items, *, scheduled_for_local,
    is_ad_hoc)` — returns the subset of items to insert as
    `task_checklist_item` rows (used by the generator and stay
    bundle worker; ad-hoc tasks always include every item).
  - `reorder_checklist(items, ordered_keys)` — preserves item
    identity by `key` while updating `ordinal`.
- The `key` is what `asset_action.checklist_item_key` (§21) and
  reports use to track an item across template revisions.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Invalid RRULEs rejected at write time (typed
      `ChecklistRRULEError`, payload includes the bad string)
- [ ] Duplicate or empty `key` rejected
- [ ] `expand_checklist_for_task` filters by RRULE when
      `is_ad_hoc=False`, includes all items when `True`
- [ ] Reorder preserves keys + completion state on the existing rows
- [ ] Unit tests cover RRULE filter on a representative date set
      (monthly, every-Nth-week, every-6-months)

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tasks_checklist_template.py -xvs
```

title: feat(payroll/pay-period): CRUD + draft → locked → paid state machine
description:
## Problem / goal
Pay periods are the windows over which payslips are computed. They
walk a strict state machine: `draft → locked → paid`. Locking
freezes the inputs; paid means money was disbursed.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Pay period",
  §"Period close", §"Transition to paid".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/payroll/models.py`
  (`PayPeriod`).

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/payroll/periods.py`:
  - `create_period(ctx, *, starts_at, ends_at)` — refuses overlap
    with another non-draft period.
  - `lock_period(ctx, period_id)` — only from `draft`. Captures
    `locked_at`, `locked_by`. Triggers payslip recompute (Phase 8
    task `p8.payslip.compute`) for every active engagement.
  - `mark_paid(ctx, period_id)` — only from `locked`. All payslips
    must have a `paid_at` timestamp recorded externally (or be
    cancelled).
  - `reopen_period(ctx, period_id)` — locked → draft only; rejected
    if any payslip already paid.
- Capability `payroll.manage_periods`.
- Emit events `pay_period.locked`, `pay_period.paid` for digests.
- All transitions audit.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Overlap detection works
- [ ] State transitions enforced; bad transitions return 409
- [ ] Locking triggers payslip recompute (smoke)
- [ ] Paid state irreversible without reopen
- [ ] Audit + events emitted

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_pay_periods.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_pay_periods_api.py -xvs
```

title: feat(payroll/pay-rules): CRUD + effective windows + per-user currency
description:
## Problem / goal
A `PayRule` defines a worker's hourly base + premium multipliers
(overtime, night, weekend) over an effective window. Multiple rules
per user are allowed; selection by date is left to the payslip
compute step.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Pay rules",
  §"Pay-rule selection when multiple rules overlap".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/payroll/models.py`
  (`PayRule`).

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/payroll/rules.py`:
  - `create_rule(ctx, user_id, *, currency, base_cents_per_hour,
    multipliers, effective_from, effective_to=None)`.
  - `list_rules(ctx, *, user_id)` ordered by `effective_from desc`.
  - `update_rule(ctx, rule_id, **fields)` — refuses changes after
    a payslip in `paid` state has consumed the rule.
  - `delete_rule(ctx, rule_id)` — soft delete (set `effective_to`
    to now).
- Validation:
  - Multipliers in `[1.0, 5.0]`.
  - `effective_to > effective_from` when set.
  - Currency in ISO 4217.
- REST under `app/api/payroll/rules.py`.
- Capability `payroll.manage_rules` (manager / owner only).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD endpoints + audit rows
- [ ] Validators reject invalid multipliers / currency / window
- [ ] Locked-period rules immutable
- [ ] OpenAPI regenerated

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_pay_rules.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_pay_rules_api.py -xvs
```

title: feat(leave): leave-request CRUD with state machine
description:
## Problem / goal
Workers need to request time off; managers need to approve/reject;
either side may cancel before the start date. The state machine is
`pending → approved | rejected | cancelled`, with no back-edges except
explicit reopen.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` (worker self-service),
  `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Leave"; `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md`
  §"leave"; `docs/specs/12-api.md` §"/leaves".
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeeLeavesPage.tsx`
  (worker view of own leaves), `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/LeavesInboxPage.tsx`
  (manager queue).
- DB tables in `p1.time` (Leave model already exists).

## Implementation notes
- New service `app/services/leave/service.py` taking
  `WorkspaceContext`; methods `create`, `cancel_own`, `update_dates`
  (only while pending), `list_for_user`, `list_for_workspace`.
- `create` defaults `status = pending`, `requester_user_id = ctx.user_id`.
- `cancel_own` valid in `pending` or `approved` (latter requires
  the start date to be in the future); flips to `cancelled`.
- Audit every transition (old → new status, actor, reason).
- Validation: `starts_at < ends_at`; both align to whole days in the
  worker's timezone (use the tz utility from `p0.util`).
- Approval / rejection logic stays in `p4.leave.approval` — this task
  ships the CRUD, the cancel-own path, and the state-machine guards.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Workers can `POST /me/leaves` and see their own leaves only.
- [ ] Managers can `GET /workspaces/{ws}/leaves?status=pending`.
- [ ] State transitions enforced: `pending → cancelled` allowed by
      requester or manager; non-pending transitions in this task
      reject with 409.
- [ ] Updating dates while `pending` rewrites starts_at/ends_at and
      audits old/new values.
- [ ] Audit rows for create / update / cancel.
- [ ] Unit tests cover happy path + each illegal transition.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_service_leave.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_api_leave.py -xvs
```

title: feat(employees): employees service + workspace-scoped CRUD
description:
## Problem / goal
Owners and managers need to add people to the workspace, edit their
profile, archive them, and reinstate them. v1 collapses "employee"
into `users` + per-workspace `work_engagement` (see §05 "User (as
worker)"); the service must keep those rows consistent across the
lifecycle.

**Scope narrowed (2026-04-24)**: this task is now *only* the
EmployeeService + the four workspace-scoped routes listed below.
Avatar upload was moved to cd-6vq5 (bare-host `/api/v1/me/avatar`,
which §05 explicitly says is self-only and therefore must not live
under `/w/<slug>/`). The DB prerequisites `work_role` /
`user_work_role` (cd-5kv4) and `work_engagement` (cd-4saj) are now
hard blockers — this task cannot start until both land.

## Routes in scope
- `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/users/invite` — extend the existing invite
  route (see "Reused invite route" below).
- `PATCH /w/<slug>/api/v1/users/{id}` — partial update of profile
  fields the caller is permitted to change.
- `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/users/{id}/archive` — soft-archive.
- `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/users/{id}/reinstate` — reverse archive.

Explicitly OUT of scope (moved elsewhere):
- `POST /me/avatar`, `DELETE /me/avatar` → **cd-6vq5**.

## Reused invite route — IMPORTANT
The `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/users/invite` endpoint already exists at
`app/api/v1/users.py:224-276`, backed by
`app/domain/identity/membership.invite`. This task REUSES that
route rather than adding a new one.

Extension: Task D extends the accept-side path (the
`_activate_invite` helper) to **pre-seed a pending
`work_engagement` row at accept time, not at invite time**. This
preserves §03's passkey-gated accept-time security model — nothing
workspace-scoped is seeded until the invitee completes the passkey
challenge. Do NOT move the seeding to the invite-creation path.

See also cd-1hd0 / cd-4o61 (feat(identity/invite) — work_engagement
+ user_work_role invite sub-payloads) for the broader invite
payload shape; this task can leave those downstream tickets alone
and only seed a minimal pending engagement row.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` §"User (as
  worker)", §"Work engagement", §"Archive / reinstate";
  `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`users`, §`user_workspace`,
  §`work_engagement`, §`role_grants`;
  `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Invite + magic link";
  `docs/specs/12-api.md` §`/users`.
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeesPage.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/EmployeeDetailPage.tsx`.
- **New service**: `app/services/employees/service.py` taking
  `WorkspaceContext`; expose `invite`, `update_profile`, `archive`,
  `reinstate`. (No `set_avatar` / `clear_avatar` — that's cd-6vq5.)

## Implementation notes
- Invite flow continues to issue a magic-link token via the
  existing auth helper; only the accept-side `_activate_invite`
  path gains the pending `work_engagement` seed.
- Archive is soft (`users.archived_at`) AND archives the
  `work_engagement` for this workspace (set `archived_on`) AND
  every `user_work_role` in this workspace. Idempotent.
- Reinstate reverses archive (nulls `archived_on`, clears
  `users.archived_at` only if the caller is a deployment-level
  owner and the user has no other active engagements — default to
  the workspace-only reinstate path unless spec says otherwise).
- Every mutation writes an `audit` row via `app/adapters/audit`.
- All reads/writes pass through the tenancy filter; no raw
  `select(User)` without `WorkspaceContext`.

## Depends on
- **cd-5kv4** — `work_role` + `user_work_role` tables must exist.
- **cd-4saj** — `work_engagement` table must exist before accept-time
  seeding is possible.
- cd-6vq5 (avatar router) is NOT a blocker — it lands in parallel.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/users/invite` creates a `users` row
      (or reuses existing one) and a `user_workspace` row. Pending
      `work_engagement` is created at **accept time** inside
      `_activate_invite`, NOT at invite time.
- [ ] `PATCH /w/<slug>/api/v1/users/{id}` updates only fields the
      caller is permitted to (`users.edit_profile_other` for
      managers; identity-scoped self-edit otherwise).
- [ ] `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/users/{id}/archive` archives engagement
      + every `user_work_role` in this workspace; idempotent.
- [ ] `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/users/{id}/reinstate` reverses archive;
      idempotent.
- [ ] Audit rows created for every mutation.
- [ ] Unit tests cover happy path + idempotent archive + accept-time
      engagement seeding (nothing seeded at invite-create time).

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/services/test_service_employees.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_api_employees.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/identity/test_invite_accept.py -xvs
```

title: feat(auth/tokens): delegated agent tokens (delegate_for_user_id, short TTL)
description:
## Problem / goal
Agents (§11) act on behalf of a human user. A delegated token inherits
the user's `role_grants` rather than carrying explicit scopes; lifetime
is short by default; revocation is fast.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Delegated tokens",
  §"Per-user agent approval mode" (cross-link to §11).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Delegated authority",
  §"Approval modes".
- Phase 1 table: `agent_token` (delegating_user_id, label, prefix,
  hash, scope_json, expires_at, revoked_at, last_used_at).

## Implementation notes
- Endpoint `POST /api/v1/auth/tokens` (or
  `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/tokens` with `{delegate: true}`) reuses
  the API-token mint path but:
  - Forces `scopes = []` (delegated tokens carry no static scopes).
  - Sets `delegate_for_user_id` from the **session user** — never
    from the request body.
  - Refuses creation when the calling identity is a token (no
    transitive delegation).
  - Default TTL: 30 days, capped by workspace setting.
- Auth middleware: when a delegated token presents, resolve the
  delegating user's `role_grants` for the requested scope and apply
  approval-mode gating (§11). Audit `actor_kind="user"`,
  `actor_id=delegate_for_user_id`, `agent_label=token.label`,
  optional `agent_conversation_ref` from the request header.
- 401 with clear `error="user_archived"` when the delegating user is
  archived or has lost every grant.
- Listing + revoke share the API-token surface; the UI distinguishes
  delegated rows by the `delegate_for_user_id` column.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Delegated token cannot be minted from another token (passkey
      session only)
- [ ] `scopes` always empty on delegated rows
- [ ] Authority follows the user — granting a new role to the user
      affects every live delegated token immediately
- [ ] Approval-mode hooks fire on every mutation
- [ ] Archive of delegating user → 401 on next request
- [ ] Unit + integration tests, both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_agent_token.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_agent_token_authority.py -xvs
```
Manual: mint a delegated token via the dev UI, drive a write through
it, confirm `/approvals` pauses for an annotated route.

title: feat(auth/recovery): passkey self-service recovery via magic link
description:
## Problem / goal
A user who lost their passkey-bearing device must be able to recover
without a manager. Email + magic link → enrol a new passkey → revoke
old credentials. Audit must show the chain end-to-end.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Self-service lost-
  device recovery", §"Recovery paths".
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Self-service lost-
  device & email-change abuse mitigations".
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/recover/*`.
- Reuses `p2.magic-link` and `p2.passkey.register`.

## Implementation notes
- Endpoints (all bare host, `tenant_agnostic()`):
  - `POST /recover/passkey/request` `{email}` → issues a magic link
    of `purpose=recover_passkey`. Per §15 the email is sent even if
    the address is unknown (no enumeration); audit records what
    actually happened.
  - `GET /recover/passkey/verify?token=…` → consumes the link and
    returns a recovery-scoped session sufficient to enrol a new
    passkey, **not** to make any other workspace mutation.
  - `POST /recover/passkey/finish` → registers a new passkey via the
    bootstrap path, then revokes **all** existing
    `passkey_credential` rows for the user and revokes every
    `sessions` row except the current recovery session, in one tx.
- Throttle by `email_hash` and `ip_hash` (§15 abuse mitigations);
  reuse `app.auth.signup_abuse.check_rate` (rename to neutral name
  if needed).
- Audit: `audit.recovery.requested`, `…verified`, `…completed`,
  with `actor_kind="user"` (or `"system"` for the request step when
  the email is unknown).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Unknown-email request still returns 202; audit row distinguishes
      hit vs miss
- [ ] Successful recovery revokes all old passkeys + sessions for the
      user (single tx)
- [ ] Recovery-scoped session cannot perform any non-recovery action
- [ ] Rate limits enforced (per IP + per email)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests on both DBs

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_recovery.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_recovery_full_flow.py -xvs
```
Manual: simulate device loss in dev — drive `/recover` end to end with
Playwright + a new virtual authenticator.
close_reason: Landed with autofix fixes from cd-veq. Spec-compliant per §03 / §15; step-up, kill-switch, router wiring tracked separately.

title: feat(domain/stays): turnover task auto-generation from reservation events
description:
## Problem / goal
Subscribe to \`reservation.upserted\` events and auto-generate one-off
turnover \`occurrence\` rows in the gap between check-out and the next
check-in on the same unit, respecting \`property_closure\` (no
turnover on closed dates) and per-unit lifecycle rules.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Stay task
  bundles" (after_checkout / before_checkin / during_stay), §"Airbnb-
  style edge cases" (same-day turnovers, owner stays).
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"reservation",
  §"occurrence", §"property_closure".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/StaysPage.tsx\` (turnover
  badges), \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (TURNOVER_BUNDLES).

## Implementation notes
- **Cross-context coupling**: Tasks-context service lands in Phase 5
  (\`p5.tasks.*\`). For now expose a thin port
  \`app/ports/tasks_create_occurrence.py\` that the stays job calls;
  ship a no-op test double until Phase 5, but make the contract
  precise.
- \`app/domain/stays/turnover_generator.py\` subscribes to
  \`reservation.upserted\` via the Phase 0 event bus.
- Resolve next stay on same \`unit_id\` ordered by \`check_in_at\`;
  compute the gap window.
- Skip if the gap intersects any \`property_closure\` (call into
  \`closure_service.list_closures\` over the gap).
- Skip if \`guest_kind\` is filtered out by the rule's
  \`guest_kind_filter\` (default rules exclude \`owner\`).
- Same-day turnover handling per spec: time-box the bundle to the gap.
- Idempotency key: \`(reservation_id, rule_id)\` so re-fired events
  don't create duplicates; use the spec's edit-in-place rule when the
  shift is < 4h.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] One occurrence per (reservation, rule); re-firing is a no-op
- [ ] Closures suppress turnover generation
- [ ] Owner-kind stays don't fire default rules
- [ ] < 4h reschedule patches in place; >= 4h regenerates
- [ ] Cross-context coupling documented and gated by the port
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests cover happy + closure + owner + reschedule paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/stays/test_turnover_generator.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/stays/test_turnover_e2e.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(domain/stays): poll_ical APScheduler job — fetch, parse, dedupe, upsert
description:
## Problem / goal
Recurring background job that polls every enabled \`ical_feed\`,
parses VEVENTs with \`icalendar\`, dedupes by \`(feed_id, external_uid)\`,
upserts \`reservation\` rows, and converts "Blocked"/"Not available"
VEVENTs to \`property_closure\` rows. Publishes
\`reservation.upserted\` per change for downstream turnover gen.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"iCal feed" →
  "Polling behavior" (covers \`If-None-Match\`/\`If-Modified-Since\`,
  Blocked detection, rate limiting, parse-error issues).
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"reservation",
  §"ical_feed", §"property_closure".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/StaysPage.tsx\` (rendered
  feed-driven stays).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/jobs/poll_ical.py\` — APScheduler job; honours
  \`ical_feed.poll_cadence\` (default \`*/15 * * * *\`).
- Fetch via the validated SSRF-guarded HTTP client (re-using the
  validator from \`p3.ical.registration\`); pin resolved IP between
  resolve and connect.
- Parse with \`icalendar\`. Dedupe key in DB:
  \`UNIQUE(ical_feed_id, external_uid)\`; use \`INSERT … ON CONFLICT
  DO UPDATE\` (Postgres) / \`INSERT OR REPLACE\` (SQLite, portable
  via SQLAlchemy upsert).
- Convert VEVENTs whose \`SUMMARY\` matches the spec patterns
  ("Not available", "Blocked", "Airbnb (Not available)") to
  \`property_closure\` with \`reason = ical_unavailable\` instead.
- Publish \`reservation.upserted\` with \`{reservation_id,
  feed_id, change_kind: created|updated|cancelled}\`; publish
  \`property.closure.created\` when a Blocked VEVENT yields a closure.
- Surface parse failures as an \`issue\` row against the feed; record
  \`last_error\` on the feed.
- Per-host rate limit; respect \`Retry-After\` on 429.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Re-poll is idempotent (same VEVENT → no duplicate row, no event)
- [ ] Cancelled VEVENT marks \`reservation.status = cancelled\`
- [ ] Blocked VEVENTs become closures, not reservations
- [ ] Events published per change with the documented payload shape
- [ ] Parse error increments \`last_error\`, doesn't poison the loop
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests against captured Airbnb/VRBO/Booking/Google fixtures

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/jobs/test_poll_ical.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/stays/test_ical_poll_idempotent.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(domain/stays): ical_feed registration + URL validation + provider auto-detect
description:
## Problem / goal
Register an iCal feed against a property/unit. Validate the URL
synchronously (scheme, SSRF host resolution, reachability probe),
detect the provider from the URL host pattern, and persist with the
feed disabled until the first probe succeeds.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"iCal feed"
  (fields, polling, **SSRF guard**, supported providers).
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"ical_feed".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\`
  (Stays/Calendar feeds tab), \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (ICAL_FEEDS).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/stays/ical_service.py\` —
  \`register_feed\`, \`update_feed\`, \`disable_feed\`,
  \`delete_feed\`, \`list_feeds\`, \`probe_feed\`.
- URL validation port: \`app/ports/ical_validator.py\` with an HTTPS
  scheme check, IP-resolution check (reject loopback / RFC 1918 /
  link-local / multicast), 2 MB / 10 s probe with same-origin
  redirects only. Implementation (\`adapters/ical/validator.py\`)
  reuses the SSRF helper used elsewhere.
- Provider auto-detect from host: \`*.airbnb.com\` → airbnb,
  \`*.vrbo.com\` / expedia → vrbo, \`*.booking.com\` → booking,
  \`*.calendar.google.com\` → gcal, else generic.
- URL stored envelope-encrypted (per spec §15) — depend on the
  storage adapter for that.
- \`probe_feed\` re-runs validation on demand and updates
  \`last_polled_at\` / \`last_error\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] HTTPS-only enforced at registration AND at probe
- [ ] Private/loopback IPs rejected at both validation and fetch
- [ ] Provider auto-detected; manual override allowed
- [ ] URL persisted envelope-encrypted (no plaintext at rest)
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Unit tests for each provider + each SSRF rejection path

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/stays/test_ical_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/unit/adapters/ical/test_validator.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/stays/test_ical_registration.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(domain/places): property↔workspace junction — transfer, share, revoke
description:
## Problem / goal
Manage the multi-belonging junction (\`property_workspace\`): invite a
second workspace, change membership_role, revoke access, transfer
\`owner_workspace\`. The owner workspace is the only party allowed to
grant/revoke; managed/observer rows are operational/read-only.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Multi-belonging
  (sharing across workspaces)" — covers role semantics, agency
  switching flow, PII boundary.
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"property_workspace".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\`
  ("Sharing & client" tab).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/places/membership_service.py\` —
  \`invite_workspace\`, \`accept_invite\`, \`revoke_workspace\`,
  \`update_membership_role\`, \`transfer_ownership\`,
  \`list_memberships\`.
- Authorization: only members of the \`owner_workspace\` row may
  invite/revoke/transfer; managed/observer can list themselves out
  but cannot mutate.
- Enforce invariant: exactly one \`owner_workspace\` row per property
  (transfer is two-step in one TX: demote old to observer or revoke,
  promote new).
- Surface \`share_guest_identity\` toggle on managed/observer rows
  (PII boundary, §15).
- Audit every mutation; emits \`property_workspace.invited|accepted|
  revoked|role_changed|ownership_transferred\` log lines.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Only owner_workspace members can invite/revoke
- [ ] Exactly one owner_workspace per property invariant holds
- [ ] Revoke cascades visibility (managed workspace stops seeing it)
- [ ] \`share_guest_identity\` defaults false; toggling audits
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests cover all roles + denial paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/places/test_membership_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/places/test_membership_flow.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(domain/places): area service — CRUD scoped to a property with ordering
description:
## Problem / goal
Area CRUD with stable display ordering, a one-level-deep nesting
invariant, and the auto-seed flow for \`kind = str\` properties (Entry,
Kitchen, Living, Bedroom 1, Bathroom 1, Outdoor, Trash & Laundry per
unit).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Area",
  §"Auto-seeded areas".
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"area".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\`
  (Areas tab), \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (AREAS list).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/places/area_service.py\` — \`create_area\`,
  \`update_area\`, \`delete_area\`, \`list_areas\`, \`reorder_areas\`,
  \`move_area\`.
- Reorder is bulk: takes \`(area_id, order_hint)\` pairs and writes in
  one transaction; emits a single audit row "reordered N areas".
- Reject moves that would create depth > 1 with \`AreaNestingTooDeep\`.
- Hook into \`property_service.create_property\` and
  \`unit_service.create_unit\` so newly-created \`str\` units get the
  seeded set; respect the table in §"`kind` semantics".
- Workspace-scoped reads via the property join.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Reorder is atomic and emits one audit row
- [ ] Nesting > 1 level is rejected
- [ ] STR property creation auto-seeds the spec area set per unit

title: feat(domain/places): unit service — CRUD scoped to a property
description:
## Problem / goal
Unit CRUD with the spec invariant that every property always has at
least one unit; single-unit properties auto-create a default unit at
property creation time and the UI hides the unit layer.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Unit",
  §"Invariants", §"Welcome overrides merge".
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"unit".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\`
  (Units tab), \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (UNITS list).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/places/unit_service.py\` — \`create_unit\`,
  \`update_unit\`, \`soft_delete_unit\`, \`list_units\`,
  \`get_unit\`.
- Hook into \`property_service.create_property\` to create the default
  unit (\`name = property.name\`, \`ordinal = 0\`).
- Enforce \`UNIQUE(workspace_id, property_id, name)\` at the service
  layer with a clear \`UnitNameTaken\` error before the DB integrity
  error fires.
- Refuse to delete the last remaining unit of a property
  (\`LastUnitProtected\`).
- Audit every mutation; \`WorkspaceContext\` required.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Property creation auto-creates one default unit
- [ ] Cannot delete the last unit (returns \`LastUnitProtected\`)
- [ ] Duplicate unit name in same property is rejected
- [ ] Cross-workspace access is denied
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Unit + integration tests pass

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/places/test_unit_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/places/test_unit_crud.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(domain/places): property service — create, update, soft-delete, list
description:
## Problem / goal
First-class CRUD for properties with workspace-scoped listing,
soft-delete, and the multi-belonging bootstrap that creates the initial
\`property_workspace\` row (with \`membership_role = owner_workspace\`
and a label) so the new property is visible to its creating workspace
from the first commit.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Property",
  §"`address_json` canonical shape", §"Multi-belonging
  (sharing across workspaces)".
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"property",
  §"property_workspace".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertiesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (PROPERTIES list).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/places/property_service.py\` — pure use cases
  (\`create_property\`, \`update_property\`, \`soft_delete_property\`,
  \`list_properties\`, \`get_property\`).
- Public surface \`app/services/places/__init__.py\` re-exports the use
  cases as the only allowed import path from other contexts.
- Every method takes a \`WorkspaceContext\`; reads filter through the
  Phase 0 tenancy filter on the \`property_workspace\` join.
- On create: insert \`Property\` then \`PropertyWorkspace\` row with
  \`membership_role = "owner_workspace"\` + caller-supplied label
  (defaults to \`property.name\`); both writes inside one transaction.
- On write: copy \`address_json.country\` → \`property.country\`
  back-fill rule per spec.
- Audit every mutation via the Phase 0 audit port.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Create yields one \`property\` + one \`property_workspace\` row
- [ ] Update is workspace-scoped; cross-workspace update is denied
- [ ] Soft-delete sets \`deleted_at\`, hides from default list, audited
- [ ] List filters by workspace via \`property_workspace\` join
- [ ] Country back-fill works in both directions
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes; no \`Any\`
- [ ] Unit tests cover happy + cross-tenant denial paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/places/test_property_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/places/test_property_crud.py -xvs
\`\`\`
close_reason: Shipped: property service with CRUD + multi-belonging bootstrap + address/country back-fill + audit on every mutation; tenancy-scoped; unit + integration green on SQLite (Postgres via CREWDAY_TEST_DB).

title: feat(db/llm): model_assignment + agent_token + approval_request + llm_usage + budget_ledger
description:
## Problem / goal
Tables supporting the LLM/agent layer — capability→model map, agent
tokens (delegated), human-in-the-loop approvals, per-call usage, and
budget ledger.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"model_assignment",
  §"agent_token", §"approval_request", §"llm_usage",
  §"budget_ledger", \`docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/db/llm/models.py\`:
  - \`ModelAssignment\`: id, workspace_id, capability
    (\`receipt_ocr|nl_task_intake|daily_digest|staff_chat|…\`),
    model_id, provider, created_at.
  - \`AgentToken\`: id, workspace_id, delegating_user_id,
    label, prefix, hash, scope_json, expires_at, created_at,
    revoked_at, last_used_at.
  - \`ApprovalRequest\`: id, workspace_id, requester_actor_id,
    action_json, status (\`pending|approved|rejected|timed_out\`),
    decided_by, decided_at, rationale_md.
  - \`LlmUsage\`: id, workspace_id, capability, model_id, tokens_in,
    tokens_out, cost_cents, latency_ms, status, correlation_id,
    created_at.
  - \`BudgetLedger\`: id, workspace_id, period_start, period_end,
    spent_cents, cap_cents, updated_at.
- Alembic migration \`0017_llm\`.
- Register in tenancy registry.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`ApprovalRequest.action_json\` is a JSON blob (pydantic-
      validated at the service layer)
- [ ] Agent tokens hashed with sha256; prefix for listing
- [ ] Unit tests cover CRUD

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_llm.py -xvs
\`\`\`
close_reason: Shipped: ModelAssignment, AgentToken, ApprovalRequest, LlmUsage, BudgetLedger tables + alembic migration e5f6a7b8c9d0. All tenancy-scoped; portable CHECK on approval_request.status ({pending,approved,rejected,timed_out}) and llm_usage.status ({ok,error,refused,timeout}); unique index per workspace on (capability), (period_start, period_end); prefix composite index for token listing; FK rules per spec (workspace CASCADE, user SET NULL to preserve audit trail, model_id soft reference awaiting llm_model registry).
