title: chore(deploy): Dockerfile single + docker-compose dev/prod profiles
description:
## Problem / goal
Make the app runnable as a single SQLite container and as a compose
stack (Postgres + Caddy + MinIO) from day 1. No code depends on
deployment mode — same image everywhere.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md\`
  §"Single-container", §"Compose", \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\`
  §"Environments".
- Mocks compose at \`mocks/docker-compose.yml\` is the reference for
  port layout / Caddy config.

## Implementation notes
- \`deploy/single/Dockerfile\`: multi-stage — stage 1 builds
  \`app/web/dist\` (Node 22); stage 2 \`python:3.12-slim\` copying
  \`app/\`, \`cli/\`, \`dist/\`, \`migrations/\`. Entrypoint runs
  \`alembic upgrade head\` then \`uvicorn app.main:create_app
  --factory\`.
- \`deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml\`: services \`web\`, \`worker\`
  (external scheduler), \`postgres\`, \`caddy\`, \`minio\`, \`mailhog\`
  (dev-only profile).
- \`deploy/compose/Caddyfile\` terminates TLS, proxies to web,
  loopback-binds the admin surface.
- Healthcheck: curl \`/healthz\`.
- Web only binds on \`127.0.0.1\` by default; compose exposes via
  Caddy.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`docker build -f deploy/single/Dockerfile -t crewday:test .\`
      succeeds
- [ ] \`docker run crewday:test\` starts and \`curl localhost:8000/healthz\`
      returns 200
- [ ] \`docker compose -f deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml up\`
      brings the stack up
- [ ] Caddy serves over HTTPS in the staging profile

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
docker build -f deploy/single/Dockerfile -t crewday:test .
docker run --rm -p 8000:8000 crewday:test &
sleep 5
curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8000/healthz
docker compose -f deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml up -d
\`\`\`

title: feat(adapters/llm): OpenRouter client
description:
## Problem / goal
OCR + NL task intake + digest + staff chat all run via OpenRouter.
Adapter handles API key auth, model routing, streaming, usage accounting.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md\` §"Providers",
  §"Model router", \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Adapters/llm".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/llm/openrouter.py\`: \`OpenRouterClient\` impl using
  \`httpx.AsyncClient\`. Methods: \`complete\`, \`chat\`, \`ocr\`,
  \`stream_chat\`. Return a \`LlmResponse\` with \`tokens_in\`,
  \`tokens_out\`, \`cost_cents\`, \`model_id\`, \`latency_ms\`.
- Secrets via \`settings.openrouter_api_key\`.
- Rate-limit handling: retry w/ backoff on 429, surface
  \`LlmRateLimited\` to the caller after exhaustion.
- Capability router (budget-aware model pick) is in
  \`app/domain/llm/\` — that's a later Phase 12 task; the adapter just
  dispatches.
- Record-replay: pytest fixture that records real calls into
  \`tests/fixtures/llm/*.json\` and replays without a network in CI.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Recorded fixture test runs in CI without network access
- [ ] 429 triggers retry + eventual \`LlmRateLimited\`
- [ ] Usage stats are populated on every response
- [ ] Unit tests cover complete / chat / ocr + streaming + error paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_llm_openrouter.py -xvs
\`\`\`
close_reason: OpenRouter client landed with selfreview fixes applied.

title: feat(adapters/mail): SMTP mailer impl
description:
## Problem / goal
Magic links, digests, invoices go over email. Adapter is SMTP for v1
(MailHog in dev; real provider in prod).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\`,
  \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Adapters/mail".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/mail/smtp.py\`: \`SMTPMailer\` implementing \`Mailer\`.
  Uses \`aiosmtplib\` or \`smtplib\` in a thread executor (pick one and
  justify in a short docstring).
- Envelope sender from \`settings.smtp_*\`; return-path matching
  \`bounce+<token>@\` so future bounce webhooks can correlate.
- Retries: 3 attempts w/ exponential backoff.
- Dev mode uses MailHog on \`localhost:1025\`.

## Acceptance criteria

      send a message and assert headers
- [ ] Retry path triggers on transient SMTP errors
- [ ] Unit tests cover success + retry + permanent failure

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
docker compose -f tests/docker-compose.test.yml up -d mailhog
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_mail_smtp.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(adapters/storage): LocalFsStorage content-addressed blobs
description:
## Problem / goal
Receipts, evidence photos, asset docs, payslip PDFs need a storage
adapter. Default is \`LocalFsStorage\` writing under
\`\$CREWDAY_DATA_DIR/uploads/<first-2-of-hash>/<hash>\`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Adapters/storage",
  \`docs/specs/21-assets.md\`.
- Content-addressed by SHA-256 of the bytes.
- \`sign_url\` returns a short-lived \`itsdangerous\`-signed URL routed
  through the API (no direct FS exposure).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/adapters/storage/localfs.py\`: \`LocalFsStorage\` implementing
  \`Storage\`. Atomic write: stream to \`<hash>.tmp\`, rename on
  finalize. Dedupe when the hash file already exists.
- \`sign_url\` returns \`/w/<slug>/api/v1/files/<sig>\` paths.
- S3 implementation is specified but out of scope here — leave
  \`app/adapters/storage/s3.py\` as a stub docstring pointing to the
  Phase "deploy" S3 task.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`put\` writes under \`\$DATA/uploads/ab/abcdef\u2026\`
- [ ] Re-putting identical bytes is a no-op (same hash)
- [ ] \`sign_url\` round-trips through a test verifier
- [ ] Unit tests cover put / get / exists / sign + expired-signature
      rejection

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_storage_localfs.py -xvs
\`\`\`
close_reason: LocalFsStorage content-addressed blob store landing with 46/46 tests (put/get/exists/delete, sign_url round-trip, expired-signature rejection, symlink + path-traversal hardening). See cd-tzw for self-review outcome.

title: feat(events): typed event registry + in-process synchronous bus
description:
## Problem / goal
Cross-context writes flow through events. v1 is synchronous and
in-process, but the contract is transport-agnostic.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Boundary rules" #3.
- Every typed event has: \`name\` (dotted), \`workspace_id\`,
  \`actor_id\`, \`correlation_id\`, \`payload\` (pydantic model).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/events/registry.py\`: \`@event class TaskCompleted(BaseModel):
  ...\` decorator that registers event types under a stable name and
  exposes a schema for serialization.
- \`app/events/bus.py\`: \`EventBus\` with \`subscribe(event_type,
  handler)\` (decorator) and \`publish(event)\`. Synchronous dispatch
  within the current UoW. If a handler raises, the UoW rolls back.
- \`app/events/__init__.py\` re-exports the decorator + bus instance.
- Initial registered events (placeholders; real payloads per spec):
  \`task.created\`, \`task.completed\`, \`task.overdue\`,
  \`stay.upcoming\`, \`expense.approved\`, \`shift.ended\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Registering an event type twice raises at import time
- [ ] Publishing an event invokes every subscriber synchronously, in
      deterministic order
- [ ] A subscriber exception bubbles up (rolls back the UoW)
- [ ] Unit tests cover register / subscribe / publish / ordering

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_events.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(tenancy): FastAPI middleware resolving /w/<slug>/\u2026 \u2192 WorkspaceContext
description:
## Problem / goal
Every authenticated HTTP surface lives under \`/w/<slug>/\u2026\`. The
middleware:
1. Parses the slug from the path.
2. Validates against the slug regex + blocklist.
3. Loads the workspace row (via identity context, once it exists —
   Phase 2 wires the real call; stub initially).
4. Verifies \`user_workspace\` membership for the authenticated actor.
5. Returns \`404\` if not a member (never \`403\`, per §01).
6. Binds a \`WorkspaceContext\` via \`tenancy.set_current\`.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Workspace addressing",
  §"WorkspaceContext".

## Implementation notes
- \`app/tenancy/middleware.py\`: \`WorkspaceContextMiddleware\`
  (Starlette BaseHTTPMiddleware). Skip-paths (bare-host routes) are
  hard-coded per §01.
- Correlation id: if the request carries \`X-Request-Id\`, use it;
  else generate a ULID. Log it, put it on \`WorkspaceContext\`.
- For Phase 0 the membership check can be a stub that always passes
  when \`X-Test-Workspace-Id\` is present. Phase 2 (identity) replaces
  the stub.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Request to \`/w/villa-sud/api/v1/ping\` sets the context
- [ ] Request to \`/w/<unknown>/api/v1/ping\` returns 404
- [ ] Skip-paths (\`/healthz\`, \`/signup\`, \`/login\`, \`/w\`,
      \`/docs\`, \`/openapi.json\`, etc.) don't attempt slug parsing
- [ ] Unit tests cover every branch

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_tenancy_middleware.py -xvs
\`\`\`
close_reason: Middleware landed + self-reviewed under cd-a4b. All acceptance criteria met: scoped /w/<slug>/api/v1/ping binds ctx, unknown/reserved/bad slugs return 404, bare-host skip paths pass through un-scoped, 126 unit tests green.

title: feat(logging): structured JSON logger + secret redaction filter
description:
## Problem / goal
Every mutation is audited (§15) and every secret must stay out of logs.
A central structured-logger setup with a secret-redaction filter is the
foundation.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md\` §"Secrets never logged",
  \`docs/specs/01-architecture.md\` §"Runtime invariants" #7.
- Redactor masks: \`token\`, \`cookie\`, \`Authorization\`, \`passkey
  credential id\`, anything matching \`SecretStr\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/util/logging.py\` with \`setup_logging(level: str)\`:
  - Root logger \u2192 JSON formatter (structlog-compatible shape with
    \`time\`, \`level\`, \`logger\`, \`msg\`, plus any kwargs).
  - Attach \`RedactionFilter\` that scans every record's \`args\`,
    \`kwargs\`, and serialized \`msg\` for the masked patterns and
    replaces with \`***REDACTED***\`.
  - Bind the correlation id (\`audit_correlation_id\`) into the record
    if a context var is set.
- \`app/main.py\` calls \`setup_logging\` at startup.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`logger.info("auth ok", token="s3cr3t")\` logs \`***REDACTED***\`
- [ ] JSON output parses as valid JSON per line
- [ ] Correlation id threaded via context var appears in records
- [ ] Unit tests cover every redaction pattern

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_logging.py -xvs
\`\`\`
close_reason: feat(logging): structured JSON logger + secret redaction filter delivered. Selfreview (cd-962) applied a bugfix for malformed-format exception handling.

title: Self-review: remove VirusScanner Protocol + NullVirusScanner
description: Selfreview pair for the virus-scanner rip-out. Verify: nothing references VirusScanner/NullVirusScanner/_StubScanner/scan_status anywhere; no dead imports; spec 15 subsection gone; cd-v0iz closed wontfix.

title: chore(api/tasks): remove VirusScanner Protocol + NullVirusScanner — out of proportion to threat model
description:
Rip out the virus-scanner seam added in cd-jl0g + cd-ba5c.

User feedback: "totally overblown and useless". crew.day is a small self-hosted property-management app for vacation-rental managers and the cleaners they invite. The MIME sniffer (cd-ba5c) is the real control — bytes claimed as image/png must be a real PNG. ClamAV-on-every-photo is enterprise-SaaS theater for a threat model that doesn't exist.

## Scope
Delete:
- app/adapters/storage/virus.py (NullVirusScanner)
- VirusScanner Protocol + VirusScanResult value object in app/adapters/storage/ports.py
- virus_scanner kwarg + threading in app/domain/tasks/completion.py::add_file_evidence
- get_virus_scanner FastAPI dep + app.state.virus_scanner wiring (app/api/deps.py, app/api/factory.py)
- _StubScanner test fixture and all virus-scan-related test cases (test_virus_scanner_*, test_default_scanner_warns_once, test_evidence_infected, test_*scan_status*)
- EvidenceInfected typed error + its 415 mapping in router
- audit row scan_status field
- Spec 15 "Virus scanning" subsection
- Close cd-v0iz as wontfix

Keep MIME sniff (cd-ba5c) entirely — that's the genuine control.

## Acceptance
- pytest tests/unit/tasks/ tests/integration/api/test_tasks_routes.py tests/tenant/ -x -q passes
- mypy --strict app/ clean
- ruff + lint-imports clean
- No reference to VirusScanner / NullVirusScanner / scan_status anywhere in code or specs

title: test(integration/auth): test_kill_switched_user_no_mail_and_audit_lands flakes under sequential auth-scope run (pre-existing)
description:
## Problem

`tests/integration/auth/test_recovery_full_flow.py::TestRecoveryKillSwitch::test_kill_switched_user_no_mail_and_audit_lands` passes standalone but fails when run inside the broader sequential auth-scope:

```
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/ tests/integration/auth/ -x -q -n0
```

The failure mode is the missing `recovery.disabled_by_workspace` audit row at the assertion in test_recovery_full_flow.py:625:

```
assert len(disabled) == 1
E       assert 0 == 1
E        +  where 0 = len([])
```

Standalone:

```
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_recovery_full_flow.py::TestRecoveryKillSwitch::test_kill_switched_user_no_mail_and_audit_lands -x -q -n0
1 passed
```

## Provenance

* Confirmed pre-existing on baseline (stash cd-9i7z working-tree changes → still fails identically).
* Surfaced by /selfreview on cd-9i7z (cd-aavd).

## Fix direction

Likely a workspace / kill-switch state pollution from an earlier test in the run order. Candidate causes:

* Process-local store (recovery sessions, throttle, kill-switch list) leaking across tests.
* Audit-log filter in the assertion catching a row from a sibling workspace by accident.
* A fixture cleanup that runs in the wrong scope.

Reproduce with:

```
uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/ tests/integration/auth/test_recovery_full_flow.py -x -q -n0
```

then bisect the unit/auth files to find the polluter.

## Acceptance

* `uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/ tests/integration/auth/ -x -q -n0` passes deterministically.
* The original passes-standalone shape is preserved (no scope-only fixture hack).

## Notes

cd-9i7z's autofix run (cd-aavd) deselected this test from the sequential gate so the run could complete; the deselect is a workaround, not a fix.

title: feat(api/tasks): wire ChecklistItem evidence_blob_hash through asset pipeline
description:
Companion to cd-jl0g. Add a router surface (e.g. PATCH /tasks/{id}/checklist/{item_id}/evidence) that attaches a blob_hash to a ChecklistItem via the same Storage port + virus-scan + size-cap pipeline used by add_file_evidence. Acceptance:
- Endpoint returns 200 with updated checklist item carrying evidence_blob_hash.
- Reuses the per-kind MIME allow-list / size cap / virus scan from add_file_evidence.
- Audit task.checklist.evidence.add (or equivalent).
- Integration test with PNG fixture.

Storage seam is already in place from cd-jl0g; just need the new endpoint + domain seam.

title: feat(storage): wire a real VirusScanner backend (ClamAV / vendor)
description:
**Context.** cd-jl0g shipped the asset-pipeline wiring for task evidence (`POST /tasks/{id}/evidence` for `kind=photo|voice|gps`). Per spec §15 "Input validation" every upload MUST be scanned before it lands in the blob store; the seam is the new `app.adapters.storage.ports.VirusScanner` Protocol with `app.adapters.storage.virus.NullVirusScanner` as the default no-op (returns `unknown`, logs one WARNING per process so operators learn the deployment shipped without protection).

**Scope.** Replace `NullVirusScanner` with a real backend in production deployments. Two concrete options:

- **ClamAV via `clamd`** (`socket / TCP`). Default deploy lives in the same docker-compose stack as the API; the scanner streams the bytes through the local socket and returns `clean | infected | unknown`.
- **Cloud vendor REST** (Google Cloud Threat Intel, VirusTotal API). Per-deployment opt-in.

**Wiring.** App factory selects the implementation from `settings.virus_scanner_backend` (`null | clamav | <vendor>`), constructs the appropriate `VirusScanner`, and stashes it on `app.state.virus_scanner`. The completion router (`app/api/v1/tasks.py::upload_task_evidence_route`) reads it through a new `get_virus_scanner` dep and threads it into `add_file_evidence` (today the domain seam falls back to `NullVirusScanner` when the dep isn't passed — that fallback drops once the dep lands).

**Acceptance.**
- `settings.virus_scanner_backend = "clamav"` boots a real scanner; uploads of EICAR test bytes return 415 `evidence_infected`.
- Settings cascade table in `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` lists the knob with default `null` and §15 cross-reference.
- Integration test against a `clamav/clamav-debian` testcontainer (or a stub TCP server) covers clean + infected + scanner-down paths.
- Operator runbook documents the bring-up + key-rotation contract.

**Out of scope.** PDF script-stripping (separate `pdfid` wrapper per §15); EXIF stripping (a sibling task — `documents.extraction` already covers re-encoding).

**Filed by cd-jl0g coder.**
close_reason: wontfix: server-side virus scanning is out of proportion to crew.day's threat model (small self-hosted app for vacation-rental managers + cleaners). MIME sniff (cd-ba5c) is the genuine control. User feedback: 'totally overblown and useless'.

title: ops(devstack): readyz drift check on dev-stack bring-up
description:
## Problem
Long-running dev-stack containers can drift behind the alembic head (entrypoint only runs `alembic upgrade head` at startup). Symptom: tests fail in confusing ways far from the root cause (e.g. cd-t2jz appeared as a magic-link 409 mystery; actual cause was missing audit_log.scope_kind column from a newer migration).

## Fix
Add a wrapper to `scripts/dev-login.sh` and the docker compose bring-up flow that probes `/readyz` and yells loudly when status != ok, naming the failing checks (e.g. `migrations`).

Suggested check:
`curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8100/readyz | jq -e '.status == "ok"'`

## Acceptance
- Dev-stack drift is surfaced at bring-up / login time, not at first failing test.
- Operator gets a one-line remediation hint (e.g. `docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml restart app-api`).

## Notes
Surfaced by cd-t2jz.

title: ops(test): per-IP throttle conflicts with xdist on magic_link_mailpit tests
description:
## Problem
`tests/integration/auth/test_magic_link_mailpit.py` has three tests that hit the magic-link request endpoint, which is rate-limited to 5/min/IP. When xdist runs them in parallel from the same IP, two get throttled and skip via `_request_or_skip`.

## Fix
- `@pytest.mark.xdist_group("magic_link_mailpit")` to pin tests in the file to a single worker.
- Session-level mutex fixture (or sequence them via a class) so they run back-to-back, deterministically, without throttle-induced skips.

## Acceptance
- All 3 tests in the file run (no skips) under `uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_magic_link_mailpit.py -n auto`.

## Notes
Surfaced by cd-t2jz.

title: fix(test): test_util_hashids tampering test is time-dependent flaky
description:
## Problem
`tests/unit/test_util_hashids.py::TestTampering::test_flipped_byte_raises_token_invalid`
intermittently fails when run alongside other tests, observed during
selfreview cd-e7hx. The test:

```python
token = encode_token({'k': 'v'}, _SECRET, _SALT)
last = token[-1]
swap = "A" if last != "A" else "B"
tampered = token[:-1] + swap
with pytest.raises(TokenInvalid):
    decode_token(tampered, _SECRET, _SALT, max_age_seconds=60)
```

The token is signed with `URLSafeTimedSerializer`, so the signature
tail varies with `time.time()`. Flipping the last char to an arbitrary
'A' / 'B' substitution can — with very small but non-zero probability —
land on a value the b64 decoder accepts and that itsdangerous still
parses without raising `TokenInvalid`. The test passed in isolation
during selfreview but failed once when bundled with other tests; once
it has been hard to reproduce, but the time-dependence is structural.

## Repro
Currently flaky under specific time/environment conditions. Run the
full suite repeatedly; expect intermittent failure of the tampering
test only.

## Acceptance
- Tampering test deterministic across at least 100 consecutive runs.
- Either:
  - flip the byte at a position that is guaranteed to land inside the
    HMAC output (post-`.` separator in the itsdangerous payload), or
  - use `monkeypatch` / `freezegun` to pin the signing timestamp so
    the token shape is deterministic.

## Notes
Surfaced by selfreview cd-e7hx. Not a regression caused by the cd-lxp5
fix — pure pre-existing test brittleness.

title: feat(auth/session): apply archived_at gate to cookie-session validate
description:
## Problem
Discovered during cd-et6y selfreview. The verify-time archive gate now blocks delegated tokens and PATs (§03), but `app.auth.session.validate` is unchanged — an archived user with a still-valid session cookie can still authenticate browser requests.

## Fix
Extend session validation to read `users.archived_at` and reject with 401 (or invalidate the session) when set. Match the typed error code shape used for tokens (`subject_user_archived`).

## References
- cd-et6y (sibling token-side enforcement)
- app/auth/session.py

title: verify-time enforcement of delegating-user grant liveness
description:
Spec §03 'Delegated tokens' / 'Personal access tokens' both require 401 when the delegating / subject user 'loses every non-revoked grant'. cd-et6y landed the archived_at half (users.archived_at -> 401 with delegating_user_archived / subject_user_archived). The grant-loss half is deferred:

- v1 role_grant has no revoked_at column (revocation is a hard DELETE today, see app/adapters/db/authz/models.py and app/auth/recovery.py:399-408).
- 'no live grants' today is observable by 'no rows in role_grant for (workspace, user)', but DELETE-on-revoke means a user who never held a grant looks identical to a user whose grant was revoked.
- §02 follow-up x1xh / cd-x1xh lands the role_grant soft-retire shape; once revoked_at is on the column, app.auth.tokens.verify can extend with a 'no live grants anywhere' (delegated) / 'no live grants in any workspace' (PAT) check.

Acceptance:
- After role_grant.revoked_at lands: extend app.auth.tokens.verify so a delegated token whose delegating user has no active (revoked_at IS NULL) grants in the token's workspace returns 401 with error = 'delegating_user_inactive'; same shape for PATs across all workspaces with error = 'subject_user_inactive'.
- Update spec docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md with the two new error codes.
- Tests: live grant -> 200; revoked grant only -> 401; mix -> 200.

Dependencies: blocked-by the eventual role_grant.revoked_at column (track under §02 follow-ups; possibly cd-x1xh).

title: follow-up(api/me): GET /me/availability_overrides self-list endpoint
description:
Spec docs/specs/12-rest-api.md §'Self-service shortcuts' lists GET /me/availability_overrides as a self-only list of every user_availability_override (any approval state) keyed to the caller. cd-6uij implemented POST but not GET — out of scope for that task.

Add the listing handler. Mirror the GET /user_availability_overrides shape but force user_id = ctx.actor_id and drop the ?user_id= filter. Return cursor-paginated rows as in §12 'Pagination'.

See app/api/v1/me_schedule.py, app/api/v1/user_availability_overrides.py for the existing list pattern.

title: follow-up(api/me): /me/schedule task window in property-local time
description:
The /me/schedule aggregator currently filters Occurrence.starts_at against UTC bounds derived from the [from, to] date window. Tasks scheduled in a property timezone that crosses the UTC date boundary (e.g. Pacific/Auckland or America/Los_Angeles) may end up off-by-one in edge cases.

Refine the aggregator to compare on scheduled_for_local with property timezone awareness, OR document the v1 simplification on the spec page.

See app/domain/identity/me_schedule.py aggregate_schedule(), tests/integration/identity/test_me_schedule_api.py.

Pre-req: GET /me/availability_overrides list endpoint (also in §12 but out of cd-6uij scope) lands first if the front-end consolidates around the richer surface.

title: DRY: hoist visible-property fan-out into shared helper
description:
Both `app/api/v1/places.py::_visible_property_ids_for_worker` (cd-yjw5) and `app/api/v1/employees.py::_load_property_ids_by_user` (cd-g6nf) walk `role_grant` rows for a user and fan out across `property_workspace` × `Property` to compute 'which properties does this user see in this workspace'. The two copies share a reason to change (whenever the property-grant scoping rules evolve, e.g. when `role_grant.revoked_at` lands or when the property-scope owners group ships).

Hoist into a shared module — proposed location `app/authz/places_visibility.py` (the package docstring permits direct `app.adapters` imports; the function is fundamentally an authz projection). Single helper signature:

```python
def visible_property_ids_for_user(
    session: Session,
    *,
    workspace_id: str,
    user_id: str,
) -> set[str]: ...
```

Both call sites consume the same return shape. The places.py variant currently takes `workspace_property_ids` for caching; the shared helper either takes that explicitly too, or runs its own `PropertyWorkspace × Property` join (cheaper to do twice than to leak the parameter shape into the public API).

Acceptance:
- Both call sites in `places.py` and `employees.py` consume the shared helper, with no in-line copy of the role_grant fan-out logic.
- Existing tests in `tests/unit/api/v1/places/test_properties_api.py` and `tests/unit/api/v1/test_employees_api.py` still pass without modification (the projection contract is preserved).
- The shared helper has its own focused unit test under `tests/unit/authz/` covering: workspace-wide grant fan-out, property-pinned grants, mixed grants, retired-property gate, no-grants empty set.

title: fix(tests/tenant): add app.domain.tasks.templates.read_many to repository parity gate
description:
Pre-existing failure surfaced during cd-r4gp selfreview.

`tests/tenant/test_repository_parity.py::TestRepositoryParityGate::test_every_method_covered_or_opted_out` fails with:

  AssertionError: repository methods discovered without a cross-tenant case: ['app.domain.tasks.templates.read_many']

The bulk read helper was added in ab05253 (templates_by_id sidecar) but the parity test wasn't updated. Either:
1. Add 'app.domain.tasks.templates.read_many' to `COVERED_METHODS` if it's covered by the ORM filter seam (likely — read_many goes through `scoped_select`), OR
2. Add it to `tests.tenant._optouts.REPOSITORY_METHOD_OPTOUTS` with a justification.

Quick win, ~5 min. Discovered while running the full test sweep on main; not caused by cd-r4gp.

title: Decide on workspace-scoped vs bare 'my-schedule' query keys
description:
## Problem
\`app/web/src/lib/queryKeys.ts\` defines \`qk.mySchedule(from, to)\` as
\`[\"w\", slug, \"my-schedule\", from, to]\` (workspace-scoped per §14
\"Workspace-scoped query keys\"), but every actual consumer + invalidator
uses a bare \`[\"my-schedule\", ...]\` key:

- \`useInfiniteAgenda\` queries with \`[\"my-schedule\", \"infinite\", monday]\`.
- \`SSE INVALIDATIONS\` handlers in \`sse.ts\` invalidate \`[\"my-schedule\"]\`.
- \`ScheduleDialogs\` + \`BookingProposeDialog\` invalidate the same.
- \`qk.mySchedule\` is referenced only in its own unit test —
  no production code.

This means:
1. Switching workspaces does NOT isolate the schedule cache (a
   defence the rest of \`qk.*\` provides). A worker switching
   tenants could in theory see another workspace's leaked cells.
2. \`qk.mySchedule()\` is dead code — calling it would NOT be
   invalidated by SSE.

## Options
- **A**: Make \`useInfiniteAgenda\` use \`qk.mySchedule(...)\` (or a
  new \`qk.mySchedulePages(monday)\`) and update SSE handlers to
  invalidate via \`qk.*()\` so workspace scoping is preserved.
- **B**: Delete \`qk.mySchedule\` and document the bare-key
  exception, accepting the workspace-isolation gap (with
  invalidation on \`workspace.changed\` covering the leak risk).

A leans \"do it the right way\"; B preserves the cd-ops1 mock
contract verbatim.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Decision recorded in \`docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md\` with rationale.
- [ ] Either \`qk.mySchedule\` is wired in or deleted; tree consistent.
- [ ] \`workspace.changed\` SSE handler proven to clear the schedule
      cache on tenant switch.

title: feat(api/sse): emit user_leave.upserted and user_availability_override.upserted events
description:
## Problem
Today the worker schedule SPA (cd-ops1, `/schedule`) refreshes its loaded window when leaves and availability overrides are approved or rejected by piggy-backing on the umbrella `approval.decided` / `approval.resolved` SSE events. That works for the approval transition itself but misses two cases:

- A worker self-creates a pending leave / override (no approval emit until a manager rules on it).
- A manager edits a previously-approved leave / override without bouncing it back through approval.

The SPA's keys (`['my-schedule']`) and `qk.leaves()` / `qk.meOverrides()` need a refresh in those cases; until then the worker has to refresh the page to see their own pending rows.

## Proposal
Emit two new SSE event kinds from the backend:

- `user_leave.upserted` — fired on insert OR update of any `leave` row.
- `user_availability_override.upserted` — fired on insert OR update of any `availability_override` row.

Add them to the spec §14 'Event kinds' table and to the EventKind union in `app/web/src/lib/sse.ts`. Web side already invalidates `['my-schedule']` + `qk.leaves()` / `qk.meOverrides()` on `approval.decided`; the new kinds should fan out to the same surfaces.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Spec §14 lists the new kinds.
- [ ] `app/api/transport/sse.py` emits both kinds on the relevant DB writes.
- [ ] EventKind union + INVALIDATIONS table in `app/web/src/lib/sse.ts` carry both kinds.
- [ ] Per-kind tests in `app/web/src/lib/sse.test.ts` assert the schedule + leaves + meOverrides invalidations.

## Context
Filed by cd-ops1 SchedulePage port.

## References
- `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §'Event kinds'
- `app/web/src/lib/sse.ts` (EventKind union, INVALIDATIONS map)

title: feat(web/manager): drag-to-reorder checklist steps on TemplatesPage with debounced PATCH
description:
## Problem / goal
cd-65kn description called out: "Drag-to-reorder steps must keep server-side ordering stable — debounce the PATCH."

The current mock `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx` and the verbatim port at `app/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx` render checklist steps as a static `<ul>` with no drag handler, no PATCH wiring, no order field. Reorder is a missing affordance, not a regression.

## Technical context
- `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx` (mock, source of truth for layout — must be updated first per /specs flow).
- `app/web/src/pages/manager/TemplatesPage.tsx` (production port — kept verbatim to mock).
- `app/web/src/types/task.ts` `ChecklistTemplateItem`.
- Backend `PATCH /api/v1/task_templates/{id}` (`app/api/v1/tasks.py:962`).

## Implementation notes
- Update the mock first (semantic CSS only, no Tailwind utilities).
- Use a lightweight drag library (e.g. dnd-kit) — confirm via /frontend-design before adding a dep.
- Debounce the PATCH (~400 ms after the last drop) so a multi-row reorder hits the server once.
- Optimistic update against the React Query cache; rollback on `onError`; `onSettled` invalidates.
- Server-side ordering: PATCH carries the full `checklist_template_json` array in the new order; service writes it last-write-wins.
- Keyboard accessibility: "Move up / move down" buttons next to each row for non-pointer users.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] User can drag a checklist step to a new position.
- [ ] PATCH sent debounced (one request per drag burst).
- [ ] Optimistic order survives network round-trip; rollback on 4xx/5xx.
- [ ] Keyboard equivalent works.
- [ ] Updated mock + app/web in lockstep.

## Discovered during
selfreview cd-s1i8 of cd-65kn — verbatim port preserved the mock's lack of reorder.

title: feat(sse): wire task_template.upserted / task_template.deleted SSE event kinds
description:
## Problem / goal
cd-65kn (TemplatesPage verbatim port) calls for SSE invalidation so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear without a refresh. Today there is no `task_template.*` SSE event kind:

- Spec `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §SSE-driven invalidation does not list it.
- Client `app/web/src/lib/sse.ts` `EventKind` union doesn't include it.
- Backend emitter `app/api/transport/sse.py` doesn't publish it.
- Mock `mocks/web/src/lib/sse.ts` doesn't dispatch it.

So the page can never receive a live invalidation when a manager edits a template in another tab.

## Technical context
- `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"SSE-driven invalidation" / §"Event kinds".
- `app/web/src/lib/sse.ts` (`EventKind` union + `INVALIDATIONS` map).
- `mocks/web/src/lib/sse.ts` (mock dispatcher — keep in lockstep).
- `app/api/transport/sse.py` (server emitter; needs the kind added to its publish set).
- `app/api/v1/tasks.py` task-template POST/PATCH/DELETE handlers (publish call sites).
- `app/web/src/lib/queryKeys.ts` `taskTemplates()` key — invalidation target.

## Implementation notes
- Add `task_template.upserted` and `task_template.deleted` to the spec §"Event kinds" list.
- Add the same two kinds to `EventKind` and `INVALIDATIONS` in `app/web/src/lib/sse.ts`. Both invalidate `qk.taskTemplates()`. `task_template.deleted` also invalidates `qk.schedules()` (a deleted template makes schedules referencing it stale).
- Mirror in `mocks/web/src/lib/sse.ts`.
- Backend: emit `task_template.upserted` from POST and PATCH handlers, `task_template.deleted` from the soft-delete handler in `app/api/v1/tasks.py`. Workspace-scoped frame; payload `{ template_id, ... }`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Spec §14 lists both event kinds.
- [ ] `EventKind` (app + mocks) includes both, with handlers wired.
- [ ] Backend POST/PATCH/DELETE on `/api/v1/task_templates` emit the right kind.
- [ ] e2e: edit template in tab A → tab B's TemplatesPage refetches without refresh.

## Discovered during
selfreview cd-s1i8 of cd-65kn — out of scope of the verbatim port.

title: feat(web/public): wire AcceptPage to GET/POST /api/v1/invites/{token} once cd-0b5l ships
description:
## Problem / goal
`app/web/src/pages/public/AcceptPage.tsx` is a verbatim port of the mock at `mocks/web/src/pages/public/AcceptPage.tsx`. It is currently static — hardcoded names ("Maria", "Élodie"), property labels ("Villa Sud", "Apt 3B"), and email ("maria@example.com"); the new-vs-existing branch is selected by a `?state=existing` query param rather than by the invite payload; clicking "Accept" only flips a local `useState` boolean.

Once the backend lands (cd-0b5l: `GET /api/v1/invites/{token}` introspect + URL-shape alignment for the accept route), this page must be rewired to the real API:

- Drop the `?state=` demo branch; read `state: "new" | "existing"` (and inviter name, workspace name, grants list, recipient email) from the `GET /api/v1/invites/{token}` payload.
- Replace the local `setAccepted(true)` with a `useMutation` against `POST /api/v1/invites/{token}/accept` that activates the pending grants and redirects the new session to the workspace landing page.
- For the new-user branch, wire the "Register this device" button into the WebAuthn registration ceremony (`/signup/passkey/finish`-style flow scoped to invites — exact route to be confirmed once cd-0b5l defines it).
- Loading and error states (use `Loading` / `EmptyState` from `@/components/common`).
- SSE invalidation on the relevant invite events.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] No hardcoded copy left — all visible names/properties/email come from the API payload.
- [ ] `?state=` query param removed; branching is server-driven.
- [ ] Real `useMutation` against the accept route; `useState`-only flow gone.
- [ ] Passkey-registration ceremony wired for the new-user branch.
- [ ] Visual diff against `mocks/web/src/pages/public/AcceptPage.tsx` stays within the AA tolerance from cd-cmpy's acceptance criteria.

## Depends on
- cd-0b5l (GET /invites/{token} introspect + URL-shape alignment)

title: feat(api/sse): emit expense.created / expense.submitted / expense.cancelled
description:
## Problem
The web SPA's SSE dispatcher (`app/web/src/lib/sse.ts`) now handles three new expense lifecycle events that the server doesn't yet emit:

- `expense.created` — invalidates `qk.expenses("mine")` + `qk.dashboard()`. Fired when a worker drafts a new claim.
- `expense.submitted` — invalidates `qk.expenses("all" | "mine")` + `qk.dashboard()`. Fired when a worker submits the claim for approval.
- `expense.cancelled` — invalidates `qk.expenses("all" | "mine")` + `qk.dashboard()`. Fired when a worker withdraws a draft / submitted claim.

Until these emit, the SPA still works (the create/cancel mutations invalidate locally), but cross-tab coherence drops: a manager watching `/dashboard` won't see a fresh submission until they refocus the tab.

## Reference
Existing emitter: `app/api/transport/sse.py` (`_INVALIDATIONS` map). Pattern: see `expense.approved` / `.rejected` / `.reimbursed` already there. Spec: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Event kinds" + `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` (lifecycle).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /api/v1/expenses` (create) emits `expense.created`.
- [ ] `POST /api/v1/expenses/{id}/submit` emits `expense.submitted`.
- [ ] `DELETE /api/v1/expenses/{id}` (or the `cancel` route, whichever applies) emits `expense.cancelled`.
- [ ] All three emit a payload of `{id: string, status: ExpenseStatus, ...}`.
- [ ] Spec §14 "Event kinds" lists the three new kinds.
- [ ] Tests cover each emitter.

title: feat(expenses/claims): de-duplicate attachments by (blob_hash, kind)
description:
## Problem / goal
A worker can currently attach the same receipt blob to a claim twice (same `blob_hash`, same `kind`, same `pages`). Storage is content-addressed so the underlying blob is shared, but logically this is a duplicate row in `expense_attachment` — the manager approval UI would show the same file twice and the OCR worker (cd-95zb) would do redundant work.

## Technical context
- Service: `app.domain.expenses.claims.attach_receipt` (cd-7rfu).
- Self-review of cd-7rfu (cd-qcvq) flagged this; the implementing coder noted "mock allows; real UI probably shouldn't".
- The DB does NOT enforce a unique index on (claim_id, blob_hash, kind) today — adding one is the cleanest fix.

## Implementation notes
- Add a `UniqueConstraint('claim_id', 'blob_hash', 'kind')` to `ExpenseAttachment` plus migration.
- Translate the IntegrityError to a domain `AttachmentAlreadyExists` (subclass `ValueError`, 409) so the SPA can show "this receipt is already attached" instead of a 500.
- Decide: same blob with different `kind` (receipt vs invoice) — allow? Probably yes, the worker may be re-classifying. Same blob with different `pages` — probably no, pages should be derived from the blob, not asserted twice.
- Update the API route (cd-t6y2) to surface the 409.
- Tests for the dedupe + the 409 route mapping.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Attaching the same blob_hash + kind to the same claim raises an `AttachmentAlreadyExists` 409
- [ ] Different kind on the same blob is still allowed
- [ ] Migration ships the unique index
- [ ] API route returns 409 with clear envelope
- [ ] Tests cover happy path, dup-rejection, and kind-difference branch

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_expense_claims.py -k dedupe -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_expense_claims_api.py -k dedupe -xvs
```

title: feat(mocks/web/admin): design + add filter UI to AuditPage (actor_kind, action, time range)
description:
## Problem / goal
The current production AuditPage (`app/web/src/pages/admin/AuditPage.tsx`,
ported verbatim from `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AuditPage.tsx` in cd-kxh5)
has no filter UI — it lists every deployment-scope audit row. The
parent task cd-kxh5 originally asked for filter controls (actor kind,
action, time range, with URL search-params round-trip) but the mock
itself never received those controls, so the verbatim port couldn't
deliver them either. Spec §0 audit / §14 admin call for filterable
audit for incident response and compliance.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin" → `/admin/audit`.
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/AuditPage.tsx` (currently 63
  lines, no filter UI).
- **Backend (in flight)**: cd-jlms — `GET /admin/api/v1/audit` with
  `?actor_kind=&action=&since=&until=` query params.
- **Production page**: `app/web/src/pages/admin/AuditPage.tsx` (63
  lines, byte-identical to the mock).

## Implementation notes
- Design the filter UI in the mock first (per the team's mocks-first
  workflow), get it visually approved, then port verbatim into
  `app/web`.
- Filter state must round-trip through URL search-params so a
  filtered audit view is bookmarkable / shareable.
- TanStack Query key includes the filter object so URL ↔ cache
  stay in sync (replace bare `qk.adminAudit()` with a parametrised
  factory).
- Preserve every existing column + the actor-tone chip + timestamp
  format from the current page.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Mock ships filter controls (actor_kind chip group, action
      autocomplete, since/until date pickers, clear-all link).
- [ ] Filter state round-trips via URL search-params; reload
      preserves the active filter.
- [ ] Production page byte-identical to the redesigned mock.
- [ ] `qk.adminAudit` accepts the filter shape; cache invalidates
      correctly per filter.
- [ ] `admin.audit.appended` SSE invalidation still works under
      every filter combination.

## Test plan
```bash
pnpm -C app/web test -- --run
pnpm -C mocks/web test -- --run
npx playwright test tests/web/admin-audit.spec.ts
```

## Depends on
- cd-jlms (backend filter query params)

title: bug(dev): scripts.dev_login fails with 'no such column: workspace.settings_json'
description:
## Problem
Running `docker compose exec app-api python -m scripts.dev_login --email me@dev.local --workspace smoke` on the dev stack (as of 2026-04-24) fails with:

```
sqlite3.OperationalError: no such column: workspace.settings_json
```

This blocks any smoke-test / Playwright-screenshot verification flow that depends on the dev_login stdout cookie (per `AGENTS.md` §Session bootstrap). Discovered while porting cd-fmlk (HistoryPage) — had to fall back to compile-time parity only.

## Likely cause
The `workspace` SQLAlchemy model carries a `settings_json` column that the database on disk doesn't have. Schema drift between the ORM model and the migrations / bootstrap SQL.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `scripts.dev_login` prints a `__Host-crewday_session=...` cookie without traceback.
- [ ] Schema matches the ORM — either a migration adds `workspace.settings_json` or the model drops the field to match the migrations source-of-truth.
- [ ] CI covers the dev_login path so this doesn't drift again silently.

## Repro
```
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml exec app-api python -m scripts.dev_login --email me@dev.local --workspace smoke
```

title: feat(api/me): /me/history aggregator — tasks+chats+expenses+leaves feed
description:
## Problem / goal
The /history page (cd-fmlk) calls `GET /api/v1/history?tab=tasks|chats|expenses|leaves`. Only the mock backend implements this; the real FastAPI app doesn't, so the freshly-ported production `HistoryPage.tsx` at `app/web/src/pages/employee/HistoryPage.tsx` renders "Failed to load." until this aggregator ships.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §worker history, `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` for the endpoint contract.
- **Payload shape**: `HistoryPayload` in `app/web/src/types/dashboard.ts`:
  ```ts
  interface HistoryPayload {
    tab: 'tasks' | 'chats' | 'expenses' | 'leaves';
    tasks: Task[];
    expenses: Expense[];
    leaves: Leave[];
    chats: { id: string; title: string; last_at: string; summary: string }[];
  }
  ```
- **Mock reference**: `mocks/app/api_routes/history.py` (or wherever the mock aggregator lives).
- **Auth**: worker-scoped — the feed reflects the calling user only.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `GET /w/<slug>/api/v1/history?tab=tasks` returns the caller's completed+skipped Task occurrences.
- [ ] `?tab=expenses` returns the caller's approved+reimbursed+rejected expenses.
- [ ] `?tab=leaves` returns approved leaves for the caller.
- [ ] `?tab=chats` returns archived agent chats (format per `HistoryPayload.chats`).
- [ ] Unknown `tab=` → 422.
- [ ] Anonymous → 401.
- [ ] Paginated (cursor or page= query param, per §12).
- [ ] Returns stable shape matching `HistoryPayload` in `app/web/src/types/dashboard.ts`.

## Test plan
- `pytest tests/api/test_history.py` covering the four tabs + auth + pagination boundaries.
- Once live, revisit cd-fmlk to close the visual-parity Playwright screenshot diff.

title: feat(api/tasks): 409 approval_required gate on requires_approval actions
description:
**Context.** Spec §12 defines a `409 approval_required` envelope with an `approval_request_id` for actions marked `requires_approval=True` in the catalog. As of cd-sn26 neither the action catalog (`app/domain/identity/_action_catalog.py`) nor the authz enforcer raise a dedicated `ApprovalRequired` error — the plumbing is absent.

**Scope.**
- Extend `ActionSpec` with `requires_approval: bool = False`.
- Add `ApprovalRequired` to `app/authz/` raised from the enforcer.
- Wire the HITL approval_request creation + 409 envelope in the router error map (`app/api/v1/tasks.py::_http_for_task_mutation`).
- Surface the 409 under `POST /tasks/{id}/assign` + `/cancel` for actions that gain `requires_approval=True`.
- Integration test: a capped action → 409 with `{"error": "approval_required", "approval_request_id": "<ulid>"}`.

**Dependencies**
- Likely blocked by the `/approvals` surface under `app/api/v1/admin` / LLM HITL (not in cd-sn26 either).

title: time router pagination — migrate /time/shifts and /time/leaves to spec §12 envelope
description: Spec docs/specs/12-rest-api.md Pagination pins the collection envelope as {"data": [...], "next_cursor": "...", "has_more": ...}. Shipped /time/shifts (cd-whl) and /time/leaves (cd-31c) still use the older {"items": [...]} shape. Newer identity routers (work_engagements, work_roles, user_work_roles) already use the spec envelope. This inconsistency will break SPA assumptions once bigger result sets exist. Acceptance: GET /time/shifts, /time/leaves, /time/me/leaves respond with data/next_cursor/has_more. Service layer accepts limit + after_id cursor. Tests migrated. Spec §Leave requests + §Shifts updated. OpenAPI regenerated.

title: fix(util/redact): ruff SIM103 + E501 on _looks_like_hash
description:
app/util/redact.py:275 trips two ruff rules on main:

* SIM103 — `if len(value) >= 40 and all(...): return True; return False` should return the condition directly.
* E501 — the line is 120 chars (cap 88).

Not shipped as part of cd-dcfw because the scope was identity routers; caught during /selfreview of cd-dcfw (cd-ana2).

## Fix
Collapse to `return len(value) >= 40 and all(c in ALPHABET for c in value)` and hoist the alphabet into a module constant so the line stays under 88 chars.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `ruff check app/util/redact.py` clean
- [ ] `pytest tests/unit/util/test_redact.py -q` still passes

title: feat(db/expenses): integration tests + FK promotions
description:
Integration coverage for the expense_claim / expense_line / expense_attachment tables landed by cd-lbn, plus the FK promotions that cd-lbn deferred.

## Integration coverage (tests/integration/test_db_expenses.py)

Cover what unit tests cannot:

- Multi-attachment cardinality (0, 1, N rows per claim) with a real DB.
- CASCADE on claim delete sweeps lines + attachments.
- RESTRICT on work_engagement delete blocks when a claim exists.
- CASCADE on workspace delete sweeps the whole expense ledger.
- CHECK constraint violations raise IntegrityError (state, category, currency length, pages=0, negative amounts, autofill_confidence out of 0..1).
- JSON llm_autofill_json round-trips on both SQLite + Postgres.
- Tenant filter behaviour: two workspaces, list returns only current workspace's rows; bare SELECT without WorkspaceContext raises TenantFilterMissing.
- Mirror tests/integration/test_db_payroll.py shape.

## FK promotions (follow-up migrations)

Soft-refs left by cd-lbn — promote once parent tables land:

- expense_claim.owed_destination_id → payout_destination.id
- expense_claim.reimbursement_destination_id → payout_destination.id
- expense_claim.property_id → property.id (coordinated with §05 property_workspace intersection)
- expense_line.asset_id → asset.id (§21)
- expense_attachment.blob_hash → file.id (once §02 Shared tables 'file' table lands)

Each promotion needs a backfill migration that validates existing rows before the FK is added.

## Spec refs

- docs/specs/02-domain-model.md §'Core entities (by document)' (§09 row), §'Shared tables'
- docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md §'Expense claims', §'Payout destinations', §'Amount owed to the employee'

## Context

cd-lbn landed the three-table schema with soft-refs for parent tables that haven't landed yet. This task covers the real-DB CRUD tests and tracks the FK promotion work so nothing gets lost.

title: fix(util/redact): ruff SIM103 + E501 on is_opaque_token
description: Repo-wide 'uv run ruff check app tests' fails on two pre-existing findings in app/util/redact.py:275 (SIM103 + E501) — the last if-return can be collapsed, and the inline base64url alphabet pushes the line to 120 chars. Introduced in commit a0612ce (cd-a469). Hoist the alphabet to a module-level frozenset constant and collapse the helper to a single return. Discovered during cd-drir selfreview of cd-rpxd; out of that review's scope.

title: refactor(api/identity): cursor pagination on workspace API tokens + /users listings
description:
Spec `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §"Pagination" requires every listing to be cursor-paginated. Two identity listings are fixed-cap today:

- `GET /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/tokens` (`app/api/v1/auth/tokens.py`) — returns every row, no cursor. 5-row per-user cap + 10 users per workspace means practical size is ~50 rows per workspace, so not an urgent perf problem, but still drifts from the spec contract.
- `GET /w/<slug>/api/v1/users/...` (once the listing endpoint exists — cd-jinb / cd-dcfw) must ship with cursor pagination from day one.

`GET /me/tokens` and `GET /auth/passkey/...` stay fixed-cap (5 PATs per user, ~5 passkeys per user) — those are explicitly capped by guardrails in §03, not paginated.

Parent: cd-rpxd

title: feat(api/identity): push-tokens HTTP router (native-app delivery, §02 + §14)
description:
Spec `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` §Auth pins the device-push-token surface for the future native app:

```
GET    /api/v1/me/push-tokens
POST   /api/v1/me/push-tokens
PUT    /api/v1/me/push-tokens/{id}
DELETE /api/v1/me/push-tokens/{id}
```

Not implemented today. Reserved surface per spec: until push delivery is wired + deployment-level FCM/APNS credentials are provisioned, `POST` returns `501 push_unavailable`; `GET` + `DELETE` must always be live so a sign-out can prune a stale row even on a deployment with push off.

Implementation notes:
- Bare host, passkey session only (self-only; owner can't see another user's push tokens).
- Body: `{platform: "android"|"ios", token, device_label?, app_version?}`.
- Audit rows on register / refresh / unregister.
- `user_push_token` DB model may also be missing — check `app/adapters/db/identity/`.

Parent: cd-rpxd

title: feat(employees/reinstate): scope reinstate sweep to archive-time window
description: v1 reinstate_employee clears deleted_at on every archived user_work_role in the (user, workspace) pair — not just the rows the corresponding archive marked. If an operator had manually ended a single role before the archive, reinstate brings it back. Spec §05 'Archive / reinstate' describes per-row reinstatement. Fix: scope the reinstate sweep to the archive-time window (audit diff already carries archived_user_work_role_ids). Code: app/services/employees/service.py::reinstate_employee. Spec: docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md. Discovered by selfreview of cd-dv2 (cd-3x4), flagged RISKY non-blocking.

title: feat(employees): cross-workspace reinstate clears users.archived_at
description:
Spec `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` §"Archive / reinstate" scope #3 calls for a reinstate path that clears `users.archived_at` when the caller is a deployment-level owner AND the user has no other active engagements. cd-dv2 landed the workspace-local path only (`app/services/employees/service.py::reinstate_employee`). This follow-up must:

- Add `archived_at` + `updated_at` columns to the `users` ORM model + migration (\§02 "users").
- Define the deployment-level owner check (not in the current action catalog — needs an `owners.deployment` permission or equivalent).
- Add a scan that aggregates `work_engagement` across every workspace for the target user before clearing the identity-level `archived_at`.
- Expose a route `POST /api/v1/users/{id}/reinstate?scope=deployment` (or equivalent header flag) to trigger the deployment-level path.

Dependencies: depends on the broader users-table widening that also lands `full_legal_name`, `phone_e164`, `preferred_locale`, `emergency_contact`, `notes_md`, `agent_approval_mode`, `avatar_file_id`, `primary_workspace_id`.

title: chore(boundaries): restore CLI contract — crewday._codegen legitimately imports app.api.factory
description:
## Problem

`uv run lint-imports` reports the "CLI forbids app server internals" contract as BROKEN because `crewday._codegen.load_live_schema()` imports `app.api.factory` to boot the FastAPI app and read `app.openapi()`.

This is pre-existing drift from the cd-codegen commit (3bbed51). The codegen is a build-time-only tool that legitimately needs to materialise the OpenAPI schema from a live app instance; the edge is not a runtime leak.

Caught while landing the sibling-boundary contract under cd-qetp. Out of scope for that task (which focused on domain-sibling independence), but worth closing before the CLI contract becomes toothless.

## Fix options

1. **Whitelist the codegen edge** via `ignore_imports` on the CLI contract. One-line fix:
   - `"crewday._codegen -> app.api.factory"` plus the transitive edges `app.api.factory` pulls.
2. **Extract OpenAPI to a committed JSON file** (already at `docs/api/openapi.json`) and teach `generate_surfaces` to read that instead of booting the app. Cleaner long-term; the codegen becomes a pure transformer with no runtime deps.
3. **Subprocess boundary**: `load_live_schema()` shells out to a separate Python process that boots the app and dumps JSON to stdout. Heavier; probably overkill.

Option 2 is the right answer — the codegen should be transform-only. Option 1 is the stopgap that lets the contract go green today.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `uv run lint-imports` passes without `BROKEN` contracts.
- [ ] Either the codegen stops importing app server internals (option 2) OR the CLI contract carries a documented ignore_imports exception with a clear rationale and follow-up tracking back to this ticket.

## References
- Pre-existing from commit 3bbed51 (`feat(cli/codegen): build _surface.json from openapi.json + _exclusions.yaml`).
- Caught during cd-qetp (sibling-boundary contract landing).

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: Selfreview on cd-qetp (task cd-aukd) found that import-linter's forbidden contract only needs the root edge crewday._codegen -> app.api.factory ignored — it stops walking once that edge is whitelisted. The original PR listed 15 transitive edges (factory -> routers, middleware, worker, etc.); all were redundant and would have silently rotted as app.api.factory evolves. The whitelist is now a single entry. Option 2 (teach codegen to read the committed openapi.json) is still the right long-term fix.

title: feat(audit): bare-host identity audit seam for /me endpoints
description:
The bare-host /me routers (app/api/v1/auth/me.py, me_tokens.py, logout.py where it writes through session.invalidate_for_user) currently either skip audit or re-derive app.auth.session._agnostic_audit_ctx()'s synthetic zero-ULID workspace/actor shape from scratch.

**Status update (cd-xtlz self-review of cd-6vq5):** me_avatar.py now writes identity.avatar.updated / identity.avatar.cleared audit rows using its own local _identity_audit_ctx() copy — the same pattern magic_link.py, recovery.py, signup.py, session.py already use. The "no audit" gap is closed for the avatar surface; what remains is the *consolidation* work below.

A dedicated tenant-agnostic identity audit seam should land so every identity mutation (PAT mint/revoke, avatar set/clear, display-name changes, future /me/email/verify consume) carries a consistent forensic trail. Requirements:

- Extract the synthetic-ctx factory (6+ copies of _agnostic_audit_ctx across app/auth/* and app/api/v1/auth/me_avatar.py) to a shared helper in app/audit/ or app/auth/ so the zero-ULID idiom has one canonical definition.
- me_tokens.py (PAT mint/revoke) currently skips audit entirely — add identity.token.minted / identity.token.revoked rows with the acting-user id in the diff.
- logout.py — confirm the session.invalidate_for_user path's audit row is sufficient or needs an identity.session.cleared row in addition.
- display-name changes on /api/v1/auth/me — no endpoint yet, but the seam should be ready when it lands.
- Spec update in docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md §Audit to document which bare-host events emit audit + the canonical action names.

Cross-ref: cd-6vq5 (avatar row shape pins the canonical 'identity.<thing>.<verb>' action naming + before_hash/after_hash diff idiom).

title: Self-review: feat(db/work_engagement): add organization + pay_destination FKs once those tables land
description:
## Problem / goal

Auto-fixing self-review of the changes made under cd-0ro4. Catch
bugs, missing pieces, and unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-0ro4** (main task must be complete first).

## How to run

Run `/selfreview` in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from
cd-0ro4.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Run the quality gates after fixing.
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

See `.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md`.

## Acceptance criteria

- All BUGS from the self-review fixed.
- All MISSING pieces completed.
- All RISKY items mitigated (or justified in a task comment).
- Linter, formatter, type checker, affected tests all pass.
- Fixes committed and pushed.

title: feat(db/work_engagement): add organization + pay_destination FKs once those tables land
description:
## Problem / goal

cd-4saj landed the work_engagement table with three soft-ref columns
carrying no real FK constraint:

- supplier_org_id -> future organization.id
- pay_destination_id -> future pay_destination.id
- reimbursement_destination_id -> future pay_destination.id

The columns are plain String because neither organization nor
pay_destination exists yet. Once those tables ship, land a migration
that promotes each column into a real FK (matching the spec 02
section work_engagement definition exactly).

## Technical context

- Spec anchor: docs/specs/02-domain-model.md section "work_engagement"
  (lines 843-873) names the three FKs.
- Parent tables pending:
  - organization (not yet filed; will accompany section 22
    clients/vendors)
  - pay_destination (not yet filed; will accompany section 09 pay
    pipeline)
- Current column definitions live in
  app/adapters/db/workspace/models.py (class WorkEngagement) and
  migrations/versions/20260424_1900_d0f2a3b4c5e6_work_engagement_cd_4saj.py.
  Soft-ref rationale documented on both.
- Naming convention for FKs: fk_work_engagement_<col>_<ref_table> via
  app.adapters.db.base.NAMING_CONVENTION.

## Acceptance criteria

- Alembic migration ALTERs work_engagement to add three FKs:
  - supplier_org_id -> organization.id (ON DELETE RESTRICT; a
    supplier disappearing should not silently orphan engagements).
  - pay_destination_id -> pay_destination.id (ON DELETE SET NULL is
    safer; losing a payout target must not cascade to
    work_engagement deletion).
  - reimbursement_destination_id -> pay_destination.id
    (ON DELETE SET NULL for the same reason).
- Migration is reversible via downgrade().
- Schema fingerprint parity test still passes on SQLite + PG.
- Orphan-row sweep: before adding the FKs, delete or repoint any row
  whose soft-ref id does not resolve; otherwise FK creation fails.
- Update WorkEngagement docstring + module docstring to drop the
  "soft reference" language for these three columns.

## Dependencies

- blocks: nothing immediately
- blocked by: organization table landing; pay_destination table
  landing

## Follow-up for cd-4saj

Filed per cd-4saj's PR direction; the soft-ref columns landed
knowingly, and this task is the commitment to promote them as soon
as the parent tables exist.
