title: feat(api/inventory): stocktake endpoints + paginated per-item history + new reports
description:
## Problem / goal
Surface the stocktake flow and the infinite-scroll ledger over REST
so the manager web can drive them. Adds:

- Paginated per-item history (``/inventory/{iid}/movements`` with
  ``before=`` cursor).
- Stocktake session endpoints.
- New report endpoints: production_rate, shrinkage, stocktakes.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: ``docs/specs/12-rest-api.md`` §"Inventory" (updated
  2026-04-22).
- **Service dependencies**:
  - cd-4qgt (movement service) for the history read
  - cd-14m3 (stocktake service) for the session endpoints
  - cd-n6ga (schema) for everything
- **Companion existing task**: cd-t9ur already covers the core
  inventory routes; this task extends it. Open question — split
  into sub-tasks or amend cd-t9ur? Going with a **sibling task**
  so the core CRUD work stays shippable independently.

## Endpoints (§12 inventory block, full)
- ``GET /inventory/{iid}/movements?before=<iso>&limit=<n>`` →
  ``{items, next_cursor}``. ``limit`` clamped to ``[1, 50]``;
  cursor = the page's oldest ``occurred_at``.
- ``POST /properties/{pid}/stocktakes`` → create session
- ``GET /properties/{pid}/stocktakes`` → list
- ``GET /stocktakes/{sid}`` → detail + draft lines
- ``PATCH /stocktakes/{sid}/lines/{item_id}`` → save one line
- ``POST /stocktakes/{sid}/commit`` → batch commit
- ``GET /inventory/reports/production_rate`` — 30-day avg
  produce by item × property
- ``GET /inventory/reports/shrinkage`` — sum of theft + loss (+
  unexplained audit_correction) over window
- ``GET /inventory/reports/stocktakes`` — session activity list

## Implementation notes
- Cursor pagination: order by ``(occurred_at DESC, id DESC)``,
  use keyset pagination (not OFFSET). Cursor payload is just the
  ``occurred_at`` — ties broken by id.
- All quantity fields serialise as JSON numbers per §08
  'Precision contract' (not strings; server uses ``Decimal`` but
  json-encodes via the standard float-preserving adapter).
- Idempotency-Key: required on ``/stocktakes/{sid}/commit`` since
  a retry after a network blip could otherwise re-commit.
- ``x-agent-confirm`` annotation on ``POST /inventory/{id}/adjust``
  already exists (per cd-t9ur); the stocktake commit endpoint
  gets the same annotation since it writes movements in bulk.
- Scope filtering: everything under ``/w/<slug>/api/v1`` with
  the workspace filter applied via the shared RLS + app-level
  middleware (§15).
- OpenAPI schemas for new response shapes; Schemathesis green
  under tag ``inventory``.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All 8 endpoints land with the request/response shapes above
- [ ] Cursor pagination returns stable pages (no duplicates, no
      gaps) across inserts concurrent with paging
- [ ] Commit endpoint is idempotent under Idempotency-Key replay
- [ ] Response decimals carry the full precision (``0.3`` not
      ``0.3000000001``)
- [ ] OpenAPI includes the new operations + schemas; Schemathesis
      green
- [ ] 403 when the caller lacks ``inventory.stocktake`` /
      ``inventory.adjust``

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_inventory_stocktake.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_inventory_history_pagination.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_inventory_reports.py -xvs
uv run schemathesis run app/openapi.json --tag inventory
\`\`\`

title: feat(inventory): stocktake service (open/save-line/commit batch with source_stocktake_id)
description:
## Problem / goal
Property-wide inventory reconciliation (§08 'Stocktake'). One
``inventory_stocktake`` row per session; observed counts collected
as draft lines; on commit, a transactional batch of
``inventory_movement`` rows lands with a shared
``source_stocktake_id`` so the daily digest can summarise the
session as a single audit handle.

Per-item adjust is covered by cd-4qgt's ``adjust_to_observed``.
This task handles the property-walk case.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: ``docs/specs/08-inventory.md`` §"Stocktake (property-wide
  reconciliation)".
- **Depends on**:
  - cd-n6ga (schema: ``inventory_stocktake`` table + ``source_stocktake_id`` column)
  - cd-4qgt (movement service — we call it for each line)
- **Service location**: ``app/services/inventory/stocktake_service.py``.

## Implementation notes
- Draft lines live in-memory on the session while open; each
  ``PATCH /stocktakes/{sid}/lines/{item_id}`` upserts into a small
  ``inventory_stocktake_line`` table (session-scoped, not in the
  permanent ledger) keyed by (stocktake_id, item_id). Uniqueness
  per pair.
- On commit: begin tx, for each non-zero ``(observed - on_hand)``
  delta call ``movement_service.reconcile(item, delta, reason, note,
  source_stocktake_id=sid)`` — a thin wrapper that writes one
  movement + updates on_hand under the item row lock. Reason is
  the one the user chose per line (defaults to ``audit_correction``).
- Zero-delta lines are skipped silently.
- ``completed_at`` is set atomically with the movements.
- Abandon path: a worker job (``stocktake_gc``, daily) marks
  sessions older than 24 h as ``completed_at = now`` with
  ``note_md = 'abandoned — no movements written'`` and deletes
  the draft-line rows.
- Concurrency: a running stocktake does NOT block concurrent
  task-driven consume/produce. The per-item delta is computed at
  commit time against the current on_hand (re-read inside the
  tx), not the value when the draft line was saved. Rationale:
  §08 'An open stocktake does not block…'.
- Permissions: ``inventory.stocktake`` action — see §05 catalog
  entry added in the same /specs pass.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] ``open(property_id)`` creates the session row and returns it
- [ ] ``save_line(sid, item_id, observed, reason, note)`` upserts
      the draft
- [ ] ``commit(sid)`` writes one movement per non-zero delta under
      a shared ``source_stocktake_id``, sets ``completed_at``,
      returns the written movements
- [ ] Zero-delta lines yield no movement
- [ ] Concurrent task completion that mutates ``on_hand`` between
      ``save_line`` and ``commit`` uses the **commit-time** on_hand
      as the baseline (test this)
- [ ] Session can only be committed once (second call returns 409
      / raises ``AlreadyCommitted``)
- [ ] Abandoned sessions auto-close after 24 h via ``stocktake_gc``
      with a note
- [ ] ``inventory.stocktake`` permission check enforced
- [ ] Unit + integration tests cover open, save_line, commit, idempotency,
      concurrency, abandonment

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_stocktake_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_stocktake_commit.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_stocktake_gc.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(db/inventory): reason enum + produce/stocktake columns + effects_json + apply_on_task setting
description:
## Problem / goal
Implement the DB schema called for by the 2026-04-22 /specs pass on
§08 inventory: task-driven production, richer reconciliation reasons,
stocktake sessions, decimal precision contract, free-text `unit`, and
the single `inventory.apply_on_task` setting.

**No production deployment has occurred** — the unshipped Alembic
migration ``migrations/versions/20260420_0909_0aa2a5606810_inventory.py``
can be rewritten in place to emit the final schema directly. There is
no data migration of legacy values, no legacy-key fallback, and no
back-compat aliasing. Specs (§02, §08) are canonical; code follows.

This is the **foundational** unlock for the produce + reconcile work —
nothing downstream (movement service extension, stocktake service,
new REST endpoints, web drawer) can land until the schema allows it.

## Technical context
- **Specs**: ``docs/specs/08-inventory.md`` §"Reason taxonomy",
  §"`inventory_stocktake`", §"Precision contract", §"Inventory
  effects on task completion", §"Setting cascade";
  ``docs/specs/02-domain-model.md`` settings cascade row for
  ``inventory.apply_on_task``.
- **DB layer**: ``app/adapters/db/inventory/models.py``.
- **Unshipped migration**: ``migrations/versions/20260420_0909_0aa2a5606810_inventory.py``
  — edit directly (or replace) to emit the final shape; no data
  migration step needed since no prod rows exist.
- **Mock source of truth**: ``mocks/app/mock_data.py``
  ``InventoryMovement`` literal enum carries the full 12-value set.

## Implementation notes
### 1. ``inventory_movement.reason`` enum
Target value set (CHECK constraint on SQLite, ENUM TYPE on Postgres):
``restock | consume | produce | waste | theft | loss | found |
returned_to_vendor | transfer_in | transfer_out | audit_correction |
adjust``.

Emit this directly in the migration. Do **not** include a
``receive``/``issue`` rename step — those never shipped.

### 2. New ``inventory_movement`` columns
- ``source_task_id`` (ULID FK, nullable) — feeds the consumption
  hook task cd-yxdh.
- ``source_stocktake_id`` (ULID FK, nullable) — links a movement to
  its reconcile session. Null for routine writes.

### 3. New ``inventory_stocktake`` table
Columns match §08: ``id PK, workspace_id FK, property_id FK,
started_at, completed_at (nullable), actor_kind, actor_id, note_md``.
Indexes: ``(workspace_id, property_id, started_at DESC)``.

### 4. ``task_template`` + ``asset_action``: ``inventory_effects_json``
List shape: ``[{item_ref, kind: 'consume'|'produce', qty: decimal}, …]``.
No legacy map-shape handling — the field is new in this schema.

### 5. Cascade setting
Single row: ``inventory.apply_on_task`` (bool, default ``true``,
scope W/P/U/WE/T). Also add ``inventory.shrinkage_alert_pct`` (int,
default 10, scope W/P) for the shrinkage digest alert. **No legacy
``inventory.consume_on_task`` fallback** in the resolver.

### 6. Precision contract
- All decimal columns (``inventory_item.on_hand``, ``.reorder_point``,
  ``.reorder_target``, ``inventory_movement.delta``, effect ``qty``)
  declared as ``Numeric(14, 4, asdecimal=True)`` from the start.

### 7. ``inventory_item.unit``
- Free-text ``TEXT NOT NULL``. No CHECK constraint, no enum type.
  UI layer surfaces a pre-filled suggestion picker (``each``, ``pack``,
  ``kg``, ``liter``, ``roll``, ``set``, ``bottle``, ``L``, ``stère``,
  ``btl``) with a custom-entry fallback. DB just stores whatever
  string the operator wrote.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Alembic migration applies cleanly on SQLite **and** Postgres
      (``alembic upgrade head`` green in both CI shards)
- [ ] ``inventory_movement.reason`` accepts the 12-value spec enum
      (no ``receive``/``issue``)
- [ ] New columns ``source_task_id`` and ``source_stocktake_id``
      present on ``inventory_movement``
- [ ] ``inventory_stocktake`` table present with spec'd columns + FKs
- [ ] ``task_template`` and ``asset_action`` carry
      ``inventory_effects_json`` (list shape); no
      ``inventory_consumption_json`` column remains
- [ ] Settings cascade catalog carries ``inventory.apply_on_task``
      (scope W/P/U/WE/T, default true) and
      ``inventory.shrinkage_alert_pct`` (scope W/P, default 10);
      **no legacy key fallback** in the resolver
- [ ] ``inventory_item.unit`` stored as free-text ``TEXT`` — no CHECK,
      no enum
- [ ] All decimal columns declared ``Numeric(14, 4)``
- [ ] ``app/adapters/db/inventory/models.py`` and ``__init__.py``
      docstrings reflect the spec vocabulary (no 'slightly different
      taxonomy' caveat)
- [ ] ``uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_inventory.py
      tests/integration/test_db_inventory.py`` green on both DB shards

## Test plan
### Automated
\`\`\`bash
uv run alembic upgrade head          # SQLite shard
CREWDAY_DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://... uv run alembic upgrade head
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_db_inventory.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_db_inventory.py -xvs
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. Seed a workspace; insert an ``inventory_movement`` with
   ``reason=produce`` and a ``source_task_id`` — commit.
2. Insert an ``inventory_stocktake`` + 3 movements with
   ``source_stocktake_id`` — commit.
3. Resolve ``inventory.apply_on_task`` via the cascade resolver;
   confirm it returns ``true`` by default and picks up a property-level
   override.
4. Read a template whose ``inventory_effects_json`` carries both
   ``consume`` and ``produce`` entries — confirm the service layer
   returns the typed list shape.
5. Insert an ``inventory_item`` with ``unit='stère'`` — confirm it
   accepts without constraint violation.

title: Self-review: chore(web): self-host fonts (vendor Google Fonts locally)
description:
## Problem / goal

Auto-fixing self-review of the changes made under **cd-upkl**. Catch
bugs, missing pieces, and unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-upkl** (main task must be complete first).

## How to run

Run \`/selfreview\` in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from
cd-upkl.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Run the quality gates after fixing.
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

See [\`.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md\`](../selfreview/SKILL.md).

## Extra things to look for on this task

- Any remaining reference to \`fonts.googleapis.com\` or
  \`fonts.gstatic.com\` in HTML, CSS, JS/TS, or docs (including
  \`docs/specs/\`).
- CSP / security headers still listing the now-unused Google Fonts
  domains — dead entries should be removed.
- Font subsets or weights that were imported but aren't actually
  used (over-vendoring ships dead bytes on every page load).
- Font subsets or weights that were used but got dropped in the
  vendor step (watch for FOIT / fallback flashes on manager/worker
  pages).
- \`package.json\` / lockfile churn that wasn't committed, or
  lockfile that diverges between \`mocks/web/\` and \`app/web/\`.
- Build artefacts (\`dist/\`) accidentally committed.
- CLAUDE.md or spec docs (\`docs/specs/14\`, \`docs/specs/16\`) still
  claiming we load fonts from Google.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All BUGS from the self-review fixed.
- [ ] All MISSING pieces completed.
- [ ] All RISKY items mitigated (or justified in a task comment).
- [ ] Linter, formatter, type checker, affected tests all pass.
- [ ] \`rg -n 'fonts\\.(googleapis|gstatic)\\.com'\` across the repo
  returns nothing (or only inside this task's body / comments).
- [ ] Fixes committed and pushed.

title: chore(web): self-host fonts (vendor Google Fonts locally)
description:
## Problem / goal

Both SPAs currently fetch fonts live from `fonts.googleapis.com` /
`fonts.gstatic.com` on every page load. Self-hosting gives us:

- **Privacy**: no third-party call on first paint (no IP leak to
  Google from the user's browser). GDPR-relevant — hosts in the EU
  have been fined for pulling Google Fonts directly.
- **Reliability**: app works on restricted networks (corporate
  firewalls, offline-ish cottage WiFi) and when Google Fonts is slow
  or blocked.
- **Performance**: served from the same origin, under our cache
  headers; no extra TLS handshake; works with our CSP without needing
  `font-src https://fonts.gstatic.com` and `style-src https://fonts.googleapis.com`.
- **Offline / self-hosters**: the self-hosted app must not depend on
  an external CDN at runtime.

This task vendors every recurring external asset we serve today. Right
now that's **only fonts** (see Technical context); no other CDN
scripts/styles/images are referenced.

## Technical context

**External assets currently referenced** (audited 2026-04-21):

- `mocks/web/index.html:8-13` — preconnect to
  `fonts.googleapis.com` / `fonts.gstatic.com` + stylesheet link for
  Fraunces (opsz 9..144, wghts 400/500/600/700/800), Inter Tight
  (400/500/600/700), JetBrains Mono (400/500).
- `app/web/index.html:8-13` — same three families, same weights.
- No other CDN scripts, styles, images, or icon fetches found.
  `lucide-react` is already bundled via npm.

**Stack**: Vite 5 + React 18 TS SPA (see
`mocks/web/vite.config.ts`, `app/web/vite.config.ts`). Vite leaves
absolute `https://` URLs untouched during build/dev, so local fonts
must either ship as an npm package or live under `public/`.

**Convention**: semantic CSS only (see `CLAUDE.md` §"Application-specific
notes"). Font stacks are referenced from stylesheets in
`mocks/web/src/styles/` (and `app/web/src/styles/` if it mirrors).

**Spec touchpoints**:
- `docs/specs/14-web-app.md` — frontend architecture
- `docs/specs/16-deployment-and-networking.md` — self-hosting rules;
  "never bind to public interface" + self-hosters must not depend
  on third-party runtime calls
- If a CSP is documented (check `docs/specs/09-security*.md`), update
  it to drop the Google Fonts allowances.

## Implementation notes

**Preferred approach**: add the `@fontsource-variable/*` (for
Fraunces variable) and `@fontsource/*` (for Inter Tight and JetBrains
Mono) npm packages, import them from a small central `fonts.css` (or
similar in the existing styles directory), and drop the `<link>` /
preconnect tags from both `index.html` files.

Concrete packages:

- `@fontsource-variable/fraunces` (covers the 9..144 opsz variable
  axis, all weights in one file)
- `@fontsource/inter-tight` — import 400/500/600/700
- `@fontsource/jetbrains-mono` — import 400/500

These ship WOFF2 + a CSS file with the `@font-face` blocks; Vite
bundles them into hashed assets under `/assets/…` automatically, so
we get long-cache headers for free.

**Apply to both SPAs** — `mocks/web/` and `app/web/`. Keep the
imports in one file (`src/styles/fonts.css` or the existing entry
style) so future font changes are a single edit.

**Don't introduce**:
- Manual WOFF2 files in `public/` (harder to version-pin, breaks on
  upstream font update).
- A shared workspace package just for fonts — premature; revisit
  only if/when a third surface (site) also needs the same stack.
- `@fontsource` subsets we don't use (e.g. latin-ext, cyrillic) —
  import only the `latin` subset unless a product reason demands
  otherwise.

**Site (`site/web/`)** is out of scope for this task — Astro with
different SEO needs. File a follow-up if `site/` also references
external fonts.

**CSP**: if the app emits a CSP header (check
`app/api/middleware.py` or wherever security headers live), remove
`https://fonts.googleapis.com` from `style-src` and
`https://fonts.gstatic.com` from `font-src` in the same change so
we don't ship dead allowlist entries.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `mocks/web/index.html` no longer references
  `fonts.googleapis.com` or `fonts.gstatic.com` (no `<link>`, no
  `preconnect`).
- [ ] `app/web/index.html` no longer references
  `fonts.googleapis.com` or `fonts.gstatic.com`.
- [ ] `mocks/web/package.json` and `app/web/package.json` each have
  `@fontsource-variable/fraunces`, `@fontsource/inter-tight`,
  `@fontsource/jetbrains-mono` as deps.
- [ ] A single `fonts.css` (or equivalent) in each SPA imports only
  the weights/subsets in use, and is imported from the entry CSS.
- [ ] `docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build`
  rebuilds cleanly; served pages show Fraunces / Inter Tight /
  JetBrains Mono with no network requests to `fonts.googleapis.com`
  or `fonts.gstatic.com`.
- [ ] Built `/assets/*.woff2` files are reachable under
  `http://127.0.0.1:8100/…` (same-origin) and return 200.
- [ ] If a CSP is currently configured, it no longer references the
  Google Fonts domains.
- [ ] Visual smoke: headings (Fraunces), body (Inter Tight),
  monospace (JetBrains Mono) render identically to before.
- [ ] `grep -rE 'fonts\\.(googleapis|gstatic)\\.com' mocks/ app/`
  returns nothing.

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
# Type + lint (no regressions)
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml exec app-api ruff check .
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml exec web-dev pnpm -C /app typecheck || true

# Grep assertion (should print nothing)
rg -n 'fonts\\.(googleapis|gstatic)\\.com' mocks/ app/
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. `docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build`
2. `curl -sI http://127.0.0.1:8100/ | head -5` — expect 200.
3. In a browser DevTools Network tab, load
   \`http://127.0.0.1:8100/\`; filter by \"fonts.\" — expect **zero
   requests** to googleapis/gstatic. Fonts should load from
   \`/assets/*.woff2\` same-origin.
4. Dev-login + open one page per surface (worker, manager) to confirm
   typography is unchanged (no FOIT, no wrong fallback).
5. Run once on a machine with \`fonts.googleapis.com\` blocked at the
   hosts level (or in DevTools → Block request domain) — app must
   still render with the right fonts.

## Notes for the implementer

- Keep the commit atomic. One commit per SPA is fine; don't roll in
  unrelated tweaks.
- Update \`docs/specs/14-web-app.md\` if it currently claims fonts
  come from Google.
- If you find additional external recurring assets while doing this
  (CDN scripts, icons, analytics loaders), **don't** expand scope —
  file a separate Beads task.

title: feat(web/public): /signup SPA page + ceremony (email → verify → passkey)
description:
## Problem / goal

Self-serve signup is a first-class §03 flow and the backend
endpoints are live (POST /api/v1/signup/start, /verify,
/passkey/start, /passkey/finish). The SPA has no /signup route —
app/web/src/App.tsx only wires /login, /recover, /recover/enroll,
/accept, /guest. Per spec §03 "Self-serve signup", §"Redemption",
a visitor must be able to enter email + desired slug, click the
verification link, and enrol a passkey without operator help.
Today the full flow requires a backend call via curl.

## Technical context

- Backend: app/api/v1/auth/signup.py — all four endpoints wired.
- Frontend scaffolding to reuse:
  - app/web/src/auth/passkey-register.ts — decodeCreationOptions
    + encodeAttestation (factor if signup needs them too);
  - app/web/src/pages/public/EnrollPage.tsx — close analogue of
    the verify-then-register flow;
  - RecoverPage.tsx — email-entry shape + enumeration-guarded
    202 confirmation view.
- Spec: docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md §"Self-serve signup",
  docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md §"Workspace selector".

## Implementation notes

- Three SPA pages + routes:
  1. /signup — email + desired_slug + (optional) captcha token.
     Submits POST /signup/start. 202 → confirmation view;
     409 slug_taken / slug_reserved / slug_homoglyph_collision /
     slug_in_grace_period → inline error with the suggested
     alternative where provided.
  2. /signup/verify?token=... — consumes GET /signup/verify. On
     success forwards to /signup/enroll?signup_session_id=...
     (or the signup-finish equivalent exposed by the backend).
  3. /signup/enroll — runs navigator.credentials.create() against
     /signup/passkey/start, submits to /signup/passkey/finish,
     refreshes /auth/me, lands the user on their workspace.

- Respect capabilities.settings.signup_enabled: hit /healthz or a
  dedicated capabilities probe to render the "closed on this
  deployment" view when the server has the flag off.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] /signup (bare-host) is reachable without auth and drives the
      three-step flow end-to-end to landing on /dashboard.
- [ ] Capability-off rendering shows a clear "signups are closed"
      view referencing the operator.
- [ ] Captcha token (Turnstile) is forwarded on /signup/start when
      capabilities.settings.captcha_required is true.
- [ ] Unit tests + Playwright smoke cover the golden path.

## Test plan

- docker compose exec web-dev npm run test + a Playwright recording
  committed under .playwright-mcp/.

title: test(web/public): EnrollPage + passkey-register ceremony coverage
description:

app/web/src/auth/passkey-register.ts without dedicated tests. The

title: test(api/auth/me): unit + integration coverage for GET /api/v1/auth/me

title: test(auth): test_db_failure_is_swallowed_without_raising flakes under redaction filter
description:
## Problem

After commit `a365eef` (cd-szxw/cd-0txr) unified the alembic-caplog fix for `test_passkey_login.py::TestAutoRevokeCredentialFreshUow::test_db_failure_is_swallowed_without_raising`, the test now fails with a new root cause when run after the rest of `tests/unit` (in file order, something earlier in the suite installs the log-redaction filter on `app.api.v1.auth.passkey`).

The captured log message reads:

    clone-detected auto-revoke failed on fresh UoW for id='***REDACTED***'

…but the test asserts `cred_b64 in rec.getMessage()`. With the credential id redacted, the assertion always fails.

## Reproduction

    uv run pytest tests/unit -x -q
    # fails at tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_login.py::TestAutoRevokeCredentialFreshUow::test_db_failure_is_swallowed_without_raising

    uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_passkey_login.py -q
    # passes (file-local run — redaction filter never installed)

## Options

1. Drop / opt-out the redaction filter for the logger under test.
2. Assert on a different invariant (e.g. record exists at ERROR level with the expected `reason` kwarg) instead of the raw credential id.
3. Include a stable-but-non-PII correlation token (e.g. hash prefix of the credential id) in the log line and match on that.

Option 2 is lightest and preserves the privacy property (credential ids are PII-adjacent; redacting them in logs is the right production behaviour — the test should not need to see them).

## Surface

Surfaced by cd-c0a5 (self-review of cd-c4nn) `pytest tests/unit -x -q` run; the flake predates cd-c4nn and is unrelated to the envelope fix.

title: chore(api): retire HTTPException(detail=dict) idiom in favour of DomainError subclasses
description:
## Problem / goal

Per the module docstring on `app/api/errors.py:27-32`:

> Migration is deliberately out of scope: the existing routers still
> raise `LookupError` / `ValueError` / `PermissionError` and the
> default Starlette/FastAPI handlers continue to translate native
> `HTTPException`. Follow-up Beads tasks convert service modules
> to the `DomainError` hierarchy; landing the seam first lets
> those conversions ship as small, reviewable diffs.

`cd-c4nn` fixed the symptom (dict-shaped `HTTPException(detail=...)`
now spreads into the problem+json envelope instead of being `str()`'d
into a Python-repr string). This task completes the migration by
retiring the `HTTPException(detail={"error": "..."})` idiom across
routers in favour of raising a typed `DomainError` subclass at the
service layer.

## Affected files

At the time `cd-c4nn` landed, `rg -n 'detail=\{' app/api/` hit:

- `app/api/deps.py` (1 call site)
- `app/api/v1/auth/tokens.py` (2 call sites)
- `app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py` (multiple)
- `app/api/v1/auth/signup.py` (multiple)
- `app/api/v1/auth/magic.py` (multiple)
- `app/api/v1/auth/recovery.py` (multiple)
- `app/api/v1/auth/invite.py` (multiple)
- `app/api/v1/time.py` (multiple)
- `app/api/v1/users.py` (multiple)

## Why now

The `cd-c4nn` handler fix is backwards-compatible — every existing
call site still produces a correct envelope on the wire — but the
idiom carries three costs:

1. Every symbol is named inline as a magic string rather than
   traced to a typed exception class. Harder to grep, harder to
   refactor, no compile-time verification.
2. The `app/api/errors.py` module docstring describes the typed
   path as the intended target; leaving the router-side idiom in
   place is an active invitation for new code to copy the wrong
   pattern.
3. Service logic stays transport-agnostic when it raises
   `DomainError` subclasses (a CLI or worker sees the same
   exception hierarchy a web request does — spec §12 + §13).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every `HTTPException(detail={"error": ...})` in `app/api/`
      converts to a raise of a `DomainError` subclass
      (`NotFound`, `Conflict`, `Validation`, `Forbidden`,
      `Unauthorized`, `RateLimited`, or a new domain-specific
      subclass when the existing catalogue doesn't fit).
- [ ] `rg -n 'detail=\{' app/api/` returns no hits.
- [ ] Integration tests that previously asserted on the envelope
      body continue to pass — the fix landed in `cd-c4nn` makes
      both shapes produce the same wire body, so this is a
      purely internal cleanup.
- [ ] Every new `DomainError` subclass has a spec §12 `type`
      URI and an entry in `_DOMAIN_STATUS_MAP`.

## Refs

- `cd-c4nn` — handler fix that unblocked this (closed).
- `cd-waq3` — original RFC 7807 seam landing (closed).
- `app/api/errors.py:27-32` — docstring that calls this out as
  the follow-up shape.

title: Mocks SPA: swap public-page anchors to react-router Link under /mocks/ prefix
description:
Four <a href=/...> anchors in the mocks SPA bypass React Router basename (set to /mocks in dev-stack compose). Clicking them at dev.crew.day/mocks/ causes a full-page reload to the bare origin, landing on the production app/web SPA instead of staying in the mocks preview.

Locations:
- mocks/web/src/pages/public/LoginPage.tsx:22 (/recover)
- mocks/web/src/pages/public/LoginPage.tsx:26 (/accept/demo-abc123)
- mocks/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx:64 (/login)
- mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ApiTokensPage.tsx:336 (/me)

Swap each to react-router-dom <Link>. Link respects BrowserRouter's basename automatically, so both standalone (base=/) and dev-stack (base=/mocks/) resolve from the same source. Pre-existing issue surfaced by cd-sgpl's broadened scope; deferred from cd-0zdk self-review because the anchors weren't in that diff's scope.
acceptance_criteria: At http://127.0.0.1:8100/mocks/login, clicking each of the four anchors stays under /mocks/. Standalone mocks (localhost:5173 without base) still navigates normally.

title: Self-review: chore(api/auth): trim aspirational invalid_scopes / scopes_required strings
description:
## Problem / goal
Auto-fixing self-review of the changes made under cd-u3e3. Catch bugs,
missing pieces, and unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-u3e3** (main task must be complete first).

## How to run
Run \`/selfreview\` in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from cd-u3e3.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Specific checks:
  - **Grep completeness**: \`rg -n 'invalid_scopes|scopes_required' app/\` should return zero hits. Check \`tests/\` too — if tests reference these symbols, they were testing something the code doesn't do; update or delete.
  - **Docstring honesty**: the replacement text should not overclaim. Read it cold — does it accurately describe what a 422 looks like today?
  - **§03 cross-check**: if §03 still implies scope validation the code doesn't do, surface as a follow-up observation.
  - **§12 alignment**: the /auth/tokens block should need no changes. Confirm.
- Run the quality gates after fixing.
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

See \`.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All BUGS from the self-review fixed
- [ ] All MISSING pieces completed
- [ ] All RISKY items mitigated (or justified in a task comment)
- [ ] Linter, formatter, type checker, affected tests all pass
- [ ] Fixes committed and pushed

title: chore(api/auth): trim aspirational invalid_scopes / scopes_required strings from token-router docstring
description:
## Problem / goal

\`app/api/v1/auth/tokens.py:29-31\` (module docstring) advertises:

\`\`\`
422 invalid_scopes / scopes_required — malformed or empty
  scopes body.
\`\`\`

**No code path raises either symbol.** The router accepts an empty dict (\`scopes: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)\`) and \"malformed\" scopes flow through FastAPI's default Pydantic validator (emits \`type: validation\` problem+json via the §12 RFC 7807 seam, not a typed symbolic error).

Two options:

### Option (a) — trim (recommended for this task)

Edit the docstring to remove the two lines. Replace with a note saying empty scopes are OK and unknown scope keys are not validated at the API layer.

### Option (b) — implement (defer to a separate Beads task if wanted)

Validate scope keys against §05's action catalog at mint time; raise \`invalid_scopes\` for unknown keys. Require non-empty scopes for scoped tokens (§03 says delegated tokens MAY have empty, scoped tokens SHOULD NOT). This is a meaningful feature expansion with §05 dependency — not this task's scope.

## Technical context

- **Key files**: \`app/api/v1/auth/tokens.py\` module docstring (lines 1-40), \`MintTokenBody\` (~lines 102-115)
- **Related**: cd-wd78 / cd-8p4y reconciled §12 / §03 to reflect code reality; this task brings the docstring in line.
- **§03 reference**: \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md:606\` says delegated tokens have empty scopes; the scoped-token \"Creation\" section should say scoped tokens get at least one scope key. If §03 implies a non-empty-scopes invariant that the code doesn't enforce, that's additional drift — flag for Option (b) follow-up if found.

## Implementation notes

- Keep it trimmed + honest. Don't invent text that the code then has to grow into.
- If you find §03 implies validation the code doesn't do, either:
  1. Trim §03 too (match code), or
  2. Leave §03 alone and file Option (b) as a follow-up Beads task.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Token-router module docstring no longer advertises \`invalid_scopes\` / \`scopes_required\` symbols
- [ ] Replacement text matches actual behaviour: empty scopes OK, unknown scope keys not validated at the API layer (validation would be a separate feature)
- [ ] \`rg -n 'invalid_scopes|scopes_required' app/\` returns zero hits in production code (the symbols are gone from everywhere)
- [ ] §12 \`/auth/tokens\` block still accurate (should not require changes — cd-wd78 already aligned it to reality)
- [ ] Integration tests at \`tests/integration/auth/test_tokens_pg.py\` still pass

## Test plan

### Automated

\`\`\`bash
rg -n 'invalid_scopes|scopes_required' app/
# Expect: zero hits in production code
pytest tests/integration/auth/test_tokens_pg.py -x -q
# Expect: still green
ruff check app/api/v1/auth/tokens.py
\`\`\`

### Manual

1. Read the trimmed docstring top-to-bottom; confirm it's honest and operator-useful.
2. Read §03 \"Creation\" section; confirm no remaining prose implies a validation the code doesn't do (if any, file Option (b)).

## Refs

- Surfaced by cd-8p4y (selfreview of cd-wd78)
- Companion: cd-wd78 (reconciled §12), cd-geob (§03 body example), cd-c4nn (error envelope)
- Option (b) follow-up (not filed automatically): \"feat(api/tokens): validate scope keys against §05 action catalog + require non-empty for scoped tokens\"

title: sec(auth/passkey): delete challenge row on verification failure in complete_signup / complete_recovery
description:
**Context**

cd-qx1f shipped single-use-on-failure for `webauthn_challenge` rows at the HTTP router surface (`/auth/passkey/register/finish`, `/auth/passkey/signup/register/finish`, `/auth/passkey/login/finish`). The new `_delete_challenge_fresh_uow` helper runs inside the router's `except` block and lands the delete on a fresh UoW so the row is burned even when the primary UoW rolls back on the raise.

**Gap**

`app/auth/signup.py::complete_signup` and `app/auth/recovery.py::complete_recovery` call `passkey.register_finish` / `passkey.register_finish_signup` **internally** (not through the HTTP router), inside their own UoWs. On any verification failure during these flows, the primary UoW rolls back, and the challenge row survives until the 10-minute TTL — the same replay window cd-qx1f closed on the router surface.

**Why it was deferred**

cd-qx1f scoped the fix to the router layer so the helper lived next to its siblings (`_write_login_audit_fresh_uow`, `_invalidate_for_credential_fresh_uow`). The signup / recovery service seams warrant their own small refactor — probably a `passkey.burn_challenge_on_failure(challenge_id)` helper exported from `app/auth/passkey.py` so both the router and the service callers can reuse the same fresh-UoW pattern.

**What to do**

- Move `_delete_challenge_fresh_uow` (or a sibling) into `app/auth/passkey.py` as a public helper — the HTTP router is not the only caller.
- Wire it into `complete_signup` (around `passkey.register_finish_signup`) and `complete_recovery` (around `passkey.register_finish`).
- Extend tests in `tests/unit/auth/test_signup.py` and `tests/unit/auth/test_recovery.py` to assert the challenge row is gone after any failure shape (verifier rejection, subject mismatch, expired).
- Update `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"WebAuthn specifics" bullet to drop the implicit "router-only" caveat — the invariant becomes universal across every finish path.

**Acceptance**

- [ ] Challenge row deleted on failure when signup/recovery internal callers invoke register_finish*
- [ ] Shared helper between router + service callers (no duplication)
- [ ] Tests cover InvalidRegistration, ChallengeSubjectMismatch, ChallengeExpired for both service paths
- [ ] Spec §03 bullet updated — invariant is universal

**References**

- `app/auth/passkey.py::register_finish`, `register_finish_signup`
- `app/auth/signup.py::complete_signup` (line ~924)
- `app/auth/recovery.py::complete_recovery` (line ~965)
- `app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py::_delete_challenge_fresh_uow` (cd-qx1f helper)
- `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"WebAuthn specifics"
- Parent: cd-qx1f

title: docs(spec): reconcile recovery endpoint name + payload between §03 and app/api/v1/auth/recovery.py
description:
## Problem

Spec §03 and the implementation disagree on the self-service
recovery endpoint:

| Dimension | Spec §03 "Request" | app/api/v1/auth/recovery.py |
|-----------|--------------------|-----------------------------|
| Path      | POST /api/v1/auth/recover/start | POST /api/v1/recover/passkey/request |
| Body      | {email, break_glass_code?} | {email} |
| Response  | {status: \"sent_if_exists\"} | {status: \"accepted\"} |

The mismatch means anyone reading the spec to scope a downstream
change (ADR, docs, SDK) wires the wrong URL and the wrong field.

## Scope

Decide per-field which side is canon, then align both:

1. **Path.** Current implementation is `/recover/passkey/request`
   — already live, has tests, covered by the router factory. The
   spec's `/auth/recover/start` was written before implementation.
   Probably cheapest to update the spec; but if the team prefers
   the /auth/ prefix for routing clarity, a server-side rename is
   also valid.
2. **Payload.** `break_glass_code?` must be accepted server-side
   for the step-up branch to work (see sibling Beads task on the
   body extension). Either way, spec + code must match.
3. **Response envelope.** Spec says `sent_if_exists`, code says
   `accepted`. Pick one; the client only reads the 2xx boundary
   anyway, so the string is internal-facing.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Spec §03 and router implementation use identical endpoint
      path, body shape, and response envelope.
- [ ] `/update-openapi` run after the reconciliation.
- [ ] Client (`app/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx`) and CLI
      follow the canon name; a grep for the loser of each field
      returns zero hits outside this migration's changelog.

## Context

- Spec: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Self-service
  lost-device recovery" → "Request" section.
- Router: `app/api/v1/auth/recovery.py` (`build_recovery_router`).
- Client: `app/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx`.
- Found during /selfreview of cd-gids; related to the
  break_glass_code acceptance task.

title: web/login: role-check ?next=/admin/... before bouncing already-signed-in users
description:
## Context

When an already-authenticated **non-admin** user lands on `/login?next=/admin/some-path`, `LoginPage.tsx` honors the `?next=` param verbatim and mounts `<Navigate to='/admin/some-path'>`. The `/admin` routes sit behind `<RequireAuth>` (satisfied) but **outside** `<WorkspaceGate>` — no role gate. If the admin shell does not enforce its own role check, a phished worker could be driven to admin surfaces they shouldn't see. If it does, they would land on an unexpected surface with no context for why.

## Scope

- Audit `AdminLayout` / all `/admin/*` routes for a role guard (deployment-admin scope, §14).
- If a guard exists: a worker hitting `/login?next=/admin/...` gets stuck on a permission-denied screen they didn't ask for. Either the LoginPage should filter `next` against the user's role *before* bouncing, or the admin shell should redirect non-admins back to `RoleHome`.
- If no guard exists: that is the bug; add one in `AdminLayout` and a test.

## Acceptance

- Unit test in `app/web/src/pages/public/LoginPage.test.tsx` (or a new `AdminLayout.test.tsx`) covers: signed-in **worker** hitting `/login?next=/admin/dashboard` ends up on `/today` (role home), not `/admin/dashboard`.
- The admin shell enforces the `admin` grant-role via `<RequireAuth role='admin'>` or equivalent.

## Refs

- Uncovered during selfreview of cd-4z54 (LoginPage, cd-qbp5).
- Spec: docs/specs/14 (§Admin shell), docs/specs/03 (role semantics).
- sanitizeNext already blocks off-origin, so this is strictly same-origin privilege-escalation phishing surface.

title: feat(web/auth): map remaining WebAuthn DOMException names to typed errors
description:
`app/web/src/auth/passkey.ts`'s `mapNavigatorError` covers:
- NotAllowedError, AbortError → PasskeyCancelledError
- SecurityError, NotSupportedError, InvalidStateError → PasskeyUnsupportedError

The WebAuthn spec also lists `TimeoutError`, `UnknownError`,
`ConstraintError`, `NetworkError` (plus a few transport-level variants).
These currently fall through to a generic `Error` — the LoginPage will
render a vague "could not sign in" instead of a helpful retry or
fallback hint.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `TimeoutError` → new `PasskeyTimeoutError` (retry hint)
- [ ] `UnknownError` / `NetworkError` → new `PasskeyTransientError`
      (retry-with-backoff hint)
- [ ] `ConstraintError` → `PasskeyUnsupportedError` (credential
      constraint not satisfied)
- [ ] `PasskeyUnsupportedError` carries a discriminant `kind` field so
      LoginPage can branch on cause without `instanceof` fans
- [ ] Unit tests in `passkey.test.ts` cover each mapping

Discovered during cd-iack self-review of cd-kc7u.

title: feat(web/auth): WorkspaceGate — surface admin deep-link for deployment admins with no workspaces
description:
A deployment admin (is_deployment_admin via /me) who has zero workspace
grants currently lands on the `<WorkspaceGate>` "No workspaces yet"
empty state with only a Sign-Out button. They should have an
`/admin/dashboard` affordance.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] WorkspaceGate's empty state reads `user.is_deployment_admin` (or
      whatever the /me payload exposes)
- [ ] When true, render a primary "Open admin console" button that
      navigates to `/admin/dashboard`
- [ ] Test coverage for both branches (admin + non-admin) of the empty
      state

## Context

This is purely UX polish; the current behaviour is safe (no surface is
exposed by mistake), just dead-end. Admins can still manually type
`/admin/dashboard` — the WorkspaceGate only gates the
`<Route element={<WorkspaceGate />}>` branch, and `/admin/*` sits inside
`<RequireAuth>` but outside `<WorkspaceGate>` (see
`app/web/src/App.tsx` routing tree).

Discovered during cd-iack self-review of cd-kc7u.

title: spec: idempotency §12 — reconcile stored fields + name Idempotency-Replay header
description:
## Problem / goal
Spec \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Idempotency" currently says:

> Server stores \`(token_id, idempotency_key) -> (status, body_hash)\` for 24h.

The implementation (cd-z6fk) stores \`(token_id, key, status, body_hash, body, headers, created_at)\` because a replay must re-emit the exact response bytes — a bare \`(status, body_hash)\` cannot. The spec wording is too terse to be literally implementable. Reconcile the two.

Second drift: \`app/api/middleware/idempotency.py\` emits an \`Idempotency-Replay: true\` response header on every cache hit. The header isn't named in spec §12; implementation's module docstring previously claimed it was. The docstring has been fixed to flag it as an unspecified convention, but the spec should pick it up.

Third drift (smaller): §12 does not mention the 5xx skip policy. cd-4p2p's self-review added the guard that 5xx responses are NOT cached (so transient failures can be retried); this matches Stripe / AWS behaviour and is the only safe default. Worth spelling out in the spec.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Spec §"Idempotency" updated to say: stores \`(token_id, key, status, body, response_headers, created_at)\` keyed on \`(token_id, key)\` uniqueness; TTL 24 h. Body hash is derived on insert to detect \"same key, different body\" conflicts (not a separate stored field).
- [ ] Spec names the \`Idempotency-Replay: true\` response header, with the retry-vs-replay client contract.
- [ ] Spec notes the 5xx skip policy: server-error responses are not persisted — the retry re-executes the handler. 2xx/4xx responses are terminal and replay.
- [ ] Spec §"02-domain-model.md" gains an \`idempotency_key\` entity section matching the implementation (columns, indexes, TTL, no FK on token_id).

## Context
- Implementation: \`app/api/middleware/idempotency.py\`, \`app/adapters/db/ops/models.py::IdempotencyKey\`.
- Tests: \`tests/unit/api/middleware/test_idempotency.py\`, \`tests/integration/api/test_idempotency_replay.py\`.

## Priority
P2 — no runtime impact, but the spec is the source of truth and should not diverge. Folds naturally into the next pass of \`/audit-spec\`.

title: feat(tasks/generator): dialect-aware INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
description:
## Problem / goal
The scheduler worker's INSERT path
(``app/worker/tasks/generator.py``) does a SELECT pre-flight to
report ``skipped_duplicate`` counts and relies on the partial
unique index
``uq_occurrence_schedule_scheduled_for_local`` (cd-22e migration)
as a backstop. If two ticks race (two APScheduler instances, a
manual CLI tick overlapping the hourly tick), both will pass the
SELECT and one will hit ``IntegrityError`` at flush time, rolling
the whole tick back.

Today this is a documented constraint ("one tick at a time per
workspace") rather than an enforced one. Harden the INSERT with a
dialect-aware ``ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`` so the race is handled at
the DB layer and the worker reports the racing row as a duplicate
instead of raising.

## Technical context
- Files: ``app/worker/tasks/generator.py`` (the ``session.add`` +
  ``session.flush`` pair).
- Spec: ``docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md`` §"Generation"
  step 3.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] SQLite and Postgres both use the dialect-native
      ``INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`` form against
      ``uq_occurrence_schedule_scheduled_for_local``.
- [ ] Integration test that simulates two concurrent ticks (or
      pre-inserts the duplicate row between the SELECT and the
      INSERT) keeps the row count stable without raising.
- [ ] ``GenerationReport.skipped_duplicate`` counts the racing row.

title: feat(tasks/generator): unit-aware closure filter
description:
## Problem / goal
The generator's `_covered_by_closure` helper
(app/worker/tasks/generator.py) currently treats every
`PropertyClosure` as property-wide — the spec
(docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md §"property_closure" /
§"Interaction with assignment and generation") asks for a
``closure.unit_id`` match against the task's ``unit_id`` when the
closure is unit-scoped.

Not a bug today because the v1 ``property_closure`` model does not
yet carry ``unit_id`` — cd-8u5 lands that column alongside the
richer unit surface. Once the column exists, the generator must
filter closures by ``unit_id`` too.

## Technical context
- File: ``app/worker/tasks/generator.py`` (``_covered_by_closure``).
- Spec: ``docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md``
  §"property_closure" + §"Interaction with assignment and generation".
- Depends on cd-8u5 (unit column on ``property_closure``).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Generator skips the candidate only when either
      ``closure.unit_id IS NULL`` or
      ``closure.unit_id == task.unit_id``.
- [ ] Unit test covers both the property-wide and unit-specific
      closure shapes.

## Depends on
- cd-8u5 (property/unit domain service).

title: Migrate tenancy middleware _not_found() to RFC 7807 envelope
description:
Context
-------
cd-waq3 migrated FastAPI-handled errors to application/problem+json
(RFC 7807). The tenancy middleware 404 path at
app/tenancy/middleware.py:278 (_not_found()) still returns
application/json with {"error": "not_found", "detail": null}.

This creates two live wire contracts for 404s: workspace-path 404s
(unknown slug, cross-tenant miss) return the old envelope, while
FastAPI-handled 404s (bare /api/..., /admin/api/...) return RFC 7807.
test_unknown_slug_scoped_context_path_404s at
tests/integration/api/test_openapi_shape.py:169 currently asserts the
old shape.

Acceptance
----------
- _not_found() builds the problem+json envelope via
  app.api.errors.problem_response (or a shared helper).
- Response has Content-Type application/problem+json and body matches
  the §12 shape.
- Spec §15 byte-identical + timing-identical (±5 ms) requirement is
  preserved: both "unknown slug" and "exists-elsewhere" branches
  still route through the same helper.
- test_unknown_slug_scoped_context_path_404s and any sibling tests
  updated to assert the new shape.

Out of scope
------------
- Rewriting §15 spec (already done as part of cd-waq3 self-review).

title: test(auth): alembic fileConfig in migrate_once fixture disables app.auth.session_cookie logger for subsequent caplog tests
description:
## Problem / goal
Pre-existing test-ordering flake: when an integration-auth test that
triggers the session-scoped `migrate_once` fixture (tests/integration/conftest.py:148)
runs BEFORE a unit test that asserts against caplog records from
`app.auth.session_cookie`, the unit test fails.

Concrete reproducer:
```
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_abuse_guards.py tests/unit/auth/test_session.py::TestBuildSessionCookie::test_secure_false_falls_back_to_dev_cookie -q
```

Root cause: Alembic's `migrations/env.py:40` calls
`logging.config.fileConfig(config.config_file_name)` without
`disable_existing_loggers=False`. The default-True flips
`disabled=True` on every logger not named in `[loggers]` in
`alembic.ini` (root/sqlalchemy/alembic). Every downstream
`app.auth.session_cookie._log.warning(...)` call is then silently
dropped because its logger is disabled, and `caplog` sees no records.

## Technical context
- `tests/unit/authz/test_enforce.py:792` already documents this pattern
  and works around it locally by restoring `propagate`/`disabled` on
  the specific logger inside the test fixture.
- The proper fix is in `migrations/env.py`: call
  `fileConfig(config.config_file_name, disable_existing_loggers=False)`.
  This keeps alembic's handler-attach behaviour while leaving every
  application logger's state untouched.

## Implementation notes
- Change `migrations/env.py` to pass
  `disable_existing_loggers=False` to `fileConfig`.
- Add a regression test under `tests/integration/test_logging.py` that
  imports a logger (e.g. `app.auth.session_cookie`), triggers the
  `migrate_once` fixture, and asserts `logger.disabled is False`
  afterwards.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Integration fixtures that trigger alembic fileConfig no longer
      flip `disabled=True` on application loggers
- [ ] The reproducer command above passes
- [ ] A regression test guards against recurrence

## Test plan
```
uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_abuse_guards.py tests/unit/auth/test_session.py::TestBuildSessionCookie::test_secure_false_falls_back_to_dev_cookie -q
uv run pytest tests/ -q
```
notes: Additional reproducer on pristine main (no cd-7huk changes needed): `tests/unit/test_main.py::TestSpaCatchAll::test_missing_dist_logs_warning` also fails alongside `test_secure_false_falls_back_to_dev_cookie` under the same root cause. Both assert caplog.records for loggers (`app.auth.session_cookie`, `app.api.static`) that alembic's fileConfig has disabled via `disable_existing_loggers=True`.

title: pkg: add include-package-data for app/abuse/data/ so disposable_domains.txt ships in a wheel
description: app/abuse/data/disposable_domains.txt is loaded via importlib.resources.files('app.abuse').joinpath('data', 'disposable_domains.txt'). The pyproject.toml has no [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] include-package-data or package-data stanza, so a future wheel build would omit the file and break the signup abuse guard at runtime. Needs a pyproject.toml add (or MANIFEST.in) before any wheel-publishing step.

title: ops(ci): refresh-disposable-domains.yml weekly GitHub Action to regenerate app/abuse/data/disposable_domains.txt
description:
## Problem / goal
Spec §15 "Self-serve abuse mitigations" — *"The bundled list MUST be
refreshed weekly by a CI job `refresh-disposable-domains.yml` that
pulls from the pinned upstream dataset, regenerates the in-repo file
(`app/abuse/data/disposable_domains.txt`), and opens a PR."*

cd-7huk landed the file move + the `# generated YYYY-MM-DD` pin + a
unit test that fails when the pin is > 30 days old. A GitHub Action
stub to do the periodic refresh was skipped because the original
task's AC was (a)/(b)/(c) only. This task adds the workflow.

## Technical context
- **Blocked by**: nothing — just needs someone to write the workflow.
- **Parent**: cd-7huk landed everything else around the file.
- **Upstream dataset**:
  `https://github.com/disposable-email-domains/disposable-email-domains`
  (MIT) — pin a release tag rather than tracking main.

## Implementation notes
- Add `.github/workflows/refresh-disposable-domains.yml`:
  - Cron schedule: weekly (Monday 06:00 UTC is a reasonable slot).
  - Pull the pinned upstream dataset, overwrite
    `app/abuse/data/disposable_domains.txt` (preserving the leading
    `# generated YYYY-MM-DD` pin as the first line, bumped to today's
    UTC date).
  - Run the unit test suite to confirm the pin parses.
  - Open a PR with `gh pr create` if there is any diff.
- **Do not** auto-merge — an operator reviews each refresh in case
  the upstream list suddenly grows or shrinks substantially.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `.github/workflows/refresh-disposable-domains.yml` exists
- [ ] Workflow runs weekly and opens a PR when the list is stale
- [ ] PR carries a bumped `# generated YYYY-MM-DD` pin on line 1
- [ ] `tests/unit/abuse/test_disposable_domains.py` passes against
      the refreshed file

## Test plan
Workflow-only — the existing unit test is the CI gate that catches
drift. Manual trigger (`workflow_dispatch`) should also be supported
so operators can refresh on-demand.

title: docs: reconcile shift vs booking terminology across specs
description: docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md §Bookings says bookings replace clock-in/clock-out, but cd-whl added a lightweight shift entity (app/domain/time/shifts.py) for explicit clocking. The relationship between shifts and the pay pipeline (booking_billing, pay_period_entry) is unspecified. Needs: (1) decide whether shifts feed into pay or stay complementary; (2) update §09, §02, and §12 to say so clearly; (3) possibly add shift to the Mermaid ER diagram in §02.

title: DRY: consolidate _resolve_version into one module
description: app/api/factory.py and app/api/health.py each define their own _resolve_version() function with the same importlib.metadata lookup. The sentinel strings differ ('0.0.0+unknown' vs 'unknown') which prevents a trivial dedupe, but a shared helper in app/util/ (or app/api/health.py re-exported) would unify the logic. Non-blocking; pick up when either module needs a touch.

title: docs: task_template missing updated_at per §02 convention
description: The §02 domain-model spec states 'created_at, updated_at on every row' as a blanket convention. The task_template ORM model (app/adapters/db/tasks/models.py) and the cd-0tg CRUD service (app/domain/tasks/templates.py) only carry created_at — updated_at is absent from the model and from every migration. Either add the column (migration needed) and have _apply_body set it on every write, or document the deliberate exception in §06 / the model docstring. Discovered during the cd-0tg documentation review.

title: Integration-suite db_session SAVEPOINT isolation quirk on pysqlite
description: The shared db_session fixture in tests/integration/conftest.py uses a SAVEPOINT pattern to isolate each test's writes. On SQLite (pysqlite), INSERTs committed through the outer transaction leak to sibling tests on RELEASE SAVEPOINT due to a long-standing driver isolation-level quirk. Evidence: tests/integration/test_health.py (cd-leif) had to introduce a clean_heartbeat fixture that DELETEs every worker_heartbeat row around each case. This works but is brittle — every new cross-tenant ops table will hit the same quirk.

title: feat(identity/invite): work_engagement + user_work_roles invite sub-payloads
description: Spec §03 lists work_engagement + user_work_roles as optional fields on POST /api/v1/users/invite. Phase 1 cd-jpa omits them because the backing tables do not exist yet. Add InviteRequest fields + wire them through membership.invite + _activate_invite once the tables land.

title: feat(identity/invite): fold /invite/complete into passkey-finish callback
description: Today the new-user branch of accept is a two-stage handoff: /invite/accept burns the magic-link nonce, then the SPA runs the passkey ceremony, then the SPA POSTs /invite/complete {invite_id}. complete_invite gates on passkey-presence so a guessed invite_id cannot activate grants, but the cleaner design is for register_finish_signup (or a dedicated register_finish_invite) to invoke complete_invite directly as part of the passkey-finish transaction. Drops a public endpoint + tightens the atomicity of user.enrolled. Referenced as cd-nzxs in app/api/v1/auth/invite.py.

title: feat(identity/invite): property-scoped + organization-scoped invite grants
description: cd-jpa v1 only accepts scope_kind='workspace' on invites; 'property' and 'organization' scopes are rejected with InviteBodyInvalid. Adding them needs the property_workspace cross-check already in role_grants + the organization table (not in Phase 1). Drop the restriction in _VALID_SCOPE_KINDS and extend the validator when both blockers clear. Referenced as cd-nzxq/cd-nzxr in app/domain/identity/membership.py and app/api/v1/users.py.

title: feat(identity/membership): nightly invite TTL sweeper
description:
`invite.state` flips `pending → expired` when `expires_at` lapses. Today nothing sweeps: a stale pending row sits forever.

## Technical context
cd-jpa lands the 24h TTL + a state column but not the sweeper. `signup_gc` already prunes stale `signup_attempt` rows via `app.auth.signup.prune_stale_signups` — follow the same shape.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `prune_stale_invites` iterates invites where `state='pending' AND expires_at <= now` and flips them to `expired`
- [ ] Runs on interval via APScheduler alongside the existing signup_gc
- [ ] Tests cover the flip

title: feat(identity/membership): binding_org_id scope_transfer on invite grants
description:
Accept `binding_org_id` on invite grant entries so a client-surface invite can land against an organization scope once the `organization` table exists.

## Technical context
cd-jpa accepts `scope_kind='workspace'` only (Phase 1). The spec §03 mentions `{scope_kind, scope_id, grant_role, binding_org_id?}` as the full shape; the mock at `mocks/app/main.py::api_users_invite` accepts `property` and `organization` scopes but the production `role_grant` CHECK constraint + `_VALID_SCOPE_KINDS` in `app.domain.identity.membership` only permit workspace for now.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `organization` table lives in the schema
- [ ] Invite body accepts `scope_kind='property' | 'organization'` with the right cross-references
- [ ] Property-scope grants cross-check `property_workspace` like `app.domain.identity.role_grants` does today

title: feat(identity/membership): work_engagement + user_work_role on invite
description:
Extend `POST /w/<slug>/api/v1/users/invite` to accept `work_engagement` + `user_work_roles` sub-payloads once their backing tables land.

## Technical context
cd-jpa deferred these because the `work_engagement` / `user_work_role` tables are not part of Phase 1. The mock at `mocks/app/main.py::api_users_invite` already accepts them, but the production domain service + model only land the grants + permission-group memberships.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Invite body accepts `work_engagement` + `user_work_roles` alongside `grants`
- [ ] Sub-payloads land on the `invite` row as pending rows
- [ ] On accept, every downstream row (`work_engagement`, `user_work_role`) lands atomically
- [ ] Tests cover the expanded payload

title: docs(inventory): reconcile unit + reason enum vocabularies between models and specs
description:
## Problem / goal
Original drift: the v1 inventory schema in cd-bxt clamped narrower
enum vocabularies than §08 described. Between that gap and the
2026-04-22 /specs revision, the specs widened considerably.

**Status as of 2026-04-22**: resolved on the docs side. Specs (§02,
§08, §20 glossary) are now internally consistent and describe the
final vocabulary. The schema arm is implemented by **cd-n6ga**
(edit-in-place of the unshipped 2026-04-20 inventory migration —
nothing has been deployed, so there is no legacy data to migrate).
This task is retained as the historical record of the drift; no
further work is required once cd-n6ga lands.

## Drift summary (for the historical record)
### Drift A — ``inventory_item.unit``
- **v1 impl**: closed enum ``ea | l | kg | m | pkg | box | other``
- **Final spec (2026-04-22)**: **free-text** ``TEXT`` column;
  UI surfaces a pre-filled suggestion picker with custom-entry
  fallback. No enum, no CHECK constraint, no migration when a new
  unit is needed.

### Drift B — ``inventory_movement.reason``
- **v1 impl**: ``receive | issue | adjust | consume``
- **Final spec**: ``restock | consume | produce | waste | theft |
  loss | found | returned_to_vendor | transfer_in | transfer_out |
  audit_correction | adjust``

### Drift C — quantity type
- **v1 impl**: ``Numeric(18, 3)``
- **Final spec**: ``Numeric(14, 4)`` with round-half-even (§08
  'Precision contract')

### Drift D — cascade-setting rename
- **v1 impl**: ``inventory.consume_on_task``
- **Final spec**: ``inventory.apply_on_task`` (no legacy fallback —
  the old key never shipped)

## Resolution path
All four drifts are folded into **cd-n6ga**, which rewrites the
unshipped 2026-04-20 migration to emit the final schema directly.
There is no data migration (no production rows exist) and no
legacy-key fallback in the resolver. Close this task when cd-n6ga
lands.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] cd-n6ga lands, implementing the final schema per the drifts
      summarised above
- [ ] Module docstrings in ``app/adapters/db/inventory/`` no longer
      carry the 'slightly different taxonomy' caveat
- [ ] This task is closed as ``resolved-by cd-n6ga``

Refs:
- cd-n6ga (implementation)
- cd-bxt (the original v1 slice)
- §08 revision 2026-04-22

title: docs(specs): reconcile instructions §02/§07 with cd-bce implementation
description:
## Problem / goal

The cd-bce implementation of `instruction` + `instruction_version`
has landed and diverges from specs §02 and §07 in three deliberate
ways. The code was pinned by cd-bce's task brief — the specs need
the reconciling edit pass now.

## Divergences to resolve

1. **Scope enum width**.
   - Impl (cd-bce): `scope_kind ∈ {template, property, area, asset, stay, role, workspace}` (7 values).
   - Spec §07 line 13+40: `scope ∈ {global, property, area}` (3 values) + separate `property_id` / `area_id` columns.
   - Resolution owner must decide: widen §07 to match the 7-value implementation (task-brief preference), or document a projection layer. Either way, §07 must end consistent with the DB.

2. **Child table naming**.
   - Impl (cd-bce): `instruction_version`, column `Instruction.current_version_id`.
   - Spec §02 line 274–275, §07: `instruction_revision`, `current_revision_id`.
   - Resolution: rename the spec to `version` (impl's choice), or document `version` as the DB-layer synonym for `revision`.

3. **`instruction_link` deferral**.
   - Spec §02 line 275 lists `instruction_link` as a canonical table in §07. Not implemented under cd-bce (explicitly out of scope — captured in the module docstring). Spec should flag it as deferred to a follow-up task rather than implying it's current.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"instruction" / §"instruction_version" match the landed schema (column names, scope enum).
- [ ] `docs/specs/07-instructions-kb.md` scope-enum section agrees with the 7-value taxonomy or documents the projection.
- [ ] `instruction_link` deferral noted in §02 + §07 with a pointer to the follow-up task.
- [ ] `/audit-spec` pass after the edits confirms code-spec parity.

## Technical context

- Landed under cd-bce.
- Flagged by selfreview cd-rtc.
- Implementation: `app/adapters/db/instructions/models.py` — see the module docstring for the rationale a spec edit should absorb.

## Out of scope

- Any runtime / migration change (schema is fine as shipped).
- Widening the domain CRUD surface to cover the deferred `instruction_link` table — that is cd-oyq + follow-ups.

title: docs(specs): update §12 and OpenAPI with /recover/passkey/* endpoints
description:
## Problem / goal
cd-atj landed \`POST /recover/passkey/request\`, \`GET /recover/passkey/verify\`, \`POST /recover/passkey/start\`, and \`POST /recover/passkey/finish\` under the v1 API. The spec (\`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\`) and OpenAPI catalog need to reflect the new surface.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` — endpoint table.
- **OpenAPI**: run \`/update-openapi\` once the router is mounted (\`cd-ika7\`).
- **Router**: \`app/api/v1/auth/recovery.py\` (already has OpenAPI-friendly response_model + summary on every route).

## Implementation notes
- Blocked on cd-ika7 (app factory) — once routers are wired, the OpenAPI generator picks them up.
- Add a row in §12's endpoint table for each of the four routes + error symbols (rate_limited, invalid_token, recovery_session_not_found, too_many_passkeys, invalid_registration, challenge_expired, challenge_consumed_or_unknown).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] §12 endpoint table lists all four recovery routes
- [ ] OpenAPI schema includes them after \`/update-openapi\`
- [ ] Error-code catalog (§12) mentions recovery-specific symbols

title: security(tenancy): gate Phase-0 test-workspace stub behind CREWDAY_PHASE0_STUB env flag
description:
The WorkspaceContextMiddleware Phase-0 stub (cd-7y4) unconditionally trusts X-Test-Workspace-Id and X-Test-Actor-Id headers, granting manager-level WorkspaceContext to any caller who knows a valid slug. There is no feature flag or env var guarding this code path.

Risk: if the binary is deployed before cd-9il lands, any attacker with a valid slug can supply X-Test-Workspace-Id and receive manager-level access to any workspace_id they choose.

Fix: add CREWDAY_PHASE0_STUB=false (default) to app/config.py Settings. In WorkspaceContextMiddleware.dispatch, wrap the Phase-0 block in 'if settings.phase0_stub_enabled:' and 404 when the flag is off. Tests set the flag explicitly. cd-9il removes the block entirely.

Reference: app/tenancy/middleware.py lines 263-285, reviewed in cd-7y4 reviewer pass.
close_reason: Phase-0 stub fully gated behind CREWDAY_PHASE0_STUB env flag. Default False in app/config.py:135; app/tenancy/middleware.py:767 checks the flag BEFORE reading any X-Test-* header; test_stub_header_ignored_when_flag_off asserts the header is inert when the flag is off. cd-9il removes the attack surface by running the real resolver.

title: docs(specs): reconcile LastOwnerMember HTTP status — 409 code vs 422 would_orphan_owners_group
description:
## Problem

Two surfaces disagree on the error shape for refusing to remove the
last `owners@<ws>` member:

- **Spec** `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"permission_group"
  §"Invariants":

    > A write that would leave it empty fails with 422 `error =
    > "would_orphan_owners_group"`.

- **Implementation** (cd-ckr) raises a `LastOwnerMember` domain
  exception. The router will map it to `409 Conflict` with
  `error = "last_owner_member"` (the symbolic reason is already
  baked into `write_member_remove_rejected_audit`'s `diff.reason`).

Same mismatch applies to user archival ("sole active owners member
across the deployment" — same `would_orphan_owners_group` code in
§02).

## Decision needed

Pick one and update the other side:

1. **409 + `last_owner_member`** — code stays, spec updates.
   Rationale: the operation is a conflict with current state (there
   IS an owner, but only one), not a validation error.
2. **422 + `would_orphan_owners_group`** — spec stays, code renames
   the exception + response shape. Rationale: business-rule
   violation; matches the spec's existing "sole active member"
   archival code.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Decision recorded in a short ADR or inline in the spec
- [ ] If (1): `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"permission_group"
      §"Invariants" + §"users.archived_at" updated to
      `409 last_owner_member`
- [ ] If (2): `app.domain.identity.permission_groups.LastOwnerMember`
      renamed (e.g. `WouldOrphanOwnersGroup`), `reason` field in
      `write_member_remove_rejected_audit` + audit payloads updated,
      router status-code mapping updated, affected tests updated
- [ ] `docs/specs/12-rest-api.md` reflects the chosen code
- [ ] OpenAPI regenerated via `/update-openapi`

## Refs

- `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"permission_group" §"Invariants"
- `app/domain/identity/permission_groups.py` `LastOwnerMember` docstring
- Flagged in cd-ckr self-review (cd-qaj)

title: docs(spec/15): update §Self-serve abuse mitigations — disposable-domain 400 vs actual 422, disposable_domains.txt path
description: Spec §15 says a blocked disposable domain returns '400 disposable_email'. Implementation returns 422. The coder's code comment in DisposableEmail docstring acknowledges the drift ('spec §15 stipulates 400; the app's broader convention uses 422'). Documenter should update §15 to say 422 and reference the convention. Additionally the spec references app/abuse/data/disposable_domains.txt but the file landed at app/auth/disposable_domains.txt — spec freshness path needs updating.

title: refactor(auth): deduplicate _hash_with_pepper across magic_link / signup / api/v1/auth/signup
description: _hash_with_pepper is defined identically in app/auth/magic_link.py:289, app/auth/signup.py:278, and used via import in app/api/v1/auth/signup.py:60. There are also two copies of _HKDF_PURPOSE='magic-link' (magic_link.py:172 and signup.py:132). All three should share one definition in a shared app/auth/_hashing.py helper. Noted as triplication by coder in cd-055 self-summary. Blocked until cd-7huk lands the shared throttle refactor to keep the diff atomic.
close_reason: Deduplicated in cd-055 selfreview (commit e67be90): hash_with_pepper extracted to app/auth/_hashing.py; magic_link.py + signup.py + api/v1/auth/signup.py all import from there

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: Addressed in cd-055: shared helper landed at app/auth/_hashing.hash_with_pepper; magic_link.py and signup.py now re-export the alias, api/v1/auth/signup.py calls the canonical helper. Ready to close.

title: sec(auth/signup): Turnstile verify should use SSRF guard not bare httpx
description: app/auth/signup_abuse._verify_turnstile uses httpx.post directly. Per spec §15 §SSRF, any server-side fetch must go through app/net/fetch_guard.py once that module lands (cd-7huk or a parallel task). The URL is pinned (not user-supplied) so this is low severity today, but the pattern should be consistent. Tag for wiring when fetch_guard ships.

title: Update spec/12 REST API for passkey/start + passkey/finish signup routes
description: spec/12-rest-api.md line 335-336 only lists POST /api/v1/signup/start and POST /api/v1/signup/verify for the self-serve signup surface. The implementation (app/api/v1/auth/signup.py, cd-8m4 + cd-3i5) also exposes POST /api/v1/signup/passkey/start and POST /api/v1/signup/passkey/finish. Additionally, the spec/12 description of /signup/verify ('magic link redeem + WS provisioning') is now stale — verify only flips verified_at; workspace provisioning moved to passkey/finish. The documenter should update spec/12 with the full 4-route signup surface and accurate descriptions.

title: spec/03 homoglyph guard: document 5→s substitution and rn→m mid-word false positives
description: app/tenancy/slug.py adds a 5→s digit substitution in _DIGIT_SUBSTITUTIONS that the spec (§03 'Homoglyph guard') does not mention — the spec only says '0 → o, 1 → l'. The code comment attributes it to '§03 spec' but the spec text doesn't include it. Additionally, the rn→m pair substitution is global (applies mid-word), meaning 'corner' folds to 'comer' — this is a false-positive rejection path if 'comer' is taken. Both items should be explicitly addressed in spec §03 to give the implementation a canonical reference. Note: 5→s is a conservative default that may be intentional; the spec just needs to say so.

title: Handle duplicate start_signup (same email+slug within TTL) without 500
description: In app/auth/signup.py, start_signup blindly INSERTs a SignupAttempt row. If called twice with the same (email, desired_slug) within the 15-minute TTL, the UNIQUE constraint uq_signup_attempt_email_slug fires an IntegrityError that propagates as an unhandled 500 to the client. The model docstring in app/adapters/db/identity/models.py line 404-407 describes the intended behavior: 'treated as a duplicate — the caller may get a fresh magic link for the same attempt row, but the row itself does not duplicate.' Implementation should: (1) SELECT the existing pending attempt, (2) send a fresh magic link against it, (3) return 202 — making the endpoint idempotent. No test covers this case. Related: cd-3i5.
close_reason: Fixed as part of cd-3i5 selfreview (commit 4a09880): start_signup now idempotent on (email_lower, desired_slug) retry, invalidates prior nonce, refreshes TTL

title: Fix slug_taken 409 to include suggested_alternative per spec §03
description: spec/03-auth-and-tokens.md §"Self-serve signup" says '409 slug_taken returns a suggested alternative'. The /signup/start handler currently returns only {"error": "slug_taken"}. Need to generate a suggested slug (e.g. appending a suffix like -2, -3) and include it in the response body as {"error": "slug_taken", "suggested_alternative": "<slug>-2"}. The module docstring in app/auth/signup.py line 27-28 acknowledges this requirement but it wasn't implemented. This is a spec gap that affects UI/UX quality on the signup flow.
close_reason: Fixed as part of cd-3i5 selfreview (commit 4a09880): SlugTaken now carries suggested_alternative; router serialises it in 409 body

title: doc(spec): add /auth/magic/request + /auth/magic/consume to docs/specs/12-rest-api.md
description: /auth/magic/request and /auth/magic/consume are implemented in app/api/v1/auth/magic.py but §12 only lists the old /auth/magic/send name (admin re-issue path) and doesn't document the new self-service request endpoint. The documenter agent should update §12 with these two new bare-host endpoints after cd-4zz is merged.

title: test(auth/magic-link): HTTP-layer router tests (TestClient)
description: There are no FastAPI TestClient tests for build_magic_router. The 429-vs-202 enumeration guard semantics at the HTTP layer are untested: does RateLimited produce 429 from /request (it does, but the docstring says 202 always)? Does ConsumeLockout NOT advance the fail counter (it doesn't, correct)? A test_magic_router.py with a pytest fixture using starlette.testclient.TestClient would cover these. Non-blocking nit from cd-4zz review.

title: doc(auth/magic-link): add max_length to MagicConsumeBody.token
description: MagicConsumeBody.token has no max_length constraint. While itsdangerous fails fast on a garbage token (~1ms for 1MB), defence-in-depth suggests adding max_length=2048 or similar. Nit from cd-4zz review.

description:

See \`.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md\`.

## Specific things to double-check

  response shape indistinguishable from the happy path.
- \"Mailpit receives zero messages\" uses a bounded, deterministic
  wait, not a race-prone \`sleep(1)\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All BUGS from the self-review fixed
- [ ] All MISSING pieces completed

title: test(auth/recover): verify recovery email in Mailpit as part of cd-gids flow
description:
## Problem / goal
**cd-gids** builds the RecoverPage (email → magic link recovery). Once
Mailpit is live in dev (cd-5ypd), extend the recover flow's test
coverage so an agent can prove the recovery email actually leaves the
app, lands in Mailpit, and the link recovers the session.

## Technical context
- **Depends on: cd-5ypd** (Mailpit), **cd-gids** (recover flow exists),
  **cd-o62m** (reusable Mailpit helper).
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` §\"Recovery\" and
  \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\`.

## Implementation notes
- Reuse the polling helper from cd-o62m.
- One happy-path (known email → recovery link works) and one abuse
  path (unknown email → no disclosure via response timing / Mailpit
  contents). The abuse path must assert Mailpit receives **no**
  message within a bounded wait, not just \"we got a 200\".
- Skip cleanly when the dev stack isn't running.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New test asserts the recovery email arrives in Mailpit with the
      expected subject, renders a working consume URL, and restores a
      session.
- [ ] Unknown-email test asserts Mailpit receives zero messages inside
      the wait window AND the HTTP response looks identical to the
      happy path (no timing / content oracle).
- [ ] \`pytest\` against the test passes with the dev stack up; skips
      cleanly when down.
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` / \`ruff check\` pass.

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build
pytest tests/integration/auth/test_recover_mailpit.py -xvs
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. Open \`http://127.0.0.1:8100/recover\`, submit a known email.
2. Confirm the recovery email in Mailpit; click the link.
3. Repeat with an unknown email; confirm Mailpit stays empty and the
   UI response is indistinguishable from the happy path.
