title: test(auth/signup): verify signup email in Mailpit as part of cd-3i5 flow
description:
## Problem / goal
**cd-3i5** adds the self-serve signup flow (email → magic → passkey →
slug). Its acceptance today only proves the token path end-to-end via
in-process mocks. Once Mailpit is live in the dev stack (cd-5ypd),
extend the signup test coverage so an agent can prove: signup form
submission → real SMTP dispatch → message visible in Mailpit →
consuming the Mailpit link completes onboarding.

This is the follow-up the user asked for: agents implementing
email-driven flows must verify the actual email, not just the domain
side-effect.

## Technical context
- **Depends on: cd-5ypd** (Mailpit in dev compose), **cd-3i5** (signup
  flow exists), **cd-o62m** (reusable Mailpit helper).
- **Key files**:
  - Signup endpoints and email template (added under cd-3i5).
  - \`tests/integration/auth/\` — signup tests live here; add a
    Mailpit-backed variant rather than replacing existing coverage.
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` §\"Signup\" and
  \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\`.

## Implementation notes
- Reuse the polling helper added in cd-o62m — do **not** reinvent it.
- Pick **one** representative happy-path signup (valid email, valid
  slug, passkey registration stub) and one failure path (unclaimed
  magic-link nonce after TTL). Don't re-test every edge case from
  cd-3i5; target the email leg specifically.
- Skip cleanly when the dev stack isn't running.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New test asserts the signup-initiated email arrives in Mailpit
      with the expected subject, renders a working consume URL, and
      completes the session.
- [ ] Test is additive — does **not** delete or weaken existing signup
      unit/integration tests.
- [ ] \`pytest\` against the test passes with the dev stack up; skips
      cleanly when down.
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` / \`ruff check\` pass.

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build
pytest tests/integration/auth/test_signup_mailpit.py -xvs
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. Open \`http://127.0.0.1:8100\` and sign up as a fresh email.
2. Inspect \`http://127.0.0.1:8025\` — confirm the signup email with
   the expected subject + link.
3. Click through; confirm passkey prompt + slug confirmation.

title: chore: ChallengeAlreadyConsumed is defined and exported but never raised
description: In app/auth/passkey.py, ChallengeAlreadyConsumed is defined as a LookupError subclass, documented as a 'reserved alias of ChallengeNotFound for clarity at call sites', exported in __all__, and included in _DomainError, but is never actually raised anywhere in the codebase. On replay, the challenge row has been deleted so _load_challenge raises ChallengeNotFound, not ChallengeAlreadyConsumed. Either raise it explicitly in register_finish after detecting a consumed challenge (would require flagging rows instead of deleting), or remove it and update the docstring/test to reflect that replay always surfaces as ChallengeNotFound.

title: chore: CHECK constraint name in WebAuthnChallenge model has redundant prefix
description: In app/adapters/db/identity/models.py, WebAuthnChallenge.__table_args__ declares the check constraint as name='ck_webauthn_challenge_subject'. The NAMING_CONVENTION template is 'ck_%(table_name)s_%(constraint_name)s', so the resolved name becomes ck_webauthn_challenge_ck_webauthn_challenge_subject (double prefix). The migration uses op.f() which also resolves to the doubled name, so model and migration are consistent and there is no functional bug. The fix is to rename in the model to name='subject' so the convention produces ck_webauthn_challenge_subject, and update the migration to match. Requires a batch-alter migration for SQLite.

title: fix: passkey_credential.aaguid column missing from DB model (spec §03 Privacy)
description: The spec §03 'Privacy' says we store: credential ID, public key, sign count, AAGUID, transport hints, nickname, last_used_at. The aaguid field is present on PasskeyCredentialRef (value object) and in the audit diff, but is missing from the PasskeyCredential SQLAlchemy model and the identity migration (cd-w92). A future migration (new to this task or a follow-up) should add the aaguid String column and populate it on register_finish. Pre-dates cd-8m4 (established in cd-w92) but cd-8m4 is the first place that touches the credential write path.

title: feat(capabilities): probe webauthn_configured via make_relying_party at boot
description:
## Context
cd-gox scaffolded the WebAuthn RP config module (app/auth/webauthn.py). The task prompt mentioned an optional v1 nicety — adding a `features.webauthn_configured: bool` field to `Features` in app/capabilities.py, set by `_probe_features(settings)` after `make_relying_party(settings)` succeeds — deferred to keep cd-gox tightly scoped.

## What to add
- New bool field `webauthn_configured` on `app.capabilities.Features`.
- In `_probe_features`, wrap `make_relying_party(settings)` in try/except `RelyingPartyMisconfigured` to set the flag. A mismatched rp_id shouldn't crash capability boot — the flag going false will feed the health endpoint (§16) and the admin UI (§14).
- Update existing capability tests in tests/unit/test_capabilities.py with a case asserting the flag is true for a valid public_url / dev fallback, false when misconfigured.

## Out of scope
- Any bind-guard integration (that lives separately in cd-… — consult /resume-work).
- Flipping the flag at runtime (mutations to `Features` are forbidden; re-probe happens on process restart).

title: test(util): make test_flipped_byte_raises_token_invalid deterministic
description:
## Problem / goal
`tests/unit/test_util_hashids.py::TestTampering::test_flipped_byte_raises_token_invalid`
is flaky. It flips the last character of a signed token and asserts
`TokenInvalid` is raised, but a single flipped character doesn't always
produce an invalid signature — depending on which character was swapped
and which bit flipped, the resulting token is still cryptographically
valid. Observed failure: ran full suite once, hit `DID NOT RAISE`; re-ran
the single test and it passed.

## Technical context
- File: `tests/unit/test_util_hashids.py` TestTampering::test_flipped_byte_raises_token_invalid
- Root cause: flipping one URL-safe base64 character `last -> 'A' or 'B'`
  sometimes produces another valid signature output.
- The intent of the test is "tampering is rejected", so the fix is to
  make the tamper deterministic:
  - Option 1: flip every character in a loop, asserting at least one
    flip triggers `TokenInvalid`.
  - Option 2: use `hypothesis` to sample flip positions.
  - Option 3: deliberately mutate the signature bytes (decode the base64
    tail, xor one byte, re-encode).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Test passes deterministically across 100+ runs
- [ ] Test still proves tampering is detected end-to-end

## Test plan
```bash
for i in {1..100}; do uv run pytest tests/unit/test_util_hashids.py::TestTampering::test_flipped_byte_raises_token_invalid -xvs || break; done
```

title: docs(specs): reconcile task vs occurrence vocabulary with cd-chd v1 slice
description: Spec §02 uses 'task' and 'task_checklist_item' where cd-chd's v1 db slice uses 'occurrence' and 'checklist_item'. State machine is narrower too ('pending|in_progress|done|skipped|approved' vs §06's richer set). Documenter should either update §02/§06 to match the slice, or widen the slice. Discovered during cd-7r6 selfreview.

title: feat(tenancy/orm_filter): support scope-through-join for tables without workspace_id
description: unit, area, property_closure belong to a property via property_id; workspace boundary is enforced via property_workspace. Today these tables are left unregistered because the filter auto-injects workspace_id which doesn't exist on them. Extend orm_filter.py to either (a) walk the query's joins to verify property_workspace is joined with a workspace_id predicate, or (b) raise TenantFilterMissing when a bare SELECT hits an unregistered scope-through-join table. Discovered during cd-i6u.

title: fix(tenancy): SQLAlchemy event dispatch quirk — listener registered on sessionmaker but Session.dispatch empty
description:
## Observed behaviour
Building multiple sessionmaker instances in sequence, each bound to the same engine and each receiving an install_tenant_filter() call, can leave the third+ session with an EMPTY session.dispatch.do_orm_execute list even though event.contains(factory, 'do_orm_execute', hook) returns True. The hook silently does not fire and tenant-filter enforcement is bypassed.

## Repro (as of 2026-04-19, SQLAlchemy 2.x)
Reliable with three consecutive tests in tests/integration/test_audit_writer.py (commit → rollback → filter-missing) when filtered_factory is function-scoped and rebuilt each test. The third test's fresh factory reports event.contains==True but list(session.dispatch.do_orm_execute) == [] and the SELECT runs unfiltered.

## Workaround shipped
Module-scoped filtered_factory in the integration test (one sessionmaker per file). Idempotence of install_tenant_filter keeps this safe. Documented inline.

## Risk
Any future production code path that builds multiple sessionmakers on the same engine chain (e.g. per-request sessionmaker for request isolation) could silently drop the tenant filter. The _default_sessionmaker_ is singleton so today's runtime is unaffected, but this is a latent trap.

## Possible root causes
1. SQLAlchemy 2.x sessionmaker event propagation uses a weak-reference dispatch cache that the third factory's creation evicts.
2. Interaction with the join_transaction_mode='create_savepoint' sessionmaker in tests/integration/conftest.py::db_session — even though we don't use that fixture in the filtered test, the factory still goes through the Session class dispatch.
3. SQLAlchemy's _Session.dispatch lazily composes from the sessionmaker and Session-class dispatches; some ordering produces an empty composition.

## Follow-ups
1. Minimal repro outside pytest (pure script).
2. Report upstream if confirmed.
3. Consider moving tenant filter installation to the Session class directly (one-time, at import of app.adapters.db.session) so per-sessionmaker churn cannot drop it.

## Related
- cd-3yhd — sibling heisenbug where get_final_froms() on a cold ORM Join silently loses a side.
- cd-52k / cd-ehf — consumers of this filter.

title: feat(tenancy/orm_filter): recursively rewrite scoped tables inside WHERE subqueries
description:
## Problem / goal
The current tenant filter only walks `stmt.get_final_froms()`. Scoped
tables embedded in WHERE-clause subqueries, scalar subqueries,
correlated selects, or UNION legs (e.g.
`select(Task).where(Task.property_id.in_(select(Property.id)))`)
pass through unchanged: no `TenantFilterMissing` raised, no filter
injected. The outer target name is what the walker sees; references
buried deeper are invisible.

## Current mitigation
- The module docstring of `app/tenancy/orm_filter.py` documents the
  hole (top-level FROM only).
- A regression test
  (`test_where_clause_subquery_scoped_table_is_currently_unfiltered`)
  nails down the current behaviour so any unintentional change trips.
- Developers writing these shapes must thread `workspace_id` into the
  inner query by hand until this lands.

## Proposal
Recursively descend the compiled statement (e.g. via
`sqlalchemy.sql.visitors.traverse`) and either (a) rewrite each
scoped inner reference with a `WHERE alias.workspace_id = :ctx`
predicate, or (b) fail closed when no safe rewrite is possible. Pair
with the existing `tenant_agnostic()` escape for legitimate
cross-tenant analytics.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `select(Bar).where(Bar.id.in_(select(Foo.id)))` injects a
      `foo.workspace_id` filter (Foo scoped).
- [ ] Same without a ctx raises `TenantFilterMissing(table='foo')`.
- [ ] Correlated subquery (`Bar.any(...)`) and UNION legs handled.
- [ ] `tenant_agnostic()` still bypasses.
- [ ] No regressions in current 21 orm_filter tests.

## Spec
`docs/specs/01-architecture.md` §"Tenant filter enforcement".

## Dependencies
Discovered during self-review of cd-52k (see cd-3cq).

title: test(util/hashids): replace flaky random-byte flip with deterministic tamper
description: tests/unit/test_util_hashids.py::test_flipped_byte_raises_token_invalid can pass even on a flipped byte because the random flip may re-sign a valid signature. Replace the random.randint byte flip with a deterministic mutation of the signature portion so the test is 100% reliable. Discovered during cd-n0l selfreview.

title: docs(specs/16): reconcile env-var naming with app.config
description: §16 "Environment variables" table uses `CREWDAY_BIND` (host:port), `CREWDAY_STORAGE`, `CREWDAY_WORKER=inprocess`; `app/config.py` (landed under cd-ec1) uses split `CREWDAY_BIND_HOST` + `CREWDAY_BIND_PORT`, `CREWDAY_STORAGE_BACKEND`, `CREWDAY_WORKER=internal`. Pick one source of truth and update the other: either rewrite the §16 table to match the code (simpler: split bind already avoids a parsing step at the bind-guard layer) or rename the config fields if the spec wins. Also reconcile `.env.example` with whichever wins. Discovered during cd-zyo selfreview of cd-ec1. Refs: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Environment variables", `app/config.py`, `.env.example`.

title: test(load): locust scenarios — 100 workers clocking in + 10k occurrences listing
description:
## Problem / goal
Load tests catch performance regressions before customers do. The
§17 budget is "100 workers clocking in at 08:00" and "Task list
render for a property with 100k tasks history". This task lands a
Locust harness with both scenarios plus the budgets from §00.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"Load" (Locust
  scenarios, pass criteria from §00),
  §"Quality gates" → release gates ("Full Locust load" before
  release).

## Implementation notes
- New `tests/load/` with `locustfile.py`:
  - `ClockingInUser`: simulates 100 workers calling `POST
    /shifts/clock-in` over a 60 s window (Poisson distribution
    around 08:00); steady-state 1 RPS thereafter for 5 min.
  - `OccurrencesListUser`: 10 concurrent users paginating through
    a 10k-task history with keyset pagination; asserts p95 latency
    against the §00 budget.
  - `TurnoverDayUser`: 5 simultaneous task completions with photo
    uploads (5 MiB JPEGs) — the upload-throughput stress.
- Seed harness: `scripts/seed_load.py` writes the 10k-task history
  for the listing scenario. Idempotent, deterministic.
- Targets: a locally-run dev container by default; CI nightly
  runs against an ephemeral compose stack.
- Pass criteria asserted in `--exit-code-on-error 1` mode so a
  budget breach fails CI.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All three scenarios run locally against
      `docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml`
- [ ] Clocking-in scenario: 100 users, p95 < 500 ms over the
      60 s burst window
- [ ] Occurrences-list scenario: p95 < 250 ms on 10k history
- [ ] Turnover-day scenario completes 5 photo uploads each within
      the §00 upload latency budget
- [ ] Nightly CI job runs the full set with seeded data; failure
      blocks the next release

## Test plan
```bash
uv run python scripts/seed_load.py
locust -f tests/load/locustfile.py --headless -u 100 -r 10 \
  --run-time 5m --host http://127.0.0.1:8100
```

title: feat(i18n): pybabel extraction + per-locale JSON bundles + CI gate
description:
## Problem / goal
Without an extraction pipeline and a CI gate, translation keys
silently rot: a developer adds `t("foo.bar")` and the catalog
never learns about it until a translator complains. This task
wires `pybabel extract → update → compile`, generates per-locale
JSON bundles for the SPA, and fails CI on missing keys.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/18-i18n.md` §"Library choices" (`pybabel`),
  §"Rules followed in v1" (rule 1: tests assert no raw strings).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/17-testing-quality.md` §"Quality gates"
  (the new `i18n-keys` gate fits next to `cli-parity`).

## Implementation notes
- Add `babel` config (`babel.cfg`) listing extractor rules for
  Python (`gettext`-style) + Jinja2 (`{% trans %}`) +
  TypeScript/TSX (`t("…")` regex extractor).
- New `scripts/i18n_extract.py` runs `pybabel extract → update →
  compile` for every locale and additionally writes the SPA's JSON
  bundle (`mocks/web/src/i18n/bundles/<locale>.json`) by walking
  the compiled `.mo` files.
- Locales shipped: `en-US` (default, fully populated), `fr` and
  `es` placeholders (en-US strings copied verbatim — translators
  fill them later). `qps-ploc` is generated, not hand-translated.
- CI gate `i18n-keys`: runs the extractor against the working tree,
  diffs against the committed catalog, fails on a non-empty diff
  (same posture as `cli-parity` / `surface-parity`). The fix is to
  run `make i18n-extract` and commit.
- Pre-commit hook surfaces stale catalogs locally.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `make i18n-extract` regenerates `.po`, `.mo`, and JSON
      bundles deterministically
- [ ] CI gate fails on a missing key; PR diff shows what to add
- [ ] `fr` and `es` placeholder bundles are present and load
      without runtime error (UI shows English text under each)
- [ ] `qps-ploc` bundle is generated automatically; `?locale=
      qps-ploc` shows accented + inflated copy
- [ ] Pre-commit hook detects the same staleness condition

## Test plan
```bash
make i18n-extract
git diff --exit-code app/i18n/locales mocks/web/src/i18n/bundles
uv run pytest tests/unit/i18n/test_extraction.py -xvs
```

title: feat(i18n): translation store + t(key, params) helper + locale negotiation
description:
## Problem / goal
v1 ships English-only, but every user-facing string must already
flow through a translation seam so that adding fr/es/pt-BR/tl-PH
later is a translation job, not a code refactor. This task lands
the seam: a Babel-backed catalog on the server, a typed `t(key,
params)` helper on the SPA, and a single locale-resolution chain
(per-user pref → `Accept-Language` → workspace default → `en-US`)
shared by both.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/18-i18n.md` §"Library choices" (Babel +
  `lingua` + Jinja2 i18n + ICU MessageFormat), §"Rules followed
  in v1" (every string via `_()`/`t()`, no concat, plural via
  `ngettext`), §"Locale resolution chains" (UI / document /
  notification chains), §"Currency formatting" (Babel
  `format_currency` server, `Intl.NumberFormat` browser).
- **Bounded contexts**: every domain context owns its own message
  keys; the `messaging` and `notifications` contexts (Phase 10)
  consume the server-side renderer for emails.

## Implementation notes
- Server: add `babel` + Jinja2 `i18n` extension. New
  `app/i18n/__init__.py` with `get_locale(request) -> str`,
  `t(key, **params)`, `format_date / format_currency / format_number`
  helpers. Catalog under `app/i18n/locales/<bcp47>/LC_MESSAGES/`.
- Email templates use `{% trans %}` blocks; render path takes a
  `locale` parameter (notification chain) frozen at queue time.
- Web: new `mocks/web/src/i18n/` module with `t(key, params)` hook
  + provider that reads the JSON bundle for the negotiated locale.
  Resolves locale from `users.preferred_locale` (when wired) →
  `navigator.languages` → workspace default → `en-US`.
- Pseudolocale `qps-ploc` (English with accents + 30% inflation)
  enabled by `?locale=qps-ploc` for layout stress-tests.
- `LOCALIZED_ENUMS` catalog so model enum labels live in one place.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `t("login.title")` resolves to the en-US value; missing key
      raises in dev, falls back to the key in prod
- [ ] Locale negotiation chain returns the expected value for each
      precedence step (unit-tested truth table)
- [ ] Email template render produces identical output today as
      before the seam (regression baseline)
- [ ] `?locale=qps-ploc` round-trips through every shell
- [ ] No raw user-facing string survives in any new code added in
      this task (lint pass)
- [ ] Unit tests: `format_currency` matches §"Currency formatting"
      examples for fr-FR vs en-US

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/i18n/test_locale_resolution.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/unit/i18n/test_format_helpers.py -xvs
cd mocks/web && pnpm vitest run src/i18n
```

title: feat(demo): null mailer, LLM cap, demo_gc cron, abuse rate limits
description:
## Problem / goal
Demo is an unauthenticated surface; it will be scraped, fuzzed,
and used to burn LLM budget. This task wires every guardrail
required for safe public exposure: outbound integrations bound to
no-op adapters, LLM capabilities clamped to a tight budget +
free-tier models, and a 15-min `demo_gc` worker that purges
expired workspaces.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/24-demo-mode.md` §"Disabled integrations"
  table (SMTP, webhooks, iCal, passkeys, magic links, OCR, voice,
  digest, anomaly), §"LLM capability scope" (only
  `chat.manager`/`chat.employee`/`chat.compact`/translate/
  `tasks.nl_intake` run live, on `:free` models),
  §"Abuse controls" (10 mints/IP/h, 60 writes/WS/min, 10 turns/WS/min,
  5 MiB/file, 25 MiB/IP/day, 32 KiB text cap),
  §"Garbage collection" (`demo_gc` every 15 min + nightly orphan
  check).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe C →
  GC and scheduled jobs on demo" for the disabled-jobs list.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/config/demo.py`: declarative integration map (capability
  → null-impl). Asserted at boot — every disabled capability has a
  no-op adapter wired or a `501 Not Implemented in demo` route.
- Mailer null-impl writes `email_delivery` rows with
  `suppressed=true` so the UI continues to render "Email sent".
- Webhook delivery short-circuits to
  `webhook_delivery.status='suppressed_demo'`.
- LLM router (Phase 12) reads a demo-mode override pinning model
  suffixes to `:free` and applying the per-WS rolling cap
  (`$0.10/30d` default) and the global daily cap
  `CREWDAY_DEMO_GLOBAL_DAILY_USD_CAP` (default `$5`).
- New worker `demo_gc` (15-min cadence) and `demo_usage_rollup`
  (60 s) registered in the worker registry; on demo, disable
  `ical_poll`, `digest_compose`, `anomaly_detect`, `email_retry`,
  `webhook_retry`, `sync_llm_pricing` per spec.
- Edge rate-limit middleware (mint throttle, mutation rate, LLM
  rate, upload cap, payload cap) configurable via env.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every entry in §"Disabled integrations" returns `501` or the
      documented stub success
- [ ] `chat.manager` runs on a `:free` model under demo
- [ ] Hitting the per-WS LLM cap returns the §"At-cap behaviour"
      response
- [ ] `demo_gc` purges a workspace whose `expires_at < now()` and
      every `workspace_id`-keyed row + uploads dir
- [ ] Mint throttle (10/IP/h) returns 429 on the 11th attempt
- [ ] Nightly orphan-row check reports 0 orphans on a clean run
- [ ] Demo deployment never sends a real email regardless of
      `SMTP_*` env

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/demo/test_disabled_integrations.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/demo/test_abuse_controls.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/demo/test_gc.py -xvs
```

title: feat(demo): scenario fixture loader (workspace + users + schedules + stays + tasks + expenses)
description:
## Problem / goal
On first visit to a `?scenario=<key>` URL, the demo server mints a
fresh `workspaces` + `demo_workspace` row, seeds it from the YAML
fixture under `app/fixtures/demo/<key>.yml`, and binds the
visitor's signed cookie to it. Resolving relative timestamps
(`T-2d`, `stay:+7d`) keeps every demo "current".

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/24-demo-mode.md` §"Seeding" (relative
  timestamp resolver, ASCII cast, pre-bundled `_media/`),
  §"Scenarios shipped with v1" (villa-owner, rental-manager,
  housekeeper).
- **Spec**: §"Visitor lifecycle" → §"First visit" — mint flow,
  cookie binding, signed-cookie validation.
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/mocks/mock_data.py` is the canonical
  cast and should be the source of names where practical.

## Implementation notes
- New `app/fixtures/demo/`: three YAML files plus `_media/` blobs.
  Each fixture declares `default_persona`, `personas` allowlist,
  workspace metadata, properties, units, role grants, schedule
  templates, stays, in-flight tasks, and a few past expenses.
- New `app/demo/seeder.py`: `seed_workspace(scenario_key) ->
  workspace_id`, called from the demo cookie middleware on cache
  miss. Resolves relative timestamps against `now()`. Re-uses
  existing service layer (`PropertyService`, `UserService`,
  `TaskTemplateService`, `StayService`) — never raw SQL.
- Cookie minting + `__Host-crewday_demo_<scenario_id>` plumbing
  also lands in this task (one cookie per scenario, signed via
  `itsdangerous`).
- Idempotency: re-seeding the same scenario produces structurally
  identical rows (ids differ but counts and shapes match, asserted
  in tests).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All three v1 scenarios seed successfully with no errors
- [ ] Relative timestamps resolve to absolute UTC, no future
      `created_at` outside `[now-30d, now+30d]`
- [ ] Cookie minted with correct flags
      (`__Host-`, `Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=None; Partitioned`),
      single-binding, scenario-name-scoped
- [ ] Tampered or stale-key cookie treated as absent (re-mint path)
- [ ] Unit tests for relative-timestamp resolver
- [ ] Integration test: hit `/app?scenario=rental-manager` cold,
      assert workspace count delta and a coherent task list
- [ ] Loading two scenarios in two iframes yields two distinct
      cookies and two distinct workspaces

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/demo/test_seeder.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/demo/test_mint_flow.py -xvs
```

title: feat(demo): CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE env flag, capability + UI banner
description:
## Problem / goal
Demo mode is a per-deployment kill-switch (`CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE=1`)
turning a regular crewday image into the demo server at
`demo.crew.day`. Every other Phase 18 task hangs off this flag.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/24-demo-mode.md` §"Deployment shape" and
  §"Visitor lifecycle" — the env var + bootstrap guard that refuses
  to enable demo mode against a non-demo `CREWDAY_PUBLIC_URL` /
  prod `CREWDAY_DATABASE_URL`.
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe C —
  Demo deployment" for the compose snippet and DB-denylist guard.
- **Capability registry**: `docs/specs/01-architecture.md`
  §"Capability registry" — `runtime.demo_mode` is the new probe.

## Implementation notes
- Read `CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE` in the existing pydantic-settings
  config; surface as `settings.demo_mode: bool`.
- Bootstrap guard runs in `app/bootstrap.py` after config load:
  refuse start if `demo_mode and not _is_allowed_demo_url(public_url)`
  or if `database_url in CREWDAY_DEMO_DB_DENYLIST`. Exit non-zero
  with the spec-quoted error message.
- Register `runtime.demo_mode` capability so downstream code can
  probe via `caps.has("runtime.demo_mode")` instead of reading env.
- Inject a single banner component slot into every server-rendered
  shell ("Demo data — resets on inactivity") gated by the
  capability. SPA reads the value from `/api/v1/runtime/info`.
- `demo_workspace` table migration added (see §24 entity table).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE=1` + non-`.crew.day` `CREWDAY_PUBLIC_URL`
      refuses to boot with a clear error
- [ ] `CREWDAY_DEMO_MODE=1` + a URL on the `CREWDAY_DEMO_DB_DENYLIST`
      refuses to boot
- [ ] Capability registry exposes `runtime.demo_mode`
- [ ] Banner renders on every shell when demo mode is on; absent
      otherwise
- [ ] `demo_workspace` migration applies cleanly on SQLite + PG
- [ ] Unit tests cover guard truth-table; integration test boots the
      app under demo env and asserts banner + capability

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/bootstrap/test_demo_guard.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/demo/test_demo_flag_boot.py -xvs
```

title: feat(web/shared): StyleguidePage — design tokens + component library preview (dev/staging only)
description:
## Problem / goal
A static page enumerating the palette, typography, shape, motion,
and every common component (buttons, chips, cards, stat cards,
progress bars, etc.). Its primary job is **visual-regression
canary**: any change to `tokens.css` or `globals.css` shows up as
a pixel diff here first, before reaching real pages.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Design language"
  (palette, typography, shape, motion); §"Public" route block
  notes `/styleguide` is dev + staging only.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/StyleguidePage.tsx` (plus its CSS:
  `mocks/web/src/styles/styleguide.css`).
- **Components**: self-contained — only the `Camera` icon from
  `lucide-react` and ad-hoc `swatch`, `type-row`, `type-sample`
  semantic classes.
- **API endpoints**: none (static page).
- **Types**: none.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/StyleguidePage.tsx`; copy its CSS verbatim
  too.
- Mount only when `import.meta.env.DEV === true` or when an
  explicit `STAGING` env flag is set; production builds must 404
  the route per spec.
- Page is intentionally static — no queries, no SSE, no
  permission gate (it's behind the dev/staging build flag, not
  authz).
- The page must reference `tokens.css` directly (not duplicate
  hex codes) so a token change diffs visibly.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Page renders identical to mock at all breakpoints.
- [ ] Production build does not include the route (404 in prod).
- [ ] Hex codes in the page come from `tokens.css` (no
      duplication).
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes (this is the
      canary — must be exact).

## Test plan
```bash
npx playwright test tests/web/styleguide.spec.ts
# Manual: verify route 404s on a production build.
```

title: feat(web/shared): SchedulerPage — standalone weekly rota grid (read or edit per role)
description:
## Problem / goal
The standalone scheduler is the weekly rota tool: who is on, where,
when. It is mounted in the manager surface for editing and in the
client portal as a read-only view scoped to the client's properties
(first-name + role only per §15 cross-workspace visibility).



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Scheduler" — used in
  workspace setup (manager) and in `ClientLayout` as read-only;
  §15 cross-workspace visibility (`first name + work role only`
  for clients).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/SchedulerPage.tsx` (scheduler-specific
  selectors live in `mocks/web/src/styles/globals.css` — no
  dedicated `scheduler*.css` file exists; selectors move across with
  the `globals.css` copy in `cd-pjus`).
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `PageHeader`, `Loading`, `useRole`
  (from `@/context/RoleContext`).
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/scheduler/calendar?from=&to=`
  returning `SchedulerCalendarPayload`.
- **Types**: `SchedulerCalendarPayload`, `ScheduleAssignment`,
  `ScheduleRulesetSlot`, `SchedulerTaskView`, `SchedulerUserView`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/SchedulerPage.tsx`.
- Reuse the existing weekday helpers (`startOfIsoWeek`, `addDays`)
  and the `WEEKDAYS` constant from the mock.
- Role gating: `useRole()` decides edit vs read-only. In the
  client portal the server pre-redacts user names to first-name +
  role only; the client never sees a last name or contact handle.
- SSE invalidation: subscribe to `schedule.assignment.changed`,
  `schedule.ruleset.changed`, `occurrence.changed` to refresh the
  calendar query.
- Mounts under both `/w/:slug/scheduler` (manager) and
  `/w/:slug/scheduler` (client portal) — same component, different
  layout shell.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Weekly grid renders with assignments + rule-set slots
      identical to mock at desktop and tablet breakpoints.
- [ ] Manager role can drag / edit; client role cannot.
- [ ] For clients, no last names or contact info appear in any cell
      (verified by integration test).

title: feat(web/client): QuotesPage — quote list + accept / reject (approval-gated)
description:
## Problem / goal
Clients see quotes their agency has sent for work on their
properties. Acceptance triggers a work order; rejection closes the
quote. In production the click is approval-gated (§22); the mock
applies the change in-memory and emits the SSE event for parity.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Client portal shell"
  → Quotes; §22 clients-and-vendors §"Quotes" §"Acceptance is
  approval-gated".
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/client/QuotesPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Chip`, `Loading`; `formatMoney`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me`,
  `GET /api/v1/work_orders?client_org_id=<oid>`,
  `GET /api/v1/quotes?client_org_id=<oid>`,
  `POST /api/v1/quotes/{id}/accept` (server creates an
  `approval_request`),
  `POST /api/v1/quotes/{id}/reject`.
- **Types**: `Me`, `Property`, `Quote`, `WorkOrder`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/client/QuotesPage.tsx`.
- Accept POST returns either `{ status: 'pending_approval',
  approval_request_id }` (production) or `{ status: 'accepted',
  work_order_id }` (mock / approval-bypass dev mode). Render the
  pending state per the mock (a chip + waiting copy).
- SSE `quote.changed`, `approval_request.changed` invalidate the
  list; the chip updates when an approver acts.
- Preserve the mock's accept / reject confirmation copy.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Lists quotes for binding-org clients only.
- [ ] Accept enqueues an approval request and shows pending chip.
- [ ] Reject closes the quote with an audit entry.
- [ ] Permission gate: client role.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_client_quotes.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/client-quotes.spec.ts
```

description:

Clients see vendor invoices billed to one of their binding orgs —
status, amount, due date — and can upload proof-of-payment files
once an invoice is approved. Clients cannot mark anything paid
themselves; the workspace owns that flag.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Client portal shell"
  → Invoices; §22 clients-and-vendors §"Vendor invoices" +
  §"Proof of payment".
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/client/InvoicesPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Chip`, `Loading`; `formatMoney`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me`,

  `POST /api/v1/vendor_invoices/{id}/proof_of_payment` (multipart
  body, appends to `proof_of_payment_file_ids`).
- **Types**: `Me`, `Property`, `VendorInvoice`.

## Implementation notes

  `app/web/src/pages/client/InvoicesPage.tsx`.
- Upload control only renders when invoice `status == 'approved'`
  per spec — preserve the mock's inline file picker (drag + click).
- On upload success, optimistically append the file id to the

title: feat(web/client): BillableHoursPage — booking_billing rollup, redacted of pay rates
description:
## Problem / goal
The client sees the agency's hourly billing for work performed on
their properties — totals per booking, per worker, per period.
Worker pay rates are deliberately **not** shown; only the agency's
charge rate (`booking_billing.hourly_cents`).



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Client portal shell";
  §22 clients-and-vendors §"Redactions" — never expose
  `pay_rule.rate`, only `booking_billing.hourly_cents`.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/client/BillableHoursPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Chip`, `Loading`; `formatMoney`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me`, `GET /api/v1/users`,
  `GET /api/v1/organizations`,
  `GET /api/v1/booking_billings?client_org_id=<oid>` (one call per
  binding org, then flatten — mirrors mock).
- **Types**: `BookingBilling`, `Me`, `Organization`, `User`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/client/BillableHoursPage.tsx`.
- The server endpoint MUST strip pay-rate fields from the response
  payload when called by a client role (defence in depth even
  though the schema doesn't expose them).
- Group rows by booking; show worker name (first name only per
  §15 cross-workspace) + role + hours + agency rate + total.
- TanStack Query keyed by `qk.bookingBillings(orgIds.join(","))`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Page renders identical to mock with the redacted columns.
- [ ] Server response never includes `pay_rule.rate` for a client
      caller (verified by integration test).
- [ ] Sums round correctly to the displayed total per booking.
- [ ] Permission gate: client role on the active workspace.
- [ ] Playwright screenshot diff vs mock passes.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_client_billable_hours.py -xvs
npx playwright test tests/web/client-billable-hours.spec.ts
```

description:
## Problem / goal
The client (a property owner whose home is run by an agency) needs
a read-only directory of every property they own that the agency
manages — with closures, current stay, and an "open agency
workspace" link.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Client portal shell"
  (around `/w/<slug>/properties` under `ClientLayout`); §22
  clients-and-vendors §"Client portal".
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/client/PortfolioPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `Chip`, `Loading`.
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/me`,
  `GET /api/v1/organizations`,
  `GET /api/v1/properties`,
  `GET /api/v1/workspaces`,
  `GET /api/v1/property_workspaces`,
  `GET /api/v1/stays`.
- **Types**: `Me` (incl. `client_binding_org_ids`), `Organization`,
  `Property`, `PropertyClosure`, `PropertyWorkspace`, `Stay`,
  `Workspace`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/client/PortfolioPage.tsx`.
- Filter is `properties.where(client_org_id IN
  me.client_binding_org_ids)`. Server-side scoping is the source
  of truth; the client is a defence-in-depth filter only.
- Render closures + current-stay chip with mock copy and tones.
- Wire SSE `property.changed`, `stay.changed`, `closure.changed`
  to invalidate `qk.properties()`, `qk.stays()` resp.
- Preserve the read-only stance: no edit affordances.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Lists only properties the user is bound to via
      `client_binding_org_ids`; nothing else leaks.

- [ ] SSE keeps the list live (closure added → chip updates).

title: feat(web/admin): ChatGatewayPage — provider config + inbound test + dispatch health
description:
## Problem / goal
Configure the deployment-default WhatsApp / SMS provider, see
template approval state, send a test inbound message, and observe
dispatch health (last-error, queue depth). Per-workspace overrides
appear here too.



This page is the live (production) version of the mock. The mock is
the authoritative visual source — the production page **MUST look
exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this. Refactoring
and extracting helpers is fine (and encouraged where it improves
readability), but the rendered output must be byte-equivalent to the
mock at every supported breakpoint.
## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Admin" →
  `/admin/chat-gateway`; §23 chat-gateway spec — webhook,
  templates, overrides.
- **Mock to copy verbatim**:
  `mocks/web/src/pages/admin/ChatGatewayPage.tsx`.
- **Components**: `DeskPage`, `StatCard`, `Chip`, `Loading`;
  `Copy`, `Check`, `MessageSquare` from `lucide-react`; status /
  template tone maps.
- **API endpoints**:
  `GET /admin/api/v1/chat_gateway/providers`,
  `GET /admin/api/v1/chat_gateway/templates`,
  `GET /admin/api/v1/chat_gateway/overrides`,
  `POST /admin/api/v1/chat_gateway/test_inbound`.
- **Types**: `AdminChatProvider`, `AdminChatProviderTemplate`,
  `AdminChatOverrideRow`.

## Implementation notes
- Copy mock TSX verbatim into
  `app/web/src/pages/admin/ChatGatewayPage.tsx`.
- Copy-button (clipboard) preserves the mock's animation +
  `Check` swap on success.
- Test-inbound submits a fake webhook payload and renders the
  dispatcher's response (correlation id, agent invoked, latency).
- SSE `chat_gateway.provider.changed` and
  `chat_gateway.template.changed` invalidate the relevant queries.
- Preserve every status tone mapping (connected→moss,
  error→rust, not_configured→ghost; approved→moss,
  pending→sand, rejected→rust, paused→ghost) and copy.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Provider, template, override sections render identical to
      mock with correct chips.
- [ ] Test-inbound flows end-to-end through the dispatcher and
      shows the result inline.
- [ ] Webhook URL clipboard-copy works with the success animation.
- [ ] Permission gate: deployment-admin only.

## Test plan

```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/web/test_admin_chat_gateway.py -xvs

```

title: feat(web/manager): workspace document library with extraction badge + retry
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager browses every document attached anywhere in the workspace (manuals, warranties, leases, regulations), with the §21 extraction status badge per row and a retry control for owners / managers.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/DocumentsPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/documents` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, ExtractionBadge, ExtractionDisclosure.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/documents, GET /api/v1/assets, GET /api/v1/properties, POST /api/v1/documents/{id}/extraction/retry, GET /api/v1/documents/{id}/extraction/pages/{n}.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/DocumentsPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- The Extraction badge vocabulary is fixed (`pending`, `extracting`, `succeeded`, `failed`, `unsupported`, `empty`) — match §14 / spec exactly.
- Page text is lazy-loaded one page at a time; never prefetch the whole document.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/documents`.

title: feat(web/manager): outbound webhook subscriptions with delivery log
description:
## Problem / goal
Manager subscribes external systems to workspace events (task.completed, expense.approved, ical.poll.completed, …) and inspects delivery attempts + retries.

This page exists today as a high-fidelity mock; production must look
**exactly like the mock** — same DOM, same semantic CSS classes, same
responsive behaviour, same copy. Designs were settled during Phase
17a/spec work; this task is implementation, not redesign.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` §"Owner/Manager (under
  /w/<slug>/)" and the page-specific paragraph(s).
- **Mock (source of truth, copy verbatim)**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/WebhooksPage.tsx`.
- **Route**: `/w/<slug>/webhooks` — rendered under `ManagerLayout`.
- **Referenced components**: DeskPage, Chip, Loading, EventPicker, DeliveryLogDrawer.
- **API endpoints consumed**: GET /api/v1/webhooks, POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/webhooks, GET /api/v1/webhooks/{id}/deliveries.

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/WebhooksPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/manager/`**.
  Do NOT rewrite JSX, do NOT rename CSS classes, do NOT swap icons,
  do NOT collapse panels — preserve byte-for-byte where possible.
- The mock already uses `useQuery`/`useMutation` with
  `fetchJson` and the shared `qk` query keys. Keep them; only
  adjust if an endpoint signature genuinely changed when the
  corresponding Phase 14 router landed (note divergence in PR).
- Replace any inline fixture data with the production query — but
  only when the mock still has fixtures (most don't; most already
  call `/api/v1/*`).
- **Wire SSE invalidation** via the shared SSE bus (Phase 16
  `sse-wiring`): subscribe the page's query keys to the relevant
  domain events so concurrent edits from another tab/agent appear
  without a refresh.
- **Loading and error states** must match the mock's
  `<Loading />` + "Failed to load." pattern (already present).
- **Permission gating**: wrap the page in the route-level
  `requirePermission` guard from `p4.permission.enforce`; the
  guard renders the standard 403 panel — no bespoke fallback in the
  page itself.
- **No new design tokens, no new utility classes.** If the mock
  references a class that doesn't exist in `mocks/web/src/styles`
  yet, that's a bug in the mock — fix it in the mock first
  (`/specs` skill) and let the styles flow through unchanged.
- HMAC secret is shown once on creation, never afterwards — same pattern as API tokens.
- The event picker uses the same canonical event catalog Phase 12 publishes; do not hardcode the list.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] **Visual parity**: Playwright screenshot of the new page diffs
      pixel-clean against a screenshot of the mock at the same URL
      under the dev stack (`http://127.0.0.1:8100`). Capture both
      desktop (1280×800) and mobile (375×812) viewports under
      `.playwright-mcp/`.
- [ ] All interactive affordances on the mock work end-to-end (click,
      mutate, optimistic update, SSE refresh from a second tab).
- [ ] `<Loading />` while pending; "Failed to load." on query
      failure; no infinite spinner on 4xx.
- [ ] `requirePermission` gate applied at the route; a user without
      the relevant capability sees the 403 panel and never paints
      the page chrome.
- [ ] No `console.error` in dev, no React key warnings, no
      hydration mismatches.
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints (≥720px desk vs phone) preserved; no
      regressions on the layout the mock established.

## Test plan
- `cd app/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/manager` — unit tests
  for any page-level helper extracted from the mock.
- Playwright e2e (`tests/e2e/manager/<page>.spec.ts`):
  - smoke: page loads against seeded fixtures, primary CTA fires;
  - screenshot: diff vs the same route under `mocks/web/` baseline
    (committed under `tests/e2e/__snapshots__/manager/`);
  - permission: a token lacking the gating capability lands on the
    403 panel.
- Manual sanity check at `http://127.0.0.1:8100/w/<slug>/webhooks`.

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text:
2026-04-22 /specs revision: the mock at mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InventoryPage.tsx gained three production-visible features that must flow through 1:1 in the verbatim copy:
1. **Per-item drawer** with timeline-ruled ledger (.inv-drawer, .inv-history, .inv-history__dot, staggered reveal). Opens on row click.
2. **Infinite-scroll history** — useInfiniteQuery paginating GET /api/v1/inventory/{id}/movements?before=…&limit=… at 8/page, with an IntersectionObserver sentinel rooted on the drawer aside so the trigger fires from the drawer's internal scroll (works on smaller-desktop viewports that need to scroll past the hero + adjust form first).
3. **Stocktake sheet** launched via 'Start stocktake' per property; POSTs /properties/{pid}/stocktakes then /stocktakes/{sid}/commit.

Copy the shared useCloseOnEscape hook (mocks/web/src/lib/useCloseOnEscape.ts) into app/web/src/lib/ so scrim-backed drawers close on Esc across the whole app — see the parallel task for that hook (it's used by DayDrawer and LlmPage too).

title: feat(api/messaging): notifications, push tokens, digests, chat channels and messages
description:
## Problem / goal
Messaging router — notifications (read state, list), push tokens
(register / list / delete), daily/weekly digests, and chat
channels and messages (including the chat-gateway inbound surface).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Messaging",
  \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\`,
  \`docs/specs/23-chat-gateway.md\`.
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/me/NotificationsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/components/NotificationBell.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/team/ChatPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/team/ChannelDetailPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/me/SecurityPage.tsx\` (push tokens).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/messaging.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`messaging\`.
- Push token POST may return 501 \`push_unavailable\` per spec
  §12 until deployment FCM/APNS is provisioned; GET and DELETE
  always live.
- Notification \`PATCH /notifications/{id}\` flips \`read_at\`;
  bulk \`POST /notifications:mark-read\` accepts an array.
- Chat channel + message CRUD; messages stream in via SSE
  (consumed elsewhere, not this router) but are POSTable here.
- Chat-gateway inbound: \`POST /chat-gateway/inbound\` accepts
  signed webhooks (HMAC) — not workspace-scoped, runs at the
  deployment level (mounted on the deployment-root router but
  schema-defined here for completeness).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD + mark-read routes for notifications
- [ ] Push token POST returns 501 when feature off; GET/DELETE
      always work
- [ ] Chat message POST emits SSE \`chat_message.upserted\`
- [ ] Chat-gateway inbound validates HMAC; bad sig → 401
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`messaging\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_messaging.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_messaging_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(api/billing): organizations, rate cards, work orders, quotes, vendor invoices, client portal
description:
## Problem / goal
Billing router — clients & vendors and everything that flows
between them and the workspace: rate cards, work orders, quotes,
vendor invoices, plus the client-portal read surface.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Clients, work orders,
  invoices (§22)" (full subsection including
  \`property_workspace\` invites).
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ClientsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/RateCardsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/WorkOrdersPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/QuotesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/VendorInvoicesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/client-portal/web/src/pages/PortalPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/billing.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`billing\`.
- Sub-resources: \`POST /work-orders/{id}/lines\`,
  \`POST /work-orders/{id}/invoice\` (transitions
  \`completed -> invoiced\`),
  \`POST /quotes/{id}/send\` (audits + emails),
  \`POST /quotes/{id}/decision\`.
- Client-portal endpoints under \`/portal/...\` accept a portal
  token (issued via \`POST /property-workspace-invites\`); they
  expose only what the spec defines as portal-visible.
- Money in integer cents; multi-currency supported via the
  organization's \`default_currency\`.
- Idempotency on every mutation; cursor pagination on every
  listing.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Routes for organization, rate-card, work-order, quote,
      vendor-invoice
- [ ] Work-order \`POST /invoice\` creates linked invoice +
      transitions state
- [ ] Quote send + decision endpoints audited; SSE on decision
- [ ] Portal endpoints reject missing/invalid portal tokens
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`billing\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_billing.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_billing_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

description:

QR codes), actions (service / repair / replace / inspect / read

## Technical context

  \`mocks/web/src/pages/worker/AssetScanPage.tsx\` (QR scan).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/assets.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`assets\`.
- \`GET /assets?qr_code=...\` returns 1 row or 404 (workspace-

- \`POST /assets/{id}/actions\` is the hot path; idempotent.
- \`POST /assets/{id}/documents\` accepts a multipart upload via

- Asset-type endpoints support \`default_action_catalog_json\`
  validation against a JSON schema.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] QR-code lookup workspace-scoped; cross-tenant returns 404
- [ ] Action POST is idempotent
- [ ] Document upload references an existing blob_hash from /files

title: feat(api/inventory): items, movements, reorder rules
description:
## Problem / goal
Inventory router for items (with SKU + barcode lookup), movements
(receive / issue / adjust / consume), and reorder rules. The
movement endpoint is the hot path — workers scan a barcode and
post a one-line movement.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/12-rest-api.md\` §"Inventory".
- **Mocks consuming**:
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InventoryPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/worker/InventoryScanPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ReorderRulesPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- \`app/api/v1/inventory.py\`: APIRouter with tag \`inventory\`.
- \`GET /items?barcode=...\` returns a single match; 404 if
  unknown.
- \`POST /movements\` accepts \`item_id, delta, reason,
  occurrence_id?\`; idempotent via Idempotency-Key (workers'
  retries are common on flaky wifi).
- Reorder rule CRUD; \`GET /items?below_reorder=true\` lists
  shortage items for the dashboard.
- All numeric quantities serialise as decimal strings to avoid
  float drift.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Item, movement, reorder-rule routes
- [ ] Barcode lookup returns 1 row or 404
- [ ] Movement is idempotent (Idempotency-Key replays)
- [ ] Decimal serialised as string ("3.5"), never float
- [ ] Schemathesis passes for tag \`inventory\`

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/api/v1/test_inventory.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/api/test_inventory_routes.py -xvs
\`\`\`

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text:
2026-04-22 /specs revision widened the REST surface (§12 'Inventory'). This task now also needs to expose:
- GET /inventory/{iid}/movements?before=<iso>&limit=<n> — cursor-paginated ledger, returns {items, next_cursor}.
- POST /properties/{pid}/stocktakes — open a stocktake session; returns the session row.
- GET /properties/{pid}/stocktakes — recent sessions for the property.
- GET /stocktakes/{sid} — session detail + draft lines.
- PATCH /stocktakes/{sid}/lines/{item_id} — persist observed + reason + note for one line.
- POST /stocktakes/{sid}/commit — materialise non-zero deltas as movements tagged with source_stocktake_id and set completed_at.
- GET /inventory/reports/production_rate / …/shrinkage / …/stocktakes (see §08 Reports).

All numeric fields carry decimal quantities (no integer milli-units); see §08 'Precision contract'. Depends on the schema widening (cd-lsw0 follow-up) and the new stocktake service task.

title: feat(cli/overrides): admin init, auth passkey register, whoami, tasks close
description:
## Problem / goal
Some flows are multi-step (workspace bootstrap, passkey registration)
or want a friendlier shape than the raw OpenAPI verb (`whoami`,
`tasks close`). These ship as hand-written commands that override or
augment the codegen output.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"Composite commands",
  §"Overrides directory".
- **Auth**: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` (passkey ceremony +
  attestation).

## Implementation notes
- New directory `cli/crewday/_overrides/` — every `.py` file exports
  a `register(root: click.Group)` function called from `_main.py`
  after the generated commands attach. Overrides may **shadow**
  generated commands (same name wins).
- Implementations:
  - `crewday admin init --workspace <slug> --owner-email <email>` —
    POST workspace, seed `owners` group, register the calling user
    as owner, mint and print an API token.
  - `crewday auth passkey register [--label <name>]` — guides the
    user through the WebAuthn registration ceremony via the API
    helper endpoints; prints credential id + label on success.
  - `crewday whoami` — calls `GET /me` and prints user, active
    workspace, role, token expiry, profile name in human form.
  - `crewday tasks close <id> [--note <md>] [--evidence <path>...]` —
    fetch occurrence, upload evidence blobs, transition to `done`,
    print summary.
- Each override has a `--help` block describing the multi-step
  flow and the underlying API calls.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All four composites listed in `crewday --help`
- [ ] `admin init` is idempotent (re-runs return the same workspace)
- [ ] `auth passkey register` works against the dev API end-to-end
- [ ] `whoami` prints workspace + role + token expiry
- [ ] `tasks close` uploads evidence + transitions state in one call
- [ ] Unit tests stub the HTTP client; integration tests hit dev

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_overrides.py -xvs
crewday admin init --workspace test --owner-email me@example.com
crewday whoami
```

title: feat(cli/config): ~/.config/crewday/profiles.toml multi-environment + default switching
description:
## Problem / goal
Users juggle multiple environments (dev / staging / prod / their own
demo workspace). `crewday auth login` writes a profile entry; `crewday
config use <name>` switches the default. The active profile drives
base URL, token, default workspace, and output preference.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/13-cli.md` §"Profiles", §"Config file".
- **XDG**: `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/crewday/profiles.toml` (fallback
  `~/.config/crewday/profiles.toml`); permissions `0600`.

## Implementation notes
- `cli/crewday/_config.py`:
  - Dataclass `Profile(name, base_url, token, default_workspace,
    output, ca_bundle)`.
  - `load() -> Config` reads TOML; `save(cfg)` rewrites with `0600`
    perms (atomic via tempfile + rename).
  - `active(cli_override) -> Profile` honours `--profile`,
    `CREWDAY_PROFILE` env, then `default = "<name>"` in the file.
  - Subcommands wired into `_main.py`:
    - `crewday config show` — print active profile (token redacted)
    - `crewday config list` — list all profiles
    - `crewday config use <name>` — set default
    - `crewday config rm <name>` — remove
  - Token storage: prefer OS keyring (`keyring` package) when
    available; fall back to TOML with file perms enforced.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] TOML round-trip preserves field order
- [ ] File perms forced to `0600` on every write
- [ ] `--profile` > `CREWDAY_PROFILE` > `default` precedence
- [ ] `config show` redacts the token (last 4 chars only)
- [ ] Unit tests cover precedence, redaction, atomic write

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest cli/tests/test_config.py -xvs
crewday config list
crewday config use dev
```

title: feat(web/employee): EmployeeAssetPage — read-only asset info + log action
description:
## Problem / goal
Worker's view of an individual asset (§21). Read-only metadata + history + the ability to log an action (service / repair / inspect / read meter). Managers get a richer view elsewhere; this page is the field-tech variant.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` (authoritative).
- **Mock to copy verbatim**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx`.
- **Target file**: `app/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx`.
- **Referenced components**: `PageHeader`, `Chip`, `Loading`, `AssetIcon`. Types: `AssetDetailPayload`. Helper `fmtDate` (en-GB locale).
- **API endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/assets/{aid}` returning `AssetDetailPayload`; `POST /api/v1/assets/{aid}/actions` (kind, notes, meter_reading, optional photo).

## Implementation notes
- **Copy `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx` verbatim into `app/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx`.**
- Replace any fixture / mock data with real TanStack Query calls
  against the listed API endpoints. Preserve every `useQuery` /
  `useMutation` call shape — the mock already wires them as if to the
  real API.
- Preserve **every CSS class name** unchanged (semantic CSS only —
  no Tailwind, no inline `style=""`). Visual diff against the mock
  must be empty.
- Preserve every responsive breakpoint and conditional layout (mobile
  vs desktop). Re-test the page at the same widths used by the mock.
- Wire SSE invalidation on relevant events: subscribe via the shared
  `EventSource('/events')` (from `p16.sse-wiring`) and call
  `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... })` for the keys
  this page reads.
- Loading and error states must be present (use `Loading` /
  `EmptyState` from `@/components/common` as the mock does).
- Worker has no edit affordance — only `/actions` is writable from here.
- Preserve the optimistic mutation in `markDone` exactly.
- Subscribe to SSE `asset.updated` to refresh history.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual parity with the mock — Playwright screenshot diff vs
      `.playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx.png` is empty (or within a tiny
      anti-aliasing tolerance).
- [ ] Every interactive feature works against the **real** API — no
      fixture data left behind.
- [ ] Loading and error states render correctly (forced via DevTools
      throttling + by hitting the error response path).
- [ ] SSE invalidation refreshes the page when relevant events fire
      (verified by emitting a test event and watching the query
      refetch).
- [ ] No new utility/atomic CSS classes; every class semantic.

## Test plan
```bash
# 1. Snapshot the mock (one-time; baseline lives in .playwright-mcp/).
cd mocks/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/mocks-employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx.png

# 2. Snapshot the production page and diff.
cd app/web && npm run dev &
# capture .playwright-mcp/app-employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx.png
# diff against the baseline — fail on any pixel delta beyond AA tolerance.

# 3. Smoke e2e (Playwright):
npx playwright test tests/e2e/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx.spec.ts
```

title: feat(llm/digest-gen): friendly prose around structured digest stats
description:
## Problem / goal
The daily digest job (§10, `p10.digest.daily`) assembles
structured facts (overdue tasks, upcoming stays, anomalies)
deterministically; this capability writes the friendly prose
wrapper. The structured data is authoritative — a post-generation
check rejects + rewrites prose that contradicts the numbers.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Daily digest /
  anomaly detection".
- Cross-ref: §10 "Daily digest" — consumed by
  `p10.digest.daily` (the cron orchestrator).
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`anomaly_suppression`.

## Implementation notes
- New module `app/domain/llm/capabilities/digest_gen.py` with
  `compose(ctx, recipient_user_id, structured_payload) ->
  DigestProse`.
- Prompt template under capability `digest.compose` (self-
  seeded per §11). System prompt pins "do not invent numbers
  or names; rephrase only".
- Post-generation validator: extract every number, currency
  amount, and proper noun from the prose; assert each one
  appears in the structured payload (or is one of a small
  whitelist of stop-words). On mismatch, regenerate up to twice;
  on third failure, fall back to a deterministic template.
- Recipient locale honoured (§18) — the prompt loads the
  recipient's preferred language and tone from
  `p12.preferences`.
- Anomaly ranking + one-line explanations per anomaly per the
  spec; ignored anomalies are filtered out by the orchestrator
  via `anomaly_suppression` (this capability does not read
  the table itself).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A digest with no anomalies and one overdue task produces
  prose that names the task and the assignee verbatim.
- [ ] Prose that introduces a hallucinated number is regenerated;
  third failure falls back to template.
- [ ] Recipient with French locale receives French prose.
- [ ] At-cap workspace triggers the deterministic-template
  fallback (no LLM call attempted).

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/llm/capabilities/test_digest_gen.py`
  with golden structured payloads.
- Integration test wired in alongside `p10.digest.daily`.

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(web/employee): IssueNewPage — worker flags a problem (creates task + manager notification)
description:
## Problem / goal
Lets a worker raise an issue from the field — broken thing, missing supplies, safety hazard. Submitting creates a task (or issue record) and notifies the manager. Categories: damage / broken / supplies / safety / other; severities: low / normal / high / urgent.

This page is the live (production) version of the mock at
`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/IssueNewPage.tsx`. The mock is the authoritative visual source — the production page
**MUST look exactly like the mock**. The user has emphasised this.

## Technical context

title: feat(agent/preferences): per-workspace tone, approval mode, blocked actions
description:
## Problem / goal
Workspace owners and managers tune their embedded agent: tone
(plain / formal / playful), default approval mode for new users,
and a denylist of capabilities/actions the agent will refuse even
if the calling user could in principle perform them. Layered with
property and per-user preferences (§11 "Layers").

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md` §"Agent preferences"
  (Layers, Workspace preferences, Property preferences, Your
  preferences, Which capabilities receive preferences,
  Authoring and visibility, PII posture, Size budget, Storage
  versioning audit, Not a substitute for structured rules).
- Domain: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §`workspace.settings`
  (extends with `agent_prefs_md`, `agent_blocked_actions[]`,
  `default_approval_mode`).
- Mocks: `mocks/web/src/components/AgentPreferencesPanel.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/components/AgentApprovalModePanel.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- Add columns / settings keys: `agent_prefs_md` (markdown,
  capped at the per-spec size budget), `agent_blocked_actions`
  (jsonb array of operationId or capability keys),
  `default_approval_mode` ('bypass'|'auto'|'strict', default
  'auto').
- Resolver `resolve_preferences(ctx, capability) ->
  PreferenceBundle` walks workspace → property (when in scope)
  → user, concatenating prefs in the documented order.
- Runtime injects `PreferenceBundle.text` into the system
  prompt under a stable header so prompt caching works; blocked
  actions short-circuit at tool-dispatch time with
  `403 action_blocked_by_preferences`.
- CRUD via `PUT /api/v1/workspace/agent_prefs` (manager+),
  `PUT /api/v1/users/me/agent_prefs` (self). Mutations write
  `audit.workspace.agent_prefs_updated` with the diff.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Preferences round-trip via API; the new default approval
  mode applies to **new** users (existing users keep their mode).
- [ ] Blocked-action list refuses the matching tool dispatch with
  the documented 403.
- [ ] Resolver concatenates layers in the spec order; size budget
  enforced.
- [ ] Audit row written on every change.
- [ ] Empty preferences ship a stable empty header (so prompt
  cache key is stable).

## Test plan
- Unit tests under `tests/domain/agent/test_preferences.py`.
- Integration test: changing tone in the panel reflects in the
  next agent reply (LLM stub).

## Cross-cutting
- Every LLM call accounts in the workspace 30-day budget envelope
  (§11 "Workspace usage budget"); refused calls do not write
  `llm_call` and surface `budget_exceeded` to the caller.
- Every mutation writes an `audit_log` row (§02), attributed to the
  delegating user with `token_id`, `agent_label`,
  `agent_conversation_ref` and `correlation_id` populated when the
  call comes from a delegated agent token (§11 "Agent audit trail").

title: feat(chat-gateway): agent_dispatch_sweep APScheduler job (restart safety net)
description:
## Problem / goal
Webhook + dispatcher race against worker restarts: a message can
land, the dispatcher process can die mid-handle, and
\`dispatched_to_agent_at\` stays NULL. Periodic sweep finds those
stragglers and re-publishes \`chat.message.received\` so the
dispatcher catches up.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/23-chat-gateway.md\` §"Routing" →
  §"Inbound" (restart-safety guarantees).

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/workers/chat_gateway/sweep.py\` scheduled by APScheduler
  every \`30s\`.
- Query: \`chat_message\` rows where \`channel.kind='chat_gateway'\`,
  \`dispatched_to_agent_at IS NULL\`, and \`created_at <
  now() - 30s\`.
- For each row, re-publish \`chat.message.received\` (the dispatcher
  is idempotent — see \`p13.gateway.dispatcher\`).
- Bound the batch (max 200 per tick) so a backlog doesn't pin one
  worker tick.
- Per-row try/except — one bad row mustn't kill the sweep.
- Metrics: emit a counter for \`sweep.requeued\`; gauge of pending
  count.
- Audit \`chat_gateway.sweep.requeued\` per message.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Picks up rows older than 30 s, never younger
- [ ] No double-dispatch when the original handler eventually
      catches up first (covered by dispatcher idempotency test)
- [ ] Per-row failure logged + audited; sweep continues
- [ ] Bounded batch size respected
- [ ] Unit tests cover happy-path + bounded-batch + per-row failure

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/chat_gateway/test_sweep.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/chat_gateway/test_sweep_e2e.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(billing): client portal data API (org-scoped read: hours, invoices, portfolio)
description:
## Problem / goal
Read-only API the client portal SPA calls. Scoped to the client's
own org: properties they own (portfolio), hours billed
(billable hours), and invoices/quotes addressed to them.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\`
  §"Client surface (client login)", §"Acceptance authority",
  §"Redactions".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/client/PortfolioPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/client/BillableHoursPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/client/InvoicesPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/client/QuotesPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- New router \`app/api/client/portal.py\` mounted under
  \`/client\`. Auth: client role grant only — workspace + org
  bound to the session.
- Endpoints (all org-scoped via \`role_grant.scope_organization_id\`):
  - \`GET /client/portfolio\` — properties owned by client
  - \`GET /client/billable-hours\` — accrued hours grouped by
    work_order + property + week
  - \`GET /client/invoices\` — issued invoices (status, total, PDF
    link)
  - \`GET /client/quotes\` — pending/sent quotes with accept link
- Apply spec §"Redactions": strip staff names, strip cost
  components, only show client-facing rates.
- Cache-friendly (\`Cache-Control: private, max-age=30\`).
- Audit \`client_portal.viewed\` with the slug accessed (no PII).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Client A cannot see client B's data even with crafted query
      params (tested with two clients in the same workspace)
- [ ] Redaction rules enforced (staff names absent, no internal
      cost columns)
- [ ] All four endpoints return paginated, sorted, deterministic
      data
- [ ] Unit + API + authz boundary tests

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/client/test_portal.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/client/test_portal_authz.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/client/test_portal_endpoints.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(billing): work_order CRUD + hour accrual from shift.ended event
description:
## Problem / goal
A work order tracks billable hours and price for a job at a property
for a client. Hours accrue automatically when shifts close on a
property linked to an open work order. Publishes
\`work_order.completed\` when manager marks it done.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\` §"\`work_order\`",
  §"State machine", §"Invariants",
  §"\`booking_billing\`" (cross-context accrual).

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/billing/work_orders.py\`:
  \`WorkOrderService(ctx)\` with
  \`create / get / list / update / mark_in_progress / complete /
  invoice\`.
- Subscribe to \`shift.ended\` on the event bus: when the shift's
  property has an open work order for its org, append
  \`(shift_hours_decimal, hourly_rate)\` to \`total_*\` cached
  columns. Use SELECT … FOR UPDATE to keep the cache consistent.
- API \`app/api/billing/work_orders.py\` standard REST + a
  \`POST /work-orders/{id}/complete\` action.
- On \`complete\`: validate state machine, persist, publish
  \`work_order.completed\` with payload
  \`{work_order_id, total_cents, total_hours_decimal}\`.
- Audit every state transition.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Status transitions match the spec state machine; bad
      transitions → 422
- [ ] Cached \`total_hours_decimal\` / \`total_cents\` exact across
      concurrent shift-end events (test with two concurrent
      handlers)
- [ ] \`work_order.completed\` event fires exactly once per WO
- [ ] Cannot edit \`rate_card_id\` once shifts have accrued
- [ ] Unit + API + integration tests for shift-driven accrual

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/billing/test_work_orders.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/billing/test_work_orders_api.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/billing/test_work_order_accrual.py -xvs
\`\`\`
