title: feat(billing): rate_card CRUD with service→cents/hr map and active windows
description:
## Problem / goal
Rate cards bind a service catalogue to per-hour pricing for a given
client (or as a workspace default). Multiple cards per org with
non-overlapping active windows; resolution picks the card whose
window contains the work date.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\` §"Billable
  rates" → §"\`client_rate\`", §"Rate resolution".

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/billing/rate_cards.py\`:
  - \`RateCardService(ctx).create(org_id, label, currency,
    rates: dict[ServiceKey, int_cents], active_from, active_to)\`
  - \`resolve(org_id, service_key, on=date)\` → cents/hr or 422 if
    no card covers \`on\`.
- API \`app/api/billing/rate_cards.py\`:
  - \`GET/POST /organizations/{org_id}/rate-cards\`
  - \`PATCH /organizations/{org_id}/rate-cards/{id}\`
- Reject overlapping windows for the same (org, service) pair on
  insert/update.
- Audit \`billing.rate_card.created/updated\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Overlap detection across active windows enforced
- [ ] Resolution picks the card by date (boundary inclusive on
      from, exclusive on to)
- [ ] Currency must match the org default unless explicit override
- [ ] Unit tests cover overlap + resolution edge cases
- [ ] API tests; OpenAPI updated

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/billing/test_rate_cards.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/billing/test_rate_cards_api.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(billing): organization CRUD (client/vendor/mixed)
description:
## Problem / goal
Organizations are the billing parties: clients (we invoice them),
vendors (they invoice us), or mixed (both). Foundation for rate
cards, work orders, quotes, vendor invoices, and the client portal.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\` §"Design summary",
  §"\`organization\`", §"Starter rows".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/OrganizationsPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/billing/organizations.py\`:
  \`OrganizationService(ctx)\` with \`create / get / list / update /
  archive\`.
- Validate \`kind ∈ {client,vendor,mixed}\`; cannot demote a mixed
  org to client if vendor invoices exist (and vice versa) — return
  422 with the conflict reason.
- API \`app/api/billing/organizations.py\`:
  - \`GET/POST /organizations\`
  - \`GET/PATCH /organizations/{id}\`
  - \`POST /organizations/{id}/archive\`
- Default-currency from workspace settings; tax_id is opaque text
  (per-jurisdiction validation out of scope).
- Audit \`billing.organization.created/updated/archived\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Cannot delete an org with dependent rate_cards / work_orders
      / quotes / vendor_invoices — archive instead
- [ ] Kind transitions validated against existing artefacts
- [ ] Listing supports filter by kind + text search on name
- [ ] Unit + API tests; OpenAPI updated

## Test plan

uv run pytest tests/api/billing/test_organizations_api.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(messaging): chat_message send + list + attachments + chat.message.sent event
description:
## Problem / goal
Send a message in a channel, list a channel's history with
keyset pagination, attach blobs (image, file, voice). Publishes
\`chat.message.sent\` so other contexts (LLM agent, chat-gateway
bridge) can react.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"In-app
  messaging" → §"Agent-message delivery".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/components/chat/ChatComposer.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/components/chat/ChatLog.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/employee/ChatPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/messaging/messages.py\`:
  - \`send(channel_id, body_md, attachments=[blob_hash, ...])\`
  - \`list(channel_id, before=None, limit=50)\` keyset by \`created_at,
    id\`.
- API \`app/api/messaging/messages.py\`:
  - \`POST /chat/channels/{id}/messages\`
  - \`GET /chat/channels/{id}/messages?before=...\`
- Markdown sanitised (allow basic formatting + autolink, strip raw
  HTML / scripts).
- Emits \`chat.message.sent\` via Phase 0 event bus with
  \`{channel_id, message_id, author_user_id, channel_kind}\` payload.
- SSE \`chat.message.created\` invalidates the channel's TanStack
  query for connected clients.
- Audit \`messaging.message.sent\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Message persists with author + ordering preserved across two
      same-millisecond sends (id tiebreak)
- [ ] Attachments must already exist in the storage layer (FK by
      blob_hash); else 404
- [ ] Markdown sanitiser strips \`<script>\` etc. (test included)
- [ ] \`chat.message.sent\` fires exactly once per send
- [ ] Unit + API + event-bus tests

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/messaging/test_chat_message.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/messaging/test_chat_message_api.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/messaging/test_chat_message_event.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(messaging): chat_channel CRUD (staff / manager / chat_gateway)
description:
## Problem / goal
Channels are the container for chat threads. Three kinds: \`staff\`
(everyone in workspace), \`manager\` (managers only), and
\`chat_gateway\` (auto-created per inbound contact).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"In-app
  messaging" (channels & roles); kind=\`chat_gateway\` integrates
  with \`docs/specs/23-chat-gateway.md\` §"\`chat_thread\`".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/ChatChannelsPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/components/ChatChannelsMeCard.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/messaging/channels.py\`:
  \`ChatChannelService(ctx)\` with \`create / list / get / archive /
  add_member / remove_member\`.
- Visibility: \`staff\` returned to every workspace member; \`manager\`
  filtered by capability \`messaging.manager_channel\`;
  \`chat_gateway\` returned only to staff with the
  \`chat_gateway.read\` capability.
- API \`app/api/messaging/channels.py\`:
  - \`GET /chat/channels\` (paginated)
  - \`POST /chat/channels\` (manager-only for \`staff/manager\`;
    auto-create only for \`chat_gateway\`)
  - \`PATCH /chat/channels/{id}\` (rename, archive)
- Audit \`messaging.channel.created/archived\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visibility per kind enforced — manager-only never leaks to
      worker
- [ ] Archive is soft (\`archived_at\`); list defaults to active only
- [ ] Workers can post to \`staff\`; not to \`manager\`
- [ ] Unit + API tests; OpenAPI updated

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/messaging/test_chat_channel.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/messaging/test_chat_channel_api.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(messaging): send_daily_digest worker with LLM body fallback
description:
## Problem / goal
Per-user morning email summarising today: scheduled occurrences,
overdue items, pending approvals, and the pay-period state. Body
copy comes from the LLM (Phase 12 capability \`daily_digest\`); falls
back to a plain Jinja template when the capability is unmapped or
budget-blocked.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"Daily
  digests".

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/workers/messaging/daily_digest.py\` scheduled by
  APScheduler at the user's local 07:00 (compute via
  \`property.timezone\` for managers, \`user.timezone\` for workers).
- Pulls per-user data from query helpers (occurrences read, overdue
  count, pending approvals, latest \`pay_period\` state).
- Renders via the LLM client (\`capability='daily_digest'\`); on
  exception or empty result, fall back to
  \`templates/digest_daily.{subject,body_md}.j2\`.
- Writes a \`digest_record\` row; sends via \`NotificationService\`
  (\`kind=daily_digest\`).
- Skips users with no actionable items unless they've opted in to
  "always send" in preferences.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] One \`digest_record\` per (user, day); idempotent re-run
- [ ] LLM call respects budget ledger; fallback path covered
- [ ] Empty-day users skipped unless opted in
- [ ] Unit tests cover both LLM-success and fallback paths
- [ ] Integration test: 3-user workspace, verifies one email each

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/messaging/test_daily_digest.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/messaging/test_daily_digest_e2e.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(messaging): web push delivery worker with retry + stale-token cleanup
description:
## Problem / goal
APScheduler worker that turns queued \`Notification\` rows into actual
web-push deliveries to every active \`push_token\`. Retries with
exponential backoff; deletes tokens the provider tells us are gone.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"Channels"
  (web push), §"Delivery tracking".

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/workers/messaging/web_push.py\`:
  - Polls \`notification_push_queue\` (small staging table) or
    consumes the in-process job queue from \`NotificationService\`.
  - Sends via \`pywebpush\` with VAPID keys from workspace settings.
  - Backoff: 30s, 2m, 10m, 1h; drop after 5 attempts.
  - On \`410 Gone\` / \`404\` from the provider, delete the
    \`push_token\` and audit \`messaging.push.token_purged\`.
  - Updates \`push_token.last_used_at\` on success.
- Concurrency: per-workspace fan-out, bounded (max 10 concurrent
  HTTP calls per worker tick) to avoid thundering herd.
- Restart safety: take a row-level lock on the queue row so a
  second worker doesn't double-send.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Successful deliveries marked + \`last_used_at\` bumped
- [ ] 410/404 deletes the token, audited
- [ ] Backoff respected; 5xx retried, 4xx (other than 404/410)
      logged + dropped
- [ ] No double-send under two-worker race (locking covered by
      test)
- [ ] Unit tests with a mocked pywebpush; one integration test
      using a fake endpoint server

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/messaging/test_web_push_worker.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/messaging/test_web_push_e2e.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(messaging): web push subscription endpoints + VAPID key mgmt
description:
## Problem / goal
Browsers call \`POST /notifications/push/subscribe\` to register a
\`PushSubscription\` (endpoint + p256dh + auth). \`unsubscribe\`
removes it. VAPID public key is served so the SW can subscribe.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §"Channels"
  (web push), §"In-app messaging" → §"Agent-message delivery".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/components/ChatChannelsMeCard.tsx\` (the
  notification preferences surface).

## Implementation notes
- New router \`app/api/messaging/push.py\`:
  - \`GET /notifications/push/vapid-key\` → returns base64url public.
  - \`POST /notifications/push/subscribe\`
    \`{endpoint, keys:{p256dh,auth}, ua}\` → upsert by
    (\`user_id\`,\`endpoint\`).
  - \`POST /notifications/push/unsubscribe\` \`{endpoint}\`.
- VAPID keypair lives in workspace settings; rotated via CLI (out
  of scope here). Public key cached in-process, refreshed on
  settings change SSE.
- Validate endpoint origin against an allow-list of providers
  (Mozilla, Google, Apple) to dodge SSRF amplification.
- Audit \`messaging.push.subscribed\` / \`unsubscribed\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Subscribe is idempotent on (\`user_id\`, \`endpoint\`)
- [ ] Endpoint URL must be https + allow-listed host
- [ ] Unsubscribe returns 204 even if row was already gone
- [ ] VAPID public key endpoint cached for 5 min
- [ ] Unit + API tests; OpenAPI updated

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/messaging/test_push_token.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/messaging/test_push_endpoints.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(assets): action log (service/repair/replace/inspect/read) + document uploads
description:
## Problem / goal
Every interaction with an asset (serviced, repaired, replaced,
inspected, meter-read) is an `AssetAction` row carrying who, when,
notes, evidence, optional meter reading. Documents (warranty,
manual, invoice, photo, other) are uploaded via Storage and listed
on the asset page.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/21-assets.md` §"Asset actions", §"Asset
  documents", §"Asset-action to task integration".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/assets/models.py`
  (`AssetAction`, `AssetDocument`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AssetDetailPage.tsx`
  (action timeline + docs tab),
  `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/DocumentsPage.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx` (worker
  records a read / quick service from the field).

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/assets/actions.py`:
  - `record_action(ctx, asset_id, *, kind, performed_at?, notes_md?,
    meter_reading?, evidence_blob_hash?)` — `performed_at` defaults
    to `now()`; emits `asset.action.recorded`.
  - `list_actions(ctx, asset_id, *, kind=None, since=None,
    until=None)` ordered by `performed_at desc`.
  - `next_due(ctx, asset_id)` — computes from default-actions
    `interval_days` per spec §"Next due computation".
- Service `app/domain/assets/documents.py`:
  - `attach_document(ctx, asset_id, *, blob_hash, filename,
    category)`.
  - `delete_document(ctx, document_id)` — soft delete (`deleted_at`).
  - `list_documents(ctx, asset_id, *, category=None)`.
- Capabilities `assets.record_action` (workers OK),
  `assets.manage_documents` (managers).
- Audit + SSE on every mutation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Action CRUD; meter readings stored numeric; evidence FK valid
- [ ] `next_due` returns the soonest action across the catalog
- [ ] Document upload validates mime + 25 MB cap
- [ ] Workers can record actions on assets at their assigned
      properties; cannot delete documents
- [ ] All mutations audited

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_asset_actions.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_asset_actions_api.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_asset_documents_api.py -xvs
```

title: feat(assets): asset CRUD + QR code gen/regen + property/area placement
description:
## Problem / goal
Concrete asset instances (this villa's boiler, this kitchen's
fridge) attached to a property and optionally an area. Each asset
gets a workspace-unique QR token used for the scan endpoint.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/21-assets.md` §"Assets", §"QR codes"
  §"Token format", §"URL pattern", §"Printing".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/assets/models.py`
  (`Asset`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AssetsPage.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AssetDetailPage.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/AssetScanPage.tsx`,
  `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/EmployeeAssetPage.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/assets/assets.py`:
  - `create_asset(ctx, *, asset_type_id, property_id, area_id?,
    label, purchased_at?, warranty_ends_at?, metadata?)`.
  - `update_asset(ctx, asset_id, **fields)`.
  - `move_asset(ctx, asset_id, *, property_id, area_id?)` — separate
    method so the move event is distinct in audit.
  - `regenerate_qr(ctx, asset_id)` — issues a new token and
    invalidates the old one (audited; warns the user via SSE).
- QR token via `app/util/tokens.py::short_token(workspace_id, asset_id)`
  using HMAC of `(workspace_id, asset_id, nonce)` — base32 12-char.
  Unique constraint per workspace already in DB.
- PNG renderer in `app/adapters/qr/`:
  - `render_qr(token, *, size=512, label=None) -> bytes`
    using `segno`.
  - `GET /api/assets/{id}/qr.png` (cap `assets.view`) returns the
    PNG; cached via etag.
- Capability `assets.manage`.
- REST under `app/api/assets/assets.py`.
- Audit every mutation; emit `asset.changed` SSE.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD endpoints + audit
- [ ] QR token unique per workspace; collision retried
- [ ] Regenerate invalidates old token (404 on old scan)
- [ ] Move records both old and new placement in audit
- [ ] PNG endpoint returns valid PNG decodable by a QR reader

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_assets_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_assets_api.py -xvs
```

title: feat(assets/asset-type): CRUD + default action catalog
description:
## Problem / goal
Asset types are templates (boiler, pool pump, fridge, vehicle,
linen-set) carrying a default action catalog (e.g. boiler →
[service, descale, replace anode]). Every workspace gets a
pre-seeded base catalog they can fork.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/21-assets.md` §"Asset types (catalog)",
  §"default_actions_json shape", §"Pre-seeded catalog".
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/assets/models.py`
  (`AssetType`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/AssetTypesPage.tsx`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/domain/assets/types.py`:
  - `create_type(ctx, slug, name, icon, default_actions=[])`.
  - `update_type(ctx, type_id, **fields)`.
  - `delete_type(ctx, type_id)` — refuses if any `Asset` references
    the type (use soft delete via `archived_at`, not row removal).
  - `list_types(ctx, *, include_archived=False)`.
- Default-action shape (pydantic):
  `[{ "kind": "service", "label": "Annual service",
      "interval_days": 365, "warn_before_days": 30 }, ...]`.
- Workspace bootstrap seeds the §21 base catalog (idempotent).
- Capability `assets.manage_types`.
- REST under `app/api/assets/types.py`.
- Audit on every mutation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CRUD endpoints + audit
- [ ] Default-action JSON validated by pydantic
- [ ] Archive instead of delete when in use
- [ ] Bootstrap seeds the base catalog exactly once per workspace

## Test plan

```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_asset_types_api.py -xvs
```

title: feat(inventory): movement service (restock/consume/produce/adjust/transfer) + on_hand cache
description:
## Problem / goal
The inventory ledger is append-only `inventory_movement` rows; the
denormalised `on_hand` on `inventory_item` must always equal the
sum of deltas. The movement service is the only writer to that
column.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/08-inventory.md` §"`inventory_movement`",
  §"Adjustments", §"Transfers between properties"; the on-hand
  cache invariant is implicit (recomputed from movements).
- DB tables from `p1.inventory`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/services/inventory/movement_service.py` with
  `receive`, `issue`, `adjust_to_observed`, `consume`,
  `transfer` (one call → two rows in a single transaction with
  shared `transfer_correlation_id` in `note`).
- Each call: insert movement, update `inventory_item.on_hand`
  via `UPDATE ... SET on_hand = on_hand + :delta` to avoid lost
  updates under contention; row-locks the item for the
  transaction.
- `adjust_to_observed(observed_qty)` computes
  `delta = observed - on_hand`, writes a single
  `audit_correction` movement (per spec §"Adjustments").
- `consume` accepts an optional `source_task_id` and `actor_kind`
  defaulting to caller's actor. Negative `on_hand` is permitted
  (per spec §"Consumption on task completion": staff > model).
- Emit `inventory.item_changed` event (used by `p7.reorder` and
  the SSE invalidation bus).
- Audit row per movement (the movement table itself doubles as
  the audit trail per §15 — but the cross-cutting `audit` row
  still goes for consistency with other services).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each method writes exactly one movement row and updates
      `on_hand` atomically.
- [ ] Concurrent issues against the same item never lose updates
      (test with parallel sessions if Postgres adapter, serialised
      under SQLite).
- [ ] `transfer` writes two movements in a single tx; rollback on
      either side rolls both back.
- [ ] `consume` with `source_task_id` carries it through.
- [ ] `inventory.item_changed` published exactly once per
      committed movement.
- [ ] Unit tests + an integration test for transfer rollback.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_inventory_movements.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_inventory_transfer_rollback.py -xvs
```

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: 2026-04-22 /specs revision: this service must now also emit **positive-delta** produce movements (task-driven output — e.g. laundry produces clean sheets, sheet change produces dirty sheets). Add a produce(item_id, qty, source_task_id) method mirroring consume. Both share the settings gate, renamed to inventory.apply_on_task (legacy inventory.consume_on_task resolves to the same value on read). Stocktake-driven writes route through batch_reconcile(session_id, lines) — one movement per non-zero delta line, all tagged with source_stocktake_id. Depends on the schema widening in cd-lsw0 / the new schema task. See §08 'Reason taxonomy' and 'Inventory effects on task completion'.

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: 2026-04-22 follow-up: scratched the legacy-fallback note — nothing is deployed, so the service resolves inventory.apply_on_task directly with no legacy key lookup. Method vocabulary now: restock, consume, produce, adjust, transfer (no receive/issue). See cd-n6ga for the schema, §08 for the full reason taxonomy.

title: feat(inventory): item CRUD with SKU + barcode uniqueness
description:
## Problem / goal
Inventory items are scoped per property within a workspace. SKU and
barcode must be unique per (workspace, property) so scanner-driven
flows resolve unambiguously.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/08-inventory.md` §"Model" (`inventory_item`
  fields), §"Barcode scanning"; `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md`
  §"item"; `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` action
  `inventory.adjust` (the action covers manual adjustments; CRUD
  itself uses `scope.edit_settings` at workspace level).
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InventoryPage.tsx`
  (manager catalog view).
- DB tables from `p1.inventory`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/services/inventory/item_service.py` with `create`,
  `update`, `archive`, `restore`, `list`, `get_by_barcode`,
  `get_by_sku`.
- Uniqueness enforced at the DB layer with a partial unique index
  `(workspace_id, property_id, sku) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL`
  and similarly for `barcode_ean13`.
- `unit` validated against the spec's enumerated set
  (`each|pack|kg|liter|roll|...`); free-form rejected.
- Soft delete via `deleted_at`; restore clears it.
- `unit_cost_cents` updated whenever a `restock` movement records a
  new value (denormalised cache; movement service writes through —
  out of scope here, but the column lives on the model).
- Audit on every mutation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /workspaces/{ws}/properties/{p}/inventory/items` creates
      item; duplicate SKU or barcode → 409.
- [ ] `PATCH` updates allowed fields; unknown fields rejected; SKU
      change re-validates uniqueness.
- [ ] Soft-delete idempotent; restore idempotent.
- [ ] `GET ?barcode=...` returns single item or 404.
- [ ] Audit row per mutation.
- [ ] Unit tests cover uniqueness collisions, soft-delete + create
      with same SKU (allowed), unit validation.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_inventory_items.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_api_inventory_items.py -xvs
```

title: feat(instructions): scope resolver returning merged applicable instruction set
description:
## Problem / goal
The worker app and agent both ask "which instructions apply to this
context?" Inputs are some combination of (template, property, area,
asset, stay, work_role). The resolver returns the deduplicated,
ordered list with provenance, applying the inheritance order spelled
out in spec §07.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/07-instructions-kb.md` §"Resolution: which
  instructions apply to a given task?"; ordering: more specific
  first (area > property > global), then explicit links
  (template/schedule/work_role/task) appended with provenance
  badges; `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"instruction_link" for
  the polymorphic link table.
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionDetailPage.tsx`
  shows what links exist; the worker-side render lives in
  `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/`.

## Implementation notes
- Pure function `resolve_instructions(ctx, *, property_id=None,
  area_id=None, template_id=None, schedule_id=None, task_id=None,
  asset_id=None, stay_id=None, work_role_id=None) → List[ResolvedInstruction]`.
- Each `ResolvedInstruction` carries the instruction id, current
  revision id, body_md, and `provenance` (`scope:area` |
  `scope:property` | `scope:global` | `link:task_template` | etc.).
- Walk in spec order; stop adding once an instruction id is
  already present (highest-specificity provenance wins).
- Pull only `status=active` instructions.
- Unit-test the matrix: each provenance, dedup behaviour, empty
  context (returns globals only), area implies property.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Returns globals when no property given.
- [ ] Returns globals + property when property given, no area.
- [ ] Returns globals + property + area when both given, with area
      first in order.
- [ ] Explicit links (template/schedule/work_role/task) appended,
      each with the right `provenance` badge.
- [ ] Same instruction reachable through two routes appears once,
      labelled with the most-specific provenance.
- [ ] Archived instructions excluded.
- [ ] Unit tests for every quadrant of the resolution matrix.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_instructions_resolver.py -xvs
```

title: feat(instructions): instruction + revision CRUD with auto version bump
description:
## Problem / goal
Owners and managers author instructions (SOPs, house rules, safety
notes). Every body change must mint a new immutable
`instruction_revision` and re-point `current_revision_id`; readers
always see the latest revision.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/07-instructions-kb.md` §"Data model",
  §"Editing semantics"; `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md`
  §"instruction", §"instruction_revision";
  `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` action `instructions.edit`.
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionsPage.tsx`
  (list + filter), `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionDetailPage.tsx`
  (editor + revision history teaser).
- DB models from `p1.instructions`.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/services/instructions/service.py` with
  `create`, `update_metadata` (title/tags/scope without minting a
  revision), `update_body` (mints a new revision, monotonic
  version), `archive`, `restore_to_revision` (ships in
  `p6.version.history` — leave the seam).
- Body change detection: hash the markdown post-normalisation; only
  bump version if the hash changes (matches mock UX where save is
  idempotent).
- Scope validation per spec constraint table: `global` ⇒ no
  property/area; `property` ⇒ property set, area null; `area` ⇒
  both set and property mirrors area's parent.
- Tag list: trim, dedupe, lowercase; cap at 20 tags per instruction.
- Audit on every mutation including the revision id minted.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /workspaces/{ws}/instructions` creates instruction +
      first revision atomically; `version=1`.
- [ ] `PATCH /workspaces/{ws}/instructions/{id}` with body change
      mints a new revision and increments version monotonically.
- [ ] Metadata-only edit does NOT bump the version.
- [ ] Scope constraint violations → 422 (field-specific).
- [ ] Archived instruction can be re-listed with `?include=archived`
      but cannot be edited until restored.
- [ ] Audit rows include `revision_id` for body edits.
- [ ] Unit tests for body-change detection, scope validation, version
      monotonicity, archive idempotency.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_service_instructions.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_api_instructions.py -xvs
```

title: feat(workspace): owner-only settings (name, timezone, locale, currency)
description:
## Problem / goal
A workspace's identity-level settings — display name, default
timezone, default locale, default currency — are mutable but only by
members of the `owners` permission group on the workspace. The rest
of the settings cascade lives elsewhere; this task ships the four
top-level fields plus their audit + cache-invalidation hooks.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"workspace" (the
  base columns), §"Settings cascade" (this task is the
  workspace-layer for the four named keys); `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md`
  §"Surface grants at a glance" (owners as governance anchor);
  `docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md` for the settings UI surface.
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/SettingsPage.tsx` (one
  surface; this task delivers the API + service the page targets).
- Authorization: `scope.edit_settings` on `workspace` scope, but
  the four root-level fields are restricted to `owners` per spec
  intent — gate via `groups.manage_owners_membership`-adjacent
  check using the `owners` group.

## Implementation notes
- Service `app/services/workspace/settings_service.py` with
  `update_basics(ctx, *, name, timezone, locale, currency)`.
- Validate timezone against IANA tz database; locale against the
  shipped locale list (`p0.util`); currency against ISO-4217.
- Changing timezone DOES NOT rewrite stored timestamps (UTC at
  rest); display-time conversion is responsibility of the
  frontend/property tz override layer.
- Bump a `workspace.updated_at` column so SSE subscribers can
  refresh the workspace picker.
- Audit row per change with old/new values per field.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `PATCH /workspaces/{ws}/settings/basics` accepts the four
      fields, partial update, ignores extras.
- [ ] Non-owner caller → 403.
- [ ] Invalid tz / locale / currency → 422 with field-specific
      error.
- [ ] Audit row written with field-level diff.
- [ ] Unit tests: valid update, partial update, each validation
      failure, unauthorized caller.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_workspace_settings.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_api_workspace_settings.py -xvs
```

title: feat(leave): manager approval flow + conflict detection + leave.decided event
description:
## Problem / goal
A manager working the leaves inbox must see, per pending request, any
shifts or scheduled task occurrences that overlap the requested range
so they can make an informed call. On decision, an event fans out so
downstream services (assignment generator, notifications) can react.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` §"Worker settings"
  (note worker view), `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Leave",
  `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md` §"Conflict detection",
  `docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md` §"Events" for the event
  shape, `docs/specs/12-api.md` §"/leaves/{id}/decision".
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/LeavesInboxPage.tsx`.
- Event bus from `p0.events` — publish `leave.decided` (payload:
  `leave_id`, `decision`, `decided_by`, `decided_at`,
  `conflicting_shift_ids`, `conflicting_occurrence_ids`).

## Implementation notes
- New `LeaveDecisionService.decide(leave_id, decision, rationale)`
  takes `WorkspaceContext`; permission-gates on (currently
  hard-coded — wire to `permission_rule` resolver once `p4.permission.enforce`
  lands) `manager` grant_role; stamps `decided_by`/`decided_at`.
- Conflict detection helper queries `Shift` + `Occurrence` for the
  requesting user where `(starts_at, ends_at)` overlaps the leave
  window; returns ids only (UI fetches detail).
- Atomic: decision + audit + event publish in the same transaction
  (use the outbox pattern from `p0.events`).
- Conflicts do NOT block the decision — they're advisory. The
  manager can still approve a leave that overlaps a shift; the
  downstream assignment service is expected to react to the event
  and unassign / reassign.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /workspaces/{ws}/leaves/{id}/decision` with
      `{decision: approved|rejected, rationale_md}` flips status,
      stamps decision metadata.
- [ ] `GET /workspaces/{ws}/leaves/{id}/conflicts` returns
      overlapping shift_ids + occurrence_ids.
- [ ] `leave.decided` event published exactly once per terminal
      transition; replay-safe (decision is immutable post-decide).
- [ ] Audit row records prior status + new status + rationale.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: no conflicts, with conflicts, idempotent
      replay, unauthorized caller.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_leave_approval.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_leave_approval_event.py -xvs
```

title: feat(time/shifts): auto-open on occurrence start, auto-close on completion
description:
## Problem / goal
For schedules where the worker's "shift" is implied by the task itself
(housekeeper arrives = shift starts; checklist done = shift ends),
auto-open and auto-close shifts driven by occurrence lifecycle events.
Configurable per template / property.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Bookings"
  on time-sources; `docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md`
  occurrence states; `docs/specs/00-event-bus.md`.
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx` shows
  shift status driven by occurrence cards.

## Implementation notes
- Subscribe to event-bus topics from Phase 0:
  - `task.occurrence.started` → call `shifts.open_shift(..., source='occurrence')`
    if a config flag (`auto_shift_from_occurrence`) is set on the
    property OR the template.
  - `task.occurrence.completed` → close any matching open shift
    (same user, same property) opened from this occurrence
    (link via new column `shift.source_occurrence_id` nullable).
- Migration `0018_shift_source_occurrence` adds the FK column +
  index.
- Idempotency: re-delivery of the same event must not open a
  duplicate shift (lookup by `source_occurrence_id`).
- A worker who already has an open manual shift on the same property
  is a no-op (audit a `shift.auto_open_skipped` row).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Occurrence start opens a shift when config enabled
- [ ] Occurrence completion closes the auto-opened shift
- [ ] Duplicate event delivery is idempotent
- [ ] Manual shift in flight blocks auto-open (with audit)
- [ ] Config flag respected at both template and property level

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_shift_from_occurrence.py -xvs
```

title: feat(time/geofence): radius enforcement on clock-in (per-property toggle)
description:
## Problem / goal
When a property has geofencing enabled, clock-in must verify the
worker's reported lat/lon is within `radius_m` of the property's
fence centre. Out-of-fence attempts are rejected (or warned, per
config) and audited.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/09-time-payroll-expenses.md` §"Bookings"
  geofence references; `docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md`
  geofence settings.
- **Existing models**: `app/adapters/db/time/models.py`
  (`GeofenceSetting`).
- **Mocks**: `mocks/web/src/pages/employee/MePage.tsx`
  (geofence rejection toast).

## Implementation notes
- `app/domain/time/geofence.py`:
  - `check(ctx, property_id, lat, lon) -> GeofenceVerdict`
    (`ok | outside | disabled | no_fix`).
  - Haversine in `app/util/geo.py`; reuse if present.
- Wire into `open_shift` (Phase 8 task `p8.shift.crud`):
  - Accept optional `client_lat`, `client_lon`, `gps_accuracy_m`.
  - If property has enabled `GeofenceSetting`, require fix; reject if
    distance > `radius_m + gps_accuracy_m`.
  - Tolerance / mode (`enforce` vs `warn`) is feature-flag per
    property — extend `GeofenceSetting` with `mode` enum
    (`enforce|warn|off`).
- Audit row on every rejection (`shift.geofence_rejected` with
  distance + accuracy).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] In-fence open succeeds
- [ ] Out-of-fence with `mode=enforce` returns 422 + audit row
- [ ] Out-of-fence with `mode=warn` succeeds, audit + SSE warning
- [ ] `mode=off` (or feature absent) bypasses checks entirely
- [ ] Missing GPS fix on enforce → 422; on warn → audit only

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_geofence.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_shifts_geofence.py -xvs
```

title: feat(domain/stays): guest welcome pages — /w/<slug>/guest/<token>
description:
## Problem / goal
Public, no-login guest welcome pages served by signed tokens
(\`itsdangerous\`). Shows wifi, house rules, check-out checklist,
emergencies. Token includes \`property_id\` + \`reservation_id\` and a
short TTL; never exposes \`workspace_id\` directly.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Guest welcome
  link" — covers fields, what the page shows, privacy + 410 Gone
  semantics.
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"guest_link".
- **Auth**: \`docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md\` for the signed-token
  format.
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/public/GuestPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (GUEST_LINK fixture).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/stays/guest_link_service.py\` — \`mint_link\`,
  \`revoke_link\`, \`resolve_link\`, \`record_access\`.
- \`itsdangerous.URLSafeTimedSerializer\` keyed off the deployment
  secret; payload \`{stay_id, property_id, exp}\` — no workspace_id.
- Slug obfuscation via the Phase 0 hashids util; route is
  \`/w/<workspace_slug>/guest/<token>\` per existing URL grammar.
- Page renderer merges welcome data: \`stay.wifi_password_override\`
  > \`unit.welcome_overrides_json\` > \`property.welcome_defaults_json\`.
- Filter check-out checklist to items where
  \`guest_visible = true\`.
- Revoke / expiry → 410 Gone with the spec's wording; no stay payload.
- Access log records hashed-IP-prefix + UA-family only (last 10).
- No cookies; no JS beyond the minimal manifest.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Token format signed with TTL; tampered token returns 410
- [ ] Revoked / expired link returns 410 with the spec wording
- [ ] No cookies set on the guest page
- [ ] Welcome merge order matches the spec
- [ ] Equipment section appears only when setting + visible assets
- [ ] Access log truncates to last 10 + hashes IP
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests cover happy, expired, revoked, tampered token paths

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/stays/test_guest_link_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/stays/test_guest_welcome_page.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(domain/stays): stay_bundle generation driven by property templates
description:
## Problem / goal
Generate \`stay_bundle\` rows (welcome, deep-clean, etc.) at stay
ingestion based on property-level lifecycle rule templates. A bundle
groups N occurrences under one logical "stay-task package" so the UI
can render and edit them coherently.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"Stay task
  bundles" — covers trigger types (\`after_checkout\`,
  \`before_checkin\`, \`during_stay\`) and edit semantics (4h shift
  patches in place; bigger shifts cancel + regenerate).
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"stay_bundle".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/StaysPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyDetailPage.tsx\` (Stay rules
  tab), \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (STAY_BUNDLES).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/stays/bundle_service.py\` —
  \`generate_bundles_for_stay\`, \`reapply_bundles_for_stay\`,
  \`cancel_bundles_for_stay\`, \`list_bundles\`.
- Reuses the same \`reservation.upserted\` subscriber as turnover-gen;
  bundle generation is the broader code path that includes turnover.
- Rule lookup walks property → unit cascade for lifecycle rules.
- Honour \`guest_kind_filter\` on each rule.
- Edit semantics per spec: < 4h shift on \`after_checkout\` /
  \`before_checkin\` patches scheduled times in place; otherwise
  cancel \`scheduled|pending\` tasks with reason
  \`stay rescheduled\` and regenerate.
- Each created occurrence carries \`stay_task_bundle_id\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] One bundle per (stay, rule); idempotent on re-fire
- [ ] All three trigger types exercised
- [ ] Edit-in-place < 4h, regenerate >= 4h
- [ ] Cancellation reason recorded on regenerate path
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests cover all three trigger types + edit semantics

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/stays/test_bundle_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/stays/test_bundle_lifecycle.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(domain/places): property_closure CRUD + clash detection + event
description:
## Problem / goal
Property closures (planned downtime, owner blocks, renovations) with
clash detection against existing schedules and stay-driven occurrences
so managers see what they're stepping on. Publishes
\`property.closure.created\` for downstream subscribers (turnover
generator suppresses on closed dates).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/04-properties-and-stays.md\` §"iCal feed"
  ("Polling behavior" → closures from \`Blocked\` VEVENTs),
  \`docs/specs/06-tasks-and-scheduling.md\` (\`property_closure\` is
  the surface that stay/lifecycle rules consult).
- **Domain model**: \`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md\` §"property_closure".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/manager/PropertyClosuresPage.tsx\`,
  \`mocks/app/mock_data.py\` (CLOSURES).

## Implementation notes
- \`app/domain/places/closure_service.py\` —
  \`create_closure\`, \`update_closure\`, \`delete_closure\`,
  \`list_closures\`, \`detect_clashes\`.
- \`detect_clashes\` returns existing schedules + stays whose date
  range overlaps the proposed closure; UI shows them as warnings,
  user confirms anyway.
- On create/update: publish \`property.closure.created\` (or
  \`.updated\`) via the Phase 0 events port with payload
  \`{property_id, starts_at, ends_at, reason, source_ical_feed_id?}\`.
- Honour the \`source_ical_feed_id\` lineage from iCal upserts; manual
  delete is allowed and not recreated unless the upstream VEVENT
  reappears.
- Workspace-scoped via property join; audit every mutation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Clash detection returns overlapping stays + schedules
- [ ] Event published exactly once per mutation, payload validated
- [ ] iCal-sourced closures carry \`source_ical_feed_id\`
- [ ] Manual delete is sticky until upstream re-asserts
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` passes
- [ ] Tests cover clash + event publishing + lineage

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/domain/places/test_closure_service.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/places/test_closure_clash.py -xvs
\`\`\`

## Problem / goal
`mocks/web/src/pages/public/AcceptPage.tsx` and `app/web/src/pages/public/AcceptPage.tsx` both use `<section className="panel panel--inset">`. The `panel--inset` modifier is not defined in either `mocks/web/src/styles/globals.css` or `app/web/src/styles/globals.css` — the section currently falls back to base `.panel` styling.

Other panel modifiers exist (`.panel--article` at line 1250, `.panel--danger` at line 1251 in both files), so the slot is there. The intent of "inset" was likely a tighter padding / recessed background for an in-card panel; needs a quick design call.

## Acceptance criteria

title: feat(db/tasks): widen occurrence.state CHECK to include 'overdue'
description:
cd-me3q self-review exercise surfaced that occurrence.state's CHECK constraint does not include 'overdue', so read_task round-trip tests had to skip that state. The completion module's comment acknowledges the gap (the widening migration lands with the spec-drift follow-up filed alongside cd-7am7). File as its own task so it doesn't get lost.

## Fix
Alembic revision that widens the CHECK on occurrence.state to include 'overdue' (Postgres + SQLite portable — write via batch_alter_table + CheckConstraint(... name='ck_occurrence_state')). Verify the auto-overdue sweeper ( revert_overdue / task.generator) can land an overdue row end-to-end afterwards.

## Acceptance
- Migration adds 'overdue' to the CHECK
- revert_overdue integration test with an overdue source state passes without force-patching metadata
- Downgrade removes 'overdue' cleanly

title: chore(tasks/templates): skip no-delta audit on PATCH /task_templates/{id}
description:
cd-me3q self-review found that update_template (app/domain/tasks/templates.py) always writes a task_template.update audit row even when the PATCH body is identical to the current row (e.g. a retry or an idempotent client PUT). Spammy + wastes audit storage without aiding reconstruction.

## Fix
Mirror the shape now in app/domain/tasks/oneoff.py::update_task: diff before vs after (via _view_to_diff_dict) and skip write_audit when they match. Add a unit test that PATCHes with an unchanged body and asserts no audit row lands.

## Acceptance
- No audit row when the PATCH is a no-op (before == after)
- Unit test coverage for the no-op path
- Existing "field changed → audit row written" coverage still passes

title: feat(api/schedules): window-based preview (?for=30d) + cursor pagination on comments/evidence
description:
**Context.** cd-sn26 shipped `GET /schedules/{id}/preview?n=<int>` — the §12 spec surface is `?for=30d`, i.e. a window-bounded preview. The domain helper `preview_occurrences` already takes `n`; a follow-up should add a `window_days` / `until_local` shape.

Also on the same surface: `GET /tasks/{id}/evidence` currently returns the full set in one page because per-task evidence counts are small. Before that assumption drifts, wire a proper cursor via `CursorPage` (the envelope already carries `next_cursor` / `has_more`).

**Scope**
- `preview_occurrences(..., window_days=...)` or a new `preview_until` helper.
- `GET /schedules/{id}/preview?for=30d` accepts ISO-8601 duration notation.
- `list_evidence` gets an `after_id` + `limit` cursor.
- Router updates to match.

**Acceptance**
- `?for=30d` returns every occurrence in the 30-day window.
- Evidence list paginates via cursor with >500 rows.

title: leaves + shifts — timezone-aware whole-day snap helper
description: cd-31c defers the p0.util tz-snap helper that would align Leave.starts_at / Leave.ends_at (and, by extension, shift windows) to whole days in the worker's local timezone. The helper would live under app/util/time_zone/ alongside the geofence / rota helpers if/when we land it. Today leaves accept any UTC-aware datetime verbatim and persist it as-is; the UI chooses the granularity. Acceptance: add app/util/time_zone/ (or similar) with snap_to_day(ts, tz) -> datetime; wire into LeaveCreate / LeaveUpdateDates validation so a worker in Pacific/Auckland can request 'April 12 2026' and land midnight-to-midnight NZ time in the DB; update spec 09 to require the snap; extend tests with dst-boundary + international-date-line cases. Blockers: none (app.util.time_zone stub may exist for other features by then — check first).

title: docs(spec/11): reconcile workspace_budget/workspace_usage spec names with DB names (BudgetLedger/LlmUsage)
description:
## Problem / goal
docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md §'Workspace usage budget' refers to
two conceptual tables by the names workspace_budget (for the cap) and
workspace_usage (for the materialised aggregate). The code actually
ships them as BudgetLedger and LlmUsage respectively
(see app/adapters/db/llm/models.py). The drift is harmless today
because both conceptual fields live on a single DB table
(BudgetLedger owns period_start / period_end / cap_cents / spent_cents),
but the spec names would mislead any new reader.

## Technical context
- DB: BudgetLedger (period + cap + spent aggregate) + LlmUsage
  (per-call rows).
- Spec: references workspace_budget (cap) + workspace_usage
  (rolling aggregate) as separate conceptual tables.
- cd-irng (test summary) flagged the drift.

## Implementation notes
Two options; pick one via AskUserQuestion:

1. Update the spec to say 'BudgetLedger carries both the cap and the
   materialised aggregate'; drop workspace_budget / workspace_usage
   as names. Cleanest, matches the code.
2. Split BudgetLedger into two tables (workspace_budget for the cap,
   workspace_usage for the aggregate). More faithful to the spec but
   costs a migration and changes the record_usage / check_budget
   transaction shape.

Option 1 is probably right: the unified ledger lets a single row-lock
cover both reads + the bump, which is what record_usage relies on.

## Acceptance criteria
- docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md §'Workspace usage budget' reads
  consistently with app/adapters/db/llm/models.py.
- Or, alternatively, a migration splits the table.

## Related
- cd-irng: landed the budget envelope on top of BudgetLedger as-is.

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: selfreview (cd-myh4): spec files that reference the drifted names are docs/specs/11-llm-and-agents.md, docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md, docs/specs/20-glossary.md. docs/specs/02-domain-model.md already uses BudgetLedger / LlmUsage (correct). Test code paths: tests/domain/llm/test_budget.py, tests/integration/test_db_llm.py, tests/integration/test_schema_parity.py — these use the DB names; no rename needed. Recommendation: Option 1 (update the spec to say 'BudgetLedger carries both the cap and the materialised aggregate'). The domain-model spec is already aligned so the landing surface is small. Rename the §11 subsection 'Meter' body + 'Cap' body + §16 worker registration label; keep the two conceptual functions ('meter' vs 'cap') as prose separators so readers with the old mental model still land on the right rows. Glossary entry: add 'BudgetLedger → the single DB row per-period that pairs the cap with the cached aggregate; spec originally called the two halves workspace_budget + workspace_usage.'

title: feat(llm/router): cross-process invalidation for the model-assignment cache (Redis pub/sub or LISTEN/NOTIFY)
description:
## Problem / goal

``app/domain/llm/router.py`` caches the capability → chain
mapping in-process with a 30 s TTL and an
:class:`LlmAssignmentChanged` SSE-bus subscriber that drops
entries on admin edits. The in-process bus is synchronous and
per-process (``app/events/bus.py``): if the deployment ever
runs more than one uvicorn worker or a dedicated agent-runtime
process, a worker that didn't *publish* the edit keeps its
stale cache until the TTL expires — bounded but real staleness.

Today's §16 deployment recipe is single-worker, so this isn't
a production hazard yet. File as P3 and unpark when a
multi-worker or multi-process recipe lands.

## Technical context

- Single-process trade-off documented in the module docstring
  (``app/domain/llm/router.py`` top-of-file,
  §"Single-process deployment assumption").
- Option A: Postgres ``LISTEN/NOTIFY`` on an ``llm_assignment``
  channel. Cheap, no new infra; only works on Postgres (SQLite
  deployments stay single-process anyway).
- Option B: Redis pub/sub. Needs a new dependency but works on
  both DB backends and is the path already implied by the
  future §23 notification-bus work.
- Bridge shape: whichever option lands, each worker runs a
  background task that listens on the channel and publishes
  :class:`LlmAssignmentChanged` on its local in-process bus, so
  the existing router subscriber code is reused unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A documented cross-process invalidation mechanism is in
      place before any multi-worker deployment ships.
- [ ] Module docstring in ``app/domain/llm/router.py`` updated
      to reference the new mechanism (replacing the current
      "Single-process deployment assumption" caveat).
- [ ] End-to-end test simulating two workers (two
      :class:`EventBus` instances + the bridge) proves an edit
      on one side invalidates the other within one round-trip.

## Out of scope

- Other caches with the same single-process limitation — if
  they appear, they reuse whichever bridge this task lands.

title: sec(auth/recovery): close kill-switch timing side-channel in request_recovery
description:
## Problem / goal
cd-jdhm landed the workspace kill-switch (`auth.self_service_recovery_enabled`). The self-review surfaced a residual timing side-channel in `app.auth.recovery.request_recovery`:

- **Hit branch** (known + not kill-switched): user lookup + HMAC + nonce insert + mailer delivery + audit.
- **Miss branch** (unknown): user lookup + CPU burn (HMAC) + mailer delivery (unknown template) + audit.
- **Kill-switch branch** (known + kill-switched): user lookup + role_grant+workspace join + fresh-UoW audit commit. NO mailer.

The mailer delivery is the largest latency component (tens to hundreds of ms), and the kill-switch branch skips it entirely. An adversary with wall-clock access to the endpoint can distinguish the kill-switch case from hit/miss and learn that a given email belongs to a workspace with self-service recovery disabled — leaking operator policy across the internet.

§15 currently mandates constant-time responses only for the account-existence case (`No workspace enumeration` at docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md:783), but the kill-switch outcome is adjacent: it reveals workspace policy, which is an operator choice but still non-public.

## Technical context
- Spec: `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §"Workspace kill-switch"; `docs/specs/15-security-privacy.md` §"Constant-time cross-tenant responses" + §"Self-service lost-device & email-change abuse mitigations".
- Code: `app/auth/recovery.py::request_recovery` (the gate at ~line 736); `app/auth/recovery.py::_audit_recovery_disabled_by_workspace` (fresh UoW).
- Parallel-path reference: `_burn_cpu_for_miss_branch` balances the HMAC cost across hit/miss; no analogue exists for kill-switch vs. hit timing.

## Implementation notes
Two viable approaches:

1. **Balance the kill-switch path to match the hit branch.** Mint + discard a magic-link token (HMAC cost), pay the mailer round-trip by sending to a null-sink or to the user's own address with a no-op template, commit the fresh UoW at the same moment as the primary UoW. Adds real mail traffic on every kill-switch request — cost may be acceptable given the kill-switch is a deliberate rare state.
2. **Equalise via a synthetic delay.** Record the p95 of the hit-branch cost once at module load, sleep the kill-switch branch to land in the same band. Fragile against future perf changes but cheap.

Option 1 is more robust; option 2 is cheaper. Prefer option 1 if the mailer cost is tolerable.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Decision recorded (option 1 vs option 2) with rationale in the Beads issue.
- [ ] Kill-switch branch wall-clock latency overlaps hit-branch latency within ±5 ms under a steady-load harness (same threshold §15 mandates for the cross-tenant guard).
- [ ] New `test_kill_switch_timing_parity` in `tests/integration/auth/test_recovery_full_flow.py` asserts the timing band.
- [ ] Spec §15 "Self-service lost-device & email-change abuse mitigations" extended with an explicit kill-switch-timing bullet.
- [ ] No change to the 202 wire contract or the existing audit row shape.

## Dependencies
Depends on cd-jdhm (closed).

title: docs(spec): reconcile /api/v1/me/tokens revoke verb — DELETE (impl) vs POST /revoke (§12)
description: cd-i1qe's /api/v1/me/tokens router exposes DELETE /{id} for revoke; spec §12 lines 381-382 document POST /{id}/revoke instead. The workspace-scoped surface (DELETE /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/tokens/{id}) uses DELETE, which the PAT implementation followed for consistency. Reconcile by either: (a) updating §12 to DELETE /me/tokens/{id} to match the workspace pattern (preferred — spec already uses DELETE for the sibling /me/push-tokens/{id}); (b) reworking the router to POST /revoke. Also: §12 documents POST /rotate and GET /audit on /me/tokens which are not implemented yet (same TBD state as on the workspace surface). Noticed during cd-8ax2 selfreview.

title: docs/feat(auth/tokens): reconcile 50-per-workspace cap vs 5-per-user-per-workspace in service + §03
description: §03 'Guardrails' pins 'Workspace cap: 50 live scoped + delegated tokens per workspace' with an error code 'too_many_workspace_tokens'. The cd-c91 implementation in app/auth/tokens.py enforces _MAX_ACTIVE_TOKENS_PER_USER = 5 per user per workspace and raises 'too_many_tokens' — a STRICTER but DIFFERENT cap. Either: (a) add the 50-per-workspace cap alongside the 5-per-user cap, wiring the 'too_many_workspace_tokens' error code and documenting both; or (b) update §03 to drop the workspace-wide 50 cap and keep only the 5-per-user cap. Noticed during cd-8ax2 selfreview of cd-i1qe — not a cd-i1qe regression but worth filing. Acceptance: the spec and the service agree on the cap vocabulary + count, and /w/<slug>/api/v1/auth/tokens surfaces the right 422 error code on the cap trip.

title: feat(api/auth): add operation_id + x-cli + x-interactive-only to /auth/tokens
description: §12 requires every FastAPI route to declare its own operation_id. The workspace-scoped /auth/tokens router (POST, GET, DELETE) does not carry operation_id, x-cli, or x-interactive-only annotations today — noticed during cd-8ax2 selfreview. The /api/v1/me/tokens sibling router (added in cd-i1qe) already carries auth.me.tokens.{mint,list,revoke} so the pattern exists. Apply the same shape to POST/GET/DELETE on /auth/tokens (tokens.mint / tokens.list / tokens.revoke) and regenerate docs/api/openapi.json via /update-openapi.

title: chore(tests): reformat tests/unit/adapters/test_mail_smtp.py
description: ``ruff format --check tests/unit/adapters/test_mail_smtp.py`` fails on 1 block around line 557 (kwargs folding). Formatter drift from a previous ruff version. One-line fix; not touched in this selfreview to stay in scope.

title: authz: role_grants.revoke reads is_owner_member without the cd-mb5n lock (spurious refusal)
description:
## Problem

In `app.domain.identity.role_grants.revoke`, the check `is_owner_member(session, workspace_id=ctx.workspace_id, user_id=row.user_id)` runs **before** `count_owner_members_locked` (cd-mb5n) acquires the owners-group lock. A concurrent `remove_member` that removes `row.user_id` from `owners@<ws>` between the two reads can cause `is_owner_member` to return `True` even though, by the time the lock is acquired, the user is no longer an owner.

Consequence: `count_owner_members_locked` then returns a count that is one **less** than what it would have been; `owner_count <= 1` can trip and raise `LastOwnerGrantProtected` for a revoke that would actually have been safe. This is a false-positive (spurious refusal), not an invariant violation — so the workspace state stays consistent, but the UX is stricter than intended.

## Why P3 and not P2

The refusal is the "safe" side of the race. The invariant holds. User sees "can't revoke — last owner protected" when they could technically have done so. Fixing it properly means moving the `is_owner_member` read under the same lock (simple), but there is no urgency beyond the minor UX inconsistency.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Move the `is_owner_member` check inside the lock window (likely by folding it into `_owner_guard` as a combined "is this user an owner AND how many owners are there" helper).
- [ ] Concurrency test case in `test_last_owner_race.py` that exercises the specific race: thread 1 `revoke(A-grant)`, thread 2 `remove_member(A from owners)`. Assert no spurious `LastOwnerGrantProtected` when the sequence leaves at least one owner behind.

## Refs

- cd-mb5n (introduced the lock; this tightens its scope)
- `app/domain/identity/role_grants.py` `revoke`
- `app/authz/owners.py` `is_owner_member`
- `app/domain/identity/_owner_guard.py`

title: docs(spec): document dev-profile Vite HTTP+WS proxy in §14 / §16

Add a short subsection to §14 and/or §16 that covers the above so

title: Self-review: feat(web/lib): useCloseOnEscape hook + wire across scrim-backed drawers
description:
## Problem / goal
Auto-fixing self-review of the changes made under cd-ukfr. Catch
bugs, missing pieces, and unintended consequences before they ship.

**Depends on: cd-ukfr** (main task must be complete first).

## How to run
Run ``/selfreview`` in **autofix mode** against the commit(s) from
cd-ukfr.

- Do NOT enter plan mode.
- Do NOT ask the user to triage findings — you are the triage.
- Apply fixes for every BUGS, MISSING, and RISKY finding.
- Skip NITPICKS unless trivially safe.
- Particular focus: every scrim-backed drawer in the production
  SPA at HEAD has the hook wired (grep for ``__scrim`` and
  ``role=\"dialog\"`` aside patterns); no double-close against
  native ``<dialog>``; event-listener cleanup on unmount.
- Commit the fixes, push, and close this task.

See ``.claude/skills/selfreview/SKILL.md``.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All BUGS from the self-review fixed
- [ ] All MISSING pieces completed (especially: any drawer
      without the hook wired)
- [ ] All RISKY items mitigated (or justified in a task comment)
- [ ] Linter, formatter, type checker, affected tests all pass
- [ ] Playwright e2e for Esc-close remains green
- [ ] Fixes committed and pushed

title: feat(web/lib): useCloseOnEscape hook + wire across scrim-backed drawers
description:
## Problem / goal
Pressing ``Esc`` should close every scrim-backed drawer across the
production web app, matching the browser-default behaviour for
native ``<dialog>`` modals. The mocks shipped a small hook at
``mocks/web/src/lib/useCloseOnEscape.ts`` (2026-04-22 /specs pass) —
this task ports it to ``app/web/`` and wires it into every drawer
the production SPA implements as it catches up to the mock
inventory.

## Technical context
- **Mock reference implementation**:
  ``mocks/web/src/lib/useCloseOnEscape.ts``
- **Mock consumers**:
  - ``mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InventoryPage.tsx``
    (``InventoryDrawer``)
  - ``mocks/web/src/pages/employee/SchedulePage.tsx``
    (``DayDrawer``)
  - ``mocks/web/src/pages/admin/LlmPage.tsx``
    (prompt drawer)
- **Rule**: skip when the event target sits inside a native
  ``<dialog open>`` — the browser handles that.
- **Spec**: ``docs/specs/14-web-frontend.md`` §"Keyboard and
  accessibility" (informal — this is part of the baseline UX
  contract rather than a numbered rule).

## Implementation notes
- Port the hook byte-for-byte into ``app/web/src/lib/useCloseOnEscape.ts``.
- Wire into the production ``DayDrawer`` (when ``SchedulePage``
  lands, covered by its own task) and ``InventoryDrawer`` (under
  cd-q99o).
- The hook stops propagation after closing so nested drawers
  don't also close on the same keystroke.
- Call from within the drawer component, gated on the prop that
  means 'drawer is open' — most drawers are early-returned on
  ``null``, so ``useCloseOnEscape(onClose)`` at the top of the
  rendered path is enough.
- Any new scrim-backed drawer added later should use this hook;
  make it part of the drawer-building checklist in
  ``.claude/skills/frontend-design/`` if that exists.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Hook file at ``app/web/src/lib/useCloseOnEscape.ts``, identical
      contract to the mock version
- [ ] Wired into every scrim-backed drawer in the production SPA
      that's landed by the time this task runs (grep for
      ``__scrim`` / ``aside role="dialog"`` patterns)
- [ ] Native ``<dialog>`` modals still close via browser Esc — hook
      does not double-fire
- [ ] Playwright test: on the inventory page, open a drawer, press
      Esc, assert drawer is gone
- [ ] Vitest: hook cleanup removes the ``keydown`` listener on
      unmount (spy on ``removeEventListener``)

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/e2e/web/test_drawer_esc.py -xvs
cd app/web && npm run test -- useCloseOnEscape
\`\`\`

title: chore(api/auth/me): plumb real workspace timezone/currency/country/locale once cd-n6p lands
description:
## Problem / goal

app/api/v1/auth/me.py currently hardcodes WorkspaceSummary
timezone=UTC, default_currency=EUR, default_country=FR,
default_locale=en because the v1 Workspace row doesn't carry those
columns yet (cd-n6p owns adding them). The shim is documented in
_DEFAULT_* constants in that module. Track the cleanup so once

and the SPA's per-workspace locale can reflect operator settings.

## Technical context

  _COUNTRY / _LOCALE.
- Upstream: cd-n6p (owner settings — timezone + locale columns on
  the workspace row).
- SPA consumer: app/web/src/types/core.ts Workspace.

## Acceptance criteria

      columns directly from the Workspace model.

## Test plan

  carries them.

title: chore(dev): mailpit port 8025 conflicts with tubededentifrice-mailpit on dev host
description:
The `crewday-mailpit` container defined in `mocks/docker-compose.yml` cannot start on the dev box because port 127.0.0.1:8025 is already owned by `tubededentifrice-mailpit-1`. Nothing in the crew.day compose stack depends on Mailpit at boot, but the magic-link / signup smoke tests will fail until an operator reassigns the published port.

Reproduction:

    docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d mailpit
    # -> 'Bind for 127.0.0.1:8025 failed: port is already allocated'

Options:
- Bind crewday-mailpit to a different loopback port pair (e.g. 1026/8026), matching the sibling fj2 / tubededentifrice patterns.
- Skip the mailpit service entirely in the shared dev stack and document that magic-link verification runs against the host's existing mailpit instance.

Not urgent for cd-sgpl — the rest of the stack (app-api, mocks-api, web-dev, mocks-web-dev) came up and the acceptance criteria passed. Filed during cd-sgpl to keep the follow-up honest.

title: feat(api): admin DELETE variant for POST /users/{id}/reset_passkey (owner-initiated revoke)
description:
## Problem / goal

cd-hiko shipped the owner-only DELETE /auth/passkey/{credential_id} for self-service passkey removal. The cd-hiko docstring (app/api/v1/auth/passkey.py:312, app/auth/passkey.py:822) explicitly defers the owner-initiated-on-behalf-of-another-user variant to the existing POST /api/v1/users/{id}/reset_passkey seam (docs/specs/03 §"Owner-initiated worker passkey reset"). That seam rides on the re-enrolment pipeline (drops ALL passkeys for that user, then mints a magic link).

A narrower admin-DELETE (revoke ONE credential for another user — e.g. a worker left their old phone somewhere but still has the new one) is not yet implemented.

## Scope
- Decide whether the narrower admin revoke is needed, or whether "reset all + re-enrol" is sufficient coverage. Discuss in a short AskUserQuestion before implementing.
- If implementing:
  - Mount under an admin-scoped router (e.g. DELETE /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/passkeys/{credential_id}).
  - Require owners-group membership on every workspace the target user belongs to (same gate as users.reissue_magic_link).
  - Audit passkey.admin_revoked with {target_user_id, by_actor_id} in the diff.
  - Invalidate the target user's sessions with a distinct cause: passkey_admin_revoked (so forensics can distinguish self-service from admin action).
  - Tests: unit + integration covering auth, last-credential behaviour (admin may or may not be allowed to remove the target's last credential — decide in scope).

## Refs
- app/auth/passkey.py revoke_passkey() (self-service baseline)
- docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md §"Owner-initiated worker passkey reset"

title: feat(web/public): preserve ?next= across login ↔ recover navigation
description: ## Problem A user who arrives at `/login?next=/property/abc` and clicks "Lost your device?" lands on `/recover`, losing the `next` param. After they recover (or click the back-link), they land on their role home, NOT the page they originally wanted. The two bare-href links involved: - `app/web/src/pages/public/LoginPage.tsx:170` — `Lost your device? …` - `app/web/src/pages/public/RecoverPage.tsx:218` — `← Back to sign in` Both drop the query-string. ## Scope - When `/login` has a `?next=`, propagate it into the `/recover` href as `/recover?next=` — only if `sanitizeNext()` passes, so we don't round-trip an attacker's crafted value. - When `/recover` is given a `?next=`, the "← Back to sign in" link must carry it back to `/login?next=` — again post-sanitise. - Both useLocation() lookups, with `sanitizeNext` wrapping the parsed value before assembling the href. ## Acceptance - [ ] `/login?next=/property/abc` → click "Lost your device?" → URL is `/recover?next=/property/abc`. - [ ] `/recover?next=/property/abc` → click "← Back to sign in" → URL is `/login?next=/property/abc`. - [ ] `/login?next=https%3A%2F%2Fevil.example/` → click recover → URL is `/recover` (crafted value dropped by `sanitizeNext`). - [ ] Unit tests in LoginPage.test.tsx + RecoverPage.test.tsx. ## Context - `sanitizeNext` already exists at `app/web/src/auth/onUnauthorized.ts` and is covered by `onUnauthorized.test.ts`. - Found during /selfreview of cd-gids; low-severity UX follow-up, safe to schedule after the spec reconciliation lands.

title: idempotency: request-body size cap before hashing
description:
## Problem / goal
\`app.api.middleware.idempotency.IdempotencyMiddleware.dispatch\` calls \`await request.body()\` unconditionally. An authenticated client can therefore POST an arbitrarily large body and force the middleware to buffer the full payload in memory before hashing — a multi-GB body would OOM the worker.

Today the only mitigations are:

- The middleware only runs for authenticated (token_id-bearing) requests, so an unauthenticated attacker cannot trigger the read.
- The reverse-proxy (Caddy in dev, the deployment's ingress in prod) typically enforces its own body limit.

Neither is strong enough by itself. Add a per-middleware byte cap (default 10 MB, configurable via settings) and return 413 \`payload_too_large\` when exceeded — BEFORE buffering the whole body.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`IdempotencyMiddleware\` honours a \`max_body_bytes\` setting (default 10 MB).
- [ ] Streaming read stops early if the threshold is crossed; returns 413 problem+json.
- [ ] Unit test exercises the over-cap path.
- [ ] Doc (either spec §12 or §15) names the cap + behaviour.

## Priority
P3 — mitigation exists at the ingress layer; this is belt-and-suspenders defence in depth.

title: idempotency: batch-delete on TTL sweep to avoid giant DELETE on big backlogs
description:
## Problem / goal
\`app.api.middleware.idempotency.prune_expired_idempotency_keys\` currently issues a single \`DELETE FROM idempotency_key WHERE created_at < cutoff\`. With a modest request rate (say 10k POSTs/hour × 24 h = 240k rows), that's fine. With a burst (retry storm, abusive client, or after a missed sweep window) the table can hold millions of rows and a single DELETE locks the table on Postgres / churns through WAL on SQLite.

Add an optional \`batch_size\` parameter that loops \`DELETE ... WHERE id IN (SELECT id ... LIMIT batch_size)\` until no rows are deleted. Default to something reasonable (10_000) so operators can tune.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] \`prune_expired_idempotency_keys\` accepts \`batch_size: int | None = None\` (None = unbounded, matching today's behaviour).
- [ ] When \`batch_size\` is set, the helper loops deleting ≤ batch_size rows per iteration until the round-trip returns 0.
- [ ] Returns the aggregate rowcount across batches.
- [ ] Unit test exercises the batched path (seeding e.g. 25 rows, batch_size=10, confirms 3 round-trips and 25 deletions).
- [ ] Scheduler wiring (once APScheduler lands per cd-pnbn / cd-3p3z) passes a sensible default batch_size in production.

## Priority
P3 — not a blocker. The table stays modest under normal load; this is insurance against a bad actor or a long scheduler outage.

## Links
- cd-z6fk: original middleware landing.
- cd-4p2p: self-review that identified the gap.

title: Add HTTP-level test for DELETE /users/{id}/grants owners-guard
description:
Context
-------
app/api/v1/users.py:283 delete_grants wires the LastOwnerMember
domain refusal to a 422 would_orphan_owners_group envelope and a
forensic audit row written on a fresh UoW.

Current coverage:
- Unit: domain service raises LastOwnerMember when appropriate.
- Integration probe: error envelope shape when any handler raises
  LastOwnerMember.

Missing:
- End-to-end HTTP test that exercises the actual route.

Acceptance
----------
- New integration test under tests/integration/api/ that:
  - Seeds a workspace with a single owner.
  - Authenticates as that owner.
  - Calls DELETE /w/<slug>/api/v1/users/<owner_id>/grants.
  - Asserts 422 status + application/problem+json content-type.
  - Asserts RFC 7807 envelope with type ending in
    /problems/would_orphan_owners_group.
  - Asserts exactly one member_remove_rejected audit row on a fresh
    read (proving the forensic write survived the primary UoW
    rollback).
  - Owner still has grants + session after the refusal.

title: nit(tasks/templates): escape LIKE metacharacters (%, _) in list_templates q filter
description:
Post-cd-0tg follow-up.

app/domain/tasks/templates.py:679 builds the 'q' LIKE needle as f'%{q.strip().lower()}%' without escaping the SQL LIKE metacharacters '%' and '_'. If a user types 'kitchen_deep' or '10% off', those characters are interpreted as wildcards and match more rows than typed.

Not a security issue (queries are parameterised, no injection) but a subtle UX trap.

Fix: add an escape helper like needle = '%' + re.sub(r'[\\%_]', r'\\\0', q.strip().lower()) + '%' and append ESCAPE '\\' to the LIKE (SQLAlchemy's like accepts escape=). Verify on both SQLite and PG.

Keep ASCII-safe case-folding for now; multi-lingual search is a separate concern per docs/specs/18-i18n.md.

title: refactor(auth/magic_link): public accessor for just-minted token
description:
`app.auth.magic_link.request_link` emits a signed token inside the generic magic-link template; callers who need the signed URL for a different email (e.g. the invite surface in cd-jpa) resort to a `_CapturingMailer` shim that parses the URL out of `body_text`.

## Technical context
See `app.domain.identity.membership._CapturingMailer` — works but is fragile to template drift.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Carve a public `request_link_token` or similar that returns the signed URL without triggering the mailer
- [ ] Update `membership.invite` to use the new accessor
- [ ] Drop `_CapturingMailer`
close_reason: Fixed inline during cd-o5u selfreview: request_link now accepts send_email=False and returns the signed URL, membership.invite uses it directly, _CapturingMailer removed.

title: refactor(tenancy/csrf): lift shared _is_skip_path + SKIP_PATHS into app.http.skip_paths
description:
## Problem
`app.auth.csrf._is_skip_path` duplicates verbatim the `_is_skip_path` helper + `SKIP_PATHS` import pattern from `app.tenancy.middleware`. Both the tenancy middleware and the CSRF middleware consult the same bare-host skip list for the same reason (health/auth/static entry points don't carry the cookie session), and the csrf module's own docstring flags this as a 'split this constant out' candidate if the lists ever diverge in intent.

A future middleware (e.g., rate-limit layer, audit layer) would be a third consumer — that's the 'rule of three' DRY trigger.

## Scope
1. Create `app/http/skip_paths.py` exporting `SKIP_PATHS: frozenset[str]` and `is_skip_path(path: str) -> bool`.
2. Replace `app.tenancy.middleware._is_skip_path` with an import from the new module; keep `SKIP_PATHS` re-exported for backcompat.
3. Replace `app.auth.csrf._is_skip_path` with an import from the new module; drop the private helper.
4. Update tests that import `SKIP_PATHS` from either module — both should keep working if the re-export stays.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Single source of truth for skip-paths + match rule
- [ ] Tenancy + CSRF middlewares both delegate to it
- [ ] Unit tests green on both modules

## Notes
Discovered during cd-cyq self-review (cd-7m6). No behaviour change; pure refactor.

Refs: `app/auth/csrf.py:110`, `app/tenancy/middleware.py:121`, `docs/specs/03-auth-and-tokens.md` §'Sessions'.

title: test(tenancy): clarify empty-slug middleware behavior and add catch-all guard test
description:
The test test_empty_slug_returns_404 (tests/unit/test_tenancy_middleware.py) passes because FastAPI's router returns 404 for /w//api/v1/ping — not because the middleware actively rejects it. The middleware calls call_next (no WorkspaceContext set) and the router 404s incidentally.

The test comment says 'not parseable' implying middleware rejection, but the actual path is: _parse_scoped_path returns None -> middleware passes request through -> FastAPI 404s.

If a catch-all route like @app.get('/w/{rest:path}') were registered, it would receive this request without a WorkspaceContext and respond with bound=False.

Fix: 1) Update the test comment to accurately describe the mechanism. 2) Add a test that registers a /w/{rest:path} catch-all and asserts that /w//api/v1/ping does NOT reach it with a bound WorkspaceContext (currently passes because FastAPI's {rest:path} does match it, returning bound=False — verify this is acceptable or add middleware-level rejection of empty slugs).

Reference: app/tenancy/middleware.py _parse_scoped_path + dispatch, tests/unit/test_tenancy_middleware.py::test_empty_slug_returns_404

title: docs: reconcile cd-ckr task text — five system groups vs spec's four
description:
## Problem

The cd-ckr task description lists **five** system groups to seed on
workspace bootstrap:

> The hook seeds `owners@<ws>` (system=True, slug=`owners`,
> capabilities = full action catalog) + the four other system groups
> (`managers`, `workers`, `clients`, `chat-gateway-agents`).

`docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"permission_group" §"System groups"
canonically lists **four**: `owners`, `managers`, `all_workers`,
`all_clients`.

The implementation (cd-ckr) followed the spec — it seeds four groups
only. The `chat-gateway-agents` slug is NOT present anywhere in
production code. The integration test
`tests/integration/identity/test_owners_governance.py::TestFourSystemGroups`
pins the exact four so any drift would fail loudly.

## Decision needed

1. **Four groups** (status quo) — update cd-ckr description to match,
   retire the `chat-gateway-agents` reference. Chat-gateway agents
   are identified via `api_token.role='agent'` (§03 "Machine
   tokens"), not a permission group.
2. **Five groups** — add the fifth to `_NON_OWNERS_SYSTEM_GROUPS`,
   update the spec + integration test's `_EXPECTED_SYSTEM_SLUGS`,
   and document what `chat-gateway-agents` actually constrains at
   resolution time.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Decision recorded and cd-ckr description updated accordingly
- [ ] If (1): cd-ckr description edited, no code change; note added
      to `docs/specs/05-employees-and-roles.md` §"Machine tokens"
      clarifying chat-gateway tokens do NOT get a system group
- [ ] If (2): `app/adapters/db/authz/bootstrap.py::_NON_OWNERS_SYSTEM_GROUPS`
      extended, `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"permission_group"
      §"System groups" amended, test updated
- [ ] `/audit-spec` run once the decision lands

## Refs

- cd-ckr task description (source of the drift)
- `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"permission_group" §"System groups"
- `app/adapters/db/authz/bootstrap.py` `_NON_OWNERS_SYSTEM_GROUPS`
- `tests/integration/identity/test_owners_governance.py::TestFourSystemGroups`
- Flagged in cd-ckr self-review (cd-qaj)

title: Self-review: test(billing/quote): verify quote-send email in Mailpit as part of cd-xesk flow
description:
