title: test(billing/quote): verify quote-send email in Mailpit as part of cd-xesk flow
description:
## Problem / goal
**cd-xesk** adds quote CRUD + send (mailer) + accept/reject. Once
Mailpit is live in dev (cd-5ypd), extend the quote-send coverage so an
agent can prove: POST /quotes/{id}/send → real SMTP dispatch → message
in Mailpit → accept link on the embedded URL flips the quote's status.

## Technical context
- **Depends on: cd-5ypd** (Mailpit), **cd-xesk** (quote flow exists),
  **cd-o62m** (reusable Mailpit helper).
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\` (quote flow —
  verify the exact section when cd-xesk lands), and
  \`docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md\` §\"Transport\".

## Implementation notes
- Reuse the Mailpit polling helper from cd-o62m.
- Assert on: subject template, presence of accept/reject links, and
  that hitting the accept link (as an unauthenticated client,
  per-link token) transitions the quote to \`accepted\`.
- Don't re-test quote CRUD — cd-xesk owns that. Target the email leg
  specifically.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] New test proves quote-send email lands in Mailpit and the
      embedded accept link works.
- [ ] \`pytest\` passes with the dev stack up; skips cleanly when
      down.
- [ ] \`mypy --strict\` / \`ruff check\` pass.

## Test plan

### Automated
\`\`\`bash
docker compose -f mocks/docker-compose.yml up -d --build
pytest tests/integration/billing/test_quote_mailpit.py -xvs
\`\`\`

### Manual
1. Create a quote via the UI / API against the dev stack.
2. Send it to a test address; confirm Mailpit receives it.
3. Click accept in the rendered email; confirm quote status flips.

title: feat(marketplace): vendor directory + service requests + match flow scaffolding (deferred-feature seam)
description:
## Problem / goal
Marketplace is **deferred** in v1 (§25 explicitly: "Not in v1");
the design exists so v1 doesn't quietly foreclose the feature.
This task lands the **scaffolding only** — the entity tables,
the capability gate (`settings.marketplace_enabled`, default
`false`), the API skeleton with all routes returning 404 unless
enabled, and the placeholder web pages — so a future enablement
is a translation + UX polish job rather than a months-long build.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/25-marketplace.md` §"Design summary" (one
  marketplace per deployment, auth-required, discovery-only,
  `platform_fee_event` ledger, capability-gated default off),
  §"Entities (shape)" (`marketplace_listing`, `service_request`,
  `marketplace_match`, `platform_fee_event`),
  §"How it plugs into §22" (auto-creates a
  `property_workspace_invite` on accept; `accrue_platform_fees`
  worker), §"Privacy and cross-workspace boundary" (listings
  publishable, requests semi-public, match acceptance triggers
  §15 cross-workspace redactions).
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/16-deployment-operations.md` §"Recipe D"
  (managed SaaS is the intended host).

## Implementation notes
- New migration `marketplace_v1`: creates the four tables exactly
  as §25 describes (`marketplace_listing`, `service_request`,
  `marketplace_match` with `source_match_id` FK back into
  `property_workspace_invite`, `platform_fee_event` append-only
  ledger). Tables are deployment-scope (no `workspace_id`); they
  reference workspaces by FK.
- New domain module `app/domain/marketplace/`:
  - `listings.py`, `requests.py`, `matches.py`, `fees.py` services.
  - All actions gated by `caps.has("settings.marketplace_enabled")`;
    when off, the service raises `FeatureDisabled` and the API
    returns 404.
- API routes under `app/api/v1/marketplace/`:
  - `GET/POST /marketplace/listings`, `GET/POST
    /marketplace/requests`, `POST /marketplace/matches/propose`,
    `POST /marketplace/matches/{id}/accept` (unconditionally
    approval-gated — `x-agent-confirm` annotation required),
    `POST /marketplace/matches/{id}/cancel`.
  - Every route 404s when `settings.marketplace_enabled = false`.
- Web pages (placeholders): `mocks/web/src/pages/marketplace/
  ListingDirectoryPage.tsx`, `RequestBoardPage.tsx`, `MatchPage.tsx`.
  Pages render an "enable marketplace in deployment settings"
  empty state when capability off.
- Worker `accrue_platform_fees`: subscribes to
  `booking_billing.resolved` + `vendor_invoice.approved`; writes
  one `platform_fee_event` row per matching pair; idempotent on
  the (match_id, source_kind, source_id) tuple. Disabled when
  capability off.
- Capability seam `features.postgis` registered (default off);
  GeoJSON columns stored as JSONB on PG, TEXT on SQLite — point-
  in-polygon done in application code via Shapely until PostGIS
  is enabled.
- All entity types added to the LLM tool surface only **when**
  `settings.marketplace_enabled = true`; default schema does not
  expose them to the agent.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Migration applies on SQLite + PG; `features.postgis` capability
      is registered (default off)
- [ ] Every API route returns 404 when capability off; documented
      operator setting flips it on
- [ ] When on, `marketplace_listing` CRUD + `service_request` CRUD
      + `marketplace_match.propose / accept` end-to-end works
- [ ] Match acceptance auto-creates a `property_workspace_invite`
      with the correct `source_match_id` back-pointer
- [ ] `accrue_platform_fees` writes one ledger row per qualifying
      `booking_billing` / `vendor_invoice` event; never updates
- [ ] Cross-workspace privacy redactions kick in on accept
      (guest identity hidden unless `share_guest_identity=true`)
- [ ] Web placeholder pages render correctly under both states
- [ ] OpenAPI document reflects the routes only when enabled
      (spec build flips the include flag)

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/integration/marketplace/test_capability_gate.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/marketplace/test_match_invite.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/marketplace/test_fee_accrual.py -xvs
cd mocks/web && pnpm vitest run src/pages/marketplace
```

title: feat(billing): vendor_invoice record + PDF upload + approve/pay state
description:
## Problem / goal
Record an invoice received from a vendor org, attach the PDF, and
push it through receive → approved → paid (or disputed). Backs
the manager's accounts-payable surface.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\`
  §"\`vendor_invoice\`", §"Invariants",
  §"Approval flow", §"Proof of payment (client upload)",
  §"Payment-due reminders".

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/billing/vendor_invoices.py\`:
  \`VendorInvoiceService(ctx)\` with
  \`create / attach_pdf / approve / mark_paid / dispute / list\`.
- PDF upload via the storage layer (Phase 0); records
  \`pdf_blob_hash\` only.
- \`approve\` requires capability \`billing.invoice.approve\`;
  \`mark_paid\` requires \`billing.invoice.pay\` and a \`paid_at\`,
  payment method, optional proof-of-payment blob.
- State machine guarded; bad transitions → 422.
- Audit each transition; publish \`vendor_invoice.paid\` so a future
  reconciliation worker can match it.
- Compute \`due_at\` reminder windows for the digest worker (date
  math only, sending lives elsewhere).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Cannot approve an invoice without a PDF attached
- [ ] Cannot mark paid without method + paid_at
- [ ] Unique constraint (\`vendor_org_id\`, \`invoice_number\`) — dup
      detection surfaces a clear error
- [ ] State transitions audited; capability checks tested
- [ ] Unit + API tests; OpenAPI updated

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/billing/test_vendor_invoices.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/billing/test_vendor_invoices_api.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(billing): quote CRUD + send (mailer) + accept/reject
description:
## Problem / goal
Draft, send, and track acceptance of a quote to a client. Sending
emails the client a tokenised acceptance link; accept/reject
toggle status and timestamp.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: \`docs/specs/22-clients-and-vendors.md\` §"\`quote\`",
  §"\`lines_json\` shape", §"Acceptance",
  §"Supersession and rejection".
- **Mocks**: \`mocks/web/src/pages/client/QuotesPage.tsx\`.

## Implementation notes
- New \`app/domain/billing/quotes.py\`:
  \`QuoteService(ctx)\` with
  \`create / update / send / accept / reject / supersede\`.
- \`send\`: render Jinja template, mail via Phase 0 mailer with
  HTML + plain-text parts; embed signed accept token (HMAC, 30-day
  expiry) targeting \`/q/{id}?token=...\`.
- Public accept/reject endpoint validates token + idempotently
  updates status; logs a \`billing.quote.accepted/rejected\` audit
  with \`actor_kind=guest_token\`.
- \`supersede\`: cloning quote points back via
  \`superseded_by_quote_id\`.
- Editing locked once \`status != draft\`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Cannot edit a sent quote; must \`supersede\`
- [ ] Token verifier rejects expired / tampered tokens (test)
- [ ] One email per send; resend allowed and audited
- [ ] Public accept/reject works without a logged-in session
- [ ] Unit + API + e2e mailer tests

## Test plan
\`\`\`bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/billing/test_quotes.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/api/billing/test_quotes_api.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/billing/test_quote_send_accept.py -xvs
\`\`\`

title: feat(inventory): task.completed hook emits negative-delta consumption
description:
## Problem / goal
When a task whose template carries `inventory_consumption_json` is
completed, the system should emit one negative `consume` movement
per item in that map, attributing the movement to the completing
user and the source task. Failure to consume must NOT fail the task
completion (per spec §"Consumption on task completion": "soft
coupling").

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/08-inventory.md` §"Consumption on task
  completion", §"Asset action consumption"; settings cascade key
  `inventory.consume_on_task` resolved against the completing user
  (per §02 settings cascade).
- Event: `task.completed` from `p5.completion`; consumed deltas via
  the movement service from `p7.movement`.

## Implementation notes
- Subscriber `app/services/inventory/consumption_subscriber.py`
  on `task.completed`:
  1. Resolve `inventory.consume_on_task` setting for the
     completing user (via the cascade resolver — re-use, do NOT
     re-implement).
  2. If false, return.
  3. Read `task.template.inventory_consumption_json`; for each
     `(item_id, qty)`, call
     `movement_service.consume(item_id, -qty, source_task_id)`.
  4. Wrap each in its own try/except — log + emit
     `inventory.consumption_failed` audit on failure, never raise
     (soft coupling).
- Negative `on_hand` permitted; downstream digest surfaces it
  (`inventory.stock_drift` event handled by `p7.reorder` /
  notifications, not here).
- Asset-action consumption (per spec §"Asset action consumption")
  flows through the same path because asset actions copy
  `inventory_consumption_json` onto generated task templates —
  no separate code path needed; verify in test.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Task completion with non-empty consumption map emits one
      movement per entry; `source_task_id` set.
- [ ] Consumption disabled by setting → no movements.
- [ ] Movement service failure on one item does NOT block the
      others nor raise to the completion event.
- [ ] Items not in the workspace silently skipped + audit warned.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: enabled / disabled, partial failure,
      asset-action-derived template path.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_inventory_task_consumption.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_inventory_task_consumption_event.py -xvs
```

comments.author: tubededentifrice-dev
comments.text: 2026-04-22 /specs revision: rename scope to 'inventory effects hook' — the subscriber now emits **both** negative consume AND positive produce movements in a single transaction, driven by task_template.inventory_effects_json (list of {item_ref, kind: consume|produce, qty}). Old field name inventory_consumption_json is gone. Setting gate renamed: inventory.apply_on_task (legacy inventory.consume_on_task still resolves). Task template field: inventory_effects_json with entry shape {item_ref, kind, qty}. Asset-action path is unchanged (§21 action copies effects onto generated templates). See §06 'Completing a task' #3 and §08 'Inventory effects on task completion'.

title: feat(inventory): reorder rule engine + low-stock notification
description:
## Problem / goal
When stock for an item drops to or below its `reorder_point`, the
system should ensure exactly one open restock task exists and emit a
`inventory.low_stock` event so notifications and external dashboards
can react. Hourly worker job runs the check; movement events trigger
incremental re-checks.

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/08-inventory.md` §"Reorder logic"; event bus
  `docs/specs/10-messaging-notifications.md`.
- DB tables from `p1.inventory`; restock task creation depends on the
  task service (eventually `p5.assignment` will own task creation
  helpers — until then, call the existing template service directly).

## Implementation notes
- Worker job `app/jobs/inventory_reorder.py` registered with the
  scheduler (runs hourly).
- Subscriber on `inventory.item_changed` for incremental triggering
  on the items just touched.
- For each item where `on_hand <= reorder_point` and no open
  restock task exists for (property, item), create one from the
  property's "restock" template (default name "Restock {item}",
  role-assigned via the workspace's `restock_assignee_role`
  setting — fall back to `property_manager`).
- Emit `inventory.low_stock` (payload: workspace_id, property_id,
  item_id, on_hand, reorder_point, restock_task_id).
- Idempotent: if a restock task is already open, no second one is
  created and no second event is fired (dedupe on item_id).
- Audit row when a restock task is auto-created (action
  `inventory.auto_restock_created`).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Below threshold → restock task created + event fired (once).
- [ ] Subsequent dips while task is open → no new task, no new
      event.
- [ ] Restock task completion is handled by the existing task
      completion flow (out of scope; only verify the seam).
- [ ] Items above threshold ignored.
- [ ] Hourly job is idempotent.
- [ ] Unit tests + integration test exercising full
      movement → low-stock → task → completion → movement loop.

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_inventory_reorder.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_inventory_reorder_loop.py -xvs
```

title: feat(instructions): version history view + restore-to-revision
description:
## Problem / goal
Authors need to see the change history of an instruction and roll
back to a prior revision. Restore is implemented as "mint a new
revision whose body equals revision N's body" — never mutate or
delete an existing revision (immutable per §07).

## Technical context
- **Spec**: `docs/specs/07-instructions-kb.md` §"Editing semantics"
  ("every save creates a new revision"), §"Bulk operations"
  (rescope mention); `docs/specs/02-domain-model.md` §"instruction_revision"
  (immutable).
- **Mock**: `mocks/web/src/pages/manager/InstructionDetailPage.tsx`
  shows the revisions side panel.

## Implementation notes
- Service methods on the existing instructions service:
  - `list_revisions(instruction_id)` — paginated, newest first,
    each row carries author, created_at, change_note, version.
  - `restore(instruction_id, revision_id, change_note)` — copies
    body_md + summary_md + attachments from the chosen revision into
    a brand-new revision (via the regular `update_body` path).
- The new revision's `change_note` is auto-prefixed with
  `"Restored from v{n}: "` if no explicit note is given.
- Audit row records `restored_from_revision_id` alongside the new
  revision id.
- Endpoint: `GET /workspaces/{ws}/instructions/{id}/revisions`
  + `POST /workspaces/{ws}/instructions/{id}/restore`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] List endpoint returns revisions newest-first with author +
      change_note.
- [ ] Restore creates a new revision with the prior body, increments
      version, never mutates the source revision.
- [ ] Restoring to the current revision is a 409 (no-op explicitly
      rejected — keeps the ledger clean).
- [ ] Audit row carries `restored_from_revision_id`.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: list pagination, restore happy path, restore
      to current rejected, restore by non-author allowed (only the
      author of the new revision is the caller).

## Test plan
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_instructions_versions.py -xvs
uv run pytest tests/integration/test_api_instructions_revisions.py -xvs
```
